reviews.comment: CSYE 6225 is a must have with a lot hands-on. Prof Alomari teaching this course is so engaging. You learn linux, public and private cloud, virtualization, and even architecture. He give access to private cloud Pextra and AWS. Many assignments and quizzes. He is one off the most knowledgeable in this topic and very caring and help us a lot.

This is my second course with Dr. Alomari.He designed the LLMOps class and gave us a full textbook that he made and all the slides. He is always prepared and supportive. I also took CSYE 6225 with him. We had weekly project updates with others in class asking questions. Was excellent experience. I respect his work and appreciate him a lot.

I took DAMG7245 with Professor Alomari. It is heavy on AWS Glue and Sagemaker. Many hands-on labs and a lot of practice. Almost half of each class is practice. Provides us with options for projects and extra credit. His expectations are a little high. He prepared all material and did a fantastic job. Definitely recommend any course he teaches.
The teachings were very nice

The course (big data analytics) was very intense. Too many assignments including labs from three courses of AWS Academy. As long as you do you assignments each week, it is manageable. The professor is very knowledgeable and professional. He allows late assignments when asked. A lot of AWS handson Glue and Sagemaker.

I took DAMG 7245 with Dr. Alomari. We just took our final exam. It was really a tough course but useful. Over 15 assignments. some on AWS academy. some on Glue and Sagemaker. I worked really hard. He prepares his own material. no textbook. Also gave us access to three AWS academy courses. Even though  it was intense but no regrets.

I just completed CSYE6225 with Professor Alomari. First, this course is a must have with him. He mixes the course between knowledge and practice. I never expected I will be able to use cloud resources this fast and with the breadth of services we learned.He also gave us access to his company private cloud software. Add to this that he is very nice

I took cloud computing course with Professor Alomari based on recommendations and I definitely recommend this course. This course has both lectures and hands-on. Almost each class was half dedicated for hands-on. Labs and assignments are heavy but definite worth it. I learned a LOT. HWs are demanding but very useful. Great Prof and very kind.

I took CSYE6225 with Professor Alomari. Be ready to learn and practice. Very information heavy but very spaced throughout the semester. He also keeps taking survey and adjust things based on the feedback. Always share the feedback with all of us. Never seen a professor so well organized and knowledgeable. He has a lot of industry experience.

Had no experience cloud and after I took this class, I feel I learned a lot. Professor Alomari is supper efficient. Very well organized. prepares own slides. He also took feedback using google forms multiple times and adjusted based on our feedback. I registsred another course with him next semester. I would take any course he offers.

By far the best. I took cloud computing with Professor Alomari in Silicon Valley campus. His passion really shows. Second half of each lecture is hands-on work. He prepares the material and shares the slides. Quizzes, labs, midterm, and final. An optional project (extra credit). I would take any class he offers.

I took the cloud computing course CSYE6225 with Professor Alomari, and he was outstanding. His passion for the subject really shows. He prepared his own slides and did hands-on demos every week. The assignments varied in difficulty, which helped me learn a lot. too many easy quizzes. A must take course. Only in person in Oakland.

It really sucks because he genuinely cares a lot and wants everyone to succeed. But his lectures are all over the place, he'll make mistakes not only when doing example problems but on the exams itself, in which case he'll just omit the entire question usually. Diff eq is brutally difficult course.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do the homework, he is very nice and there to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: He is actually so sweet, I've taken him twice, those who rate him low honestly it might just be not their learning style. He is very organized and if you show up to class you will get SO much info on what tests or quizzes are on. He uses a lot of direct homework problems for quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: occasionally makes mistakes. exams are tough.

It's somewhat annoying that he doesn't use slides and writes everything on the whiteboard by hand, but he is ultra passionate about what he teaches and loves to answer questions if you're confused. I'm not going to lie and say he's the best professor in the math department, but he's better than the bottom half of math dept. professors.

Can def see how he's disliked. Very hit or miss in terms of lectures. Some are good, others are very disorganized. He'll spend most of the class on an example, make a couple mistakes and then decide to not ever go over that topic again. However, he is so easy to approach out side of class and is willing to help and set up up as many meeting as u ne

He's nice and cares about his students. His lectures could use some more organization; his quizzes can be poorly written; sometimes he takes a while to reply to emails; but he's very approachable and always willing to set up meetings if you can't make his office hours. Just show up with an effort to learn and you'll be good

Do not take this professor. You will not do well and he is completely inaccessible outside of the classroom. You spend hours and hours studying and his lectures are beyond useless. He also puts no review material or notes on Canvas so basically ChatGPT will be your professor. Just don't do it.

He runs himself in circles and often makes mistakes. He is very hard grader and uses very hard examples in class while learning the material. Would not recommended at all. He is a genuine guy just not a good teacher.
Would often make mistakes in class until someone points it out. The instructor is down to earth and takes a long time to respond to emails.
poor feedback, but funny instructor. There are likely better professors out there.

His classes are pretty useless they have zero structure to them whatsoever. Instead, answers questions regarding the homework problems. If there aren't questions, he will wait until there are questions and he will often make mistakes when doing examples. In addition, he doesn't post practice quizzes/tests and responds to emails many days after.
Instructor can be occasionally awkward and skips steps during lecture. The test material is insufficient compared to the difficulty of the exam.

It's disheartening to see a professor who genuinely cares about students fall short. He's easily one of the kindest professors I've had, but the cons outweigh the pros. He often comes in unprepared, skips steps in explanations, and gives difficult quizzes graded on just one or two questions. His test prep is poor, and the study guides are lacking.

Good professor. Weekly quizzes are based on homework, so doing the homework and showing up to class sets you up for an A. He's nice, learns names, and will give you points back if you think you were graded unfairly. Class isn't easy, but putting in the work is worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Enthusiastic! He must have had little patience before, but made obvious effort to change. Kind & clearly wants to create positive experience for students. Always very patient with questions makes sure to be most helpful for everyone. 
Homeworks only had answers, no worked out solutions so hard to correct your mistakes without going to office hours.

this is the worst professor i've ever had in my entire life. he makes absolutely no sense. i hate the fact that I'm paying for this class. if northeastern wants to be a good school it should get decent professors. no one I have talked to has had anything good to say abt this guy
he is a very bad professor. take anyone but him. i have to go to other professors class or teach myself because he makes absolutely no sense.

Spends 30 or 40 minutes diving into a random problem that does not look like the homework or the test. Fragments the big picture so much that everything feels disconnected from each other.  Basically self taught in exam week.

This is a very bad professor. He spends an abnormal amount of time reviewing a single proof and never gives any examples. He also makes a lot of mistakes. You basically have to teach yourself the whole course.

Its been a week with this guy and I already am super confused. He is all over the place and spends 40 minutes asking if people have questions but he doesn't solve any problems. The homework is super hard because he doesn't do any example problems. Would not recommend.

he is a great guy but i did not like his teaching style at all. the homework is optional but if you do not do it you will fail the course. quizzes are pretty much taken from the textbook. exams are difficult. i would not recommend

This professor is terrible. Don't choose this professor. His emails are basically never answered, and the test and midterm exam will often be wrong, have to chase him after class to get the points back on the line, don't choose, otherwise the course is doomed. And he won't post any answers to any practice tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Hoffmann really cares about the students and the course. He'll make sure that you understand the content before moving on and stop for any questions to make sure that you understand the course. The course in general does move pretty quickly and the homeworks (although not mandatory) are really good for understanding the material.

Super awesome guy, makes funny jokes in class, cool references, keeps a very difficult class fun. that being said, he does NOT teach to the test. . The optional homework shouldn't be optional,  If your looking for a good grade, dont take his class, but if you want a solid understanding of calc 3, and dont care about your grade, hes a super fun prof

He's a caring professor but terrible at teaching. He spends class time just explaining the mathematical reasoning behind topics, confusing me, rather than giving us relevant examples. I don't feel as though he properly prepares us for the quizzes (which can be difficult). However, he is at least lenient with the grading and gives partial credit.

it's sad bc he's a good guy but a terrible professor. just should not be teaching calc 3 or any calc because i have yet to see him solve a problem in class without making a mistake, but he expects us to solve more difficult problems on quizzes. do NOT take him , at least for a summer class you will regret it
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Homework is optional - definitely do ALL homework if you want to do well on quizzes. The weekly quizzes are very hard, but prepare you well for exams. Very nice professor, although disorganized.
A kind and enthusiastic teacher but don't know how to teach

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!! He gives textbook problems for homework that do not prepare you for the difficulty of the quizzes. He had mistakes on the mid term review and the actual mid term exam itself (of which he showed up 5 min late). During class he makes mistakes constantly throughout the lecture and expect students to catch them.

Do not take a class with professor Hoffman. He spends large portions of the class answering useless questions and his explanations are often very unclear. He makes mistakes on the board at least 5 times per class and relies on students to point them out. He doesn't give quiz reviews and makes assessments much harder than the assigned homework.

The teacher is a horrendous with poor knowledge of teaching material, making mistakes and expecting a "heartfelt apology" to make students forgive him. He struggles to make calculus 3 feasible, especially during an accelerated summer semester. Quizzes and exams lack homework-related questions, making it difficult for students to learn the material.

I found Professor Hoffman's lectures to be well organized and cover everything, and he was good at answering questions. They had the perfect amount of deeper explanations of how things work in diff eq. for an otherwise mostly computational type math class. It seemed like really he wanted his students to understand the how and why of the topics.
he doesnt teach anything and expects the students to learn everything on their own. half the material on quizzes isn't covered in class careful

Really nice guy and he makes sure that you understand the material before continuing. He's friendly and will listen to anyone willing to talk to him. Quizzes every Thursday, lowest 2 are dropped. Study or they may spring up on you

I had Dr. Hoffman for diff eq. I don't really agree with the other posts saying he was a poor teacher or didn't care about students. He was always available outside of class, made an effort in class to help students understand the material, and overall was very friendly and had good lectures. I would recommend.
Not good. Let the class be hijacked by the same students asking useless questions every class. Went over some material WAY to slowly causing him to go over other material WAY to quickly.

Dr. Hoffman is an instructor that clearly cares about his students. He will stop at nothing to make sure that his student's understand the material if any questions are asked. That being said, often times this will lead to the pacing of the class grinding to a halt at times, leaving the material covered to be very surface level. Use the textbook!

He does seem like a nice person but MY GOD. No one is forcing you to be a professor. I had him for diff eq and the class is more or less entirely out of the textbook. There wasn't much info for quizzes and tests online so lecture attendance was mandatory. Also had no practice material for the second test. Mostly just felt like he didn't care.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was such a confusing professor. I struggled so much in class because his way of teaching is so confusing. My roommate has him for diff eq while I have Lupi, and I am definitely learning more. Aaron is not very clear, and while he is accessible outside of class, his explanations are very confusing. He does care about his students.

not the best teacher but the class is not too hard so if you attend lectures and do the hw, it should be fine. he's super sweet and cares a lot about his students and their comprehension, which I think is rare in a professor. very lecture heavy so if that is not your type of learning, don't take him.

Not a good professor. Repeats the most basic things twenty times without going over the complicated content anymore than one example. I did well in the class since I'm somewhat familiar with the topic, but if you are not take someone else.

Worst professor I've ever had. He is such a sweet guy, and it is clear he cares about his students. However, he cannot teach AT ALL. If anyone would ask questions his answer would confuse us more. We learned different things than other professors taught, and he went into depth on things we didn't need to. TAKE ANY OTHER PROFESSOR!!!

I will first start this off by saying I have taken Calc 3 two separate times, failing the first time with Garcia. This time I took it with Hoffman. He seriously cares about the input of everyone of his students and slows down the class for people to ask questions. He is a saint when it comes to giving relevant examples. I urge you to take Hoffman!

I had Professor Hoffman for an online semester. He doesn't use class time effectively and doesn't explain concepts super well. If you really don't care about the class and just want to get through, he won't be very tough on you, but if you want to fully understand the material, be prepared to teach yourself. Super nice guy, but a poor teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Caring

reviews.comment: I had Professor Hoffman this semester online. He's really caring and supportive. Sometimes his lectures are not that organized and he might make mistakes, but he always tries to answer every single question and explain the concepts again if needed. He will always try to talk to you and figure out if something's not working for you in class.

One of the best professors I've ever had. Yes, he is sometimes a little disorganized, but he cares so deeply about the concepts and his students. Was really willing to work with me when I was in a tough spot. Also, I personally LOVE that he goes very deep into derivations and theory, his classes are not about rote memorization, thank god.

Hoffman is horrible at communication and organization. He often apologizes for making mistakes or not grading quizzes, but never does anything to make up for that. His philosophy seems to be that if you mess up, its your fault, never his. If you already understand the content, youll be fine, but if its your first time learning it, good luck.

He really cares about actually teaching not just getting though the class. Acknowledges the importance of getting a good grade so he often curves assessments and is constantly reaching out to provide extra help.

Hoffman was in love with teaching but spent far too much time on derivations and the theory. We spent the whole lecture deriving something and he would rush through one practice problem at the very end of class. I'm taking it with a different prof this semester and it's so much better. Friends who didn't drop have to retake it anyways.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Hoffman is very kind and willing to address questions in class and at office hrs. However, it was very hard to know what was expected of us on quizzes/tests because what he lectured in class didn't always match the textbook. This means a good deal of tutoring and txtbk reading was required. But he seemed passionate and I liked the class (Diff'l)

Hoffman doesn't ever seem to have a lesson plan. It's like he makes up examples on the fly and makes constant mistakes. He does care and he does seem to try in the moment, but doesn't teach well because of a seeming lack of preparation. If you go to him after class he is very helpful but he isn't good in lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Dr Hoffman is a self-centered, arrogant, and ambitious instructor. He was over energetic to an extent that his lectures seemed very disorganized. He was not as familiar with the curriculum as other instructors. He was difficult to approach in person.
he is the best professor I know and its the best to get good gpa
enthusiastic professor!

Ildar is one of the best teachers i have met. He was very well prepared for the course and had many exercises to help us understand the new concepts. Apart from that, he cares a lot about the extracurricular activities of the students. He's such an excellent teacher, his classes are in high demand and hard to secure.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great cs professor, especially for novelty for computer science. Give good feedback on homework.
N/A

super chill guy, but his class can get quite boring. especially in a big lecture hall, it's difficult to hear him. goes too fast sometimes and doesn't really give you time to do the examples yourself (important if you prefer that). his explanations aren't the clearest, but the course isn't too difficult.

Overall pretty good guy, not the best at teaching the material but not bad at all. He derives everything which is cool but can be boring and also not help sometimes. The homework can take a while Aswell.

Great lecturer, put all the equations we needed for the quizzes on the board, he also brought demonstration objects to every ILS which were cool to see. He was also really helpful when doing the group problems and provided guidance.

Baris allowed for late submissions on homework with no penalties and would extend deadlines if everyone needed it. He even moved our second exam to be take-home since he wanted the class time to properly cover the material before the final

Honestly a very good professor. He genuinely wants to see his students succeed, and he usually allows formula sheets. He derives equations so that we can understand the concepts but that can be a bit confusing, so don't be too worried about lecture. Make sure you mostly know how to tackle the homework (which is harder than the exam/quiz questions).
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I could not have had a better re-introduction to physics than through Dr Altunkaynak (or Baris as he likes to be called). Physics is physics, so studying and preparation is going to be needed regardless of the professor; however, Baris does the most to make sure you are prepared and honestly just wants to see you succeed.
Super respectful and incredibly considerate. Patient and provides a significant amount of preparation and review for exams. Most enjoyable math class I have ever taken!

Evil human being, any other professor would be better. Can't teach the subject at all and is rude to her students. She treats the course like a review even tho most in the class have never taken the class. Very hard grader as well.
Bad professor. Can not explain concepts to undergraduate students. Take anyone else, help your self in the long run.

Just a bad teacher. She is terrible at getting students to understand material and overall is rude. Also her office hours are online only and seems to get mad that you show up to ask questions. I retook the course with a different professor and got a B-. take it with ANY OTHER professor if possible.

Your Grade comes down to two tests and a final. Lectures are rushed and don't always align with the quizzes. Will grade harshly and sometimes incorrectly. Try to avoid her, but if you must take her, start studying early. Go over your graded test to. She knows her stuff but lacks the ability to teach that knowledge to others.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tough grader but its a fair process. She wants you to do well and even though there are a lot of quizzes she drops so many of them that you get many chances to do well. Practice tests and quizzes are helpful. She wants you to do well and is very nice. Yes you do have to study, its a math class.

(I actually took math1213 with her but that's not an option on the drop-down) Reading reviews of other students, it sounds nothing like my experience in her class. She was very kind and patient when you struggled with concepts, and is a great explainer. Her tests are copy-and pastes of the study guides just with different numbers, so very easy.

first half of sem was so easy, then all of a sudden went completely downhill. practice quizzes became nothing like the actual quizzes, went from 100% to 50%. i am relatively good at math, and i actually like calc, and i show up to all lectures. she makes soooooo many mistakes especially on test reviews so its difficult to actually learn

Her lectures are kinda easy to follow during the 1st QUARTER of the semester but she oversimplifies things that dont need to be simplified. She only takes the highest 5 quizzes, but she grades harshly. her lecture and hw questions arent overwhelmingly hard, but it doesnt prepare you for quizzes. Its not worth taking her class.

Literally the worst teacher I have ever had. She is extremely condescending and isn't helpful outside of class. She treats this course like a review course for engineers alone, so if you haven't taken the class before do not take her. I did so much tutoring but still couldn't keep up because of quizzes included topics barely touched in class. Awful
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Where do I even begin, If you do not have a very strong foundation of calculus before this course DO NOT take it with Professor Gibbins. The quizzes and exams within this course do not match the difficulty of her lectures at all, they are extremely hard! She grades very hard as well with barely any partial credit. 0/10! Trust me!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I failed calc w another professor and idk why Gibbins has such bad reviews!! It's like night and day, she is so much nicer and her style is great for visual and auditory learners. Ppl may find her rude but she just has a dry sense of humor and can be sarcastic. That aside, you can tell she really cares and goes out of her way to provide extra help
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Gibbins is good, though there are daily quizzes, low grades dropped. They aren't hard but need to pay attention in class. I LOVED that she gave practice exams that were EXACTLY what to expect for the actual test (not the same questions, but the same format/types of questions). Good at calc? take her you will do great. She is a fair grader!
She is really really bad. She should not be a teacher, she is very rude and gets mad when you dont understand. Shes very condesending.

She is a great professor in my mind, does homework session every night so you could ask her questions constantly. She does make mistakes in class sometimes and I see the test averages are horrible. So if you know what you are doing with your math, you can choose her since she gives out help. But if you are bad at math, not her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The worst teacher I've ever had. Constantly makes mistakes in class and in exam reviews. I gave up on using her solutions to study due to the sheer amount of basic arithmetic errors. The average grade for her second midterm was a 61, and the first was barely a 70. She offers no curve and always responds with slight attitude. Do NOT take her class.

no homework,  pop quizzes (drops your lowest quiz every 4 quizzes) 2 exams + final. hard to access outside of class, few office hours. does problems as examples and the practice exams are like the real exams. definitely dont take her if you have no calculus experience, but if u pay attention and do the practice problems u should be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
I went in with no calc experience terrified, but landed a B. Her lectures are DENSE but she outlined each step clearly, plus lots of examples to practice.

4/5 bc you only get points from tests, plus she made a few mistakes in class that if not pointed out by students, would leave us with incorrect answers to study from. Overall, great!

Not sure why so many low ratings, maybe she improved as a professor. She does do a lot of example problems so if that's how you learn take her class, if not I suggest a different professor. She is very understanding and cares about her students.

Very fast paced lectures and made mistakes everyday. Expected things said once to be memorized by the next day. Quizzes were always either very fair or insanely hard with no in between. She also always has a slight attitude and it feels like she thinks her students are dumb. The one good thing I will say is that a lot of partial credit was given.

Just sort of did examples every class. The quizzes and tests are a lot harder than what she did in class, and little was explained. The rare times a review sheet was made helped, but it didn't really make up for not learning it in the first place. Little partial credit for us even though she always messed up the examples in class too.

Thank goodness I took some calculus in high school. While some of her lectures are pretty understanding, some aren't and whats worse is the test are more difficult and advanced that I never seen in any calculus course. While I did enjoyed some of her puns, the course would be very difficult if you come in with very little experience in calculus.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Honestly, cannot even believe she is a professor. Sometimes you learn stuff that she says we wont get tested on. Why are we learning it then? Her exams take forever to do and are 10x harder than what we learn, and she wonders why people dont do well. My tutor explained things much better than her. Pick any other Prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Gibbins has been the worst professor I have ever had. The class consisted of poor lesson planning, useless homework and unnecessarily hard problems. Exams were very hard and often contained lessons we weren't quizzed on. Her lectures proved to be inadequate for the final. Dumpster fire of a class brought on by pure laziness. AVOID AT ALL COST

Prof Gibbins obviously cares about math and students, but her teaching style did not work with me at all. Ive struggled with calc in the past, but Gibbins teaching just made me more confused. Additionally, so much unnecessary hw, only two exams + final (exams that were much harder than anything we did in class), and hardly any partial credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Probably the worst teacher I have ever had. When Prof Gibbins teaches, she makes so many mistakes and often asks the students if what shes doing is correct. Her quizzes and exams are more difficult than what you do in class, she gives minimal and confusing feedback and grades so harshly. Shes a sweet person, but she really should not be teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Not the best professor I had, to sum it up, you will have to do tons of unnecessary work. The homework for me averaged from 50 to 70 problems a week. Combined with poorly explained material, this makes the subject very difficult, as half of the problems are not explained in class. The tests are also very hard, so my advice: pick another professor

Aliska genuinely wants you to learn. She takes your best quiz scores, and drops the rest, with ample room for extra credit. She also has an excellent sense of humor and writes on her iPad, which makes reading her notes super helpful. Everything is very digital, which makes things painless, and the practices closely resemble the real deal for exams.
Best summed up in one word: Yikes.

The single worst professor I have ever had. She doesn't know how to explain anything, and fails to engage with her students at all. She gives out an unnecessary amount of classwork and homework, half of which the material she doesn't go over in class. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS IF YOU HAVE THE CHOICE!

Worst professor I've ever had. She puts questions on the quizzes and tests that are WAY harder and longer than the homework problems, and she doesn't even respond to emails. She has no energy and always sounds like she doesn't wanna be there when she's giving lectures. Doesn't give a lot of partial credit or feedback either.

Worst teacher you can have. If these reviews aren't enough she is the same Aliska Gibbins that comes up for Florida Institute of Technology and got away by making a second account. Take ANY OTHER professor. She does not care about her students and made my life a living hell while in her class. She does not respond to emails either. I can counite...

The professor is decent. She teaches clearly and follows the syllabus. She is really good at preparing students for exams (she makes practice tests & questions). Sometimes she doesn't explain fully. I would recommend her if you have taken calculus before/are good at calculus. She teaches mostly by showing lots of examples.

Gibbins was the worst teacher that I've ever had. Her lectures consisted of doing problems on the board using new material without explanations. She expected you to learn on your own but the textbook provided was not helpful. She is a very tough grader and partial credit is almost non existant. Absolutely would not take again.

Great professor. Clear about the syllabus and what is necessary to know for the tests. Posts all her notes online. Really good about leaving time for students to ask questions and explaining when students don't understand. Available outside of class. She is great at preparing us for tests and making sure we all get good grades. Highly recommend.

Zero clarity in what we were doing and on how to solve problems. She just sat in her wheelchair on her iPad, at least her dog was cute. She did give credit for hw but the tests were roughhh. Avoid at all costs especially if you didn't take BC calc in high school.

Its never clear what you're supposed to be learning. Just teach yourself everything from the textbook and the internet and use class for examples and practice. She never explains what shes doing in class. Its an easy grade if you know what you're doing but if you're learning the info for the first time its very hard to actually learn anything.

FINA 3303. Mid-semester replacement professor. While the transition was unexpected, the teaching style didn't quite land. Claimed to cut off interesting materials saying for 4-level class. The exams were surprisingly difficult and focused on meaningless conceptual points. The practice tests do not reflect actual exam style and difficulty.

96% of the grade is just quizzes and exams. 4% is participation. Good person at heart but does not budge to even the most genuine requests. Tests are in class on lockdown. If he is your last resort, trust me and please skip to take this class next semester. Not worth getting a C.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Boring, just read the slides

He's a good person and extremely knowledgeable on the finance industry as he's a former MD and even ran his own hedge fund. However, tests are hard and make up your whole grade, and lectures are un engaging. Hides your grade until the final and homework's have constant errors/bugs. Wouldn't recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Participation is weighted through cold calls.. had some embarrassing experiences. Exams are just like practice tests (which is nice), but they're still hard. Gives lots of credit for showing work. Excel only, no calculators. (prior excel knowledge not required, will learn). Ranked grading (not traditional), I think it works in students favor lowkey

I think his teaching style is a little much, as his practice problems have you trying to figure out things that he doesn't go over in the lecture. He will reason this with "I want you to know more"; when I'm studying for a test, I don't want to pour my energy into things that won't come up. I think similar teachers won't kill your GPA like he does.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great Professor. He has a strong financial background and gives good advice outside of just lectures/tests. His homework assignments are a little annoying but most of your grade is assessment-based. He prepares you well, and will do anything he can to give you extra points - he genuinely wants you to learn.

He worked at a hedge fund and provides great knowledge in the finance sector. There is homework for each chapter on Respondus which is annoying but he gives two attempts each so it's easy to do well. He makes good practice tests for each chapter/exam. There are 3 exams + the final which are heavily weighted so be prepared for those. 100% recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Difficult, boring and strict
He's a great and helpful teacher. He's a fast lecturer, but I like his class, it's interesting. It's not that bad if you use the PQs and HWs as guidance.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Good teacher, the subject feels easy and repetitive. Paid no attention in class learned everything before the test and was fine.
Great and knowledgeable professor. Had 4 tests, but all were manageable.

BEWARE! The tests are stupid and too specific. Asks you to memorize 3 chapters for a test and then will only ask you 6 questions on the test that are niche and u would be lucky to remember the answer. he's aware that his tests are like this, but does nothing to make a change. Surely there is a better way to test students on alternative investments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
He said "Malaya... or whatever her name is..." when talking about Malala.

He also said to forget sustainability and the environment because marketing is more important than using recycled plastics.

Butler is a great professor if you want to be pushed to think outside the box and challenge yourself in a new way as a brand marketer. I enjoyed the semester project and his lectures. My biggest issue is his unfair grading scale that is extremely demotivating to students who spent so much time and money on this class to meet his expectations.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: For MKTG 3720: Professor Butler is a great guy who really cares about his students. The class is structured very loosely, and can be kind of abstract at times. A lot of this class can be boiled down to if you have a good group that's willing to put in work to create a good final product. However, the class is interesting and you can learn a lot.

Great Professor who cares about each student. Case every week and 2 presentations during the semester with some lectures. you don't know your grade until the end of the semester but he really emphasizes participation and being engaged in class. Great class if you want to go into marketing but, it is a lot of work. Couldn't recommend enough!
great guy, had him in spring 2025

He's awesome. He would start out the class sharing his weekend stories, and they were always hilarious. He's super chill and caring; he takes grading seriously and will give you great feedback, so make sure to put effort into your assignments. Also, you don't know what grade you have until the end of the semester, which is a bit scary.

Prof. Block is great!! He has a great personality and really cares about his students. He gives plenty of time for writing assignments which makes them very manageable. He also helps his students to learn new ways to think about writing which was great!! Definitely take his class!!

Mr. Block is the perfect First Year Writing teacher; His assignments are very manageable and provide good learning to anyone looking to improve their writing. He respects your time and doesn't assign tough or unreasonable assignments. He constantly takes student feedback and actually constructs the rubrics to meet the needs of the students.

Dr. Block gave me a fantastic advanced writing experience. Dr. Block made the class incredibly fun and engaging. He clearly cares a lot about his students and loves teaching. There are only 3 major assignments during the semester and he gives you ample time to do each. If you need to take this class, I'd highly recommend Dr. Block

I love him. I'm not the best in English classes but I loved his class. He truly cares and asks you about your day every day. I feel like I learned a lot from his class. He gives you a score rather than a grade for your assignments, which I feel like made it easier to get an A. I'm really glad I had him as my first semester in college.
Great Prof, will take his higher level class!
Not friendly to everyone

Super easy class, and he is a very chill professor. It is really easy to get an A as long as you do all parts of each project (assignment itself, peer review, and personal reflection) -- there are four projects throughout the semester. He grades with rubrics and leaves thoughtful feedback about each assignment -- doesn't use Canvas.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It is clear that he cares about his students. He takes time at the beginning of class to ask the students how they are doing. He doesn't use Canvas, so if you want to find out your projected grade you just have to ask. Super nice, very funny, and great feedback.

Super easy class, doesn't expect too much from his students (just do the work). Super easy to get an A (don't think he gives anything below a B). You will have 4 writing projects that are mostly topics of your choosing. Even if you hate/are bad at writing you could get perfect scores on all projects
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Attendance is mandatory. There are only 3-4 papers and peer reviews with each offering a unique writing style or thought process. Professor Block himself is relatable and keeps his lectures interesting despite the material. Unfortunately, he has extremely poor handwriting which makes notetaking a little difficult.
Block is very good at making a fun and interactive class environment. He gave us donuts in our last class together too.

He is very caring about his students and makes class interesting. He always starts class with a daily story and tries to really create a class "community" by inviting others to share their stories. I really felt connected to my classmates by the end. Can be tough grader but as long as you put effort into the assignment/follow feedback, you're fine.

Really loved taking his class. I never thought I'd say this about advanced writing in biz, but I wish the class was longer. He gave really great feedback on each writing assignment and is easy to reach via email or office hours for anything. If you try your best and make revisions often based on his feedback and the peer reviews, you'll do well.
Block tries to make this class and his assignments interesting. The class is based off of 4 main projects/papers and they're all interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Block was a great professor! Essays were actually fun to write (we got to choose our own topic within a theme). Besides writing our own essays, we also had to write many peer reviews. Very fun class! To get a good grade you really had to listen to the feedback from the professor and peer reviewers.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Aaron is one of the best professors ever. He spends time at the beginning of class to take about your day which is nice. He cares about his students. Not too much work. You don't find out your grade until the end which is interesting. Super nice guy and funny. Take him if you can.

I really enjoyed Aaron's class. Started off every day with stories and he shared some of his own about weird weekends he had and stuff. Super casual class with not a lot of work, so make sure you do well on every assignment. He does use the full class time, though, but his lectures aren't at all boring and he's really funny.

He's really funny and honestly doesn't give that much work. Sometimes we spend half the class just talking. It's super interesting though, and he's not a tough grader, just make sure you fulfil the requirements and show effort. He loves it when people participate. Always takes attendance. If you want to take an easy and chill class, take his.

All you have to do is show up to class. He does talk a lot, and it's very easy to get lost in what he's saying, but he did have interesting things to say, and he's clearly very passionate/experienced with the subject. Lots of field trips around the city, which were cool.
Basic intro to arch class, lots of fun field trips, some boring guest speakers, only graded on attendance
Attendance is mandatory but all he does it talk, super easy
fun and relaxed professor, very passionate about architecture!

Dan Adams is one of the most inspirational teachers Ive ever had. His lectures are interesting and his perspectives are really helpful in critiques. Hes also funny and laid back. I absolutely recommend taking his classes even if you dont plan on majoring in architecture.
Very inspirational and knowledgeable teacher in design tactics, highly recommend him for that course.  Get him to show you his projects in his firm, it is very interesting!
Wish he still taught the fundamentals studio.  Dan was amazing!
I had a great freshmen year studio experience with him. Wrote me a stunning recommendation letter for co-op. Very thankful for him.
Very good.

Talks like a machine gun but he's an interesting person. Make sure you don't put mulches in your design because he hates mulches. Very knowledgable and goes beyond the syllabus. The down side is that the class often finishes late, which is quite frustrating sometimes. Overall he's a great professor who has a good sense of humor.

He's very knowledgable, probably one of the best architecture professors in Northeastern University. But you will need to put in a lot of good and hard work to impress him. He also talks a lot so you'll always need to stay behind after class for at least an hour.

He's a good teacher who knows what he's talking about but he talks so much that you are sometimes forced to stay over an hour after class before he critiques you.  You'll learn the most from him but you're also assigned the most work.
Great professor, lots of fun, and very interesting. Assigns a lot of work and is quite tough with the grading but is very informative.

This studio teacher is such a jerk. The amount of work he assigns is nearly impossible to complete in the allotted time. He'll look at your work for 2 seconds, laugh in your face, and tell you that your work is hard to look at.
Super boring lectures (he just reads the slides). Very very difficult to learn anything without just reading the textbook on your own.
I learned a lot in this class. Great selection of business cases related to Fintech. Team work was fun too and I transacted using cryptocurrencies for the first time!

Enjoyed this class. Mainly lecture based and had a mix of online assessments and group projects on interesting topics. Professor Koparan was very accessible and was always down to help if you had questions. Would highly recommend if looking for a chiller FINA class.

I took fintech in summer 1 and although the pace of the course felt a bit expedited due to summer scheduling, it was super fun to explore fintech landscape and Alper creates a friendly classroom environment. Assignments are helpful and I also learned a lot about cryptocurrencies.

Very enjoyable lectures and friendly classroom atmosphere. I really liked group project which is about creating a startup idea and investor pitch at the end. Alper is extremely knowledgable about Fintech and makes concepts easy to understand.

I really enjoyed this class and even at 8 am Professor Koparan made lectures engaging and interesting. Some assignments are in class and there are hands-on exercises to experience cryptocurrencies and decentralized finance. A must have class if you are interested in Fintech!

FINA4604 is a super dense finance class in nature covering Levels 1-3 of the CFA curriculum and dives into highly quantitative topics. Unless you're super passionate about fixed income I wouldn't advise taking this class unless you're ready to read through a 900 page textbook and spend 6-10 hours a week on homework

This is a very technical and difficult course. I would not take it unless you are interested in Fixed Income. It covers topics from Level 1-3 of the CFA and it takes a lot of work outside of class to do well. I spent 6-8 per week outside of class studying. Alper cares about the material and his students. I learned a ton in this class.

Hard grader, exams extremely hard, midterm 1 20%, midterm 2 20%, final 30%. assignments consist of mcq and excels very hard and complicated. if u miss class get ready to figure out how to do assignment/hw. didn't know this class was tailored for CFA. work hard or else ur grade is gonna have a big drop.

I'm in Fixed Income Securities class and it is going great so far! It is not an easy course but Professor Koparan makes it engaging and practical. Hi is always available for questions and care about student success. He is one of the best at Northeastern and highly recommended!

Do not take fixed income securities with Alper Koparan. He is extremely disorganized and doesn't have the answers for homework figured out before the test, so you can't study effectively. Tests make up a large portion of the grade, but his test questions are not specific whatsoever and he solves them differently from how he teaches them in class.

Prof. Koperan is one of my favorite profs at NEU. He is extremely kind and caring and genuinely a good person. His Fintech class is relatively easy as long as you do the readings & study. He's always available outside of class too. He breaks down complex concepts into really easy ones. Take his class!!!!

she doesnt know how to teach. all she does is examples on the board and we copy it down. she doesnt explain what she is teaching. she doesnt understand that we dont know the material. the other day I asked her a question, she came to me to help and then got mad when i still couldnt understand after she tried to "help". you have to teach yourself.

her class isnt the worst thing i've ever had but your grade is almost entirely made up of tests. her class is unbelievably boring. she talks so slow and has no energy or enthusiasm whatsoever. her lecture are fine but im not learning much from it. if this is your only option it is not sadie piatt bad, but i would get someone else if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The professor is very kind. There are a total of 8 quizzes, and the lowest one will be dropped. The grade is mainly based on exams and quizzes, which is not very good for people who are not great at taking exams. If you have any questions, just ask, and you can also find her during office hours. I think it's very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Weekly quizzes but they weren't super difficult if you went to class and did the problems on the textbook. Also drops the lowest quiz, but don't trust the bad ratings very fair teacher IMO.

She was one of the first professors to make me retain math. She's super kind and understanding. Weekly quizzes were helpful in testing understanding. I don't know why she gets bad ratings because she's always super clear and knowledgeable.

The lectures were very unclear and did not help me learn the material. The course relied heavily on constant quizzes without providing the instruction needed to prepare for them. Expect to teach yourself most of the content, and it will be especially difficult if you do not already have a solid calculus background.

I don't understand the negative reviews! She is very patient and open to answer questions.  As someone who isn't naturally great in math, I found this class one of the best I've taken. The lectures were pretty clear to me, and she repeats old concepts. Quizzes based on the homework assigned so they tend to be pretty fair.

She does not care about her students, when I went to office hours she was very rude and when I did not understand something she said "how can you not understand?? these are the most basic things" she isn't very kind i would avoid her.

She is not bad at all, super sweet and explains things well. Don't be afraid to ask questions in her class or go to office hours, she's very approachable and wants everyone to do well! If you struggle with math it can be hard, but she's not the issue. Only graded harshly on the group project

Just not a good teacher overall and her grading policy is awful. Would never take her class again if I had the opportunity as well as she is very difficult to understand because of her broken english.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Super easy if you did calc in high school. The teacher is nice but her lectures are useless, i stopped going after a while. Quizzes almost every week but she takes them exactly from homework, just changes the numbers. If you do the exact homeworks she assigns the day before you will do very well.
The quiz she gives out have nothing to do with what she teaches. She also gives out quizzes every week which is insane. Never take her unless you wanna suffer through a sem.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is very hard to understand with her heavy accent and she doesn't make sure the students understand the material. The pace she teaches at is too fast for students to learn and it isn't efficient. Her grade policy is god awful and I never want to have a professor like her again.
Terrible at explaining concepts

Super bad experience. Her lecture videos doesn't help at all and I literally have to spend extra time seeking resources outside of the classroom. Also doesn't respond to emails. Don't take her class unless you want a miserable semester

By far the worst class that I have taken at Northeastern. She cannot teach (its not the accent, she just cannot teach) She's constantly giving a test and she does not spend time helping students, and if you do, she won't actually help you. Horrible professor, unless your a math whiz don't take her class unless you hate yourself.

Straightforward lectures, was sweet and willing to stop and further break down a problem in class. Gave a good amount of study material for quizzes and tests. Available during office hours. Would sometimes host a class on Zoom.

This class if very manageable and actual first teacher to teach me how to use my calculator! Professor Bai provides study guides that are very similar to the exam. We have a group project that you can resubmit for better grade. There are 7 exams but she drops your lowest quiz AND test score! Caring professor and accessible outside of class!
tests you on stuff you never learned. good luck if you're in her class and aren't a math whiz. wish i had another prof. this sucked
One of the worst professors I have taken a class with at Northeastern. I have no idea why she is still teaching with all those negative reviews here.

There are weekly quizzes for this class and the class is too long for what you learn every class. Sometimes we just sit there waiting for the time to end instead of her letting us leave. She is really bad at explaining the concept and the only helpful resource are the videos posted on how to do the lessons but it's not even her videos.

Very, very, very poor teacher. Don't take this class unless you want to have a miserable semester and not learn absolutely anything. Hard to understand her because of her accent and she does not know how to teach.
she is the worst

Guys if you're not willing to study/ put in effort dont come to college lol. I agree it's easier if you've taken calc before but all the resources for you to learn are given to you. She's a little frustrating but math is math and if you study you'll be fine. Go to her office hours
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is getting a lot of hate on here but her class is not hard if you put in effort, not only in class but if you don't understand knack is a great resourse. There are quizzes every week but if you attend class and ask questions you should be fine. She's very nice and caring and always willing to answer questions and responds to emails fast.

I feel lost in this class. Vantage will cost you $139. Hard to reach outside of class, but there's no point anyways. I wish the “keys” she posted included steps instead of just the answer. I'm so lost lol

Honestly, if you're anxious about math or calc, this class is HARD. I am struggling so much. She doesn't answer questions directly, instead says "we covered it in class." The final (cummulative) is worth 40% which I think is abismal. Idk if all professors are teaching the concepts she is, but it honestly all seems more complicated than it should.

Do not take if you've never taken pre-calc or calc before. She's hard to understand and moves onto different topics really fast. Quizzes (no calculator) are in the first 15 mins of class and tests are 45 mins. The examples done in class jump from 0 to 100 on the actual tests. I've never felt so dumb as much as I do sitting in that class...
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: honestly, what was that?? Idk why no one tells you about how bad she is, it really is impossible to learn anything in this class because of how she teaches. I wish I had read the comments here before taking a class with her.

Honestly made calc so easy to understand. I think the lectures were very well explained, and we did plenty of examples to solidify learning. Definitely need to pay attention and practice, but I never felt overwhelmed by the workload or difficulty of the course.
If I had not taken calculus in high school I would have no idea what her lectures were about. She is a very nice person, but her lectures were near impossible to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: terrible at teaching w/ a heavy accent. goes too fast. If you leave for 5 mins she literally jumped 5 topics ahead. Quizzes every week, She moves onto the next topic before the quiz. Some of the things on the quizzes isn't even taught. Grade only depends on exams. Office hours didn't help. She includes Calc 2 & 3 into a Calc 1 course for business.

Dr. Bai is very caring, wants to see her students succeed in class, and she uploads the lecture notes on Canvas. Not a difficult class if you've taken Calc in high school. Quizzes every week (40% of your grade), 20 points each, 1 midterm and 1 final exam. However, she moves too fast on important topics sometimes. All/most exams graded by her TA.

if you've taken calc before, it is very doable and honestly shouldn't be that hard. Her lectures were useless to me so I stopped going and relied heavily on the textbook to learn. Her quizzes are usually 15 minutes and 5 questions. Grade is heavily relied on the weekly quizzes and final.

Professor Bai is a really sweet and caring professor. Unfortunately, I just failed to understand her lectures, even as someone who took calculus in high school. She goes really fast and I can never write notes fast enough to keep up with her. If you zone out for even a second, she's already explained two topics and moved onto the next.
she seemed really nice, but the way she explained things didn't make sense to me. I'm also not very math-minded, but for a very low-level course this seemed unnecessarily difficult

please. incoming economics freshmen. DONT TAKE THIS!! i beg you. I know at least 10 people who dropped this class. shes so sweet, but you will not do well unless you are a math whiz who aced ap calc bc in highschool. Drop it and take it with a different professor in a different semester. DO YOURSELF A FAVOR!!!! I have no idea how anyone did well.

I don't know who writes the good comments in here but none of the classmates/people I know who took a class with her liked her and her teaching style. Very hard to follow during the lecture and not accessible outside of classes.

Taking calc with her will be your worst nightmare if you have never taken calculus before/if you are an international student. Professor has a very hard to understand accent, so you have to do a lot of self-learning. She covers the material in class very quickly and you can't join the class online; wouldn't help you if you are struggling in class.

Rekha is a very good professor, I would say I am willing to take her course again so many time. I like her teaching style, she will show you the definition of sth and then go through enough examples to help you understand it. I like her class so much. I could say she inspired me a lot on math, and I change my major to math after taking her class.
NEVER TAKE HER - literally the worst professor to take this class with

She's nice but her lectures are unclear. If you've taken calculus before, you'll do well. If you haven't, prepare to do a lot of self-learning. The directions for the final presentation are unclear. She told my group we did it wrong, so the other groups changed their projects according to what she told us, but still told them they did it wrong.

Class was very easy given I've taken Calc previously. She goes in depth with her lectures and very good at explaining. One group project and she drops the lowest test and quiz, great for keeping your grade up. textbook homework for every unit, good practice. FINAL IS WORTH A LOT (40%) !!!!

I Love Her teaching ways, which first tell you the formulas then bring you some exercises, this can help me understand how those principles works and remember them. Also, although she has several paper test, you have the chance to drop one of the tests which you receive the lowest grade.

She makes this class 100000% harder, and it's impossible to understand what she is talking about during her lectures most of the time. I have to study everything by myself because she cannot explain what she teaches clearly.
Classes are basically copying things from the board but not really engaging.  As a professor, she wasn't helpful at all, I had to study by myself.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Bai was very sweet, but I had no idea what she was talking about most of the time. Her lectures tended to be very confusing and she was not always clear when answering questions. A majority of your grade is based on a midterm and final exam, which I found to be pretty unfair. There was a quiz once a week that were a small % of your grade

Professor Bai is a clearly enjoys teaching calculus for business. She goes through some of the topics very quickly and without many explanations, but she always completes a worksheet with the students and is totally opened and patient when receiving a bunch of questions. The grade consists of a group project, five tests, homework and a final exam.

Only take this class if you've taken calculus before because her lectures will only make sense to you that way. Otherwise, she just teaches the basics and then goes 0 to 100 in her examples. For the group project, she doesn't give helpful feedback and will just tell you what's wrong. She's not sympathetic whatsoever toward extensions/ makeup exams.

This professor is so sweet and caring. Her lectures are very informative and she goes through a great amount of examples that helps you really understand the topics. Because she does a lot of examples you also hardly ever open the textbook. She also creates a pdf with review material for each exam which touches on all topics for the upcoming exam

If you have taken Calculus before and you can teach yourself, then you should not go to her class unless for exams. She has a thick accent that you cannot understand what she is saying most of the time and i know for sure if I did not take Calculus in my senior year of highschool, I would have definitely failed this class.

Very straight-shooting teacher. Works lots of examples in class, and gives very straightforward lectures. Not a super showy personality in the classroom, but she's interesting to talk to after class if you'd really like to. Classic good teacher, would take again. (Can be a bit harsh grading quizzes, but she drops the lowest 2)

I have taken calculus before, but her lectures were very clear and helpful. Great syllabus with clear expectations and tons of practice problems. She always asks if we have questions or need her to slow down. Easy homework and fair tests. Easy group project.

Explains every topic very well, but to a basic level. You will often find yourself understanding the very basics of how to do something, but need to figure out the depths of it on your own. Provides plenty of practice and is very nice in and out of class. If you try you will do well, even without attending class more than once a week.

Lectures are very boring but helpful if you're having trouble in the class. 5 tests (drops lowest test), 1 semester long group project, and 1 final. Very easy class if you've taken any kind of calc class before, but may be difficult if not. Not much feedback on tests. In my opinion, it was more valuable to self-study than attend her classes.
She is a good professor but teaches at a really fast pace. She gives a lot of tests which are not that hard if you study a lot. She covers a lot in one class so don't skip any classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Taking Rekha Bai's class was one of the worst things ever. Half of the time, you cannot understand her words because she doesn't use the mic and she writes poorly on the chalkboard. She also gives tests with problems that were so far from the difficulty we learned. Beware of the semester long group project and a lot of tests. Final worth 40%.

Professor Bai was a great lecturer who clearly explained all the material you needed to know. If you needed clarification, it was explained in the textbook or by going to her office hours. She provided review material for every test that made it very easy to know what you needed to know to be prepared. There is also a semester long group project.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She does not explain things clearly. Uses a chalkboard and erases with her hand making it impossible to take notes. Her tests are often and extremely difficult if you havent been crunching for a week. Her classes are all but interactive and, when asked for an extension, she refused.

I took her Calculus - Business/Economics course as a part of NUAccelerate. Her lectures are mind numbing and the examples she explains are far from the difficulty of the homework. I basically taught myself the entire curriculum. Furthermore, she includes a semester long group project with vague instructions doesn't provide any clarification or help

Professor Bai is a nice person, but she is awful at explaining. If you are good at Calc or have previous knowledge, then this might not be too bad. However, if you are average at Calculus, do not take the class with her. Moves quickly, explains badly and very confusing. Do not recommend.

Professor Bai is the best math professor I've ever encountered. She is very helpful and willing to answer any questions. Her lectures are very clear and homework are relatively easy. She gives very clear instruction on quizzes and exams. Be sure to practice the questions in her "class pak" then you will definitely achieve high score.

Really nice but awful at explaining. The lectures are so easy but the homeworks and the quizzes are so much harder. Wouldn't recommend if you have not taken calc in highschool or struggle with math in general.

I took this class through NUStart. The material was okay considering it was Calculus and I had taken a precalc course in high school. Either way, she is very nice but can be hard to understand. Her teaching style is not my favorite. I only passed because I went to many office hours with her, TA's and an another tutor.

I'm not sure why her recent ratings are low, but Professor Bai is sweet and interpersonal throughout the lectures despite the online format due to COVID-19. Textbook reading isn't needed as she provides ppt slides and worksheets (which the tests/quizzes are based on) and there aren't any graded hw assignments. Doing textbook problems will save you
Long and boring lecture. Depend on yourself
She is the best professor to take for 1231. She very good explaining and as long as you pay attention you should get an A-/A.

Overall, she's nice, but her class can be very boring because she lectures at the board the whole time. Her explanations can be confusing at times, but if you work hard and make an effort it's an easy A. There are quizzes every single week and the homework is all online but isn't due until the end of the semester.

Took the course as a 2nd year and my class was mainly freshmen. they were sometimes really rude to her, but Professor Bai was a sass master and shut them down. She was very willing to give one-on-one help and allowed you to correct mistakes in your group project for points back. I enjoyed her teaching so much that I took her for MATH 1231 as well.

Rekha is one of the best math professors I've ever had! She is easy to understand, although she can be quiet at times. She has students do problems in class -- they aren't for credit, but they help. She is very available in office hours and is kind to students; she'll really put in extra effort to make sure you understand. Quizzes are reasonable.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: NICE PROFESSOR BUT MAKES THE COURSE SEEM HARDER THAT IT IS.

Precalc with her was honestly a huge waste of my tuition money. Yes the class is easy, but maybe a little too easy that it makes it hard to take it seriously and stay on track. She is very condescending if you are a weak math student, and won't reply emails or provide extra help to students outside of very limited office hours.
This class was a show up, sleep for 100 min, and get an A. I couldn't stay awake in the class, and the professor stretches out about 15 min of material for the full class time.

I saw some people mentioning her strong accent, but it's really not a big deal. She's very nice and really chill. On the rare occasion that people jokingly express being stressed in the class, she'll just calm everybody down. Also, she's really thorough when introducing mathematical concepts.

This is my second class with Professor Bai. I think she has a very specific teaching style which works for some people and not for others. Fortunately, she worked for me really well. She gives many quizzes but they are short and she will tell you exactly what to expect. I think she is a great teacher for some but not for others.
This class was comically easy. If you have a calc requirement, definitely take Rekha. There's no reason you shouldn't get an A in this class.

Rekha Bai talks in a pretty thick Indian accent, but if you pay attention relatively well and go to class most of the time it is an easy A. Quizzes are assigned regularly, and the lowest quiz grade is dropped, so it is easy to bring your grades up. Would recommend taking math with her if you are a business or econ major!

Very unhelpful for those who struggle with math, doesn't reply to emails. Yeah, there tends to be the students who are more math savvy who pretty much just fly through the class and grades in success. She is dull, difficult understand and approach, and has no personality to try and be more approachable. Her explanations are unclear.
A little hard to understand at first but you get used to her after a while. She is very very helpful and sweet. Loved her!!
Great teacher! She's a little hard to understand at first, but pay attention in class and do all the homework (not graded) and you'll do very well

She might not be the best orator, but her classes are well organized. Though it seems like she just got off the boat she has a lot of experience. She's approachable, open to questions and brings clarity to the subject.
GREATTTT Professor

Definitely a good teacher who cares for her student's success and who, despite her strong accent and monotonous voice, explains everything very well. Average grader and has lengthy office hours. Just do the work, revise the material several times and you'll do well.

Ok. I hate the class but with her its easy yet very boring. No need to do the hw or anything. Key to succeeding would be to master the guide and practice problems they make you buy. Do it the night before and you are set. Quizez are easy, Mid terms are okay but the FINAL is challenging to say the least.

She is a solid teacher. Not exactly interesting or the best but definitely teaches you the material. Pay attention, come to class, and if you need it schedule office hours because she doesnt exist on campus otherwise. I suggest read the book if you dont understand then ask in class. She likes questions. She has an accent-meh.

She teaches in a very organized, logical sequence, but does not mind stopping to address questions. She provides time for in class practice and circulates to make sure that everyone can do the work, even if they do not ask for help. She always graded quizzes in a timely fashion and very fairly. She is also flexible, moving the quizzes around.

Ms. Bai was a good teacher, she made everything very clear while she taught. You really only need the book for half of the class so if you can just share with someone in class you're golden. The final is cumulative so you need to make sure you study hard. Overall an easy class.
She is a tough teacher, she WILL make you work a lot and she has a difficult accent to understand. Nevertheless, her class was fun and interesting. Likes to assist students in case they struggle.
Overall a good teacher. could be a lot clearer and more caring. If you are not doing well in the course, don't expect her to help you at all, so work really hard.
Has an accent. Very nice lady and will help you. Like 7 quizes she drops the 3 lowest. Final and midterm are hard but an overall easy class. Great Prof!

She'll like you and make an effort to help you if you show up everyday. she was really mean to the kids that didn't show up. She likes students that work hard even if your not particularly skilled. Hard to understand.

I usually struggle in math, but Prof Bai was very clear and made sure I knew the subject matter. She gives you review the day prior to a quiz and it is almost identical to the quiz itself. drops the lowest two quizzes. One of the best math teachers I've ever had.
Great professor. Small accent but she is nice and understands where your coming from. Teaches well.
If you've taken high school calculus this class is a breeze.  She's a nice lady too, and you can probably get away without buying the book.

An extremely easy class if you do her review worksheets. They are practically copies of the actual quiz. She's a very nice lady, but the class is far from exhilarating....pretty boring in fact. But hey, can Calculus really be exciting?
easy class, amazing professor...

Her accent is not hard at all to understand. She is a really great professor and will gladly help you if you need it. Her quizzes are very fair - you get a review before each one that is exactly like what will be on the actual quiz.
Nice lady, if you go to class, it's easy to get an A. A little bit hard to understand her accent, but not too bad. Her quizzes are exactly like her quiz reviews.

If you have to take a calc class i would definitely recommend Bai! The project was easy and the quizzes were exactly like the quiz review she gives you for homework. I had a solid A in the class until the final, which i failed and brought me down to a B. The final is 40% of your grade and is MUCH harder than you will think.

Prof. Bai is a sweet, quiet Indian woman (slight accent, no problem).  If you want to learn real calculus, then this class is not for you.  Besides the few basic calculus concepts, I often felt like it was 10th grade algebra and not college calculus.  However, since the course is required, get Bai, it's an easy class made easier by an awesome prof.
professor Bai was a great teacher just do the class packet cuz thats basically all the quizzes are based on. Her final is hard and its worth 40 percent of your grade

Great math teacher. Nice woman, who wants you to do well. Takes attendance. Uses the textbook, but you really don't need to buy it. I stopped looking at it after the first few weeks.  Course only gets semi-difficult towards the end when the actual calculus begins.  Distribution was... 15% group project, 30% quiz, 15% midterm, 40% final.
Nice professor, very helpful, easy, clear, no homework.
she's a great teacher. EXCEPT for one thing. you better be damn certain that you do well on the final. my avg in the class was an 82.2 but my overall grade was a D+ because of my final.

Awesome professor. Wants her students to do well in the class. I never had a problem understanding her. I wasn't that interested in taking calc at first and it ended up being my best class this semester.
Great teacher, not totally easy, but no really really hard, i liked her overall!

Amazing woman.  She's a truly caring teacher who knows how to convey her material.  I paid attention, did my homework and got an A.  I don't see what everyone is talking about with her accent.  She's just as understandable as anyone I've ever talked to and I don't know one person in my class who had trouble comprehending her.

good teacher, has an accent but still understandable. sometimes move a little fast but just ask her to slow down. quizes alwasy exactly what she says they will be. so far as a frosh, best teacher ive had

She was a teacher that yes, did have an accent, but i didn't find it to be a problem, while she did move fast it was a fairly reasonable pace, her tests were always what she said they were going to be, no bull, overall good teacher
I've never liked math, but calculus is easily my favorite class this semester b/c prof. bai makes it simple and clear- plus she's really nice and can be funny! I love those quiz reviews
couldnt be any easier- gives practice quizzes that are the exact format of tests, even for midterm/final.  lets you drop 2 worst grades.  very nice woman.
All the quiz reviews are pretty much the same as the quizs you get.  She goes over anything you need help in during class and is very helpful.  Plus she's really nice.
Excellent teacher. Goes slow and makes sure all students understand material. Goes over quiz material, project is easy and lectures are clear.

i could not undestand a word she said, i had to change classes, and besides, she moves extremely quickly, i was doing bad becuase of her accent, and her rapid explanations and assumptions that everyone knows what they are doing.
Quizzes were easy, but I still learned stuff from her class. She is soft spoken, but covered all the material needed to do well on the final, great teacher.
The class is boring, shes a straight-forward teacher. You have to remember to practice doing the problems and pay close attention the 1st 2 weeks, or else the class is a struggle.
great teacher.... quizes, midterm, and final are exactly like the practice versions she gives you... hard to understand but very helpful
