a little hard to get used to but an easy 100
Very Easy Class. Very Easy Teacher. Practice quizzes are exactly like actual quizzes. Honestly this is the easiest calculus class that you will ever take!!
Test and quizes are the same as practice quizes she gives out in class.Class is boring as hell and the group project sucks

She can't speak very good english, but she knows what she's talking about.  The practice quizzes are EXACTLY like the real quizzes, which rules.  She is fair and she doesn't take attendance.  I have never taken calc before, and I'm doing well!
This class would have been a whole lot easier if she spoke fluent english. Class is pretty easy. Gives you practice quizzes that are the same as the quiz you take.
No Comments
No Comments
IF you have trouble with your grades and try to email her do you get a response NOOOOOOOOOO definetly not cool
Nice teacher. Clear and understandable. Easy quizzes and always prepares you before tests.
Prof Borkin is a very chill teacher. Great woman, love her
Prof. Borkin is a strong lecturer and clearly passionate about the topic. The course pushes you to think deeply about how visualizations influence interpretation.
easy class, not a lot of work tbh.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Fantastic course. Prof. Borkin provides real examples from research and industry. The class helped me build a strong portfolio piece for visualization work.
The class content is interesting, but it can feel disorganized at times. Prof. Borkin clearly knows the material, but some assignments require a lot of interpretation.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the most useful classes in the data science curriculum. Prof. Borkin emphasizes both technical skills and communication, which is incredibly important for data scientists.
Excellent instructor. Prof. Borkin gives great feedback on projects and encourages creativity in visual design. The class requires effort but is extremely rewarding.

This class changed how I think about presenting data. Prof. Borkin constantly shows real-world examples and explains why certain visualizations succeed or fail. Highly recommend taking this course with her.
One of the best professors I've had in the data science program

Great class overall. Prof. Borkin is passionate about visualization and pushes students to think critically about how data should be presented. The workload can be heavy because of the programming and writing components, but the assignments are meaningful

Prof. Borkin is an excellent professor for DS 4200. She clearly understands visualization at a deep level and explains concepts like perception, color theory, and encoding in a way that actually sticks. The projects are challenging but very relevant to real data science work. If you put in the effort, you will learn a lot.
you have to go to clas or else the teacher punishes you. so hard to learn if you dont go to class!!!
Class was pretty challenging.
I learned a lot about Excel and Tableau in this class. Really helped prepare me for my coop.
great teacher not that much work. def take her if you can get into her class
Her lectures are interesting.

In my 2 years of going here I have never left a rating, but Prof Borkin is the most inspirational teacher I have ever had. Lectures arent mandatory but I go to them because they are so amazing. I hope every student at northeastern has a chance to have a class with her.
Prof Borkin is such an easy A

The lectures are repetitive and mostly intuitive, but then homework is unnecessarily long and time consuming with convoluted instructions. The altair and matplotlib tutorials were the only useful aspect of this class but were not even a significant part of the course. The course is 98% writing short essays and busy work and 2% coding.

In my 4 years at Northeastern I have never rated a professor before but this class is so awful that I felt I needed to protect future students. This course has an unbelievable amount of work and the strictest grading policy I have seen in all my DS courses. The professor is extremely passionate and knowledgable but has no sympathy for students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Unfortunately if you are a DS major you are required to take this class - just be prepared to be bombarded with time consuming, virtually useless assignments. The class is lengthy and no information you learn is useful. This is the first DS class I have not learned anything in. If you can take this class with another professor I'd recommend it.
though enthusiastic in her presentations, she has a poor class structure and the content is repetitive and useless.

Her class is prolonged and the content is virtually useless. The service learning aspect prevents you from making any sort of visualizations that could help you in the real world. The coding tutorials are barely touched upon while it's more focused on writing 400-word essays weekly. If you want to take this class take it with someone else.

She is a very enthusiastic speaker and her lectures are good if you're interested in visualizations, but grading takes a ridiculously long time and I have emailed her 3 different times and she has failed to respond each time. The group project at the end of the semester is whatever and homework is fine, I just wouldn't take this class again.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The entire lectures are made up of her explaining visualizations, and she's great at it. But from homework 5 and after you are required to code in D3 which the TA teaches for about 40 mins once a week. I'm sure he was great but just not a good teacher, and the starter codes are not enough to complete the homework that make up most of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Even when you think you studied enough. It's never enough.

He knows his stuff, but his lectures are extremely boring and dry. He also has zero leniency in his syllabus, so if you get sick for more than three days or have a family emergency, your participation grade is tanked. Tests are difficult and he doesn't answer any clarifying questions during the test. Not the worst professor, but avoid if you can.

Bunce is overall not a bad teacher, but his lectures are so boring. He is redundant while lecturing, and I honestly learned better just watching the videos he recorded. Tests are all 50 MC questions and were not too bad. The class isn't that difficult, but I wouldn't take this class with him if your teacher being engaging is important to you.

Prof. Bunce is kind, caring and very knowledgeable who genuinely wants students to succeed. The course has 3 open response exams (lowest weighted less), 6 reflection articles (lowest dropped) and poll everywhere attendance with 2 drops. If you put in the work, you'll do well.

My complaint with Dr. Bunce was that his lectures were boring and very slow paced. All of his lectures are recorded which was really helpful. There are 3 exams, the lowest scoring of the 3 he weighs the lowest, he drops one quiz grade, and gives 1% extra credit at the end for trace. This gives you plenty of opportunities to get a good grade.
He is a great guy, and the class is easy enough if you are a behavioral neuroscience major. But im sorry, the lecture are a bunch of repeated information that makes me disengage.

A lot of materials in a semester, but exams were okay as long as u put in adequate effort. He also gave out 1% boost for your overall grade for completing the trace evaluatio,n which I think is very generous.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I wouldn't encourage people to take this class unless necessary. Lectures are uninformative but mandatory. I had to teach myself the content before each exam. Incredibly harsh grader and gives no extra credit. Grade is 3 exams and 6 reflections. He's a nice guy, but this class was unnecessarily hard. @BNS students: take a different BNS core.

Theres so much content to learn esp. if you havent taken and bns classes before. Bunce is super passionate but I couldnt keep up with him and there was so much to memorize. Your grade is only composed of quizzes and exams so theres not a lot you can do to raise your grade. Attendance is also mandatory. Great guy but a lot of content to learn.

Professor Bunce is very understanding and an excellent lecturer. Getting an A is attainable if you're willing to put in a lot of work. He films all his lectures on Panopto (very helpful), and all of the questions on the Test can be found in the lectures. However, you will need to rewatch every lecture before an exam to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Bunce is super passionate about what he teaches. His exams are based on lecture material and he is a very fair grader. Your effort in this class corresponds to your grade. Bunce is supportive, kind and accessible outside of class. Wants the best for his students. Material can be complicated, so go to class. Great for BNS students.

Dr. Bunce is honestly too disliked, he's great. Tries so hard to make class engaging even with dense material. Wants students to do well. You have to actually come to class to succeed, but if you pay attention and study the exams should be pretty fair. Weekly quizzes and poll everywhere help buffer grade so it's not just exams.

If you don't memorize each slide (and each midterm is about 80-90 slides each) you are cooked. All of his questions are so specific and he gives no other opportunities to raise your grade, even when half the class (I'm not joking) get below a D.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Bunce is a great professor. He explains things clearly and thoroughly and welcomes questions. Exam questions are heavily borrowed from weekly quizzes and PollEverywhere. Your grade is almost entirely based on exams, but he weights your lowest one the least. Attendance is via PollEverywhere, you can miss 3. Lectures are recorded.

Dr. Bunce is great! Exams are extremely fair; mostly all content comes from lectures, and pay attention to what he says (and not just the words on the slides). You don't really need the textbook content for the exams, maybe 1 or 2 questions (I would still skim it though). I personally enjoyed his lectures. Learned lots of useful information.

Professor Bunce is wonderful. His neuroanatomy course is moderately fast-paced, but he always reemphasizes important concepts. He is encouraging, and he provides you with everything you need to succeed (like recording all the lectures!). He is also helpful during office hours, and his tests are very fair. I hope to take another class with him soon.
He is really fun and a generous grader. He tends to go on tangents during lecture but they are usually pretty interesting. His office hours are super helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Bunce reads from a slidedeck, resulting in boring, monotonous lectures. On the bright side, his tests are an easy 50 MCQs that are pulled straight from the slides. I would take him again for the class, even if it is a little boring.

This man talks A LOT. Sometimes it does also feel like he grades based off of a set grade he decides to give you (I've lost points on tests and gone to office hours and he has said that I got the right answer but didn't answer it how he wanted me to even though he says we can answer it however). He does seem interested in what he teaches though.
He looks evil. PPT not designed for self-study-iers, class good, not boring

- lectures can sometimes be boring and material is made more complicated than necessary 
- exams are not too hard if you put in the effort and study (taken from slides and lecture)
- material is interesting and he is very clear when communicating the material 
- Good prof. and if your willing to put in some effort you will do in the class
professor bunce literally bombards students with information

He is a great professor thats passionate about what he teaches. Sometimes his explanations of pathways are a little rocky but then again they aren't easy so its not his fault. Can get caught up in tangential discussions which is good if you are curious but can be boring for some.

Bunce is probably the nicest person I have ever met. He clearly cares about all of his students and wants them to do well. Sometimes his lectures feel like a lot, and he can be monotone at times, but all lectures are recorded, so you can review anything you did not catch in class. The tests are multiple-choice, and no textbook is needed.

Nice teacher, lectures can be monotone and dry, but its valuable information condensed and made easier to understand. Lectures are recorded so it's easy to go back to if you miss class. Exam questions are pretty straight forward if you study the material. If you go to his office hours, he is very supportive and will answer any questions.

Professor Bunce is clearly a very knowledgable and passionate member of his field. His lectures can be pretty dry, but he's always excited to answer questions. I would say to make sure you are actively listening, because his tests often have material that he doesn't put in the notes. Despite that, tests were very fair, not necessarily easy though.

Professor Bunce is great! While his lectures themselves can be a little dry at times overall the course material was interesting and his exams are super fair as long as you study. He is really accessible both at office hours and via email and overall is just a really nice guy!
He is pretty good at making long, information dense lectures bearable. However, your entire grade is based on 5 multiple choice tests.

Professor Bunce really cares about his students and has a strong passion for neuroscience. Lectures can sometimes be a lot to take in, but if you visit office hours, he'll help clarify any ambiguous topics. Exams are very fair; there aren't any surprises. Overall, a great professor.

Absolutely loved Professor Bunce and his Neuroscience of Aging class. His quizzes and exams are easy as long as you show up to class and review the lecture slides beforehand. Overall a great professor, you could tell that he was really passionate about the topics we discussed in class and was very, very helpful when I went to his office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Bunce is very knowledgeable. The lecture slides are all posted on Canvas but most of the information isn't summarized in the slides, so you have to take good notes in class. Always posts lectures on canvas. Moves pretty fast, but repeats info a lot and answers questions. Pay attention in class, study/do the readings, and you should be good.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Bunce is great! He knows his stuff and is clearly into what he teaches. He is super organized and has very clear expectations- great class would take one with him again :)

Prof. Bunce is a good guy. He's passionate about neuroscience, and you can tell in his classes. His lectures can be drawn out and repetitive at times, but he gets across the information. Grading rubrics are important, and he sticks to them. Tests are very specific but doable, so be ready to study to know all the information.

Dr. Bunce is a nice man and seems to care about his students, but if I'm being honest Neuro of Aging with him gave me a headache. He goes on super long tangents in class and then expects you to know stuff he mentioned once for the tests. Overall I found him super confusing, but at least he was easier on the poster project and write ups.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: Had Spring 2020 so half online. OH MY GOSH the lectures are so boring. Do not be sleep deprived or you WILL fall asleep. Counter that with the fact that they are PACKED with information, and every detail will be tested in the quizzes (50 multiple choice questions). Grade is completely made up by quizzes. At least he records every class.

Prof. Bunce is well knowledgable and whatnot but I honestly find him irritating. He makes the material seem more complicated than it is which makes me anxious, and his tone makes him seem stressed out as well. He can spend the first half of class outlining what we did the class before. Tests are multiple choice and can be tricky.

I really enjoyed the content of this class but get ready to study a lot. No hw. 3 exams, 3 quizzes, if you don't do well, your grade will suffer. Definitely rewatch his lectures to study because some questions relate to very small parts mentioned once. If you memorize well, you'll do fine. I liked Jamie despite it all but I get the boring part.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This man's class made me want to switch majors. In fact, my roommate DID switch majors after taking his class. He is dry in his lectures and will put you to sleep. His tests are all MC but there are few of them, so if you bomb one you're screwed for the rest of the class. I actively avoid taking him as a professor for any other class.

Professor Bunce's Biopsychology class is incredibly boring. His lectures are dry and full of information. His multiple choice tests and quizzes are specific and very challenging. The questions do not seem representative of the material covered in lecture. I would not recommend this, at it was by far the hardest PSYC class I have taken.

One of my favorite professors at Northeastern. Material can be tricky but he is a very fair grader and not hard to do well in his class as long as you study before exams. Everything you need to know for exams he posts on Blackboard. He also posts all of his lectures on Tegrity so attendance is not mandatory. He really cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Amazing and clear lectures. He is the best lecturer I've had at Northeastern. You need to attend or at least watch the lectures online. The material is not easy and a bit complex, and you have to show you understand it in the exams, but if you watch the lectures and read the papers you should be fine.

I had Bunce for Neuroscience of Aging and Biopsych and I really enjoyed his classes. As long as you pay attention during his lectures and take notes, you will do fine! He's super nice and willing to help and it's not hard to figure out what he is looking for on tests.

Bunce is a genius, but I am not. His tests are VERY specific. I have never worked so hard ina class in my life. I spend my spare time listening to his lectures. He is an amazing person, but I find myself always struggling to keep up in his lectures. It is A LOT of info very fast. Only take neurobio with him if you had him for biopsych!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Bunce is a boring lecturer. He posts his powerpoints on blackboard, but he actually reads his lectures from a manuscript that he doesn't share with the class. Thank God he records his lectures because this man talks WAY too fast to keep up. Quizzes and tests are all MC, but very very specific so study all details. Prepare to spend HOURS studying.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is a very nice guy that is passionate about neuroscience. He gets pretty boring during the lectures. Never really paid attention during class and did just fine in his class. His homework assignments included LOTS of reading. Tests and quizzes were all short answer, but I thought they were pretty easy if you studied the readings and lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Best advice is to listen to his recorded lectures! All the information/ examples you need to do well on the exams/ quizzes are things he has said in lecture, you just have to pay attention. Overall, he is a very knowledgeable and fair professor, don't be afraid to go to office hours!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Would take again

reviews.comment: Doesn't know how to make the class interesting, so its hard to maintain attention and he includes too much material. Exams are open response, and it seems impossible to get an A. Don't recommend to anyone.

Don't take this Professor. 1) Talks way too fast- you will fail his quizzes and exams due to his useless lectures. 2) Reads off of a script in class, but DOESN'T share it with us- as if digesting the material isn't enough to do. 3) A dozen worthless article write ups. 4) Takes points off for ANYTHING, almost impossible to get an A. Oh he's boring!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--SO MANY PAPERS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: I took this class as an elective bc it sounded interesting and it turned out to be a nightmare. His slides are empty, no words even though he reads from his notes that he could share. Talks too fast to be able to type everything so you have to listen to his recordings. Quizzes and tests are open responses which makes the whole thing even harder.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Bunce is a solid choice to get started in the neuroscience path. He is sociable, knows what he is talking about, and is very accessible outside class. He wants you to do well. His tests are all short answer, and they are tough if you don't study. Put in the work and be interested in the material. You'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Professor Bunce was one of the best professors I have had. There are around 15 articles that you read throughout the year and you are expected to write a short one page summary of each article. His quizzes and tests are open answer, but all tests are things that have been reviewed multiple times in class. Fair grader and does his best to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: i LOVE dr. bunce. such a nice professor, and whenever you need him just go to office hours or shoot him an email. very accommodating and willing to talk about class as well as longer-term goals. i had him for bio psyc and this course (neurobio) was very similar. didn't require much extra effort, notes were the same as from before.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Bunce really is a gem. The material can be dry, and it's hard for him to always make up for that, but he tries. He's kind, brilliant, and really wants his students to do well. I learned quite a bit with him.

Dr. Bunce was a great professor! He really cares about his student's learning and is always available for extra help. His classes are lecture heavy but they are interesting and the slides are always online. Definitely a tough class but that's expected of the course material.
He's very monotone. It's not hard content, but it is very much based on his lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Need to put in some effort to pass. There's a lot of material, but if you pay attention in class and read the textbook you should be fine. Tests are based more on concepts and systems than details. He can lecture quickly, but if you ask him a question he's very good at explaining, and is available outside of class, beyond posted office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: lectures be drier than the saharan desert
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Straight forward class with interesting material. No need to get the textbook. Has his own powerpoint slides that directly translate to the testing material. Worth taking if interested in the subject.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Prof. Bunce is a good person but his lectures are long and boring. The topic is really interesting but it is hard to concentrate for the entire class time. You will do fine on quizzes and tests if you study (or if you can manage to pay attention). On the other hand, he really knows his stuff and will help if you go to office hours.

Dr. Bunce is honestly my favorite professor at NU so far. The course is difficult, but he is ALWAYS willing to help. I even went into his office hours one day and asked him how he suggest I study for quizzes and exams, and he was extremely helpful. You can tell he really loves the subject which is infectious. Can be a boring course, but worth it!

Dr. Bunce is quite interesting, if you're interested in the topic. Very knowledgeable and clear about what he is teaching, will always answer questions clearly. Lectures can feel long but if you write down his clarifying statements about the slides and listen it will be fine. Super nice guy as well. Tests are  MC straightforward pathways/concepts.
Not that exciting of a class. However, he definitely knows what he's talking about so I would imagine that if you were really interested in a course topic you would find his class interesting.
Boring.

The class itself isn't that bad but her lectures are ridiculously dense and repetitive. She requires a textbook that she claims is needed for the course but refuses to answer questions about its content and barely features it on the exams. Worth noting that her friend and frequent collaborator wrote the book.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do yourself a big favor if you love yourself. Don't take her. Very disorganized person in general, she doesn't know how to use technology, and despite the fact she knows her subject, she doesn't communicate effectively. She doesn't now how to communicate at all.

One of the worst professors I have ever had. Professor Berent's slides are extremely confusing, misrepresentative of the exam questions, and does not explain the information well even in office hours. Get ready to spend hours studying.

She is not as bad as everyone is saying imo, it is just a difficult class. She is a good teacher however and I think attending class and going to office hours will benefit you. Attendance is mandatory. Final is optional. I got an A by attending lecture, rewatching lecture recordings, and practice mcq. The final replaced my lowest grade.

very nice person but not a good prof. if you miss one lecture you'll be left wondering what is going on. i had to miss a few due to athletic events and it became confusing quickly after. also don't know how to post the canvas hw right half the time so you'll end up missing deadlines you didn't even know exsisted.

This course is not as difficult as everybody says it is. Tests are all multiple choice, and the cumulative final is optional. Professor Berent is super nice, but you HAVE to show up to class to do good otherwise you'll be lost; can't learn from slides alone.
I dropped this class three weeks before finals bro, one of our tests the class mean is 60 something... please do yourself a favor dont fxxking take this class

Take this class if youre super interested in learning sentence structure, if youre not, then don't take it. Class material is not interesting. Heavy course material, I genuinely don't see a point of taking this class lol if it's not mandatory.

She's one of the sweetest prof i've ever had. lecture is a bit heavy, you really have to lock in and focus during class. homework are super easy. tests are all multiple choice. materials are quite abstract, not much of psychology related content. highly recommend to go to her office hour if taking this class just so she can recognize you.

This was the most tedious class as it is so lecture heavy and the tests were ridiculous. She has the most vague answers to questions, mostly going on tangents and is just genuinely difficult to comprehend. The class is way too long and there has to be a better way to learn the material. Do not take this class as a psych major-- do yourself a favor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She doesn't even know what she's teaching, and sometimes so political for no rzn???? Tests make no sense btw.

She was a super sweet and knowledgable professor. If you actually pay attention and try in class it's a very interesting course and unique field of study. The no technology policy seemed helpful, but some students really did not like it.
Nice person but very poor professor. Unorganized and repetitive.
LOVE THIS CLASS TAKE IT
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I wouldn't call this class an easy psych elective, but if you go to class, pay attention, and take notes you will really be fine! I thought the topics were really fascinating but could be a bit linguistics-heavy if that's not something you're interested in. Her research is also really impressive. Only con is her slides can be quite disorganized.

The theories and concepts presented in the class often lack logical coherence. The grading of exams seems to be subjective. While she is a pleasant person, as an educator she is not supportive or helpful. Additionally, the practice tests look nothing like the actual exams, diminishing their usefulness as preparatory tools.

Do not take this class unless you have an interest in linguistics. Not a psych class, and sounds more interesting than it is. Go to one of her office hours at the beginning of the semester because it's nice for her to know you in such a big class. Classes can be really boring and feel long, so it's hard to pay attention. Don't buy the textbook.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. She was genuinely one of the most unhelpful professors I have had at Northeastern. She is honestly sometimes just rude and even will just choose to not answer students' questions in class. Her study guides/practice tests are not helpful and she gave us our last exam on the last day of classes for the semester (not a final).

Don't understand the low rating, she's one of my favorite profs I've had at Northeastern. People are just complaining because the class is heavy on linguistics I guess? Just pay attention in class - the lectures are interesting, and if you stay present (for the admittedly long class block), you'll do well on the exams.

She knows a lot about the subject but I honestly really flopped in this class and I was trying...she's not a good lecturer. Don't take this class unless you have a genuine interest and maybe even background in linguistics- an interest in psych isn't enough
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Only take this class if you have an interest in linguistics or have taken a linguistics class in the past. It is marketed as a psych class but it is really a linguistics class and by the time I figured this out it was too late. There are three exams and a final that make up most of your grade. She knows a lot about language but I struggled to learn

she's not horrible but she's not good. she moves so slowly and wastes time at the start of class doing a review of last class and repeats herself too much. her lectures are boring and not engaging. the course content could be so interesting but she presents it in the most boring way possible. she greatly exaggerates the difficulty of the tests
Interesting topic and very cool to be taught by a professor with experience in the field

Although this class can be hard, looking back it's been my favorite class at Northeastern. The topic is truly fascinating and you can tell Dr. Berent is passionate about the subject. She's an expert in the field, and though she doesn't talk about it, her own research is fascinating if you look it up! She's so kind and cares about her students.

I loved her class! She was definitely passionate about the material and wanted us all to succeed. Although the class can be slow at times, if you pay attention to the lectures and study, you will do fine on the tests.

The class was very easy, but also painfully slow. It made learning the information easy though - everything was very simple as she seems to think we are all dumb. The material was very interesting and the professor was clearly passionate and wanted everyone to understand the material.

Dr. Berent offers a very interesting course. Linguistics is a challenging subject and she did a great job of conveying the information in a more simplistic way. You must attend lectures! I would not recommend taking this course if you are not interested in the areas of linguistics or psychology. She is VERY open to receiving questions and feedback.

She is very intelligent, if you like to think and are creative this class if for you. She is difficult- but the class is very interesting and her class made me a stronger student. If you spend time with the material and put in effort you should be good- if you like to do minimal studying do not take this class. I think she was great.

the worst professor I've ever come across. She doesn't care about students and everyone saying she was good, I don't know if they were actually in her class. She doesn't care about you at all and she also has no idea how to teach. You won't learn a thing, don't waste your money.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Trust me don't take her
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Not a good lecturer. Clearly has a mastery of the subject but is not good at transferring that knowledge to students. Not engaging at best, very confusing at worst. Spends so much time on one slide you completely lose track of where she is in her overlapping metaphors and tangents. Stuggled to pay attention.

I don't think this course should be a psychology course, it should be called "history of language". It is not related closely with Psychology! Also, the professor is awful, even the TA is better than her!!

This class covers complex topics and she does an excellent job making them more accessible. Paying attention and asking questions is crucial to understanding the material as her initial delivery of the information isn't always the best. She's done a lot of really interesting research in the field that was fascinating to learn about.

Challenging, interesting class. English is not her first language, but she most DEFINITELY is a fluent English speaker, and an intelligent one at that. Attendance is essential to understanding the material. She is clearly passionate about linguistics, and wants to help students understand the subject. Power points are clear cut and simple.
It is ironic that she teaches a language class without the ability to convey her materials clearly in a universal language. Power points are super messed up.....
Loved her

English skills are absolutely not good enough to each a class in English, let alone about complex ideas in the english language. Cannot understand questions, so she cannot answer questions with relevant answers.  VERY Frustrating. Expect a challenge. (I wish someone could just sit in for one class to assess her ability, not worth the money)

Dr. Berent could improve her lecture skills, as she sped talks in her lectures. She often "taught to" the exams and as such, was unable to foster real learning. The course content drastically varied, and the grading was too test-oriented. Suggestion: a legit written component, instead of unhelpful BB homework, such as writing a paper.

She's really not as bad as people think. If you show up to class and pay attention you'll be fine. Her powerpoints are very descriptive, but you won't understand what's going on in them without actually going to class and listening. If you want to be lazy and not go to class, don't take this class. If you don't go, you'll be lost. Tests are easy.

Though the teacher wasn't really helpful...if you did assignments and studied the powerpoints, it's really not that bad at all. I did not find the class all that difficult, but I did most of the learning on my own
AWFUL AWFUL AWFUL!! Pointless class. You cannot understand her and when you ask for clarification she just repeats the same thing and keeps going. DO NOT TAKE IT!it will only hurt you

God awful teacher. Can't speak English, the book is very unhelpful, powerpoints are very vague and she doesn't explain concepts well.  You can't even look up concepts because the book is not really a textbook. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!
No Comments
totally incomprehensible.  the material really shouldn't be this hard to understand, but she makes it impossible.  read the powerpoints online and hope for the best.

WORST CLASS AND TEACHER OF MY LIFE! do not take! she cannot speak english and is impossible to understand! the material is interesting however difficult to understand without clear explination, wich she does not give! she is not open is others opinions  favors those who fluently speak a second language. only class i have ever had to take pass fail!

Interesting ML course and material. Exams are very hard and will need to be massively curved. Homeworks are easy. Kind of an odd guy, he is very worried about people recording the class or distributing his lecture slides online among other things.

The class consistently runs 20+ minutes overtime despite already being 3.5 hours. Homework is straightforward, but exams are closed-book, closed-note, no cheat-sheet allowed. No practice exam or study guidance for exams beyond “just review the slides.” Lectures aren't recorded, and slides are uploaded days after class.

I enjoyed Professor Braun classes, the material is up to date and provides great NLP basis. The books he refers to are great and he takes time to explain concepts in the class. His exams are difficult but he applies curving to the grades. His classes are theoretical and his homeworks and project require to apply the concepts using python.

Easy homework (unlimited attempts), easy quizzes (2 attempts), and lectures are very clear. He's accessible outside of class and helps anyone who needs help. You have 6 makeup attempts for assignments you did poorly on/forgot to do. Exams are also easy; he helps if you ask him. Don't cheat, doesn't make sense. Also, the final exam is take-home.

This is the most peak professor ever. He let us introduce ourselves during the first day of class and I mentioned that my favorite show was Hunter Hunter, and he actually looked it up in front of the whole class. TALK ABOUT PEAK. Deserves a raise after that one for sure.

Bradley was great for psych stats; grade is made up of TYK, homework, quizzes, tests, and a presentation. Quizzes were taken in groups, and the tests were open-note for a section every time. Tests also mirror some of the quizzes. Lectures are clear and concise (we got out early almost every day), and the course was overall not too difficult!
Good guy. Funny. Easy A if you do the work.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great teaching methods! More then fair exams and great professor. 10/10 would recommend.

Love this professor so much. Gave so many opportunities to learn and internalize the information. Spent a lot of time on extra videos, spreadsheets, and practice tests that helped so much. Quizzes are open-note and mirror exams. Homework is simple with 2 attempts. Not much classwork. Easy to do well if you pay attention. God bless Kristopher!!!!!!!

I LOVE Kristopher Bradley. I cannot recommend him enough. He is so passionate and clearly cares about each and every one of his students being successful in his course. He puts in so much effort outside of class that he is so proud of, and we are proud of him too. Not too assignment heavy, pretty easy A especially if you do the quizzes. TAKE HIM!!!

No reason you should not be getting an A in this class. Group quizzes, 2 attempts on homework and TYK quizzes, and he drills the content into your brain. Also, super nice and funny guy, and very flexible about retaking quizzes and how you do the final presentation.
Good professor, class is an A if you put in the work.  Kristopher is also very caring of his students and smart.

Ok yes I got an A, but I don't think I learned a whole lot. Love Bradley as a guy but his class is basically guaranteed to pass so it kinda takes away the actual learning and more about just getting random stuff done for your grade.

If you want a zero-effort course, Bradley is your guy, which is not a recommendation in my opinion. It's frustrating that there is nothing that differentiates a hard-working student from a lazy student. He is just okay in my opinion.

Quizzes: In class taken as a group, open note open everything, he sometimes even lets you check your answers before submitting it (For formatting purposes). Tests: Take home open note untimed. Classwork: None Homework: some (very easy graded for completion). Final: Take home very easy to get an A on of you follow the lecture notes as you take it.

Very knowledgeable and caring. Was very supportive & lenient outside of class. Class was graded by weekly open-note group quizzes, HW assignments, and 4 open-note take-home tests (including the final). Easy class, easy A.

If you want an easy A or just want to happily learn about Social Psych, than take his class. He's funny and extremely nice on if you miss any assignment. He want's to see everyone do well. The final is also optional..

I feel fortunate to have taken Professor Bradley for both Statistics in Psychology and Social Psychology! He is an amazing teacher who teaches you a lot and also is very caring. His assignments and tests are very straightforward and he is understanding about possible absences. I highly recommend him if anyone is looking to take a class with him!

This class was a huge disappointment. I have never been less motivated to attend a lecture course in my entire college career, and my attendance reflected that. The slides needed much more detail, and many of our assignments were moved earlier, reflecting that this class was not well-organized.

Had Bradley for PSY31 at Tufts. GREAT MAN, EASY A (and he does it on purpose!) Tests and quizzes are open-note. Quizzes are done in groups. Easy final project. Easy final exam (open-note, online, no time limit). Easy homework only graded based on completion. Many extra credit opportunities. I am so grateful for this man. Has an adorable son.

This was one of the best professors I've ever had at Northeastern. I ended this class with a 103%. As long as you apply yourself, pay great attention in lectures, and take notes you'll be fine. He also offers extra credit so take advantage of that. I did and it helped me end with an amazing grade. Overall a nice caring guy, I recommend him a lot.

Professor Bradley is a very nice guy with a passion for psychology, and it shows in his lectures. The class was relatively easy and there were a lot of discussion posts to boost your grade. However, you have to read the textbook to know everything on the test, as some of it just isn't covered in lecture.

One of my favorite professors. Dr. Bradley is very easygoing but knows what he is talking about, so it made learning statistics enjoyable. Quizzes are in-class with groups, and tests are take-home, open-note. He allows you to re-do questions you got wrong on exams and gives you the points back. No complaints at all. Definitely take him if you can.

dr. bradley is an extremely nice and overall great guy. it's easy to get an A if you do well enough on the tests (which pull some questions directly from practice quizzes + textbook), since the rest of your grade depends on open-note group quizzes, group discussions, and one project. you should read the textbook in addition to the lectures though

One of the best professors I've had. Makes the lectures very easy to follow and understand and really wants students to succeed. Very understanding and helps all the time. Really easy to do well in this class as long as you show up to class, pay attention and follow the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor, took him for summer 1. Graded by 3 tests, homework, in-class quizzes, and a final. All homework and tests were done at home and online with no time restrictions. Lectures were easy to understand and he explained how to use SPSS easily. He offers extra credit throughout and it's actually quite fun to do. He's very understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very easy professor, does assign homework but is very understanding when it comes to grades and requesting regrades. Tests are mostly online and open book, so definitely recommend.

Super interesting class, but an easy A. Classes mostly consist of lectures and participation assignments which are often discussion questions. These you can complete with a group. The final project was a group 15-20 min presentation on a topic of your interest from the year. These get a little repetitive becuase they go on for several weeks.

Dr Bradley is a good lecturer and I learned a lot! Also, all the quizzes are with groups in class and you can ask as many questions to him as you want. The exams are take home and online which definitely reduces stress as well. He is very open to feedback and wants you to succeed. Would recommend

Great professor! He is super interested in what he teaches, and his lectures are easy to understand. Tests are SUPER similar to practice exams, and he wants you to do well. Grade based off 3 tests (optional final can replace worst score), canvas "participation" questions, and group/open note quizzes. Take this class!!

Professor Bradley was a great professor! Allowed for quizzes to be taken open notes and in groups and as a professor overall was super understanding and caring, definitely relates to students by being human.

Even though attendance was not necessarily mandatory, there were graded assignments based on in-class discussions. The tests were very straightforward and built off of the online group quizzes we took in class. There was one group project at the end of the year which was not very difficult and an optional final if you wanted to boost your grade.

Professor Bradley used one class (of the week) for a lecture and the other two for a discussion which was really interesting. I found that to be more effective in learning and reeled me to attend class. We had a quizzes, discussion questions, 2 exams, and a group project (and an optional final). He kept class very chill and questions open-ended.

was a good introduction into psychology, very lecture based- barely any material on the posted slides which makes it difficult if you're a visual learner or miss class. lots of group discussions on topics, tests and quizzes are heavy on vocabulary so as long as you learn that you should do well.
She is great, super helpful homework. If you don't use AI, you will learn a lot from her homework

The format of the class is very clear with well defined lectures, labs, homework, and other assignments. There is a huge availability of resources and a lot of help available including Piazza and office hours. The professor is extremely reasonable and accessible, and if you do the work assigned without the use of ChatGPT, you will do well.
do not take her class
I thought this was a great class. Will thoroughly prepare you to improve your coding design skills.

Professor Bonaci's ability to fluently explain why she is doing something in the code speaks volumes to her knowledge in computer science and object oriented design. You can also tell she genuinely cares deeply about your learning experience and will help you if you're struggling. I recommend taking any of Professor Bonaci's courses if you can!

Professor Bonaci is well versed in the topic of OOD and makes each and every lecture interesting and full of helpful information to help students also become knowledgeable about OOD principles. She makes sure that every student is prepared to succeed, given that they put in the work and shows a respectable amount of effort on each assignment.

Professor Bonaci is the best professor, she cares about her students and would love to address problems for students. She gives us a clear explanation of the conceptual ideas and gives us examples to demonstrate the concepts. Her homework is well-designed for practicing the conceptions we learned in class.

Professor Bonaci goes above and beyond to produce a great learning experience. She organizes her lectures in a way that allows for review of the material as well as introducing new content. Professor also takes time to ask for feedback and designs her assignments in a very thoughtful way. Highly recommend taking this course with Professor Bonaci!

Tamara inspired the class to think about issues associated with Data Science, AI, and ML. We read a lot of papers and were also asked to find papers relevant to the domain on our own. As an MS student who was taking an advanced-level course, this was the course that gave me a clear-cut idea of what I should be pursuing in the future.

This professor's professional abilities are sorely lacking. Teaching style is confusing, material remains unclear. Availability is virtually nonexistent, feedback is a rare sighting, and rudeness seems to be their forte. To top it off, even emails go unanswered. Overall, not a stellar educational experience.
Not a great professor at all.

The professor doesn't understand OOD concepts and is a terrible teacher. She barely uploads course materials on time, and she seldom walks codes but just reads the slides during lecture time. She made the course unnecessarily confusing. You might ask a simple question, and she will give you an answer as if you're doing brain surgery. DO NOT TAKE!

Professor Larson is a phenomenal ORGB professor. Although the content is a bit boring, she truly does care about her students and makes her lectures engaging. The exams are all short answer but you get a one page cheat sheet that helps. Out of all the ORGB profs, she is probably the best choice!

Passionate about teaching and knows her material very well, but is a very tough grader. Class is usually engaging, but ~23% of grade comes from participation, so be on your game from day one. Attendance is very important for doing well in this class - if you pay attention and do your readings you will be just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: OrgB shouldn't be a required class to begin with, but it is, and do not take it with her. So unclear with grading, its so objective. Too many assignments towards the end.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Caring towards her students - most memorable professor I've had. Lectures are engaging with group activities/discussions. Participation matters and she gives enough opportunities to participate. You get a 2-page cheat sheet for exams and textbook is free. Exams are SAQ but you'll do well if you write a good cheat sheet and have attended class.

Very difficult class because of the way she chooses to grade, we clearly understand concepts, but so that everyone doesn't get A she grades very tough, which is also heavily specific/even at times opinionated.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Way too opinionated grading. Dont take puts off for not explaining the answer when the question doesn't say explain(Especially when you dont for other questions!). Way too harsh a grader for no reason. The content is almost middle school level, and she grades way too harshly for what it is.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Larson knows her material well and is good at getting the whole class to participate. The only thing I would say is she is a tough grader on papers, and she assigns way too many things during finals. If she spaced these out and gave better directions for her papers I would have enjoyed the class a lot more.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great graduate professor, the online format was difficult but she tries her best to make things engaging. I learned a lot from this class, it really helped tie the rest of my learning together!

The best professor I've had in the business program. She cares about students and wants you to do well. The grade is primarily based on a couple of tests (you are allowed a sheet of notes) and a large final paper. Good feedback, enjoyable class, and I learned a lot.

Professor Larson is a really great professor and has amazing lectures where you learn a lot, and makes the class interesting. Her class is very material heavy though, and unlike other ORGB professors, does not have as many papers, projects, or presentations, so her tests carry a lot of weight and can be very complex and tricky.

I had this course from 8am-noon on Saturday mornings - which could have been BRUTAL - but Barbara does a great job of holding it together, keeping the lecture interesting, and keeping the class fairly engaged throughout. Also, very willing to make herself available when needed. If she's not in the office, she'll tell you to call her cell.
He's nice but it's a bit hard to hear him because he speaks very quietly and softly. The course itself is incredibly difficult and graded on 3 exams each worth 30%.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you can avoid Luca, do it. He is not a good teacher. His lectures are just him doing practice problems but he doesn't explain anything and just starts doing it and gets it wrong half the time. Then he gives 3 tests that are a combined 90% of ur grade and hw is the other 10%. He grades the hw so hard my hw average was lower than my exam grades

letter grade dropped by 2 whole letters just because submitted four homeworks late :((((( like im sorryyyy i had stuff to do
gpa dying
late policy is all the others after the first late assignment get zeros >:(

the professor is a very nice guy but i did not like his teaching style at all. just sort of talks at you while doing the problems. exams are each worth 30% of your grade and are three questions each, easy to get partial credit but also difficult to lose a lot of points on.

DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! Very smart person, but doesn't know how to teach AT ALL. The only thing he does in lectures is examples, which are wrong half the time. Notably the exams don't even reflect the homework or exam practices. Office hours he is extremely distracted and has to do other things every couple of minutes. AVOID AT ALL COSTS
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! He is very smart, but is unable to explain it to students. His homework is graded harsh and so are the 3 exams that make up the entire grade, not to mention they don't relate to homework or anything done it class. The class average for each midterm has been from 50-70. This class does not make sense. AVOID!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE FOR STATICS! YOU WILL NOT LEARN. DOES NOT KNOW HOW TO TEACH AND SPENDS THE ENTIRE LECTURE DOING ONE PROBLEM WITHOUT EXPLAINING THE CONCEPTS AND STEPS OF SOLVINF. HAS SLIDES BUT NEVER USED THEM TO EXPLAIN. CANNOT TEACH PERIOD. DO NOT TAKE.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He just does examples for the class without really explaining what he's doing.
Does not teach, all he does is example problems the entire class. Avoid him for statics at all costs. Nice and friendly guy, and grades decently, but you will end up learning better online.

Prof Caracoglia is a nice and approachable person, but his lecture is very hard to follow. Most of the students cannot follow what he is doing. Tests are not as hard as other professors and he gives generous partial credit if you try.
Prof Caracoglia is a friendly and caring person. However, his lectures are extremely unclear and most students cannot follow. His tests are easy, though. He gives generous partial credit on exams.
very kind and helpful

I had Luca for Statics. He's pretty funny and overall a nice guy. You have to really pay attention to him to learn, he is a genius but he speaks quietly and with an accent. His tests are pretty hard. He makes a lot of mistakes in lecture, but does not tolerate your mistakees on tests! Grade are typically scaled.. A lot.
He's a brilliant teacher. Always willing to help. Had a great time in his class. Highly recommended.
Overall, he is a decent professor yet fails to explain  the lesson easily. He isn't a very grader, and he isn't fair with exams but if you work hard in his class you ought to be ok

He is a brilliant guy who struggles from a language difference. I took Structural Analysis 1 which wasn't easy. He definitely knows his stuff, but has a hard time communicating concepts. However, if you don't understand something, he'll give you 110% to help you out. You just have to be patient with him. He's a great guy.
Tough but fair
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof Charvet's tests and grading are tougher than average. Negative reviews probably from students not putting any effort and expecting to coast anyways. CLosed book and expects you to know most of the material. Fair teacher, knows a lot and uses real-life examples.
Worst professor ever!! Never take già class! Very hard-graded. Hates every international student
I was interested in Supply Chain before taking this class...Worst Professor I ever had! DO NOT TAKE HIM!

Worst Professor EVER! Doesn't want to give full credit (always finds some lame excuses to not give 100%), and the only way you can get a good grade is by memorizing his incredibly boring slides. Quizzes are hard and final exam is comprehensive. DO NOT TAKE HIM!!!

If you expect an easy A, don't take this class. Don't expect to get 100% for particiation just by being in the class. My experience have been great with Prof. Charvet. His class actually prepared me for the real world. When you get out of school, don't expect much hand holding by your managers.
Hard hard hard class. Hard grader, boring and way too much HW. So much reading every day and crazy about participation. Got D for participation for attend every class, and speak out once per class.

Worst professor in the supply chain dept.  takes the subject of transportation and makes it even more unbearable.  assigns way too much reading for each class and online book is difficult to read.  professor simply does not know how to teach effectively.  Slides are confusing and tests are hard.  Class is much harder than it should be.
I was very interested in Supplychain management before I took this class. He should learn how to teach.

His tests are extremely difficult.  Assigned a simulation to do every week, before he taught us the principles behind it.  Does not speak clear and is very hard to understand.  Must read and try very hard to do well in the class.

Truly one of the kindest people I've ever met. I can't speak highly enough of him. If you reach out for help or need an extension, he's incredibly understanding and willing to work with you. I highly recommend taking any of his classes, he's genuinely supportive and accommodating.

I absolutely LOVED Craig. He is such a gem and deserves so much more attention than he's given. His lectures can be boring, but he loves to expand on topics and really help students understand. He's accommodating for his students and really cares about his students. He's the sweetest man ever.
Professor Roberston is so funny, gives great content and lectures. His class definitely isn't an easy A, it's kind of a lot of work but not super hard work.
This guy is the best. Inspirational. Really just living his best life writing unintelligably on the chalkboard. If you do the readings and go to class an A- is practically guarenteed.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very passionate about his subjects, but he gets it if you aren't. Very clear about expectations and genuinely just wants everyone to learn something from him.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Craig is awesome! He makes class super interesting and has the best sense of humor. Would highly recommend his classes.

Robertson is one of my favorite professors I've had here.  He's funny and engaging in his lectures, and he is very passionate about his subject and wants you to get excited about it too.  He's very accommodating and understanding if you speak to him.  It's a lot of reading, but nothing too bad.  I highly recommend this class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I am not a fan. Craig is extremely biased and I never once was given an article to read that provided a viewpoint that wasn't his. This class was dull, useless and had no educational value. Also regardless of if I spent 1 or 20 hours on a paper I always got a B. If you want an easy useless class go ahead, but not if you want to learn anything.
Amazing professor with awesome teaching style. He is very clear. Do the readings and be open to understand what you may not have thought about before this class. can't stress enough how amazing.

Robertson is very smart guy when it comes to media and cultural theories. The class is an absolute ****, as the topics are really abstract, but Robertson manages to pull you in with his unique teaching style. He has a great sense of humor. You must read, and do not BS the essays, he'll see right through it. He also gives a ton of comments on work.

While this class had a dry topic, Professor Robertson made it pretty exciting and interesting.  The classes were dicussions rather than lectures and he valued participation.  Grades were based on 2 long and 4 short essays. He's also very funny.

Very interesting class with a fun teacher.  The class is discussion based and Robertson did a good job of keeping the class going.  He is also pretty funny with a dry sense of humor in an annoying briton kinda way.  He's also got a funny New Zeland accent.  Craig Robertson rocks hard and often.  He's got a black belt in keeping it real.

Class is OK, videos can be long but the requirements aren't much each week, class def depends on your final project group. Project requirements are long word dumps (think 2-5 pgs) and you can expect to lose points on things you SWEAR aren't there but actually were: 5 words about something needing a trait n losing an entire half letter grade for it
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Extremely heavy workload. Expect to have a major project due every 2 weeks, on top of tedious course modules due every week. And on top of that, a final project which has an extremely long rubric of items, while you are forced to work in a team of only 3.

Also, the professor comes off as really pretentious in his assignments i.e. smile emoji spam.

THIS MAN IS A LEGEND. The lecture videos are so well done, and he is passionate and ready to help outside of class. If you want to make games as a hobby, or are pursuing the major, start by taking a course with this guy. ++

So much homework. SUPER HEAVY WORKLOAD!!! Sometimes one homework was not due and he already released another one. Homework has extremely long instructions. Takes up to 24 hours to finish. Submission's a pain in the ass. Does learn a lot and make a lot of projects but none are useful unless you tryna be a game programmer.

Avoid unless super interested in Unity/Games. You can learn a decent amount if you apply yourself, but be warned that Calgar has some... let's just say issues when interfacing with his students, and that's if he manages to reply to your emails at all. If you do take this class, follow assignments exactly, and just avoid Calgar. Then you're fine.

DO NOT take HCI with him if you actually want to go into UI/UX+already have experience working with clients. This class is only helpful for students who are minimally interested in UX design because he doesn't teach industry-standard tools. He teaches the design process as if there's only one way to do it and uses Axure for the course and not Figma

Took him during the summer for the summer classes in Seattle, can be a bit condescending at times but can be funny. This class is based off of a group project so if you get a crappy team, be prepared to do all of the work yourself, get a good team, this class will be a breeze.

Sarcastic, condescending, and spends a disproportionate amount of class time expressing disappointment and frustration at students.  It would be better for everyone if the professor could maintain a level head and positive attitude, and put more effort into understanding why students are not enjoying the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Yildirim is the GOAT, and easily one of the most respected and beloved professors in Khoury. I've gone back to his lecture notes to prep for interviews, since he does an excellent job of explaining things effectively + concisely. If you're going to take DS3000, make sure you take it with Caglar!

Cared a lot about students and put a lot of effort to make sure even the people who could not make it to live lectures be able to understand the materials and participate with other students for group projects. Well organized lectures and materials, inclusive environment. I'd take all classes with him if he were teaching them! Highly recommend. <3

Without a doubt, one of the best classes I have taken! Prof is super friendly and loves Harry Potter! Course is extremely well structured and everything is laid on the table in the first class. Gives ample time to complete the big assignments and encourages class participation. He's very responsive to questions and makes sure the load is balanced.

The Professor did an AMAZING job curating online course content. He had prerecorded lectures with accompanying notebooks that could be watched at the students convenience. On alternating class days there were (very) short lab activities. He was also easily accessible, and sent out weekly correspondences reminding us of upcoming activities.

Brilliant, funny, and clear professor. You almost felt sad for him - he was enthusiastic beyond belief but the long class and intro level material meant noone was paying attention by the end. You can work on a cool ML project come the end of the class as well, and the class is usually taken just when you want projects for your resume. 100% take him

Quizzes are amazing, can help with grade. Homework assignments are straight forward, Can Be Done. No in-class tests and no final exam, but there is a final group project. Project is mainly stats- and presentation-based. There's also in-class assignments, so that gets you to class, but they're 10% in total. Great professor, major hogwarts fan.

Calgar's great! No exams. The few homeworks, in-class exercises, and quizzes were all doable. He offers extra credit, really listens to the class, and is understanding if assignments or quizzes are missed or need extensions. Would highly recommend.
Very passionate about what she teaches. Lectures are informative and content is engaging.

Prof. Pereira was a great professor! You can tell she cares deeply about the topic and is experienced within the field, yet she's still lighthearted, approachable, and easily accessible.There are two bigger group assignments (with the same people), so it really depends on who you get paired with, but she has a system for ranking effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super sweet! So much reading to do tho, you will most likely not even try to do it at the end of the semester. Weekly discussion posts but only like 20 mins, but you have to pay for the program. Two group projects with assigned groups, but relatively light is you have a good group. She's very easy to talk to and passionate about the topic.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects

reviews.comment: She's super nice and understanding, but lots of group projects with the same pre-assigned group, so cross your fingers you get a good one. Leanient grading, but there is a discussion board every week.

Prof Pereira was sweet, but I'm not sure I would take her again. I liked this course, and you were assigned a group to work throughout semester which I found valuable -- very project-based and several weekly readings.

A lot of pаpers to writе, but they're not tоo hаrd/lоng, and you have lots of time for them. The exams were hаrd at first, but I eventually understood the formаt and what to study for. Overall, a good professor, mеdium diffiсulty class, but hоnestly, еasy if you are gоod at memorization.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: really nice guy, everything you need is on the slides and the final study guide- personally im not good at math so i struggled- but the way the course is structured with a big part of your grade based on HW you should be fine unless you literally don't try at all.

I loved Dr. Eidson's class! He was super sweet and got to know all of us individually which I think is rare!! His lectures are thorough and there are plenty of easy assignments to raise your grade (you drop the 2 lowest assignments too). There was 2 extra credit assignments at the end. :)

Classes get boring because he sometimes rambles a little bit. Information is not very organized in the lecture slides, so there are a lot of gaps in information that you won't have for the tests. However, if the class doesn't perform to his expectations he gives extra credit. He was a nice teacher, just not many resources given to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Incredible lecturer, very smart and knows what he's talking about. Your grade is composed diversely -- online quizzes, three exams (plus final), and writing assignments. Offered extra credit for every exam bc averages were lower than he wanted. Tests are easy if you follow the study guide. Super nice, and accessible outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
4 quizzes, 4 HW assignments, 4 electronic HWs. Can be boring, but it's just the nature of the course and he does his best. He's a very good lecturer and cares ab ur success. 

I already took stats in high school, so I definitely was not the most checked in. If you haven't taken it, as long as you put in some dedication and effort you will be fine.

He is super nice and an engaging lecturer. His class is paper based to a ridiculous degree. He does not allow calculator shortcuts and we barely use excel or spss. We run ANOVAs by hand. I didn't even think that was possible. There are easier stats profs. This is old school stats you will never use again. If that's your thing, then he's great.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS the exams are like 50 ON PAPER mcqs and are impossible to study for, he rambles on in his lectures and his study guides are so broad compared to the specific questions he puts on the exams. theres writing assignments but they barely count so its all based on these awful exams. wanted to drop and withdraw the whole time
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Eidson was a pretty caring guy, and he did try his best to make cognition interesting for us. Tests were pretty much entirely based on lectures, and he released all topics the exam would cover before said exam. he also gave tons of extra credit to curve his test averages up to 85.

Prof Eidson is genuinely such a nice and relatable person. If you get the chance to take a class by him I def recommend. He gives so many extra credit options and the homework is just poll everywhere. I went to class just because how much I enjoyed him as a Professor. I will be taking him for my lab in cognition too!
AMAZING professor. So caring and makes assignments extremely clear to understand. Cognition Lab is a heavy class but he makes it super manageable. He's incredible!!

He is nice, makes the lectures pretty interesting in the way he explains things, the tests carry a lot of weight. Just the slides aren't enough to do very well on the tests, but if you attend lecture and pay attention you'll be fine.

Cognition is an objectively boring class. Eidson does his best to make it interesting, but I feel his lectures do not do a good enough job at preparing you for his tests. That being said, If you're a natural at memorization, you would have to put in 0 effort for an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I forgot there was a test one day and I got a 55 bc I winged it and I could not recover my grade (fully my fault lol)... professor is super sweet, fun, and extremely engaging. The only bad thing is how fast he goes through his notes.. get ready to self study but this class is way easier than the other sections.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Eidson is a great professor for foundations but, his tests were rated pretty heavily (21% each). There are three tests which make up the majority of the grade. There are VERY EASY writing assignments, poll everywhere questions, and extra credit for 5 points on each test. Super nice professor and very caring of students.

FYI, Eidson has switched from his previous online take-home test model to lockdown browser (in person with extra credit) so do with that what you will. Overall great guy and his lectures were engaging. He was available outside of class and is always happy to help if you reach out.
I'll give him some leeway because this was his first time teaching this course (I think), but I can't recommend. Everything felt rushed and underprepared.

great guy. conveyed the material in a way that's easy to understand and made stats fun. the only annoying part is the Excel/SPSS assignments, which were a pain and a bit useless in my opinion. however, the class is pretty easy and he drops your lowest quiz and homework grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Edison changed his testing from take-home, open notes to in-class w a lockdown browser (w/o adjusting the test). That being said, he gave extra credit to help offset the difference in grading. Honestly, he is a great teacher and truly cares about his students learning the material. You have to study for the tests, but that is why we are at NEU.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Edison is such an awesome professor! He really knows what he's talking about, and he cares so much too. He explains things so well, and his exams were exact copies of what he discusses in class. He tends to go for the full lecture time, but personally I almost never got bored. I would 100% recommend this class!
Very fair. What he said he would test is exactly what he tested. Never tried to trick you.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easy to pass class, but impossible to reach professor, assignments aren't graded for like years, if you like getting extra help from your professors, don't expect it here. You'll probably pass but won't understand everything you're doing. If you want an easy A, this class is for you, but if you actually want to understand the content, it's not.

Teacher was very nice and taught the class well. Exams weren't hard if you understood the homework, nothing tricky. Would sometimes have a hard time reaching him outside of class, but other than that the class was easy
Professor Eidson is very caring and an amazing professor. His lectures are good, tests are online and open-book. Homeworks are easy to do. This was a very chill class. 100% recommend!

Professor Eidson is super funny and easy to follow in class (he made stats fun!). Grade is based on 4 tests, 4 electronic data sets, 4 graded homeworks, and a final. The grading was super fair, and the lectures prepared me  for everything. Textbook is optional, but I was disappointed at how difficult he is to reach outside of class.

grades with exams and papers but are extremely easy, writings can be practically stories and informal just connecting the topic to your personal life. exams are also online and his study guide goes over everything covered honestly copy and paste the study guide into a doc and fill it out as the units go easy A.

Really nice guy and super easy class. 4 tests which are open-note and an open-note final as well. Extra credit was also offered on the final. However, he's very hard to reach and never responded to my emails, and took forever to grade exams and papers. All that aside, interesting class for an easy A.

The best prof at NEU! This is my 2nd class with him and I'd just like to say - if you can take any of his classes, do it! His teaching style really helped me navigate the process of writing research papers. Feedback is super specific, improving my 2nd drafts. No exams. Grp project was an experiment you come up with and was such a fun experience.

Professor Eidson is one of the best professors I've had so far. Cognition class was overall easy, textbook is not required, only assignments are 8 writing assignments (short) total and exams were open note. Agree with the other comment about class being like a podcast and sometimes I find it hard to pay attention. Could be just me though

TBH Prof. Eidson is one of the nicest guys. The issue with him is that he is impossible to contact. Never responded to multiple emails I sent through the semester. Quizzes exactly like the HW. Lecture isn't necessary because he reads right off the slides. Many examples in slides and an easy A if you submit everything. Easy class would recommend.

Love Dr. E! He's such a great guy. No readings!! Almost no outside work other than a few one-page writing assignments and studying for the exams. Exams were all open note. I thought cognition would be hard, but it was super chill and interesting. (His name is Robert on the official registration for anyone confused.)
