Cognition with Dr. E was a very interesting course! Lectures were very informative, and required little outside learning. Textbook is not required, but I found it helpful. Also tests are open note! There aren't many assignments, just two writing assignments per unit and only 5/8 are required. Overall, I'd recommend that anyone takes Cognition!

Dr. Eidson is a really good professor. His lectures are clear and easy to digest while also being informative. The work is super manageable. Cognition is a good class if you want to learn a lot but not do a ton of outside work.

Really entertaining classes; he always tries to keep his students engaged. Homework assignments were manageable and actually contribute to your overall grade. Exams were a little difficult, but if you take good notes in class, you will be fine. No need to teach yourself via textbook. He's a great professor and I wish more were like him!

I genuinely can not recommend Professor Eidson enough: he is easily the best professor I've had. He's caring, kind and more than willing to help students at all times. His lectures are interesting and convey a lot of information in a thoughtful way. He also offers many ways to boost your grade, but does not overload you with work. 100% recommend!

Dr Eidson is amazing. He is kind, calm, and very approachable, gives great feedback and makes class discussions really interesting and funny. In 4612 you watch a bunch of movies and analyse them (amazing for a film buff, good fun for others) can't rage about Dr E enough, a really great prof (plus fair grader) HIGHLY RECCOMEND

Prof Eidson was a fantastic professor; one of the best I've had at NEU. He's caring and approachable and really wants all of his students to succeed in his class while also giving us skills we can use in research beyond his class.

If you like podcasts, you'll like this professor. Prof. Eidson seems to have adopted the speaking mannerisms of someone delivering a podcast. The downside is that he often overexplains simple concepts, which draws out the class.

prof Eidson is an alright professor, he makes the material easy and honestly does care about his students, he's just the slowest grader I've ever had. he even has TA that grades quizzes for him, but we get those grades back like a month after we've actually taken the quiz. That's the only frustrating thing about him , otherwise he'd get 5*.

One homework packet due before each test. Tests are exactly like the homework. You have to do Data Sets on SPSS and Excel which are a pain, but he grades them easily. Nice teacher. Lectures have plenty of examples.
He's an interesting professor but class never starts on time and he takes forever to grades exams.

Talks in circles. A lecture that could take 20 minutes takes 2 hrs bc he drags everything out. Promises that assignments/grades/study guides would be posted by a certain day and then didn't post until 3+ days later. His exams are mostly multiple choice with one open response at the end. Very nit picky and takes of points off for irrelevant stuff.

Dr. Eidson was a great professor for Developmental Psych this semester. Only 4 exams, 6 writing assignments, and no final which was super nice. He is always willing to help you and is very accommodating, just be sure to let him know if you have to miss class for any reason and he'll be understanding.

He's a really nice guy who just wants his students to succeed. He was the most accommodating professor I've had in terms of open office hours or scheduling things around my schedule. He made sure we really understood statistics rather than just memorizing the computations, which I appreciated.

This Professor really knows his stuff. I thought this class would be difficult but his teachings are interesting. Class style is mostly lecture but you can learn a lot. He is fair and respects his students on an individual basis. I would highly recommend him to anyone needing to take Cognition for their PSYC major or minor.

Dr. Eidson is a young professor who cares about his students. He is intelligent, but not uptight. He tries hard to make class interesting and gives detailed feedback on lab report drafts. I didn't like how he always returned them a few days to a week later than he said he would though- he should either make realistic claims or not make them at all.
It's an okay class. I personally don't like lecture-heavy classes. Professor is nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: so so boring but not too hard

Prof Santelices is amazing! She makes her lectures engaging and always ties them to personal stories and research. When she assigns papers, she lets you send drafts beforehand for feedback and is very accessible in and out of class. She is kind, accommodating, and overall incredible. She will change the due dates to what works better for students.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Claudia was an AMAZING professor. I took her biology class and had absolutely 0 complaints. If you pay attention in class, do the notes, and just put in effort, you will do just fine. The quizzes and tests always align with what she teaches in class, which is always appreciated as a student.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She is a great teacher! Clearly explains the material, with interesting personal stories. Workload is quite manageable and requires little work outside of class.

Claudia was very sweet! You could tell she cared about her students and wanted them to succeed. Her lectures were very interesting and her grading and tests were very forgiving. Her unique perspective as an anthropologist with interests in public health shaped the scope of the class in an interesting way too!

Claudia is the best! She's so easy going and wants all of her students to do very well in her class. She's always willing to work with students about anything, so don't be afraid to ask for assignment extensions or extra help with something. DON'T buy the book!! You don't need it, she'll assign readings but then she goes over everything in class.

Claudia is a fantastic professor. She is clearly passionate and experienced in her subject and her stories tie into her lectures well. FLEXIBLE: if whole class agrees will move due dates. BREAKDOWN: 4x 1 page reflections, 4x (usually take home) quizzes, midterm, final, one long paper. manageable workload/INTERESTING lectures. PAY ATTENTION IN CLASS

Amazing professor. She is caring, approachable, and gives inspiring lectures that are based on her own experiences. She wants her students to succeed in class and will help you achieve an A if you put in the work.

Professor Santelices is so kind and is willing to work with students who need quiz retakes, missed class, and allows the class to pick midterm and quiz dates.  She gives great feedback and uses her own research as examples so lectures are engaging. I loved her class and didn't need to spend much time outside to get a good grade.

Really liked the class and most quizzes and reflection papers were take home. As long as you try, she will give you a good grade. There were 4 quizzes (3 take home), 4 reflection papers (3 take home), a midterm and a final. She is very kind and sweet, she has come a long way from her previous reviews because the class and canvas were organized.

The class is super easy and she is a very easy grader. Everyone in the class got 100 for every reflection paper and take-home quizzes. the Lecture slides are boring and mostly common sense, so you do not need to study hard to get a 100 on her exams. Check canvas! She changes deadlines and posts assignments without mentioning them in class.

Class material is pretty dry but prof did pretty well making it engaging with her own stories/examples. Very kind person who wants you to succeed. Assignments are chill and based off the material on slides

Prof. Santelices was so understandable and very kind when it came to class! She is easy to talk to, and for me personally, finding a professor I can converse with in Spanish is nice. She has been one of the many public health class professors I really enjoyed having. Amazing person, but the lecture is boring. Quizzes and reflection paper are easy.

One of the best professors I've had. I took Anthropology of Latin America as a Spanish minor and totally changed my worldview. She shares her personal experiences in Chile as well as fieldwork, and shares profound, often unheard perspectives on the "third world" countries, capitalism, indigenous resistance, etc. I was captivated every lecture.

I found her lectures super interesting and she wasn't a super harsh grader. You need to study the material on the slides really well. The tests weren't hard but definitely harder than what we learned in class... She's not super great at communicating and will definitely forget your name even after meeting her multiple times; not very genuine.

Prof Santelices is a caring and enlightening professor. Amazing stories and experience in Anthropology. A bit iffy with technology and expect all assignments to be pushed back and delayed in grading. However, she really makes an effort to know everyones name and hear everyone out. IMO amazing professor, love coming to lecture. PARTICIPATE!

The class was an actual waste of money, as in the fact that many classes were cancelled and recorded for online. The most helpful part of the class is the slides, and I did actually come away from the class with helpful info. But was it worth the money spent on the class, no. Look for another class or even better, another professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I loved her! She was one of the most caring professors at Northeastern! Through her personal stories, it is clear she is a professional and teaches for her love of learning. Her class was very lecture-heavy, but her material was always accessible, and students learned a lot. Grades depend mostly on exams, quizzes, and projects, not attendance.
Very kind, understanding woman. Barely knows how to use technology. I barely went to class and did well - if you stay on top of your coursework and STUDY THE CLASS SLIDES you'll do well.

Claudia is a really genuine, caring professor who has amazing life stories and lectures that brought me to tears on several occasions. It hurts to admit that she is all over the place with technology and deadlines, if she had a little more structure I'm sure she could be amazing.

Obviously cares a lot about the subject and has interesting stories, but lectures were hard to follow. Assigns way too much pointless reading. Assignments and exams were confusing, complicated, long, and frustrating. Not good with technology. Attendance was not taken but missing lectures does not help for later on. Class was all over the place.

Kind, passionate, good lectures. Everything else not so good. Horrible with technology. Extremely busy outside of class and didn't seem to have time to actually be a good professor. Her assignments can be confusing, especially the tests. Cancels class often, which is not a good thing. Could be good if she actually had time to focus on the class.

Dr. Santelices made lectures more interesting by adding her own experience of working in the field. You could really tell she was passionate about public health and how she has contributes and how we as students can further contribute. Assignments were based off of videos. As long as you pay attention to lectures and review, you'll pass. :)

Cullen is a good prof, there are 3 tests which you don't really even have to study for, if you just paid attention to class you can get an A on every test, There are a lot of readings for homework but they aren't that bad, theres also some anime episodes/movies and other movies for homework which was cool. She is a nice person overall!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really nice, grades fairly, interesting, and offers good feedback.

Prof Cullen is very knowledgeable and her passion is infectious. The course consists of a few papers and 3 exams, all of them were very straightforward. Be aware that you don't watch the films in class and therefore have to watch them on your own time.

Professor Cullen is so considerate and easy to talk to. She really knows her stuff and makes lectures very interesting and relevant to students' interests. Fairly easy course with short-answer HW questions and a few papers, but you definitely learn a lot.

Professor Cullen is truly a great professor! The class is quite easy but very informative and I enjoyed every assignment we did. The homework consisted of watching anime, reading manga, and other pieces related to Japanese culture. I can confidently say I was excited to come to class every time and really took away a lot in the end!

She's an amazing teacher. I loved the class (Japanese pop culture) so much, we focused on many issues and interpretations in japanese history and modern culture. There's two short papers (2 pages ish) and two question sets (10-questions each, but you have to be concise), and one final research paper (5-6 pages). Classes are interesting

Professor Cullen is great! For CLTR1700, Cullen really knows her stuff and makes class engaging. Sometimes lectures are tied to hw assignments, but hw is really not much work. Even the question sets and essays were kind of fun! If you want an interesting, low-work class, take CLTR1700 w Cullen! Highly recommend.

Prof Cullen is very nice and she really enjoys what she teaches. The lectures and homework are super interesting, I wasn't familiar with Japan's pop culture before taking the class. She made us watch anime and read manga which was a completely new experience for me. Very interesting class with engaging papers and no exams.

She's nice, class has some really cool discussions. Tests are fair (especially since they are take home). I spent a couple hours on each paper and did well. The open-ended research paper was cool, great class to learn more about modern japan//recent history.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I got 100% on this course, very easy if you put in the effort. I don't know how difficult it usually is, but during the pandemic where exams are take-home I got 100% on both midterm and finals, although I did spend quite a bit of time on it. If you spend around 5-6 hours for each assignment you are pretty much guaranteed an A.

Prof Cullen is respected, knowledgeable, and kind-hearted. Her lectures are clear and very interesting, and I love her use of videos and real-life examples. Some of Japanese culture is weird but she handles everything very well. Her class inspired me to take a dialogue to Japan, and I felt well informed thanks to her class.

Professor Cullen gives off a "mom" vibe. She's clearly very knowledgeable about the content and going to lecture is required, but she always discusses things in a kind and interesting way. This class was interesting (I even found myself enjoying the readings we had for homework), and the exams weren't bad either. Would definitely take her again.

Prof Cullen's class was so fun and unique. I learned so much about Japan that I didn't know before, and she was so knowledgable and clearly loves what she teaches. Her tests are fair and the final presentation and paper was so fun! Keep an open mind about some of the stranger parts of Japanese culture and you'll get a lot out of this class!
Such boring lectures
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The professor is a hard class. Not hard to get a good grade just a lot of work.
Prof Dunne is definitely a challenging class but gives many opportunities to succeed. If you put in the effort, you get an A.

Professor Dunne might be knowledgeable about data visualization, but when it comes to teaching an academic class he simply seems like he doesn't know what he is doing. His lectures are just talking about random topics as he reads from slides he made years ago. Assignments have nothing to do with the lectures and you will not receive any help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Worst professor, I have taken in my life! Claims he teaches a design class but all of his hw and projects are technical. Takes multiple days to respond to emails. When you need help on the hw, well good luck with it because you will find none as the professor will only point out console messages and the TAS for the class are simply clueless.

Prof is clearly passionate  Imagine putting hundreds of hours into a disorganized class and going 2 1/2 months without having any idea about what your grade is, risking your ability to graduate or maintain a scholarship etc. or even learning and using that knowledge in the next assignments. TAs are bad,  don't take this unless you're nasty at JS.

If you can take this class with another prof, pls do. I can't stress this enough. There are no clear grading standards and he takes points off as he pleases. It's so frustrating. The TA's are horrible too. I have had such a terrible experience in this class

Terrible professor! Doesn't teach anything remotely related to the HW in lectures. Very harsh and completely unreasonable grader takes off many points for  ridiculos things such as not including name or not tagging pages on GradeScope. Class is so disorganized and the professor seems clueless about how to run an academic class. Do not take!

Grades take 2+ months to be released and homework assignments are useless. Lectures are boring and not related to the homework assignments. The professor holds everyone to a very high level while making the TAs teach class sometimes and not grading anything. However, this class ended up being very easy because you don't end up learning anything.

Professor Dunne is one of the better professors I have had at Northeastern. 
He isn't the most socially savvy, and his lecture structure could use some work. 
But he is a very genuine and caring person who is passionate about the topic of Data Vis. He is very helpful in office hours, and just wants his students to succeed if they put the effort in.
Worst professor ever. Biggest mistake I made is taking this course. Non-sense and waste of my tuition

Waste of my tuition. Don't know what I've learned in this "writing class". All kinds of inexplicable demands for HWs and Projects. Always get 0. Only spend one class teaching how to write code, assuming everyone is skilled in programming in JS, Python. Don't take the course with this daydreamer with obsessive-compulsive disorder.

Worst professor I've ever seen. Two quizzes with 6 multiple choices questions worth 10 points of the total grade. Don't know what he's doing in lecture, showing ridiculous pictures in slides. I taught myself JavaScript-d3, HTML, CSS, Python, Tableau the whole semester. Making last-minute grade change without reason. No recording during the pandemic

The worst professor I've ever taken. The syllabus is a mess. The only thing I got out of the class was all the bad habits I acquired while teaching myself javascript (d3), html, and CSS at the same time. Will give 0 on assignments. Mandatory attendance/participation during COVID, no recordings. makes last-minute grade changes. Very slow email.
shes the best
Pretty easy class make sure to do the homework on time, everything is through canvas and cengage, caring professor, attendance not necessarily mandatory, one duo project that isn't too difficult
A very nice lady, teaches effectively & makes learning fun. Material is relatively easy. Take her class!

Professor Dupree is very nice and caring. Her lectures are easy to understand and she makes sure students are really thinking about the material. However, her voice can be a little too good at putting you to sleep. Her lectures are all recorded, so most people skip often, and her test are all take-home and open note. TAKE HER CLASS.
Prof Dupree is super straight-forward with all of her assignments and exams which makes it easy to do well in this class. Very understanding professor and overall a chill class!

Guys at first I was like I'm so scared of economics so I went to every class, but one day the class was freezing and I kept hearing noises so I was like maybe I will stop going a little and I did some work on my own w/ A+. She is very generous with what she gives; open note at home exams, drops lowest grades, etc. Take her she is kind
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Exceptionally easy but extremely boring. Lectures are just her reading slides and telling tangential stories with no point, which sucks because she's clearly passionate/knowledgeable. Still recommend though. 1 test is dropped (including final if you don't wanna take it). 3 HWs are also dropped. Textbook is expensive and mandatory for assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I never went to class, attendance wasn't mandatory. She is a very nice person. HW is long but online and so are tests. very easy class take her for sure

Professor Dupree is knowledgeable and excited to teach. Class was overall easy ... tests were all online and considered "open note, open resource, can do it with friends, etc" (her words). Very few people attended lectures, and they were not very engaging, so I felt I didn't learn as much as I wished. Wish she got to know the few who came to class!

Professor Dupree was great. She clearly cares about economics and comes to class prepared and interested. Class was okay, it was hard to learn deeply in such a large lecture hall, and many times I felt myself zoning out. Overall, it was great, and Professor Dupree did a great job teaching a large lecture.

Lectures were extremely dry, she just regurgitated the slide decks provided by the textbook. I got a good grade, but all tests were assigned through Canvas so basically everyone cheated and no one went to lecture. Nice professor and easy class but you don't really learn anything

Very kind professor and great course. Yes, there are quite a few homework assignments and 5 exams, but nothing too hard. Content is very understandable and class is quite easy. Would definitely recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was great. So easy, you literally just have to complete the weekly HW assignments which are all online and ALL of the tests are take home (including midterms and the final). Her lectures are good too you just have to show up and want to learn. I highly recommend taking!
easy class, just do homework and ur chilling, all tests are take home open book but if you need help you can get it very easily, occasional assignments, but hella easy.

Jill is great. The class is easy, don't worry at all. She responds to emails quickly and is very caring, willing to work with you. Doesn't take off points for late assignments. Tests are open-note, asynchronous. I would take this class again. It was a relief to have an easier class like this one.

Lectures were a bit all over the place. Textbook reading is unavoidable as there are quizzes on the reading every class (they're easy though as long as you did the reading). When writing the essays, it's best to attend office hours to make sure you're going in the right direction. Class was really interesting at times but overall a little boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Grading seemed random at times - on multiple occasions, it was unclear why I was getting points taken off. She would get somewhat defensive when asked about this. Lectures can be hard to pay attention to as they directly reflect reading we are told to do before class; hearing everything twice was boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: i loved prof dupree's class! the whole semester for american econ history was based on 1 book, and we covered 2 chapters a week. discussions are student-led and very engaging! short reading quizzes every class made by students, and total of 4 papers throughout the sem (1 paper per book section). very understanding and lenient grader!
She is nice professor, but her classes don't really help with tests. She usually grades assignments at long intervals, which makes me confused about grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Not the greatest lecturer, she reads of the slides which are not very thorough. There are many unit tests, they are not difficult if you read the textbook and do the homework. You do need to self-teach yourself but the class isn't very difficult.

Second time taking her, have taken Conflict Ec 1916 and now American Econ History 3470. In 3470, we had 3 papers about one long book (800pgs), Ages of American Capitalism by Levy. Late grader. Class is very participation based; you teach a few chapters of the book for a week as a group. Fair grade. She loves what she teaches.

Easy class overall if you aren't interested in economics. Take it asynchronously if you want less work. There is a big group project which takes a lot of time each week, so make sure you have a good group and you get to choose your group in the beginning of the semester.

I don't know why this professor got such bad reviews, she's entertaining, kind, caring, responsive, and easy grader. There are reading, graded outlines and 3-4 test units but the concepts is so easy to understand it's really not stressful at all. I don't think the in person lectures add anything to the textbook but she gives detailed explanations.

So far, definitely the most useless class and professor I've had at Northeastern. Professor sets her things up for 10-15 mins each day while we sit in complete silence and do nothing. Her teaching is poor, takes very long to explain concepts, and doesn't really do any in-class examples to prepare for the test. Would not recommend
Took Conflict Econ 1916. Hard material, not recommended as first econ course. Gave make up exams. IMO lectures too slow for content. Great TA Roy.

As long as you do the homework and readings, you'll be fine. Late homework is accepted with a 10% penalty 3 days after it was due, you'll have 5 tests and the lowest is dropped, and your lowest two lowest homework grades. All in all I think she's pretty fair, not that much interaction bc it's online, but she emails back pretty quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The entire class is asynchronous and relies only on the textbook. You're graded on tests, homework and a group project. It's heavy reading and homework but the project isn't that difficult. She explains how to do the proper steps and as long as you're coordinated with your group you should be fine. Only take this if you're okay with reading online.

Took Professor Dupree asynchronously during Summer II and did not enjoy it in the slightest. She doesn't lecture, just assigns textbook chapters and homework. Overall pretty easy to do well if you put in the work, but not a fun course.
Easy class fully virtual easy A as long as you do the work
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class is extremely easy to pass and all exams are easy. She drops your two lowest homeworks and your lowest test score. There is a multi-part group project but she gives clear instructions and walks you through it step by step. I did however take this class online and not sure how in person class is.

Took this class online and asynchronously, so barely interacted with the professor at all. Everything is taught through textbook readings and homeworks, no lectures. Basically a self-taught course but easy to get an A if you put in the work. One big group project, easy grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: This is a terrible class to take online. I've never met the professor or directly interacted with her, and I'm pretty sure it's the same for everyone else in the class. There is a ridiculous amount of reading every week, too many exams, and the group project is such a pain.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Her class is pretty okay if you read the textbook, but I barely learned anything. Literally no interaction with the student.. way too much homework sometimes...
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Here are something I wish I knew before taking Dupree's ECON1115 class -it's completely asynchronous (besides going to office hours ONCE, I've never met her) -it's all taught through online textbooks (if you don't read you're done) -be ready to schedule yourself. 1 hr of recitation is all you get. I made a time table so that I'd get stuff done
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Very nice. I'm not sure what people are on about with her NUstart/online class being difficult. The readings and homeworks each week can be done properly in 3-4 hours just by reading through and comprehending the material before doing the homework problems. Tests are shortish and are no problem if you study a bit - or Google the answers.

ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE. How are these teachers so disconnected from there students? Ridiculous amounts of work for an intro econ class. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS ONLINE. The group project is an ABSOLUTE NIGHTMARE and this lazy piece of crap literally doesn't do any work. She makes US grade each other's work. Such a waste of time and money

For an intro online class during a pandemic, it's WAY too much work and a confusing format. 2 homeworks per chapter (20 chapters), discussions, 5 tests, 5 parts to a group project, and peer reviews. Barely any teaching done by prof. I'm so confused where to go for what assignment. This prof was not meant for online teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: The format is absolutely terrible. Apart from one group project the whole class is conducted through Cengage Mindtap, a textbook software. All homework assignments and tests are conducted through this and most of the actual teaching as well. The prof is available outside of class but not super helpful. Overall a waste of time and money.

Like all other reviews, Professor Dupree is highly relaxed in terms of attendance. You cannot show up all semester and still receive an A regardless. There is a presentation at the end of the semester which you will have to go. I gave her a 4/5 because you will not learn in class, all exams and HW are online and in Chegg. The textbook is $100!

Definitely the easiest macro theory class as it is not quantitative heavy at all. Make friends with the smartest kids in the class because she lets you do homeworks and exams together (Exams are take home). Also get a tutor for the exams. Not sure how much I ended up learning through this method but she is a very nice professor and is very caring.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dupree is a very kind person and professor. She is very accommodating and understanding. The tests and quizzes are online, which makes doing well in the class much easier. One nice thing is that she does actually teach, for those who want to learn. She goes over the presentations and clearly understands and conveys the material. Great professor!

First level of difficulty is called "show up and you'll pass". In this class you don't even have to show up to pass. Test and quizzes are taken online, you can find all the answers and solutions in the internet. Final project she will just take a look at the poster and discuss it with you. If you have a heavy semester - take it, easy A.

Professor Dupree is a great choice if you're looking for a laid back class. Attendance wasn't mandatory, and all the tests and homeworks were online; this makes it very easy to get a good grade. However, with this system, you'll only learn anything if you put the effort in, which is pretty much optional. Overall, she is very nice and accommodating.

Prof. Dupree is great! She's super funny and amusing tbh. She really knows what she's talking about, but might overlook the fact that the students do not so be sure to ask questions. All assignmentsincluding midterm and finalsare online so it's fairly easy to get a good grade. Just read the power points, show up to class, and do your work.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I would not recommend this professor for Risk Management. The expectations for assignments and exams were often unclear, which made it difficult to understand what was required to succeed in the course. I think the course could be improved with clearer instructions, better organization, and more consistent communication about expectations.
She's inprofessional and awful try to avoid her class if you can.

Kristen cares about students and makes herself available outside of class. She also uses examples from work and real life. She is not the easiest grader but she is fair and she makes her expectations very clear in class. Students who dislike her are pissed that they can not cruise through class with minimal effort and everything handed to them.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: To much work need to be done in six weeks, don't choose her. She is f terrible and mean, I can't learn anything in her class and everything i do is suffering

Don't be fooled by other reviews—this professor is a nightmare. She's excessively strict, mean, and does whatever she wants, regardless of how impractical it is. Classes are never finished on time and are often kept after class.This is all because she likes to chat and quickly get into the topic of the course without being serious.
A very good professor who will answer students' questions very patiently. Her final exams are given in class, but she will review the exams in week 5.

All assignments are group assignments. Group members were randomly assigned. Easy grader. Professor is friendly and replies to students' texts very fast. There is an in-person final exam before the end of the class, and every week, she will talk about the exam scope. The cell phone is not allowed to use during class. No breaks for her class.
Do not recommend. Lecture heavy, every other word is "umm". Lectures are taken straight from the textbook. Did not learn much.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had taken Risk Management with her. She is exceptionally good with her lectures. All assignments are group assignment and she takes open book final exam. Her assignments really help to understand the subject in a more practical way.
The class was exceptional. She really inspires you and makes you understand everything, she takes the time to make sure each student understands her class. I will definitely take this class again

Kristen is a great professor. She inspires me as a successful woman and a disciplined professor. She gives her best to make students involve in the projects. I am more excited to be in her class which is so far the best class in my grad life.

Take orgo with literally any other professor!! Her lectures are lengthy and confusing, and she doesn't explain concepts well. Her exams are very difficult and require you to have an extremely good understanding of orgo to apply concepts. She rarely does guided practice with the class and you're expected to know how to do all of the homework!!

Honestly one of the worst graders possible, tests are unfairly graded, lack of support and understanding, the practice exams are nothing like the real exams so no point in doing those. Her lectures are heavy and don't teach you much at all. I recommend taking another professor.

Danahy is a very nice person, but her tests are impossible. Studied for hours on end, yet still felt completely unprepared for each test. The topics covered between the last test and the final are rushed and poorly covered, so there was no shot the "final redemption" policy would apply. Love Pensive Cat, but not this class.

As a ChemE who had Danahy for Orgo 1, this was my favorite class of the semester. Puzzle sets were short and sweet. Office hours make the semester enjoyable. I always go into her exams thinking they're going to be harder than they are. I'm coming out of the course feeling like I know how to do useful things with Orgo. Her humor is icing on the cake

I am reading all of these bad reviews and truly wondering if I was the same class as y'all…Like yes her exams are hard but the comments about there being no support are just false. She has 3 hours of office hours per week, responds to emails in like 10 minutes, and has 3 excellent TAs. She is also extremely kind and nonjudgmental.

Danahy is an amazing teacher who really cares about her students. This course is of course challenging, but she sets us up for success. Her exams are all free response, so if you prefer MC,  take it with another teacher. I really enjoyed this class, and it has made me appreciate chemistry more! My D in her class is a reflection of me not her!

Super nice and caring; very accessible and approachable outside of class for extra help. Lectures are given via her iPad, and her notes and weekly homework are very useful to learn from. If free-response exams and "content mastery" aren't your thing, take the other professors with MC tests.

Take someone else if you can. I loved orgo 1 and got an A, but did terrible in this class. Exams are extremely hard and despite studying nonstop, I felt hopeless. The exams have content that we never covered. Some puzzle sets were easy, and some were very hard. She also doesn't curve, give extra credit, etc. She's nice, but the class is so so hard.

Honestly liked her for orgo 1 and did well. However, this class was so bad for orgo 2. Insanely hard exams and randomly pulls out mechanisms no one has ever seen. I studied like 30 hours a week and still felt blindsided on her exams. Take Matthews instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DON'T TAKE HER. Her exams are impossible. She says, "I'm not trying to trick you." SHE IS. I spent so many hours a day studying, but nothing prepares you for the exams. Grade is basically just 4 exams, including the cumulative final. No practice is similar to exam questions because she pulls questions out of yk what. Take Staton or Mathews

This class (and her) made me cry on multiple occasions. I've studied hours on end, practically dedicated my life to this class, and never was it enough. her exams are nothing like the ones she gives us from ‘22, they are so so much more difficult. do your self a favor and please do not take her, this class is actually the most crazy thing ever…

Her exams do not reflect the material learned. I studied 15 hours straight every single day and understood all practice problems and practice exams. Yet, every exam consists of questions that are a mix of things and ultimately something we didn't learn. Her exams ARE NOT FAIR. Orgo is not easy, but she makes it harder. She gives no support. NOTHING

Do yourself a GPA favor and mental favor (emphasis on mental) and do not take her. I thought I could give her the benefit of the doubt, but the exams are not AT ALL similar to what we learn. To put it in perspective, someone fully balled their eyes out LOUD in the middle of the final. Multiple people left in tears with empty pages. Don't take her.

if you didn't do well in ochem1, do not take ochem2 with danahy. the exams and homeworks are insanely difficult. people told me ochem1 was harder than 2 but this class was so much harder. this class was miserable. take any other prof.

Dr. Danahey is an amazing professor! I took her class for both Orgo 1 and 2 and enjoyed both classes. Her lectures work best for visual learners because she essentially takes notes with you. She is extremely kind and wants her students to succeed. Responds to emails fast. GO TO OFFICE HOURS!!!

I liked her so much better for Orgo 1. Her lectures got very hard to follow and we never got to do guided practice in class. I wanted to like her but it was so hard to do well in this class, she is incredibly stingy with partial credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Danahy is absolutely wonderful. Incredibly kind and truly cares about her students. Her syllabus can make the class seem daunting, but if you put in effort she will genuinely love you and bend over backwards to help you. Read the textbook before lecture, come with questions, go to office hours, and you'll be fine!

She was a very hard grader and all tests were very difficult. She teaches in lecture very well but that is it. She provides no support, gives no extra credit and doesn't curve. Be prepared to study for your life for this class.

In all honesty she is a very sweet professor but she is extremely difficult. Orgo is a hard class no matter who you have, but she doesn't help. No curves, extra credit, or test corrections. Her exams are extremely difficult much more so then previous years and practice problems. I shed blood, sweat and tears and still ended with a C.

Professor Danahy is awesome. The textbook reading are not super important if you understand the lectures. The puzzle sets are really good practice for exams. She is so helpful and kind during office hours. Her lecturing style is keeps you engaged during class and she gives time for practice problems during which she walks around and offers help.

I dedicated my life to studying for this course. Did every problem in the textbook; they helped, but only to a certain extent. Some exam questions aren't like any practice. BIGGEST ADVICE: GO TO OFFICE HOURS and ask questions, no matter how "stupid", as long as it helps you. An A starts at 91.5(she rounds), + final redemption grade. Super nice lady
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Lectures are great- she writes notes with you. Everything is covered in class, but it's your responsibility to study. Exams are hard, so it's important u REALLY understand the material. Super approachable & nice, quick to respond to emails & addresses all ur concerns. Difficult course but she gives u more resources to study with than other profs.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She is very kind and so knowledgeable in chemistry. However, the textbook problems and practice do not compare to the difficulty of exams, which make up mostly all of your grade. She is a very tough grader. If you take orgo with her you will learn a lot, but do know that it is very tough

Such a kind professor. But to be honest, her test are NOTHING like the assignments and previous exams as she claims, PREVIOUS ONES R 10x EASIER! She puts EVERYTHING reviewed in class on exam, class avg is usually in the 70s or low 80s. Classes are so hard to sit through and you get exhausted and bored by the end. Ur in for a treat, a GPA bust :(

Danahy is a great lecturer and qualified, but her tests are extremely difficult and the final was worse. She recommends you do the textbook problems + her homework which may seem helpful but come no where near the difficulty of the exams. She's super sweet but her class is very difficult to pass with a good grade :( If you are pre-med save your GPA

There are many mixed reviews about her, but to be completely honest, she is an amazing professor. Chemistry has never been a strong subject for me, but her lectures and office hours show that she is such a great teacher and genuinely wants you to do well. During finals week she provides so many study guides and resources and her TAs are amazing.

Professor Danahy is very sweet and kind. I really enjoyed having her as a professor and would say that she is really great at teaching the material! My only recommendation is that you also need to practice a lot outside of lectures to really grasp the concepts which will be on the tests. I recommend attending office hours when you can.

Great professor and overall really good lectures. Exams get progressively harder (including the cumulative final). Textbook problems are OK for grasping a basic understanding of topics, but come nowhere close to the level of difficulty on exams. Tough grader and not very generous with partial credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Danahy is very kind and a good professor with engaging lectures, however this class is very difficult. I found the cumulative final was very tough and way harder than the practice. In addition to puzzle sets and poll everywhere there are 3 exams then the final for the overall grade. I definitely learned a lot, though.

The reviews lied...yes Danahy is a good lecturer but her exams are very difficult making it hard to pass with a good grade. Her TA's aren't helpful because they don't understand her assignments and always cancel. The textbook does not help since her questions on exams are totally different than textbook practice problems.

DO NOT TAKE THIS WACK CLASS HER EXAMS ARE SO HARD IT'S INSANE AND HER FINAL WAS A MILLION X WORSE THAN ANYONE COULD EVER IMAGINE. Now you may think I'm dumb or smth but no I did good 2/3 exams but the last killed grade and the final was EXTREMELY hard. No curves, corrections, or extra credit. Good luck if u take her :(

Genuinely might be the best professor I've ever had. I've taken organic chemistry before and love it so that was definitely an advantage but for someone who is brand new and/or scared of orgo, she is AMAZING! You definitely need to read the textbook and do ALL the extra problems. I used a lot of notecards and she was always super easy to email!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Organic chem is hard and while Danahy teaches well in class her exams are EXTREMELY difficult and she is a tough grader. Her TA's aren't helpful bc they dont even understand her weekly puzzle set assignments. I would not recommend or take her again because if you mess up one exam you're done for grade wise.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She was a very kind and approachable woman. If conceptualizing content is not easy for you, don't take her. The tests were extremely hard. We were not given much material to study from. We had 2-3 hw to study from for that exam and maybe some textbook problems that did not have much to do with what was going to be on the exam.
Sweetest lady ever.  Sometimes lectures can get confusing but going to extra help definitely helps.  She cares and wants you to do well.

I loved Prof Danahy for Orgo 1! Her lecture style makes it very easy to follow along. Utilizing her and TA's office hours was essential, though. I wish she provided more difficult practice problems/materials as her tests were pretty difficult. She is great with partial credit, though, and truly cares about your success in the class!

I absolutely love Professor Danahy. She is so amazing at what she does. Her lectures are incredible and make ochem seem easy. Her homework is based on participation, and is so helpful for studying because her tests are based on similar questions. Answers emails quickly, and has lots of office hours. Take her if u want to fall in love with chemistry
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This is probably, if not the best, organic chemistry teacher! I had her for both organic chemistry 1 and 2, and I got an A in both classes! The most important things are pay attention during class, ask her questions, and review content. You really need a deep understanding of the material to do well. She really cares about her students doing well!

If you honestly want to be prepared for Orgo 2 and fully comprehend Orgo 1 you should definitely take this class with Jude Matthews. Jude may come off as aggressive but she will honestly give you all the material you will need to succeed unlike Danahy. Danahey doesn't teach to learn but to pass with low grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Danahy was one of the best professors that I have had at NU! She did a very good job of explaining the topics in a way that students could easily understand. She provided many examples in class and was very willing to answer questions and re-explain topics. Overall, she is a very kind-hearted professor who wants to see you succeed!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I have taken Danahy for Orgo 1 and 2 and she has been the best prof I could ask for! Exams were open response and personally, that was difficult, but she gives partial credit. Find someone to study with who genuinely wants to help you (there's some stingy pre-health kids), but also utilize office hours and tutoring. Textbook also kind of helped.

As much as Matthews is hyped up, Danahy is a great option for orgo. She emails back super quick if you ask a question and I found her office hours very helpful. The tests are hard, especially the final, but if you put in the work you can absolutely get an A. Participation is taken via Poll Everywhere and you need to complete 90% of questions.

Danahy is a solid option for orgo. She's super sweet and her lectures are very easy to follow. As long as you do the recommended reading and the practice problems you'll get a decent grade. There are three exams and they are graded pretty harshly, it's all free response and she doesn't give a lot of partial credit. Definitely learned a lot though.

My favorite professor I've ever had at NEU. She is SO caring, helpful, and intelligent! I HIGHLY recommend doing the readings before classes, textbook questions after class, rewriting notes, and going to office hours once a week. Lectures are fast paced, but her tests are very predictable and manageable if you stay on top of things. Take her class!

Such a genuinely nice person. I've never had professor be so caring about her students. She wants people to do well and is interested in you as a person. That being said, her class is not easy and her final is hard if you are not the best at orgo. She is probably the smartest professor but very down to earth.
Really good at explaining things, the material on the tests are exactly like in class.

It was a privilege to have Danahy for Orgo 1 - while a bit shy, she is incredibly coherent and intelligent, and more importantly, good at teaching. Her mandatory lectures can be fast-paced at times, though she explains concepts coherently and they MUST be complemented by the textbook and her weekly puzzle sets. 

Also, DO NOT bomb the first exam.
best teacher ive ever had. really wants students do to well

explains things well
- skimming the reading before class and doing the recommended textbook problems within 24 hours of the lecture makes the A
- good review materials and review sessions
- the puzzle sets are basically practice tests, if you study them you will know everything you need to know
- a lot of helpful office hours

TAKE DANAHY. I was so scared of orgo coming into this class but she made the concepts so much easier to understand. Her tests are difficult, but she doesn't throw you any curve balls. Go to office hours! She's definitely a professor you want to form a relationship with.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The best professor I have at at Northeastern, she is extremely clear about what she expects you to know and she's great at explaining concepts. Tests are open ended; she is very generous with partial credit, and the content of the test is predictable. If you really study an A is within reach.

Her lectures are very important, orgo 1 is just pretty overwhelming to begin with but she made it better for me. It's just not an easy A. It depends if u like lecture heavy and open-ended exams or not

Professor Danahy is one of my favorite professors that I have taken here at Northeastern. She truly cares about how students do in her class and also gives lots of partial credit. She makes the material extremely digestible which is nice for Orgo.

I enjoyed orgo 1 but I feel like her open response tests were just overwhelming for no reason. She gives partial credit but honestly your enjoyment of the class depends on if you enjoy her style of teaching tbh.

Danahy is a very generous grader but her exams are open response and difficult. For Orgo 1 she was fine, but I wish I took Jude for Orgo 2 bc Danahy's overcomplicated and difficult exams felt like we were being set up to get low grades. She is super kind and office hours are sort of helpful. Get a tutor and study immediately after a lecture.

Organic chem is a hard class no matter the professor. Danahy does a decent job explaining the topics. Read the textbook and beware that topics build on each other. Homework is hard but for completion. Exams are very hard but you get to understand her style. Gives generous partial credit. Helpful during office hours. I am taking her again!

Same guy as the previous post. If you want to actually do well, TAKE HER, NOT JUDE. Jude requires tons of work outside of lecture, and her only redeeming quality is multiple choice tests (you can't get partial credit!!) Take Danahy, thank me later.

AWESOME. Prof. Danahy is everything you could ask for for a challenging course like orgo. Her handwritten lecture style is far better than a slideshow style, and she gives examples for every topic presented. I believe that she is the best possible orgo professor, and an A in the class is very manageable if sufficient preparation is done.

Attendance is mandatory. Teacher is very nice but has a tendency to overcomplicate topics when explaining. Exams are open response and she does not curve or give extra credit. Even peer tutors would be confused on the homework she would give. Nice person, did not enjoy the class at all. Had to learn most of the content myself outside of class

If you're someone who benefits from handwriting notes, having a relationship with your profs, and values a smaller class size Danahy is your professor. HW is in the form of puzzle sets which are challenging but so helpful for exams, office hours are accessible, and lectures are actually understandable. Orgo isn't easy but she makes it manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She's a nice professor, however her teaching style is definitely different. Her exams focus on the process of why something is the way it is more than the products, i.e explain why this carbonyl is more nucleophilic, or why is this compound have a certain composition? Take a different prof with multiple choice exams and not a cumulative final......

This woman is so slept on. She is wonderful! If you put in the work and aks questions you will get it. She is so willing to help and responds to questions and emails so very fast. She is always willing to help and is the cutest human I know! I love her soo much! TAKE HER! Better than Jude!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Love the way she explains chemistry! Lectures are clear and easy to understand. Tests are fair and class is manageable.

Prof. Danahy teaches Orgo 2 really well. The tests are open-ended, not multiple choice, which makes studying for them trickier since you need to know the content, but there's lots of partial credit. Going to class and taking good notes is important to learn the material, the puzzle sets also give a good insight into what test questions are like.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: 3 unit exams and 1 final exam, puzzle sets are very hard so I recommend the peer tutors for help. She is always available outside of class. Orgo is very difficult but she makes it bearable. Exams are mostly open ended but she gives a ton of partial credit. Definitely take her if you have the option.

Kelley is the sweetest prof ever!! She cares so much about all her students and is very passionate about what she teaches. The exams, though open-ended, were a lot easier than the homework assignments and she's a very lenient grader (she will defs give you points wherever she can)

She is thorough with her lectures and her exams are somewhat difficult but very fair. There are some open ended questions however you can get partial credit for these. There are a few homework assignments graded on completion which boost your grade a little. She also takes time to answer every question during class and in office hours.

I loved Professor Danahy. She's passionate about chemistry. She writes down examples and notes as she talks about them and focuses on concepts that may not otherwise be focused by other teachers. The homework she gives is hard, but is graded for effort/completion. She prepares you well for the free response exams - nothing comes as a shock.

Danahy was one of the few professors that I've had where she seemed like she wanted us to learn something. I found that she explained topics clearly and was happy to answer any question that we had, either during lecture, office hours, or over email. Exams are exactly what she teaches, and homework really prepares you for exam material.

Professor Danahy is one of the best professors that I have had. She is always available and super helpful in her office hours. The exams are open response, which was difficult, but she gave fair partial credit. She returns emails and grades very quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is such a nice professor! I didn't prefer her teaching style - several concepts were made very confusing and overcomplicated. I had gone to her office hours multiple times and I didn't feel like she would help explain problems and often left confused. Her homework assignments were also very tricky that it confused my peer tutors at times.
She is passionate about chem! Personally, I did not enjoy her style of teaching. I came to her when I was struggling and she did not try to help. Her example problems even confused the peer tutors.
Homework was difficult but prepared you for the free response exams. Questions were a bit confusing at times. Very accesible outside of class and really cares
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great prof that loves chemistry. She has some great lectures that you need to answer a poll to get credit for. Very information dense, but that's how organic chemistry is, and she drills the ideas into your head. She really wants her students to understand everything instead of memorizing. Very tough hw but it teaches you how she wants you to think

I had Danahy for orgo 1, great lecturer and good at explaining. She always responded to emails quickly. However the puzzle sets are very hard and prepare to study beyond the lecture material. She gives a lot of partial credit on tests. Overall I recommend her but her class is a little difficult, I thought I did a lot better than the grade I got.

I had Prof Danahy for CHEM 2311 but she was an absolutely amazing professor. Takes her time explaining tough concepts in class, is truly caring about her students, and is always accessible out of class for office hours and fit you in outside of them as well. Exams are open response but A LOT of partial credit. HW is great bc it preps for the exam.

Absolutely love her! She is always available outside of class and over email. Tests can be intimidating due to free response, but if you understand the homework and lectures you will be fine. She clearly cares for her students and hints at what may be on the test. Also gives lots of partial credit on tests.

If you are a very technical person, then this class might be good, but it's not for everyone. 

He makes it very difficult to understand the concepts if you don't have prior understanding. Repeats lectures every week. Started off the semester by warning us that "we will probably fail." Thinks its ok to grade work like 3.4859 USING AI TO GRADE!
hes actually the chillest mf so funny and is such a lil caring man

Martin a G. the guy offers inspirational lectures I mean are we da??? If you fail this class u stupi alls you gotta do is raise yo hand ery now and then he even clap back wit a lil giggle its ma favorite class. We eve get a wacky wednesday last class he brought in his action figure collection,I tried on his light up batman mask. Luh this guy fr

He thinks it's okay to give a student a 89.9876 on an exam and not round. He gave one student in the class a 6.99 for participation. He is on a high horse, and you don't learn a thing, saying you'll probably fail.

I wish I read RMP earlier. The entire class is just memorizing jargon. I'm convinced that he designed the introductory MIS course to steer students away from the department. Good for him, because I'm never taking such a course again, especially when he's teaching. Avoid at all costs.

Just back to remind everyone that he designed MISM 2301. Yes, all the 10 odd sections who take this class every semester learn the same outdated jargon filled stuff because he does not care to change anything about it. He needs to go.
Honestly, he shouldnt be that bad for you, if you actually put in some work.

I hope he stops teaching. Spending a third of each class “revising” the previous class while skipping slides in the current class is ridiculous. Easy material made unnecessarily hard. Pointless jargon, outdated class materials… I could go on. Avoid this class at all costs.

I have no words. I have learned nothing from this course, and I got an A with 95% attendance. I found out today that his professor is responsible for the course being designed in this manner. Hundreds of students learn nothing in this class every semester due to him. I hope NEU changes something because this should not fly with the amount we pay.

Pros: Excel, Figma, group work doesn't trash your grade, professor Dias is lively and has (dry) humor, theme Thursdays are fun, provides feedback even for hw, literally tells you "this will be good for your notes..."
Cons: 1> hw due per week, required textbook ($$), some topics felt less necessary than others, lectures can get boring, grading
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Frustrating course. Slides have little to no text. Prof spends 20 minutes talking about the previous lecture which can easily be avoided. Then he skims through the current lecture while going off on tangents at every turn. Case studies are graded pretty randomly. Exams are weird, and require you to spew out the same jargon he said in class.  Avoid!

I got a good grade in this class, but let me make this very clear - every lecture is boring. This professor cannot talk for 5 minutes without going on tangents, and it feels intentional because there is nothing worth talking. As others have mentioned, you may do well in the class depending on your notes, but you are guaranteed to learn nothing.
Enjoyed having Professor Dias this semester. The tests were online, open-book.  Assignments and tests weren't too difficult as they were all based on how well you could take notes.

If style over substance were a professor, you've got your guy. If you think positively of him, it's likely that you got a good grade. But if you care about learning, you can't help but be disgusted because you'll learn absolutely nothing that's useful. It blows my mind that this is a mandatory class for me. Makes me pity the MIS folks.

legit one of my favorite profs ever. the class is a lot of work but he does his best to really help students do well and feel comfortable with the class. tbh if you do badly it's kinda ur own fault cuz he has so many good resources and office hours are super helpful. as long as you do the reading and put effort into work you should be just fine
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This is a weird class. The professor talks a lot, and yet when you leave the class you don't feel like you've learned anything. The class materials seem terribly outdated. It's a shame that he is the one who's in charge of this course at the university and is largely responsible for how bad this is. Busy work with no learning.

He's mostly fair, there are a lot of case studies, exams are a bit hard. He definitely cares about his students. I feel like there wasn't a lot of learning in the class, but there also is, it's a really weird class. MAKE SURE TO TAKE NOTES when he says "You might want to write this down." He is a good a person, but I didn't really like the class.

Subpar professor that teaches with enthusiasm, however is overly critical of work. Wastes 20 minutes every class engaging students and covering the previous classes material. The rest of class is spent covering slideshows that are visually incredibly difficult to understand.

I honestly don't even know what this course was about. I would participate in class but not even know what I was saying. I'm think he sometimes doesn't even know what he's saying either. He goes off on many tangents which don't help and won't ever answer a question directly. Really weird course and I'm still just as confused as I was at the start.

A submitted assignment that's complete & accurate won't get a 100; thorough case study submissions will get a 3.6 out of 4 unless they go above and beyond. Because of this and the fact that every point of the assignments count to a total of 100, I found it hard to get above a B+/A-. Still, he def cares about his students and wants them to succeed.

Professor Dias's class is definitely more difficult than other MISM courses but you will learn so much from him. The class is based on weekly case studies and he gives very detailed feedback on them. Class participation is weighed very heavily, participation every class is expected. Hard but definitely worth it, Professor Dias is great.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Extremely professional. sometimes felt like an etiquette-esque class. Focus on the main ideas, I found that its one thing then 4 little things and that was primarily it. Exams are online slightly difficult but are just how and why you would implement certain things. Case studies are short yet you have to be clean and extremely precise and detailed.

If you take MISM2301 with Martin Dias, you will need to put a lot of effort into this class to get a good grade. He grades your participation on each class session (which was intentionally kept secret as far as I could tell) so if you don't go to class you're kind of screwed. But if you put in the effort you realize he's a dope professor.

Professor Dias was great. As long as your participate and you put in effort into the case studies, you will do fine. I personally got an A- because I did not participate in class discussions as much as I should, so I could have gotten a higher score if I did.. MISM 2301 is a hard course, but Prof. Dias is the best one there is so take it as u will.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: The only reason that people wouldn't get an A in mism is because they don't participate. Graded on case studies, at home quizzes, at home (REALLY HARD. but graded leniently) exams, and participation. Case studies were definitely time consuming (not hard to get a good grade if you read rubric). Sit in the front row and participate and you are fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: My favorite professor! He's passionate about teaching! His class can be daunting since it requires high engagement and performance, but he is really understanding. He has an uppity personality that some people may be taken aback by, but his being himself is part of what makes his teaching great. Assessments are 100% fair. Don't be disorganized.

as far as professors go for MISM, Dias is pretty good. He's a really nice guy who cares about students and is available outside of class. The assignments are pretty tough and exams are the hardest I've taken at NU. Still, I think he is one of the better professors for information systems and an overall good choice!
Amazing professor , bad course

Passionate about the course, but grades very harshly considering he skims over concepts for the test in class that are not even explained on the slides or the textboo. Emphasizes participation, but students are less inclined to participate because of his tangents and his extra obnoxious personality. If you want an A don't take this course

I think Dias is a great professor, but I hated the course. The course is just simple concepts stretched so thin like a trojan on a gourd. I don't know why this course is a must for CSBA students who take courses from both colleges. I really hope Dias gets to teach a higher-level course. He's passionate and knowledgeable and I think he'd be great.

Prof Dias is super enthusiastic about the course. The course itself is honestly pretty hard to understand if you aren't tech inclined and he made it as easy to understand as he could. If you constantly participate, really focus on textbook readings, and are able to keep up in lectures, you'll do well. Assigns 1-2 cases a week
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dias is great, if you're willing to work hard. He is very clear about his expectations and expects you to bring 100% to everything you do. If you aren't willing to work hard or learn, don't take his class. He LOVES what he does and is always willing to meet outside of class to go more in depth. He can get carried away on tangents during lectures.

Smart guy, terrible teacher. He is knowledgeable about MIS but can’t get the concepts across to students in a meaningful manner. Its obvious from his feedback that he only glances over homework and grades them based on how many buzzwords you throw around. As other students have said he is incredibly condescending

All of the reviews here are entirely correct. Made the mistake of reading these reviews and still taking the course. Don't make the same mistake. The class makes no sense, and he throws around meaningless buzz words that he rarely ever takes time to explain. Sometimes it feels like he does not even know what he's saying.

MISM just makes no sense in general. Idk why they teach it, everyone I talked to says the same. Given that this course makes no sense, this guy goes out of his way to be a tough grader. To get an A- or an A, you have to tryhard every single class and schedule a meeting in the beginning of the semester on "how to get a superior grade" :/

Its not even the confusing instruction that bothers me (use technical word salad in assignments and your solid). Professor Dias is just an obnoxious dude, something I sensed from the first day of class. Ive observed on more than one occasion him embarrassing well-intentioned students for no good reason. Hands down the worst professor Ive had.

Prof. Dias is passionate about MISM2301. He assigns 2 cases studies a week along with take home quizzes that both rack up to be a large portion of your grade. 3 exams, first one is very difficult while the last 2 are very similar, but you get used to his "style." PARTICIPATE -- it will help you stay engaged in the class because it can get boring

Unless youre 100% focused the whole class you cant pass. His grading is horrible, even if you try so hard you can never get a full grade. The class is the hardest class that I ever took, my friends took the same class but different professor, and it was much easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Don'. Take. Him. I'm a CS kid and his class is harder than my cs classes. Easy concepts are twisted and forced into irrelevant case studies for the exam. He rounds grades to the 20th decimal place just to increase your anxiety. The slides barely cover the textbook. Atleast 2 quizzes and case studies are due every week. He makes it hard on purpose.

Professor Dias' lectures are very hard to understand. A lot of the information within the textbook and lectures isn't applicable to the case studies. He grades case studies strictly and tests (there are 3) more leniently. It's pretty hard to get an A in this class so if you're trying to protect your 4.0, you probably shouldn't take this professor.

Literal hardo for no reason. Dias thinks that his class is the center of the universe and assigns a ton of work, however his feedback sucks and his lectures are hard to understand. He also grades participation for each individual class, something he didn't make clear at the start. Worst prof I have ever had, stay away.

Dias is definitely passionate about MIS which really shines through his lectures, not many professors are as enthusiastic as him about a base level course. On the other hand, he is very unclear when giving feedback and is a very strict grader even about simple hw assignments. He also makes tests unnecessarily difficult and quizzes 1-2 times a week.

Person below me is the teacher's pet, go figure. Dias holds a high standard for his students and none for himself He waited until the last month to grade everything and is extremely condescending and unhelpful with all of his feedback. I can confidently say I didn't learn a thing in his class and was a total waste of time. Never again.

Dias is incredibly passionate and knowledgeable, I guarantee anyone who rated him poorly wasn't really paying attention. It is difficult to get an A, but if you have any remote interest in the topic and aren't just taking it for a grade-booster, I would definitely recommend. Amazing lectures and you NEED to go to class to understand the slides.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Extremely clear lecturer, very passionate and a great guy. The class content is not the most interesting, but he definitely explains the topics clearly (you will learn!). Difficult to get an A, but very easy to get a B/B+ w/ minimal effort. Due to the amount of work due, grading was handed back fairly late. If you're a serious student, take him!

The topics are fairly straightforward, but he makes them much more confusing. He is very disorganized but has rigid structure for what you can/cannot say and do. He goes on lots of tangents and gets distracted easily. He is very unclear about his tests. No clarity into how he grades or assesses you. Has high expectations for you but not himself.

Professor Dias is very knowledgeable and clearly knows what he is talking about when it comes to MISM. With that said, his case studies can be difficult at times, but his tests are graded more leniently and are open note. Overall a really great guy who is amazing at teaching and connecting with his students. Not an easy A though so be ready to work
The most awful professor I have met. He does not lecture in a logical way and his presentation slides are nonsense. Plus you never know what grade you will receive.

Of the harder MIS teachers. Easy grader for tests. Hard grader for case studies. Participation and preparation matters a lot to him. The textbook is useless. His lectures are boring, but if you don't actively pay attention, the slides make no sense. Its hard to apply his lectures to case studies. This class was hard for no reason compared to others
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dias is super passionate about MIS but makes the class more difficult than it should be. Lectures are often hard to follow, exams are challenging and stressful but graded leniently, and case studies take a long time to do them right. If you're looking for an easy class, avoid Dias, but if you want to work hard he will teach you a lot!

This class was one of the biggest wastes of time and money I have ever experienced. This dude is really fired up about the class which is cool, but I learned absolutely nothing. The grading makes no sense, and he gives almost no feedback. He sets deadlines but can't keep any himself. Overall just a really bad class with a really mediocre prof.

One of the more difficult professors I've had. Definitely a smart man, and he's engaging. However, his grading is not straightforward and very ambiguous. Its very easy to get a B or B+ with adequate participation, but getting an A is very very hard (94+). Participation is HUGE and his slides are incomprehensible if you don't follow along in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Only take him if you're serious about the class. Absolutely phenomenal lecturer. Has extensive knowledge in his field, and committed to students' success. Class material needs careful work, participation is a huge deal, but everything positively compounds on each other if you work for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: if you do not answer questions in class, your participation will be 4/10 even you come up every class. 10 minutes quiz before each session, 1 case study every week, 3 exams, no final.

Loved his classes! So engaging and keeps MIS material exciting (and funny too). Exams are pretty challenging but he's definitely worth it! His depth of knowledge in MIS and technology is overwhelming! Although I'm probably going to end up with a B+ in his class and I'm typically a straight A student, I don't regret taking his class.

If you don't want to participate daily in this class, do not try and get an A. If you want a B, then this class is an easy B regardless of how hungover you are. Case studies are all on course hero, its worth the $15 a month for an easy B+ or A-

Dias was one of the most life changing professors I've had in my life. So passionate about MIS, his lectures force you to listen to him and furthered my fascination in MIS. Tests are rather difficult (open book, but notes didn't personally help that much) so he makes you work for it. In office hours, he is more than willing to help you do well.

Dias is a very passionate, very great teacher. He reiterates the most important topics to assure everyone understands the foundation and is very energetic.
