He really cares to make sure students learn, regardless of major or even if no-one wants to be there. Participate and you'll be his best friend. He really appreciates participation.
tough grader!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: DiasBEST professor ever. I'm NOT naturally smart (never a 4.0 student) & didn't have prior MIS knowledge but took MIS in Summer 1 (2x faster than Fall/Spring) & got an A. If you're willing to put MANY HOURS into case studies & studying you'll succeed. Dias will prepare you for coop like no other by challenging you & make $60k tuition worth it!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Dias is exactly what I look for in a Professor. He is knowledgeable, fair, and he treats students as his equals. The class requires effort, and is probably more difficult than the same course taught by other professors, but Professor Dias will ensure each student receives the grade they earned. He is a role model to future educators.
Makes boring material exciting and interesting, I enjoyed his class and found him to be a fair and clear grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: MGSC 6204 Managing Information Resources was a require MBA course focused on IT systems. This class had very little value to me and a heavy workload for a 1.5 credit class. The only positive experience I had was with Prof Dias. He kept us engaged every class and was a joy to listen to. Great professor! Boring subject that had very little value!

Dias is a great professor. So passionate and enthusiastic that he makes a dull subject interesting. He's also one of the most articulate guys I've ever met. The course will challenge you and class participation is a big part of your grade, but his teaching style facilitates discussion so the participation is natural. If you want to learn, take Dias
he's good but he wont just give you a grade

Dias is an amazing teacher and one of the best I've had at NU. Im not a "tech" person and he was able to actually get me engaged in this dry MIS class material. You need to participate and do the HW! FYI you can google online quiz q's for answers. Definitely need to study for tests and read the grading criteria, it really helps to score higher.

He is great! One of the best professors I have had a NU. He makes the dry material of MISM interesting and engages all the students in class. You have to come to class to learn/pass the class. But if you work hard he will notice and he's willing to help anyone who asks for it.

Professor Dias is awesome- the topic is not the most interesting business class but he makes it practical in terms of being a business person. He is one of the most intelligent and insightful teachers I have had at Northeastern and is the only teacher one should consider when taking MIS.

He is very passionate about MIS. He makes a boring subject kind of exciting. He has a good sense of humor. He will always try to do something different to keep your attention. Go to office hours and participate and you will see a difference. Grading is fair. Tests and Cases are challenging but they are the same for all MIS classes.

He is the only prof. teaching the MIS class that changed the way things are done, making students think "outside the box". I personally found that i wasnt falling asleep in his class, but subject is not interesting. All exams are heavy on analysis and absolutely no memory.

DIas is very passionate about teaching and keeps classes interesting. He gives his own version of the material, which seemed good at first, but by the end caused the class to be really hard. Avoid if you don't want the hardest tests ever.

Practice problems are so different from test
Never curves even when tests are hard
No empathy for students
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really awesome professor. Never took chemistry before, but he helped me understand everything. Even when I bombed a test, I still felt proud of what I learned, and I eventually improved. Gives lots of extra credit on exams, has good policies, and tries to help you succeed. Just a really great professor to have, especially for such a hard course.

Very caring and fair professor. He genuinely wants his students to succeed and cares about their wellbeing. Easy to get help outside of class. Tests are a bit difficult and require critical thinking but he gives 3 extra points on every test. Lots of homework but ample time to complete it. Only critique: study guides often didn't match actual tests.
He was such an amazing chem professor. He made the concepts seem so simple and was so nice. The homework wasn't too bad, just a little tedious.
loved him! just do the homework and go to office hours if needed

Very kind and accessible and a great, great professor (like the best I've had thus far). Material was tough but if you come to lecture and go to office hours he's great at explaining things. Also gives extra credit and practice problems on Aktiv are super helpful on exams, and he'll replace your lowest midterm score so a good grade is very feasible

Super kind and caring, and great at teaching (like the best I've had yet). The material is hard but going to lectures and office hours made it seem very simple. Very feasible to succeed in the course if you put effort in, he also gives extra credit which is nice and will replace your lowest midterm exam grade.

If you're taking CHEM1161, take it with Dan Matthew. I would recommend the chem ramp up (it was extra credit but it was actually helpful). An A is totally achievable by completing all the homework (unlimited tries) and studying using the practice exams and aktiv problems. Of course, try your best to stay proactive the class can move fast at times.

The actual goat. I had zero chemistry knowledge coming in and finished with an A, and that's all thanks to Dr. Dan's teaching! Take notes on the homework, study the practice exams, and focus on what you're struggling with. Also the ramp up is helpful if you have no idea (like me) what to expect coming into a chem course. Thank you!!!

I would absolutely recommend Prof Matthew for gen chem!! If you attend class regularly and do the homework assignments you'll be set up for success in his class. He's a good lecturer and easy to follow, he takes notes during class that you follow along on. Attendance is mandatory, but he allows a couple of absences. He's also super nice :)

By far the best Chem professor I have ever had! He cares about how the students are learning more than grading, and his lectures really made me understand the concepts so much more clearly. Plus, he gives many opportunities for extra credit. Recitation can feel like more of a chore but you get a bump up in your grade if you go to all of them.

BEST PROFESSOR SO FAR! I was so worried about taking a good chem professor because the class can be difficult, but what you put in is what you get out. Biggest advice is follow along lectures, and you will be perfectly set. I went to almost all office hours and he bonded with all the students, taking hours out of his week and day to help. love him!

DAN MATTHEW AKA THE BEST PROF EVER!!!! He is an amazing professor. He really knows how to teach. He gives 4 midterm exams and a cumulative final exam. If you score higher on the final than one of your midterms he will replace your lowest midterm grade with the final exam grade. He gives tons of practice and is willing to meet with you anytime!

I LOVE DR.DAN! He is one of the best professors I've had here at Northeastern. His lectures are straightforward and the activ practice questions he gives really help for his exams. He really helped me enjoy chemistry and solidify concepts that I forgot in high school. If you understand the material well, the exams should be straightforward.

Dr. Dan is the best! If you're taking chemistry, definitely try to take it with him. He takes notes with the class, so the lecture stays at a good pace. He's willing to reexplain anything if a student is confused. He's so easy to reach outside of class and his office hours are very useful.

I love this man. So amazing at seeing what works and does not work for students and changing his teaching style around that. There were so many resources for the exams. He is so kind and very approachable at office hours. You will always get 100 on the hw because you get unlimited tries.
Good professor and makes the most out of lectures. Notes are really helpful, and going to his office hours are too.

Cool dude. Genuinely cares about his students understanding the course content. This slows down his lectures heavily, but he helped me actually learn some things I had memorized and forgotten in high school. Though while the material was often familiar and low-level, his tests reached into dimensions class time couldn't dream of.

Take him for Gen Chem! He can be reached outside of class and really cares about his students. Also, instead of having slides for his lecture, he writes out his notes along with his class, so it goes at a natural pace. He structures his class with the textbook (not essential), but you do have to purchase Aktiv (practice problems). Overall, great!

Challenging for those uncomfortable with chemistry. He did not curve any exams but he gave extra points at the end of them. If you make a good-faith attempt, but come up with a D by the end of the semester, he will boost your grade so you will pass. He is a safe bet, but the other professor did curve exams which would've been extremely useful.

Dan is one of few professors I've had who genuinely cares about students. If anything were to happen, whether its falling behind, simply sleeping in, or an emergency, he's gracious and understanding. Chemistry is not easy but he taught it well. Homework and class work is easy to get 100s on despite the tests being difficult, so avg grade is boosted

Dr. Dan cares a lot about his students and wants you to succeed. He provides a lot of study/review resources for exams. I liked his teaching style too-- he walks you through the notes/example problems on his iPad. Highly recommend!
Dr. Dan was an amazing teacher. He did a great job at explaining the material in a simple way. He also gave us tests from the previous semesters to help us prepare for exams, which was super helpful.

Overall the class was pretty good. I like his lecture style (he writes his notes in notability) and he cares a lot about his students. The one thing I did not like was how he stated he purposely wanted the tests to be harder. Better than other professors for gen chem.

Dr. Dan is a fantastic professor. You can tell he really cares about his students by how he always stops to answer questions. His tests are very reasonable and have questions that appear in the homework.

If you want to have a (fairly) easy time in gen chem take it with Dr. Dan. I withdrew from chem class my 1st semester and thought just I wasn't cut out to understand the material. That all changed with Dr. Dan. He's a great lecturer and cares a lot about his students. He's very personable and hosted in-person review sessions before every exam.

I liked his teaching style (actively writes out notes during lecture) and his tests were fair. He is generous with partial credit. An etextbook and 2 online accounts are required to be purchased for this class.

dr dan is a tougher prof than people say - he even prefaced this semester by saying he knew on rate my prof he was known to be easy, but he's not anymore. this class isn't impossible, lectures are good (though a bit rushed), he's amazing with extra credit on tough exams. my biggest piece of advice? stay on top of lectures and USE THE TEXTBOOK.
I was not a fan of the way he lectures. His notes were not conducive to review after class. he also mentioned that his goal is to make his exams harder than past semesters.

Great professor, despite his tests being a bit challenging. Writes his notes with the class, making it easy to understand and visualize. His practice tests were not like the actual exams in terms of difficulty, but if you understand the material you should be fine if you study outside of class. Great guy, super nice and approachable.

Dr. Dan was fantastic! Such a caring individual who puts in the effort for you if you are willing to do the same. Exams were slightly difficult, but as long as you prepare with the resources he provides, you should succeed.

great, intelligent professor with riveting lectures on fascinating content. very engaging and allows abstract concepts to make sense. 
class moves very fast DO NOT fall behind, exams do not match up to practice tests or problems in terms of difficulty. exams are tricky and challenging. hard to earn a good grade. deep concept knowledge guaranteed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I had Dr. Dan during spring 2023 and he was one of the best professors I've had here! His lectures were so helpful. He usually takes notes while teaching in front of the class and makes them accessible for everyone at any time. Notes are clear and everything on the tests will be stuff that has been in the lectures. He's genuinely a great guy too!

He started off the year strong and was a great prof. He made chemistry understandable. However, his exams are way harder than they need to be. He said he purposely made them harder than past exams. He often would recognize that they were harder then they needed to be b
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dan's pretty easy. 3 exams before the final and attendance is mandatory but he takes attendance by giving out a code at the beginning of class so if you have friends in the class it is helpful. The exams are pretty easy but get harder as the semester goes on just because of the material. He posts all his notes online so it should be fine.

Awesome guy, made me love Chemistry after I hated it in hs. Honestly a very nice dude that goes over all the content clearly in his lectures and his tests are all very fair. If you show up to class and do HW then you will be fine, he also gives out a lot of extra practice problems that prepare you well for the tests.

I dreaded chemistry coming into college but Dan has made my chem experience amazing. His lectures are clear, not overwhelming or over complicated. Would 100% take him if you think chem is not your best subject because he makes the material understandable. VERY generous when grading exams. Exams are difficult and long but manageable.

Wonderful professor. Reminder, always go to his lectures, unless you want to miss a TON of materials. Class pace was incredibly fast but his lecture and explanation were super detailed. Exams are VERY DIFFICULT but very fair. Get ready to study a lot for this course, and if you do you will learn a lot and in good shape.

Prof. Matthew is an amazing lecturer. Instead of slideshows, he opens up pages in notability & writes notes with you which is a much more effective way of learning/teaching chemistry. His tests are also fair, simply practice & make sure you fully understand important concepts (conceptual questions are asked). There is also always a bonus question!

Dan is super approachable, and knowledgeable about chemistry. His office hours and extra help sessions were helpful, and he posted lots of practice problems. It wasn't all good though, he would say his class was too easy in past semesters and purposely made exam questions more difficult or trick questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Although I really enjoyed his lectures, his exams tended to be extremely difficult. He does give practice exams before every exam, and when I would take those, I would get high 90s. Then when it came to the actual exam, the questions would be different formats and would ask you to do things that were never taught in class.

Dan is great at explaining. Only thing I would say is that they can word some test problems weirdly, and it just throws me off. I don't understand why they can't just clearly state open response questions. Other than that, great professor, there were 3-4 exams + 1 final. Go to class, do your homework, and study a lot, and I think you'll do well.

Professor Matthew is a great professor. He explains concepts very clearly and cares a lot about his students and the subject. Tests were not too difficult as long as you studied, and the weekly homework assignments had unlimited attempts, so they were good practice.

DAN SLAYS but it was a difficult course due to the material. As long as you stay on top of the homework and do the practice exams you will do good.Dan is really caring and his office hours help a lot.
As long as you put the work in you can get a decent grade.

Expectations are very clear. Most of the class finished with an A- or an A. Lectures are easy to follow and he makes the concepts easy to understand. Go to the class the week of Thanksgiving for extra credit!!

Dr. Dan is amazing! He is very clear about grading. There were 3 midterms and the final exam. Participation is mandatory and he gives weekly homework assignments. He explains everything so clearly and always answers questions.

Dan is such a great teacher. He makes concept very simple to understand. His office hours are very helpful.   Exams are fair and he post a lot of practice exams/questions (+ easyy bonus points on every exam). Attendance is mandatory every day which is meh. Other than that, no other complaint, loved the class and the teacher.
I love Dan!!! Everyone loves him. He makes the information so understandable and you can tell he really cares. He is very accessible outside of class.

Professor Matthew made general chemistry a breeze! I hadn't even thought about chemistry since sophomore year of high school and he made everything engaging and easy to understand. He posted practice exams that were almost identical to his tests. Class averages were consistently above 80%. Every test has a bonus question for extra credit.

Overall, the class is not too hard, he teaches well, the only complaint is sometimes it is frustrating that class is mandatory especially if you have taken a course like physics already and know some of the material.

explains everything in a way that's easy to understand and will take the time to make sure you understand topics. assigns weekly homework but it can normally be completed in about an hour. tests are very fair and he gives you points whenever he's able to on non-multiple choice problems
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Probably the best instructor I've ever had - take Dan if you can! He is very clear and lectures while he projects his own notes which is super clear. Fair grading, accessible, and great explanations. I took chem in high school but this class has left me with a totally higher level of understanding.
You can just tell he really care about his students and how they do. He is very reasonable with grading and genuinely teaches well. I would take more of his classes for sure
Took Chem1161 from him in Spring 2022, very easygoing and is a very fair grader. Super accessible outside of class and does his best to make sure all of his students learn the course material

I loved professor Matthews' class. I had such a hard time with chemistry in high school, but he made me enjoy it. There's weekly homework assignments (they're fairly long), 3 midterms, and a final (score will replace your lowest midterm grade), as well as attendance in lecture and recitation that goes into your grades. Work hard and you'll succeed!

Professor Matthews is the best professor I have had so far at Northeastern. He gives amazing lectures and makes sure students thoroughly understand content, and is always available outside of class for help. He also only tests on what he lectures, so there are no surprises on his exams. For new chemistry students I couldn't recommend him more!

Amazing professor! I hated chem back in high school but he made the class enjoyable and he taught concepts extremely well. He is always willing to help and there are many different tasks that make up your grade, so it's not just based on exams. Take the class, you won't regret him as a professor.

Take this class! Dr. matthew makes all the material very interesting and does a great job explaining it, he also uses his ipad to write lecture notes during class which makes it easy to keep up. Clear grading and gives a good amount of partial credit. Weekly homework assignments and exams make up most of the grade.

Really good teacher! He explains concepts very well and is always willing to help and answer questions. Also a really nice guy. He gives fun bonus questions on tests like, "What's your favorite place to go in Boston?" and they're usually worth a lot of points. Chem is hard and you do need to study, but he makes it a lot easier and more fun.

Wonderful professor, his lectures are very clear and easy to follow. If you struggled with chemistry take him, as he takes the time to explain theoretical concepts. I would definitely take a course with him again.

Dr. Dan was a really good professor for gen chem. He took the time to explain the concepts in class through his interesting teaching style which is writing on his iPad and we copy down notes. His tests were very true to what we learned in class so you wouldn't expect any surprises on the test if you study all the material from class.
Great for chemistry - teaches well and is understanding

Wonderful professor, goes every extra mile to help with student understanding and is great at explaining theoretical concepts in understandable ways. Also just a super cool dude, really patient and makes a hard class (chem 1 and 2 accelerated) understandable for students. Would definitely take a course with him again.

Dan is a really knowledgable professor. He's clear, direct, and caring for his students. I really enjoyed learning with him, and I think that other student's did as well.  Homework was genuinely helpful.  Unlimited attempts and no point penalty for wrong answers. He encourages guesses in lectures because that's how you learn.  Highly recommend!

Best professor I've had at neu so far. Love the way he teaches lectures; he basically writes the lectures on his iPad as he is teaching you, making them very easy to follow and visualize what he is talking about. He explains concepts simply and clearly, and is very accessible for office hours. 10/10 would take again

Dr. Dan is the best professor I've had at Northeastern. I took gen chem 1 and 2 with another professor before and withdrew from the class because I was struggling and unsupported but I have done well in this class. Dr. Dan is an amazing teacher, his lectures are easy to follow, and he cares deeply about his students' success. A rare find, amazing.

The content of this class is not of personal interest which might have influenced my rating, but the lectures are confusing with weirdly organized slides and she has an irrelevant assignment every class for each table group. The worst part she rarely gives full score on any of the assignments, including participation so forget an A!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Do not listen to any of these reviews. This class is minimal work and prof is so sweet. It is extremely hard to do bad in this class. Both tests are online. The only thing that may affect your grade are the HW analysis but it is just really hard to get an A. With that being said I have not received lower than a B on one. TAKE THIS CLASS I PROMISE.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: the worst class ever actualyl pointless and has NOTHING TO DO WITH MY MAJOR. so much work for no reason i couldnt care less about computers
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The worst. How is this her primary job? She's reading stuff of her slides. I just hope she retires and spares future students the torture.
Good professor
There are likely better options out there.
dry lectures & grades aren't rounded up.
What a bore! She really needs to reevaluate her career choices. Currently the worst professor I had and probably the worst professor I'll ever have.

Lots of small writing assignments, and you need to make sure to specifically follow her writing format. (Bullet points only, no big paragraphs). Lectures were overall not super interesting, and there was a lot of groupwork throughout the class. The 3 tests are all online, and I think that she grades lighter on you if she likes you, so participate
I'm convinced that all her positive reviews are written by her. Such a terrible professor whose lectures are a snooze fest. I've got a good grade, but didn't learn anything at all. What a joke.

such a sweetheart in the class. can be a mix of lectures and group work in the class (i hated the group work). 3 tests which are all online and 1 final project and assignments were sometimes difficult to understand since she wanted specific requirements (no paragraphs, but bullet points instead). only annoying thing is she kept us overtime.
She is super sweet and definitely appreciated if you make any sort of effort to participate, certainly recommend her!
Decent class, but she definitely favored some groups over others when grading group projects

She is such a sweetheart! Despite the reviews, her grading really isn't that tough if you make sure your responses are thorough and you follow her format. DO NOT write case studies or discussions in paragraph form. Also make sure to participate, because that grade really matters to her. Overall, the course was easy if you follow instructions.

Although professor soule can be a very tough grader, the class is not super difficult. So long as you do the assignments on time and put effort into it, as well as attend class and participate, it should be an easy A-. She is a very kind professor and cares about what she teaches. Just put in the effort and you'll be fine.
dry lecturer, but the class is okay. there is a lot of HW that are reading assignments and quizzes on it. Tests are not too hard if you study the case analysis well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Nice person, but an extremely dry lecturer and does not teach well. She barely explains concepts in class, and spends the entire time explaining complex concepts we were rarely tested on. You are expected to read the textbook and know everything before classes, which makes you question what you're paying for.

Don't expect an A, because she does not give any. You will not learn anything helpful in this class. Almost all of the topics are outdated, and only the surface is scratched for each subject. Attendance is mandatory, but either way, no one is paying attention. B+ is very easy to get as long as you put minimal effort into the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Nice professor but unclear guidelines on written material make it impossible to improve after she gives you 80s on every case study which is worth most of your grade. Even if you talk to her in person she is still vague with how she wants you to complete the assignments. If you want an A in this course don't take her.

Dry lectures which feel outdated, have few examples, and just dull material, and still expect participation in every class. Papers every week and 2-3 small quizzes every week. Begins the class by saying A is basically not attainable, and she stayed true to her words; no one got an A. Very demotivating thing to start with. Never curves.
DO NOT TAKE HER! Her lectures are not engaging and her grading is very unclear. You will never get an A in this class because she does not give them. Stay away from this class if you can.
sometime take attendance. She covered lots of material without digging further, literally learn nothing from her class. lots of quizzes and boring classes.

Very lecture heavy in class but she likes to include some group discussions a few times a week. She wants you to read a chapter of the book every few days, but you can get by withtout reading. There are case studies as assignments every week, which are not difficult just very boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: WARNING:DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS!A lot of case studies and she grades harshly, providing little feedback. No one ever gets 5/5, highest always 4.4/5 when they're worth ~25% of your grade. 2 tests that were also very difficult w/ questions unclear/broad. You need at least a 94 to get an A so not only does she grade hard, you also need to work harder.

very average professor. my biggest gripe was how slow she was with grading and a bit unclear on her criteria. when she did provide feedback though, it was decent. she tried her best to make class engaging, even though it usually didn't work. they're revamping this course though so hopefully it's better whenever you take it.

Grade based on Individual + group case studies, chapter quizzes, 2 exams + Capstone assignment. Exams are kind of confusing, they follow the case study format and aren't multiple choice. You'll do well if you follow the case study format. Lectures are kind of confusing but she is super sweet and will help if you ask her. Quizzes aren't difficult.
This class is hard, and this professor makes it harder. Don't ever take it. I prepared so much before every class, but still confusing what the professor is talking.

Professor Soule is a nice lady, but a horrid grader.The material is given from the text and is supported using case studies. She doesn't offer individual corrections so that one may learn,rather she posts broad guidelines. The exam I took was the broadest, most unclear exam ever.Little to zero instructions were given. No one knew what was expected.

a lot of quizzes but its easy, a ton of individual and group case studies that she grades really hard on and doesn't provide individual feedback so you have no clue what you did wrong, very condescending, doesn't respond to emails, tests are stupid hard. i learned nothing. thank god this isnt my concentration.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Soule was very kind and always available to answer questions. She valued student feedback and worked very hard to make this boring material engaging and encourage conversations about the material. If you used vocabulary and frameworks she presented in class you can get an A on every assignment.

Prof Soule is kind and enjoys what she teaches. Unfortunately she does not know how to teach undergraduate students business statistics. She rarely does examples in class, you have to figure everything out by yourself, is unclear, and if you arent an expert with excel youre in major trouble. She made the class more difficult than it needed to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Handed a lot of case studies, seldom get feedback.
HW assignments every Friday and an hour-long simulation every week. So give or take around an hour and a half of homework per week. Quizzes are easy, the final is take-home and online.

I swear every good rating on here he wrote himself cause the amount that he brings up his Rate My Professor is insane, especially the first week. Literally used AI to try to catch us using AI and spent his entire break doing it. Marketing is such common sense the majority of the time and he overcomplicates it so much.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Lectures are mandatory but irrelevant. He loves to talk about his old job at Bose- he should probably stay there for everyone's sake. He's reachable and knowledgeable, but a questionable teacher. Tests are hard and irrelevant to class discussions, and he never clearly states what's on them, so you're anxious all class not knowing what to write down
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: hard quizzes. so much reading, not worth it
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: switching my major to finance because he made me hate marketing SO MUCH
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Don't take
He curves down so only a certain percent of class can get As. Yes it will grow you, but yes it will also take a blow to your gpa if you're into that sort of thing. Would stay away if you can

Nace is very passionate and a nice guy, however, his grading and stance on GPA is pretty bad. He believes it does not matter but in reality it really does. Teaching wise, he is very good and once you leave the semester you will have a strong understanding of marketing. I would recommend taking him but you're going to have to work really hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: (For BC)... David Nace is the Goat... tough class but rewarding if you try. Exams were difficult but I learned a lot about marketing... important to do well on group projects/participate. Has inspired me to possibly pursue a career in marketing/advertising.

Participation is really important and he ends up rewarding those who come to class by rounding their grades. The final was insanely difficult for no reason though. Overall you'll learn a lot but he does make an intro class a lot harder than it needs to be. Also don't really like his stance on GPA as he thinks it doesn't matter.

Prof nace is a nice guy but not a good teacher. His quizzes aren't THAT bad, but his final was crazy hard and everyone ik bombed it. Also, he grades harshly on the paper and in general, so A's are rare. Basically no HW tho. I think he genuinely cares about his students and wants them to succeed, but he needs to brush up on some things lol.

passionate guy who tries his best to keep lectures engaging by encouraging class participation. He writes his own tests and quizzes, which are pretty hard as you can study for hours on end, but the quiz will be on something else entirely. I liked his class in general though.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I absolutely loved this professor. He's incredibly engaging—his lectures are so interesting I don't even need my laptop. The lectures are very conversational which I enjoy, and he truly encourages you to ask questions and won't make you feel dumb. He's also super personable and easy to connect with. One of the best professors I've had!

Professor Nace is very passionate about the field of marketing, and his lectures are engaging at times. People are overreacting about the difficulty of the course. It is a lot of memorization, but if you read the textbook and put the work in, you can easily do well. He was very accessible outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof Nace is really nice and laid back and is definitely funny at times, but his class had lots of reading, lectures werent actually lectures they were discussions which felt like you were teaching for him. they were unhelpful so i stopped coming to class. Also he changes his syllabus and other course stuff all the time. class wasnt too hard tho

Class is hard for no reason. It's an entry-level class, and it shouldn't be this hard. Nobody is getting an A in my class. He also says he will keep the average grade around B and curve down if the average is higher than B. Be ready to read 40 pages textbook for each class and extremely hard exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Class is difficult for no reason. We literally were tested on things I learned in my managerial accounting class. I talked to other kids in the same class and their tests/quizzes are so much easier. He's not fair, he needs to understand that.
changes the syllabus every once in a while. Class is difficult for no reason. wouldn't take again for a marketing class

Dave Nace is the GOAT. He's lecture-heavy, but he engages you in the lectures and asks questions. He gets to know each person and really cares about you. He responds well to student feedback about his teaching. The final was online, and hard, but he warned us. If you truly want to understand marketing, take his class.

I don't entirely agree with the other reviews. His lectures were engaging (stories, etc.), and I walked away feeling I knew more than prior to starting the course. The caveat is that he gave an extremely tough final exam, and did not help us in preparing for it. He offered many opportunities for office hours and responded to my emails quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: He changed his syllabus without warning and says that he doesn't know how to help when you ask questions. The final was NOT AT ALL what he said it was going to be. Quizzes are strictly based on how much info you can fit onto a cheat sheet. Came off as understanding at first but then did a complete 180. Don't recommend at all!!
Professor Nace was an amazing guy, very personable and lots of experience, but his class was very challenging.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I feel it is fair to mention it was his first time teaching the course. HOWEVER, personally, I would NEVER take a course with Nace again. He likes to say GPA won't matter in the future and teaches like he believes that as well. I have never been so frustrated. Expect a ton of textbook reading, no study guides, and “I don't know how to help you.”

Group projects and exams are the majority of the weight. He gives poor instructions and isn't clear even after emailing him. Almost impossible to get a 100% on any assignment because he takes points off whenever he feels like it and completely ignores the rubrics HE POSTS. If you try to get points back he is passive aggressive. STAY AWAY

The class isn't particularly difficult but his instructions for assignments aren't clear and if you ask for clarification he is extremely disrespectful. He doesn't seem to follow the rubrics he posts; just taking points off that are unrelated to the guidelines given. I'm pretty sure he writes the positive reviews on here so be cautious.

run if you can... i had a bunch of deadweights for group projects and he basically told me to deal with it. did nothing to help me. i ended up doing two projects basically on my own. also the final is ridiculous, worth 40% of your grade but it isn't even clear what's on it. he grades participation harshly too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Netty, is a very humorous professor and is very easy to get along with. The only thing are his group works that are hard since he docent involve himself. He isn't too difficult to work with and is a nice guy. Its just a lot of information but he is very accessible
great class

Overall was a solid teacher. I don't understand the previous students' answers, as my class was different, so he may have changed since then. Pay attention in class, take good notes on lectures: you will get a free A. His lectures for the midterm were pretty easy if you did the work, and the same applied to the final. Based heavily on his slides.

Netty is a fun lecturer and has a good sense of humor. He's makes participating in class easy for anyone who pays attention and feels comfortable speaking in class. Netty does a good job answering questions in class and breaking down concepts. Be careful of his tests, they can be tricky and are weighed heavily.

Dr. Netty is a very passionate and engaging Professor. He pulls in a great deal of real-world examples and makes the material applicable. He facilitates class discussion and brings everyone into it. You can tell he really cares about his students and wants us to succeed. For those with an interest in IB, the course is most definitely a good choice.

I was able to take Professor Netty's class recently and had a amazing experience. Although his tests could be difficult and a good amount of the information on it came from the textbook, I felt well prepared based off of his lectures, as well as the textbook. If you show up to class, payed attention, and put in some work, you will be fine

Netty is very kind and helpful, and passionate about his job. Accent isn't bad. Lectures are boring and you don't really need to pay attention; I didn't study at all until before exams and still got As, lots of memorization required but doable. IIRC midterm was worth 15% and final was 20% of your grade. Course was boring but Netty made up for it.

Netty was a great professor. He lectures, but always gets you to think in the lectures, so participation heavily matters. Most of your grades are from tests and group projects, so you have to study and do the projects ahead of time. Overall it's a good class, but be prepared for the projects and tests.

Netty was an extremely passionate and excellent professor, information was went over in a super clear and steady pace in class, directions for how to preform well on individual and group projects were clear and matched the grades our group received, and the midterm and final did not have any unfair questions. If you participate and listen, dw.

-Class was a sham
-Final was a 25 multiple choice test worth 40% of your grade and ridiculously difficult and based on a textbook he did not use once in the class, with the lectures for the entire semester covering maybe 3 out of 25 questions
-Put in every grade 2 hours before grades closed
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He has very long lectures and that is how he makes tests. It comes off of his 300+ slides. The textbook is not required, but his practice material comes from the textbook so his practice material is not even representative of the tests. Group projects make up most of the grade and he does not intervene when group mates are bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was an amazing professor, passionate about what he was teaching. He was very helpful outside of class. You have to take notes during the classes, and participation matters. Read the textbook and keep up with the material, and you will have no problem.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class is strictly graded on the midterm and final. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS TAKE THE OTHER PROF. His power points are pretty much useless and he does not use the textbook; you'll have to read it on your own all the way thru if you even want a chance in passing. The averages for midterm and final were absolutely atrocious. Thick accent.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Your grade is really dependent on the notes you take as a lot of the test questions are from what he verbally says in class, and not just on the slides. Not sure why everyone is talking about his accent as it really wasn't an issue for me. Final wasn't that hard if you really study. He's a good professor and helps students who actually put in work!

Do not believe any of those good comment, the professor write it him-self. Just focus on those bad once, people are telling the truth. This is pretty
much the most HORRIBLE professor I would ever seen in my life. I don't even understand y Northeastern not kick him out. Barely speak english,
being rube to students who try to negotiate with him.

A charming and pleasant lecturer. 
I enjoyed attending his classes.

This guy started my villain arc story and ultimately caused me to have complete PTSD when choosing teachers. This guy might be as narcissistic as the character from American Psycho. He has no respect for his students and completely disregards their opinions. He has this mantra that he is always right and the student is wrong. Take any other teacher
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
The most outstanding professor on Oakland's campus. He's charismatic and kind. 

His lectures are fun, and the course is very organized and clear.

He gets you excited about international business!

Great professor!!

This professor understands real business. He is very knowledgeable and has a lot of industry experience.

You get a lot of practical insights on how to do international business!

Love his accent, making it almost impossible to understand the lecture. Doesn't know how to teach and grade assignments randomly with NO clear rubric. Northeastern better fire him before more student suffers.
Love him, good accent

PPT doesn't link to the textbook at all while expecting you to know everything from both the book & ppt for the test. Grades projects based on no criteria rubric & extremely unfair. A lot of the aspects he considered for grading are not mentioned in the instructions at all, but you have to hit all of them to make him satisfied. Strong accent.

HORRIBLE professor.He taught you how to cook eggs and asked you to come up with a Beef Wellington during the test. I would never take his class again,he killed my interest in InternationalBusiness. His grading rubric is vague, so he could take as many points off as he wants. He is very tough,and very mean to student's who try to negotiate with him.
Very hard final, tough questions. He said that "I don't know the reason why you can't get an A if you read the text book",but he never use textbook in class and PPT just doesn't cover the textbook
He is can barely speak English, don't take him, same reason as other people mentioned
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The MOST AWFUL teacher you could have shows grades after the due date so you can't even bother him. He could barely speak English so Idk why is he teaching. Very hard final. Take this course with the other teacher at Mills if you can.

Hard final, never teach anything. Very tough grader on ur project and debate. Very heavy accent, if you could DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM. You won't know ur grade by the end, and it will surprise you

This professor delays grade release until the semester ends. The class focuses on PowerPoint but the final is excessively difficult, with challenging questions not covered in the lectures. Even with a 12-point curve, an 85 is the highest grade, making it nearly impossible for most people to earn an A due to a low average of 64 on the final.
best of the best
love her !!!! miss her now that I'm not in her class this semester :'(
very generous grading, and easily explains the readings from the seminar during our recitation time

gokcen is the best design recitation professor there is. she cares deeply about her students, reminding them frequently to drink water so they can achieve their greatest hydrated potential. she has a good artistic vision, however she is not always the most competent when it comes to technology. overall i can't wait to take more classes from her.

She's a great professor. The class itself isn't too hard; you just have to actually understand everything and know a lot of definitions. Her final was easy too, just questions pulled from exams 1-3. Lectures are funny/engaging, and the material is interesting.

Prof. Greiner structures the course around three in-person exams with multiple-choice and open-response questions. Exam content is taken directly from the slides and study guides. The cumulative final is based on material from the previous exams, which helps with preparation. Lectures are engaging, and extra credit opportunities are available.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: i really liked this class! there is a LOT of material but she makes study guides that are pretty accurate and help a lot. the final only had one question that wasn't on a previous exam which was really nice. very interesting class!!

Dr. Greiner is an excellent professor! She's down to earth, kind, and the most knowledgeable prof I've had. You can tell she's very passionate about the field and wants her students to learn and succeed. There is a lot of material, so her classes are lecture-heavy but super interesting. Definitely use the study guides and go to her review sessions!

very passionate and knows material well. tests on paper in class (i had 500-600 flashcards for each test (3). If you work hard, you will do well. Only annoying thing was that the final was repeat qs (i get it was to pull up grades) but as someone who already had a high grade in the class, it felt like a waste of time to re-memorize old test answers
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she's very personable and flexible, i loved taking this class lowkey. grade is calculated off 3 tests, final, and attendance. lecture is engaging but there is SO MUCH material, and can be overwhelming sometimes. however the final is only made up of past test questions, so it kinda balances out. also all exams are half MCQ and half short response.

Professor Velazco overall was a great professor! His classes are discussion based so be prepared to be called on but he makes it a fun environment to participate in. I will say although he is great, his grading is a bit all over the place. He isn't very clear with his expectations and his grading reflects that.

This class is genuinely confusing, difficult tests, no study guide, many students are failing, just gives us a handbook and practice tests, which closes down the night before the actual test. Many students don't know where to start studying or where to go, as he only refers to the textbook. Tests are also really inconsistent, DO NOT TAKE AT ALL.

He doesn't teach at all in lectures. If you take this class, you're kind of on your own. His lectures are pointless. The only examples that you do in class are math calculations, which make up a small part of the exams you take. There are 3 exams in total. He does post practice exams, but he makes you use a lockdown browser to take them?? Avoid!!

Honestly the professor is not that bad the only reason he's getting a 1 star is because he is not consistent with his tests. The first exam was 90% the same as the study guide. So you would assume that's the best way to study. Nope the next two exams are completely different and doesn't match his teaching style. If u can avoid it's better

Lectures wise were not that great for me. He's ok with grading and he rounded my final grade up after I asked. I would say definitely a good professor to talk to, especially if you are into SC as a career. Be active and ask him for clarifications on assignments and you will be fine (above B). Final was online. Tests are tough, but fair game.

Prof Enio is a nice guy but structures his class like it's the only class you'll be taking that semester. Group projects are intensely difficult with untold strict guidelines and bad group mates, & he does nothing to remedy how lost everyone in the class is. Take only if supply chain is your life passion or if you hate yourself!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Genuinely evil professor, not in the way he interacts with students (he's a nice dude), but straight up just how he grades. Long assignments, terrible group projects esp if you have bad groupmates, and he's really unclear with every guideline. DO NOT TAKE unless this is the only class you're taking that sem or your rlly passionate about schm.

Professor Velazco is a great professor and a really nice guy. He moves through lectures quickly but uses good examples that the class reviews together. He keeps everyone engaged by asking for their thoughts and opinions at random. I'd definitely recommend him, but be ready to work!
He's a great professor who's always available during office hours and genuinely helpful when students need support. He does tend to call on students during class, but overall he's excellent.

He's a tough grader, doesn't give enough guidance on what he wants. For the group project (50% of our grade), not all expectations were spelled out in the syllabus. In a 1 month DOC, there was not enough time to adjust to his expectations. A lot of us were unpleasantly surprised by our final grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: he's honestly the best prf I've had. he's funny, caring, and witty. material was engaging, and even though he's a tough grader, if you stay on top of the work and put effort into it, he's way more understanding. Just show up, join the discussions, and you'll walk away having learned a ton.

Lectures were helpful, but his tests and projects just seemed wasteful. He doesn't go into depth with concepts past what the presentations say, which had useless information that never got brought up again. He wasn't helpful outside of class, and his feedback is awful. He also doesn't care about the test averages (our midterm average was a 67).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Projects are 40% of the grade, so just pray your team pulls their weight. He loves teaching but he won't curve so just be ready for a low test average which is 30% of the grade.
Prof Velazco is super passionate and knowledgeable about supply chain. Lectures are not super helpful, but the group projects are super fun.

Prof Velazco is a super nice guy and is very passionate and knowledgeable about supply chain. His lectures aren't super helpful, and he just repeats the textbook material, so if you need good lectures, this class might not be for you. no final in the class, just three exams throughout the semester. Overall would recommend.

He is a very interesting professor with lots of cool stories and engaging lectures. He is passionate about supply chain and makes his students intrigued as well. There is a lot of work and tests are not easy. There is lots of textbook HW, multiple written assignments, 3 tests, and a project. It is a lot of work but you will learn a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: I really like him regardless of how others may feel. He is strict but wake up people, it's an upper level college class in a tough major, it's not supposed to be a breeze. His class is a great refresher from my other classes that are just Exam 1 + Exam 2 + Final. There is a massive variety of projects, case studies, and other interesting content

He's awesome! I love that he does many things not just tests. There are many different projects and such. He also always has good stories about working as executive in the real world so it is not just lectures and textbook

Many of the reviews tell you to avoid him because of his harsh participation. However, I believe this is not the case. Professor Velazco provides ample opportunities for you to participate and is really just looking to see if you are engaging the classroom. As long as you put in an honest effort, you will do great things in this class!

Professor Velazco is a wonderful professor who spices things up with a wide variety of class activities. There are several different group projects, case studies, papers, and readings that make his class much more exciting than an average lecture heavy, textbook-only course. He is a tough grader on participation though, so speak up more!

Beware of the participation grade at the end of the year. It dropped my grade very low. For the case presentations, his rubric is unclear and he's a harsh grader. His tests were very difficult and didn't match his material. His exams were online with lockdown browser but it was still difficult and the class average was low but he didn't curve it.
I would recommend taking Enio's class. Slightly disorganized and vague in his requirements for papers and projects, but for the most part if you put in decent effort you will do well.

I was doing well in his class, which took a lot of work and effort to be consistent in, but he ruined my grade with participation. Beware you must participate throughout the semester or it will kill your grade by the end of the semester.

This class is based on 3 tests, that are not related to the LONG lectures given by the professor. All the projects are impossible to get a 100. He is passionate about the class, but his way of teaching is of the 20th century. If you want to learn with a mediocre grade take this class, if you want a good grade avoid it!

This class is hard. Everyone I know, regardless of professor would say SCHM is hard. The exams are also very difficult to get the grade you want on. Despite this, the professor is still amazing. He is very passionate and makes lectures fun. Of all the SCHM professors, I would recommend Velazco the most.

Nice guy but very difficult class. Could've gotten a B+ but the prof refused to bump my participation grade 5% despite actually participating in class. Most of your grade comes from the three huge tests that are throughout the semester which are confusing and cover content that was not gone over during class. Do not take SCHM with him.

He is a nice guy, it was his first semester at school. I learned a lot because I read all the chapters, not necessarily from the class. The major problem was that the exams were extremely confusing and hard.
The material taught in class didn't match what was on exams. It was difficult to prepare for exams given the differences between the information in the textbook/slides versus what was on the exams.

I defended my dissertation with no revisions! Committee says she is a qualitative chair with a quantitative mind, exceptionally sharp and analytically precise. I had a very difficult committee member, and she addressed the situation directly and effectively. She is highly organized, straightforward, and tells you exactly what needs to be done.

Amazing Chair! She tells you how she works before accepting you, is very consistent. That said, she's very selective about who she agrees to work with. If she agrees to work with you, you're a lucky winner. The committee respects her, and having her as your chair makes everything go more smoothly.

Dr. Elias is awesome! She's such a chill, friendly, and nice doctoral chair and professor. She's super organized and keeps things consistent. Plus, she's down-to-earth and easy to talk to. With her experience, you know you're getting honest advice. I highly recommend her! She's the best!

I had the privilege of working with Dr. Elias as my doctoral professor and chair. She is an exemplary doctoral professor and dissertation chair who displays exceptional care, expertise, and dedication to her students. She fostered a supportive learning environment, provided invaluable guidance, and genuinely prioritized students' well-being.

MY CHAIR! I just love Dr. Elias, she is the kindest most understanding faculty member I have dealt with during my dissertation process. She is very easy going and provides precise directions. She is well respected by the committee and very knowledgeable about data analysis processes and coding.
Best dissertation chair. She is smart and very good with Interpretative Phenomenology and coding process in general. Good chair/ committee member, committee respects her opinion.
Excellent dissertation Chair, easy to deal with as she provides great direction, guidance and knows how to align your study.
Simone is very polite and all students adore her. I wish she could teach all Edd courses. She is a very bright professor.

Dr. Elias is an excellent professor. She is accessible, understanding and very inteligent. She has been  my favorite professor in the doctoral program and a very popular professor. She is very thorough and consistent, students trust her and want her to chair their dissertations.
Simone is intelligent, yet very humble about her knowledge. Everyone loves her and her courses are very popular. You'll learn a lot in her classes.
Simone is wicked smart, intelligent, and very patient with her students. She is a great adviser and the best prof I had at NU.
Just heard that Dr. Elias left NU I had her for a DS class and she was my friend's advisor. I can only say good things about her ways of teaching, very intelligent and happy person.
Simone is a wonderful professor. She is extremely nice, and she gives great feedback.

Friendly, happy, funny, intelligent, warm, neat, caring... gotta love this professor.  She makes everything enjoyable to work on. Super nice woman,  definitely one of the best professors that I've ever had.
She's wicked smart and my favorite professor.
She does a lot of reviewing and really makes sure you understand the material. I would highly recommend this class to everyone!
She helps you to be a better person. You learn a lot from her, she is always happy and is very organized and smart.
Simone is awesome!  3
Dynamic and happy professor - Would take again.

Mrs Eilias is a good person with a good heart to help her students to be the best they can be, she is able to get something complicate and make simple to understand- Only northeastern professor I will miss after graduation probably the only one that replies e:mails, knows y name and stops to say hi and ask how y are doing when she sees you around.
Best professor @ Northeastern.

Simone rocks- and I plan to take all my electives with her because she is a kind, smart, and happy professor that helps you learn without getting burned out- you actually use what you learn in her class in real life.

She is an A+ prof! Her syllabus can be intimidating ( 25+ pages) but the way she structures the course really makes sense at the end and I never felt overwhelmed. You need to work and meet her deadlines, but she is always willing to accommodate students and help! Highly recommend. She is the best.
SUPERB teacher!!

Simone is very approachable, intelligent and has a pleasant personality, everyone loves her.  Once you are her student she will always help you. I am not even taking Portuguese anymore bc my schedule does not allow but I often visit her.  Most organized professor I know at Northeastern.

She is phenomenal, just an excellent professor  I even don't like languages but I like her class. I like the logical manner  she presents the material and how she is always happy, it makes you want to go to class, she is very knowledgeable smart and intelligent.

Nicest and coolest professor I have ever had!! She knows how to teach portuguese and she makes good emphasis on accent. If you are willing to learn, you will certainly and easily will. TAKE THE CLASS!!
Simone is the BEST!!!!!!!
She is spectacular. I also went to her dialogue to Brazil, just amazing.
She is very clear and the most organized professor I've had. Very smart.
SHE IS THE BEST!!!!!!!!

She is simply the best professor at Northeastern. Period. She is a good person, very intelligent and an amazing professor. That kind of professor you will remember your whole life. I graduated and I am still in touch with her. She is happy to offer good advice and love seeing her students succeed. I highly recommend.

Simone is a very understanding professor. I had several personal challenges during the semester and she was there for me. I even felt I did not deserve it but she is so kind. She is not only the best professor at Northeastern but also the kindest person I know.  Very smart and intelligent woman too.
Best professor at Neu ! Would recommend her to anyone who wants to learn portugues!

She is organized and business-like. I am an engineering major. Tons of business and engineering majors in her classes.  Learned a lot without the stress. No bad surprises in her class she walks the talk. 5 starts.

Best professor at nu. Her class fills fast, very fast. She was kind enough to meet with me and help me to figure out what I want to do after I graduate. Great mentor. I will declare a Portuguese minor bc of her.  NU should hire more professors like her.  She goes above and beyond to help you with everything not only Portuguese.
I LOVE  her!!!!! She is awesome. Will take her again.

Simone is amazing. She is there for you, you will see that she loves what she does and it reflects on her teaching and the way she treats everyone. All grades are posted within two days. Get to know her and she will help you beyond Portuguese. She was the only professor at NU that took the time to help me succeed and to find my passion.

She helped a lot to find job and my differential after graduating was that I learned Portuguese with her.  I recommend her.  Wonderful woman, smart, and down to earth. Her class is engaging too. Take her!

I like mrs. Elias. She takes the time to get to know you and to help you. She is fun and smart. She gives tons of feedback and chances for you to do well.  She wants you to learn Portuguese and if you follow her advice you will. At the end pays off. She is a good mentor too, if you are interested in brazil she knows a lot about it.

Literally best professor I've ever had. I'm probably going to put off graduation just to minor in portuguese. She is the most inspirational awesome professor I've ever had! Take portuguese with Simone!!!
I love this teacher! She is always happy and nice. I recommend her. She goes above and beyond to help you with co ops and everything not only Portuguese. She is very kind too.
BEST PROFESSOR EVER. IF YOU WANT TO LEARN HAVE FUN AND BE WITH A NICE AND BRIGHT WOMAN TAKE THIS CLASS.
She is so nice :) She is smart and kind too. I recommend this teacher. I learned a lot with her. I will miss her classes this semester!
She is the BEST!!!!!  I like her modern teaching style. I only take Portuguese because is with her !!!!!

Simone is great, organized and fun.  Answers emails or Facebook msgs in lighting speed. I took a hybrid.You can call or text her too if you have questions. She is always smiling in a great mood, knows everyone’s names. Class goes very fast and you don’t want to leave. She usually starts with conversation and ends with music or movie. I recommend!

Love her class, adorable professor.  I took all of my electives with her. Her classes fill fast because she is a fantastic professor and  human being. She helped me not only with Portuguese, but also with job applications. She is well connected to help you to get a co-op. I can say that I am fluent in Portuguese now, and have a job because of her.

She is a lovely intelligent and smart professor. Knows her stuff. Very approachable and down to earth  type. She has high expectations for you to speak Portuguese, and provides tons of feedback. Very active on using Blackboard. Just be sure to pay attention on due dates. Did I say she is the best dressed professor at northeastern ?

She is a great teacher, extremely helpful and fun. She will be with you all the way helping you with anything you need. She is a wonderful person as well and understands that to teach dosen't mean insert this content here and get a grade, but instead she encourages you to find your own way. SPLENDID TEACHER, DO IT!
What do you want other students to know about this professor?

Im here to defend her, shes just a silly middle aged woman. if uve taken calc before this class will be chill but do not take her class if uve never seen derivitives in ur life, she will make ur calc experience very unfortunate. but if u do the hw and go up to the board for extra credit its fairly easy to get a good grade.

Just spits out textbook definitions on the board, then does examples where she skips steps. Very hard to follow and understand in class, especially since her handwriting sucks. If you ask a question or clarification, she will belittle you. Quizzes are nothing like the hw or examples and she truly does not care about helping you succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Do NOT take this professor unless you are already very strong in math.
The class moves extremely fast and feels geared toward students who took AP Calc, not beginners

Plus, her Office hours are only available on Tuesdays, which didn't help at all.

If you're new to calculus, I strongly recommend choosing a different professor.

Worst "Professor" I have ever met in my entire life. RUN. She doesnt care about her students. She just copies the textbook on the board. She is NOT a teacher. Petition for Northeastern to access the quality of teaching.

Very frustrating teacher. Makes things much much more complicated than they need to be. A lot of self study is necessary, do not try and do this class based solely on the lectures, you will fail. I felt like we were playing the course on a harder difficulty compared to the other classes with no reward - no curve, no benefit, just a worse grade.

TLDR, Professor Wilson is EASILY the worst professor I have ever had at Northeastern. She was consistently extremely rude to students asking simple questions, gave very little partial credit, spent the entire class doing examples without explaining how to actually approach them, and was incapable of returning graded quizzes within a week.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Holy yap bro my grandma can explain a basic derivative better than good ol Elizabeth Wilson. Truly, I say you can sleep in class and learn just as much because it will be NOTHING. Notes and examples? Forget about it! You will spend more time deciphering this ancient language than learning. For my final trick, she will make your emails disappear!
do not take her unless u hate yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Created one of the worst learning environments I have ever encountered. Shockingly rude to students in class asking questions, moves WAY too quickly through material. I think she assumes students learn the material on their own and then attend class to revise, as she does not teach any material and rewrites her past notes on chapters on the board.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Pain in the A$$

God Awful. Rude to students, handwriting a mess, everything is a mess in her class, takes forever to grade things and she leaves her students incredibly unprepared for any of the quizzes she gives. Office hours are a joke, and she will not help you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She writes the textbook on the board. That's it. She doesn't really do much more to help you understand it, and she is a little bit hostile towards questions. It's not impossible to learn under her, but you better already have a great grasp of mathematics. Her grading is actually pretty lenient and she gives extra credit so it's not hard to do a B.

This is genuinely the only review I've written but I needed to come and say: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR. She is terrible. She messed up my make-up final (I had a time conflict), making me lose 20 minutes of time. She doesn't explain anything and expects you to simply connect the dots by yourself. Thinks her class is the most important thing ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I prayed I would get hit by a car on the way to class. She is rude, especially if you ask a question. She will make you sound like you're an inconvenience and that your question is stupid. She held office hours, but be prepared for her to cancel them all the time and not reschedule. She won't help her students at all when preparing for exams.

If you are interested in taking Egyptian hieroglyphics take her. I spent more time deciphering her writing as she read off a script than understanding in class. Her support is terrible so if you end up with her as your teacher .RUN. do not take this class.

I cannot emphasize enough that you should avoid taking this course with this professor. Transfer to a different professor if you value your GPA/grade in this class at all. I got a B in this class while being well above average on all of the exams. Based on the exams the average grade in the class was a 74-75. No scale or curve on anything. AVOID.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tests weren't curved and were graded harshly, many dropped the class. The midterm averages were 64 and 69, the final was 76, all unscaled. That left a class average of 74. A friend in another Calc 2 section had midterm avgs of 85 and 87. It felt nearly impossible to get an A. I scored well above average on the final and a midterm and still got a B.

Horrible lecturer. Would just go over a few practice problems, refuse any questions and make no attempt to connect with the class in any way. Please do not take her if you would like to maintain a high GPA. I did well in her class but it came at great personal investment of my outside time. If math isn't your strong suit, avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very disconnected professor. Lectures consisted of her reading of the textbook bar for bar with 4-5 example problems scattered throughout the lecture. Did not like answering questions. Very fast. Quizzes were sometimes very different from the homework.

Professor is very fast-paced and and not empathetic toward students in any way. Class averages for both midterms and the final were in the mid-70s, yet no curves or grade boost opportunities. she doesn't care much about whether students succeed or not in her class. Would never recommend unless you're very confident in Calc 2 material.

Professor is very fast-paced and not empathetic toward students. Class averages for both midterms and the final were in the mid-70s, yet no curves were offered or grade boost opportunities. She doesn't care much about whether students succeed. Would not recommend to anyone unless you're very confident in Calc 2 or it is your only choice.

AWFUL TEACHER! Lacks empathy, speeds through lectures, inaccessible via email, does. not. care. about the success of her students. The course itself isn't that bad, but GET A DIFFERENT PROFESSOR. Went to every class, took notes & did hw, ended with a C+. No one in the class got an A on the second midterm or final. no curve.

Got 100% on my final and a B+ in the class. Does not respond to emails. Unreliable in terms of getting papers back. I genuinely tried my best to enjoy this teacher and use her office hours, upon her other resources, however her lack of patience and with how she carries out her teaching, I cannot recommend her class unless you want your GPA to drop.
Genuinely the worst teacher I've had at this school, doesn't give good feedback, rude in emails and in person. Best grade on the final was an 89.5 while the median was in the 70s. DO NOT TAKE HER

Horrible. Stay away if you want to save your GPA. It is impossible to get an A (93). Final Exam highest score: 89.5, Exam 2 highest score: 91, mean and median in 70s. Exams total to 80% of your grade. "My students do well on the final". HAHAHAH. Absolute joke of a class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Elizabeth Wilson is one of the worst people that could enter your life. She brags about putting trick questions on exams, is a poor lecturer, assigns an absurd amount of homework and gives poor grades out like candy to children. I regret taking her class with every bone of my body. I hope she can reflect on her actions, and change for the better.

If you get her, DROP IMMEDIATELY! She is one of the worst teachers I've ever had to experience. In my class, we've had 60s as our average exam grade with NO curve. Around half of the students had dropped after the first exam, and she's accepted that with no regard. She's rude, inconsiderate, and doesn't care much about her student's well-being.

i took calc 2 in high school but decided to retake it and she overcomplicated EVERYTHING. she was a horrible lecturer and would basically just word vomit the textbook as the lecture notes. her handwriting is also horrendous and she's always rushing through the lecture. the homework takes forever and her tests are way harder than the class examples.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Rushes through all lectures and gives weekly quizzes. Mandatory graded homework. Strict grader of all quizzes and tests. Horrible at teaching, you are better off self studying. Bad attitude as well and will berate students in person and over email. Avoid at all costs

Horrific lecturer. Notes are copied directly from her notebook with little to no explanation. Basic concepts are overcomplicated. Quizzes and exams are unpredictable and grading is not consistent. Clearly does not care about student's success. She also has an bad attitude and has publicly berated students for asking questions. Avoid if you can.

Professor Wilson is always rushed, never leaving time for questions during or after her classes. It is impossible to get in touch with her, either through email or office hours. During exams, she publicly berates students about questions they may have, after telling us to come up to her if we have any questions.

Lectures read directly from the textbook and are explained poorly. You can tell she's too intelligent to teach a Calc 1 class. If you never took calculus prior to this class DO NOT TAKE she overcomplicates a lot of things you won't reach until Calc 2 or 3. Weekly quizzes with strict grading, offers extra credit on quizzes if you do homework.
Avoid at all cost. Homework questions are nothing like test questions, lectures do not prepare you adequately for tests and quizzes. Inadequate resources for quizzes and tests provided.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's pretty much just all around terrible. She doesn't actually teach she just writes her notes off of her notebook and never explains any of it.

Very caring and respectful, but as an instructor is not great. I went through the textbook verbatim after every lecture to try to understand what she covered. She gives extra credit on the next quiz if you go up to the board and do a hw problem. If you make a plan of what you need to work on, it's totally possible to get a good grade in her class.

do not take her. you will hate your life. she does not care about her students one bit, but once something happens to her she will drop everything to take care of her business. switch or drop before you have the worst semester of your life

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. Elizabeth has zero passion and is a horrific lecturer. Posts misleading/unclear review on canvas, and makes tests on different topics than what was on review.
