Sneaks in future topics in quizzes, and does not announce to class. Writes for hours in lecture with 0 explanation. Drop/switch if you can, Avoid class at all costs!

Awful prof. Doesn't care for students if they are sick, unclear when answering questions, and has no regard for the students not doing well. Goes by "department rules" w/ no exceptions. WORST handwriting. Mean on exams will be in the 60s and no one will get an A and still refuses to put a curve. Unpredictable w/ quizzes/exams.

Get ready to learn off lecture notes & videos. Lectures are boring & bad. Terrible prof who doesn't care about students at all. Don't know why this person is a professor, stay a researcher if you can't teach or care for students, choose a different field. Sometimes brings attitude to students. On top of all that, tough grader for such mid lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very good professor. Personally, I wasn't very good at Calculus but I'm glad I walked out with an A from that class, so she definitely had a role to play there. Lots of homework that can get tedious, but honestly, it is worth it in terms of getting the concept through to you. Weekly quizzes (with extra credit sometimes). Has good tips for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Wilson made Calc 2 very accessible, even with the fast paced class. She does a lot of example problems in class and holds a lot of office hours where she is very available to help students.
TONS of mandatory homework that takes hours to do. Mediocre lecturer, moves very fast. Did not prepare us for the final, as it was very different from every practice test we had.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great first impression when she showed up late to the first day and immediately started speed writing on the board (as she does every lecture, copying from old repeated lecture notes). Pay full attention as she's superspeeding or you'll miss the concept. Sometimes moody and rude. Lucky she has a YouTube of her old Zoom meetings so u can self study
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She was great for 1st half of Calc3, but for the other half (the hard half), it was really bad. She talks so fast that you have to relearn everything at home. The lecture examples don't help at all with the homework/quizzes/tests. She tries to challenge us in the quizzes/tests but I truly feel like she never prepared us for said challenge.

Professor Wilson writes and talks as fast as she can during the lectures, and she doesn't care if her students understand. Her handwriting is hard to read and understanding her lectures is also very difficult. She is inaccessible outside of class and the quizzes and tests are way more difficult than the classwork and homework assignments.

Dr. Wilson is very knowledgeable. Lectures and class expectations are clear, but she is inaccessible outside of class and unreceptive to student feedback. I struggled in this class because the exams were significantly harder than homework & lecture examples, students were expected to fend for themselves. I would not recommend taking her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Wilson is a caring person with a lack of teaching ability. She underprepared her students for every assessment. She did not provide ample study materials for any of the tests (save for the final) and the questions asked on quizzes and tests were significantly harder than those found in the textbook's homework and in-class examples.

Left for two weeks for a "medical absence" and left a TA in charge. Came back for a week and spent roughly a day or so reteaching the material. To say the least, this was insufficient. Exams do not match the class work and the class work does not help complete the homework. If you would like to teach yourself a math course take it with prof. Wilson

Inaccessible outside of class, poor lecturer, and generally kind of rude to students. My grades drastically improved when I stopped going to class and started just working through the textbook. I think a research professor who has to teach to keep her funding. Definitely not something she seems to care about much. Avoid if you can!

Dr. Wilson is the best professor I have ever had. I feared calc II prior to taking Dr. Wilson, but her expressions of concepts and her examples for every unit are incredibly clear. Calc II has been my favorite course so far because of Dr. Wilson, would take the course 1 million times over again.

She tends to move quickly in lecture, but it is a very fast pace class in nature. It is sometimes hard to get individual help, as she tends to only explain things one way and has very busy office hours which can sometimes turn into another lecture. Very average.

She will give you plenty of resources to study and is readily available to help students that are confused. Keeping up in class can be difficult as she writes and talks very fast. Overall she's a good teacher but if you have trouble initially grasping concepts make sure to utilize the past recorded lectures and other resources or you'll fall behind

Honestly, while I wasn't fan of professor Wilson at the start of the semester she is actually a pretty decent teacher. The class is work heavy, homework each week and a quiz, but if you work hard and, most importantly, stay on top of the content, you'll do fine! She's also very patient and it was a nice classroom environment

Professor Wilson is someone who keeps up to schedule and updates her students throughout the semester. Examples are up in advanced to keep up with the lectures and the quizzes and exams are pretty straightforward. The only issue is if you're someone who needs to reach out to your professors, that may be the only drawback, otherwise she's great.

Her teaching style is writing the theories on the board then showing different examples. I found it helpful to come prepared with questions on the homework/reviews. She's not very accommodating with quiz remakes and your final grade is largely made up of quizzes/ exams. Attendance isn't mandatory and she has her past lessons posted on youtube.

Professor Wilson is good at math but not a great teacher. However she gives extra points on quizzes if you go up and do homework problems on the board which ended up being the reason I got an A in the class. If you struggle with math, don't take her. If you tend to teach yourself anyway, Professor Wilson is a good professor for that.

Professor Wilson knows what she is doing, but sometimes is difficult to understand. However, if you take notes during class and do the weekly homeworks which are not that long, then her tests and quizzes are easy. The questions are exactly the same concepts in the homeworks, just switched a bit.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Wilson is a decent calc professor. Lectures are fast, but she always lets you ask questions. Come to class w hw done so you can get extra credit on quizzes by doing a problem on the board. Very fair - drops lowest two quizzes (weekly quizzes) and hw can be turned in late for full credit. If you need to contact her, she never answers emails on time.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I was kinda surprised by the negative reviews here. I think prof Wilson is such a good teacher. I wish I had her for Calc 1. She is a tough grader but offers extra credit, and I am never surprised by what is on a quiz or test. She is very routine about lectures and answers questions very well without being condescending. I love her teaching style!

Attendance is not mandatory but if you answer questions on the board you can get extra credit on the quizzes that happen each week. Hard to reach outside of class besides office hours. Can be overwhelming and doesn't speak in a way that is easy to understand.

Prof. Wilson likes to give weekly quizzes and nightly homework. Pros: extra credit and hw grade is for completion. Cons: she regurgitates the textbook, can't explain topics well, tests are 80% of your grade, and if you want to meet with her to go over examples, better be lucky enough with your schedule to attend office hours. She is not flexible.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Prof Wilson gives quizzes every week, usually not that challenging, but the class is mostly graded on the midterms and final. Gives opportunity to get extra credit on quizzes and drops the two lowest quiz grades. Homework is assigned every class, but she gives the whole semester to turn it in full credit. DON'T BUY THE BOOKS. Overall good teacher.

Wilson is a really good teacher, which isn't something I can say about all my professors at Northeastern. She is excellent at explaining things and shows plenty of practice exams to study from. Know that the quizzes make up a chunk of your grade, and you have to stay up with the homework for those. 10/10

I'm not sure why there are so many negative reviews on Dr. Wilson. I had her for Math Fundamentals of Games and she was honestly the best. Her style of teaching makes it a lot easier to grasp topics in linear algebra, as well as put them into practice. She responds very quickly to emails and always values her students' opinions on deadlines.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I have taken her class multiple times and you will get a good grade if you pay attention. She reviews all the homework and is very accessible. It's not a class where if you don't listen you can get an A but in all honesty just like paying attention and doing the bare minimum will get you an A.
Easy class, good professor who is clear and has reasonable but simple expectations. Good class, if not a bore.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Wilson is a good math teacher. She teaches clearly and effectively and answers all students' questions as they come. She can be quiet at times, but that isn't too much of a problem. There is a quiz every week and the final is 40% of the final grade. Overall, good teacher, good class -- nothing amazing and nothing horrible.

Either she's improved a lot or the other reviews aren't as accurate. I found Dr. Wilson's lectures to be well organized and she is very approachable and friendly during office hours. Homework packets were given out each week which was very convenient and very reflective of tests. Overall, one of the best math professors I've had at northeastern
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Her explanations are not very clear and keeps beating around the bush, which exhibit her lack of proper knowledge of the subject she's teachings. All she does is write on the board from the notes that she carries. Very rarely answers question of students properly. Students generally depend on learning from the text book. Very confusing lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I loved math until meeting Dr. Wilson. She reads directly from the textbook and can't teach or explain anything if she isn't directly reading from the book. The final is 40% of your grade and is way harder than any of the HW and classwork. I don't know anyone who got above a B in this class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: All class every class, she she lectures and barely stops writing on the board. When a student asks a question that deviates the slightest amount from her notes she's unable to answer the question. The lectures are boring, dry, and don't engage the students. Would have switched out but this was the only class that fit in my schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: While Wilson is a nice person, she is incredibly poor as a professor. Her lectures are super unorganized and she doesn't seem to know the material well evident by her answering questions with "I'm not sure," or "that's just the way it is." Quizzes and tests are based off of practices ones so memorizing those can help, but not to learn material.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Has refreshingly high expectations of students, solid professor, moves very fast in lectures so make sure you pay attention.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you like dealing with unnecessary pain and suffering, then by all means take MATH 1260 with Prof. Wilson. She is super unhelpful, dry in personality, and sets you up for failure. Her lectures are all over the place, and the online class notes that she provides do a way better job of teaching than she does. STAY AWAY! I REPEAT, STAY AWAY!
The tests are exactly the study guides she gives out. Study and memorize it and you'll pass.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: You ever read one of those books where a bunch of kids get stranded in the woods and have to fend for themselves, slowly becoming savages until they've lost all humanity? That's MATH 1342. You will be offered no guidance, leave every lecture feeling more confused than the last, slowly losing all hope and humanity until you eventually just give in.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I was her PhD student for six years. While her own career is impressive, I often experienced her intense and loud frustration or anger over relatively minor issues. Hence, regular communication felt draining and conflict-oriented, not collaborative/supportive. Her advising style did not provide substantial added value for career development.

In my experience, she is highly transactional, often leveraging connections with little reciprocity. While publicly gracious, private interactions can be unkind and volatile. Despite 25+ years in academia and many publications, she has mentored relatively few PhD students to strong career outcomes.

I applied for a postdoc she had advertised as a joint position with Stanford/Princeton/MIT, with time split across institutions. But the offer letter omitted any joint appointment. When I confronted her, she said these arrangements are not guaranteed and it would depend on my “performance”. This is the level of deception she is now well-known for.

Flatters you when she needs something from you. Then, abuses you continuously so that you do not demand your rightful academic favor from her. When challenged, throws fake apologies, without actually doing something for you. Avoid her. There are plenty of genuinely nice profs out there to work with.

Did anyone notice how she scrubbed her site clean - erased all photos of her ex-husband and dropped the “married” tag from CV, but never mentioned being divorced, as if he never existed? If she can rewrite her own past so easily, imagine how effortlessly she can erase students and postdocs from the record: no citations, false rumors, total erasure.

I can't stress enough how crucial it is to stay far away from her. Profs like her thrive on only manipulation and politics, using students/postdocs as stepping stones for their own fame and recognition. The toll this takes on students is often invisible but devastating. Keep your distance. It's simply not worth the damage.

She is the 'missing stair' of signal processing community. IYKYK. People know she's not reliable as a collaborator or advisor and needs to be "managed," but instead of calling her out, they just quietly warn newcomers and find ways to work around her without addressing the problem directly.

Her default management style is intimidation: threats, public shaming, and raised voices. Self-centered and neglectful of her students' needs, she reacts to any refusal of her petty requests with explosive, disproportionate anger. She turns a mere disagreement into a personal emergency, as if her lack of anger management is your problem.

No matter how outrageous her behavior gets, don't waste time arguing with her. She'll just deny everything outright. The best approach is to document everything and file a complaint, or cut off contact with her entirely. Don't bother trying to convince others what kind of PoS she is. Focus on getting out yourself.

Folks, don't be surprised. She has a history of frequently moving from one university to another. Unfortunately, her conduct often creates tension and discord within the department. Once people realize it, no one wants to work with her and students avoid her lab. When she's found out, she typically relocates and repeats the pattern elsewhere.

When I told her I would not continue my thesis under her, guess what was her response? "I need YOUR help to find your replacement." This is the level of cruelty, selfishness, and delusion she is known for. Even students who completed their thesis with her were left without any job, reference letter, or support from her.

God help our department! The university administrators seem completely oblivious to the harm she brings to students. How could they justify hiring someone with a known history as a harasser to students and staff? Decisions like this undermine trust in leadership and place students directly at risk.

shes not a great prof but shes not as bad as people say. definitely plays favorites but if you engage shes a good grader. classes are super boring and content is super easy, just a lot of random and sometimes tedious assignments. she just wants you to try and kind of seem engaged.

In Comparative Politics, she said "all countries strive to be democracies." Instead of comparing political systems she should be comparing her understanding of politics to that of a rock. Try to guess who'd know more.

Way too many assignments, and they were always graded randomly. Most of the assignments were also super outdated, and her lectures were pointless. Pick a different professor if you actually want to learn.

I am being way too generous in this review, and even I say to NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! She is not cut out to be a teacher and should not be employed. With that being said it is clear that she has passion for political science research and she likes to see people learn and be challenged, however she has absolutely no idea how to do it. Very frustrating.

She doesn't know what she's talking about. All her slides are AI-generated, Has a superiority complex and grades so hard which is so funny because she DOESNT KNOW WHAT SHES TAlKING ABOUT. So many questions she couldnt answer in class, and we corrected her all the time.
Worst professor ever. Please avoid her. I regret all my life choices.

Lectures were dense, loaded with irrelevant examples, + even though I took extensive notes the midterm questions made no sense and were disconnected from things discussed in class. Everyone got C's and D's on it and she did not curve. We also did random class activities that did not explain much. My saving grace was participation. Do not take.

DO. NOT. TAKE. THIS. CLASS. Or anything from Professor Osorio. Avoid at any and all costs possible. Super strict grader, honestly doesn't really know anything about what she teaches, slides could all be made on Chat, has favorites and let's it determine their grades. DO NOT TAKE. WORST CLASS OF MY LIFE. Trust me. Please. I'm begging you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: SOOO BAD PLZZZ NEVERRRRR TAKE THIS CLASS THEY ARE NOT KIDDING ITS SO BAD I TRIED TO GIVE HER A CHANCE BUT THIS COURSE IS EVIL.
Northeastern what are you doing? Please have someone sit in these classes it will take 5 minutes to know that she has no idea what she is talking about.

last year, i had this class in person, dropped it after a week (for all reasons people have stated). BUT i took this class online asynchronous over this summer and it was SO much better and more tolerable. if you have the polisci requirement and she's your only option, i would HIGHLY recommend over the summer asynchronous l

The most valuable thing I learned from this course was how to deal with difficult professors. Material is straightforward, but homework makes it seem like you're in physics, not research methods. She may be on a power trip, and almost gave me one letter average lower than what I got until I called her out. If you don't want stress, please avoid!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I could not deal with this class; the assignments were genuinely so dumb and made to be excessively long for no reason. The material is comparable to a regular AP Psych class, however, she creates assignments as if we are discovering new information that even Albert Einstein could not comprehend. I don't understand how she is still employed.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I'd rather exit through the window than retake any of the courses she teaches. Either she absolutely hates everything about being a professor, or just genuinely wants to make it as hard as humanly possible. Plus, you miss one class - pray to get at least a C.
This professor does not like her students. She is not very knowledgeable and is incredibly rude in class. You get the idea she hates teaching. Do not take this prof unless you absolutely have to.
This professor does not like her students. She is unlikable in every way and very rude to students in class.

Professor Osorio failed me at the end of the term for “excessive absences” but never informed me. I had to involve the director and the dean for her to change my grade because she refused to do so. When asked for attendance records, she couldn't produce them. She wanted to fail me because she thought she could. BEWARE OF THIS PROFESSOR.

Throughout the semester the material covered in class was straight from the homework. She changed the grading system within the last 2 weeks dramatically dropping everyones grade, I don't think anyone benefitted from it. Doesn't respond to emails only makes vague canvas posts. Only reason I got an A was because I participated in class.

Always tries to be the devils advocate. Literally so unlikeable as a person but a horrible professor on top of that. Grades so hard for no reason I think its some kind of power trip honestly. Do not take with her unless you have to. About to cook her on the trace evaluations ngl.

Do not take Eliza for any class if you actually want to learn and value stability in a teacher. Her grading criteria is unclear and her syllabus changes every five minutes. My grades fluctuated between 70 to 95 in the last week of class, despite getting above a 95 on everything. Very unorganized, makes up facts and all her documents have typos.

this is one of those few teachers in life who make learning miserable. She should quit. I didn't do bad in her class but within the last three weeks she kept changing the grading system. dropping most kids from high nineties to an A- or a B+. She was unorganized and provided limited resources. It's easy material but she ruins it

11 days before i submitted my final I had a 97%. TThen she decided to use a point scale system, and change it after the final was submitted. In the old system, (the one we had for the entire semester), i would have an A or maybe an A-

Grade dropped a letter in 2 days (despite no new submissions or actual content changes) bc she updated the SYLLABUS grading system multiple times @ end of semester. After classes were done. Will likely change it AGAIN. First sign should've been when: the midterm was out of 22, got no feedback, curved, changed scale to 50.16, then uncurved.

She completely dipped out of class for four weeks with no explanation. In the meantime, we had a guest lecturer from another university that taught me the only things I'm going to take away from this course. Incredibly vague in assignments and near impossible to reach out of class. Probably wouldn't recommend her to my worst enemy. AVOID!

Take this class if you love professors who take points off assignments for no apparent rhyme or reason, who lower your grade by 16% on the last day of classes, don't reply to emails, don't hold regular office hours, get confused by any question posed to them by students, or seem to hate anyone who doesn't agree with their opinion.

I am so glad I pay 90,000 dollars a year to receive a professor that hates her students and makes up facts based on how she's feeling that day. My favorite part of this semester was when she changed the weight of every grade on the last week and lowered my grade by 15%.

I am a tolerant student. Don't care if professor is nice, or hard, so long as I'm learning. 

But: lectures were dry, verbatim from HW. Projects were vague + graded w/ integers out of…5. 

I did well bc the content was common sense but w/ unhelpful feedback + no email responses, I did not learn anything and would never recommend this professor

Did not show up to class for four weeks straight, changes the assignments and grades on a whim, gets mad at us for not doing assignments she never even posted, and frankly overall just looks at all of her students with clear disdain. Its like she truly doesn't want to be here, and certainly doesn't care if we perform well. Not worth it don't do it.
0 material for exams review. Heavy and confusing poll questions popping up in class.

He somehow manages to move too quickly in class to follow and also be at least 1-2 lectures behind all other sections. Constantly having to use my friend's professor's slides just to finish the labs. He's quite passive aggressive and it just feels like a chore to have to be in this class.

I regret waking up every morning because I will have to suffer this class. He rushes through lectures and is passive-aggressive when students ask questions, yet we are still behind the other classes, which makes labs impossible. We just covered Lab 1 material...we're in the middle of Lab 3. For the exam, max 10 letters PER INCH on the cheatsheet.

Tests are beyond fair. Our first exam was so incredibly easy I couldn't believe it. I saw the work in my friends who are taking a different professor for this class and it seemed MUCH harder. He can be condescending and will call you out if you don't pay attention in class, but it's just because he wants you to learn. Would definitely take again.

Professor Aboelela is one of the best professors I've had at Northeastern. He is caring and understanding and can explain topics really well. He might throw comments at students who aren't paying attention and be a little sarcastic, but if you are someone who can take jokes, you will absolutely love this side of him. Highly recommend him!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very great professor, Dr. Emad is passionate about his lectures and insures his students receive the help they need if asked. Would take again.

Like most COE coding classes, it was easy if you already know some coding, and probably horrible if you didn't. Very passionate about his lectures but they can still get a bit dry at times. Exams were quite easy and the labs are pretty easy as well (and the reports are a joke). Plus all lab stuff can be split with a partner.

Prof. Emad is really caring. He puts learning first instead of giving too much homework, which is one of the best things about him. If all teachers used his approach, the COE would be a lot better, and students would learn more without just struggling to finish homework. He's simply the best.

Professor Emad is highly regarded for his teaching abilities. His lectures are so well-structured and engaging that it's difficult not to grasp the subject matter. He is, without a doubt, among the best in his field.

Amazing professor. His in class slides are detailed and easy to learn from. He goes over many examples to ensure students understand the concepts. Midterms and homework are both VERY fair. He is also just a great guy and cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Nice professor with detailed slides. Even though the attendance is mandatory, he is still a good professor. Homework is resonable and useful.
Easy exams, easy homework. Class participation is quizzes in Poll everywhere that no matter if it is right or wrong.

I thought he taught the course well. It's clear he's passionate about the subject, and knows a lot about it. The grading is generous in my experience and I wouldn't hesitate to take one of his classes again in the future.

I don't understand all the hate for Emad. Had him for embedded, and I thought his lectures were interesting, and that he really broke everything down logically to make it easy to understand for the students. Visited office hours with him a few times, it is evident that he cares a lot about his students and gets excited when we are learning!
Good as far as EECE professors go

Spends 2 hours lecturing and often does multiple chapters in one day, there is practically no practice material and what little there is will not help you for tests. Lectures are boring and it is incredibly hard to know what is important and what is filler. Does not prepare students for exams and is then confused when the class average is a 50.

Took ds4420 w/ gerber, NOT EASY, we did unfortunately have both a final and a final project, but he graded fair, was actually pretty lenient on the final exam(deserved a 30 and got a 75), and overall a great lecturer. Also provides study materials, slides nicely organized, everything posted in canvas, and excessive amounts of ec on hw&labs.
GOAT! His lectures are extremely useful for the homeworks and is overall a very nice professor.
Really good professor. I really liked the way he teaches and he cares about students as well.
Dr. Eric Gerber is cares about students. If you can go to his each lecture, I believe you can get an A and don't worry about the final.
he is caring students! don't worry about his DS4420, just follow his each lecture then you will be good in the final exam!
shoutout gerber

Professor Gerber is one of the most dedicated and passionate professors I've had at Northeastern University. Each lecture & its respective material are meticulously thought-out and display the high level of knowledge that he possesses. I also very much appreciate the subtle jokes/wits he throws into the lectures. Great guy, great class.

Gerb is an outstanding professor. Ive never seen a prof put more time and effort into planning out lectures. 

He chooses super fun data to work with: Penguins, Sports, and even Overwatch. 

He was able to immediately give a fantastic answer to every question he was asked during class, clearly knows his stuff.

Bonus points for being adorable.
Chuddd.......

Orgo 1 and 2 are very difficult classes and she covers a lot of subjects super quickly in order to fit them in. If you pay attention in class and do the homework carefully you'll understand the material. She tests very fairly and will explicitly tell you what will be on exams, also her office hours are super helpful.

She is an amazing professor. She really wants the best for every student. Yes her class is hard but orgo is hard in general. You need to put the work in, in order to do well in orgo in general. She is super nice and wants every student to succeed. Please take her and enjoy her class.

All the negative reviews are just from people who did bad in her class. Organic Chemistry is a hard class and is known as a weed out class for a reason. Her practice exams are very similar to the exams and there is a decent amount of practice. Her tests are pretty hard but fair for the content of organic chemistry. She cares and is accessible.
Retaking this class with a different professor and its going so much better AVOID
She's really good at explaining the material in a digestible way. Even though OChem is a hard class, if you put in the work for her class it really pays off.
Ferris provides way too many materials rather than focusing on a single textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are really good and make a lot of sense. Very accessible outside of class with office hours and TA's hosting recitation sessions. Biggest recommendation is finding worksheets online from other teachers and practicing those cause the aktiv PS are not at all like the tests. Orgo is bad no matter who u take it with.

Exams are impossible difficulty our averages for them were very low. Everyone who says we didn't dedicate enough be for real we have other classes that are also difficult I studied for week and half earlier and still did bad.Most of the class  ended with Cs. Other Ochem profs classes had retakes, extra credit, curves and ended with better grades.

Grace Ferris is one of the few Orgo professors who actually wants you to learn the material and do well. Is Orgo hard? Yes, it's a weed out class. But she provides extensive resources to understand the material and truly understand the class.

Very difficult class because she just expects so much compared to the other professors but if you are able to keep up you will leave her course set up so well for further classes. She's very funny/personable and I don't think she deserves the hate she's got on here recently but I will say she isn't super understanding when the class gets hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Ferris was amazing. She made organic chemistry easier than gen chem. Her lecture slides make it so easy to follow and understand the material. All you need to do is read her slides and do the exam exactly how she does in her examples and you'll get an A.

If you wish to have no life,24/7crash outs,live in Snell,no final exam practice,75%of your grade based on 4 exams that look like nothing like the lectures that you sit in everyday, NO EXTRA CREDIT, HW ARE EZ BUT TESTS 20x HARDER, STUDY GUIDES ARE FIGURE IT OUT YOURSELF, “ORGO IS MEANT TO BE HARD” SO SHE CAN SCREW U EVEN FURTHER W NO REMORSE OR HELP
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very over-hatted professor. Orgo is supposed to be very hard and other professors are easier and offer extra credit, but this shouldn't warrant bad reviews for Grace. She is an incredible lecturer and cares how the student absorb the material. If you go to class and study hard, it is very rewarding.

Professor Ferris is really nice and clearly feels passionate about ORGO. That being said, her grading criteria makes it crucial for you to spend a significant amount of time studying for you to do well. It is possible to get an A in the class it's just really really hard. However, this does make sure you really understand the material.

She explains tough concepts well and is always available for help. She's the sweetest and genuinely wants us to succeed. However, textbook/Aktiv problems are not like how she writes them, but that is the primary resource we have for practice. My biggest piece of advice is to study EVERY day. You cannot get by with only studying the week of an exam.

This class is difficult, but its orgo. While it would be nice if she were to allow a test retake, give extra credit, or add fluff to the grade -she really knows how to teach this class. She knows what she is talking about and wants students to understand all the concepts. Her office hours are so helpful and she gives to many resources for studying.

If you want a good grade, don't take her. If you want to actually learn the material then take her. Orgo is generally a tough class and it's unfair that she doesn't give extra credit or test retakes, however if you actually put in the effort and understand how to study then you can do decent in exams but it's really hard to get an A in this class.

Worst class I have ever taken in my life. Such a sweet woman but that's all she is. The homework assigned is so time consuming and irrelevant to the assigned lectures. The midterms are insanely hard and the practice tests she provides are almost nothing like the actual exams. The final was agonizing. Select a better section if you can.

She is such a sweet person, but save yourself. Not generous at all when it comes to exams, they are very difficult and she gives no EC, corrections, or curves. Class averages were usually in the 60-70 range with no curve.
Literally never again. So unhelpful shows no care if you do bad and wtf was the final.
She gives us practice exams which is a benefit but gave us no clarity or guidance on whats on the final.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is a very nice professor and has very clear and engaging lectures. Homework is helpful to solidify topics but is time consuming. The only negative is her exams are VERY difficult and you really have to dedicate a lot of time.

Grace is tough; this is true. If you're looking for an easy class to coast by, do not take her. If you truly want to understand the concepts and feel confident in your abilities, then she is the best possible. Her lectures are clear, and she gives many examples and practice problems. Put in the work, and her class will be rewarding.

I loved Professor Grace! She's a breath of fresh air. Orgo is tough, but she makes it manageable. Go to office hours and stay on top of the work, and the class is very doable. Her tests are fair, but you need to show up and put in effort. I truly gained a mentor. Don't wait until the last week to study! All-nighters will not work for orgo! TAKE HER
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: It starts out great. Then it just gets worse. She's so kind and truly does seem to care about the success of her students, but her tests and grading are impossible. The homework progressively gets harder and harder until it will take HOURS. The tests are completely incomparable to all in class work and homework. Lectures are great, but don't help.

Grace is a very kind person who is clearly passionate about organic chemistry. Her lectures style, practice exams, and Aktiv in-class questions made it seem like exams would be a breeze. Until you find out everything that you thought would be on the exam isn't on it at all and you realize you wasted hours studying to basically fail.
Her lectures are fine, but her exams and homework are awful.
do not take her.
Literally everyone is lying I study for 20 hours like a part time job and still fail.

Grace is an amazing teacher and is one of the best lecturers in chemistry. Her practice exams are exactly like her actual exams. I personally am doing well and learning so much. Compared to other orgo teachers at this school she is amazing. If you are someone who actually wants to put in work you will do well in this class. Disregard cruel reviews.

Everyone who is on here being disrespectful needs a reality check. Just because you cant study doesn't mean the professor is bad. Orgo 1 is meant to be hard. There are chances to improve grade with attendance, homework and practice tests. There are also many outside of class opportunities to get help. If you don't have the time to study dont take.

ok yes she is a good prof when it comes to explaining but her exams are just too hard and unrealistic unless you're just cracked. When I asked her for some advice and comfort, all she said was to review study guide and rest. REST??? I am always at snell and still can't do well. There's so much covered on the exam and not enough time.
You will cry a lot and your gpa will go down. Test grading is insane. No curve. No extra credit.

Lectures are pretty engaging; her way of explaining things was pretty intuitive for me. Pleasant and understanding if you have an issue during office hours. The material is really difficult, but she has it set up in a pretty digestible way. Just be ready to spend a lot of time outside of class on review and practice.

POV im at snell for DAYS on end, stomach grumbling, tired asf, nothing in front of me except my hope and dreams and my ipad obvi. Study sessions are 10+ hours. You take the exam feeling alright, tears in your eyes though. Then it gets graded and you failed. Ya i know this will probs happen to u (sorry) so dont take her class, unless u r cracked..

This professor has given me nothing in life. When I think of life, I immediately think about my life before this class and how much better it was. Mind you this professor went from a 4.1 to a 2.7. I want to change my major because of her. I think my life expectancy just insanely dropped because of this lady because THIS CLASS TOOOK YEARS OFF LIFE.

Beware. If you even want a chance of getting anything above a B- do not take this prof. She grades extremely harshly and does not ever consider another opinion, especially during regrading. She makes up her own "rubric" or criteria for what she wants because of her superiority complex. You will not touch an A in her class, and lots of homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: I got well above average in class 100% in the other categories, and still don't have an A. Other teachers at least curve!!no test corrections, no ability to move up a grade. Study for about an entire week before exams still dont do as well as id like. Doesnt regrades even when I wrote the same thing as the answer key, at least give me one point.

This teacher has a very unfair curriculum with no curve, extra credit, and abhorrent grading. She genuinely grades so hard and expects us to read her mind on tests. Other teachers offer curves and extra credit, I know people in other sections of Orgo 1 that have 100. How is that fair? It's not. I studied 30 hours and did not do as well as I wanted.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This review is for Organic Chemistry 1. The grade breakdown is that almost the entire grade is dependent on the exam grades (3 Exams, 1 Final), with around 10% from other work. If you are struggling with the content, she offers NO opportunity to improve your grade (make-up exams, notecards), which can make it hard to pass the class. Never again.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Grace Ferris is a great person and I appreciate her humor. However, I spend 2 weeks studying for her exams and still don't do that well. Her lecture slides and homework problems are so much easier than the exams. Every single other Orgo teacher gives some sort of grade redemption except for her so keep that in mind when you're picking classes.

2311:  Studied for more than a week and still  did bad every exam. Other ochem professor offers something to help curves, notecards, exam corrections but she gives absolutely nothing. She just reads slides and shows ridiculously easy examples nothing like the exams. Homework is long and brutal, the textbook is useless, and office hours don't help.
Chopped cheddar cheese
changed my major in the middle of lecture. i think that speaks for itself.
I literally purposely stopped in front of the T while walking to class in hopes i wouldnt make it
just run far away

I have shown up to every lecture, I went to office hours, took extra notes from the lectures, and I would still NEVER take this class with her. She reads off the lectures, which you can do at home. The homework is always 40+ questions, and the practice exams never reflect the actual. RUN. I would rather retake Physics 2 than Orgo with HER.

Great professor. I definitely would recommend taking her for Ogro 1 and 2. She wants you to succeed. Orgo is hard to begin with so do not blame the professor blame yourself if you are not putting in enough work. Do the homework, do some practice problems and you will be fine.

I would rather walk through the fiery pits of hell than walk into her lecture. She's a nice professor, sure, but probably the worst professor in ALL of my eons. I never see my home, my family, my life because I'm consumed by orgo. If you can LITERALLY take any other professor, do it. Because if not. ALL of the life in your eyes will become drained.

Please NEVER take her. I never see my home anymore and i am constantly at snell because of this lady. This makes my life 10 times harder and she does not do corrections or retakes so actually makes me want to give up. If you like crying and being miserable take her, but if you like life, don't.

Teaches by literally reading off a lecture for an hour and 40 minutes and expects all students to fully understand everything. Tests are nothing compared to what we do in class and my class averaged 67 and 70 for the first exams. SHE DOES NOT CURVE, GIVE RETAKES, OR ANY EXTRA CREDIT ANYONE WHO SAYS SHE DOES IT LYING. God awful teacher don't take

Grace Ferris is an angel that actually made gen chem as well as orgo concepts make sense, it is not an easy class but she explains the material clearly and in depth. I got a C- in gen chem but am doing well in orgo thanks to her. GO TO CLASS. If you miss even one you will have a VERY hard time. If you work hard you will definitely succeed.

One of the best science professors I have ever had. Lectures were easy to follow along, and she explained concepts and reactions incredibly well. Not much homework, but exams were somewhat difficult. Spend a few minutes each day outside of class rewriting reactions and going over her slides before exams, and an A is very doable. Exam redo is nice.

I loved having Grace as a professor so much! She really cares about her students, and teaches in a way where you really get to understand the material. Her class may be slightly harder to get an A in compared to other ORGO profs, but if you really want to learn the material, taking her class is the way to go. 100% recommend her, best ORGO prof imo
One of the best science professors I've had. She truly explains material well, and her lectures are easy to follow. Exams are not easy, but if you devote time to studying, an A is very achievable.
great teacher but wouldnt round up my grade 0.2% to bring me to the next letter grade.

I absolutely adore this woman. It's obvious that she actually cares about her students and genuinely wants them to do well. I really liked her lectures, her slides were very organized, and she explained things really well. I went to her office hours every week just to absorb knowledge because she was so welcoming and inviting and such good vibes.

Grace is such a great teacher! Super nice and breaks down concepts very well especially if you go to her office hours. Took me after the first exam to get used to her style of exams, but there are no surprises everything covered in lectures is what she tests you on. At the end of the semester you can also retake an exam which helped me a lot
She is a great professor, and honestly should teach every chem class. However, her exams are very specific and challenging. She does provide good buffers tho.

I LOVED Grace. She was a great lecturer and made the material easier to understand. Her PowerPoints and annotations were very helpful. While her tests were hard, she did cover all the material in class but sometimes focused on smaller parts that weren't emphasized in class. Very funny and made the class enjoyable. Would definitely take her again!

Homework/lecture is super easy. Tests are not. Tests are sometimes worded strangely and have topics barely touched upon in class. But, her exam redo policy is very generous and can only help your grade, so A's and B's are very attainable.

Professor Ferris was a really great professor for organic chemistry. Her tests were tough but if you prepare and work hard throughout the semester you can do well. She makes an effort to get to know all her students and even has an exam redo policy where you can retake one exam at the end of the semester

AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Loves the subject, but somehow doesn't realize that he's teaching an introductory level course. Material is scattered and hard to follow. Don't bother emailing him, he doesn't care. Has tests that make up majority of your grade despite having no way to practice the problems or see any previous tests beforehand.

Honestly, run. I was forced to take this class because I need it to graduate soon and it was the only asynchronous section available. For an ONLINE & asynchronous section, he makes it too much work. 1/2 the work you do is practically useless.

P.s. most of the reviews below seem fake. Good luck, and i hope you take this as a warning.

Professor Fombelle is a knowledgeable instructor who knows how to lecture very effectively. He ties his lecture slides into real-world examples and all slides are made by him. He cares about attendance and will call you out for showing up late, but he means very well and cares for all of his students (especially those who show up ready to learn).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This class is the reason I switched my major to marketing, class is never boring, he is honestly a very fair grader, values creativity for projects and just overall effort. Make sure you pick a good group for the projects as its the key to success. I would take this again in a heartbeat make sure to read theres easy pop quizzes when he feels likeit
He definitely expects participation and is very respected in his field. He has lots of real world advice he uses during his lectures which I love.

Paul's lectures were very interesting to say the least. His tone and jokes are pretty entertaining which got me engaged. He can seem arrogant or judgmental to some, but I enjoyed watching lectures and thought his approach was unique and funny. Class comes with a lot of material to remember though, so make sure you're studying for those tests.
If you're taking this class because you think it's asynchronous and easy, then avoid it. Takes a good amount of time and effort, but relatively easy. TA grades most of the work.

Most of the bad reviews are from when he taught the class online a few years ago, ignore them. Paul is a great professor, engages with the class, and there are many times everyone is laughing in his class. It takes work to get an A but it is manageable and I definitely learned a lot. One of the best professors Ive had. Wish he gave makeup tests tho
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Has a passion for what he's teaching, which really comes through in his lectures, and a good sense of humor. Read textbook and engage in class, and you will do well. Tests were all mcq so as long as you read they're easy. Not much graded homework other than group project. Fombelle made 2201 my favorite class of the semester, would take him again!

TA grades almost every assignment; material was clear and easy to understand but his exams were strangely difficult in comparison to what was in the textbook (coming from someone who read the entire thing -- you need to in order to do decent). He's a good guy and clearly passionate about what he's teaching, which makes lectures easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: His lectures were very interesting and you can tell he likes what he's teaching. He does have a bit of an ego but he is funny. Attendance is very mandatory and you really have to take notes on the textbook to do well on tests. He didn't allow any laptops/ipads in class but it was honestly fine. Workload was very manageable too.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment:
Frankly I would take a class by this professor on watching paint dry, and I'm confident I would come away with, if not new insights, an enthusiasm for the topic previously assumed impossible    
Attendance is very mandatory but honestly talk to him about it, he genuinely wants to see you succeed.
Not much homework other then group projects.

Prof. Paul is funny but a little self-centered. He really encouraged participation, but as someone who is introverted, I never felt comfortable answering questions because of his fast pace and sometimes abrasive attitude. The tests/assignments aren't that hard as long as you put in effort. Good lectures. Would take again if I really had to.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Fombelle does not deserve the low ratings that he gets on here! I was originally so worried to take his class due to the Rate My Professor-- but they were very wrong. He is one of my favorite professors and I am going to take another class with him. He is hilarious and genuinely cares.  Seriously, take his class. He is the best.

Tests were terrible - asked really oddly specific questions from book. I did so well on everything else in the class but because tests were brutal and weighted so heavily, I didn't get as good of a grade as I would have liked. Passionate about the material but also a bit arrogant. Part of simulation grade is on performance compared to classmates.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Paul is such a nice guy, but the asynch marketing class was weirdly semi hard. It's not hard to do well, but don't take this class thinking you'll get an easy A because it's intro level. TA grades HW and there are only 3 pretty heft/hard tests. Watch lectures, REALLY read the textbook, try your best on the simulation, and ur all good.
Take literally any other marketing professor and you will be 10x happier

Don't take this guy. Seriously. Any other marketing professor will infinitely more helpful. He grades everything extremely strictly, which for an intro to marketing course is a bit ridiculous. If you want to have a decent GPA stay away.

I took his MKTG2201 asynchronous so I cannot speak on the in-person quality but I definitely recommend. Assignments were creative and fun to do and exams were easy as long as you studied. This was a required course, but he made marketing interesting and a class that I thoroughly enjoyed. Easy A as long as you do your work!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I took Professor Fombelle this year and I loved him. He's a fun and comedic, yet serious, guy and is super caring. While there is a lot of reading, it's pretty much just the textbook. Participation is mandatory but you learn so much in each session it's really worth going to class. Be respectful and he will have respect for you in return.

I took the asynchronous version of Intro to Marketing with Fombelle. Please do not make the same mistake I did. Lectures rarely align with chapter, tests are insanely hard, homework is a waste of time. He makes this subject unnecessarily hard and has completely averted me from marketing. Also decided to assign double the weekly work on a test week
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Entertaining enough that I can listen to lectures without falling asleep

Paul is a really nice prof. I took his course twice, both MKTG2201 and MKTG4502. He is very kind and friendly, and especially fun. The course is kind of hard but I definitely learned a lot and enjoy taking this course.

One of the most unlikeable people you will ever encounter. Brags about how his "success", shows pictures of his boat and cars, etc. I was sick and he got mad at me and threatened my grade because I had to miss class. It creeps me out how different he is outside of class than his nice guy act. 

Have to admit he does give good lectures though.

Professor Fombelle is the best professor at Northeastern. He cares about his students. Getting an A in this class is difficult, but you will learn more in it than with any other professor. He has high expectations for his students, but matches that with high expectations of his own teaching ability. Take this course, you won't regret it.

Mr. Fombelle was the harshest, strictest, most critical professor ever. He assigns exorbitant amounts of homework, grades harshly, and is extremely unaccommodating. Most online professors have open-note exams: not him. He forces students to record audio AND video during exams. I started out excited to learn about marketing, left despising it.

Prof is knowledgeable & experienced, lectures do a good job of summarizing what matters from the book, & if you want to see him outside of class he's pretty accessible. Unfortunately, his attitude is like a high school teacher's in that he takes strict attendance every class, mocks students for minor issues, and grades much harder than is necessary
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The general trend of his class is 1) he will mention he has tenure at least once every class 2) he is so afraid of people cheating on his tests he will literally create so many incoherent questions that don't make sense "to trip up cheaters" that messes up everyone 3) not go over all the questions you get wrong on tests so you can't learn from them
modern day embodiment of a business major - this isnt a compliment
not worth your time
do not take this class with him for the love of god - most people i know dont do well on the tests and that impacts your grade SEVERELY
assign tons of homework and let students figure things out by themselves

He tries to be a good professor and has good experience within the field, but he doesnt actually care about his students like he claims. He is sarcastic and harsh and unhelpful when it comes to actually helping you despite his caring claims.
One of my favorite profs. Super caring and funny. He makes you work for an A but keeps class engaging, especially if you participate. :)

paUL was a great marketing professor. He can come off as intimidating, but he really cares about his students. He makes you put in the work in so don't skip classes and pay attention (he will call you out on it). Overall, I learned a lot and had a funny professor and classmates (1:35PM). Hopefully this will appear on one of his presentation slides
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Amazing professor you won't regret taking his class. He's very understanding and wants to help you succeed as long as you come to class and read the textbook.

Professor Fombelle is one of the most inspiring people youd ever get to meet. 11/10 recommend taking this class: be prepared for your life to be changed. He is so passionate and excellent at what he does and it shows in every class youll take with him. Leaving this class, theres NO WAY you wont want to take a concentration in marketing.
Most enjoyable class I've taken at Northeastern. Fun lectures. Tests can be a bit tough but won't be too much of a problem if you read the textbook.

Warning: This class will change you forever. You will become very intolerable of any bad service / marketing, become an expert complainer to service providers, and always judging how much companies couldve done better. You can never turn it off. And hes not sorry about it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Younger prof but on tenure. Really loves the class and has good experience in it. Definitely need to be engaged in class to get a good grade, but its easy enough. Big consulting project for the Zoo, but its pretty interesting. If you are interested in Marketing in any form of service, highly recommend. Connor's trip at the end of the semester!

Paul Is a great professor. He gives a lot of homework, but the homework makes sense. The syllabus looks intimidating but the work is really manageable. He is also very helpful and will be willing to meet with you outside of class. He makes real world connections to the material and class feels more coproduced as oppose to be lectured at
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Hands down the best professor at Northeastern, no competition. Paul makes every class engaging, content interesting, students laugh - and loves every minute of it. This is the first class at Northeastern I actually never skipped - my attendance record would show that's pretty incredible. Take one of his classes even if you don't have too.

He will likely show this at the start of your sem with him, and frankly, I hope he does. This is my only prof review. Even though I def wasn't his best student, Paul was the best Prof I could've ever had. Challenged me for the better, for that I can't be more thankful. Bring your A game every day, and he will ensure you grow in more ways than one.
Great professor and a very enjoyable class. I highly recommend taking his class, I definitely would again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Best teacher ever. He can be tough and sarcastic but he is a great resource to have on campus. He truly care about his students that show that they want to be in his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: The worst class I ever had. The professor does not care about individual student. Exams are hard even if you read the whole book. If you like to raise your hand in front of 40 students, take his class, or don't. He even would not remember you. And BTW, he will take a screenshot of these commends on rate my professor and discuss in class. Have fun:)
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: I loved Paul so much. I found that students either loved him or hated him. Get on his good side (participate and talk in class) and he will love you too. Easy B, hard A class. Be prepared to do work, although it isn't at all overwhelming. I would recommend Paul to anyone. (dont skip class- he takes attendance)

Good dude. Pretty Funny. Dont show up late, hell be annoying about it same with leaving to go to the bathroom. Other than that really great class, not too much homework just reading the chapters before class and he'll ask you what was covered. Tests are reasonable.

Paul is a cool guy. He will give a few pop quizzes to make sure you are reading. He likes class participation since he gets bored without it. He will get distracted if you get up out of your seat or are surfing the web o your labtop/phone so don't do that. He takes attendance so definitely show up. Tests are easy if you know how to study.

Paul is a great professor. He makes every class relatable by providing a lot of examples  and enjoyable by showing videos and pictures to help teach course content. He is very engaged with the class and acts pretty much like one of the bros. To do well you must read the textbook and show up to class on time-he knows when you don't! Easy B, hard A.
Amazing Professor. He is the most hilarious professor in NU.

I made a rate my professor account solely because of the bad rating Fombelle just got. He is the best professor ever, and every kid I know absolutely loved him. Build your entire schedule around getting into his class. Extremely nice and helpful, and makes the class fun.

Bad? I'm not saying he's a bad teacher. I'm sure he's very good in doing his work but he's very rude and impolite!!! He doesn't respect his students at all. I was so scared when I was in class with him because he was always making jokes about students and treating everyone very badly. Someone liked him; the majority of us don't!

Professor Fombelle is a really funny and charismatic teacher. He is an extremely hard grader, and you would most likely get a letter grade higher taking marketing with another professor. Participation was worth a lot and it was hard to earn. Also, he grades the group projects extremely hard. He is a great professor but a very tough grader

Take him! Tests are do-able as long as you pay attention in class and read beforehand. Participation is very important, as is reading the chapters before class because sometimes he'll throw a pop quiz in there.  He keeps the class interesting, has a great sense of humor, and you learn in an enjoyable environment.
The in-class participation is really ridiculous. The presentation  is hard to get higher than B+.
One of the best professors Ive had at Northeastern. Classes are fun, you never get bored. Read the chapters and you will be fine. Exams are fair and not too hard.

Not a good upper level professor. Pop quizes and his participation requirements are ridiculous, and patronizing to say the least. His tests are also horrible- just memorization of unimportant tables/flow charts from the book. Learned very little in this class. He's also incredibly pompous.

Honestly I believe Fombelle was a great intro teacher but does not understand how to teach an upper level course. Other than the fact that he is pompous and arrogant, he treats his students as if they are freshmen. The reading pop quizzes and a paper stating every time you participate is busy work and not appropriate for junior/senior level course
Lectures are engaging and fun. Never a boring day. Definitely take him if you have the opportunity, one of the best professors I've had at Northeastern

Professor paUL is a gem of Northeastern.  He is totally worth rearranging your schedule for-- do whatever it takes to get him.  Make sure you express your love for Best Buy/hatred for nature/sports in the first class and you'll be golden.

Best Professor. My concentration was Finance, I am now double majoring in Marketing and Finance, only because of him. He is one of those teachers that inspire you with their teaching. Absolutely love his sense of humor and wit! If you get an opportunity to take his class don't miss it!! -- but be willing to work.
Prof. Paul is the BOMB. Tests are straightforward and clear.  His lectures are engaging and he is very relate able.  I can't wait to take another class with him.

actually runs a dynamic, interesting class which most cba professors do not. lots of funny videos/jokes, keeps mood light. tests are totally reasonable, reviews everything beforehand. no homework whatsoever besides the simulation. has a lot of interesting experience and stories - really knows his stuff. 100% recommend

Good Professor. Just read the textbook before class and participate. His exams were very straight forward. He also has you play a fun marketing simulation game. Also, he likes to be impressed by the work you do so be creative.
Not a good teacher.
One of my favorite professors ever. he's a cool guy and you get work done while having fun in class. if you read the book you'll do fine. definitely recommend getting him

One of the best professors I've had! He's young and relatable and communicates the concepts in a way that was easy to understand. His tests are not difficult as long as you take notes during the powerpoints he presents in class. He's big on participation so as long as you show up to class and participate in the discussions, you'll be fine.

He was a GREAT professor and made class really interesting. We did a really fun simulation that took up at least one class a week. Tests were easy as long as you wrote down the answers in the review sessions and knew the vocab from the book. Must participate at least once in awhile.

I had no interest in marketing prior to taking this class, but may consider changing my major after having Paul as a teacher. By far the best professor I have had to date. He is the equivalent to a friend in college that happens to teach the class, but has had an immense amount of experience in the marketing world.   Change your schedule to get him

Professor Huq is a great supply chain professor! You will need to put the work in to do well but it is worth it. He is extremely passionate about the material and has lots of stories to share about his time in his career. I will say, he doesn't update the syllabus so be sure to double check with him if something seems off.

Took 3310. He would constantly give us the wrong information (especially when it came to the algorithm/work problems) and then when we were completing them wrong, would try to gaslight us. He truly believes he's never wrong. Also used outdated textbook slides for his lectures, never replied to emails, and had extremely unclear grading criteria. ugh
This professor is not clear about expectations.

I took Professor Huq's morning SCHM 3310 course. A lot of his information was out of date and just flat out not right. Professor Huq also always thought he was right and could never be wrong. The tests were really weirdly written for no reason, and difficult to understand what he was actually asking. You need to participate a lot if you want an A.

I took SCHM3310 with professor Huq and the class was very enjoyable especially if your interested in transportation. The professor is very knowledgable on the topic and makes the class an engaging discussion based class. Participation is very important to your success. Group projects have a large affect on your grade so you do them well.
run! DO NOT take his class

The class content was really boring but he cared about it. Very knowledgeable in the subject. If you didn't participate he didn't like you. If you disagreed with him, he did not respect that. The simulation assignments take a long time but aren't too hard once you get the hang of it. He curves grades which is really nice. Nice guy, ok professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Took this class online, and it was A LOT of work. Definitely not unmanageable, but probably the most time-consuming async class I have taken at Northeastern. Exams are difficult and heavily weighted (60%). The professor was easy to meet with - hosted office hours twice a week for two hours each and was willing to meet outside of those times as well
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Terrible. His solution sheet for his practice exam will have tiny tiny differences in the question itself making the answer completely different and you have to figure it out, he will not tell you. His due dates are incredibly inconsistent, he will not provide clear guidelines or rubrics, then grade very harshly. The final module also takes 6 hours

Prof. Huq was great! His lectures are interesting, and I enjoy his teaching style. If you read the textbook chapters and participate, the class is engaging, and you will do well on tests. His office hours are helpful. The simulations are time-consuming but enjoyable and a nice break from regular work. I would 100% recommend you take him!
I love Dr. Huq. He's like a father to me.
excited to teach and enjoys supply chain. If you put in any respectable level of effort in participating he will like you

HORRIBLE. I got the highest score for 3 out of the 4 exams but still get an A- because he gave me a 7/10 for my participation grade even when i went to all of his classes. Don't take him if you don't want to ruin your gpa.
Avoid if you can. Not good at all.

He is hilarious and entertaining and clearly actually passionate about the subject. The simulation modules will kill you once you get to module 6 it takes like 5 hours on your first try at least

As long as you put in the bare minimum participation wise he will lowkey love you and you'll probably do well.

Avoid at all costs!! His Canvas page is extremely hard to follow with two or three different due dates for the same assignments. My group never knew when anything was due and he would be very vague in his emails when we asked for clarification. He doesn't grade anything until the end of the semester, so all his feedback is useless.

I have never had a worse professor in life, avoid at all costs. He does not seem to care about students actually doing well in the class and he doesn't know how to teach an asynchronous course effectively. None of his content is original. He doesn't provide consistent due dates. Terrible teacher NOT a professor.

professor huq is a great person and a great professor! he is very engaging and cares about schm a lot. the drawbacks of this class are the group presentations and projects. there is no clear rubric for presentations, and the simulation is ridiculously long (it took my grp around 7 hrs!!) his grading seemed to be relative to the rest of the class

Professor Huq was not a bad professor but he also wasn't good. The class was pretty hard and I didn't think that the material was all that interesting which made it harder. The class was pretty straightforward and if you study for the tests and quizzes you'll do well. The simulation module was super long though so be prepared for that.

Dr. Huq is very knowledgeable, I learned so much in this class. The quizzes (10%) are multiple-choice and pretty difficult (I did fine with the curve). The midterm & final (25% each) also curved. They were easier than the quizzes because they were more problem-based. There is a graded simulation with unlimited attempts. Good to know stats before.

He is a harsh grader who lets you go to his office hours but doesn't explain. He says this is the answer and assumes the students know why. Exams are challenging, and everyone dislikes his teaching style because he reads off the slides. I participated and never missed a class, but still got lower than a 7/10 for participation. Avoid him SCHM2301
2 + 2 = 5 ahh professor
This guy's really giving me a hard time right now
Gets straight to the point, teaches you what you need for the quizzes, cracks a few jokes. Weekly quizzes, optional homeworks
Never graded works, was always late to office hours, and didn't abide by the honor code. Avoid!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you took AP calc in high school (and did well), you don't need the textbook, save the 20 bucks. Professor would sometimes just not to show up to class with no warning, so beware of wasted class time. Lectures are pretty mundane, but all of the examples he gives are exactly what's on the test, so do the problems he provides.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you haven't taken any type of calculus in high school or any math close to calculus, don't take his class. He teaches the class as if everyone in the class has to same level of math skills.  AVOID him as much as possible. No homework is given, just examples from the textbook but it's not much help. Office hours are based on his free days.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I thought he was pretty funny, and also a very caring professor. Can be slow at responding but he does get back to you eventually and is super flexible. If you're sick, he'll let you take the quizzes during OH and is super helpful as well. Overall, his teaching/lectures aren't great but he's a nice professor in general. Personally, I recommend him.

If you want an organized professor and/or have only taken pre-calc, DO NOT TAKE FROM HIM. He was either late or didn't go to our classes. His class is graded on easy quizzes and moderate tests. He doesn't teach all the content on the final, which is why people find it hard. He teaches by doing practice problems and doesn't explain how to do them.

Overall, a chill and nice guy. If you've taken any level of calc before, the class is a breeze. The average on all tests/quizzes is about 85%. He posts lecture notes, which are clear and straightforward, and the assessment problems are basically the SAME as the practice ones. Makes jokes and is approachable outside of class.Take him for bus/econ!!

Not as bad as people make it out to be. You do have to do a lot of learning on your own, but he makes sure that all the resources you need are available to do so. Quizzes very often but im pretty sure thats just the math department. Overall not too hard grading, but just not that good of a teacher unfortunately. Chill dude

He can't teach. He's very uninterested in everything he talks about except personal stories. He is funny, but not forgiving in any way. Doesn't matter if you have problems at home or how sick you are; you must take the exams and quizzes. Doesn't respond to emails. Doesn't explain what he is doing only just goes through examples with no explanation.

Where do I start?I love this professor so much. He is always ready to help if you actually try in this class and are respectful. He is very good at explaining concepts in a very clear and easy to conceptualize way, also he makes lectures fun.Quizzes+exams were extremely fair. I would totally take him again if I had to take another class he teaches.
Was always super late to lectures, cancelled office hours with no heads up ahead of time (No show), and even fell asleep once. STAY AWAY!!

This professor waited to grade 8/11 quizzes/tests in the class until the last week. I went to his office hours throughout the entire term and asked him to grade my work only to get the answer "I'm working on it". Was always 10-15 minutes late to lecture, never responded to emails, and constantly missed office hours without any notice. STAY AWAY!

This professor DOES respond to emails, but many times will not show up to his own office hours. When you do eventually get him in office hours, he can be condescending with comments insinuating we should already know certain material. The issue is that is material he was supposed to be teaching us which he did not do effectively.
Only take this professor if you are willing to learn everything by yourself.

I do not say this lightly Assad Fotovatian is the worst professor I have ever had. I had severe medical issues during his semester, his behavior towards me was so bad (he told me *in writing* my surgery wasn't necessary, and kicked me out of multiple classes) that the school gave me a full refund for the semester. Beyond that, super awful lectures

Class is terrible. The flipped learning setup doesn't work at all. You teach yourself everything. The professors barely explains anything in class and expects you to already know the material. It's completely unhelpful, confusing, and honestly a waste of time. If you actually want to learn, this is not the class or professor for you
Never responds to emails
He is unfair at grading
I am fully convinced that the reviews that all give him 5s are himself writing those reviews
Awful teacher

He is a really sweet guy that is genuinely really invested in calc. You honestly get out what you put in, and in my opinion he makes it easy for you to succeed. He gives practice quizzes and tests on the material that are essentially the same as the actual test/quiz. One test and quiz dropped. He basically just writes the notes out in lecture.
