reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I really hated going to his lectures! He made the experience awful. Always 10-15 minutes late and his class really tests my patience. Quizzes EVERY WEEK and never responds to emails and never respects my accommodations! Basically he was too lazy to accommodate me and decides to intimidate me… i hate him with every fiber of my being

his tests are almost exactly like the reviews he posts, but there are 4 tests and 3 quizzes (your lowest quiz AND test grade are dropped). Very nice guy, definitely one of the best business calc professors here. His classes are basically just lectures, and participation is not mandatory, but he will answer your question in class if you're lost
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is just a mean teacher and not care about student at all, strong ego but not good at teaching.
If you aren't familiar with or somewhat skilled in Calc of any kind RUN AWAY!

He was unavailable for his schedule office hours most of the time. When I tried to schedule other meeting times, he was dodgy because of other obligations. Also, his lecture style is very sporadic as he makes numerous mistakes and his lecture writing is messy and hard to follow with the many boxes he uses.

Had to self study a lot of the content because in his lectures hes just yapping and doesnt explain what he's even yapping about. He's always passive aggressive but plays it off as a joke. Very bad experience in his class.
Terrible professor. I do not recommend
Horrible professor!
Although I love him as a person, he is not very good at teaching

Never take a class with this professor if you can avoid it. There are better options where you will learn more, have less stress, feel respected, and understand what is happening in class. I feel like I learned all of calc through a textbook, so why am I paying for tuition if it is all self taught?? STAY CLEAR

The fact that you're even looking at these reviews means that you're considering taking one of his future things. Lemme tell you something: dont. You're doing yourself a huge favor if you NEVER take a class with him. Any alternative is a better alternative
Awfully pronouncing and egotistical and all he talks about is how great how own prowess is
Unnecessarily difficult
Does not explain things his lecture at all
Final is way harder than any of the quizzes and the quizzes are EVERY week
Inflexible with grading. Took off half the points for one question and gives no partial credit for some work i show
Really awful experience with this guy. Dont take any classes with him he will track your GPA
You're simply living life on unnecessarily hard mode by taking this teacher
One of the worst professors I have taken a class with at Northeastern. I have no idea why he is still teaching with all those negative reviews here.

By far the worst class that I have taken at Northeastern. He cannot teach (its not the accent, he just cannot teach) he is constantly giving a test and he does not spend time helping students, and if you do, he won't actually help you. Horrible professor, unless your a math whiz don't take his class unless you hate yourself.
I literally have to spend extra time seeking resources outside of the classroom. Also doesn't respond to emails. Don't take his class unless you want a miserable semester
Terrible at explaining concepts
His lectures are a huge wake up call that the teachers in college are just way worse than they were back in high school
The quiz he gives out have nothing to do with what he teaches. He also gives out quizzes every week which is insane. Never take him unless you wanna suffer through a sem.
Worst professor I've ever had at Northeastern. Never came to class, boring lectures, and holier than thou attitude. Take this course with literally anyone else
Just not a good teacher overall and his grading policy is awful. Would never take his class again if I had the opportunity as well as he is very difficult to understand because of his broken english.
Trust me i work hard this is the only withdrawal on my transcript for a reason

tests are NOTHING like the homework. passionate professor, but unable to teach effectively/doesn't prepare you for the exams. don't get too close with your classmates, they'll probably drop. take that as a sign and join them while you're ahead makes an alr difficult course even more unbearable. horrible, HORRIBLE prof.
helll nawww, to the naw naw naww (hell to the naw)

I came into this class excited, hopeful, but most of all... naive. Beneath the guise of a , caring, eager-to-help professor lies a sinister ulterior motive: to torture innocent math/eng minds. When I say run, wallahi, I mean every word of it. Class averages are literally in the 50s
Simply does not care for your learning
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Old and unengaging. Tests are much harder than what he teaches and some of the things on his tests literally are not methods of what he teaches.

If you really want to learn differential equations. This teacher will not help. The content is dry because it's all just memorizing plug and chug and if you're taking this class you're either a engineer or math major If you're an engineer you won't like this class because its separated from your applications and the latter its separated from theory

If you look around the room during the first day or quiz/test days there is a stark difference between the amount of people in the lectures. That is telling! The reason why is because his lectures are very unimportant. It is grueling to sit through the entire lecture and heat him drone on through topics that can honestly be summarized up in 5 min.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Basically self studied the entire class and skipped lectures because he is so uninspiring. Forces you to memorize things. Here's the gist: heres 100 scenarios to every single possible combination of first/second order/ homogenous/linear/autonomous/ and every permutation possible. Completely a waste of time. Does not explains what he is teaching
EMOTIONAL DAMAGE
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Definitely one of the better calc professors. He posts all previous quizzes and exams to help students study and he's very fair in grading on tests. Would definitely recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I've taken this class twice and I can say, Assad genuinely helped me understand the mechanics of Calculus! I have issues with understanding certain concepts of calculus but his teaching style (thorough lecture, step to step explanation, expansion of confusing spots in explanations, and many examples) helped me understand the material a lot!

DO NOT TAKE IT WITH ASSAD. I had Assad last Spring for Diff Eqs and his communication was just absolutely atrocious. He repeats the same thing over and over and it honestly made me hate Differential Equations so much even as a math major. The quality of his lectures are so poor; he always just makes me wanna fall asleep.

All around good professor. The material is complicated and sometimes the explanations can be confusing, he writes a lot on the board, lots of example problems in class, and lots of quizzes and tests to see your progress. The finals was very difficult imo, as I did great in the class until the final. Good teacher, stay on top of the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Sweetest professor I've ever had. We had quizzes two times a week but if you paid attention in class they weren't bad at all. Most of the time the quiz questions were exact copies of problems we did in class. We also got to bring a cheat sheet for every quiz and test. The tests were harder than the quizzes, but you should do fine if you study.

He is a very good professor. The class was graded only on tests which made it more difficult but he dropped the two lowest quizzes. The in-class midterms were nowhere near as difficult of the final. I had a 97 going into the final but ended with a B because of the final even when I got 10% above the average so study A LOT for the final.

Great Professor, this was my second time taking Diff Eq's and he was so much better than my first professor. For Diff Eq's if you can't get loopi, Assad's the second best professor. Is a physics guy so if you're into that, you'll love Assad.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Is a funny professor but tests are way too harsh and the class average was extremely low. Average on the final was a 67, so get ready for this class to tank your GPA. Only graded from 6 exams and the final with no extra credit or anything

Assad is such a sweet guy; he is very understanding and truly wants you to succeed. 
I only took pre-calc in high school so this class was def challenging for me, but he gave us extra credit to keep our grades as high as possible before the final, which is 40% of ur grade. He drops ur lowest test grade too, which helped my grade a lot. I recommend!

Really kind and caring professor. I thought he did an amazing job explaining the concepts. His quizzes and tests were quite easy and homework was optional. He does teach a bit slow and get behind, he doesn't always cover everything and the final is harder than his tests. If you want an easier semester but more work during finals, take Assad.

Doesn't know how to work canvas at all and won't ever respond emails. Takes favorites. I was heavily disliked by him so therefore he would single me out and dock me test points he didn't do for others. Grade calculated is done poorly because there's only 6 things + the final. You have to do well on everything. No calculator, everything on paper.

Super kind, teaches through examples. You honestly don't even have to go to class to succeed. Gives practice quizzes/exams that are just like the actual thing. He confuses himself a little sometimes but nothing crazy. Definitely would take him again

love the man. Just go to class, take notes (he writes everything down on the board you don't even have to listen to him), do the practice tests and quiz a day before your tests or quizzes and you're set. Super easy. HW is fun but not necessary as long as you're able to solve the problems you've taken in your notes, you're good. Just go to class!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Had him for Calc 1 & Physics 1 ILS, reasonable grader but confuses himself at times. semester 1 vs semester 2 his teaching has declined. He made calc 1 a more confusing than it should have been tbh. ALWAYS late, funny & cool guy though. Calc 1 is a lot of lecture and examples w little explanation of actual concepts, but ILS lectures aren't helpful.

Very nice professor, but his lectures aren't great. He doesn't explain the topics you are learning he just does example problems on the board. So be ready to teach yourself the topics outside of class. Exams can be tricky as well.

Very nice and funny guy but your graded entirely on test scores which kinda sucks, participation isn't mandatory but he did start taking participation near the end of the semester because if you attend class he says he will bump your grade up a bit

Overall not a bad professor, he drops 1 out of 6 quizzes during the semester. He doesn't require homework but in return your whole grade is based on your quizzes/tests/final. Does a lot of examples in class for things on the quizzes/tests/final. He also gives out practice tests for everything which is the same questions just different numbers.

Took Calc 1 in Fall 2024. 6 quizzes, and only the top 5 are graded. 2 midterms, 1 final worth 40% of the grade. Class was alright, very lecture-heavy, and he is NOT tech savvy, lol. Everything (syllabus, practice tests, etc) was just posted into the 'Files' tab on Canvas. Also he never has slideshows, he just writes stuff on the board. He is funny.

He seemed like a genuinely nice guy but very forgetful. I did not have him for the physics class but for physics ILS. While I got along with him well, his explanations at the beginning were a bit confusing, and he kept forgetting to grade the quizzes of students who went to the DRC for test taking. I received my quizzes back RIGHT before exams.

MATH 1241- love love love! Definitely recommend having him as a professor. Everything is super straight forward. If you go to class, pay attention, and do the practice exams, you will do well. 6 tests that are each 10%, final is 40%. Attendance isn't mandatory and neither is buying a textbook, but I would recommend doing so.

Best professor I've had so far! As long as you go to class and do the homeworks this class isn't bad at all. Lot's of the examples that he does in class are really similar to the questions on the exam and you get a cheat sheet for the exam. He's super funny and I would really recommend going to his office hours. Final was hard compared to midterms.
The Group problem is hard and he hardly ever explain it.

Physics 2 with Assad was a cakewalk over Summer 1. Super easy exams, similar in difficulty to the practice exams. Some questions on the homeworks and quizzes can be challenging, but the most challenging questions won't be on exams. Lectures are great but are very long and slow paced. Physics II Lab isn't hard, but is a time sink; some bad TAs.

Assad is a great professor. His lectures are very clear and goes through many practice problems in class. The practice quizzes and tests were exact representations of the tests and quizzes given in class. He cracks jokes throughout lecture and is very kind.

Assad is the best professor I've ever had! He makes class funny and enjoyable and explains difficult concepts really well. Outside of class I really recommend going to office hours  he is very kind and is always willing to help.
This is by far the best profession I have had at Northeastern. His study guides laid out clearly what was going to be tested on the 6 exams we had before the final.

Assad is the best. His lecture are very clear and he tells you exactly what you need to know for quizzes and exams. He does tons of practice problems that are similar to assessments. He is so sweet, I recommend going to his office hours whether you have questions or just to chat. I wish I had him for physics 2, he's truly the best!

Assad was great. He derives formulas and does primarily practice problems. He's often late to class. Has an accent, but easy to understand. He gives notes on common mistakes and drops the lowest quiz. The practice exams he posts are exactly like his actual exams. Just find the textbook online for free. Easy if you took BC and just need refreshers.

He provided the notes online and his practice quizzes closely resembled the real ones. However, your grade is entirely made of quizzes/tests -homework/participation has no impact. No extra credit was offered. He mainly wrote on the board, rarely explaining the material. He was often late to class. The average class attendance rate was about 50%.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This guy does not know how to teach and at 10pm the night before final gave 2 more topics to learn

Assad was the best math professor I've ever had throughout my school life. He is also the funniest teacher I've had and his jokes would always make me laugh which I felt was needed in a calculus class. He made everything so clear and his practice tests were very similar to the real ones which prepared me very well. I 100% recommend taking his class

Assad is a very caring and funny professor. I had him for Calculus for Business which is a very easy class especially if you've taken calculus before. If you haven't taken calculus before, the way he explains concepts may be very confusing. He is very knowledgable though and will take the time to explain something to you.

AWFUL prof. His lectures are terrible, not clear, and all over the place. The only reason I did well in this class was bc I had taken the material before and the example exams he posts are exactly like the ones he gives.He is not reasonable when it comes to needing accommodations and comes late to class by 10 mins almost every class. Easy A tho.

Assad is a good guy. Really funny and clearly knows the subject. He's very receptive to feedback when a student points out an error in his work. For his calculus one class, if you show up to class, take notes, and spend maybe 30 minutes after every lecture studying, there's no reason you shouldn't get at least a B.

Prof. Fotovatian is a great professor. He is funny and clearly loves physics. Grades are from homework, biweekly quizzes, and the midterm and final exams, but there is a ton of extra credit on the homework and he is always willing to help. The difficult part of the class is the lab reports since they are worth 15% of the lecture course grade.

The lectures mainly consist of learning through examples, which is helpful if you take notes. He is also very clear about what is on exams, making studying streamlined and less stressful. 

Great professor- I recommend you take him for Calculus 2, Physics 1, and Physics 2. He often tells funny stories which help make the class interesting.

I had this prof for physics 1 and this course was much less well taught. It felt like the lectures were poorly planned and I had trouble following the content. There would be questions on the hw and quizzes that we hadn't learned yet. The material was pretty easy so it wasn't that bad and he's a really nice guy, just very diff than physics 1.
Great professor. Lecture interesting

Lecture heavy, homework is completely optional, and if you have taken calc before, it is an easy A. Grade is based off of 6 exams and the final. Practice exams are super similar to the actual exams, and he is very understanding. He can get distracted during class, so sometimes he is hard to follow. I personally loved him!

Professor Fotovatian is definitely a safe option for Calculus. Not going to lie, sometimes he would make me super mad cuz a lot of times his lecture examples had a lot of mistakes. But overall, he's a super fair grader and super nice, so still a really good professor. Way better than some other calculus professor that I hear of.

Grade is entirely based on tests but there is sometimes bonus and he drops the lowest one. Not the clearest teaching and sometimes class feels all over the place. You'll be fine if you've taken calc before. Practice exams are super useful and he gives optional homework.
best teacher i've ever had at Northeastern!!!!!
i love him. BEST PROF i've ever had at northeastern. he makes physics seem simple compared to other professors. homework can be a bit challenging but exams are straight from his lectures.

He was a very good lecturer and gave practice problems that were exactly like the tests and quizzes. The homework took awhile but was worth getting good grades on the midterm and final. Would recommend studying the reviews and the in-class problems, don't waste time or money on the textbook. Overall, would recommend for this class.

He doesn't engage much with students but seems like a great guy and gives very clear lectures. Gives practice quizzes and exams that are exactly like the actual thing. Not very accessible outside of class but the class is pretty self-explanatory. Don't have to come to lectures but it's best to bc then you don't have to look in the textbook.

Showed up to class late and never explained the concept; he only teaches the concept and not the application. You could probably do well if you've taken calc 2 before, but as someone who didn't, he didn't explain enough for me to understand completely. He also barely went to his office hours (at 8 am), and his lectures can be hard to follow.

He may be a better physics than calc teacher, but for calc 2 I can't recommend him. He is okay, but very often late and disorganized. He doesn't explain concepts as thoroughly as he could, but I don't think he's awful. Definitely an okay option if you're good at calculus or have taken it before.

This class feels like my dad taught me Calc II... in the best way. From the subtle dad jokes to the comprehensive variety of examples to the warm way he teaches during office hours, I enjoyed it. 40% of your grade is a final, and another 40% are two midterms, so beware, but the rest are quizzes (and the lowest two are dropped!). Class is fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Stay away if you can. He's a great guy and is really nice but doesn't explain what he's doing. The whole class consists of him writing on the board and you copying it rather than actually learning.

The only physics professor who actually knows how to teach! He actually makes me not hate physics. He explains and derives the equations carefully so that you actually understand the questions instead of *just* plugging in numbers. He can go a little fast, but if you spend time with the material or go to office hours to ask, u'll do great.

Professor Fotovatian was such a professor. For Fall 2020 semester he only graded quizzes (9 and dropped your lowest grade), the final, and midterm. For the quizzes it was exactly what he went over in class and for the midterm and final it was essentially the practice exam he gives. He is a very fair grader and even gives you his number to reach out
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great professor, however I did feel better when I went for tutoring sessions outside of class to help me prep for exams. As long as you understand the problems on the boards and on quizzes, you'll be ready for the exams. Weekly quizzes.

Took him at UML for Calc II. He's a great professor that doesn't make the subject overly difficult. Gives quizzes about every two weeks which are straightforward, and provides practice exams which are basically the same as the actual exams. Lectures can be boring, but he explains everything clearly and prepares you well. Won't need textbook at all.

He is a good professor and clearly knows what he is talking about. He gives good explanations. However, sometimes he would stray from the material or show up to class late and not leave us enough time to do work. So, you just need to be patient. But overall, really great professor!

I took Calculus I with Professor Fotovatian and his lectures were always enjoyable. Not only did he explain the important concepts in detail but he also made them easy to understand. He is a great teacher and he tends to be pretty funny as well. I am happy to say that I am taking Professor Fotovatian's section of Calculus II next semester.

Really Nice guy - Dad Jokes and innocently funny anecdotes litter his class. Uses loads of colorful chalk, has all his notes on blackboard, does lots of examples in class. Tests are super easy if you listen in class, reading not necessary.

I'm so glad I took physics with professor Assad! He is AWESOME! He is so helpful and always available after class for help. He even provide his students with his personal phone number so we can text him with any questions about problems. Exams are based on the lecture and the quizzes! Quizzes are from lectures and he will drop the 2 lowest grades

He's super funny and nice, if you pay attention, show up, and do the homework, it's a very straightforward class. He loves his job and thinks he's hilarious (which he is). Very generous with partial credit but also teaches very well so you actually know what's going on

He has a great personality to lecture. He sometimes doesn't finish his sentences, but he'll always make sure you have what you need for the quiz/test if you're paying attention. I suck at algebra and Physics, and I regret not going to his office hours. I can't stress enough to go to his office hours. Overall one of my favorite professors.

Literally the nicest professor ever. Very understanding and is always wiling to look over grading for quizzes and tests. Very good at giving partial credit, has lot's of opportunities to boost your grade. Available to reach outside of class. Very funny during the lectures. Would take him again and recommend to other students.

Assad cares about his students and wants all of them to do well. His lectures can be chaotic, but some light textbook reading before class will help you keep track of his direction. He makes an effort to actually explain the application and relevance of different concepts, and he's available outside of class for help. He even gave us his number.

He was the nicest professor! I would recommend him in the future! Grading was very clear, 10 quizzes with 2 dropped. Lab grades are factored in which helps. You can bring a cheat sheet to the midterm and final but everything is clear as to what will be on the exams.
The professor goes out of his way to make the class fun and makes jokes along the lectures. He is a very kind person and is accessible to students when they have doubts.

He is extremely specific about what will be on the weekly quizzes, midterm, and final. He even gives examples of the exact problems during lectures! If you just go to the lectures and take notes, it's an easy class to pass, even during the harder units. Also gives cheat sheets during all quizzes and tests :)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Fotovatian was really nice and caring of his students needs. He grades homework and weekly quizzes fairly. The in class notes are almost always representative of what the quiz will be about; however, the homework isn't as helpful for preparing for quizzes. He is a bit disorganized when providing the notes, so paying attention is key.

The grading was really clear and when we had a problem or uncertainty about the grading, the professor well explained and fixed the grade. He gives the quiz and the test based on the class material so it is best to not skip the classes.

Professor has a great personality and is very helpful when answering individual questions. During Lecture, it may be hard to follow what he is writing since he writes all over the blackboard. If you pay attention, you'll be able to understand what he is teaching. Stay on top of weekly quizzes and homeworks.
Great!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Ferd is a great teacher and an expert at his field. However, progress reports has been hell as of fall 2025. Piazza is kind of a mess as well, and a hit or miss whether the teachers will respond to the questions or not. It is better to access his help during office hours or after class. Class could be boring due to the nature of the topic.

the assignments were released on Friday and the material is fully taught by Wednesday or even Thursday, which makes us rush the assignment to done by Friday evening. the exams were decent, but the level of difficulty is way harder than the practice exams. very messy and I'm guessing the people that did pass somehow used ChatGPT to succeed

Excellent lecturer, clearly an expert in topic and genuinely wants to teach you what he knows. Willing to take the time to go back and explain topics differently to make you understand. However, it's impossible to know how you're doing. Most assgnmnt/proj grades only released after finals, and sometimes impossible to get a reply on Piazza.

He is a funny teacher and good lecturer to a degree, can come off as boring at times. However, his class is completely disorganized. We don't know how we are doing in the class because he takes months to grade one assignment.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's a nice guy but I'm giving a terrible rating because he has not returned grades in months and completes ghosts his students online. It's totally fine to be behind on grading but it's not okay to drop all communication to your students. We still have 5 ungraded assignments and the deadline for all grades is in 2 days.

Ferd is a fun professor, I had him for another class before & it was good. CS3650 just SUCKS. Assignments 2 months ago? Not graded. OHs? TAs constantly cancel w/out updating. Piazza? No responses. This class's so disorganized & terrible. Sad part is, Ferd is still a 1000x better than the other professor that was teaching this course. Save yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
Assignments are very difficult and haven't been graded in months, lectures are very boring. Seems to be passionate, but can't convey his knowledge to students.

Tough class, but more importantly none of our assignments or exams have been graded in months. Student's questions on piazza are ghosted entirely.
Assignments are very difficult and haven't been graded in months, lectures are very boring. Seems to be passionate, but can't convey his knowledge to students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: They literally ignore most of the questions you ask about on piazza, and the homework assignments from TWO months ago are still not graded. This class literally just leaves you to figure it out on your own since you don't know what's right or wrong since they literally dont answer anything
please grade the homework or exma please please please please

Ferd has not returned a single assignment in the last 2 months. It's not okay to keep your entire class in the dark about their grades. When students message about this on Piazza, they are ignored. Also, very confusing lectures...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ferd is knowledge+passionate about computer systems. I am not. He assumes too much prior skill and covers complex topics quickly that are hard to understand. The assignments are too complex+its hard for me to follow his lectures as he uses complex language. Better to cover fewer topics students understand than many complex ones that students don't.

somehow I feel I don't learn in lectures. he goes through some examples, but when I do the assignments I feel sort of lost. his office hours help me with the assignment. the exams are hard. i'm lucky i didn't have him for Fundies 1, 2, or OOD, i would've learned nothing if he was my professor

I don't often praise professors... he is a 5 star Systems professor. It's clear he knows all the material and also knows how to explain that material so you understand (you can always ask for a re-explanation and he will spend time to make sure you get it). Lectures are top tier. If you can take his course, take it. Thanks!

Overall a good professor, lectures can be dry at times but he makes up for it by being helpful and very flexible when needed. The overall course was well paced though I would definitely suggest going to class to avoid being lost. Ferd is a kind and open teacher who shows care and I would definitely be willing to take a class from him again.

Super nice professor, very cooperative, and answers every question in the best way he can. Sometimes he goes very fast through topics, but overall I had a great experience with Ferd. Amazing professor.

I think CS2500 has one of the worst class structures of any classes I've taken. It's not practical and seems all over the place. Teaches irrelevant concepts. Vesely is cool but this class is pretty awful.
Good lectures, but project/assignment requirements weren't always clear, announcements varied in where they were posted, and grades took FOREVER to get back.

Very dry lecturer with a monotone delivery. Explanations are overly technical and lack empathy for students. Takes over a month to grade assignments, which is especially frustrating during finals week when assignments are still due. Not an enjoyable experience.

Professor Ferdinand has very dry lectures as other posts have mentioned. He tends to go on tangents and very frequently, homework does not have much to do with the lectures. Also, he is not very accessible outside of class, which is a problem when homework are not very detailed and riddled with vague instructions.

Very poor communication when updating assignments, withholds information about assignments for no reason, extremely boring and uninformative lectures. He and his TAs took over a month to grade most of our assignments (only a few hundred lines of code each), and offered no real feedback. He is associate for a reason.
Wasn't a huge fan. Lectures were long and dry, and he often went on tangents/got sidetracked easily. Assignments took a VERY long time to grade, sometimes almost a month.

I am currently in his CS2800 class. It is absolutely terrible. I have taken 12 classes at Northeastern University so far. This is, by far, the worst. There is no structure, no help, no preparation for anything that'll get graded, no communication between instructors and professors, etc. The bottom line is that this class is miserable as a student.

I am taking CS2800 with him, and it is his first time teaching it. He is unhelpful, there is no structure, the course website never gets updated, we use an IDE from the 1950s, and it is in ACL2, which is absolutely terrible.
Ferd is a great prof, he posts class notes 1-7 days after class which is nice but can also be annoying if he hasn't posted it.

I get what other people meant by he's wholesome since he tries being uplifting and encouraging. Lectures are basically him coding on screen how to do problems in Racket. He posts copies of his notes to Canvas which is nice. He's one of the professors though that will ask questions and wait a while for a response. Doesn't do exam reviews :\

Ferd is a very kind person and is not intimidating at all. His lectures can be a little bit boring but I think that's more cuz the class material is boring. He knows what he's talking about and you learn a lot in class. However, they take long to respond on piazza and emails sometimes.

Ferd is one of my favorite Khoury professors so far. He is passionate and caring, lots of energy during lecture, and has a good sense of humor. He did a good job at conveying lecture material and explaining concepts. Take his advice and don't code in class. Sit in the front if you can, and stick around after class if you have any questions.

CS2500 is a course taught by Vesely and Shivers together, and man it is a rough time. The syllabus with REQUIRED READINGS was updated once with week 1's readings and never again. Lectures are difficult to follow without prior knowledge from other coding or the textbook. Homeworks are difficult and tests are super important. Discouraging class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Ferd is the most wholesome professor I have ever had. He truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He tells you on the first day of class not to code during lectures and is rightfully hard on people who do. Sit in the front row and it will be your favorite class, I promise. I wish he'd ask for our names though. 10/10 prof

He is very wholesome and kinda awkward, and wants you to succeed in his classes. Ferd tries to make the subject matter as interesting as possible. He seemed to care about giving clear and comprehensive lectures on the topics, and would go over material as needed when that didn't happen.

Ferd is one of the better professors in Khoury for me. If you're taking CS4530 you've met a fair share of professors who can't teach to save their lives but goes out of their way to be a donkey on piazza. Decent lectures and he dropped a lot of mind-bogglingly stupid requirements profs from other sessions had (public peer review), A+ in my book.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: if you don't sit in the front row, you won't learn anything. He seems like a nice enough guy but I sat a few rows back and I could never really hear anything he was saying. all the stuff I learned was from the online videos, lectures were just kind of going over what you should already know from the vids and I didn't find them very helpful tbh

Vesely judges will judge you if you have stupid questions or if you try to correct him during class with the code. I learned nothing from him because he just codes in front of you as if you know already. I had to rely on lecture videos from Prof. Derbinsky (my respects to him). I would definitely never take this class with Vesely again.

As someone who had never coded before, I felt a bit neglected at times. It was a little hard to conceptually understand what was being taught. He was such a nice professor though. Really into computer science.

Although Ferd means well he ends up being a bit lacking as an instructor. His lectures are rather dry and, due to how CS2500 is structured, he has very little input in helping you fix grade-related issues. He's useful during one-on-one office hrs (only open at very specific times on Tues) to ask conceptual questions but that's about it :(.

Ferd is one of the nicest professors I've had. You can tell he is really passionate about what he is teaching and definitely knows a lot about it. He doesn't judge at all if you have stupid questions or if you get something wrong. He is also willing to admit when he makes a mistake or messes up, which is unlike some other professors I've had. 10/10

Ferd is a great professor. He has a good sense of humor and hes obviously knowledgeable. The one thing holding him back is how dull the lectures are. I wish he was a bit more engaging like some of the other professors.

Seems like a nice guy. He's always happy to answer questions during lecture, and treats the class with respect. Lectures can be a bit dull, but that seems to be more the result of the nature of the course.

Loved Ferd was an absolute treat to work with in Fall 2020 for Fundies 1. I talk to him consistently now as a Teaching Assistant and he's such a relatable and down to earth person. His lectures are quite engaging as long as you participate!

His lectures are kind of boring, but he's also a newer teacher so I don't know how familiar he is with the material. He often gets sidetracked so you most likely will need to check out the supplemental materials to truly understand it all. He isn't mean and open to answering questions and checks in with students an adequate amount.

He brings valuable experience to every class, which helps illustrate key concepts. Every class is a lecture, and I found them all really interesting. I like the class discussions that take place, it feels very engaging and easy to participate. Great experience!

Environmental Planning is a class about regulations and planning - already a dull class. Felix does his best to provide ideas and input, but it's mainly just him reading off slides. No rubrics or guidance for assignments, although he is an easyish grader so not horrible. For a required class it's boring but easy and you can do other work during it

Big assignments are vague. He refuses to give a rubric or any specific guidelines (he didn't know what a rubric even was). Lectures are dull and subjects are outdated. I learn much more from student contributions than anything he could say. Take this class if you want to pay to become unbelievably frustrated two times a week and gain nothing.

Gave weekly quizzes on like 3 different articles each week. Big on class discussions and will randomly call on you. Felix seems to not know 100% what he's talking about and oftentimes fed wrong information and argued with students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Class was ok due to the small class discussions but Felix was pretty dull. Has relevant professional experience but cannot relate it well outside a few specific examples. there are better classes to take in the environmental and poly sci colleges for applicable environmental policy

Despite his years in the Mass. DEP, he is not very knowledgeable, or aware of, issues outside the confines of MA. Of all the professors in the ENVR Department, a true disappointment here. He has no idea how to teach, doesn't source anything he says, and makes incorrect statements all the time. Waste of time. Felt like an intro high school class.

He's a pretty chill professor, he's good at explaining problems and generally pretty amenable to partial credit if you can explain your thinking well. Super funny and down to earth, clearly cares a lot about making sure we're actually learning. Very open to feedback, the course was pretty flexible and a lot was up to what works best for us.

Farzard professor is incredibly engaging and truly cares about student learning. Lectures are clear, thoughtful, and well-structured. He creates a welcoming environment where questions are encouraged. He challenges you in the best way and provides meaningful feedback. You leave the course feeling more confident and capable.

This class was extremely difficult to follow. It is a statistics course, but very little statistics were taught but rather most of it was stated to be intuitive and that we should already know the math side. He uses a lot of AI and will answer students questions in class using AI rather than using his own degree. Very test heavy.

Honestly a joke this class used so much AI it's not even funny, the lectures were unegaging and fast paced for an introductory level course. Gave extra credit for attendance but wouldn't be my first choice, the exams were extremely difficult and be prepared to put in a lot of effort

Farzad is very nice and clearly likes the topic but he often goes on unrelated tangents. Homeworks are fine but the tests are not like what we learn in class. He uses AI to teach and spent an entire class plugging our questions into AI. He is very bad at responding to emails and hard to reach.

If you want to pass this class, then DO NOT listen to anything he tells you to study to prepare for his midterm or final exams. He told me to focus on the 2 practice exams he posted and the review questions for we over in class for the midterm and the actual exam was NOTHING like what he posted. Just focus on his slides mostly and assignments.
Good instructor, funny and informative.

If you have the option for biostats you should 100% take him. I didn't attend lectures a lot cuz he just goes through slides which I did on my own. There are weekly labs and (easy) quizzes. I recommend trying to do good on those and get help from recitation because you can get an easy B+ or A from doing well on those. Also a midterm and a final.

Professor Noubary usually did a very good job at explaining statistics concepts, but sometimes he would over-explain to the point where he bored the class and he became difficult to pay attention to. That being said, homework consisted of weekly quizzes and labs that were usually not too difficult but were annoying to do.

I was honestly really scared to take this class based on her reviews but it was much better than I anticipated. Biochem is hard but she's a great teacher. if you use the resources she gives you and actually study you can succeed. also really helpful if you have to take the mcat, i feel like i got a great biochem comprehension from her course.

The class was VERY organised and VERY structured, and Professor is very knowledgeable and caring, but can come off as condescending. Exams were based very similarly to the study guides, and there is honestly not a ton of material, so it ends up being pretty easy. But attendance is VERY important, and participation matters to make her like you

Her class was structured extremely well and her expectations were clear. The lectures were interesting and this was honestly my favorite class I took this semester. She provides practice tests for exams, which helped me a ton. However, she is condescending and rude. If you participate too much, she gets snippy. Good professor, not too nice, though.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Attendance mandatory. Tests are so wordy and like an SAT. Would not take again with this Inquiries topic, take an easier one.

I actually enjoyed her lectures and understood concepts well, but her exams are extremely difficult. They are unnecessarily complicated and if you are a slow test-taker, they are hard to finish. I've never done so poorly in class, esp with concepts I really knew/understood. Additionally, she is very condescending and not easy to approach.

prof fertuck is not horrible, but her explanations are unclear and i learned far more from youtube and other supplemental sources. exams are formatted like the SAT where there's a bunch of meaningless jargon to trip you up and it doesn't even help with finding the answer. 35% of your grade is basically padding, and trust me, you're gonna need it.

I thought that the exams were way more difficult than they had to be, and were much more difficult than the practice done in class. The entire class is a lecture besides 10 minutes of group work every class that she uses as attendance. I think she seems to be a really nice person and is very knowledgeable about biochemistry, but it was very hard.

Feel like other reviews are being overtly harsh. Lectures could be slow but were thorough + there was ample review material from past lessons which made studying for exams extremely easy. Reasonable amount of hw & other assignments given with clear deadlines, instructions & rubrics. Prof was also very understanding & made it interesting to learn.

She tries to be nice but it comes across as very condescending and treats you like a kid. Her lecture slides are fine but the way they're explained in class is very inefficient and very slow. She is very controlling and I would not recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Eh she was a good teacher and cared about how much we learn. But it did feel a little like I was being babied and even if I did know what I was doing. Tbh it's not hard to get a decent grade but I wouldn't say I looked forward to going to class.

While the content of the class was interesting her tests and class overall was a lot more difficult than the other inquiries sections. She often treated us like we were little kids with seating arrangements and the language she used when she spoke. If you have the choice, I would choose another section.

she's alright. the class is mostly graded by the 2 midterms, the poster project, and the final. the midterms are heavily based on the lectures and like the SATs. the poster project is basically easy points if you do it by the rubric. the final is basically the same as the midterms, but cumulative.
don't take if not interested in (bio)chemistry.

Professor Fertuck comes off as a caring professor, trying to get to know everyone's names. However, she only cares for attendance purposes. She is not the best socially. Tests have way too much reading. Lectures are a waste of time. However, she has all lessons written out, the material is easy to learn, and consultations for project are helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I understand that she's trying to be upbeat, but it comes off as very annoying and condescending. She talks to you like you're a kid. I asked her a question and she cut me off and walked away. She makes topics much more complicated than needed. One of the only sections for BIOL2299 that had a final. Avoid her, to be honest.

People are not lying when they say that Prof. Fertuck has an overly dramatic tone of voice, but I don't think it's meant to be condescending. She likes to focus on teaching first semester students for this section, so she seems extra because she is trying to be extra supportive.
Overall, knoweldgable and easy to spend an hour with half of the week.

Where do I begin with this woman. She is the most BIZARRE professor I've ever had. She treats her students like children with her name tags and assigned seats (ALL YEAR). She will laugh like an evil witch and screech words throughout the entire hour- making this course painful to sit through. Take this as a warning. Get as far away as you can...

I usually do not complain about my professors. HOWEVER, this woman is truly INSUFFERABLE. Get ready to leave class every day with a pounding headache. Her voice is my least favorite sound in the world if im being kind. Her screaching volume of every other word, especially her "ANDDDDD" and "EHHHHEMMM" will haunt you in your sleep. Good luck........

Professor Fertuck seems passionate about what she was teaching but she liked to waste time and the lectures felt really long. She talked to students like children and had assigned seats and daily turn and talks. Her exams were reading comprehension and required specific answers for short responses.

I could tell that Professor Fertuck was passionate about the topics; however, she likes to waste time in class and each lecture fell long. She treats students like little children with assigned seats and turn and talks at the beginning of each class. Exams were lengthy and had paragraphs of information before questions. I would avoid her if you can

Lectures are difficult to get through. Fertuck is condescending, over-explains every detail, and generally treats students like they're dumb. Exams are blocks of text that make it difficult to get to the important information. The actual information gained from lectures is diluted by hours of waste. Avoid at all costs.

Her exams resemble the SAT questions a lot. She is very condescending and in my opinion, she lacks social cues. I would avoid her at all costs. While she is extremely knowledgeable, she is not very approachable. Exams are only 30 questions, so you cannot get like more than 5 questions wrong.

Fertuck loves to go through her lectures very slowly with oversaturated explanations that take up so much time. Can't forget about the obnoxious tone or the ways she dilates her voice to speak to you like you're a 3rd grader. Exams are blocks of text that hardly contribute to helping you answer the question. Overall, decent instructor, just boring.

Fertuck was not the most enjoyable professor. As a person, she was tough to deal with and talk to. The content of the class wasn't bad - she overexplained a lot thinking we were kindergarteners. Her exams remind me of the SAT and are more reading comprehension than understanding which was annoying. She does curve A LOT. But overall, decent class.

You can tell Prof. Fertuck is very knowledgeable about the subject but she's an incredibly boring lecturer as mentioned in other reviews. Exams are wordy and could be confusing but if you pay attention in class you will do well. Attendance, PollEv, and weekly quizzes are easy points and will bump your grade up. She will also curve the exam scores.
Literally the worst person at this university. Treats kids like 5 year olds. Avoid at all costs. Prayers up to all biochem majors forced to deal with her.

She does not care about students. She will not care about you. Is extremely uptight and is genuinely one of the most uncaring professors I've come across. Save yourself and do not take her if you want to keep your sanity intact.

I don't think I've every left this class without a headache. The sounds she makes are unbearable. Lectures are hard to get through because of the time she wastes and treats you like a child. She is condescending and talks down to you. I will never take another class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor treats you like you are in kindergarten through assigned seating and turn and talks. She tends to talk down to you and if she doesn't like you, you will know. The test are difficult, wordy and very specific. Her voice is excruciating so make sure you have Advil with you because you're going to need it. Also, she wastes time in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's very knowledgable, but talks down to students so you feel like you're in kindergarten all over again. The gremlin voice is funny at first, but gets annoying VERY quickly. Exams are EXTREMELY nit-picky, require you to read a paragraph before you even get to the question, and were 18 pages (front and back) for 30 questions for reference. BEWARE

Professor Fertuck is one of the professors I would never take again. Her class was very difficult, and even asking for extra help felt pointless as she never explained things well to get a good grasp on them. She does not do a great job going over things that are important in depth for the exam. She wastes time during the lectures.

-talks to you like a child. Tests (30 mc questions) are not reflective of lecture AT ALL so theyre very difficult to study for. Office hours/ outside help are useless bc she simply repeats lectures rather than letting you ask questions. Luckily participation and assignment are easy 100s so thats a good 35% padding to your grade (youll need it)

Prof. Fertuck is one of the few, if not only professor that I will not take again. Although she is good at explaining concepts, she wastes a ton of time during lectures and does not go nearly in depth as what we were tested on. Furthermore, she had moments where she was a bit condescending to students who were needing help/asking questions.

Fertuck talks to the class like we are in elementary school. Has a big issue with earbuds and she pretends that she doesn't take attendance but she tracks who was in class everyday. Her lectures are boring and she wastes a ton of time going over only a few topics because its the way she is. She also micromanages the entire classroom, quite annoying

Fertuck is not a harsh grader and her exams are easy to pass. My only issue I had with this class was that it was so slow and impossible to pay attention in. She spoke to us like we were middle schoolers which made it really tough to listen. Other than that, the class is not horrible and she gives you a lot of opportunities to have an A

Very nit-picky with grading exams and will dock points for anything. The exams are very wordy, like other reviews said. The amount of homework is very reasonable. She's nice and somewhat understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Class overall is pretty easy except for exams. They are wordy (paragraphs of background info for a few multiple choice) and very difficult to follow. Very broad info is taught but the exams can be kind of specific. She talks to you like your in kindergarten, but is pretty nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Paying attention in class and actually doing the work she recommends (doing hw, studying a reasonable amount for exams) can get you an A. The assignments are extremely reasonable, the lectures are well-thought-out, and her excitement is easy to pick up on if you just lean into it a little bit. You'll get Exactly what you put into this class.

I felt like I was in a kindergarten classroom. Attendance and showing enthusiasm during lessons are mandatory. Content taught in class is pretty broad, yet the tests are very specific and she will dock you points if you get the question right but don't answer with enough specificity. Some opportunity to make up easy marks with take home quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Fertuck is the absolute worst professor I've ever had. Lectures mainly focus on broad stroke concepts but then the exams come around and she expects you to memorize the smallest details from her slides. The way she speaks too makes lectures hard to listen to. Also not even worth going to her for help, she just points you to tutors.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She can be over enthusiastic and her lecture can be really slow sometimes. However, she almost always finishes what she planned for class and she makes things clear to understand. Her test can be tricky but her grading is fair overall. As long as you study and attend recitations, you will do fine.

Attendance is extremely mandatory. After 3 absences, you get points off of your grade. Tests make up 55/100 points. Honestly don't even know how she gets test info from her lectures. All other assignments are easy. Speaks to you like its kindergarten. Tests have really long drawn out answers which makes it confusing and wastes lots of time.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Although she has some quirks, Fertuck is one of the best professors at Northeastern. Her best qualities are that she is a good lecturer and the class is designed in a way to motivate and help you learn. Her worst trait is she is bad at understanding and answering questions. Unlike other classes, I retained what I learned in it. Tutors are good too
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: It's really unfortunate that NEU won't hire more biochem professors because Fertuck is unbearable. Her exam questions are wordy and full of useless info that makes them hard to parse what she's actually asking for. Good luck to anyone taking this class, I suggest going to tutors and asking for past exams because she reuses 80%+ of past questions
Took this class in the summer - It was ROUGH. Exams are based about 70/30 on lecture material vs readings.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed Professor Fertuck! She makes the course material really easy to understand and is always willing to offer assistance or help to students. Her exams make you think, but they seem quite fair in their assessment of ones knowledge. I did not do as well as I would have liked in the class, but I felt the grade was deserving.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Very easy class to get a good grade in, took her for toxicology biological inquiries and it was low effort but I still feel like I learned a good bit. Her personality can sometimes be grating and she sometimes talks to the class like we're her Pre-K class. But with that being my biggest complaint, I'd recommend taking the class
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I feel bad for everyone taking Biochemistry and have no choice but to take professor Fertuck. She is by far the worst professor I've ever had here at NU. She will trick you on exams!! But my advice to you is to study her ppt slides and read the book! You have no choice but to take her! I think it's time NU hire others Biochem professors! Good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I love Professor Fertuck and her passion for teaching biochemistry. It's a hard class and she makes you work for it but the exams are very fair and based completely on the lectures and slides. Attendance is absolutely mandatory and weekly quizzes and clicker participation factor into your grade. Overall, highly recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I honestly think Prof. Fertuck is great. Her exams are very hard but do-able if you study and go to tutoring as much as possible. Don't get me wrong, it's still hard to get even a B in exam, but hey if you do, you'll feel more proud (since its harder). Although tests are hard and questions sometimes confusing, i enjoyed biochem with prof Fertuck.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you can, try to take biochem somewhere else. This professor gives impossible exams, and even if you know the material very well, it is extremely hard to have even a B as each exam is only 30 questions. She is very condescending and doesn't explain the material well. Her lectures are all over the place too. Tutoring will be your best friend!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: For an intro class, her exams are far too difficult and she tries to trick you on purpose with her questions. Does "name practice" for students in her class to make you feel like she actually cares about learning who you are, but it's so she knows who's faking attendance with the lecture clicker questions. Exam grades solely determine course grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I had Dr. Fertuck for toxicology; it was a great choice. She comes off a little strong in the beginning of the semester, but by the end I really loved her, her teaching style, and the class overall. Quizzes every week that are easy and tests are As if you study. Highly recommend

While lectures were very dense and sometimes dry, the course was definitely doable. Professor Fertuck provides slides, highlights important readings, and is available for extra help after class. Though tests are difficult, and are only made up of 30 MC qs, putting in the work will yield good results.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Prof Fertuck is an amazing person! She's the only prof who takes the effort to learn over 200 peoples names. she does talk alot but extremely approachable, sweet, and knowledgeable. is fair with the material and lecture ppts are amazing but lectures were long. really cared for students and wanted them to learn and love the subject. the tests hard
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: she tells you exactly what is on the exams and is always available if you have any questions. if look over the textbook before lectures and show up to class you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Pr. Fertuck talks too much and doesn't really focus on the essentials of the lecture. Lectures are too long and not helpful at all.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is an unreasonable person who will not hear her students out. Exams are very difficult. Out of 150 students, only 2-3 will get perfect score on exams (exams are graded out of 30). There is no curve and only 2 exams and the final. Her lectures are unbearably boring. Spent way too much time on genetics review so must prepare yourself for MCAT.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Would take again only if I need more material for my experimental noise album. Holy pretentious yapperoni and cheese.
This professor will use great opportunities to push his christian values on you. Held us captive via the attendance code until we got a prayer service bestowed on us by the Fellowship club.

Gutierrez is the most pretentious professors I've ever had. Majority of students take 1001 as a requirement, but Gutierrez expects everyone to be passionate about music. He uses a condescending tone and acts as though he is smarter than everyone else. The workload is heavy considering the course level. It's not super hard but very time consuming.

This class required me to do more homework than all of my other classes combined. The TAs take weeks to get back and a few more weeks to grade assignments. But an A- or above is very attainable, even if you put in the bare minimum effort. Find a better NU-path class. Take this only if you have to.
There is very little work for this class, and the professor is very engaging. I wish the class were smaller, but the content is interesting.

85% of the class takes this course to fulfill a NU-path, and I genuinely had more homework and projects for this class than my STEM requirements. Attendance is MANDATORY even though it is a massive lecture hall, and don't even get me started about the TAs.

I genuinely cannot stand this professor with all of my being. You know those people who become professors just to hear themselves talk? That's him. He thinks he's the next coming of christ and that he's the smartest person to ever exist. PAINFUL
The class is very interesting, easy and taught well, pretty much an easy A but the way its set up sucks. no contact with the teacher.

Professor doesn't grade assignments himself so your entire grade rests upon his TA's which are horrible and don't give constructive feedback and are unreachable. Insane given the amount of projects we have and don't receive feedback at all.
Free A for the MUSC1001 class. Midterm was open note, 17 MCQ. Doesnt mind you being on your laptop in class, and only takes attendance 1/3 of the time. Pretty much just show up and you will get an A

It is our third week, and we're already getting grades that we don't have feedback on. I got a 72% from a module assignment, and the TA is unresponsive. Most of us are not interested in or in love with music; we are mainly STEM majors trying to have ONE easy course and this professor is just being unnecessarily complicated, giving a heavy workload.
I have him right now and I already hate it. He is just one of those bad professors

Professor Gutierrez acknowledges that this is just a required course to fulfill NEU Path. However, the workload is the equivalent of high-level music major classes. The class itself isn't hard; it's just immensely time-consuming, which doesn't help if you're in a hard major like STEM. The grade breakdown is insane, with multiple projects.

I took this class asynchronously, but it was very work-intensive. There were many group discussions, and the projects were pretty time-consuming.  There is also no communication with prof, you can only contact the TA's which I thought was weird.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: The material is easy and it would be more straightforward if all the articles weren't so pretentious and pseudo-sophisticated. The course flow is so confusing. Module 2 is due before module 1, module 4 (big project) is due before module 3. I don't know if it's purposeful or a mistake but it's so confusing to follow.

I took him summer 2, I didn't interact with him much but most of the assignments are easily although they get progressively more annoying as the semester goes on, but they are never terrible. He isn't too tough with grading either.

the class was okay i took it summer 2, definitely not just an easy a class but not difficult or anything, but this guy wrongly accused me of using ai and wouldn't believe any evidence i showed, not even edit history in the doc i wrote it in. him and the ta basically called me a liar and told me to learn to write better

Professor Gutierrez is hit or miss. He is a very nice guy but as a professor is extremely arrogant and speaks in a condescending tone with fancy language to make himself sound smarter. He is an easy grader though

I don't recommend Dr. Gutierrez. Everything is done by a TA, so there's zero personal connection. The rubrics aren't clear for grading. He'll ignore any emails for extensions. The TA also ignores any requests for help. MUSC 1001 is ok except for tedious projects. The grading is slow and feedback isn't given or its just bad. The TA polices diction.

Overall, easy A course. Weekly quizzes were not timed w/ 3 attempts. Assignments and discussion posts were fairly simple. There are 3 major assignments submitted through VoiceThread which is kinda of hard to navigate if you've never used it before. Everything graded by TA (so very opinion-based) with no feedback. Email responses from Prof are quick

There were many participation-based grades and weekly quizzes, all of which were simple but a lot of modules. There were a few major assignments- also not too bad, but he ended up putting some of these grades in the week final grades were due and doesn't add comments for why you got the grade you did. Working with VoiceThread is also a nightmare.

Says things like "superfluous" and "capricious" and "trepidation" just because he knows how to use those words, not because they actually add any sort of depth or specificity to his lectures... rolled my eyes a few times. How about making academia more accessible and inclusive by ensuring your lectures are something everybody can understand?

Do not take this class if you want to get an easy A or fulfill NUPath. The TA's grade the assignments and do not give any feedback, even if they take off a significant amount of points. Dr. Gutierrez is passionate but his lectures are long-winded and he will take attendance only when he feels like it. Choose a different class to fulfill electives.

This class would probably be a little more work than you'd expect, but definitely doable, and the online midterm and final are both very easy. I don't really get the hate this professor gets for "being pretentious" (?) He just seems like a good guy trying to give a good lecture. Nothing seemed pretentious to me.

Engaging lecturer who is clearly passionate about what he teaches. He teaches with a great depth of knowledge while still being entertaining. You can get a lot out of this class as long as you come in with an open mind

The pretentious professor made the class unbearable. The assignments, although they were very simple, were graded very harshly and with no feedback. He never submitted grades for multiple assignments and when this was brought to his attention, he reacted very defensively and put the blame back onto the student. Don't waste your time and money.

Prof. G is great! There are weekly async quizzes on the readings and class notes, but they're all very short and super easy. The class material is really interesting and it's clear that he's really invested in what he teaches. The course is good for anyone, regardless of prior music experience. He's really enthusiastic and it makes class very fun.

He is clearly passionate about music and knows the topics very well. However, he is very pretentious in his lectures and has an inflated ego. Content is easy, but if you aren't a music minor/major, can be a little harder. Also, not super clear in grading criteria. Easy content but annoying teacher.

Such an incredible professor and the content was always so engaging and interesting. Prof G is very smart + this class is so much fun! Projects are fun to complete and the exams are very doable. I highly recommend taking this class, you will not be disappointed and if you show up and pay attention, you will get an A.
Had a quiz, discussion, and assignment due every week. Quizzes, midterm, and final were all online and open book. Grading rubrics were provided, but not always clear.

Professor Gutierrez's class is organized by unchecked readings (he assigns an interesting and diverse curation of articles), weekly discussion posts, weekly open-note, untimed mini-quizzes, a few large projects, a midterm, and a non-cumulative final. He's thoughtful, passionate about class materials, and an engaging, qualified professor.
Really engaging class. The lectures are pretty interesting. You can tell he is passionate about what he teaches.
Professor Gutierrez is the best!!!!! His lectures are so interesting, and he is so knowledgeable and involved. Great man. great class.

The type of professor that uses big words to sound smart, but in reality is saying a bunch of nothing. Useless class that overanalyzes the minutia of things most people don't care about or will ever study again after this.

Overindulgent explanations on topics that are less than riveting. Tries WAY too hard to sound smarter than he is. Won't stop mentioning he lectures at Harvard. Just a lot for a 1000 level course imo...

I absolutely loved this class. Definitely take it with him if you are able to. Lectures were extremely interesting and he was clearly knowledgeable about every subject he taught. I would want to talk to him about music after class just to hear more about it. I am in love with this man.
do not. do not take this class. worst class she's so annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Just no. Run.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Julia is incredibly kind and knowledgeable, but her ENGW1111 class is one of the more difficult sections of ENGW1111 offered. If you are looking to truly improve your skills as a writer, her class is for you, but you will have to work hard to get an A. If you are looking for an easy A, look for a different professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: A bit confusing

You may think the assignments are complicated and a little bit boring. However, I'm pretty sure you can gain valuable skills needed for future classes. I will definitely recommend everyone to take her courses. I would say Professor Julia Garrett is really good and helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She is very caring about her students and is passionate about including everyone. There are typically three 1400-ish essays you have to write over the course of one semester, but each one has other pre-writing exercises that you submit all at once in a folder. Attendance IS mandatory, and if you miss six classes, you fail the course so beware!
A super nice and caring teacher who is always available inside and outside of the classroom. Also gives great feedback and gets to know each student individually.

A very nice and hardworking teacher who really cares about each individual student. She will take the time to get to know you and will give you very insightful feedback on your work for future improvement.
Amazing professor who cares for her students and encourages students to participate and have different understandings of the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Prof Garrett is super nice and her class is easy. She tends to be a bit longwinded and you might catch yourself staring at the clock for the last hour of class, but she's a good teacher and definitely makes herself available outside of class. If English isn't really your thing ad you just need this class to get your English credit, its perfect.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: My favorite prof this semester! She is so kind and caring. Her students are her top priority. Her class is very discussion based and not at all lecture-y. I always felt comfortable talking in class and her homework was never too hard (sometimes heavy). During the covid-19 crisis it became especially clear how much she loves her students. Miss her!

This guy is extremely difficult, if you have the option to avoid his class, I would. He spends most of his time discussing his history with general electric and osramslovania along with his kids accomplishments. He rarely responds to emails, gaslights any confrontation of an error, and doesn't teach or care about how his students are doing. Avoid!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I wish I could take an exam on his life because he talks about it for 80% of the class time. Very picky about how he wants things done, specifically with the Excel assignments. The subject itself is not difficult at all compared to financial accounting. The exams are relatively easy and straightforward.

Easy A, the hardest part is sitting through class. He's a chill guy but just talks SO much. I somehow made it to every single class and we usually got out early, he's a caring and good guy, but doesn't know his audience. Must but Mcgraw hill. Almost all serious due dates were flexible. Final exam study guide didn't really help.

super easy class but such a boring professor. if you can drag yourself out of bed and sit through his life stories to get the attendance points, it's the easiest accounting class you'll ever take. beware that all assignments are on mcgraw hill, so you'll need to pay for that. but otherwise such an easy class

The practice tests and study guides helped a lot. Got sick once, emailed him about it, he ignored it, and dropped my attendance grade even though I literally couldn't get out of bed. Honestly talks more about his life than accounting. I basically know his whole life story that I didn't ask for, because he tends to ramble a lot during classes.

Professor Glencross truly loves accounting and cares about his students. He tells you what to study to help prepare for exams, is pretty understanding, and is available outside of class. The cons are that his class sometimes lacks engagement, and he doesn't provide many extra resources/practice questions other than the textbook and slides.

He was a super chill professor and was pretty flexible when things came up for students. He moved a midterm and was super accommodating for students that had already made plans. His lectures felt a little long sometimes, but he's a nice guy and I learned a lot in his class.

He is a very nice guy but his lectures don't really help with exams. For quizzes he does let you know what's going to be on it which is helpful. The average for the last quiz was so bad that he had to drop it the average was around 40%. Overall, he is a nice guy but not a good professor.

Professor Glencross is passionate about accounting and loves to talk about his work experience/life advice. Weekly assignments and group project are easy good grades. He tells you questions on every exam (including final), but lots of the material is self taught and many test questions are not straight forward. Lots of work put in outside class.
absolutely terrible. he does not know how to teach and thinks his students care about his family's accomplishment. i dont care! can u pls teach

I don't see why glencross doesn't have a higher rating on here. For all quizzes/exams he tells you exactly what to study for each question, minimizing study time. Weekly homeworks aren't hard. Gives out bonus points throughout the semester for the final. Lectures get repetitive but he's j a sweet old man. J show up to class & study what he says

This professor is mean forget about sick days or even wellness days if your not there even one time your grade will go down. The homework is honestly not bad but the test and quizzes were difficult. Overall not the hardest class ever if you are willing to put the work in but he was just not a good professor.
