Glencross is boring and talks so much without any of the information he says being important.He doesn't even review the slides he's presenting before class, and spends more time talking about his son's accomplishments than accounting.I survived his class by self studying everything before the test.Don't take this class if your goal is to stay awake

Not a hard professor, but does repeat the same personal anecdotes over and over again throughout the year. He's passionate about what he teaches, but doesn't know how to structure his lectures very well. Relies heavily on the slides and usually ends up repeating them back to you, so just take notes on what is posted online and you'll be fine.

He is a very nice guy, but does not know how to teach. He spends the entire class telling personal anecdotes and attempts to connect them to the lecture but it does not work. He repeats the same stories over and over all semester and barely teaches you how to do the actual material.

Glencross may be a bit eccentric in his lectures, but he gets the point across. For each of the exams, he provides a study guide as well as goes through the content that will be covered. This was extremely helpful in preparing for exams. He hosts office hours 3-4 times per week, and you can always get your questions answered. Definitely take him!

Yes, his lectures are not super engaging, but he gives out GREAT study guides (basically the exam). He does not deserve a 2.8 rating. He is pretty boring, but you will learn a decent amount and get a good grade. Take this class for an A. It's accounting, no professor's lectures are interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very boring professor, not difficult but spends the entirety of class rambling about irrelevant  life stories and advice. Didn't learn much from this course, but not hard to do well.
No comment.

This was my second time taking Financial Accounting. he is WHY I PASSED! Thank you so much Dr. Glencross... He taught so well and easy to understand. He didn't move on until everyone got it and he kept lectures interesting. Great guy.

His lectures are boring at times, and he does talk about his personal life stories a lot. His class isn't difficult if you put in some slight work though, and he's an extremely fair grader who really does want to help his students out.

Dr. Glencross is my fav prof. The last few reviews had to be written by the same dude... He relays life stories and talks about his personal life, without fail. But it did not take up a majority of classes. We spent most time clarifying back and forth with the Prof. He wouldn't move on unless we got it. Will tell you what's on the exams!

Avoid Professor Glencross's financial accounting class at all costs. His teaching is appalling, with nearly all discussions focused on his USELESS "life stories" &  achievements rather than the actual course. The class's performance throughout the semester was abysmal.

AVOID Professor Glencross's financial accounting class. He's a poor teacher, focusing mainly on his son's achievements, not the actual course material. The class final average was a dismal 67, with some test exam scores as low as a paltry 37. I got a C+ in the class, my lowest Northeastern grade. Professor Glencross should be fired immediately.

DO NOT RECOMMEND!!! worst accounting class ever. going to class felt pointless, never taught during lectures, only shared life stories (the same ones throughout the whole semester). felt extremely unprepared for the final had to teach myself. northeastern needs to get rid of this guy.. HE CAN'T TEACH!!!  and i never wanna look at accounting again.

Lectures consist of 60% monotonic speaking, 20% completely irrelevant, gibberish personal stories (such as how his son got a job [nobody cares dude]), and 10% pithy life lessons that can be deduced by a middle schooler. The best thing Northeastern can do is fire this guy as he is doing a disservice to his students.

I took his ACCT1201 class. The guy is a former shoe salesman (so he has said), which should tell you all you need to know. He's a nice guy (sometimes) but teaching is not about being nice, it's about educating your students. At that he is a complete failure.

Professor Glencross was a terrible professor and I'm so glad I will never have to take his class again. Super boring and the material is very tedious. This class is made worse due to the fact that everything we learned how to do is now done on a computer so the material is outdated. I hated this class and prof Glencross is not a good teacher at all

Lots of stories but always cover what we have to! Most application learning can only be done through homework anyways. Spoon-feeds exam content as much as possible and provides so much information on grades and expectations. Lectures are fine, it's a boring subject but he makes it enjoyable! Won't move on until you get it. Very sensible and sweet!

Prof. Glencross does care about his student's success, but he simply reads his slides instead of explaining what the slide is trying to convey. He brings up examples from his own days as an accountant that lead to other tangents about his own experiences instead of the concepts we need to learn. Learn by doing the homework and textbook problems.

Prof Glencross is one of my favorite prods. a sweet man with the best of intentions for his students. you can tell he wants us to learn, not just from the textbook/lectures, but also from his experiences. he strays from the class material, but the only way to really grasp accounting is by practicing anyway. plus keeps us awake from boring material!

Glencross is nice but his lectures are about 60% him talking about his career in accounting, 30% him telling a story he told last class but forgot, and 10% teaching the material. His tests are quite easy throughout but the final is made by the department head and you will know nothing because of his non teaching style. Prepare to teach yourself.

He is a very nice and organized professor who cares about his field and the material he teaches. Sometimes he can go off on tangents, but his stories are entertaining and we always remain well on schedule. He hopes that we all become future accountants.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Glencross is very caring and easy to talk to. I always felt comfortable in his class and he always wanted the best for his students. Lectures can be boring and the class requires a lot of studying but having him as a professor made the class more enjoyable. He is super nice and respects his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The professor does not teach very effectively. Class time wasn't very efficient as he tended to read off his presentation slides and repeat the same stories of his life as an accountant. He seems very knowledgeable about accounting but is not great at teaching the material. I learned more from self-studying on Youtube than from his lectures.

He doesn't really teach you how to do the actual maths in accounting but is more focused on theory so you'll have to teach yourself. He also tends to go off-topic by repeatedly talking about his experiences. However, he is very kind and wants his students to pass. There is a lot of homework but it is very helpful when it comes to exams.

ACCT 6200 - He's just great. I want to retake his class just for the stories about Sylvania, GE, and his current AP consulting project - hilarious. Do the HW.. He's extremely reasonable and he wants his students to succeed in understanding the way financial statements flow - he's not interested in making you memorize useless accounting jargon.

Prof Glencross is definitely a little old school but he's very kind. He makes his own study guides so the exams were very doable and the homework's easy. Tells a lot of personal stories but still covers everything he needs to in lecture. He made accounting bearable.

The guy is super outdated in every aspect in his life. Incredibly boring and inapplicable, goes on about the same stories from his life over and over again. Homework is alright, but will not curve any grades. We had a 60% mean for an exam and he just does not care if it is hard or not.
Nice guy but assigns A TON of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Really nice guy but he spends entire class telling stories of his experiences. Long winded stories that are somewhat relevant only because he was an accountant. Heavy use of power point, each slide he would tell a story. I had to do a lot of learning on my own because we couldn't get to relevant material. Not necessarily a tough teacher though
Because, He explains the topic with the examples which is easily understandable and also he is very much friendly
Super easy grader, but he has no idea what he's talking about. Drones on and on about his work experience, which is almost entirely unrelated to the subject matter.

took cost accounting with Mr. Glencross.  he relies on power point a lot, but did an overall fair job compared to others at NEU who use power-point as a brain substitute.   you can tell he enjoys teaching and is not just coming to class to earn his tenure.  if he lectured he'd have been that much better because i think he really knows his stuff.

The class is online and asynchronous which it convenient. The homework can be quite time-consuming and challenging. The exams were too difficult, a 60+ was an A, so there was a big curve. Take it if you need to do a summer class and want to stay off campus.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just dont waste the time and money. Just take it with someone else .
Rough class don't take this as it will actually nuke your gpa. Will make you question your existence. Although Asynchronous class best if you take with another professor in person.

don't listen to people saying this is a good class, 4 exams that's all your grade is nothing he asks on the test is what is on the slides no matter how many notes you take you will not be able to get a good grade for the exams, take any other teacher but him

i got disowned after this class, i can't describe how xxxxx this class is and he is, i would walk into ongoing traffic rather than ever see this man again. i hope he is very happy with all the lives he has ruined. do not take this class
i genuinely believe he hates students and wants them to suffer, and i did. i will never get back the semester i spent crying over this course. don't take him.

Yes he recycles his lectures, I understand, but he constantly updates his lectures if needed, and he's very accessible to ask questions directly. Best asynchronous course I've taken, challenging but you learn a lot. Tests were creative focusing on actual learning not just "using" what you memorized. 
Sad to see my favorite professor rated this low.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
FYI: The low ratings on here are from the few students who failed the class and won't be of use to you. 

Info you want --> There are 4 exams (25% each) and weekly HW is purely for practice. All exams are open-note (take notes from his lecture videos and you'll be well-equipped). Exams are super tough, but he ends up curving the class generously.

bro like just don't do it tbh like i thought since it's online it'll be chill NOOOOO the lecture vids r from 2016 and the tests r not related to them at all and he doesn't give u any indication of what's going to be on the test. i stopped looking both ways before crossing the street bc of this class.
Yo this jit will nuke ur grade, class is rigged. The exams are so cooked compared to what we learn. Like we're expected to build the burj khalifa using IKEA instructions.

This class was genuinely one of the most terrible experiences of my entire life. He does not teach anything, but the things he does talk about aren't even on the tests. There is no credit given for anything other than the exams, and the average for most was a 30%. The green line is looking really enticing right now.

This is genuinely the worst class and professor I have ever had in my entire academic career. He uses lecture VIDEOS from several years ago. Neither the lecture slides nor the videos are related to whatever questions he threw at us on the exams. Our entire grade is based only on exams and he made them so extremely difficult. I am so sad and tired.
Professor is not

don't take his classes please!! I wish he cared more about getting his student preparing for his EXTREMELY hard exams then just using a bunch of videos from many years ago. The concepts are exciting but I can't say I've walked out of his class learning anything. I felt like i just wasted tuition money, great guy but very bad professor.

CS3000, online algo class. I'm not sure how lecture videos from 2016 are supposed to prepare me for the hardest exams I have ever taken. Your notes will not help in the slightest. Prepare to get 50% at maximum. It does run on bell curve tho, so you probably wont fail. Your gpa is taking a beating tho.
Awful, dont choose
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE HIM

intelligent guy but bad prof. recycles lecture recordings, kinda rude/discouraging, makes exams significantly harder than homework or lectures. TAs explicitly say that the exams are made more difficult than examples from class, but he also does not allow exam cheatsheets to have any material from outside of class/slides. 40% curve tho!
Having an in-depth understanding of summations is huge for this course.
Don't really know what a good professor could have been for this course, Cauz I took this course in accelerate. But yea it was really difficult for me.

Was genuinely one of the worst decisions to take his class. He made everything incredibly rigorous and needlessly complicated, and didn't understand how busy his students are. His notes might help a bit for the homework, but you might as well give up for the tests.

He does not follow northeastern grading guidelines and is very specific about grading. I ignored the previous comments to take another professor for discrete because I saw he taught online and I wanted an online class. However, don't make my same mistake, take another professor!

Greg might be better suited for upper-level courses—his intro math class had harsh grading, with zeros for minor formatting mistakes and no partial credit. Avoid unless you're ready to commit serious time to a 1000-level class.

Taking a course under this professor was a nightmare. He fails students instantly if caught but applies integrity rules inconsistently. He flagged Exam 1 but ignored suspicious answers in Exam 3. Communication was poor, grading unclear, and stress overwhelming. Avoid him if possible — it's not worth the risk.

Very intelligent but not sure if he should be teaching an intro course to mainly freshmen. His teaching style paired with the async class isn't the best combo and can be tough at times. Very willing to meet with students at any time and tries his best to explain the mistakes we made. Grades are low, but grade mapping helps. For example, a 60+ = A.
Just wait to take this class later on. Not worth having him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took his CS5800 class, and I think his class is the best asynchronous class that I have taken. You need to spend time to understand what's happening in the slides but his slides are complete; it includes all the information that you need to know to solve exam problems. You just need to understand the material enough to explain it to others.
He grades extremely strictly and gives poor or no feedback. The given slides are somewhat confusing.

One of the worst professors I've ever had at Northeastern. Intentionally tries to confuse students and blames them for getting things wrong. Average and median are below 50 on all assignments. Makes students want to stop trying because of how difficult it is to get partial credit. Do not recommend. Just wait and take it with another professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He seems indifferent to student needs. The grading of homework and exams is excessively strict. I found no advantage in taking his classes.

This class does not follow NEU's curriculum for discrete. Prof himself confirmed this and seems not to care. Some lectures are straight-up recordings from his algorithms class. Even TAs have told me how they see this class as unfair. Professor discourages outside help from tutors or videos online. You are expected to get ~40s on exams.

Assignments and exams can be difficult. Professors and TAs have plenty of Office hours to ask for help. Professors have their own grading scale, so don't pay too much attention to superficial scores, work harder and you will get high grades. Everyone's scores may not be high, but the grading scale is different from the normal, maybe 65 is an A.

HW is graded extremely harshly and you can easily get a 0 on the HW for simple formatting mistakes. Exams are online and typically half the class scores are below 50-60. Lectures aren't great since it's asynchronous. You're better off taking the class in person with a different professor. Feedback on HW is vague and honestly not helpful.

She is the sweetest woman EVER. Made class so much fun and she is so accommodating. Truly a real-life Mrs Frizzle and I feel like I learn so much in her class. I took a gamble with this class given I had no clue what it was but god am I so happy I did,if you have the chance to take her please don't second guess it.She is so smart and awesome.
she's great just wish she was a stricter grader tbh

NUMBER ONE PROFESSOR AT NORTHEASTERN. His class is super easy BUT if you want to learn things you totally can because he is quite passionate. It is definitely what you get out of it, which I enjoyed a LOT. HIGHLY recommend!

I love professor Chichirau! Such a sweet and caring guy. His class is really simple, we have weekly quizzes that you take whenever you want, with unlimited time, and can use any resources you'd like. He records all his lectures on zoom as well.

VERY easy class. Neighborhood walk and reflection takes up one class a week. The lecture takes up the other class, but lectures are not needed at all. Discussion post and walk reflection are only weekly assignments other than the big final paper which is broken up into five smaller papers. But, he is a great guy and an interesting lecturer.
He's okay. Super easy, but honestly didn't learn much. The neighborhood walks were definitely the highlight of the class. Nice guy.

The class is fun, and he conducts weekly walks around Boston. You have to try to not get a good grade on the essays, which don't even have to have proper APA citations. Since everything is based on essays and discussion posts, he doesn't take attendance and his class was practically empty by the fourth week. This is a good elective for slackers.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: George. What a great guy. Makes you want to come to class every day, even for 100min lectures, and the weekly neighborhood walks are a great experience too. Consider yourself lucky if you get a class taught by George.
This class is an experience. George is quite a guy. Wow. Take this class.

Very cool prof! If you want a deep understanding of the mathematical side of Machine Learning, take his class. He focuses heavily on the theoretical aspects, which is rare and valuable. Despite the rigor, he is extremely understanding and patient and is always willing to go the extra mile to explain things during office hours.
Sometimes he UNSURE of what hes trying to say. Poor communication skills.

had him for cs4100 but that wasnt an option. Freest A of my life. Midterm was takehome, attendance wasn't mandatory, and homework assignments had so much starter code I was basically just uncommenting lines and submitting. Learned absolutely nothing though.
He is a highly approachable professor with a wealth of knowledge. As long as you complete the homework and put effort into a strong final project, achieving a high grade is very manageable!

This course covers conventional machine learning materials. I have taken cs6140 under another professor, which actually covered similar topics compared to this course. But that one is more profound and interesting, I guess it's due to the teaching style. This course could be more solid and easy-to-understand for whom just got into this area
Course itself is great, I have learnt quite a lot. However, I wouldn't recommend the professor to take his session on. He is great, but not good at teaching.

I learned a lot from Professor Zhang's lectures. Each presentation/class had a well-structured flow and the content was relevant and useful. He is also very well-versed in the topic which meant that additional comments were typically insightful. He also cares about students and wants to help them in any way he can.
It's ok class. Prof knows a lot in his area, but he's not a good teacher.

Professor Zhang is clearly very knowledgeable in the field of machine learning, and did an amazing job explaining difficult theoretical concepts during lectures. His class includes a reasonable mix of machine learning theory (underlying mechanisms of machine learning algorithms/techniques) and opportunities to apply them to practical datasets.

Professor Zhang is approachable and encourages students to ask questions. Office hours are well-utilized, we often go to his office after class to ask questions, and he was always patient and happy to answer.

Professor Zhang displayed exceptional knowledge and passion for machine learning, making complex topics understandable and engaging. Also, feedback was always constructive and insightful, significantly aiding in our learning process. Besides that, I love how the final project was laid out and presented which gave students many free discussions.

A must-take class if you want to claim that you understand why deep learning works. He comprehends and complements the difficult-to-self-learn textbooks very well so that the large topics fit within a semester. Through the project, he trains you how to read, critique, and build on the latest literature. He is very considerate and accessible.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Take other courses if you have chance. The grade is fair, but if you really wanted to learn something from the course, probably NO. You won't feel any passion from the instructor at all.

Professor Zhang taught this course very well and I have learned a lot from this course.

If you are going to do research, please this course. This course will prepare you very well for research.

Professor Zhang is a nice person and answers students' questions patiently.

The course is tough with often unclear lectures. Grading is fair, but a foundation knowledge is recommended. Prof focuses on theory, lacking practical examples. Would recommend other courses at Khoury unless you don't have any other choices.

Prof Zhang's class was a good mix of applied and theoretical ML techniques and was a fair grader. He involves the students in his teaching a lot which was engaging and conducive to learning, so would recommend this class! It helped me with foundations for my ML research and I think others would benefit too.

CS7140 is a challenging course, but you will learn a lot if you dive into it. Please take CS6140 machine learning before you pick this one, or you will feel lost for the entire semester. Professor Zhang is knowledgeable about the mathematical theory of ML. The classroom atmosphere is relaxed. Feel free to ask any questions when you confuse!

I found this class to be incredibly difficult and lectures/materials hard to follow. Homework required a lot of research on my own and slides weren't helpful. However, I thought the grading was fair and the TAs/professor tried their very best to ensure everyone performs well. As long as you show that you put in effort, you should be fine.

He is the best lecturer if you want to learn machine learning. He teaches tough topics effectively using his own words that makes us much easier to learn the topics by reading texts. He has clear instructions and offers support as needed on assignments so that each student can learn through these assignments.

If you have no experience with ML,take cs6140 or NLP,before taking this course. Prof. Zhang didn't provide any solid examples for each theory. I personally just learnt the topic from textbooks/online sources,and used the lectures for review.Northeastern should hire some professors who know how to teach and convey their knowledge to the students

Prof Zhang's 7140 class was focused mostly on mathematical deep learning theory. The materials were in-depth, and I learned a lot from the lectures and the assignments. The class is challenging and can get quite technical, so I can see why some students struggled with it. Be sure to allocate enough time for his class as it can be quite demanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Zhang is extremely knowledgeable in the mathematical and theoretical aspects of ML and he managed to teach the core ideas very well in this course. He was very accessible outside class (also responds to emails in minutes) and provided excellent guidance throughout the course and in picking up research topics after the course as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This is an amazing class. Prof. Zhang is very approachable. His brilliance and extensive knowledge of the field of machine learning are obvious. You can study a lot from this class, especially for the theoretical and mathematical parts. He is always available to help and efficiently explains every lesson!

If you want to learn deep learning, look at some free edX/MIT classes; if you're taking this for PhD, I'd recommend taking other sessions other than his. The lectures are super dry, often skipping showing how to solve actual problems. He didn't did do well explaining the concepts. We had to check lectures on the internet to fully understand them.
Excellent course. Prof Zhang is very polite, accessible and extremely knowledgeable in his area. Anyone pursing machine learning/deep learning will gain a lot from this course.

Took his course last year. He's probably good at his field but the course is poorly designed. Just read off the slides which just copy&paste from other universities' slides. He didn't organize them well and plan beforehand. Often changed the assignment's requirements at the last mins. Very little passion for teaching. Doesn't care about students.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He grades too tough and his office hours was not helpful at all. He has a big ego. Dont take guys class if you actually wanna do good in calc 2.
Great professor who wants and loves to see his students succeed. Couldn't recommend anymore.

Tough but fair. If you don't show up to class, you can expect a rough experience. Lectures are the main method of teaching, though a textbook is provided. Again, you need to go to class, or he will remember that you skipped. Also, he can come across harshly, but is rarely actually rude.

If you see this man listed as your teacher do whatever you can to switch. He has an insane ego and talks down to his students constantly in lectures. He is also the most unfair grader I've ever seen. he teaches like he is trying to make himself feel better about his sad little life.

Hany Sadaka is the worst teacher I have ever had in my entire life. He has an extremely annoying ego and uses his power as a teacher to make himself feel better about what I can only imagine is the saddest life a "man" has ever lived..
guy is just rude to classmates
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Took this class last semester and this is not an easy elective whatsoever, so be prepared to put in a lot of effort and probably still not do good. The class can be very confusing and doesn't make sense. Problem sets are really difficult and felt like it was almost impossible to keep up. Lectures are long too.
I'm not sure I learned much of anything in this semantics class, but I love the professor.
Professor Littlefield was awesome! Super easy course :)

Heather was possibly the best first semester professor a person could have. She was incredibly welcoming and kind, always available/willing to meet outside of class, and the perfect amount of passionate to keep students engaged. There are weekly-ish paper assignments and she left consistently leave helpful feedback. Definitely take her classes!!
heather is the best ever

Prof. Littlefield is amazing!! I absolutely loved her class. Content was so fun, and I looked forward to every day I had her class. Homework is graded fairly and pretty easy because all the content is covered well in class. I highly recommend this class/professor! My grade was above a 105% the whole semester because of extra credit. 10/10 recommend

This course was definitely harder than expected, but it was extremely fun in how tedious it was. Though the lecture videos are quite lengthy, Heather explains each topic very clearly- making the homework straightforward- and has extra credit for each module. She is easy to reach, incredibly kind, and understanding. Would absolutely recommend!

Heather is excellent! The class is a lot of work since there is so much to cover! Expect about 2-3 hours of videos per module, and a problem set for each module. Further, there is a summative problem set which is much longer. She was also accessible outside of class, and was happy to meet over Zoom! I definitely want to take another class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is awesome, kind, and her lecture videos were so interesting! However, the class was a lot more time-consuming than I was expecting. MANY hours worth of lecture videos to watch a week, and you can't get away without watching them because then you can't complete the assignments. But if you do all the work, it is pretty straightforward and easy.

Professor Littlefield is an amazing professor. I took her class Summer 1 and it was really enjoyable. Her lectures are a little long and the homework does take some time. If you are looking for an A, you just have to do the work and it's easy to. There is extra credit for every module that can add over 10% to your overall grade if you do it all.

littlefield is approachable and takes the time to care about you individually. lectures were instructional and easy to follow. no exams; grade mostly homework-based (~1-2hrs of hw/week; no readings). only downside is lecture slides are kinda bare--most explanation is done verbally; recordings aren't accessible--so prepare to pay attention in class
Heather is incredible and everyone should take a class with her if they have the chance. Super friendly and genuinely cares about the students' thoughts.

Littlefield's honors inquiries was very fun. Discussions were a huge part of what we worked on. She welcomed every question and idea, and they were my favorite part of class. It's a lot of reading but the reading is interesting, and she wants everyone to do really well. The projects are tedious, but she gives clear feedback and you'll learn a lot!

The assignments were very time-consuming and not always straightforward. However, she is very accessible for outside help as she will schedule 1:1s with you outside office hours to help on projects. Checking projects by her before I submitted really boosted my grade for the second half of the course.

Ling Analysis is a hard class but Heather did her best to make sure we all were prepared for writing our papers and doing our research. She does the best with what she has considering each intro to ling class is different meaning everyone starts with a different knowledge base. She's super understanding and is always willing to work with you.

A lot of reading, but we had really good group discussions! This was my first class in linguistics, and I really enjoyed it overall! Heather is very compassionate and genuinely wants everyone to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Nice lady. Very knowledgable. Expects way too much from her students. Avoid courses with her, if you can. The linguistics department has great professors, all of whom are much more reasonable than her.

Professor Littlefield is really funny and has interesting stories. However, I do feel that she is an overly tough grader. The assignments are interesting, but it's hard to know what she expects, and you get points off seemingly at random sometimes. I also agree with one of the other commenters that she can be somewhat condescending at times.

Incredibly difficult course that requires a lot of outside work. However, it's most definitely worth it. You will leave that class a much better student. Heather is incredibly kind and offers all sorts of help and takes all of your comments and concerns seriously.

Heather is very kind and helpful most of the time. She can also be condescending. Her assignments are very difficult and she's a pretty hard grader. She's always open to answering questions though. The key is to explain yourself in the papers that you write for her class; anything is admissible with an explanation.

Extremely nice lady, always glad to discuss the material after class and very intelligent and knowledgeable - you can tell she loves linguistics! However, Linguistic Analysis was one of the most work-intensive and harshly-graded classes I've ever taken. She's a great teacher, but almost too tough to recommend.

One of the best profs I've ever had. She's very smart and really knows her stuff. She's also incredibly helpful and HILARIOUS! I looked forward to her class every time. She is the goofiest, nicest person. Answers emails within the hour. She challenges you, but it's always within reason. love her love her LOVE her! I was so sad when class ended.

Good: super nice, passionate, incredibly knowledgable, willing to help, and energetic. Bad: apparently doesn't realize students have other classes and assigned a gratuitious amount of work (and I didn't procrastinate). Reasonable final. Tips: pick the simplest language you can and take phonology, syntax, and semantics courses first. It will help.
The class was really interesting but the work was really demanding.  She's slow on grading, so don't expect to see that paper you worked all night on ever again.

Work intensive class. Thoroughly goes over concepts and brings people of all levels to the same speed. Huge language research project of your choice - best not to procrastinate. She's tough but very fair and funny as heck. Quite dedicated and passionated about linguistics and helping her students.

nice, sweet woman. horrible class. attendance is mandatory (only allowed 2 absences..excused or not) structure of class very boring, most notes i've ever taken in a class. if intro to linguistics isnt required for you, i reccommend taking a different class.

Littlefield is an awesome teacher and person. She's sooo funny and down-to-earth, which is VERY refreshing in academia. Class has a very communal feel. My time commitment for the class is low since the only assignments are a few homeworks and a final. No book is necessary-- just take good notes and you'll be fine.
Great sense of humor and quirky lectures kept me interested and regularly attending class, but the homework kicked my ass. Probably the first class I'm failing but would recommend anyway.

She is a great teacher, and I would tell anyone to take this class. You need to spend some time on the homeworks but if you do them you will do fine on the final! its an easy class if you put in some time....she has a great sense of humor and wants to be there teaching you!

Teaches well. Good sense of humor. Homework was tough. Exams were reasonable. No need for a textbook. Has a passion for what she teaches. I recommend her for any linguistics course. She could be more clearer and give the bigger picture instead of focusing on details all the time. Great teacher. Always cheerful.
Very helpful. Really bright.  Loves the fulani culture.  I loved to learn in her class.  I  still remember the samoan culture from that book.
Challenging homework--NOT busy work. Grades attendance and participation. Good sense of humor. Expects a lot from students, but grades fairly.  You will EARN your grade. Good office hours.

Heather is an amazing teacher.  She's interested in what she has to say and makes the classes fun.  She's a tough grader, but she's usually fair.  She puts in a lot of effort and expects her students to do the same.

Prof. was a very effective teacher in this basic linguistics course.  Any problems with clarity came from the course material not from her lectures.  It was a challenging class.  However, she used a great sense of humor and good teaching strategies to explain the concepts.  Enjoyed it thoroughly.

Hard but interesting class. Very smart prof, work every class, mainly lecture. homework, midterm, and final, all difficult and time consuming. EC offered on homework. tests aren't curved. highly recommend going to class otherwise material very hard to understand.

when I asked her for help, she didnt help in the slightest. retaking this course for the second time, and I know the material pretty well and have a good understanding of it, im just a bad test taker. But when I told her this, she said that I shouldnt say i know the material because i wouldnt be taking this course again.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Her lectures are extremely easy to understand and she checks in with the class to make sure they are keeping up before moving on. Before every quiz/exam she posts what textbook exercises are going to be questions (just w/different numbers) so its super easy to get As if you practice the questions enough. 8 quizzes lowest 2 dropped, 2 exams, 1 final

Such a cool lady. Her classes are tough, and she's a tough grader, but she's very passionate about the subject matter and is super organized. Even though the class was hard, I learned A LOT. Amazing professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. Hower explained concepts really well, and she was really passionate about the material and math overall. Problem sets were graded fairly. For the tests, you are always prepared material wise and she gives so many study resources(4 practice exams, multiple extra practice worksheets, recommended exercises) if you put effort, guaranteed A.

I'm not even a student at Northeastern, but her youtube vids are saving me rn as my own linear algebra professor is boring af. Dr. Hower teaches with an enthusiasm that makes learning this a lot easier.
Sensational professor youtube videos are carrying right now

Dr. Hower is so sweet, passionate about math, and cares about her students' wellbeing and success. She uses class time well, and she's easily reachable outside of class. Office hours are a great resource. She teaches Calc 1 assuming that students haven't taken calculus before. The weekly quizzes are easy to study for with the provided practice.
Great lectures and she was so sweet I loved her and would definitely recommend her for calc 1!!

If you have to take calc 1 and you aren't very confident in your math skills, take Dr. Hower's class.  As previous reviewers have said, she has a youtube channel with all her lectures/pre quiz/exam/final videos available.  She wants everyone to succeed and she is readily available to help at office hours.  Also she drops the lowest quiz grade.

I can not say anything bad about her. She is such a great lecturer and if you fall behind she has recorded lectures on her YouTube channel. She will tell you exactly what is on each quiz/test. Even after finishing this class, I still used her recorded lectures to help me with calc 2. She is absolutely incredible and arguably the best you can take.

Hower was a great Calc 1 professor and is always available for questions or help if needed. She drops many of your lowest quiz scores and wants you to succeed. Definitely try to enroll in her classes if possible.

she is so kind and considerate! I gained so much confidence and love for math due to her teaching and guidance. very friendly and helpful through tough times this term. passionate about math (DE, integrals) which makes students love the course material. very meticulous, organized, systematic. challenging grader to make us really love problemsolving

Hower was incredible! Truly cares about students understanding the topics and some extra credit opportunities. Attendance not required but definitely helps to go to class. Gives weekly quizzes and allows time for a quiz practice worksheet the class before. If I had to take more math classes I would take them with Hower!

(M/Th 100 min) Prof. Hower is great. She is so caring and understanding and really wants you to love integrating and differential equations. She does use problems from textbook but, posts content on canvas, you don't need to buy it. Her lecture pattern is: lecture day, lecture/practice problems w/ 10min break; quiz day lecture/quiz w/ 10min break.
She is the only math professor who I have felt like I actually learned from and understood
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Hower is so passionate about math and it makes learning from her enjoyable. She cares about her students and is always willing to help. Definitely recommend taking Calculus 2 with her.

Best math course I have ever taken. She genuinely cares about her students, is quick to respond to emails, and really helpful during office hours. Definitely one of my favorite professors. If you are taking any calc class take it with Valerie Hower.
Valerie was amazing. Her lectures were engaging and she was very straight forward with what she put on the tests and quizzes. Highly suggest to anyone taking any of the calcs.
Fantastic Professor - cares about her lectures and students a lot.
Good professor great notes and explanation. Highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: AMAZING professor and person. Has to be one of the best professors at the school, if not THE best. Goes over everything you need to know (nothing excessive), super sweet, great at responding to questions, quizzes and exams match exactly what is covered. Very responsive via email, extremely helpful at office hours. HIGHLY HIGHLY recommend!!
Prof. Hower was a good teacher, I recommend her for anyone looking for someone who goes slowly over Calc 1 topics. Gives lot of review materials and has lot of videos for the course material.

She is a literal genius when it comes to math. I was shocked to hear that calc 2 was difficult but after taking her course which she teaches it really well whether in class or through her online lecture videos. She does move at a certain pace so as long as you keep up with her schedule you won't fall behind. Had her for math reasoning. Highly rec

Amazing professor! I was not much of a math person before but she makes it seem so accessible and I love how passionate she is about the topic and how willing she is to help. 10/10 definitely recommend.

Dr. Hower is amazing! She is one of the best professors I have ever had. She prepares you for each test/quiz and answers every question until the student understands. There is plenty of test prep material and videos available, and she is always down to chat if needed. The class is fast-paced and homework is optional but she is amazing. 10/10

Professor Hower is an excellent professor. Her lectures are extremely helpful and she posts recorded lectures if you happen to miss class. The way she teaches is extremely effective and she answer questions clearly. One of the best math profs I've ever had.

Hower is a top-tier teacher. She has a genuine love for both mathematics and teaching, so she's always happy to answer any questions. When I had a major technological hiccup right before a midterm, she was extremely helpful and accommodating. It's clear that she genuinely wants her students to succeed and will always do what she can to help.

Valerie Hower is GOAT. She genuinely wants to see her students winning and every lecture is also recorded on youtube for easy access. Teaching is fire and she's super generous with extensions/giving back points. Best prof I've had yet. She never fumbles the bag
Professor Howers Youtube videos saved me in discrete math!
Lovely teacher, really upbeat, and clear in explanation

Prof Hower is GREAT. She really loves calculus 2 so she really puts in her best effort into teaching. She goes over a lot of practice problems, and is a pretty easy grader. She is very clear in her lectures. DO not take calculus 2 with anyone else!
Very caring

Let me speak! I'm innocent of laziness. Of that, I swear. I showed up. Repeatedly. Across campus. In all weather. With hope in my heart and confusion in my backpack. I'm guilty of a far more monstrous crime: wanting clarity. I've been on trial for that my entire semester.
Very sweet and understanding of their students. Class is easy to pass if you participate and put in effort!
They is a very good professor, and the class is easy and just talking, but don't miss classes and finish homework on time.

It was a very chill class. Be ready to participate in discussions, but overall class was pretty chill. If you show that effort was made in your essays and homework, it's an easy A. They are understanding and allow you to make up low grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: very easy a

This professor truly cares about students, offering thoughtful feedback and creating an inclusive, supportive environment. The class is well-structured and manageable, with complex topics made easy to understand. They handle tough conversations with respect and empathy. Highly recommended for a positive learning experience!
Very easy professor. All you do is 2 essays and a presentation. Effort based class, show some effort, you get an A. Overall, highly recommend!

Slides are AI and every class is the first time he's seeing them. Nothing on Canvas, so take notes! Exam questions are subjective multiple-choice questions! Won't go over exam questions with you, when you ask questions in class,his answers are opinion. I can't believe I paid for this course, the lectures are literally Cluad!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Genuinely I wanted to drop out of this class. Severely disappointed by the lack of care and teaching quality from a class that I had such high hopes for. Do not take this class if you can avoid it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: An intro class should not have been this difficult. He always contradicted himself and often let his own personal feelings affect the test answers. If you want to remember random stats in the textbook and his own facts that he made up in class, then take the class. I wanted to drop out everyday.

He is quite boring when lecturing, and often just reads off the powerpoint. There are 5 exams in total, 4 midterms and one final. Your final grade is purely based on how well you do on all the exams, there are no chances for extra credit. Group project was easy to complete. It is a content heavy course and requires lots of memorization!!

Very long and unnecessarily detailed exams with fill-in-the-blank questions that could be random adjectives. His lectures would not tell you much, as he would just read from a long note sheet. Office hours were not very helpful, and he would not allow us to view previous exam performance at all. Your grade won't reflect your level of understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I'm sure he's better in other classes, he's a nice guy I enjoyed his lectures, but this course genuinely gave me gray hairs. It was impossible to get even an A-. I poured hours into this course as I love the topic, but it would've never been enough as his exams were unpredictable--completely new info, subjective answers, or just incorrect answers
Save yourself. The moment I was done from the final I saw god and I'm not even religious.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I fought tooth and nail in this class and I'm not dumb. Slides are poorly made. Was a little bit rude in the emails I got from him. Exam questions were crazy specific (random stats or names of people from an event). I didn't even know what to study since his lectures sometimes contradicted the textbook. Refused to round grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams are loosely based on the lectures, even though he says they are entirely based on them. It makes it hard to study for exams, and the questions are crazy specific. Overall, not a horrible professor, but would absolutely not be my first pick.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is soooo easy! Yes he goes off-topic in class and makes the same jokes over and over, but his tests are so easy it doesn't even matter. He also gave almost everyone 100% on a group project worth 20% of our grade. He changed the course over the last couple years so only look at the more recent reviews to get a real understanding of the course.

Honestly, he was a fine professor other than his slides and exams. He taught course content very well, however slides weren't organized, and exams often included typos, were wordy, or covered info not found in exams or in the textbook. He's ok professor if you need to take psych foundations, but there are definitely other professors.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As a professor he did make lectures very interesting and tried his very best to be engaging, which had made goiing to class less of a chore. Tests align more with the textbook and often focus on the small details you would least expect. Chapters in the textbook are very long and sometimes feel tedious to read. Exams are your only real grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Genuinely one of the worst professors I have had at NU. Very challenging tests that often don't follow the notes presented in class (which by the way were literally a word document that he would just read off of during lectures). Yes he is funny, but funny doesn't make up for bad teaching. Avoid him unless you want to dedicate yourself to his class
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Professor Von Strunck pays for reviews on Rate My Prof, all of the reviews are the same but in reality he is a mediocre professor who struggles to convey ideas effectively. 

He often puts answers on quizzes that contradict the textbook and align with his personal views, making it impossible to study. The hardest Psych 1101 class for sure.

one of the best profs. i had at northeastern. highly intelligent and knows his stuff for sure. i just signed up to take another class with him. come to class, do your assignments, and you will do great.
tx for a great semester prof. VonStruck. Really loved your class. keep wearing those nice suits.

Finished the final today! Amazing class, and such a great professor. Goes above and beyond to help students. I found him to be available outside of class, and his jokes are great, sarcastic, but makes you laugh every lecture you attend. Test are easy, but some questions that are discussed in class, will show up on the test, so you should attend.

I stopped reading the textbook halfway through and I still did great on tests/final. Easy class if you're willing to take your own practice tests. Group project was graded easily. I would attend class, you're gonna end up doing less work if you do. He has a very specific way of writing tests that make it easy to detect the right answers.

Loved this class. He's a funny guy, but the material covered in this course is a ton! The class attendance was very low, and no one really participated. Felt bad for him as he tried his best, but could not do more then basically read of the slides. Most passive class I ever encountered. You can learn tons, if you do the work and attend classes.

i'm surprised by the man negative comments. dr Von struck is one of the best professors i've ever had. he is very intelligent, funny, cares for students, and gives more of his private time to students like i have evern seen any professor at a different school do. i strongly recommend taking his classes if you want to learn something. hes awesome!

von Strunck is funny, but very disorganized. He relies heavily on old slides and notes that he doesn't fully understand. He goes off-topic, and exams can be unclear. The textbook ends up being the main guide. He can be hard to approach, and had many issues with my accommodations. This class is graded by only five exams and a group-graded project.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I'm really confused by all the people saying this class was difficult? It was extremely easy. The exams were a breeze, all I did for most of them was read the textbook chapters. There is also a group project at the end of the year (worth 20% of your grade) that he grades EXTREMELY EASILY. My group got a 100. He is also very funny and knowledgable.

This class was truly fun. He knows what he is talking about and makes classes really, really funny. I loved going to class as you always have a few laughters here and there, which makes the class much more enjoably. 
If you want to learn something, take this professor, you will love him.

AMAZING prof!!! yu can tell he loves teaching. he is very energetic and makes a lot of jokes in class. im a bit surprised by the negative feedback prof Van struck is getting, as i truly find him to be an amazing teacher. you learn a lot. sure, the tests can be challenging but study, come to class, and pay attention you and you will do well

WORST CLASS EVER. Doesn't teach anything and reads off slides that are terribly made. For one of the units he used AI to make the slides.... LOTS of reading to do. 90% of the questions on the exam make no sense and are worded in the most diabolical way. Grade is HEAVILY based on the exams and no extra credit. Does not care about the students.

If bad test taker avoid, 5 exams including final and a final project. Took class above this and got an A, the structure of his exams were ridiculous and almost impossible (90 passage q's/100 min)
Told students if they had a problem they could review their exams in office hours then sent an email saying "too many student are going to office hours"??

Only four exams throughout the semester. 100+ questions in 50-60 minutes.. The only way to get a grade. No graded homework, no group work, no other opportunities to get credit for anything. He does not take attendance and goes through things very quickly, and gives a very quick explanation of topics.
all exams are based on readings, which his powerpoint never includes
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Von Strunck is the best! He is super funny and engages with the class. His lectures can miss some info for the exams, so make sure you read the textbook. Lots of easy assignments to boost your grade and all MCQ exams. Make sure to attend because he gives surprise in-class assignments. This was a super easy and enjoyable class! :)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I absolutely loved Professor Von Strunck! He is super funny and engaging and he is a really sweet guy. Talking in class is definitely helpful and it's good to make sure he knows who you are. Attendance isn't technically mandatory, but I enjoyed the class I didn't really want to miss it. The class grading is exam-heavy, but if you study you're good!
he was the bomb! super cool prof, knows his stuff

Amazing Professor!! I just really enjoyed his lectures. Such a funny and caring guy. A bit sarcastic and witty, but makes lectures truly interesting. He definitely knows his stuff and may be too smart for his own good. Tests are challenging but if you study, participate in class, and do your homework you can definitely get a good grade!

Yeah I wouldn't really recommend. He was funny and the lectures seem to make sense but the tests are randomly very hard. Tons of reading if you want to be fully prepared. NO makeups allowed for tests, homework grades are strict w/ no extension. He gets the job done and the class is doable, but if you have another prof option take them instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's genuinely one of the best professors I have seen in this field. Very good at passing on his knowledge to us in the best, simplest way possible. Attendance wasn't mandatory, didn't give notes outside of what is in the tb, but his excellent humor made me want to attend class every time. Would take a class with him again if he held more of them

i enjoyed my two courses with dr. von Strunck. he is very funny, compassionate about what he is teaching and psychology and makes lectures really interesting. i loved going to my morning classes as there was always something interesting that he had to tell, always including current events. he also wears nice suits, never seen a professor doing that

Lectures just reiterated what we were assigned to read in the textbook but sometimes he contradicted what the book said which was very frustrating. Tries to be funny and witty but sometimes comes off as inappropriate. Tons of reading (if you actually read the textbook) but exams were not too bad if you do the learning curves. Graded mostly on exams
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Awful teacher. He offers no flexibility or understanding to his students whatsoever. He is very rigid and makes everything harder than it needs to be. an A in his class is a 94, but it is a 93 in all other classes.  Offers no make-ups no matter the circumstance. I Learned more from just reading the textbook on my own than I did attending lecture.

The tests are all based on the random stats in the textbook which are meaningless. He keeps on joking TREMENDOUSLY about how we chose psych because we cannot do math or any science field, which is very cringe. ( I am an international student coming from a public school in Japan so I pretty sure I can do math pretty well compared to him at least ).

Lectures are pointless if you did the reading (which you should do). A LOT of achieve textbook assignments. They are fast and easy but you should pay attention to the textbook because he pulls test questions from there. Class is a waste but you have to go because of randomized writing assignments in class. Just do the reading and follow the book.

He is a very nice and funny guy which makes his lectures pretty fun and engaging. The only thing I really didn't like is how heavily the course relies on the Macmillan textbook. All homework assignments are through Macmillan, of which there are a lot and most of the questions are very statistic based, which is not reflective of content on exams.

He is a super nice guy and funny, but the lectures are a copy-and-paste from the textbook, so you don't learn anything by going to class. This is annoying because there are pop-up writing assignments. He is very strict on grading, too, so don't expect any extra credit assignments.

Incredibly frustrating waste of money, his lectures rarely help you understand further. People stop taking notes after the first few weeks basically. I LOVED the textbook, the material + practice exams on it are incredibly informative. Great course, just take it with someone who will write fair, relevant exams and expand on the material in class.

I found his classes pointless and if it wasn't for his in class writing assignments I wouldn't go. Nothing he teaches in person is of value, as most of the test is compiled of random stats and obscure questions from the textbook. The writing assignments tend to be pointless, useless, and a waste of time. He merely would just read off the slides.

Assigned so many little assignments that did little to help me learn. It was just a bunch of busy work, just like my community college prior to coming here. Exams tested information not found in lectures. I had hoped that this course was taught by an actual clinical psychologist with experience in the field.

not a super hard class, mostly just memorization. prof is a bit uptight and some test questions are about things not covered in class. had to read the entire textbook and memorize the smallest, irrelevant details to do well (for example, what percentage of people have a certain disorder). also, we had a total of 87 assignments which is insane.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Von Strunck is a great professor. A lot of your grade is based on achieve assignments and all his tests and assignments are MCQ based. He's quite funny and cares for his students. Attendance grade is determined through writing assignments that he randomly gives. If you read and keep up with coursework, you will be fine.

AVOID! This course was frustrating due to the professor's 100% reliance on Macmillan Connect, with excessive homework and exams based on heavy textbook details. Lectures were unhelpful, slides were rushed, and writing assignments are random. Electronics weren't allowed for notetaking. Avoid this disorganized class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Says no laptops which is frustrating because it's hard to take handwritten notes and catch everything he says - but he forgets about this as class goes on. Doesn't take attendance until he realizes no one's showing up, then he starts. All homeworks are due by final day of class, which is nice but does not incentivize students. Lectures are boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really good professor. Taking another class with him starting next week. Very caring when something comes up and supportive. Knows what he is talking about and makes class super fun. Always wears nice suits.
great prof. looking forward to being in his class this fall again. gives homework assignment witch are part of your grade, 4 exams and a final. do the homework and you do well in this course
Great professor. Taking another class with him this fall.

Exams covered material that wasn't talked about in class or in the study guides. I had to scour through the textbook for hours because I never knew what would be covered on the exams. He threw a tantrum when I asked him to give me a retake exam after he forgot to give me extra time. He didn't relent until DRC forced him to. Avoid if possible.

The tests and final exam were made by the online textbook. When asked about content on the exam, he said he did not know what would be on it. Lectures were boring and mostly unhelpful. You'd be better off skipping and reading the textbook instead, but he took attendance randomly some days.

Thank you for a great and challenging semester Dr. VonStrunck. He does a lot of assignments that you can learn from for the test. If you study with Connect, go to lectures, and read your text, you will do well in this class.

just finished my final and loved this course. prof. Vonstrunk makes lectures really interesting with many case files from which you learn a lot. does a lot of group work that helps understand the material. def. knows his stuff and often too smart for his own good. ;) take him if you want to know more about disorders in psych.
Truly a nice and caring guy. Truly enjoyed this course.

prof. where were you at the final today? i had mike and ikes to give you. :( your ta was very nice but we  missed seeing you.  thank you for a great semester. really enjoyed your teaching style. have a fantastic summer.

Tough class but I learned a lot. He brings tons of books with case files to class that he reads and you have to find out what disorder it is. Laughs and says they are hard, when he well knows we'll figure it out easily. lol If you learn with Achieve, do your homework and come to class, you can easily get an A. See you in the fall prof. VonStrunk.

There's truly no need for him. Fails to communicate anything in lecture if you miss it (i.e., documentaries referenced on the final and whole in-class projects) and contradicts the textbook despite letting McGraw Hill make 100% of the tests and assignments. Succeeded in making an easy and interesting subject difficult to like, avoid if possible :)

Half textbook Connect other half himself. I know he can add questions of his own since he put a joke from lecture in there, just wish he would add more things from lecture as everything was from the book. Has really great lectures and is funny and can be deep about topics unexpectedly but its hard to focus knowing I have to read 40 pages later
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Wohoooo. We made it. Dr von strunck os truly an amazing teacher. So much knowledge. I learned so much about disorders and how to apply dsm criteria. He does a lot of case studies. He reads them and then you have to figure out what the disorder is. Truly enyoyed the class. If you want to learn take his classes.

Great guy ans knows his stuff. Unfortunately there was almost no participation from the class who made it akwaed at time. I felt bad for him at times as literally people did not engage. Funny guy with lots of good life examples. Going to take another course with him in the fall.

He is very nice, but his teaching style is extremely ineffective. His class is based on Connect; the textbook and assignments are there. Exams are taken on Connect, and the questions are generated from the textbook, so if you don't have the whole chapter memorized, you'll never get a 100. He often contradicts the textbook and confuses us.

Super nice and funny guy! Engage in his class and read the textbook and you'll be fine. DO YOUR ACHIEVE!!! Achieve is the main basis for tests and assignments and if you don't understand it you will not do well!! Otherwise, very easy to get a good grade.

While Dr. von Strunck is kind and humorous, these qualities are not enough to make up for his unfamiliarity with the course material. Every test and assignment for the class is generated by the textbook website, so reading the textbook is sufficient to do well in the class. The lectures are entertaining, but not useful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Von Strunk is a really nice guy but his lectures can be a little disorganized. However, towards the end of the semester they improved. Most of the grade is determined by tests, but 40% is through unlimited attempts on homework. Be ready to read your textbook and do the homework before the test since they are often on them.

Professor Von Strunck is a very funny and intelligent guy. He reads case studies and the lectures are interactive and fun. The tests are easy if you do the work on the textbook website. The workload is very manageable and it all relates back to the tests. He really relates himself to the discussion and interacts with the students.
Very Intelligent and funny guy. His test can be a bit hard but you lean a lot in his lectures. He really makes the material in class interesting with good examples so you better understand.

This class isn't for everyone. If you need structure like study guides and slides to learn, or if reading the textbook isn't enough, be aware that you might struggle. Exams are challenging, and the textbook costs around $100.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Von Strunck is a really funny guy who definitely wants his students to learn. However, all of the work he assigns is quizzes and assignments through an outdated textbook that are not relevant to the content on the lectures at all. Still a fun class.

He basically just read off of the slides, which seemed a bit old (ex. graphs from 2015). If you keep up with the homework you should do fine, but try to actually do the readings as well. I felt the tests had very random questions on them that no one would know the answer to, since it was never gone over in class.

Prof. von Strunck was funny and an entertaining lecturer, but the lectures themselves were pretty useless in the grand scheme of things. The tests came directly from the info from the textbook -even the smallest details you may skim over- so reading the book is extremely important to do well in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor von Strunck is an amazing Professor. Very intelligent guy who really makes you understand psychology stuff very well. Takes time to explain to students when they have questions. He asks a lot of questions during lectures and keeps the class engaged.

Great guy but terrible lectures. All the homework is based on the textbook and all tests are generated through homework questions. Fun and entertaining class, however don't rely on his lectures because they can be incorrect. He doesn't ever know how to answer students questions based on the lectures either. He'll make you laugh though.
Amazing professor. Made class fun and enjoyable. Really engaging and well in explaining difficult topics.
Just finished my final exam with Dr. Von Strunck. Great experience, and such a funny guy. Also probably best dressed professor.
Do not take unless you want to memorize a textbook in order to do well. Exams have NOTHING to do with lecture.
loved him. taking another class with him next semester.

One of the best professors I have taken at NU. Very knowledgeable, caring, and knows how to engage students. This is my third class with Dr Von Strunk, and I can only recommend him. If you want to learn, take his classes.

Had him freshman year loved him so I retook him this year for 3406. Tests are not hard at all, the rest of the grade is based on you showing up to class and completing some online activites to completion. Funny guy too always likes a good laugh and will challenge you when you are confident in your claims to push you to give a real explanation.
Prof. Von struck is awesome. Had fisrt exam yesterday and scored well. Lots of homework but counts to your final grade. It also helps on the exams. Funny guy. Knows his stuff, and super smart.

Amazing professor! Funniest guy I've ever been in class with. Makes lectures very fun and intresting. Gives good examples so you understand the material better. Lots of homework, but it's part of your grade and will help you on the exam.

Highly recommend him!
Terrible. Don't take him
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I started writing down all of my issues but ran out of words pretty quick. In short, Prof Von Strunck made many mistakes (directly contradicting the textbook) which he refused to correct or admit to, was very difficult to reach out to for help, and generally made class very unenjoyable. I have never hated psychology more.

I absolutely loved taking this course with Dr. von Strunck. A very insightful Professor with much knowledge and plenty of examples during class so you can understand and learn the different concepts of psychology. Funny guy as well with a very sarcastic humor. 5 exams determine your final grade, and the final is not a cumulative one.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Respected--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: great professor. made concepts easy to understand. so enthusiastic and funny. thanks for a great semester!

Try getting any other professor for psych but him!! I walked into class expecting it to be enjoyable, but it wasn't. His slides had mistakes in it, and when corrected, he wouldn't admit he was wrong. Very ignorant to social justice issues as well. Grade based off 5 hard exams that are textbook-based. Rude teacher who didn't want to see us succeed.

I think he is very overrated. There is no incentive of going to class because the textbook company makes the exams. The entire grade is based on 5 exams, 20% each. I attended maybe 5 lectures in total. You have to know everything on the chapters to do well in the exam. He is funny but that wasnt enough for me
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Truly an amazing professor. Dr von struck is funny and hilarious. Though lectures can be long he makes them very interesting, he knows his stuff and his knowledge is scary at times as he can always relate the info to every day life. Take his classes if you truly want to learn!
do not take any of his classes. very unhelpful and not a good teacher
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Von Strunck is a very funny and good lecturer. He provides many examples that help to better understand the concepts. Your entire grade is solely based off of 5 exams which can be very frustrating. If you read the textbook and do the practice exams you should be fine, but beware that the exams can be very tricky.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: So far i realty like his development class. He knows his stuff and is super funny. He explains the material in a way you can relate and understand. Thumbs up!
amazing professor. looking forward to taking another class with him starting tomorrow.

Very thoughtful person and educator. He would like all of his students to attend so that they can engage with the material, but he is understanding of life unfolding and barring attendance. Be sure to communicate with him if you are not able to be the best student you can be, he is there to hear it. Will miss learning from Dr. Von Strunck.

There was no point in showing up to class. He just lectured on small ideas from the textbook. The tests were made from specific details about the textbook. You had to read the textbook and do the practice Hw that's not even graded repeatedly to do well on the tests. Mcgraw Hill made the tests not him. Might as well had been an online course.

This was an absolutely great experience. Professor von Strunck was so enthusiastic about the subject and I learned a lot from him. He is funny, caring, and has a great personality. Great knowledge and you will do great if you read the book and pay attention in class.

truly an amazing professor. very funny, caring and helps students outside of class as well. his lectures are funny and you need to attend and study to do well on the exams. i would definitly recommend dr. van strunk again.

Dr. Von Strunck was fine, generally just inoffensive as a professor. He's funny, which can make his otherwise pretty dry lectures tolerable. Entire grade was based on 5 tests, all of which come directly from the textbook company. Read the chapters and do the practice problems and it's impossible to get anything below a 90.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: only graded on five exams and there's no hw besides reading. you do have to read the entire textbook to do well. tests are the same as practice questions.Professor von struck is a little witty old man and he is super sweet but he refuse to give out lecture slides. please von struck they would've been so helpful for studying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really bad teacher overall. Final grade determined by 4 midterms and 1 final, so very test heavy class. He doesn't curve and doesn't round. Seems like his slides are very old and sometimes have wrong info, so studying for the test was fully based on textbook since his lectures are so unhelpful. For a psych class, it was unreasonably hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It seems like he cares about his students, but that is not reflected in the grading. I went to him one day and he told me that tests are not representative of someone's knowledge but still refused to change the grading. The averages were terrible and there was no curve. He also doesn't post his presentation notes and blocks them while in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really good professor for a beginner psychology class. His lectures are engaging and he is always open to questions and further discussion of certain topics. He is a very caring professor and really wants his students to do well. As long as you study with the resources he provides the tests should be pretty simple, 10/10 recommend him!
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He was a solid professor and had enjoyable lectures. His tests were tough though and averages were often in the high 70s low 80s. For a class that is only graded on exams that is pretty scary. If you study the textbook you should be good though but even with that I still did not do great on the exams.

Not a fan of this man as a professor. He seems really nice, but he just cannot teach to save his life. In order to do well in this class you have to read the textbook and even then the tests are pretty hard. Don't take this class if you are bad at test taking.

Unhelpful. He puts up slides and reads them. Half the time he goes too fast and stands in the way of the board. He doesn't post the slides afterward. Everything is based on the textbook-generated exams and if you meet with him you can tell he has never even seen the questions before. Exams are 100% of your grade so you just have to teach yourself.

Dr. von Strunck is an amazing psych professor. This class is all about putting in the work. Exams are difficult. You have to memorize everything in the textbook and be able to apply the knowledge on the exams. Heavy on memorizing but exams aren't too bad. Exams make up the whole final grade. 5 exams each 20%. Very engaging and classes are fun.

Von Struncks's developmental psych class is the hardest/most involved psych classes I've taken. You have to do the readings to do well in this class, and they are pretty long.
