But it's so worth it, because it ended out being my favorite psych class ever as well! Von Strunck is the best. He's super funny and caring.

He was the worst professor I've ever had! First of all, he doesn't lecture at all. Divided us into 8 different groups and make us teach in class while he sits at the back listening to the presentations. Quizzed/tested us on content that he did not teach but insisted that he taught them. Anyone in that class can agree that he did not teach at all.

Attendance was "mandatory" but not graded, yet he targeted people who chose to not attend class by reporting us at risk to fail & emailing counselors (regardless of grade). Taught 1 lecture himself, the rest were student presentations (again, required but not graded). Tests are made from infinite (not graded) connect questions/assignments.
He's very nice and truly tries his best to make psych interesting. Tests are not too bad, just be sure to review the textbook beforehand. He tells the best jokes also!

Truly passionate about psych, tried to engage with students. Homework is time consuming but easy, lectures and practice tests are exactly like exams, and exams take max 30 mins. Posts everything online, accessible after class, but very strict about grades (no curving or leniency). As long as you do the practice tests, you will do great in the class

The most amazing professor!!! Super smart and it shows, he really knows what he is talking about! Also super funny and engaging, makes you want to come to class. The online learning structure is great and the practice quizzes are just like the exam. Loved it so much I am taking another class from him in the fall!

Professor Von Strunck loves psychology and you can notice this through his lectures. He truly loves what he is teaching and it makes the class even more engaging. The final grade is based on 5 exams and homework. As long as you pay attention, and practice before exams, you should be good!

I had Dr. von Strunck for both Abnormal and Developmental Psychology, and his teaching style is pretty much the same across both courses. He's very passionate about psychology and encourages thoughtful discussions in class. Lectures are very important to exam material, but the homework/practice quizzes are equally (or arguably more) important!

Highly recommend reading the textbook. Lectures are directly based off of the textbook; however, in class he breezes through the slides and skips important information and explanations. Exams are just like the practice quizzes. Sometimes there are a few questions that seem random, but they are definitely fair.

He is an awesome professor. He knows what he’s talking about and really wants his students to do well. Exams were online and were very straight forward if you studied by taking the practice quizzes. Always had funny stories to say and was very enthusiastic about learning!

Amazing professor. Cares so much about the well-being of all his students. Lectures were great as well but not mandatory. Showed up every day with a smile on his face. Intriguing lectures. Only grades were 5 tests which were identical to the practice quizzes. Totally recommend. Awesome guy.

Dr. von Strunck really does care about what he teaches and that makes his lectures enjoyable. The syllabus is what we stuck to the entire semester and it's important to keep track of. In Foundations, exams are 100% of our grade - however the required textbook has practice quizzes and they're basically the same as the test. Would take him again!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Absolutely love him he's so sweet and really wants you to learn. I remember reading somewhere that people found him tough so I was skeptical but I got to know him and he's super friendly and easy to reach out to. He replies quickly to emails and puts your mental health first, he really does care about you as a person not just as a student.
Investments is a hard class at Northeastern. However, Da is an amazing professor, and he is so nice . He gives you the recipe to succeed, you just need to put it together and be consistent

Amazing professor. His class is a must-take if you are passionate about finance. You will learn to understand financial concepts very intuitively, rather than just be forced to memorise them. This is incredibly helpful not only for your learning but also for your career. The class is challenging but extremely rewarding I loved every second.

An incredibly easy professor if you're willing to study for more than five minutes out of class, but expect to fall asleep in his lectures. Absolutely zero ability to captivate students and I essentially had to teach myself the course before exams. Take for lots of studying before exams, but an easy A if you're good at it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Legend, study 1 week before every exam and go to class if u want u will get an A
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His course is extremely challenging but highly rewarding. He is a very good lecturer and it is important to remember that he does his grades based off of rank. If you did poorly, don't panic, everyone else did too. I ended the class with a raw grade of a 68 I believe and got an A- because of my rank. Respect his rules and listen and you will be ok.

Hands down the best teacher I've had in college. Super knowledgeable and knows what he's talking about. Always responsive even when he's traveling. I like that he uses attendance as a way to make us read the news and comment on what's happening every week. A little worried about the exam but honestly great teacher if you care about learning.

Class is ranked and there's a lot of emphasis put on tests. Very knowledgeable on the subject and willing to answer tangential questions. Prof is chill though and is around our age so treating him almost like an equal worked well for me.

He actively wants you to fail the class, he made the second test harder simply because "too many people got As last test". There are no study guides or any material you can study to prepare for tests. Attendance is MANDATORY if you don't show up to just one class where he calls on you you will loose all your attendance points.
Class materials were not very helpful and the Prof acts in arrogant manners. If you don't enjoy teaching, do not teach!

The instructor not only talked about investing, but also incorporated the current investment context to help explain the class, which was my favorite part! I enjoyed this lecture. There is not much homework in this class. Student's final grade was based on one's ranking in the class, so, to get an A, performing better in your section is enough.

This class will truly challenge you. 2 things 30% Midterm which is take home and 40% final make up ur grade. The tests are extremely challenging in the sense that he takes what you learned and ask you to calculate things based off you intuition. The class being ranked however really helps. As I ranked 3rd in my class and received and A with a 70%.

This course is exceptionally tough, focusing mainly on exams. Midterms make up 30%, finals 40%, without a grading curve. Emphasis is on computational questions, without points for steps. Grades are base on ranking but still very challenging. Avoid this class!

Genuinely, his class is so hard to understand. If he gets one bad impression of you (even if you didn't do anything), immediately you are on his radar. His quizzes are fine if you actually understand the content. The content itself is fine. His teaching is difficult to understand and will shame you if you ask questions. Grading wise, it's absurd.

Tough grader, and not tolerant if you skip class or appear as if you are not putting forth effort. However, he shows a genuine passion for the subject, and provides very helpful examples and notes. Overall a great professor if you are willing to show up and care about the class.
complete disregard for his students success and just wants to see you fail.
He has a really big ego and doesn't seem to care about his students at all.

Sadaka grades your work like he caught you plowing his wife. He also has the ego of 2009 Kobe Bryant which I can only assume is to make up for some sad form of existence he calls life. Don't ever take his class unless you can pass calc without attending a lecture.

just a rude professor most of the time. He is really hard to connect with any issue. Honestly most of the time it feels like he just wants you to leave right when you step in office hours. Such an unpleasant class to be in most of the time. Once told me "why do you make my life harder" for no reason aswell. This is the type of professor he is.
Hard to understand, rude and unapproachable during office hours, harsh grader. If you can, pick another professer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really great professor and class is very passable so long as you show up and put in some effort. His lectures are great and I think he makes the topics easily digestible and sets you up to do well on the quizzes and exams. Just pay attention and look at hw in the textbook and you will do great in his class!

Shoutout to the person who said “if you're not religious pick a god a pray to it.” Undoubtedly the worst class I've had at northeastern. Barely any office hours, annoyed when you ask questions, and the exams had content I swear I'd never seen before. Hanai would let some students retake exams but not others which was frustrating to say the least.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hany is one of the best professors I have ever had; he is always explaining things that are directly related to the content on the tests, and he makes sure to give lots of review to prepare us as well. He is very funny, but you need to show up, otherwise you won't get any leeway.

Professor was extremely difficult to work with. He implemented a retroactive attendance grade deduction mid-semester that significantly impacted grades, without clear communication in the syllabus. Material and lecture notes was decent on their own, but the professor himself was horrible to work with. Very rigid, unapproachable, and at times rude.

He criticize me because he feels disrespected for me attending quiz and left before lecture start. I cried for an hour, he would never know I was planning to bring him flower before that class. He triggered my PTSD of dealing with a cynical father. For days I questioned whether I am bad person, with somatization of heartache, headache, depression.
If you're not religious, pick a religion and pray.

Where do I even start. Professor of doom and despair truly. He full on refused to help me on multiple occasions when showing up to his office hours saying that “I will not help you study” even though I was doing well in the class but had no issue helping others. He picks and chooses his favorites and if you're not one, good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor makes you feel stupid for asking questions or not fully understanding a topic after he explains it once. Grading is super harsh and test questions are not similar to homework. I would not recommend taking him unless you already know the topics or can understand something super quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor is not the best teacher. He makes you regret every time you raise your hand to ask a question. Even though attendance isn't mandatory, he pretty much makes it mandatory. If you miss a class, he remembers and will refuse to help. He is not the best at answering questions and makes you feel stupid for even coming to office hours.
professor Sadaka is an incredibly tough grader, taking off many points for small things. He does not believe in curved exams or partial credit despite the averages on all of his tests being so low.

Do not take this teacher. They often rant and yell at the class, creating a hostile environment. Their tests are needlessly difficult, and they have made students cry. Once, they even threatened to drop a student for being two minutes late when class hadn't started yet. They fail to cover relevant material, and their office hours are non-existant
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hanai would often degrade students when test scores were low, calling us lazy, and then tell us to drop the course. He made multiple students cry. Questions are discouraged, and on the rare occasion he answers them, he appears annoyed. Clearly very good at math but not a good teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hany bullies students and creates an environment that is unconducive to learning. Syllabus says attendance isn't mandatory, threatens to fail students who don't come to class. Acts annoyed and inconvenienced with students' presence. Questions are entirely discouraged. Made multiple students cry. The man is smart and knows what he's talking about.

Hes good at math, bad at teaching. Furthermore, his midterms and finals were much more difficult than homework or anything he taught us during a lecture. His lectures go extremely fast, often covering 4 to 5 concepts in one class with little to no examples.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Speed running all the questions, never explains any of them

Professor Hanai has a specific teaching style, but engaging with it can pay off. Attendance is mandatory - skip class at your own risk. For study prep, try using Unstuck AI alongside the textbook and office hours.

Sadaka's really passionate and wants his kids to succeed. For the most part, he's really good at explaining concepts but sometimes he's a bit disorganized. You need to show up to lectures to get a good grade in this class, even missing 1 sets you back. He's also a bit brash at times so if you can't handle getting bullied a bit I wouldn't take him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
A solid professor. Quizzes and tests are very similar to the study guides he gives out. HW is optional and often harder than the quizzes, but I'd still recommend others to do them to stay prepared.

Sadaka will absolutely take it personally if you skip class or take a wellness day; and he will notice your absence if you usually sit near the front.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I enjoyed the class, he would sometimes pick on students though. One time he called me lazy in front of everybody because I asked for help on a question. In hindsight, it's because he liked me as a student, but it really upset me. Overall, good professor, but the final being worth 40% is ridiculous and dropped my B+ to a B- because of test anxiety.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I had a B+, but dropped to a C+ (which I'm hoping gets rounded) due to my final. He is passionate about math and great at his job. My only complaint is he has an old school teaching style, where he is mean or pushes really hard for success. He called me lazy in class for asking a question. I know it was out of jokes, but kinda hurt my feelings.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hani is very hit or miss. If you are a student who shows up, asks questions, or just is present - He's good. He is NOT good for anyone who wants to skip lectures or just sleep through class. You need to come every class, pay attention and take notes. If you do this, really try to understand the topics - you'll do fine. Sucks that the final is 40%:(
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Northeastern's math department have made grading changes which sucks. 20% quizzes, 20% each midterm, and the final is 40%. The class isn't extremely difficult but beware that you're going to have to teach yourself. Hany teaches some topics better than others which is a bummer. He's also very passionate which makes the class bearable.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Grads depend on final(40%) 2 midterms(20%each) and  quizzes(can drop 2). Sometimes it's not so friendly when you ask him questions. The Lectures are easy to understand, and he likes to teach some techniques to make things easier. For attendance, doesn't accept wellness day. And could be the last credit because of absence depending on his mood.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I had credit for calc 2 and chose to retake it to solidify my understanding. I have now dropped Sadakas calc 2 course and gone to calc 3, because i felt like he was making me worse at a subject I already knew. He talks fast with a heavy accent, asks if we understand (gives us .5 seconds to speak up) before moving on. amazing at math bad at teaching

Professor Sadaka's class is very memorization-heavy and does not help gain a deeper understanding of the material covered. He is effective at teaching what is required for exams but not to truly learn the material. He seems funny and entertaining but can really be a bit abrasive towards students.
He is very passionate about mathematics, which is inspiring for any students looking to further their involvement in the world of STEM.

If you show up to class, you will succeed. He is clear about expectations and a great teacher. If you like to skip class, THIS IS NOT THE PROFESSOR FOR YOU. If you miss class, you'll miss important info (for ex: you can't have 2 one sided sheets for formulas). He can be short when answering questions in class but is wonderful in office hours.

so condescending and rude to students. would publicly humiliate students in front of the class for misunderstanding questions. would blame the class for being lazy if we did badly but he was just not a great teacher. lectures were also hard to follow and didn't prepare us for quiz/test questions. switch out while you still can.
Just a straight forward professor with some strict rules regarding tests and attending the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I always thought that orgo was the hardest course I would take but this course was beyond hard. The fact that I wasnt able to understand a single thing through weeks of review is extremely concerning. He is so fast-paced and I have never experienced this before. +He took some kid's (me) cheat sheet bc it had examples on it when he never told us.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I just took my final for this class. He spent 30 minutes waking around forcing people to give up half of their cheat sheets since they were two single sided pages instead of double sided. He is an asshole. Take someone else.

Please DO NOT take this professor! I kid you not, this entire semester he has been so passive-aggressive and it is scary to go to his class. He expects everybody to participate and raise their hands but if you say the wrong answer, he ridicules you in front of the class. You HAVE to attend class or else he'll lower your grade. Would NOT recommend!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretty standard Calc 2 teacher. Mostly just does examples and very rarely explains the theory, but if you ask him to he will. He's super passionate about Calc 2 and made me want to go to class even though it was at 4:30.

He publicly humiliated a student after catching them cheating. He also would yell at the class when we underperformed on test averages and called us lazy. He didn't assign homework but would blame us for not doing suggested problems that would be similar to the tests. We didn't know formula sheets were allowed until the last quiz. He can't teach.

Professor Sadaka is a good professor and knows the material well. He moves quickly and can sometimes be difficult to understand in class, but for the most part his lectures are good. The content is difficult, but you can succeed in his classes with the appropriate level of studying.

Hanai wants his students to succeed and is there for students in office hours. My problem with his course was his quizzes were super easy, cut and paste from the slides, but his exams were harder problems from the book. He wasn't really clear on the exam material so the whole class bombed the first test. Do the homeworks and you'll do great.
Great professor. People who say that he is bad are the ones who like to get their work done easily. Put in some effort and he will make sure you do well.

ok, let me preface this by saying I have had Hany for 3 classes at NEU- 3081, 4681, and 4682. I tanked my first 3081 test and then put my head down and studied really hard to get an A. Hany genuinely wants to help his students but you have to show up. Funny, kind, and genius mathematician. Last day of classes with him will def make you emotional.

The class was easy compared with other advanced math classes. Although he would post all the review materials online before the exam, sometimes there are no materials for review or preview because he did not do it in time. He would give extra credits through one makeup exam, but they were challenging and complicated.
He is a school teacher, not a professor. He doesn't do what he says. Would definitely not take again. Method of teaching isn't up to the professors level
One of the enthusiast professor I have attended. Tests and assignments are very tough and concepts are heavy too. Concepts taught in his are very helpful for other classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class was so hard. He gets mad at the class when the test averages are lower than he expects and is bad at answering questions. He knows his stuff but his lectures are confusing and the hw is difficult. He does drop the lowest hw and test scores, and the final is like the practice exams. I wish I dropped this class on the first day.
Disappointing

I took this class remotely and could not understand him through the mask and video. At one point in the class, he was videoing the board upside down. I ended up teaching myself through the textbook and not attending lectures. He also gave no accommodation for sick students and rarely responded to emails.
best professor I have ever had in my life. The thing is, if you follow what he says and do what he asks, you will learn a lot in his class and you will like him so much.
Prof Sadaka is one of the best professor I've ever had. He's very passionate about probability and statistics. The bad ratings on here are people who don't want to work and want to get a good grade.

Worst Professor ever. Do not fall into his trap. He'll say all good things in the lecture by being bad that I do all this for you and won't even grade you as per his stupid philosophy Regarding his teaching, there is no particular method that he follows, teaches anything out of nowhere, and would also make fun of doubts asked.

Really funny and dedicated professor with high expectations for his students, and he wants you to succeed. But he has an unorganized way of explaining things and moves too fast during class. We had 8 problem sets, 2 of the lowest were dropped. 2 midterm exams worth 20% each and a Final worth 30%. His accent makes him hard to understand sometimes.

Easily the worst professor I've had at Northeastern, also in the running for worst teacher I have ever had. His accent makes him difficult to understand and with online learning he does not read the zoom chat at all. His teaching style is basically 100% unorganized random example problems, fails to explain the reasoning behind any of the examples.

Sadaka is one of my favorite teachers so far at Northeastern. He has high expectations for all of his students but he cares very deeply about their success, which he will very often tell you at the end class. Out of the 8 problem sets he gives at least 2 are dropped and exams are very straightforward, so getting an A in the class isn't difficult.

Nice guy. I had a hard time watching his lectures live because of my time zone but he is a very solid professor. Would say attending his stats class at least didn't feel overly necessary the PPTs are excellent. He is a good dude I would definitely take him again. He does make some funny jokes during class if you are tuned in!
One of the best Professor at NU.

Prof Sadaka is a pretty good prof. Make sure you read the slides before class and you'll be fine. The bad ratings on here are people who don't want to work and have finally found a prof who challenges them. He's a great guy outside of class (always helped me even outside office hours) and very passionate about statistics.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Some people love him, some hate him. He insults confused students if they ask questions, and can't explain things multiple ways, so everyone just nods along and tries to learn it on their own later. His accent can make it hard when he lectures really fast. Build a good relationship with him by going to office hours, or don't expect to do well.

2 midterms: 20% each/ final: 30% /problem sets: 24%/ participation: 6%. Gives u 6 or more PS's and only keeps highest 4. make sure u have a good calc 1 base. he gives cool calc shortcuts. gives us life advice too lol. funny & good guy. go to class consistently, ask qs, do all PS's, and he will like u. NEVER go on phone. hes big on respect.

This dude was probably one of the worst teachers Ive ever had for math. In fact, he made me hate math so much that I switched my major. I have never seen such irresponsibility when it comes to teaching consistently across all his sections, and honestly frick this guy. I b failing his quizzes bc he did not provide quality teaching. Fat thumbs down:/

Prof Sadaka is easily the worst professor I've had at Northeastern. He's disorganized, a poor communicator, and quite absent-minded. I cannot believe I am paying this much money for this quality of education; the primary reason why students like him so much is because he just gives out a ton of A's, not because of his actual teaching.
He is amazing faculty. Lectures are sometimes heavy. Your participation makes ur grades better. Old School type but has good sense of humor. If u want to clear concepts definitely first choice.
- Interesting lectures. - Finds creative ways to teach.

He is such an amazing professor. He's always available for extra help and will explain an example a different way each time until you understand. His lectures were always filled with good examples and he was very engaging--it never felt like class was dragging on.
The course material is tough but sadaka is a great professor and a great guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: GOAT
Extra credit available. He is OK.
too good
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Best math professor I've had in a while. Made sure things were understood and gave good examples to follow. If I still had trouble, he was easily available and ready to help. Very rewarding class and you get back what you put in. You can't expect to halfass it and do well. Prof. made things much simpler
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If you were caught not going to class, you may lose a lot of points. His Lectures are not that informative but provide you with examples. Easy tests if you go over the practice tests. A lot of extra credit. I only needed to get a 70 to get an A in the final.

I'd never take his class again. He put no effort into organization - awful handwriting, mistakes everywhere, impossible to follow, didn't have clear topics for the class. Listed the wrong textbook chapters in the syllabus & some lessons weren't even in either textbook. Couldn't translate some test questions well enough to English. Taught myself.

I'm an engineering major and Prof. Sadaka is one of the best math professors I have had. He makes the content clear and is always available outside of class time to help. Don't skip class or cheat. Quizzes and tests are fine if you study for them. He's pretty understanding. Would def take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: Sadaka is totally disorganized. Notes rarely have titles so you never really know what hes teaching you. He makes everything way more complicated than it needs to be and it often feels like hes teaching material backwards. Very smart enthusiastic guy, not a great professor
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I have taken his cal2 class, his lecture is clear and understandable. However, his quizzes are either extremely hard or easy. Two one-hour tests are cruel.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Prof Sadaka is without a doubt one of the most frustrating professors I've had at Northeastern. He has a smug attitude that I can't get over and an incredibly harsh grading policy.  Any arithmetic errors, regardless of whether or not you understand the concepts, can drop you a whole letter grade on quizzes. Should've dropped his class when I could!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Hanai is very nice and willing to answer questions and help you succeed. He is randomly strict about things like being late and missing class. But he gives opportunities for extra credit, gives take-home tests, and loves when you ask questions. The only thing  is, sometimes he's bad at explaining things. need to be good in math!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Hanai. Is. A. Bro. I had him for calc 2 and he was very good at getting his point across, the only think is he can be a little scatterbrained during lectures. He gives weekly quizzes but they are VERY easy and never anything surprising. He will drop a quiz or give a bonus make up quiz if the class does bad too. Take him any chance you get.

Professor Sadaka is one of the best teachers' I've ever had. He is very intelligent, disciplined and passionate; but also very caring and he wants everyone to actually learn. He gives homework and quizes a lot, but are very helpful to learn the material. Also don't ever think of skipping the class, he is very strict about that.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Honestly I just don't like him and everything he does annoys me -- he has the most infuriating way of teaching. Every concept more difficult than it needs to be; if you miss 3 classes he drops a letter grades; he never directly answers student's questions, and alternates between whispering and shouting during the lecture. Also, super smug.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Hanai is not only a character but a solid professor as well. He makes himself very likable to listen to as he's very enthusiastic about what he teaches. He expects that you work for your grade, and is commonly willing to help you out if you have questions. Only downside is that it is sometimes hard to follow him in class if he goes on a tangent.

He's a nice guy, but he makes the course harder than it needs to be because he's convinced that you will use everything you learn. So he makes you FULLY understand every aspect of the material. Reviews for tests are pretty much the opposite of the actual test. He does give partial credit though, which is nice. Attendance is mandatory.
Don't skip class
Caring, passionate, and very entertaining. A great teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Upon first glance he seems nice because he offers extra credit.  However, he is very scatterbrained so the lectures are hard to follow and he changes the rules of the class often.  He was "nice" by giving a take home exam that then took over 10 hours to complete.  He punishes students for things he takes as a sign of disrespect, i.e. being late.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Best teacher I have ever had. His course is hard but definitely worth it, you will learn a lot with Sadaka.
Amazing professor!!!

Tests may be tough, but he gives at least one take home quiz and one take-home mid-term. The non-take-home tests are brutal though. The average for my class on Exam 1 was 66%. Quizzes are much better, it's not too difficult to average 90% or so on them.

After the first lecture, I thought that I would fail the course. However, after few classes, Sadaka became very helpful and ready to help. He is nice but strict. If you attend all classes and study well, you will get a good grade. He is a really good professor. If I had to choose any Calc classes again, I would choose him.
Is passionate about teaching and supportive of students' desire to learn. He is a fair and clear marker and explains things in a way that you can easily understand.

Hanai, Hanai, Hanai. Students let me tell you something. If you want a teacher that explains EVERYTHING there is to explain. Questions? He won't go forward on the content if 1 student does not understand the topic.  If you want a teacher that helps you to learn, to know everything and to prepare for the final exam, take him. BEST TEACHER IN NEU.

Funny and makes sure class is interesting. May seem a little strict at times, but you know it's only for your own good so don't feel bad about it. If you put in effort in your work if you struggle, he will definitely notice and help you out along the way, don't worry about that. Overall, probably the best math prof I could ever ask for!

He is such a funny and entertaining teacher. Explain concepts multiple times to make sure students get the material. Gives many quizes which gives you many chances for raising your grade.  Always reviews before midterm or final and even some quizes. It's all the packet.

Hani is the best! He's awesome at explaining, engaging, and offers so many examples. He's my favorite math teacher and actually won some awards for Best Professor in the math department. He does push students to do their best and it gets to be challenging but he is there for a ton of support either during his office hours or after the lecture.

I took this class as a bio major to fulfill one of my major electives. As someone who's background in math isn't too strong (didn't take Calc 3), I was shocked at how easy the class was. Hany is always there to help and wants everyone to succeed. I'd definitely rate him as a fantastic mathematics professor. Keep your phone AWAY.
Best math teacher I've had yet. Engaging, great lectures, always open to give extra help.

Extremely charismatic.  Has a thick Egyptian accent but it's easy to understand and not an issue.  His teaching isn't always effective and quizzes and tests can be tough.  However, if he sees you trying he'll give you a lot of ways to make up points.  DO NOT use phone or talk in class or he will personally dislike you.  The textbook is for homework

Hani is a fantastic math professor. I took this class (Calc 3 for SciEng) over summer 1 and it was great. He explained everything and clearly loves teaching. Do keep your phone AWAY - he won't like you if you have it out. And although attendance isn't mandatory, you need to attend in order to do well, as with most math classes.

I took the class over Summer 1 and he just seemed rushed. Didn't go through things well and repeatedly said "if you've had me for Calc 2 you'll understand". Well I didn't have him for Calc 2 and neither did most people... I was pretty lost.
one of the best instructors i've ever had! if he sees youre trying, he gets out of his way to help you out. grades fairly. go to class and pay attention and you will pass.

Professor Sadaka is a FANTASTIC teacher! Despite missing several days of class, due to weather, he completed everything on time before the final. He's friendly, supportive, and more than fair. Just pay attention in his class, or else he obviously won't like you very much. I definitely would recommend him to anyone.
Very helpful and nice! The material could be difficult sometimes, but Hany is the MAN

He is the best professor I have ever had for a math. Mind you that math is a very difficult subject to teach and he does a great job at helping his students understand the course material. He is willing to go out of his way for his students and is super friendly! Highly Recommended!!
One of the best professors I've ever had!

Great teacher, cares a lot about his students, if you don't understand, he'll take time to explain it again. Has a bomb collection of sweaters. As long as you put in effort and show up, you won't fail.
Generally a good teacher. It's an easy class especially if you have already taken calculus. He prides himself on his students doing well, so he'll help you as much as you need.

One of the best mathematical thinking professors. Don't skip his class. He takes it personally and if you're one of the people there when a large portion of the class isn't, he gives you opportunities to raise your grade. Explains concepts very clearly. Don't need to buy the book, everything is online.
He's a very fair grader and gives lots of extra credit opportunities, does very good job at explaining concepts. One of my favorite professors

Hani is awesome. He has a thick accent. I get that some people are bad with that, but that shouldn't ruin your opinion of him. He explains and to no end, but he's still energetic and interesting. He holds extra office hours and is genuinely concerned about how you're doing. If logic isn't your bag but you need to take it, take it with him!

This class was sooo easy and pointless. Sadaka teaches the class like everyone is dumb. He gives extra credit in class and loves participation. If you don't look interested, he gets on your case. Class is too boring to concentrate although his accent and jokes are somewhat funny. Do the easy homework and show up.

This calculus based probability and statistics class is the easiest class a math major (like me) will ever take, but probably the hardest math class a non-math major will ever take.  Sadaka is accommodating, although you may not like him at first.  He is a good guy, and he means well in what he does.  Don't miss an opportunity to take his class.

Awesome teacher!!!!  Take his class.  Has an accent but explains until a dog can understand them.  Helps you out with anything especially the project.  Makes the class fun with witty remarks.  DO YOUR HW!!  He gave us 20 pints on our quiz avg once for doing it.  O and gave us a bonus on a quiz b/c of the Red Soc winning.  Great teacher.
Hani is the man. he helps out students if they show any interest. one of my best profs ever
great prof. always making class fun and enjoyable, really wants students to do well, if u try in his class u can get a good grade

this guy is the man!  he is really intimidating at first when he gives a quiz the second day of class but hes really a great guy and a great teacher.  He always makes strange but funny jokes and just gives out bonus points randomly for thing in class.  Its really not difficult to get a good grade in this class.  As long as you try, he passes you.
Class isn't impossible, but with a final worth 40% of your grade, you're screwed if he puts uncovered material on the final. Which he did.
His summer course in stats was a breeze, I think everyone got an automatic B+.  He does have an accent that you will need to get used to, but the adjustment is pretty quick.
No Comments
Easy class for a math or engineering major.  He gives 1 makeup quiz.  You won't like him at first, but you get used to his accent.
just ask or extra credit and everything will be ok

one can not maintain good Gpa under him, if u want to increase gpa I would not suggest his class. Exams are too heavy, you need to memorize 20+ pseudo codes, and in assignments also, you will not get full marks always. He is a strict professor and would not listen to what you have to say, if he sets his mind to something.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
This course is NOT about algorithms—it's about proving trivial inequalities and guessing what
counts as "obvious." The name should be changed to "CS5800: Do I Need to Prove nº + 7n + 1 >
0 for n > 10." I signed up expecting to learn algorithms, not to play a guessing game where every
TA has a different definition of what needs to be proven.
Amazing lectures. If you do the work, you'll learn a lot.
One of the best professors I've ever had. Very clear and helpful.
No matter how much other students berate him, one can't deny that he is a great instructor for algo. He knows what he's teaching and his lecture delivery style is also clear.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor. He's always there to help his students!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: People are just fearmongering in his reviews page. He isn't a bad professor. Students just have to pay extra attention to his lectures, and that's it. Exams are very predictable and he offers ample supports to students who approach him for help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Iraklis isnt as bad as other people show him to be. They all are rating him low cuz they couldn't maintain their sanity and grades in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Disorganized rubric and poorly structured exams that expect answers in one rigid format. Even correct solutions get zero if they differ (e.g., using a stack instead of a queue). Little concern for students. Take this course only if you can memorize and reproduce CLRS exactly as expected.

I got baited into memorizing 30+ pseudocode for the final.
which i guess is alright to know 
but it didnt feel good when i didnt get to apply my 30+ pseudocode of knowledge on the 5 questions which are on the final
it feels like he said gullible is written on the ceiling and i looked up

The grading practices are excessively punitive, communication is effectively nonexistent, and the lack of transparency has caused extreme and unnecessary stress. This experience has significantly impacted my mental well-being and delayed my academic progress, and it should be formally reviewed.

If you want to sharpen algorithms, this course delivers. Lectures are structured and well paced—until the end, when it suddenly accelerates. But if you're aiming for a smooth learning curve or you're protecting your GPA, think twice: grading feels bureaucratic, partial credit is hard to defend, and the pressure can make learning feel punishing.
Run, worst professor of all time, would ask you to follow EXACTLY how the slides or the book implement the algorithm, or you lose a lot of points even if you are correct.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Algorithms is a very hard class to take in general. Materials are heavy - it's just not a fun topic. But Prof. Herc really tries his hardest to make it work in the short time frame and the knowledge people already have (1st Sem. Grads all the way to PhD).

Poor learning structure. Exams are questions directly from the textbook but the textbook is dense. His lectures help a little with the exams and homework but they are more so supplements. Also, not enough questions on exams, if you miss just one, you fail.

I am pretty sure the good reviews are fake reviews. He is a bad lecturer and barely explains anything. He insists on following the book and visiting office hours and TA's but the book is dense af and the office hours are just a repetition of the class. Given how abysmal he is at teaching it feels almost criminal how harshly he grades. AVOID!!
Worst professor ever seen in my life. I failed by 0.21% and gives direct 0 even if your approach is in correct direction. RUN!!!!

Won't Recommend. 
The grading criteria for assignments and exams is non-sensical. Each exam has 4 questions, so if you commit a mistake in one, you risk falling by a letter grade. Highly theoretical, will not help in passing interviews. Nothing to learn here, stay away. Take some other worthwhile course, and LeetCode if you wanna pass interviews.

Do not even think of taking this class, professor is very rude and does not care about students. Run away from this course and professor. I failed by 0.1%, the professor didn't consider my case, didn't reply to any emails or messages. Grades ruthlessly.  If you mess up even one of the exam, be prepared to retake the course. RUN!!!!!!!!!!

I really enjoyed being in class with this professor. His course work was very flexible and fair, his lectures were very engaging and informative. His class was the perfect length. Overall I feel this was one of the most effective educators I've had in my program this far. I would absolutely take his class again if I could.
run, don't take with him
not a good lecturer

Worst grader I have ever seen. If you do not do things by his book you will loose marks, irrespective of if your answer is right, if it is not the way he has taught the topic you will be loosing your grade. I will not recommend taking this course under him in any circumstance. He refuses to give the answers for the assignments.

agree with other comments. he is a nice guy but an awful lecturer. his slides are just copying textbooks and it is hard to understand what he talks at class. i attended all the classes and felt it was such a waste of time. he is also very tough grader and he makes everything more confusing. this is the worst course I ever took.

Lectures are informative and engaging. Course is very well organized with assignments every week, and grades released timely. 

However, not an easy A, you will need to study and may need to attend OH often. 5-8 hours a week outside of class is expected. 

Grading appears harsh sometimes, but all assignments(and exams) have some bonus points.

The professor grades based on concept clarity. If you understand and justify the concepts well, it greatly benefits you. He is clear on fundamentals and pays attention to details that matter. His challenging assignments prepare you for exams and promote growth. The subject is easy if you're strong on the basics. TAs are responsible and helpful.

This was the worst I've had. Useless lectures with empty slides. Condescending on Piazza and impossible to understand due to his accent.  Grading was insanely harsh. Lost 10 points on a 15-point problem for a small mistake! Even with the right answer, a missing step meant you were wrong.  He also refused to give us homework solutions.
Grading criteria are unclear, and TAs require excessive explanation of basic concepts with no partial credit. very frustrating and inconsistent.

This course was pretty difficult. I took it as an undergrad as part of the PlusOne program. Prof Herc is super nice and willing to help you out. The lectures are informative, but reading the textbook is essential for doing well on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He cares about his students, is a nice guy, and will answer any question, but is an awful lecturer. You can tell he is really trying to explain the concepts, but he just makes things more confusing and hard to understand. I spent the whole semester working with a tutor and he was able to explain the concepts WAY better.
Super immersive, took us to court for the second half of the semester and I learned a ton. The guest lecturers were very engaging, and he was extremely knowledgeable.
The best professor. The sweetest man who truly cares that you learn all about the subject. You will leave knowing so much about criminal justice and get real experience going to court.
Easy grading, very insightful trips but lectures can be a bit of a drag. Definitely worth it

lots of reading and papers but its the type of work that really connects what you're learning in the classroom to the real world. Judge Ireland is an amazing teacher who cares about his students. The field trips he takes you on are amazing and I realllllly recommend going to all of them!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Respected

reviews.comment: Judge Ireland is the best.  He's super interesting and a great resource for pre-law.

I loved this class! The lectures may get a bit boring since he reviews a lot of statutes in class (necessary evil) but the field trips and guest speakers he brings in are super insightful and he knows ALOT.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Not the most interesting speaker but learned A LOT in his class. Tests were very straightforward. The main highlight of this class was the field trips to the municipal, juvenile, superior, and supreme courts, and the county jail. he was the former chief justice of the Mass. supreme court so he knows a LOT of things
Lots of readings but they prepare you well for the class. Field trips were helpful and he is an easy grader if you are willing to make sure you get all the assignments completed on time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: As a CJ major,  You try as hard as you can to respect him b/c he's the former chief justice of the juvenile court of Suffolk county. His teaching is confusing. His syllabus doesn't tell you what anything is worth. We didn't even get a rubric for the final paper. The only thing I appreciated in this class was the field trips (good experience).

Hands down, the greatest professor and class ive taken @ NU (coming from a senior). The guy is a justice on the MA supreme court and was a Juvenile Justice for years. You take a field trip to secure Juvie facilities and court. Awesome experiences. But dont take this class unless you're willing to WORK - its not easy, but you can do well if you work

You can go to class and not do the readings, or do the readings and not go to class.  His lectures are completely pointless because he literally dictates statues to you the whole time!  Worst class I've ever taken.  20 page paper for undergrad too.  Talks too fast to take adequate notes and never repeats himself unless you ask. Gives pop quizzes.

My only gripe with her was her grading. She is very nit-picky with grading, never giving you a 100% on assignments and your grade falls into the balance of true/false questions where if you get one wrong, you lose 1% of your overall grade. She is very passionate but you NEED TO PARTICIPATE! Don't need to run away but be prepared to have headaches.

This professor never gives out 100% on any projects. I think highest grade she will give is 95% at most and her feedback are unhelpful. She is sweet but can be stubborn and scary at times. She cold calls often. Overall coursework was pretty light and stress free. Her midterm and finals are like quiz. This class reminds me of highschool.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Did not know how to teach but there were two tests the whole year and you could BS the assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Constantly interrupted students when they answered questions she asked the class. Has strict/odd grading requirements and doesn't give a rubric; just expects students will somehow read her mind. Least favorite class I have taken in my academic career thus far
Great
Definitely one of the worst professors Northeastern has hired. She barely speaks english so it is hard to understand her. Lectures are a tough listen.

Professor Khan cold calls you in class and if she doesn't like what you say she cuts you off and cold calls someone else.  She is nice with deadlines and will give you extra time if you give her a valid reason.  However, she grades assignments very harshly, and it is hard to bring up your grade if you make a mistake.

Although this professor is very caring she calls on students randomly and will cut off your answer, she also does not give time to think and then will judge the answer (this judgement cut down on how much students openly participated). You are also graded on very little in this class: a case study paper, 2 quizzes, and a large group project
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is fantastic and she wants all her students to succeed. She is funny, at least my class was. This class is an easy A if you show up, participate and read the textbook. There is also big group project, one paper, and two quizzes. Overall I liked this class but sometimes she won't let you finish talking.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Ivan, as a professor, is a very understanding and easy-to-talk-to person. The course ARCH 1110 is inherently challenging to teach and only really makes sense if you are an architecture major. The course covered so many things in such a short time, and for each project, you learn something new thats completely different from the week before.

Should not be considered a professor because he legit doesn't teach. Every single class, he goes through a 100 pg slideshow, critiques everyone's mistakes on drawings, then has the poor TAs teaching everything else. When we were struggling with the software, ESPECIALLY Rhino, he would get irritated and commanded TAs to scramble around. Terrible.

Class goes like this: Ivan critiques work from the previous assignment, then he has the TAs teach how to do the next assignment. If you're good with technology, time management, and willing to put in the time this class will be a breeze. If you're stuck, it's better to look up a youtube video or ask a classmate than waiting for office hours.

Ivan is arguably the worst person you could pick to teach this course. All of the learning and almost all the grading was facilitated by TAs, since he doesn't understand half the software that he teaches. Expect homework to take at least 5 hours a week, and you will have to learn most of the techniques yourself.

As long as you attend his classes you will be fine. He has office hours before classes and his study guides translate very well to the exams. If you can answer all the questions on the study guide you will do well on the test, he is not a harsh grader and the course involves almost no math.

Very old fashioned - uses chalkboard to teach. He clearly cared deeply about environmental economics, so he always had a lot to say. Not a lot of hw assignments, and he's an easy enough grader. Can be hard to follow at times bc of his lecture-heavy style, but if you pay attention you will do just fine. Does often give vague instructions on purpose

Had Bakkal for a Critique of Capitalism class, and most of my concerns came from the way he organized the class.Towards the end of the sem lectures became way more disorganized. Still, he was clear about what terms /questions would be on tests, and as long as you actually listen in lectures and do all readings you will be fine.

THIS GUY SUCKS. If you can take someone else, do it. Discussion posts are SUPER unclear and not clearly marked when due. FURTHER, the graphs he draws up in class are NOWHERE to be seen in the textbook. Please save the GPA.

Good lord. Where do I begin? I hate this man. He explains things like he doesn't want you to understand them. If you pay attention in class, it is futile. All your attention should be paid to the textbook out of class. He is extremely boring, and by far the worst teacher I have ever met. Be prepared to see points taken off for no reason. Curse you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Try and take another professor, this class is just not worth it.

Honestly don't understand the bad reviews– Bakkal is an amazing professor who gives you all the tools you need to do well in his class. You need to attend and pay attention in each class to understand the concepts, but he makes an effort to consistently review topics. Follow along in the textbook and do all his review questions, you will get an A.

Bakkal gets a bad wrap with reviews. He is an intelligent and funny man that will clearly communicate topics in a non-math way and will provide help if you ask for it. In order to get a good grade, you must attend every lecture. The reviews he gives before is significantly helpful for the tests, and opportunities for extra credit exist on tests.
He used the time very will, teaching us a lot

Professor Bakkal gives great lectures and it's clear he really cares about students memorizing and understanding the material. He's a super fair professor. Attendance is part of the grade but all of the lectures are recorded on zoom.
Professor is hit or miss - lectures can be dry, but tests are manageable with the textbook. Unstuck study was a lifesaver for study prep. Overall, a decent class if you put in the work.
Professor Bakkal was one of the best professors I've had during my time at NEU. His grading is clear and straightforward. He cares about you learning rather than memorizing. 10/10 funny man.

WORST PROFESSOR EVER. No textbook, no slides, boring dry lectures, harsh grader. The only way to study was to note down and memorize everything he writes on the board, or you will fail tests. Tests are all short answer questions, which just requires you to memorize everything.
Very interesting and knowledgeable about the history of economics. He's excellent at helping you understand concepts in layman's terms.

Good professor who gets a bad wrap because he makes you learn. His course was very lecture-based. You could read along in the textbook (and he recommends it) but in my opinion that's not necessary. Go to class, take notes, ask questions, and study for exams and you'll do great. His exam grading is odd sometimes, as is he, but overall a good course.

If youre looking to take Principles of Macroeconomics, take another professor. His lectures are ok, but he doesnt write anything down, and tests are mostly based on the textbook. He is also super nitpicky with his grading on exams and is very bad with technology. Stay away from him if you can
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: No slide, No Textbook, Only note in class, the note is a lot in each course. If you don't write note, you will be terrible, so don't take his class. The grading system of him is different from other. 200 total,4 exam: 35each, discussion: 20,attendance:10, paper: 30.A plus is over 190. if you got 30(it is not bad, over Mean) in each exam, you will..
I loved his teaching in class. You will learn so much. If u actually want to learn, take him

Great professor. Takes attendance but I loved going to class because he obviously knows what he is talking about and makes it interesting. Only downside is that the tests cover a lot of material and make up a large part of your grade but as long as you have been reading the textbook, going to class etc. you will be fine.
Amazing. U will learn so much. But u need to put a decent amount of work. Nothing extraordinary. Amazing lectures. U cannot miss class cause u will fall behind
I enojy his classes. teaches well

Pretty standard professor for environmental economics. Clearly cares about the material, and is a solid lecturer who really knows his stuff. He has a sort of old fashioned style of lectures just using the chalkboard with no slides for online review, so attendance is very important for being prepared for tests. Easy enough grader though.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lecture heavy, boring. That said, the tests are online and multiple choice. Do with that what you will. He's a nice guy just a little spacey and tests can be difficult/questions open to interpretation. If you're scared of math, don't worry, there is little to no math for this its mostly conceptual.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: best teacher ez a
he is the WORST

he is the worst prof I've ever taken in my ENTIRE life. non of the questions on the tests were related to his practice tests. his slides has 0 information, 5 slides maximum usually. AVOID HIM AT ALL COST IF YOU COULD.

The professor is very difficult and the grades are only based on exams and discussions. The exams have extremely difficult questions and do not make sense. Please do not take him if you want a good grade!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you are looking for a Macro class, do NOT take this professor. He is weird and does not make any sense when speaking. He also has the most difficult exams where the average grade was a 15%.

Professor Bakkal has by far been the worst professor I have ever had at Northeastern. Tests are worth 88% of your grade and you have biweekly discussion posts worth 12% of your grade. He only grades the discussions at the end of the semester so you have no idea what your grade actually is. He is incredibly mean and will not help you succeed. SKIP!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Bakkal's lectures are as boring as they come. His tests are very hard and he is an unusually tough grader. I would never take his class again. Best to avoid him as your professor.

I do not recommend Bakkal as a professor. There is no lesson plan, no structure, just a free fall through macro (not based). The only resource he gives is a textbook. His teaching style is just him just talking at his students about anything macro-related for the entire time and misinterpreting 4 out of every 5 questions asked. 


quizlet>bakkal
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: talks the entire time with no powerpoint or notes. gives incredibly tough exams and is very rude when students ask questions in class.
Confusing lecture plans. Over complicates subjects and makes it really difficult to follow throughout the course.
He's really hard to understand and inaccessible. Very unorganized so it is hard to know what's going on. Relied on textbook for the semester.

Professor Bakkal is not lenient at all. He doesn't give extra credit and he's a harsh grader. On top of that, his lectures are very boring and he's it's kind of difficult to grasp concepts withy him. Avoid taking him if you can.

Do not take Professor Bakkal. He is very difficult to communicate with and makes office hours almost impossible to get to. He does not care about individual students. Took him in History of econ. thought and micro theory.

I think prof Bakkal gets a super bad rep but if you are used to a more European teaching style, you'll enjoy his lectures. He does not prepare his slides beforehand and sometimes just closes his eyes and goes for it but he is very knowledgeable on Microeconomics, and you can tell he likes the material. Don't take if you don't like multiple choice.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Bakkal is amazing, I can't stress this enough he made a topic I hated Macroeconomics to a topic I loved, he is really caring for his students and very nice with everyone, its truly shown that he wants his students to learn although its a hard class he will make you love it.

Out of touch and difficult to understand. Jots down concepts on the board and there are no practice questions for us to make sure we understand them. Review sheets are very broad and oftentimes hardly related to the actual exam questions. Lectures were fast and disorganized and it was overall just very hard to learn and be successful in his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Has not adjusted well to the online learning environment. Halfway through the semester we still had to remind him to zoom into the board and record class. His sole teaching material is jotting concepts on the board and he spends the entire class rambling on about irrelevant topics. He is not receptive to students' needs and does not listen well.

Nice guy, but tells you the concepts rather than teaches them. Tests were confusing and made no sense, regardless of how much I read the textbook or studied the review sheets. There is no homework, so it is hard to understand the concepts in the context of a question. Very difficult to pass let alone get an A, best to avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Ilter Bakkal gets a bad reputation from people because he is a bit difficult to understand. He really knows what he is talking about and I learned a lot in his class. There is no homework other than reading the textbook but he will cover everything you need to know in lecture. Just copy what he writes on the board, take notes, you'll be fine.

He does not explain topics very clearly and he is not good at answering questions. This wasn't the worst thing though as the textbook can teach you everything. However what caused me to drop is that the first test had MC questions with the "correct" choice being nonsensical. Economics tests should be straightforward, not ambiguous.

This professor says one thing in class and expects another answer on his exams, that's gaslighting. In an email, he states that he's had students study hard, revises their strategies, and still fail. How can any professor say that and not think there's something wrong with their way of teaching? This class is a joke and the professor is delusional.

The worst professor I ever met at Northeastern. Avoid his course if you still care your GPA!!!!! He does not even know what he wants. "i'm expecting you figure out what you want." He is also a extremely tough grader.

You have to depend on the textbook because his lectures are hard to follow. He writes everything on the blackboard and refuses to use presentations. The exam reviews are really the only helpful thing. You have to write exactly what he wants on the exams if you don't want points taken off. He is sweet guy but just downright confusing as a professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Confused about how he teaches at Northeastern. By far the worst professor. Lectures are boring and he will stand in the middle of class and talk for an hour without a presentation. Refuses to use technology, and complains about how bad it is. Study guides are very similar to tests but if you don't say the exact phrases he's looking for, good luck.
Results on first test pretty much dictate grade in overall class. The book is critical because his explanations are usually not clear.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Had heard less that good things about Bakkal, but he is actually a super nice guy, gives good lectures, and provides great review material for the test. No bs assignments or papers. He's a bit tough on grading, but once you figure out how to answer you're golden. I actually looked forward to class, can't recommend enough.
Very difficult to understand, loses his train of thought very quickly, and doesnt prepare you well for the 5 question exams. Dont expect to get an A in participation either

Horrible professor, is very unwelcoming to questions. Extremely tough grader, and takes weeks to grade exams. Will offer very little support for you outside of class, so get ready to take this course in your own hands if you want to do well.
He's a great professor. I have taken multiple classes with him now. Most of them were a great experience however don't take econometrics with him. Any other class is easy to do well in.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: There were a lot of environmental majors in the room and they had beef with his teaching but he is a good professor. His bread and butter is economics but he has a great interested in environmental studies. If you pay attention and try to learn the material you will get a high grade. His teaching method is relaxed and won't call on someone randomly
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He was awesome. Very clear in his lectures. Good exam review sheets. If you go to class and listen, you will get a good grade.

For Macrotheory he was a good professor, this is my second class I've had with him. If you pay attention, come to class and read the textbook you have nothing to worry about. He does not try to trick students or embarrass anyone.

Professor Bakkal is great. He gives clear lectures, answers question willingly and his exams are similar to his study guides. Most exams have extra credit questions at the end, making it fairly easy to get a good grade. You don't really need to read, as long as you show up for classes and listen. There is no participation grade.

Professor Bakkal is a good professor. He knows the material and is able to get it across. If you come to the class, take notes and are present in mind you should be able to pass with a strong grade. Tests are short answer, he wants to see if you can solve a problem and explain the reason behind reactions.

The worst professor in the Economics department in Northeastern. He is bad at teaching and monotone. His exam are hard and he is a unfair grader. Even if you do well in all your exams, like I did. He will end up giving you a bad grade for participation like 4/10 which will bring you done two grades. I ended up getting a B+.

His lectures are confusing.  His tests are all short answer, which sounds great in theory but the questions are very vague which makes it extremely difficult to understand what answer he is looking for.  You can go into tests completely prepared and come out with an 80% unsure of what you did wrong. His grade scale is unfair 92% is min for  A-
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Avoid him, bad attitude, comfusing, hard system to get good grade in the class
Brilliant guy, incoherent lectures.

one of the easiest topic I've ever encountered but still couldn't get the As. He is clearly not very knowledgeable in the field and makes up for this by being extremely tough grader. Had to write two 2500 words papers,too much for a course that has 3 exams. I put down 15 hours work into papers, all I got back from him was one sentence feedback!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--SO MANY PAPERS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Not to sound extreme, but one of the worst professors I've had. If you enjoyed Environment & Society with professor Daniel Faber, stay clear of this course and professor if you value your time. He's not very knowledgable, talks slow,stutters, rambles, and he easily gets confused by both his own thoughts and questions in class. Very disappointed.

I think he gets a bad rap. Can be hard to follow in lectures but he really knows his stuff. Read the book. Pretty good about answering questions via email or after class, and pretty much tells you exactly the answer he'd be looking for on a test. Speaking of the tests, they're all short answer but they're very similar to the study guides he gives.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Vague lectures, but goes beyond the book, leaving you to wonder what to study.
Hard grader, not clear in class, do not recommend

He is a terrible teacher, although the subject matter was really interesting to me. He is very unhelpful even when you go to his office hours and ask him directly for clarification. I would recommend taking this course with someone else.

Great professor. Hard to follow at times. He gets a bad rap. He's absolutely brilliant. If you take notes, go to class and do the reading you'll get an A. if you skip class, don't read, avoid studying, you'll definitely fail. Easy to pass and easy to fail as well.

Probably one of the best profs I had here so far. If you take notes in class and listen to what he says, you will be able to get a good grade. This obviously implies going to the lectures. The book isn't necessary in his theory classes but could help.
Tests are mostly based on what was taught in the class. Thus, it's highly recommended that one goes to the lectures.
its  been two months and he still hasn't graded my paper
ASSIGNMENTS ARE NOT ALWAYS CLEAR. TEACHING NOT CLEAR. HIS CLASS IS VERY EASY TO FAIL.  AND ALSO EASY TO PASS IF YOU ALWAYS PAY ATTENTION TO DETAIL

Great guy. I'm an Econ major and I've had much worse. He's not super helpful if you go to him for help. People who say he's the worst clearly didn't try...if you go to class, take good notes and listen then you'll do fine. Especially because he essentially just wants to see that you paid attention when he was talking in class.

Definitely the worst professor I have had at Northeastern.  He does not explain concepts clearly or in any way that actively engages the student, does not explain assignments clearly, and is overall not helpful at all.  I would not suggest one of his classes to any student, and I haven't even received any grades yet.

Professor Bakkal is by far the best economic's professor I've had at Northeastern. His lectures are extremely clear and he is very helpful both during class time and office hours. Tests are very similar to his study guides. Take his classes if you actually want to develop as a economist!
Kind profesor.but VERY HARD EXAM! 188/200 total points you can get an A.almost impossible, cuz he has 4 exams, no HW, so only -3 per exam you can get A.

Overall, he was okay. Not one of the best but certainly not one of the worst. Honestly, if you actually read the chapters before class (which he tells you to do), then you'll understand the topics much more easily. I also had him for Managerial Econ and overall was satisfied. If you're engaging he has a lot more respect for you. Overall nice guy.

This professor was not very helpful. I didn't prefer his attitude, and his teaching methods were not helpful. Difficult to understand, and clearly played favorites. Didn't make a difficult/boring subject engaging or interested. Notes are intricate, but tests are not on what is written in the notes. Study guide helps some, but he is a tough grader.

Definitely one of those classes that reading/taking notes from the text is incredibly helpful. He often lets you out of class early, but tends to take attendance. The tests equire lots of studying and intuitive grasps on the concepts and their applications, but I found him to be an overall good, knowledgable guy and enjoyed him as a professor.

Nice guy, but being an econ major, he's one of the worst professors I've had. He does NOT enjoy his job in my opinion. He's not at all helpful OR willing to help if you don't understand something which makes you question why he's even bothering to be a professor. Not helpful in the least, not clear in his objectives either. Frustrating to have.

Great Professor. Love the way he teaches. Very clear in his explanations and is always there to help. This was by far the best class and professor I have had so far. HIGHLY RECOMMEND TAKING MACRO WITH HIM!!

he's the best professor i've had since i study here. he explain everything very well. really concern about the level of understanding in the material and eager to help us to be more understand. 4 exams, 20% each. study from his study guide. REALLY HELPFUL! if you master the study guide, guaranteed 90+ grades because the questions are 90% the same.

Not a good teacher. Expect to teach yourself as you'll have a hard time paying attention in class. He has a very low inaudible voice and slurs his speech which makes it even more difficult to understand him. Will include material on the test he did not teach. Attendance is mandatory. Overall avoid unless you plan to teach yourself.

Professor is a harsh grader and was not helpful during office hours. Attended class, participated, read the chapters before class, took thorough notes, but still was not able to get a good grade.  I would not recommend this class.

Class was okay at first, but this guy was not helpful at all, he basically failed our whole class and half the class dropped it. The tests were hard, even though I studied and my notes were thorough and informative. His lectures were helpful...he doesn't believe in extra credit, he thinks extra credit is for slackers.
Good teacher. Very clear when he talks. Make sure you go to class and read the book and you will do fine in the class

Sort of entertaining to watch teach. He has an interesting sense of humor. Expects you to read the chapter before class, but it doesn't really matter because he teaches everything you need to know in lecture. 4 exams 20% each and 20% for participation (attendance, questions, etc). Forget your notes and study the study guide he gives out!!!!
His lectures can be difficult to understand, but he gives ample time for questions. Covers a lot of material, but you can be successful in this class if you study.

Micro theory. 4 exams - 25% each. He grades hard and you have to know the exact definition, all questions are short answer. Good professor, takes attendance for extra credit and expects you to participate. USE THE STUDY GUIDE! Some of the questions from the guide will be in the exam! free points for that! you can get an easy A for this course.

Really nice guy and very helpful too.  Class lectures can be boring, but he always lets you out like an hour early.  His tests are in 4 chapter increments with no cumulative final or midterm.  Tests are easy if you do the study sheet he gives you and go to the review session he holds.  Really wants his students to do well, i highly recommend him

Quirky guy, I enjoyed his class.He really knows his stuff and is passionate about economics. The lectures can be a little overwhelming but if you take good notes and review them, like he suggests you really start to understand the material.The reading does help, but you can get by without it. Definitely study before tests, and do the study sheet!
Very Reasonable
Good teacher, you do need to put the work outside of class.  Study hard before tests and you will get good grades.  First exam is hardest but use study questions and you will know whats on the exam.
