Helps you if you need it. Sometimes doesn't understand your question right away but so what, he will answer your question til youre satisfied. Interesting lectures too. Recommended.
He is nice, but the exam is really hard and weast time.
SOOOOOOOOOO BAD!!! Switch out now, total ****, bizarre grader, only 4 tests.  get out now!

Pretty good prof. I've always been horrible at economics and only took this class to fulfill a major requirement but I found him to be really helpful and easy to understand. He goes over the same things repeatedly which really helps to drive home what he's trying to teach. Seems to genuinely want his students to understand everything and succeed.

The tests are based on about 15% of the study sheet that he gives you, and he expects that you know much more than just a definition of the given terms. There are four tests and each one is made up of about eight questions, each of which either having to do with graphing or intuitively explaining something. Hardest class I've taken thus far at NEU.

Take notes and write down everything he says. Tests are short but difficult. Make sure you explain yourself entirely. and hes right, you have to study at least a week prior to the test. Hard/weird grader. Say exactly what he wants or you'll getpoints off. Pretty okay teacher.

Ok professor. Study the study sheets he gives out before each exam. Make sure you know every word and an exact definition. He  wants an exact response, anything close he won't give credit for. You can read the book and do well, compare his notes to the study guide

I tried BSing the answers on the first test and it didn't work. I started giving long, detailed responses and got a 100 on every test. He's very smart and knows what he's talking about. He takes attendance only every few classes. His review sheets are  very helpful and similar to the tests. Come to class having read the chapter!

I have had Ilter for 2 courses, and in both he has not done a very good job of explaining the material.  The book is much more reliable. He often confuses himself and draws incrrect graphs. Also he does not answer questions very well. His grading is a bit too hard and he does not change grades when the answer has many options- he only looks for one

a good guy.  his classes are interesting, hes funny, and the tests arent that hard.  as long as you pay attention in class and take some notes you'll be fine.  He has review sessions before every test (but i never went to one).  he sometimes goes off on tangents in class, but are usually interesting, but sometimes brings politics into the class. :(
study his notes

He sucks at teaching, and he is such a hard grader on exams, and I put a lot of time into studying for the exams.  He wasn't helpful at all during office hours, and was actually kind of a jerk.  I'm probably going to get a D in the class, and I have a 3.2 GPA, along with making Dean's list, so avoid his class if you know what's good for you.

I <3 Bakkal! He is so good! His tests are hard but as long as you write a lot in response to the question and really work out and know the problem you're good (BS IT!) He has helpful reviews and his review sheets aren't the test but they help make sure you studied the stuff... use them AFTER you look over your notes.
Ilter is great. Presents boring material well and gives applicable examples. Polite, respectful and all-around a professional teacher.

Macro class - nice guy, presents 100% of the material upfront in class. If you get the answer wrong on a test it's because you didn't pay attention or critically think about what he was teaching. Those who complained probably have never evr had a HARD cours. He was nice and not too dull despite his slightly goofy Turkish accent. Ex "Sply and dmnd".
Got an A, tests were pretty straightforward with never any surprises, always willing to explain until you understand it, Good teacher overall

if u want a class where you dont have to show up take this..it's not that he is cool so you don't have to show up..it's that it wont help you on the tests because he sucks at teaching..so just take the class and read the book thoroughly and you should do ok on the tests
not bad. go to class. do the study guides. and you'll do fine. class is pretty dull, but not terribly difficult.

He's very helpful and fair. In order to do well, you need to show up take notes, know the study questions and thats it. If you do that, you can get an A. You can even get by w/o reading the book since all the info you need to know is on the study sheet. It can be a little boring and repetitive at times, but it helps you learn the info.
No Comments
No Comments
tests are so easy!!! he goes over everything pretty well, doesnt take attendance, gives extra credit, nice guy
hes a good teacher and good man

bakkal is a great professor. hes engaging and really cares about both the subject and the student. why do other students rate him poorly? sure, his tests require alot of thought. he wont accept multiple choice because macro isnt that kind of subject. he wants you to know the material, and you will.

Good MACRO professor.  The way to do very well is to show up to all the classes (he will pass around an attendance sheet some days when attendance is low) and to study and MEMORIZE the study sheets he gives you to work on your own for the tests.  One of the better macro teachers.

As everyone has said, his review sheets don't go over what's on the test, very hard to study for and very unclear what exactly will be on the test. Even going to class and going to class and taking notes doesn't help either. Confusing lessons and not clear at all. Terrible teacher.
horrible, unclear, tests are not like the study sheet or what he goes over in class...and im an A student in every other class this semester except his
He is the worst economics teacher ever! I am an economics major and after his class I took Macro theory and my professor told me everything I learned was useless and wrong! What a waste of my time!
Great teacher.. one of the best...

Ok, so the class is really boring, but you learn SO much in it.  If you go to class, get the notes, and do the review sheet you know exactly what is going to be on the test.  Explains everything, and is happy to help if you don't understand.  Nice guy.  If you can stay awake, I recommend this class.
VERY UNCLEAR TEACHER. POOR EXAMPLES. SOMETIMES HARD TO UNDERSTAND ACCENT. EXPECTATIONS ARE SOMETIEMS VAGUE

I've only had this guy for a couple of weeks but I already dread going to class every day.  Dry as sand, confusing as hell, and he mumbles on top of it all.  I haven't learned a thing in 8 lectures.  But I don't dislike him as a person-seems pretty decent

I am a senior now, and I really believe that he is the worst professor at Northeastern. There is no way to know how to study. He contradicts himself in his lectures. The only way to get a good grade is go to the study sessions.
VERY Easy class if you just go to every class and take all the notes he writes on the board and do the study guides. Tests are exactly what he talks about in class.
He is not a bad teacher! Boring, yes, but Ilter is the cutest--he tells you exactly what you need to know on the test, making them very easy to do well on. Always offers to help. A++
just a bad teacher

I went to every class, wrote down everything he said and didn't get what the hell he was talking about...I'm an A/B student and ended up with a C and had to spend time in the Economics lab...find another professor
Dr. Bakkal is the man.  He is perfectly clear, no suprises on the tests.  If you don't understand him, get out of economics, you suck.
Oh Ilter...ditto to what people said about going to class, taking notes, cramming before tests just to know review Qs (review sessions help) kind of boring, but not terrible,kind of feel bad for him

The class is pretty easy...The key to success in this class is to skip lectures, read the book day before the review, go to review and answer all questions on review sheet, and you get guranteed A or A- on the test, cake.
Sooo difficult to understand- can't follow his lectures. just go to review sessions and find out the EXACT answers to his review sheets- it's the only way you'll pass
This guy is so confusing. Nothing he says connects to the next topic and nothing comes out of the book so dont bother reading it. He doesnt go out of his way to help you to make it better.
No Comments
Nice man, but made things way too complicated.  Hard to understand.  I went to tutoring to pass this course and even they didn't understand him!
i have never had a teacher as confusing as this guy. I never have any idea what topic he is on and what ever he is talking about makes no sense.
1 word: LOSER i hate this teacher never take him ever
Unclear. Makes concepts hard to understand. Take notes on everything, even the non-economics examples. He expects EVERYTHING he says during lectures to appear on tests or he takes points off.
foreign and doesn't use book.  Reel foreign.
Do not use the book. Take notes from his lectures and study them to the dot! If you do this, along with completing his study guides, you will be able to get a solid B or above.
No Comments
stupid, makes the course material seem more difficult than it really is

Ingemi is a great professor, even if her lectures can be a bit dry at times. Tests are open note and not too bad, and she will go over everything in-depth at office hours. Easy points from discussion boards and extra credit at the end of the semester. Very manageable class if you pay attention, and lectures are recorded in case you don't.

Dr. Ingemi is super nice and very knowledgeable. I would recommend her for any course neuroscience/biology related. Her lectures are SUPER dense but she records them. She does Poll Everywhere in class so you have to attend lectures. Her tests are online but she makes them really tricky. There's a lot of content in this class but she's great

Very nice woman, but her teaching style does not work for me (and probably many others). Lectures are dense, and she talks extremely slowly to the point where it's impossible to stay engaged. Very hard to take notes and slides were unhelpful. However, the exams are all online, so everyone does very well.

Professor Ingemi is a great professor. Class is pretty easy to do well in, exams are online and there are lots of extra credit opportunities. She obviously cares a lot about her students and is very open to accommodations when needed. Makes jokes in lectures and is generally very chill. Attendance is based on pollev and she drops 2 classes worth.
The materials is super overwhelming. You will learn A LOT in this class.
I love her. She genuinely loves what she is teaching. Although exams tend to have trickier questions, as long as you pay attention in class, you will do well. I 100% recommend taking her.

Dr. Ingemi is a great professor who's really passionate about the course material. While a lot of the content is dense, she does a great job at explaining important concepts and is always open to answering questions. Also, quizzes and exams were both take home, but very application based and therefore required a lot of thinking and problem solving.

Dr. Ingemi is very friendly, understanding, and willing to help! The lectures are dense and have a lot of content but as long as you are present in class and take good notes they aren't too difficult. Personally, I found the content really interesting. There are poll questions that count as attendance (not graded on accuracy). Exams are online.

Prof is passionate about the subject matter and that helps make the class more engaging, but lectures are 1.5 hours of dense content. Its extremely bio heavy for a psych class. You have to pay attention to everything she says otherwise you spend a lot of time reviewing recorded lectures after of class. Overall good teacher and fair grader though

Professor Ingemi really cares about her students and is passionate about the material she teaches. The lectures can be very dense at times but if youre interested in the subject material it is a really engaging class. The class is almost completely lecture, and the grade is almost entirely tests (however they are pretty fair).Overall great teacher

Super nice and helpful. Encourages asking questions! She changed the testing style to be all open-ended when I took the class. While this made me nervous, it changed how she taught the class and made me feel like I could actually apply the skills I learned in professional settings. Lectures are dense however so you should always attend
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Ingemi was helpful and accessible. As an underclassman, the content felt like a bit much. Her lectures were packed full of detailed information so I suggest attending lectures and taking notes. Grades consisted heavily of exams, but also discussion posts and in-class participation.

This class is the most lecture heavy class EVER. The lectures are 1 1/2 hours and just filled with facts that you are expected to memorize. There isn't too much outside work but this means that a large chunk of your final grade is exams. The exams and quizzes are unnecessarily hard and she throws in so many trick questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Taking Prof. Ingemi's Biopsych class was an absolute dream. I was scared at first because of the lecture-heavy teaching style, but her slides and study guides included everything we had to know. She never turns down a question someone has in class. She seems so passionate about neuroscience and wants us all to succeed. 100% would take her again

Prof. Ingemi is a very nice lady who is very willing to help. She explains concepts well in slides and is very knowledgeable about neurobiology. The slides are pretty dense and specific, and tests can be tough because you really have to apply what you know. Also, tests are worth 80% of the grade. However, she definitely wants students to succeed.

Prof. Ingemi was definitely one of the best professors I've had at Northeastern. Her lectures were clear, but interesting. She does tegrity recordings in case you miss class (but would recommend going b/c of Tophat questions). She posts study guides for exams and is super approachable in office hours.

Dr. Ingemi was super knowledgable and (I thought) likable, but the class is 100 minutes of straight lecture. No activities, or any chance to apply what she says. The online quizzes are harder than they should be, and tests are worth 80% of the grade but she was very nice & approachable. Posts all lectures on tegrity
reviews.rating_tags: Tests? Not many--LECTURE HEAVY--Would take again

reviews.comment: Reads off the lecture slides. Blackboard quizzes are harder than they should be. Final isn't cumulative which is nice.

He is the worst professor to ask questions. the hw are confusing so i asks for clarification every time he say "read the specification" I'm like would i ask that question if the homework wasn't so confusing. i ask because the wording is confusing in the homework and he tells me to go back and reread the hw. other than that hes a great professor

The course is challenging but satisfying! I finally understood what program design truly means. There are multiple sections of this course. They all cover the same material. Mitra's lectures are by far the best.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great course! Learned a lot. If you attend lectures you will pretty much know how do the assignments and labs. A lot of people don't come to class thinking the material is easy and then they lose a lot of points in the homeworks.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Grading is arbitrary, and lectures do not cover what is on the hw. The professor heavily uses hidden autograder tests that students cannot see, and many grade marks are unfairly lost as a result. Hw's are poorly written with many typos and missing details, and are often misaligned with what the autograder expects. TA's are antagonistic.

I had experience working in industry before taking this course. But even then I came across concepts that were completely new to me. I felt like this course made me reflect on how I used to code before. While Prof. Mitra is quite organized and gives great lectures, I wish he worded his assignments less ambiguously. Overall the course is great!
CS5010 is one of my favorite classes. It made a better programmer. I took the course with Shesh and Mitra. Both were pretty great professors and very accesible.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I found this course quite useful. Mitra makes the lectures interesting. The assignments are the most valuable thing about the course. I also learned concepts I hadn't heard about before like mutation testing. Definitely recommend this class.

Rubrics are a surprise after you submit the assignment, lose points if you don't have exact test cases and way too many points are determined by code structure. Asked him to look at a regrade and he just denied it without saying anything.

This class helped me understand and appreciate the agile process. The tools we learned are very relevant. I found the lectures on security and threat modeling very useful as I hadn't done anything like this before. One of the better courses I have taken at Northeastern.

The class is very well organized. It dives deep into modern software development practices. I found the the module on threat modeling super useful. The assignments and the group project really help you apply the concepts and not just learn the theory. Highly recommend this class with Mitra

Prof. Mitra's lectures in PDP are fantastic! I love the way he engages with the students in a respectful and interactive way. I learned so much by just going to the lectures and reading the notes. Taking PDP helped me do well in my internship interviews.
PDP with Mitra is a very satisfying experience. I though I knew Java but this class really helped me understand what clean code means. Definitely recommend taking this class!
Terrible Professor. Do not take him. Doesn't even let you choose your own teammates for projects.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really useful if you want to learn full stack development

Class was taught well, I understood everything presented and enjoyed the assignments. Some assignments were stressful in terms of deadlines, but the content was very digestible. Grading criteria for assignments were harsh, but they have aided me in writing comprehensive tests in the future.
Worst professor I have ever had. He thinks lots of content makes him a good professor, but it really makes the experience terrible for everyone. He killed morale in the class on day #1.

Joydeep Mitra is by far the best professor to take PDP with. The class is inherently difficult because of all the work, the lectures are pretty straight forward though. The school work is a bit annoying but if you put the time in you shouldn't have a problem getting at least B in PDP. PDP does suck though.
Best CS Professor at Northeastern. I loved learning about ALUs.
Learned a lot and gained MERN stack experience. Mitra benches more than you.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Worst course.Overwhelming workload with project-like assignments, exams, class activities, & final project. Instructor reads from slides without ensuring student understanding. Significant portion of grades lies on last week's performance, with 25% for project & 35% for exam. If you don't perform well in final week, grades will significantly drop.

Only take this course if you want to lose your sanity. Extremely boring and unhelpful lectures with 5% for attendance. Assignment every 2 weeks which are no where related to class slides. A pen and paper based midterm exam where he expects us to pretend to be a compiler. The exam was lengthy and unable to finish within time.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CS5500. The whole class is doing MERN stack web development, using JavaScript the whole time. Yes, you can learn a lot - by yourself! So many lecture notes and theories, none of them help you do the project - which is the homework. The course content is so tough, the project is so heavy, and pen-paper exam for coding?? INCREDIBLE

Classes are EXTREMELY boring. Exams are hard. The assignments are all connected together so if you screw one up, you screw them all up. He's not very friendly. The exam grading is very strict. The course overall sucks. I have lost all interest in full stack development. Don't ever take this course under this professor.

probably the worst class I have taken at northeastern. he packs assignments and exams to the absolute maximum and he was confused when the class average was a D- on the midterm and refusing to curve it. assignments take a very long time because he wants everyone to know web dev, and is not even teaching 5500, he randomly started teaching 5610.

Doesn't really care about the overall performance of students. Very strict grader and conducts pen-paper style exams (Students have to read the code and write answers on a sheet). Even after the exam, he won't allow you to see or discuss the grading.

Additionally, his lectures are poorly designed, covered entire full stack dev in just 2 lectures!

I loved her class! I am by no means any finance genius but she truly did care about my success and was always available during her office hours. Her tests were tough but she curves grades at the end of the semester! As long as you come to class and put the work in you will do great!

I took Prof Jang's FINA3303 class and she's very knowledgeable and a caring professor but it's such a difficult class! Be sure to study hard and go to her office hours. She's very generous with grading overall which is appreciated!

She's a super nice person, but her exams are hard in the way that it's super conceptual and challenging. She's a great person, but I just couldn't find a way to do well in her class despite visiting her in office hours and studying forever.
I would totally recommend her class. Not only did I enjoy her teaching in FINA2201, but I also chose to take her Investments class the following semester. She's awesome

Really great professor! Course content was extremely clear. Slides had all the information you needed to master for exams. Her exams were fair and based off of the many in class practice problems we worked through together. She also provides practice tests before exams that reflect the difficulty of the actual test.

The professor is extremely nice and responsive to emails. I took it as an asynchronous class, but the lectures were explained well, and it was easy to follow. The only issue was that the group project was annoying to do asynchronously, but other than that, it was a good class.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Took her FINA3301 class. It was 3 exams, 2 group projects, and some hw assignments. Practice exams are helpful; the exams aren't too bad (Canvas in-person exams). Attendance is mandatory (3 absences allowed) and lectures are pretty boring (mostly reading off slides) but overall not a bad experience. Group work is very tedious

Her FINA 3301 class was good. She is the type of lecturer who understands students, she is flexible with deadlines, allows a few absences, and is chill in general. Lectures are on the dry end but occasionally says a decent joke or two. The final exam is tough.
Hard tests, but she gives good study guides. She wants to see you do well, funny at times and nice overall. Enjoyed having her be a proffesor.

Definitely take her if you can! She can be hard to understand sometimes and her tests aren't super consistent with the material but theres a huge curve and shes so sweet/caring! One of the better finance professors at Northeastern

10/10! Very based and will cold call you and make make a joke or two if you get the answer wrong but really cares about her students. Overall good at teaching, took her for Finance 2201 really not that much to be covered but she makes it interesting and def would take her if you can.

Very sweet and caring prof. She makes sure you understand the topics before moving on. She makes jokes here and there and will also ask about you. Has a slight accent, but you can understand her fine. Overall, would take her again.

Class is mostly her reading through the slides– usually rushes through the examples without giving much time for you to do it yourself. But it's really easy to fall behind on the material if you don't attend class and try to follow along with examples. She wants you to do well and gives good practice tests before assessments, plus extra credit!
John is nice! He gives back what you put in
lectures are boring . thinks too highly of himself

"Three unexcused absences will
result in automatic failure in the class." are we serious rn

I have never seen a syllabus with such strict policies (specifically for attendance). Also the fact that this intro to fonts course gives more work than any of my 4-credit class... If I had a time machine, I would go back to the add-drop period and remove typographic systems from my schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: "MidTerm (25%) + Final (25%)." For an intro to Typography course are you kidding
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: genuinely so boring and SO strict for no reason

Such a boring class, takes himself way to seriously, assigns too much work, absences are counted as unexcused unless you warn him TWO WEEKS in advance, and 3 unexcused absences is a fail. There are also two tests, a midterm and a final, and he doesn't help with learning software or anything of value

If you attend lectures regularly and ask for help on homework questions, you will do well in Professor Jin's class. He is friendly and always willing to answer questions on Piazza or in person. Tests can be challenging, but final letter grades may be curved to compensate for that. Participation can also boost your grade. Overall, great professor!
The esteemed gentleman in question endeavours to take meticulous care that the entirety of one's comprehension be satisfactorily addressed.

This class in my opinion was amazing... Professor Jin is by far one of the best professors I've had - his passion with respect to theory makes learning enjoyable. I would recommend that if you do not enjoy proofs as much, potentially stay away from this class but overall Professor Jin makes it manageable and bearable. 10/10 if I could

Tests are fair and the grading was very fair in my experience. Lectures are dry but Prof Jin was a nice guy and would take all the time you need to understand things. I think this was his second semester teaching and from what I heard previously he really has gotten his stuff together and is great now. A lot of time on HW though. Would take again!
.... don't do it, class has gotten so much harder (to the point where it feels unfair). No multiple choice anymore, all free response - no test corrections either.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I am not good at organic chemistry, but she made the class a lot easier to understand! Highly recommend!
She will treat you nice and actually help you on tests if you show up to class and ask questions. her tests and such are difficult but i made an A, just put in the work.

Jude is a good lecturer and wants you to do well, but she can have a really bad attitude and be initimidating ESPECIALLY during exams. The mcq tests are meant to trick you and have no partial credit. Don't take her unless you had her for ORGO I or want to put in hours of work every day to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Mathews is the best. If you go to class, review her slides and make flashcards, read the textbook, do your Achieve HW early & practice problems from the textbook, and show up to her office hours (NOT ON EXAM WEEKS!!!!), then you will do well. Take it from me who didn't remember a single thing from Orgo 1 lol. She's super smart and so funny. Queen

Jude is extremely intimidating and takes off points even if you email her. Grade is only based mainly on exams. She skims through her slides. I do not recommend her. Tests might be multiple choice but still are difficult
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: dr. mathews may come off as intimidating, but she is one of the most caring professors at northeastern. she gives free questions on exams, which are all mcq. i struggle with organic chemistry and i didn't have her for orgo 1, but as long as you read the textbook and do the homework questions you should be fine.

If you didn't have her for orgo 1, don't take her for orgo 2. She skims through lectures really fast, claiming we should already know the material since it's from orgo 1, but that was months ago. Her exams are relatively easy, but READ THE TEXTBOOK. Don't ask for extensions, you won't get any. Don't ask for extra credit, you won't get any.
ORGO IS HARD NO MATTER WHAT. But Jude is by far the best teacher you could ask for. You are garunteed an A- if you attend the lectures and do the assigned problems and readings.

I am not great at orgo but matthews pushed me to think about these topic from a new perspective. The white board practice days are so helpful and test content is similar to things done in class. Test all multiple choice, some homework. Compelling and respected professor.
She is the best professor at Northeastern. If you want to understand orgo 2 take this class.

One of the worst professors that I've had. She's seems very nice, but isn't a great lecturer or professor. She assumes that we have had her and skips past stuff she has taught in orgo 1, which is very frustrating. She gives no opportunity for us to ask for help on the homework because "it's graded." Don't take if you didn't have her for orgo 1!
She is amazing! She has no bs kinda thing and she grades very fairly.
She knows her stuff. Would take more classes with her again. Super knowledgeable and helps her students if you have questions.

She was very condescending and rude to students. She relies entirely on the textbook resources (power points/homework) to teach for her. She rambles in her lectures, and makes no effort to explain topics in a easily understandable way. If you are not naturally gifted at chemistry, you will not do well.

Read the textbook and show up to class. Orgo isnt that difficult if u put in the work and don't get behind. Matthews isn't gonna hold ur hand and teach u everything 1 on 1- u need to put in effort outside of class. She tests ur ability to think critically so if u can't learn from her... might be more of a reflection on you
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Took Mathews for Organic 1 & 2. She explains material well and makes test content clear. But her attitude was often discouraging—some comments felt rude or unnecessary. She knows the subject, but for me, that didn't make up for the negative classroom vibe. Looking back, I wish I'd chosen a different professor.

Professor Mathews is AMAZING! She somehow finds a way to explain complex topics more easily. She makes videos too, which you can go back and watch. Her exams are very fair, and she does practice problems in class. If you put in the time, do the homework, and don't skip class, obtaining an A/B is feasible.

She is a great teacher. She clearly outlines what's on the test, and sometimes, she even gives free answers during the exam. She is very nice, but make sure you avoid asking questions like "change my grade" cause she will do the complete opposite (I didn't do it, lol). But yeah, take her, she's great.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Mathews isn't the most approachable person; however if you want to learn and succeed in orgo, I would recommend her. She is great at explaining and lectures on the main topics of each chapter as we aren't chem majors. Attendance isn't mandatory but still go, she tells everyone that attends what exactly is on the exam. Great professor.

Orgo is a difficult class already, If you're not willing to put in the work, you will fail. If you show up, do the homework, ask questions, and read the book you will be just fine. Mathews gives fair exams; they are NOT THAT BAD. She is willing to give you all the help you need, but you have to be proactive and ask for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Unprofessional during office hours. I have witnessed her scream at a student for asking a valid question. You should still take the course with her because as awful as her personality is, she makes the material digestible & an A is possible with hard work (you don't need to put in 20 hours a week like she claims, she needs to chill & just retire)

Would not recommend. The teaching style made the material hard to understand, and explanations were unclear. Concepts weren't broken down well. Also, several overly positive reviews appeared before the semester ended—LIKE TWO ON MARCH 19 AND 21, 2025—which seems very suspicious and raises doubts about the class's true quality.

Jude may act like she's tired of dealing with students, she really does care about us and the topic. Although our grades are based largely on 4 hard 20 question tests and fairly simple homework assignments, she ended up giving us a couple of questions back on the final. It wasn't an easy class, but if you're willing to work hard, you'll do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is a knowledgeable and amazing professor. Not only is she super smart but she can communicate. she gives you all the necessary materials to do well in her class. love her

Jude is truly a wonderful professor. Many people don't like her, but that is because they don't put a full effort into Orgo. Doing well in Orgo takes more work than you expect. Put in the work. Go to office hours, even if you have to wait or are intimidated. Get to know her, and continue to show an effort, even when it is hard. You will be okay.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tough class, but Professor Mathews knows her stuff. Attend lectures, use the textbook, and try unstuck study for extra prep - it really helped me succeed.

Jude is a Baddie! She is a good lecturer and makes class engaging and fun. I love going to her class; she is very clear on what will be on the test and what the expectations for class are. She is the best professor I've had at Northeastern and would recommend.
This is what every professor should be like. Jude is clear, and concise and wants people to do well. She is very intelligent but most importantly she can communicate that intelligence.
GOATed professor. 11/10 Mathews all the way.

Mathews is the GOAT. She makes orgo so easy. She tells you exactly what is gonna be on the test in pieces in each of her lectures. If you go to class and do all the homework she give, you'll be set. She is not the best at being understanding and answering questions, so save them for the TAs, but other than that she is the best. Super chill.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tests worth 80%, hw worth 20%. Easy to do well if you put in the work and consistently review the textbook/her lecture notes, especially the whiteboard. Tells you what's on the exam beforehand and gives freebie points on the exams. Attending class is essential for doing well. Great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews is great. For reference, I took her orgo 1 and 2. She tells you exactly what will be on the test the days before when she does white board. Go to class and take notes and pay attention on white board days and do/go over textbook homework daily. For me, I still had to study outside of class for 7 hours/w but it was a very doable A.

While Professor Mathews is an amazing professor, she is not at all fair. She picks favorites and she will take off points from your exams (especially final exam) if you email her about your grade. I worked very hard in her class, despite the grade I received. Orgo is very hard and I would recommend someone who curves / does test corrections!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Matthews can be harsh at times, but she's overall a good professor. She's very clear about what you need to know for the test, but you just have to be willing to put in the time to do practice problems in the textbook.
Take her....The only people who don't like her are the ones that didn't study or procrastinated a lot.
Awful I regret taking orgo with her she made my life a living hell, I went into a deep depression because of this class.
exam averages were around 65 each time and she never curved. i know maybe 4 people who passed, everyone else i know didn't. avoid at all costs.

I enjoyed having Professor Mathews, and she really knows her stuff. Attending lecture and starting everything early will be key for success. She can be a bit harsh, but if you are respectful and show your attention to her lectures (which are helpful) she will be a delight. She is one of the best in the chem department, so try and take her courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She keeps it very real. Her class can be difficult, but she is the last reason why. If you attend her lectures, she will practically tell you exactly what's on the test. She tells you what to study, what not to study, and how to study. Content is very clear, and the textbook is very helpful. Anybody can get an A in her class.

I think she is a GREAT teacher. She knows her stuff and explains things well but can be a bit unfair at times. I had multiple friends in the class get points added to tests for no reason while other friends didn't get any points added. Her tests are multiple-choice and can be difficult.

She made orgo my favorite class and I'm lucky enough to have her for orgo 2. She is very clear about what she expects. You have to put in the time but if you show up to class and listen to her, you will do well. Lectures are very organized. Can't stress enough how great she is.

Her class was hard, but I'm sure that all sections of organic chemistry are hard. As a professor, I think she is amazing. She is funny, but most importantly, she really cares about the students who work hard, and she will do everything to help them do well in the class. Study long hours, and you will succeed.

She isn't worth the hype and i expected more. Mostly self-teach stuff from textbook and the lectures are difficult to listen to because it sounds like she's rambling. Tests all MCQ. I did everything I could study/practice wise and I still feel like it wasn't enough.

Mathews was a pretty good professor. Her outlook on lecture was to review the textbook portion that we were supposed to have already read before class. This teaching style wasn't great for me because I don't learn well from textbooks. Her lectures review what you should know but don't go deep enough for her exams. Overall, decent class.

Jude is very knowledgeable about Orgo, but her lectures were horrible. She flew through power points, and skipped certain topics because she ASSUMES that the class already knows it. Very passive aggressive, and at times can be rude. I emailed her a couple times with questions, and was either ignored or indirectly called out in class/thru Canvas.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I've ever had. Went into this class thinking I was in good hands due to the previously good reviews. Lectures were flown through way too fast to learn anything, professor constantly skipped over stuff in the notes because "we should already know it". Homework was practically irrelevant. Constantly passive-aggressive.

Knows that orgo is tough but gives you all the resources necessary to succeed. Don't expect to do well if you don't spend hours studying every week. Exams worth 80% and are all multiple choice. Passive aggressive and complains about the students at Northeastern but she's valid and funny.

Going in I was expecting a great orgo prof based on how praised she was. However, she did not live up to my expectations. Her lectures are dense and goes fast but her example problems and book problems are similar to the exam. Her exams are challenging and for the hours that I put into the course I feel like my grade does not reflect that.

I think Mathews is as fair as it gets for orgo. Her exams are multiple choice (worth 80% of grade) and are very resemblant to in class examples and lecture notes. Mathews herself is obviously very knowledgeable but spends probably 1/4 of every class making weird passive aggressive comments. If you put in the work you will do just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Took Matthews for Orgo 1 & 2. Her lectures lead directly to her tests. Her exams are fair, and she lectures well/understandably. Her class requires work in understanding the material and reviewing problems, but I would say she's NEU's best Orgo Prof. Her organization on canvas is consistent and easy to follow. If you're taking Orgo, she's the one.

She is a good prof and grades easily, but she comes off as a bit passive aggressive. I would rate her higher, but all of my interactions with her were quite uncomfortable, and she made fun of a former colleague for going into rehab. Someone in my family had this happen to them, and I didn't appreciate it.

Professor Mathews is one of the best professors I've ever had. She understands that Orgo is hard and will do anything to help you succeed. You will master the material with her help. She has a rep. for being tough and mean, but she's anything but that. I wish every professor were like her!

She was absolutely the best professor I have had at Northeastern. Super understanding and super flexible towards her students. Has a big heart and will lend a helping hand if needed. Please take organic chemistry with her, you will have a great experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: jude is witty and passionate about orgo. she sets high expectations from day one and will give supplemental material for you to understand the concepts. she's tough but she also is fair/deeply cares about her students. go to every lecture and do lots of practice to cement those ideals into your head! if you're willing to put in the work, take her.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Respected

reviews.comment: Professor Matthews is a genuinely nice person who will do everything possible to give you an A if you show up and try. She gets a bad rap for being mean and unapproachable but she actually cares if you bother to actually try and show her you care about your grade and don't just want a handout. Plus you'll actually learn the material well.

I feel lucky to have had her for both orgo1/ 2. Obviously organic chemistry is going to be difficult but she gives you absolutely everything you need to succeed. She basically tells you every single question question on the exams you just need to listen. Make every possible effort to have her in your schedule you will not be sorry! Super funny also

Professor Mathews is amazing! I had her for Orgo 1 and Orgo 2. She was so accommodating and wanted me to succeed. She did everything she could do to help me, from recommending tutors to going over problems with me. Take advantage of her office hours and emailing her with questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Really great teacher and knows her stuff well. Makes sure you know what is going to be on the test

I'm 2 days into Orgo 2 with Brian Fulton, and I already miss Jude. She is an excellent lecturer, especially considering the difficult concepts covered in Orgo. She very clearly says what will be on exams, and stars those topics in her slides so you can go back while studying. However, make sure you do the textbook problems if you want a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews is a great teacher! Orgo 1 and 2 are quite easy if you do the HOMEWORK (this is the best simulation for test questions, and lots of questions on the exams come straight from the homework). Sometimes errors on exams with typos and incorrect keys on Gradescope occurred, but she fixes the problems if you reach out.
Matthews is a great prof/very knowledgeable. A lot of learning is independent though. Be prepared to read the textbook and study long hours to get an A.

I highly recommend Professor Mathews. Her tests are fair as long as you do the textbook problems and don't fall behind. The only outside assignments are quizzes which boost your grade. If you put in the work, you will do very well in her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: 3exams and 1 final. I personally did not like her teaching style.  Doesn't do 1-1 help but she does reference good tutors to go too. Def if i didn't stuy last minute I guarantee a B+ to A-.  Her class isn't hard, no hw, only quizez which she gives 100 to at the end anyway. Grades r  dependent on the exams .

Professor Mathews' lectures were very informative and they provided us with almost everything we would need for quizzes and exams, which were multiple choice (very helpful for an organic chemistry course). That being said, she came off as passive-aggressive at times and was not very responsive to email. Despite this, I really enjoyed her class.

She teaches orgo - it's a hard class. However, I feel that she explained the material clearly and concisely as well as outlined every possible combination of tools any student could need to succeed in her class. She told us that studying orgo could be like taking a part-time job, and she's right; but, if you put the time in, you'll do great!

Prof Mathews is an awesome lecturer. If you go to class, she will tell you exactly what you need to know for the tests. Orgo is hard no matter what prof you have. Make sure to read the textbook and do practice problems!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I came into orgo 2 hearing great things about Prof Mathews. However, she was starkly different than the descriptions. Claims students are lazy and do not care and gets mad rather quickly. Is not really someone you can talk to without risking losing points on your grade. Other than that organized teacher and probably better than the other options.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Mathews covers all content needed on exams, although sometimes she goes into detail about information that is not important for the exams.Does not accept emails without penalty and is not very open to offering accommodations. Is very knowledgeable on the topic and is clearly experienced in the field.Wants students to understand the material.

She's a great lecturer and knows the content well. On the flip side, I genuinely doubt she would care if I was pushed from a cliff and paralyzed. She would still say I'm lazy for asking for accommodations and would probably take five points off my grade for emailing her. Her syllabus has the suicide hotline on it; don't expect any hand-holding.

Okay teacher but teaches to her test, not to learn. Fairly easy if you attend lecture. Homework ?s rarely useful. Very negative towards students, insinuates no one cares or tries in basically every lecture. Lots of errors in practice ?s, announcements, and tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Jude Mathews is the best professor I have ever had. Orgo is very difficult, but she makes concepts easy to understand. I had her for Orgo 1 and 2 and can not recommend her enough.

Can't stand her. Incredibly rude to students and doesn't believe in them. Constantly says that if you don't do things her way, you will fail. Very unapproachable, makes students feel bad for needing to repeat concepts. Incredibly disorganized. Emphasized memorization over understanding which is not helpful especially to pre-med students.

Best professor I have ever had. Amazing lecturer and makes concepts easy to understand. Exams are always reasonable. Took both Orgo 1 and 2 with her and had great experiences. Jude Mathews is the best.

Her syllabus is very intimidating, but she's a great professor. Entire grade is mostly exams, but she's very clear about the content that will be on each exam and it's not difficult to do well in the course as long as you attend lecture. If you're taking orgo, you definitely need to take it with Mathews.

Mathews is a great professor, she genuinely cares about facilitating her students' success and it's clear through the sheer amount of learning resources she provides. She really knows what she's doing and I highly recommend everyone to take her if they have the chance.

mathews is awesome- DO NOT TAKE ORGO WITH ANYBODY ELSE!!! Exams are straightforward and she tells you exactly what you need to know. She is very straightforward and blunt, which can be off-putting at first, but she really cares about her students and wants you to well. As long as you put in the work, you will get an A.

Professor Mathews is an amazing professor with a great understanding of chemistry. She can be a little rude or condescending at times but she is just one of those professors who will not give out undeserved compliments. If you keep up on the readings and textbook questions in this class it is very easy to get an A.

Prof. Mathews is amazing. She explains everything so well, and if you put in the time outside of class you'll do well in this class and actually UNDERSTAND orgo. Don't be intimidated by her syllabus, she's really nice and considerate. She holds a lot of office hours, but make sure to show up at the beginning of them because a lot of people attend.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Amazing Professor!! She is so caring, fair, and understanding. She has a lot of students, but is always willing to help when she can. She can come off as abrasive sometimes, but means the best. Also, her lecturing style takes time to get used to, but she breaks down all concepts in a digestible way. Put in the work, and you will definitely do well.

Had her for orgo 1 and 2. 4 exams, all multiple choice. Prof Mathews is such a gem and makes a super difficult subject very understandable. Always tells you what to expect for the exam, and never tries to trick you. As long as you put in the work outside class and GO TO REVIEW SESSIONS, you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Mathews is an amazing professor who really cares about her students. Her class is based on three exams and the final (each 25%) but the exams are very doable and she tells you exactly what will be on them in office hours and class. If you do the homework problems she recommends, read the textbook, and go to class, youll do really well!

Possibly my most favorite professor -- very good at explaining difficult concepts in an easy-to-understand way, and very clear about what you are expected to know for the exam. Spends a lot of time teaching the material as well as going over many practice problems. Always willing to answer questions in and out of class. Office hours are great!!!!

Mathews is amazing! If you go to her lectures and office hours, she tells you everything you must know the exam, and if you do the textbook problems as well, you should definitely get an A in the class. She is not trying to trick you and is always available to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews was a really good organic chemistry professor. She tells you exactly what you need for the exam and it's up to you to put in the work and do the extra problems. She's fair and wants you to get a good grade. Be sure to go to the lectures where she reviews because she goes through a bunch of practice problems that help on the exam.

Professor Mathews is the best professor you can have for organic chemistry, hands down. She really wants everyone to do well. She's speeds through lectures, but she does a LOT of practice and is super available outside of class. Go to class, review all the notes, and do the homework. You'll do great.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews is the best. Orgo is hard, but attend lectures and OH, do practice problems, and study hard, and you will do fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof Mathews is one of the best professor at Northeastern. She works incredibly hard and if you go to her OH and email her with your questions she will promptly answer. She also cares alot about her students. Orgo is hard, but she makes is easier to understand. If you do the textbook problems and follow what she says you will do well!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: THE BEST. Tells you exactly what is/isn't on exams, does practice problems exactly like the exam, gives freebie questions on exams. MUST read the textbook, watch her lectures, and do HER practice problems (don't really need to do the book ones) to do well. Advice: print out her powerpoints and annotate them during lectures. I love this professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews is very organized and tells you exactly what you need to do in order to do well in the class. It is hard, but if you read the textbook sections that she assigns and do the problems you will do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She's a great teacher and her exams are moderately difficult and are multiple choice. Going to class is essential to understand the material. She goes over problems in class that are like what you see on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: When I tell you, she is an ABSOLUTE GEM. If you have the honor and luck of getting into her class, you will get the best education Northeastern offers. Yes, her lectures may be fast, but that's just when she goes over slides. She then goes over reactions EXTENSIVELY herself. Also, dont be afraid to ask questions, she WANTS people to ask and engage
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She can be tough and intimidating, but all in all she's really helpful and tells you specifically what you need to know for tests. Definitely ask her questions if you're confused and go to class. Just don't ask her about the curve (but she keeps it secret which is kinda stressful) or when office hours are (look in the syllabus) and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Had for both Orgo 1 & 2, Spring 2020 (half online) & Fall 2020 (100% online). Jude's in-class practice problems are rly helpful, and she & her TA's are very good at explaining. If ur a slow learner like me, learn how to take textbook notes quickly, do all practice problems, & go to recitations! PS there's ZERO math so I enjoyed more than HS chem
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The only reason she would ever get low ratings is because students didn't put in the necessary work. This is Organic Chemistry; it takes work. But, if you are willing to put in the studying outside of class and attend the lectures, there is no better professor. Her teaching style is incredibly effective and she's so passionate about teaching. 11/10
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She is amazing. She tells you exactly what you need to know for the exams, which makes studying a lot less stressful. She makes sure to provide plenty of examples during lecture. Also, don't be scared by the syllabus, she is actually a very kind and approachable professor.

Professor Mathews is the best professor I've had. She is caring and wants her students to do well. She will spend significant time making sure you understand the material. Keeping up with the book homework helps a lot, but she also spends 1-2 days before the exam doing practice problems that are very similar to exam questions.
One of the best professors I've ever had. Very funny and nice. Incredibly effective teaching.

Professor Mathews is the best professor I've had at NEU! She tells students what is on the test and spends multiple days doing practice problems ahead of time. She tries to make the material understandable for all. If you talk to her she will help you a lot! Don't be scared by her syllabus - she is the nicest professor!

Her exams are multiple choice, and in class, she goes over mechanisms and types of questions she would ask many times. Take her for orgo 1 would give you a really good orgo foundation. She cares about her students deeply and that they really understand what's going on. If you do the textbook problems and listen in lecture you'll be just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Mathews is the best Chem prof I've had. She knows her subject very well and will go to great lengths to make you understand. Don't miss her lectures as she tells you the important parts and explains very well. Study hard because its orgo but if you follow her instructions to the T you will get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Jude is super caring for her students. She can come off as harsh & blunt, but if you treat her with respect she'll respect you. Provides slides for each lecture, reviews them in class, does practice problems, & assigns nonmandatory problems from textbook for hw. Tests are all multiple choice, but you def have to study a lot in order to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Make sure you sign that last page on your syllabus for the first day of class. Mathews is definitely the best orgo prof and explains everything the most, but still a very difficult class. However, she always tells us what's going to be on the test. As long as you do the textbook practice problems and really listen in class it's not too bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Jude Mathews is hands down one of the best professors at Northeastern. She can come off as stern and unapproachable, but once you get to know her she is very funny and extremely caring for her students. If you respect her, she'll respect you. Attendance is not mandatory but going to lectures will definitely help, along with doing textbook problems.

Professor Mathews is the best professor I've ever had! She cares about her students and genuinely wants them to do well. If you come to class and do the homework you will be more than prepared for the exams - she does lots of review! She's easily accessible and takes the time to answer your questions. She makes Orgo interesting and enjoyable.

Organic chemistry is a very demanding course that requires a lot of work to be put in outside of class. Given that, however, Prof. Mathews really tries to help you understand the concepts behind orgo and just wants to see her students succeed. She's funny and caring and will try her best to answer any chemistry question you have. HIGHLY recommend!!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: One of the best profs at Northeastern. Organic chemistry is hard so yeah you have to do work but her lectures are fantastic and she makes it pretty clear what you have to do for the tests. Hoping to have her next semester, would take again.
Great professor that cares incredibly for her students.

Best orgo professor. I love Jude. Her lectures are awesome compared to other orgo professors and her tests actually reflect what was taught in class. She is knowledgable and understanding. If you put in the time, you will get a good grade. I would def take her class again if I have the chance.
She is one of the best professors in my experience. She makes orgo so easy. If you just pay attention and do her homework there is nothing to worry about.

Professor Mathews is the best orgo professor! She does multiple days of review before the exam and tries her best to make sure everyone is well prepared. If you come to class and do all the homework you will do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: The class was pretty easy if you showed up and paid attention. Mathews can be very harsh and unapproachable during office hours so if you are looking for extra help I would look elsewhere

Do not take orgo with anyone else besides her!! She's amazing and cares so much about her students. I wish she could teach all of my classes because she really made one of the supposedly worst pre med classes, a breeze. Make sure you go to her lectures and her office hours and you'll be all set

Jude is honestly one of the best professors at Northeastern. She is very clear from the beginning what she expects from her students. Her exams are no surprise, so if you pay attention in class you will do well. She can be tough at times and seems difficult, but all she really wants is her students to do well. 11/10 would recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Mathews is honestly one of the most straight forward and reasonable professors. Orgo II was a breeze because of her. She explains EVERYTHING extensively and gives a TON of review problems. Just do the bare minimum and you'll do great. What a sweet lady! If you care about your GPA and want an A take mathews for orgo!

Lectures feel all over the place. Is rude to students asking questions. Isn't accessible outside of class. Wastes alot of time complaining about the students or other professors. Seems to resent when someone asks her to go over a mechanism again "we already went over this".

Mathews is the most accessible Orgo professor at Northeastern. She clearly states what's going to be on the tests and is extremely helpful outside of lecture. Keep all the notes from Orgo 1 because Orgo 2 is almost a repeat of Orgo 1 and it will help you so much the second semester of Orgo. I would only recommend Mathews to other pre med students
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: People advised me to take Mathews. At first I thought that was a bunch of bull because she's not a good lecturer, but she makes up for it through practice problems and the straightforwardness of her exams. She goes through every type of problem you need to know and if you put in the work you'll do well on her tests. Will be taking her again for 2.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Jude was so sweet. This was the hardest I have worked for a class but it was worth it. Studying hard is so worth it because you will ace the tests.

Dr. Mathews is one of the best professors I've ever had. She is extremely clear and tells you what you need to know. Going into Orgo I was definitely worried, but she makes it accessible. In my experience, doing the homework and going to class is essential for doing well on exams, and tutoring is also a great way to reinforce material.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Would take again

reviews.comment: This course is very informative and fair. Mathews is an amazing professor and she teaches organic chemistry very well. The only way you can fail is unless you don't pay attention in class and/or don't do practice problems/study. Literally show up to class and study and you'll be solid.   Good luck.  A+ Recommendation !!

Mathews is a great professor. She is very clear about what you will see on her exams. Her PowerPoints and boardwork are very helpful and you won't need to read the book if you go to every class and take great notes. You need to put in a lot of work on your own time (reviewing/doing assigned homework).
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Mathews is probably my favorite professor I have had at Northeastern so far. She is no nonsense but can also be a really funny person during lectures. She definitely cares about her students and wants them all to do well; she never tries to trick us on tests and is always very clear about what information will be on the exams.

You will definitely have to work in her class however as long a you attend lecture, do the homework, knock out a couple of practice problems, and go to office hours and recitations (not as bad as it sounds trust me) Then expect an A in her class. Good luck !
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very good teacher. Really cared about everyone understanding the class.

PROF MATHEWS IS AWESOME! She genuinely wants her students to do well and is very approachable and is always willing to answer questions about the material. As long as you come to class, follow along with her practice problems, and do the textbook hw she assigns, you will do great in the class! Plus, her accent is adorable:)

Not a great professor, she treats students like they are still in high school. Rather than ask about concepts on the test, she prefers to assess how well you memorized her class notes. Overall I would suggest one of the other professors.

Professor Matthews explains everything very easily, and is ready to answer your questions. She is very approachable and nice, which makes questions so much easier. She also tries very hard to relate the material to the real world, so you get to understand why what you're learning is relevant.

Amazing professor. Clearly cares about her students and will essentially tell you exactly what is on the exam in the lecture. Do not let the bad comments scare you; if you notice, none of them reported their grade. Organic chemistry is hard, but if you read and go to the lectures, she will make sure you do well. 100% would recommend to others.
She's pretty awesome. Missing lectures makes things difficult, she goes over almost all of the reactions that will be on the exam. When taking the exam, if you're confused, ASK HER A QUESTION!
She is really kind and nice professor, just Orgo is though.

Professor Mathews was awesome. She delivered the information clearly and made it clear what to study for the test. She makes herself available for extra help and does not try to "trick" you on exams. Organic Chem requires studying and understanding the mechanisms. Basically if you STUDY and ATTEND  OFFICE HOURS, an A+ should come easy.

Organic is hard. She's not terribly difficult but she's also not very clear. Rather than test you on what appears to be pretty simple ideas, she'll throw something on the test that you've never seen before. Annoying way to ensure not everyone gets an A since at this point, everyone works hard enough for one.
I learned a lot. Great teacher.

Worst teacher ever. Focuses too much time on seating format on test day. If she just spends as much time on teaching and reviewing as she spends on seating, maybe the students won't fail. Stopped giving us bonus pts and started to deduct pts on exams  if we don't write our name on every page of the exam front and back. Completely unnecessary.

One of the good teachers out there. She listens to each student and also goes out of her way to help explain difficult concepts such as NMR. I was expecting an easier class reading reviews from last semester, but definitely do not think so. Prof. Mathews makes Orgo 2 easy to understand and digest! Make sure to attend class and esp. review sessions.

The classis made pretty easy for you. She doesn't blindside you on the test, either. Just tests on basic concepts in very uncomplicated ways. Doesn't make you memorize mechanisms, either, just predict the product given two reactants. You can either go to her class, or read the textbook, but either way the test is an easy A. Helpful when asked, too.

You think you're better off taking this professor's class because you think she'll be easy? Don't make the same mistake many people made. You're better off taking other professors. Her test does not reflect the materials covered in class. Disorganized and inefficient, yet exams are still hard. Don't expect to get much out of the class.

Had her last and this current semester. Is very disorganized and her ppts utterly useless. In her syllabus it states that her class is optional and is only a supplement to the textbook. Her intro to lectures is to yell at her students for discussing their orgo problems and reiterating the fact that her class is not mandatory. worse teacher ever.

A great professor, very reaching out to all the students. Reviewing the materials every day is crucial as cramming a day before the exam wont gurantee you an A. After all...no matter how easy and reaching out the professor is, the course taught is Organic Chemistry.

A genuinely nice professor. Her tests can be difficult... It is organic chemistry. She is still new here and I believe working on her teaching style. If you spend extra time and put in an effort, Professor Mathews will reciprocate and do her best to help you do well. A really great woman, and I believe a great teacher.

Amazing teacher, really wants to help his students. I really enjoyed his class and found him to be one of the best professors I have had at this school. He makes the subject simple and breaks it down so that everyone can understand.

He's one of those professors whose enthusiasm for a subject wears off on you, and you find yourself excited about something as basic as intro stats. His class is lecture-heavy, but so engaging that time flies by. He's available to discuss anything you might be working on outside of class, and he did so happily (most of the time).
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I had him for INSH 6500 which is Statistical Analysis as an in-person class. I did not have a good experience with him, most of the homework took me anywhere from 3 to 6 hours for a maximum of 5 questions. Also, the projects were not good either. It took me and several other people 5-10 hours per project and we'd get different answers to them.

He is one of the best-balanced professors I've taken. Assignment due dates are clearly communicated. The workload is well-structured and manageable. His video lectures are light. The assignments are clear and well-organized, and he gives detailed instructions along with a grading rubric for all writing assignments. I would take his classes again.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Shantanu is a really good prof who cares about you and wants you to do well and will do everything he can to help you get there. His lectures are engaging and is very open to questions. Gives many practice questions for revision and midterms (2) do not contain anything out of practice. HW are usually easy-medium, shout out to TA Dayonara!

Prof Kamarthi genuinely wants you to succeed. He opens day one with that and means it. Logical concept order, great TAs, fun free-form final project. Midterms pull directly from practice problems he flags explicitly, plus grade recovery opportunities. Harder to fail than get an A if you show up, pay attention, and give some genuine effort.
Great
Excellent teacher
This assignment for you is really useful, sometimes the instruction in class he couldn't explain intuitively.

The class sizes are too high to be managed by professor. All the lectures are on PPT including mathematical proofs of algorithms which makes it hard to follow. Professor makes the efforts to make everything easy for students.
Lectures are interesting at start of the the semester. Eventually gets bored.
He is good and respects your work.
Excellent teaching and provides multiple ways of solution to easyily understand.
Great educator, remarkable experience. Arabic coaching on point. Business teaching not too great. Chinese classes are exceptional.
Really good professor he knows his information really well, I would recommend anyone to attend his classes. Classes are well structured and interesting.
Lectures are very long and boring

Took his class last semester. Doesn't teach anything. A 9$ Udemy course thought me more than his class. He is a nice person but not a great professor. I hope he takes into account his bad reviews and teaches better.

Very systematic course and Kamarthi teaches it that way. Goes through a cycle of concepts, introduction of equations, practice problems, homework assignments based on concepts learned in class. Kamarthi is a very good professor. A bit dry at times but that could be because material is also dry. Would recommend.
He cares about his material and students. He is knowledgeable.

Moody and unorganized. Grading has no consistency. If he likes you it will help. Doesn't cover material well. Dives into detail on stuff he knows well but barely skims over stuff he hasn't spent time to learn. Very little passion for teaching.

Most boring professor I ever had in my life. Lectures are painfully boring, he has no motivation and zero passion for the subject. Crazy amounts of work and very useless guy in terms of preparation for homework. Avoid at all costs.
Mr. Kamarthi was thorough in his preparation for the classes, he graded fairly and he encouraged student participation.
Top professor at NEU

Horrible. I worked in manuf. on co-op before this class, and i was excited to get into some more details. But his lack of communication and poor teaching left me stunned. Stay away from this class if you can. Who gives students a packet of computer code 20 minutes before class is over and says "learn this for tomorrow's midterm"? Way to 'teach' us.

Prof Kamarthi was a really nice guy. But the student (IE's) treated him like crap, they complained about everthing. The Tests were straight out of the problems sets he would go over in class and post solutions to on blackboard. What more could you ask for. Course was not what i expected from description but i did Enjoy the lab. Really nice guy!
Very good professor, Knows the subject and has descent personality. I would love to take a course with him again.
Good preparation for the stuff that he teaches...and not too bad homework too. I'd say he is too decent a teacher to be true.
She should go home

I am not a conspiracy theorist, but I do believe with all my heart that Aisulu is an angel. When I am not looking in her direction, I swear I see wings sprouting out behind her back, and dare I say, the glimmer of a halo on her head. Aisulu cares deeply about her students and real learning. She has a beautiful respect for the field of linguistics.

She was a great professor. She was very lecture driven but she is always willing to make sure that you understand. She will incorporate practice problems into her lectures so you have a better idea of what she is teaching us. Her homework is pretty easy if you review the lecture slides. She is very lenient and is always to help accommodate you.

The professor is extremely nice, she wants to make sure you actually learn the material. There is only 1 test. The homework is graded fairly with great feedback and she does not really mind latework. The lectures in class are long and can be boring but you will understand everything from her with no studying. I 100% recommend taking her class.

As a first-year international student, this class made an already tough transition even harder. This class lacked real content, and grading was inconsistent. Not helpful at all. Explore other sections if you can.

I think Professor Parker means well, but Thanos thought he was a savior. Save yourself, and do whatever you must to escape the annoying and constant hell that would await you if you took Professor Parker. Run. If you can't run, walk. If you can't walk, crawl. If you can't crawl, transfer. If you can't transfer, find Jesus.

SAVE your time and do not TAKE HIS CLASS. His grading is based on feelings and he's an egotistical shiny bald headed man who thinks he knows it all about business. He doesn't count wellness days as a valid absence unless someone died. Nothing you do will get an A unless u give him head. PLEASE don't ruin your college experience and dont take him
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I hoped the green line would hit me so I didn't have to go to his class. He doesn't even know what he wants to see in the projects and will critique everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Over-hated in my opinion. He's a good guy, wants to be tough on you to get you ready for the real business world. He's very business smart, but not that tech savvy. If you put in extra effort with your group project then you can get an A-. Chances are you get screwed and you get a B though.

He is sadistic and grades very randomly and strictly for no reason. He doesn't count wellness days as a valid absence and doesn't care if you are genuinely sick. He seems unsatisfied no matter how much work you put in. Don't take him.

Don't take if you care about your grades. Doesn't grade assignment and then keeps saying he will grade but then assigns another assignment. he should stop assigning more things until hes graded everything. He once cancelled class and then told us because of that missing day we dont get a review session for our next quiz.

If you want a un-athletic, slightly intelligent, and shiny bald headed man to talk AT you not TO you about business, this is just the course for you.
