I received a C as my final grade for the course, and heres why: he's self centered, I don't like bald people, & HE CAN'T TEACH. Short and sweet, DO NOT take this course. The KP shall NOT be validated.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: While I like the Ken as a person as he likes his students and pushes them to become better business people, he doesn't know how to grade and give proper feedback. He'll give one thing of feedback and deduct points for it, then say "oh it's just a suggestion! No need to actually change it!". An A IS IMPOSSIBLE!!!!! If you care abt ur GPA, DROP!
Bro was straight buggin yo. Class dummy hard and lectures odeeeeeeee. Man F this guy won't teach u NUN. Ya heard
He's overall pretty chill, sometime a bit demanding for no reason. Mixed feelings but overall, he did curve the grades at the end.
Without this course, my school's life experience would be 7/10, but now it is 1/10 thanks to him! I would be happier if I knew that this course was NOT REQUIRED sooner and immediately dropped it.

He is a terrible teacher, does not give any feedback, and when you ask for feedback, he is unclear and says that you are competing with everyone else for an A. Gives big assignments without much time to do them, and doesn't give any instruction on how to complete them well. Don't take this class its NOT required. Nothing you can do will get an A.
Not a good first impression of Northeastern, is a hard grader because he doesn't specify what he wants from his students but expects them to go "above and beyond".

egotistical businessman that peaked in college. all he does is talk ab himself and how he doesn't believe in grades so idk why he chose to be a professor. you'll never get an A. Freshmen this class isn't even required so don't bother wasting your credits and GPA with this prof.

Cancelled class 6 times over the course of the semester. Class average on the first exam was a 45% because KP made up questions and did not proof read the exam. Never once provided a substantive lecture on brand management, every class felt like a fight with him about grading. Severely outdated. Hmmm... Do yourself (and GPA) a favor and avoid!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: So bad in grading

I was only taught one lecture by him this semester. Class consisted of him talking about himself, complaining about his life, him giving us meaningless busywork, and class arguing about his grading policy. Sure he gives you a rubric, but he also has a different imaginary set of expectations that you magically have to follow in order to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: KP is a very experienced businessman. He tells helpful anecdotes about business and some of his assignments are about really interesting topics. However, get ready to do more group projects than you've ever done in your life and get ready to receive Bs on everything with no feedback. His ego is huge, he is rude to students, and he can't use Canvas.

There are no rubrics so he just makes up grades. HE DOES NOT GIVE As. If you want higher than an 89 on assignments or projects you better be ready to cure cancer. So many assignments in class that would take hours to do outside of class yet never collected some of them so it was a waste of time. You do everything in groups that you don't pick.

Ken is a tough one. Very hard grading because he is very smart and does not tolerate not trying hard at something. He hates the idea of grades and thinks they don't matter which leads to a lack of timeliness and care when it comes to his grading. He cares a lot about teaching, but maybe a little too much...
tough but good

Parker is a great guy with a good personality. He does spend a lot of time talking about himself, but I do think that his stories were interesting. If you pay attention in class and do the right type of studying you'll be okay, and have a good time. Class is weighted mostly on 3 exams and 2 semester-long group projects.

Prof Parker is a tough grader, and has stated in class that GPA is not very important. However, you learn a lot from him. He has a wealth of experience in the marketing field and a way of telling personal stories that captivates interest. If you have grit, enjoy participation, and value learning over an easy A, then Professor P is a solid choice.

Single worst teacher at northeastern. He spends the entire class talking about himself and his supposed business ventures. Has very unclear grading guidelines and so one must ask for him to tell you your grade since the one on canvas is never correct. He then just seems to make up a grade on the spot, and appears to do the same with final grades.

Likes to yap about himself. Most of his "career experience" is irrelevant to the material in class and especially on  the exams. If I wanted someone to yap at me for 1.5 hours, I would eat dinner with the Kardashians.

Grading criteria is UNCLEAR+INCONSISTENT. Study guides are UNHELPFUL for exams, containing LARGELY IRRELEVANT information. FRQs NEVER yield full credit and are W/O explanation. There's a high emphasis on participation. However, when participating, prepare to NEVER BE RIGHT. As all mighty Ken is always "right". CONDESCENDING+CONCEITED+EGOTISTICAL.

FOR BUSN 1101. Class is very lecture-heavy, and it's not required, prob best to avoid based off that. Lot's of long homework where the only way you get an A is to go "above and beyond." Group projects can be fun, and take up 5-10hrs/wk. If you came in with AP credits, take it, if not, take a required course.

You will not learn anything from the professor. I  do not feel that I learned anything besides the bike simulation, which any professor could assign. Blames lack of teaching as a different approach.I thoroughly enjoy the concept of marketing but after Professor Parker, I feel less motivated.

Very tough grader. Felt like I didn't learn much, exams were insane as questions had 7 vague multiple choice answers and were picked from random places in the book. Your pretty much guaranteed to finish in between an 80-90 no matter what you do.

Most reviews on Professor Parker are extreme. Yes he is a harsh grader, and the study guide does not relate to the exams. Class is like a business and he expects you to work hard and give each assignment your all if you participate and go to office hours you will do fine if you are looking for an easy class this is not the class you should take.

BUSN1101 at Northeastern. Ken Parker has no rubric for assignments and is a harsh grader for no reason. Thinks his class is more than it is. You can write an essay on a small prompt homework and get an 85 for no reason. Wild asl.

do not take under any circumstances, I had to take him because the class is my requirements. Doesn't teach, we have class 4 days a week and he teaches two days and let us do projects for the other two days, grades obscurely without explanation. crazy how northeastern is still letting him teach
He does nothing but talk about himself the whole class. He's also very creepy. You can get a good grade in the class if you flatter him but thats about it

Does not teach. Haven't learned a single thing from the text book. Thinks he is teaching real life material but is simply just rambling. No students are prepared for the quizzes/exams. Should not be a teacher.
Very tough grader, and claims a B is a good grade. In class, assignments can actually be fun but HW isn't clear, and professor expects unreasonable amounts of effort outside of class.

Grades based off of no clear criteria and will pit you against ur classmates in the group project for participation points. Introverted people are at a heavy disadvantage in this class. Teaches based on field experience which is never relevant to class topics and tests heavily based on book. Believes powerpoints are the pinnacle of learning. 0/5

Huge ego and he thinks he knows all but does not. Did not teach course content for more than one hour the whole semester. Expects you to know it for the exams that the department makes. Takes a long time to grade. My grade in this class is not bad, but this class caused me so much stress with the group projects that he assigns the groups.

Does not grade anything so you don't know your grade until the end. Do so many assignments and he gives unfair grades. Doesn't teach in class and just talks but attendance in mandatory. Barely accessible outside of class because he is so busy. Exams are hard and have tricky questions. I would not take again.
It is so difficult to get an A in this class. Even if you put a lot of effort into the assignments, it isn't enough.

I literally wish I changed marketing classes if I knew I would be in this class. He spends most of his time talking about himself and doesn't teach marketing 80% of the time. I mostly taught myself for the exams and even then he is the harshest grader. You won't ever know what he wants. I would not take again, the most frustrating class ever.

Loves to tell stories, so be prepared to hear about his background experience. He has a lot of experience within the field which is cool, but lectures can be boring. Emphasizes participation and group work, and exams a pretty easy because he gives you cheat sheets. Go to class because he gives free test questions.

The professor begins the class by saying, "grades don't matter." He mostly talks about his experience rather than sticking to the syllabus. As a result, his course only covers a minuscule amount of textbook material. Furthermore, the assignments lack specific instructions. Finally, he just responds "ok..." to all student comments.
Kate's teaching quality is very high. The course difficulty is appropriate, and she explains details very clearly. I learned a lot from this class.

professor ashley is really nice and attentive. she provides lots of outside of class feedback and support and very easy to talk to so i do like her lectures. the material itself though is very challenging— lots of work, 4 heavy projects throughout the semester. there are 2 exams which we can bring notes in so should be easy if you actually study.

Prof. Ashley is very by the book, rubric grading, but took FOREVER to get grades back to us. The amount spent on the class as a whole outside of going to class was very minimal, but if you don't pay attention you end up having to teach yourself everything. The final project is with a real company and gives real experience. Hardest content is R code
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Honestly my favorite class. Her lectures make material easy to understand and always has good explanations to tricky concepts. She has worksheets for each chapter that you work on as a class. Workload is super manageable with easy weekly homework. If you attend lectures you won't need to read the textbook. Tests are easy and the class is chill.
It was the best class at northeastern that I have ever taken. Exams are very easy if you study well. Gives good feedback, replies to email in seconds. Perfect professor at NEU.

Good overall, but the lectures can be kind of confusing. Shes caring and understanding, easy to reach. Although attendance is not mandatory, if you don't go to class youll get pretty lost. Organized and clear instructions/grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Never responded to my email and was late to her office hours. She can be nice, but can also be a bit passive-aggressive at times

You can tell by her lectures that she took time and effort in preparing, in hopes of making the material easy to understand. Though attendance is not mandatory, going to class, paying attention, completing the required homework, and putting in time to understand the material will help you succeed in the course.

I think she's a good professor, but she teaches like we are in middle school and it gets very frustrating. It is hard to focus in her class because she tends to describe things in a way that is way more complex than needed. She is really nice and caring outside of class and answers emails fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great professor, grades super fairly
Dr. M is kind, funny, and most importantly, knows her stuff.  I have learned so much, and I really appreciate her commitment!
My love for bio was extinguished after taking her class and I blame her.
Terrible experience. I dreaded going to class. Depressed every day.

Dr. M is the best! She's so kind and funny, and I thought she did a great job leading this course. I don't know how I would've felt if I had taken this a year ago, but I thought she was a great fit for me.
Dr. M is the best professor at Northeastern! She's like the fairy godmother of science: caring, kind, and always there for you. Don't believe the haters.
Dr. M is the best professor at Northeastern. Amazing 11/10.

I absolutely loved Dr. M for Bio Project Lab, she was incredibly helpful and clearly wanted everyone to do as well as possible! Sometimes the classes did drag on, but I blame that on the length of the classes and not necessarily her.
Did not learn much
Did not give consistent grading feedback across projects, graded either extra hard or extra easy depending on the assignment
Arbitrary grading scheme and feedback

Dr. M is an amazing professor! She is so sweet and caring for her students. She is so helpful and always available outside of class. If you put in the work the class is fairly easy. I highly recommend Dr. M

Prof. Lustilla is the best professor I've ever had. Both discussions and coursework are extremely thought-provoking and interesting! Discussions are very open and unbiased, and he takes the time to thoughtfully work through everyone's considerations. He honestly just cares about his students and wants them to learn and succeed. Take this class!

He's genuinely one of the best professors I've ever had-- super laid-back, his priority is having students actually engage with the material. He also very clearly cares and wants to get to know his class. If you haven't had a chance, definitely take a class with prof Lustila!

Professor Lustila is super kind, caring, chill, and funny. He's very passionate about philosophy and extremely knowledgable in regards to the material being taught. Class time is just a discussion based off of the readings that are really interesting and genuinely get you thinking.

My favorite human and my favorite professor at Northeastern. Every time I went to class I learned something new about life and about myself. He is brilliant and kind and everything desirable in a philosophy professor. I am so thankful I was able to learn from him.

If you want a class where the prof is extremely passionate and knowledgable about the material, this is the one for you. I took Indigenous Philosophy during the first semester it was offered, and it was my favorite class by far. Getty is hilarious, his readings are very accessible, and he totally opened my mind to all sorts of new ways of thinking.
Professor Lustila gives great feedback and I would strongly recommend taking his class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This is my first time leaving a review, but I love Professor Lustila so much I had to add on to the praise. He gives excellent feedback, is funny and passionate about the material, and a genuinely kind person who cares about his students. Missing class is hard because he doesn't use slides, but I never missed a class because I enjoyed it so much!

Fantastic class! Fantastic professor! Gives very constructive + helpful feedback on essays, and lots of it, but your grade never suffers for it. Genuinely wanted to improve my essays to get a compliment from him. I love him he was great! Funny too. Lectures went beyond just the material and wanted you to have a better scope on happiness and life.

Getty was a great professor. His lectures were very interesting and I found myself always wanting to participate in his discussions. 
However, he changed his curriculum from self-reflection to essay-based, and he does not go easy on you. He grades as if it was an English class so if you're not a strong writer don't take his class.

Getty is one of the best professors at Northeastern. He cares about his students and is always willing to help them with anything.  He intentionally sets up his course so that he can grade your philosophical writing hard, to make you improve, but still let you get a really good grade through other assignments. Overall 10/10 person and professor
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: The best class I've ever taken with the best professor. He is so kind and understanding and wants his students to succeed and learn. Going to his classes were always the highlight of my week.

Getty was so so good as a professor. His classes were very engaging and he made sure to address any and all inputs from students. I definitely grew as a person while in his class this semester. Take it if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Getty is incredible. He is by far the best professor I have EVER had. His lectures are truly amazing and enlightening. I always looked forward to his classes, and will definitely be taking a class taught by him again. He truly cares about his students and is so passionate about philosophy, and it shows. Take his classes. He is truly amazing.
Getty is him. What a guy. Every class is like a super laid-back therapy session. I have loathed almost every professor I have had at Northeastern but Getty is the man.

Dr. Lustila is super considerate of students' mental health/ general well-being. Lectures and group discussions are interesting. Discussion posts due every Friday. Assignments given often encourage self-reflection and progress. Gives amazing advice and feedback. 10/10 professor.

He makes a super dry philosophy class interesting and relevant to our lives. Allowed for discussion and personal anecdotes to contextualize hard topics, overall made a boring subject incredibly interesting. I felt like a better person after this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Best teacher I've ever had, and the only one that had me dreading the end of class. Every lecture is a deep, engaging discussion. He is an effective speaker, and his style is like throwing down big boulders of well-constructed meaning in 3-5 second intervals, giving everyone time to reflect on what is said. I found this extremely engaging.
Professor Lustila is an incredibly dynamic and interesting professor. Sometimes the lectures can drag, but that was mainly due to the time slot being too long. I would definitely take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Solid lecturer and great guy. I'm not sure that I agree with his grading scale as I think it basically ranges from 85-95, but it makes for a forgiving grade if your papers are mediocre.
Took 2330 with Getty, and just finished my third year in University, by far my favorite professor I've ever had. I've never left a rating here, but Getty deserves this, Love him.

Possibly my favorite human ever. Getty is so such an engaging lecturer and makes an hour 40 min class fly by. He is so funny. Not an overwhelming amount of work and pretty generous grader. If you ever have to take a philo class take it with him or you will be missing out!

Getty is the greatest professor of all time. Hands down. He is the most dynamic lecturer I've ever had a class with and he facilitates fantastic discussions about the material. He allows students to discuss complex, mature topics without trying to limit what we can say. He cares about students and is a fairly easy grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Absolutely amazing. Take a class with him or you'll be missing out on one of the best class experiences you may have. Super down to earth and accessible, a really genuine and smart person who you'll see as a friend rather than just a professor by the end of the class. The level of difficulty reflects that of the material; not too hard to get A

I came into the class with virtually no knowledge or background of academic philosophy or political theory at all. The readings were intimidating, daunting, and challenging, but Professor Lustila did a stellar job of making the content funny, understandable, and relatable to the human experience. He is also incredibly kind and understanding.

Getty is a great teacher. He's very good at talking about the philosophical material in ways that are understandable and even relatable, and the class meetings were entertaining. To his credit, it seems like he's always able to develop a good classroom community. In terms of work there's a lot of reading, which can suck, but that's how it goes.

Getty is a lad. Incredible lecturer who will change the way you think. Take him if you can. Also a very soft grader; just read the sparknotes before class and you're set. I was never overwhelmed with work in his class but still learned a ton. Can't ask for more than that
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If you have a chance to take a class with Prof. Lustila, I would jump at the chance. Whether you are phil or stem, he is great at facilitating discussions and helping everyone out with their understanding of the material or their papers. Funny and succinct analysis of wide ranging or confusing theory. Great guy, always willing to help outside class

Prof Lustila is the best I've had at Northeastern. As I am new to philosophy, Getty made the subject easier to understand and was eager to explain anything we didn't get the first time. He's accessible, and genuinely cares about not only the subject matter but every one of his students. He gives excellent feedback on essays and is a lovely person.

Getty is the best. He made this class so much fun while still teaching the material very well. He created an environment where I felt comfortable working through tough questions with my classmates, and his attitude was really conducive to a great learning environment. I can't stress enough how great this guy is.

This class was great! Professor Lustila was really nice and made ethics interesting to learn about. His goal was to get us to understand the content and philosophy and teach us how to write in it, and he never felt judgemental. Would definitely take this class again.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lots of reading and weekly homework. Boring lectures with occasionally interesting guest speakers. Easy class. Do your work on time and you'll get an A

he is the best of all the design lecturers. okay the lectures are long, and he does give lots of reading, but for the most part it is all fairly interesting, and you can def grasp the points by skimming through/showing up to class most of the time. overall he is very accomplished in the design world but still lighthearted and approachable. easy A.
The assignments don't reflect knowledge in the class and most of it is based on listening comprehension skills while projects can be fun very time consuming for so few credits
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: no 2 credit class should be this strict and its so lecture heavy

lee is great, and you can tell he is knowledgeable when it comes to design perspectives. attendence every class gets old after the first week, especially since the guest speakers werent always the most interesting/relevant. it is also just a very long block of time to sit in a lecture hall. a little too serious for a 2credit course, but not awful.

Another foundational design course similar to most of the foundation courses. The material feels redundant, especially the hw. The discussions are easy to write, but the seminar for this class is pretty boring and overwhelming with busy work. Another example of how CAMD needs major curriculum reformation. Would never take this class willingly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: guest speakers are irrelevant sometimes, but attendance is mandatory regardless of major and interest, which is fine but a bit annoying. Very repetitive assignments, gets tiring, lecture hall is the worst room in the building, such cramped inconvenient seating, and he keeps making people get up and move around and its really such a hassle
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: While lecture heavy Professor Lee still stands as my favorite professor. He's incredibly knowledgeable in his field and as a CEO of his own design studio he has given great advice about his practice. For someone who had never taken an art class before I found it was very easy as well as inspiring.

One of my favorite professors so far. Lectures were engaging and featured guest speakers with fresh povs. Studio felt like an open, collaborative space that made it easy to stay involved. Projects were flexible and allowed us to explore our interests, which made the work genuinely enjoyable.
Extremely complicated homework and very lecture heavy. I took this as an easy elective but the grading certainly doesn't satisfy the criteria of an easy elective.

Syllabus said the class was PASS/FAIL, but on the last day when grades were out turned out it was a letter grade class. She did not know that faculty hadn't made the class pass/fail yet and even upon clarifying in person said it was pass/fail and never bothered to check. Had to appeal the grade from C to A; the whole class trades were altered.
love her humor!

Class is boring but it's required. Weekly conversations with professors/board members/ students/etc with a reflection due. They're annoying but take like 10 mins. Anyone who says the instructions weren't clear can't read. I got a 100% on everything and I started them half and hour before they were due. Just show up and you'll be fine.

She was fine as she didn't teach the class. She refused to give you credit for the weekly assignment if you did not attend class, no matter how much notice you gave. She never made clear what the assignment requirements were which made it nearly impossible to get a full score.

Take a different prof for light work & easy A, take Larsen to learn. I worked hard but got a 97 plus 10 points of extra credit. Her lectures are only her speaking about what we read in the textbook which wasn't for me. probably wasted my time reading. the TA grades the papers & projects and she's too harsh. PARTICIPATE AND LISTEN IN LECTURE

Excellent professor with entertaining lectures. Encourages students to answer questions, and always provides additional classifications during class. Easy to access outside of class for lingering questions.
Get Larsen if you can you won't regret it!

She is an entertaining professor to listen to, gives a lot of opportunities for extra credit and we get to go to court and witness cases in place of class lectures at times. 
I cannot complain, she's great! She also gives you one or two chances to turn in a late assignment without asking or explaining.

Prof Larsen is awesome! She's super passionate about the CJ system explains her first hand experience as a Defense attorney (super cool). The quizzes are based on her lectures - if you skip class, you won't pass! There are a few extra credit opportunities which are amazing! (court view) and the final is a video presentation. 10/10 professor.

I have had professor Larsen twice now. She is a great professor, always willing to help and has very interesting and engaging lectures! The material is easy to understand when you participate, go to class, and do the homework and take notesssss!!! You definitely won't pass if you don't show up in class. I enjoyed this class overall.

She is a great professor I would 100% recommend. The class was very simple, just based off textbook chapters and in class lectures with unit quizzes and a semester long easy reflection writing assignment. Final project was an online project, very doable.

Professor Larsen may come off as funny to some, but to me she came off condescending and rude. This class was easy because it's a seminar, but she was not understanding of absence because of sickness and treated us like we were in high school.

Professor Larsen was overall a good teacher. Her lectures can be boring, but within them she covers important information that will be on her quizzes. If you follow the textbook you will do just fine, even if you don't attend lectures. There are about 5 quizzes, a couple writing assignments, and a final project. The work load is doable.
This class is all cases! Lots of reading and doing case studies. That being said, she goes over every single one during class and has a great amount of office hours.

There are definitely complicated concepts covered in this class, so it is important to go to class and participate. Do the readings, but you don't need to buy the textbook (it's over $200 and you can find the cases online). Wording on tests can be confusing. Not an easy class, but you can succeed if you do the work. Do the extra credit.

Professor Larsen is very educated in her field, and I enjoyed her lectures. It's important to read the case studies before class because she takes note of who participates. I recommend looking through her lecture slides before class as well. She offers many extra credit opportunities that aren't too difficult, which is a helpful grade booster.

Krista Larsen is a great professor and creates a lot of chances for extra credit and general redemption in her class. She's also very funny and her lectures are engaging. I would definitely take another class with her if I could.

All the people who gave her low scores obviously don't understand her humor. She's silly but fair. I've had her for three different classes and each time I got an A. The tests are hard, but if you do the extra credit you'll be fine. Become friends with her, go to office hours if you are struggling with tests, email her and she will help you.

Prof Larsen is very charismatic and really knows what she is talking about. I learned a lot in this course and found it very interesting, despite the quizzes and exams that were somewhat difficult. As long as you pay attention in class and study the cases well, you should be fine. A lot of extra credit opportunities as well. Definitely recommend.
Amazing in lectures and keeps your attention. Everything is in the book that you can find online for a free download, very kind.

Professor Larsen is a fun and solid professor overall. She makes class very fun and engages her students well. Larsen makes criminal justice fun in light of how heavy it can sometimes be - definitely would take again. Also - grading is very reasonable

She's a great lecturer and incredibly accessible during class. The quizzes are frequent and tough, but as long as you study the cases beforehand you should be fine. While she doesn't take attendance she will probably notice if you don't show up enough and will mark you down, especially because participation matters, so ask questions!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I took her Intro to Criminal Justice class asynchronously. The workload is manageable, and the course is well structured and organized. You just gotta make sure you stay on top of deadlines (which can be hard in an asynchronous class but the prof and TA issue reminders for major assignments). Plenty of extra credit options. Highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Larson is a good teacher, you could learn a lot from her. What I dislike about her is how unnecessarily rude she is to her students. She is an attorney and a professor so she has a lot of work, however, this doesn't excuse the multiple times she has been rude to me and other students for no reason.

I've seriously loved Prof Larsen. I really wish she taught more classes because I would take all of them. The material of this class can be a little convoluted at times but she does a really good job of explaining it. She will tell you over and over again that if she didn't say it in class, it's NOT the answer - and she means it!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Fantastic experience with Professor Larsen.  Wish there were more class offerings with her.  Would 100% retake her courses.
Amazing class and even better professor!

Prof. Larsen is honestly a great lecturer, but tests can be difficult if you don't know how to apply cases to the scenarios she makes up. If you pay attention in class it's doable. Her extra credit is great, four opportunities for 10 points in total added to your final grade - you can go from a B+ to an A+ if you get all the points.

Larsen's legal practice significantly interferes with class. She appears to have been completely unprepared for this class, even saying she was uncomfortable with the material. Larsen could be described as completely absent from the course for the first half of the semester. Grading & assignments are posted late; treats grad students like children.

I felt the grading of papers was extremely subjective, and unfortunately this accounts for like half of the grade. Tests are intentionally hard and questions poorly written with a lot of typos that can make the question confusing. Very inflexible with deadlines. Didnt wear a mask when in classroom w students. Overall bad experience do not rec
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The extra credit was a life saver. Cool teacher, many tests which were the only graded things, and makes up your final grade alongside extra credit. Tests are tricky but doable.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof Larsen is funny, easy to understand and relatable. She makes lectures interesting and is happy to answer questions (incl. about her own experience as a defence attorney). Lots of extra credit opportunities. The only thing I would criticise is that she references MA law a lot since she practices here - her knowledge of other states is limited.

One of my favorite professors at Northeastern. Her lectures are very helpful, and just listen and participate, and you will do well and learn a lot. She really is a funny and interesting person as well, I definitely recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Larsen is a good and clear lecturer so pay attention, take good notes and you should be fine. I found her tests to be a little difficult because she tries to trip you up, even though she claims otherwise. Be very careful of deadlines, she is extremely inflexible and not very understanding. She does give extra credit which is nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: One of the best at NEU. She's straightforward and tells you what you need to know for each case, so get ready to take notes. The tests are hard and she does try and trip you up. This is why you need to read carefully and pay attention to the class discussions and lectures. This class is no easy-A, but you'll definitely learn something.
love love love her. do yourself a favor and take her. she's amazing.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Awesome professor, makes you enjoy class. homework is basically just reading cases, and never very much. gives a decent amount of extra credit. If you're interested at all in law you'll love this class.

Prof. Larsen is an awesome teacher.  Take notes!!! And pay attention.  If you do both of those things you will do fine.  Test and quizzes are directly from the notes.  Reading is not really even necessary because she goes over every reading during class.  Awesome, funny and kind teacher.  Take her class!!!

Gives out tests throughout the semester; is very clear about exactly what needs to be studied in order to do well. Also gives a few brief writing assignments, but as she puts it herself, "Bozo the Clown could do it." Don't be lazy; come to class, pay attention and be a decent student and you'll learn a bunch. Krista is funny and kind and awesome.

Great professor. Is energetic, is great at teaching on the whim and answering questions. You must participate, must show up, and must have either read the textbook or know the topic from previous classes. Fair grader and more than enough extra credit to get through the class easily.

I thrive in classes where the teacher is interesting, lively, and helpful. This is that exact course! Probably gained the most knowledge in this class out of all my other ones this semester. If you read the cases, participate, and take good notes you will excel. Make sure you study for those exams though- they can be tricky and count significantly!

Professor Larsen is an amazing professor. She did a great job of constructing the course so that we could come prepared for discussion. She kept the material fresh, and never stodgy. Her tests/quizzes were fair and balanced. She gave students plenty of opportunities to improve their grade, and was available to students outside of class.
Very interesting. Doesnt take the size of cops often, since she is a defense attorney. Funny and laid back. Talks to fast and loud, but its worth it.

(due process) Interesting,and experienced in the field. Shes a practicing defense atty. theres 4 quizes, a mid term and a final. she also gives 4 opportunities for extra credit. if you study you can get an A.

Prof Larsen is the most entertaining professor i have had so far at school.  I was never bored during her class, she has plenty of funny stories (regarding law), and fields all questions no matter how ridiculous.  Offers tons of extra credit opportunities and with some effort you'll get an A.  Very cool lady, take her class.

Prof Larsen is the BEST professor at NEU. She is not easy but she is very good and you will learn a lot from her class. She will make you want to attend the class and honestly you will learn a lot from her. She is young and very easy going. Always willing to help. TAKE HER CLASS!!!!!!

Honestly my favorite class at Northeastern, she is the best for this subject because she is an attorney. Do the readings so you can participate, although she goes over it in class. 4 Quizzes, one dropped, 4 extra opportunities, one paper, midterm and cumalitive final. She's also very good with answering email immediately.

Nice professor, she's cool. Hard grader though, no leway really. Your either right, or you dead wrong. Class is pretty fun, just lectures everyday. Extra credit opportunities(papers) help raise your grade which is nice.
I did get an A-, BUUUUUUUUUUUT I can't say it was easy. If it were not for her extra credit options....Overall cool teacher. She's a young one. Real cool, I'd take the class
She was ok, rushed things in the end, of course had favorites...if you stay on top of your work you should be fine

Professor Larson is not like every other professor.  She talks and acts like a college student but she knows the material.She never takes attendance, but to do well on the quizzes you must attend to hear the lectures.She also gives you so many extra credit opportunities to raise your average. I may be the only one, but I thought she is a hot woman.

Good class if your interested in the subject covered. Lot of reading. She just spits out ridiculous amounts of words a class and you have write them down real fast. She's helpful but since she is an attorney everything must be EXACT or no dice

She started off as a good teacher, clear and good at making you understand the material, then she began speeding through the material things started to become unclear..seems like a professor has favorites...was difficult to deal with but overall I got an A in the class. So study and take notes and you should be fine.
She is awesome!  I really wish She was teaching another class so I could take it.  She is straight forward and doesn't mess around and has experience in what she is teaching. Easygoing.

Pros:
- Lots of time spent in Excel, great for experience
- LAMBDA knowledge definitely sets you apart from most users

Cons:
- Weekly assignments always a number 1 priority (shortest one took me 6 hours.)
- Instructions for these assignments are super unclear
- TA help isn't great
- Huge over-reliance on AI teaching
- First few weeks are brutal

Seriously avoid this class if possible. I took it because I needed a class with a lab for 5 credits and this was offered online. Serious mistake. The assignments are very hard and the professor essentially makes you use AI to learn everything and do the assignments. However, on exams, you cant use AI which makes them very difficult.

By far the worst professor I've had at Northeastern. As someone with ZERO excel experience/knowledge, I took this course thinking it would be an introductory course (1000 course). But, this professor has decided to overcomplicate topics as if we're all experts at CS, designed hellish weekly assignments, and the TAs are unhelpful.
I'm an economics and business administration major, and this class was rough. Tests were hard, tons of homework, and honestly just not worth it if you're not a CS major. Wouldn't take it again.
Assignments took hours and hours and made absolutely zero sense. If you are an Econ major and need this for reqs take either of the other courses offered and save yourself.
If you're a CSSH major (or basically just not cs and don't have much experience w/ excel) avoid taking this class. All i can say is thank god there were makeup exams otherwise I would have failed
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I spent one week in this class before dropping it. I cried myself to sleep every single night. Most of the TA's are useless except the girl one. This class is impossible if you have no cs background. Holy hell. Zero guidance, no one teaches you anything, actual hell on earth. Run, run, run, run, run, run.

This class was hybrid. The online portion was very easy, I had an A. However, the lab portion of this class was stupidly difficult. It did not match the online portion and Dr. Lieberherr did not seem to understand that this was an intro level class for non-CS majors. Assignments took HOURS to complete, and he made us use ChatGPT to teach ourselves.

I took this class as a requirement for Criminal Justice, but dropped it without a W a few weeks in. Online lecture material is taught by a different professor and is barely (if at all) related to weekly assignments. Weekly assignments take multiple hours a day to complete, they are confusing and frustrating. I do not recommend this class at all.
Run. Whole class tested 33% average score on exam 1, we all almost failed. I am an elite academic and had never withdrawn from anything ever. I withdrew from this. Run, you will not pass. Run.

Genuinely the worst course I have ever taken. This professor does not teach, and he relies on ChatGPT, yet you can not use it on exams. The TAs grade everything! It is way too difficult for an intro class, and just horrible. Do not take.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Run.
honestly save yourself while you can.

This class is unnecessarily difficult for an intro class. Assignments take 12+ hours to complete, so it just feels like the work is never-ending. Weekly lab sessions are essential because it's the only time you can get in-person help. Go to TA office hours even if you feel like your question is insignificant because the grading can be harsh.

This class was basically Excel 101 which is super helpful for future jobs. Almost all of the learning is done outside of class on your own time. We met once a week and the professor would work through problems. The assignments can be tedious and long but are doable. The use of ChatGPT plus was required for the class.

Prof Lieberherr isn't uncaring towards his students, he is just so out of touch with them he doesn't realize how unrealistic his curriculum is. The hw & online modules were extremely outdated and clearly hadn't been revised in a while. Helpful when I would come to him for office hours, thats abt it. Unreasonably difficult class. Avoid if possible.

If you have to take this course I'm sorry, it is impossibly hard due to Prof Lieberherr. The online course material is confusing and hard to follow. The in-class labs are useless, when you ask a question he will pull up ChatGPT and tell you how it will answer it. Assignments and exams are hard to read and deceptive in what part is being graded

This professor does not teach anything, only advice I have for you is to make sure to show up to class because the one-hour labs per week is useful to completing SOME parts of the 4-5 hour long time-consuming assignments. Way too much content for an introductory course, and way less teaching. Now he has implemented chat-GPT teaching, good luck.

Weekly 1 hour class barely scratched the surface of what we actually needed to know for the assignments. Highly advise against taking this class unless it's a requirement, you will be countless hours working on the weekly project. There are 2 exams each worth 25% of the grade, so you have no choice but to learn it

Online lectures are useless. They tell you the basics of functions but you must figure out how to use them. Lab sessions are essential, as he goes through assignment for the week and teaches you how to do it. Quizzes easy grade boosters, but assignments are confusing, unclear, time consuming and hard. Prof is good but class is structured terribly.

Weekly 1 hour class barely scratched the surface of what we actually needed to know for the assignments. Highly advise against taking this class unless it's a requirement, you will be spending 1 day every week cooped up inside trying to complete the assignments. There are 2 exams each worth 25% of the grade, so you have no choice but to learn it

Great professor, I just hated the class. Just make sure you do well on the assignments because that really helps, but there's an assignment due every week which took up my whole weekend. There's two exams, I got a 65 on the first exam and still ended up with an A-. The 1st exam was harder than the 2nd.

As a Psych-CJ major taking this course as a requirement, I am so thankful to have had Professor Lieberherr. Though the online portion is less than ideal, Lieberherr makes up for it by giving useful and easily digested information during class. I never thought I'd be able to understand CS in any capacity, but Lieberherr has changed my mind.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I went to Professor Karl's office hour twice. He is extremely willing to help, so if you get stuck by the homework, don't hesitate to ask him.
Class is based mainly on weekly homeworks and one project
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very good

One of my favorite professors at Northeastern. I had him for CS1100, and while my friends who had other professors were complaining, I had nothing to add. The class itself is mostly self-driven, so if you do not like that its an issue with the course, not the professor. Karl was available outside class time to help and is really such a kind person!

All learning is watching online lectures, then review is done in class. Watch them and take notes before class. Be prepared to be called on! Only 1 65 min class a week, but 10+ hours spent outside of class. Prof is quiet unless asked a question, but is very knowledgable and a sweet man if you talk to him! Hated class at first, ended up loving it!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: for the required class for a lot of different majors, prof lieberheerr was good. if something was unclear, he would explain it and make sure you understood. but the class itself required a lot of work outside of the meeting time, but thats with any professor you choose to take it with.

This was the most work i've ever had for a course so far and it was only an 1000 level course. The professor doesn't explain anything. You are expected to teach yourself all the material by watching the online lectures before attending class. Class is once a week. Not nearly enough time to understand any of the material. Don't take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I took the class twice... the first time was during my very first quarter of graduate school and I dropped it after 2 weeks because it was hopelessly more complex than I could understand.   I took it again during my last quarter and was AMAZED at the depth of information Karl presented.  He remains one of my two best graduate school professors.

The man is an enigma. He has you learn algorithms through the use of a "game", which in theory is a very novel approach. In practice, it's sometimes a little silly (especially when you play by yourself). In class, he's fairly clear and is very receptive to people asking questions. Exams are open note.
The Game is useless and even if you ask exactly what you need to turn in for homeworks, you'll still be missing something. Avoid if possible, but you probably won't be able to.

This class is like starving to death. At first, you try and try to make it through the next day, but eventually, your stomach distends, and you hallucinate the laughing specter of Karl, mocking you, as you cannot even muster up the strength to swat the flies off of your face. You, and your GPA die, just as you came into this world, alone.

The course was Secure Software Engineering. Or, at least, that's what it was called. It turned out to be a course in AspectJ and Demeter - really just him providing a forum for his students' research projects and trying to push AOP on everybody. I am told this is what ALL his classes are.

Brilliant man, wonderful professor. My only complaint is with the course itself, not with Lieberherr. The project was one that he had never done before, and didn't even know how to do himself. Talking to others who took his class, I guess this happens a lot. Surprised to find no Lieb bashers here!

Work hard and you'll learn LOTS. Lieb. is a zen sort of guy, but if you get him talking you immediately pick up on his vast ability and experience. He is willing to help any time, but can be confusing at times. Great class. Do y'self a favor and take it.

Prof Mike is incredibly sweet, yet unfortunately he's unable to stay on topic during classes and is the stereotypical "chalkboard professor" to a fault. He gives no study guides or practice exams, and the tests are nothing like the homework and almost always like the work done in class, so if you actively participate, you'll be golden.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a chiller, kind and sweet guy.

He is Very nice person, he is doing well in preparing all the materials in the class, but he does NOT know how to teach you the train of thought.He will just write down the answer on the assignment and barely explain it at all. There is a gap between teaching and homework

I took the Summer 1 asynchronous online course for ACCT 1201. I have attended his online office hours. He tries his best to help the students, despite the challenges of the online course. He supports the students through constant reminders and office hours. If you make an effort to try in the course, he will reciprocate.

Prof Mike is great! The way the class is structured you only need to do homework once a week pretty much. The content can be confusing, but he does a decent job at explaining each topic well, as well as talking through problems. Make sure you participate!! He is a fair grader, and if you stay decently on top of your work you will do really well

A genuinely sweet, caring, kind professor. He loves when you participate, even when you aren't correct. Sit near the front/participate often and he will know your name. Sometimes a bit monotone/boring, but I'd take him again. Try and read textbook before class and following lecture will be easy. Lots of practice problems done in class to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very nice but does NOT know how to teach. Really boring lectures and does not offer support to students. Really tedious in class work and he will cold call you. Attendance is mandatory and do not sit in the front row he spits

Prof Ruff is a great person and for his age taught the class well, however if you don't pay attention for a little bit you can fall behind easy. His handwriting is unreadable at times which is inconvenient because he spends most of the lecture writing on the chalkboard. Even with all that said, I would still say take his class. Great guy!

Professor Ruff is a great guy and cares about his students. The quizzes are online, multiple choice, and open note. He does not curve individual assignments, but is a generous grader and curves final course grades. He is not the best lecturer, but appreciates you going into office hours which helps.

Not the best at lecturing or explaining the actual concepts, but very caring and understanding. He gives a lot of extra credit if you participate and practice resources, but most of the actual learning has to be done but the student. Really nice guy but he should probably retire.

Very kind and funny professor, made class very entertaining and manageable. Only two major tests the entire semester so you truly have to study hard on those because they make up 50% of the final grade. Overall very good professor and very generous grader. My advice would be to do the optional hw assignments, extra credit, and use his office hours.

Overall, Michael Ruff is a very kind person who will work with you. He is very knowledgeable in accounting but is not great at teaching concepts. He tends to speak over his students and doesn't answer specific questions. If you are not great with conceptual learning I would recommend a different teacher.

Ruff is very caring and teaches the material well. Gives a lot of chances for extra credit. Only issue is the group project (10% of grade), but he also is very receptive with issues with groups. The course is very test heavy, with about 50% of your grading riding on 2 tests (20% midterm 30% final). Grading is pretty favorable too.
Stay away, there are better ACCT profs. than him
he is so funny and teaches so good
Fair grader. Enough extra credit opportunities. Submit hw and quizzes on time.

I absolutely love this professor. I really struggled with the course, but he was always available whenever I wanted to meet to discuss my quizzes/homework support. He also offered extra assignments that definitely helped me understand the material on top of the lectures/problems in class. There is a group project that will require excel knowledge!

Mike is the best! He is the sweetest professor and truly cares about all of his students, even the ones that don't participate. His lectures are very basic and easy to follow so you don't ever feel lost, and he's always willing to meet outside of class, even just to chat! He's easily my favorite professor at Northeastern, highly recommend.

Had this guy for asynchronous classes and can confidently say after seeing about 4 videos of him, I already love him. He is the sweetest, kindest guy, the kind of guy you could never be mad at. Already love him. He talks slowly, but I can put the video in 2x speed so it's easier to get through.10/10 Would rate again.

Prof Ruff tells you exactly what you need to do to succeed. If you do the homework, study for quizzes and exams, and ask questions when necessary, you'll do fine. If you put in some extra work, you'll do great. He is nice, and is understanding if you have any problems. I find his lectures to be very helpful. Do the optional assignments!

As many have said, Accounting in itself is not an easy one. But Prof Ruff helps you understand the material. Do your homework, LearnSmart, etc and you will pass. Going through end of chapter exercises will really help in quizzes/exams. Engage as much as you can during class! Prof Ruff is someone who genuinely cares for those who are keen to learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: His class was by far my favorite and he goes out of his way to make it more interesting. He also wants you to do well and if youve put in the work hell try his best to get you a high grade.

Professor Ruff was funny and easy to understand which made Accounting a lot more interesting than I thought it could be. He says that students are expected to read the textbook and spend many hours outside of class. I didn't do this and ended up doing fine. The final is very hard so get ready for that.

Professor Ruff is very caring to his students and has a lot of experience in his field. During class he does practice problems but its up to the student to teach yourself the material. The homework takes a long time to complete, and exams and quizzes are quite difficult, but Professor Ruff is pretty flexible.

This was the hardest class I took last semester, and the exams are difficult. The online class heavily relied on the textbook which was confusing at some points if you have not taken a previous accounting class. However, Professor Ruff is extremely caring, does a great job answering any questions you may have, and is flexible.

This class was SO HARD, but Professor Ruff is truly the most caring Professor ever. Hell do anything to help you understand concepts-he even stayed on an office hour zoom call with my friend for 2 hours to make sure she understood the concept. He replies to emails super fast, and is super understanding about the pandemic and your other classes.

I took this course during the pandemic online. Professor Mike is a really nice professor, though his course is extremely tough! You need to read every chapter carefully, so you are able to do homework and quizzes. There are few assignments that are optional, but they are good practice for exams. Exams are tough, so better prepare for it!
Ruff makes the intro classes very fun and I would recommend him for any accounting classes. He acknowledges the comical nuance with a lot of the material and manages to make it interesting

Most students in my class did ok. However, I usually studied acct1201 with angry tears in my eyes. The midterm and final were SO hard. I realized that I wouldn't receive an A grade right after the midterm. The quizzes were 10 MCs so be prepared. Email him, talk to him, he's very inspirational and thoughtful. Put in some work and you will be fine.

One of the most caring professors I've ever had. He sees the best in every student, and seriously wants us all to understand the material & do well. Accounting will never be the easiest or most fun class to attend, and he teaches the tough classes which I don't think he gets enough credit for. He will go to the ends of the earth for u to succeed.

Professor Ruff is a solid choice. His class can be difficult at times, but his lectures are very easy to follow and you can tell he really wants everyone to learn. He always mentions when he is accessible outside of class and gives clear expectations about what is due. The homework assignments are an easy 100 and he gives lots of time to do them.

Prof. Ruff really cares. He is super responsive to emails and is available when you need him. He curves with the attendance/participation grade, so be sure to participate and ask questions. He wants you to do well & visit the TAs too! Weekly homework, 4 quizzes each 5MCQ, 1 test &the final, and the project are grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Caring--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Respectfully, Professor Ruff is one of my least favorite professors I've had at NU. He isn't very kind, doesn't do a good job of helping students understand the material, and the tests are extremely difficult. Overall not a nice guy. Can be rude to students.

Professor Ruff is a great guy and a fair teacher. He has a lot of experience in accounting and gives good lecture; however, the majority of your learning will need to be done outside of class through the very long homework assignments or TAs. The quizzes and exams are tough and require a lot of studying and the online textbook is not the best.

One of the nicest professors I've had. Gives a lot of practice problems which are super helpful in understanding the material. 4 quizzes, each are 10 mc's, which can pull down your grade. Always holds office hours and makes sure he can reach students if needed. I wasn't the best at accounting but still loved him as a professor

Literally the nicest professor I've had here at NEU. Definitely cares a lot about his students and how well they're doing in class. All this being said, he isn't the BEST at explaining content, but the class is primarily reading the chapters so mostly independent studying. Participate in class and he'll help you out on your final grade!

Prof Ruff is a super nice teacher and really cares about his students. He is very reasonable and listens to his students. The homework can sometimes take a few hours and make you want to tear your hair out. His tests are pretty tricky and his lectures are very boring. My best advice is to practice the problems he gives you in class over and over!

Prof Ruff is definitely one of the kindest and most caring professors I've ever had. His lectures aren't the most engaging and can sometimes be confusing, but when asked questions he takes time to make sure you fully understand the material. The class itself is difficult but he's a fair grader and tries to give you as many points as possible

Ruff is your typical dad accountant making stupid jokes that he himself laughs at for a good minute. He teaches by doing many examples on the board (sometimes theyre hard to keep up with) and giving powerpoints. Tests are reasonable, doesn't curve though. Also, homework is long and tedious on an online textbook but is great practice for tests/quiz.
Prof. Ruff is a very kind person and really cares about his students,  attendance is mandatory. He is willing to explain any of your confusion... about the course. Highly recommend.

Accounting is not easy in itself. Ruff's class seems engaging and requires a lot of attention. Professor tries to be as clear as possible while explaining the material. However, his quizzes and exams are hard! He is a tough grader. You will need to participate as much as you can and attend his office hours to get some extra points in order to pass!

As has been said Ruff is a cool guy, but that doesn't excuse what was/wasn't taught in his class. His lectures are clearly stock from McGraw Hill and he does a poor job of conveying the messages and, by extension, he does a poor job teaching the material. I learned 99% of my accounting this semester from Youtube despite paying to learn in class...

This is accounting and it's not easy. Ruff is a great guy like everyone says, but his exams and quizzes hard. The book is useless for learning and connect can only help you so much. He gives projects and hw (unlimited tries) to balance grades. The final exam is extremely hard, be prepared to study a lot. You can survive the class, wont be easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor ruff, is overall a great guy. However, he is a really tuff grader. His exams, while mainly multiple choice are extremely hard. I attended every class, went to office hours and saw the TA and still struggled.  He over complicates the subjects and is heavily power point based.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He's one of the nicest professors I've had, but he's very strict when it comes to participation and attendance. He cares about his students and wants you to do well. Accounting is a tough class but he makes it bearable.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Prof Ruff is a great dude, not the best teacher by any means, but a great guy. If you haven't taken accounting beforehand or you don't learn by reading the textbook outside of class, this class - despite Ruff being a good guy - is a hard pass. He's a tough grader for how little he actually conveys in class and the homeworks aren't even relevant.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He is funny and caring to his students. You can tell that he tries hard to make people love accounting. This class requires you to read the book before class as he tends to go through stuffs quickly. He cares about his students a lot so he will try really hard to help you to understand the knowledge so make sure you reach out to him via email.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: While Ruff is hilarious and a good guy on the whole, if you haven't taken accounting prior to this class, then this class isn't worth your time. He's a tough grader, he gives no partial credit (on multiple choice questions that require a ton of work), and on the whole he's just not the best at conveying the material to the class.  Great guy though.

Tbh he overcomplicates a lot of the math, i ended up teaching material to myself cause he is not great at actually explaining the concepts. If you stick to the textbook and study a LOT for the exams you'll be fine. His class averages a B+ and he curves, which is nice. Just sad I didn't learn much from him.
Honestly, he goes pretty fast. I think he teacher directly out of the textbook. He's chill and understanding, his midterm and final were hard but at least takehomes.
Best professor I've had so far. He wants his students to succeed. Very knowledgeable and engaging and always available for support. Hilarious!!
The best professor for FINA2201!! He is hilarious and genuinely cares for his students and wants everyone to succeed.

The curve is insane and you're guaranteed a B if you submit everything, however, that's because its really difficult to do well on each exam and they're the only thing that really matters. overall most people felt really positive about him and a B+ or A- is totally attainable.
Always accesible to help students. Truly wants the students to learn.
He wants his students to succeed in his class and is very passionate about Finance. If you ever need help or have questions he will make time for you to ensure you understand the content.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great person who truly cares about his student's succeeding. He is always more than happy to answer any questions students may have, and thoroughly goes through problems in class. Professor Mukherjee also makes an effort to help students outside of class time and is always willing to work with you. Highly recommend!

Professor Mukherjee is one of the nicest people I have ever met. He is super caring, and truly wants his students to succeed. He is very knowledgable of the finance industry and would 100% recommend any class that he is teaching.
This guy is the literal GOAT. My favorite professor at Northeastern. Super nice, super knowledgeable about finance industry, overall just a great guy. Could not recommend him more.

AMAZING professor. Explains things as much as you want him to. Homework gives a good idea of what tests will look like and has multiple attempts most of the time. Lectures are easy to keep up with and he simplifies things to make you understand. Always available for questions & clarification. Wants you to learn. 100% recommend taking him.
Sick guy, very clear and makes class great. Easy tests and homework.
Nice guy, assumes kids are smarter then they are though. Will happy answer any questions you have during lecture, but it takes a couple tries to get him to really break down the issue you're having.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's a very good teacher. Super understanding and caring. Tests are not that hard, but they are proctored.

definitely avoid at all costs
GOAL IS TO FAIL STUDENTS
NEVER WANTS TO HELP OUTSIDE OF CLASS...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you want to do good in this class you can't skip lectures. This is difficult because this class is so so so boring especially at 8am. The tests are way too hard and actually made me not enjoy one of my favorite subjects. If the professor likes you you won.

I love biology, but her class truly made me question and slowly chipped away at my love for the subject. She goes extremely quickly in class, and you'll likely have to teach yourselves a lot. She records her lectures, which are convenient for studying, because anything she says could show up on the exam. Exams are SPECIFIC, so expect to study!

She can be super intense, especially on test days. Her class truly is difficult, but she will do her best to help if you go to class and show interest/effort. She was very caring and understanding with me, but important not to get on her bad side... GO TO LECTURE!! Impossible to do well if you don't

RUN. Take ANY other professor for Bio 1 or 2. Potts makes this course unnecessarily hard—harder than upper-level classes. Expects unrealistic memorization. I excelled in other rigorous courses but couldn't do well here despite intense studying and a tutor. Exam questions are purposely confusing. She offers poll extra credit but it barely helps.

Her class is manageable if it's the only difficult class you have. It's not a heavy work load besides periodic quizzes but the material/slides are messy and poorly organized for students to learn properly. She moves quickly and unclear while expecting you to memorize previous lecture details. The class is hard but she makes it harder.

Her class is hard + exams are memorization heavy, but so are most bio classes. She gives a HUGEEE amount of extra credit which boosted my grade so much. She is super passionate and knowledgeable, and I really liked her enthusiasm + flexibility. People enjoy hating on her as a way of coping about their grades, but i think her class is very doable.

if you can't memorize she isn't the prof for you. tests are SUPER specific, like she will add content she talked about one like for like 30 seconds. sometimes she can be super sweet and at others times super cold. clearly very passionate about what she does, but also expects everyone to come to class with that same mindset.

Tests are memorization-heavy and VERY specific. You can only do well if you memorize ALL the slides word for word. Lectures are recorded and she gives a lot of extra credit opportunities if you attend in-person. Lectures are a little boring and not engaging. If you're good at memorizing, her class might be ok, if not, not the best class to take.
dont take.

Honestly I think some people are a bit dramatic about Potts-Santone. I think a lot of the animosity people have towards her is just because the class is really hard. She is a lovely human and cares about you succeeding, and you get extra points for going to class. It is entirely memorization but so are most bio classes so I think it was reasonable.

Sometimes puts extra credit hints in the syllabus so make sure to read it. Also gives out extra credit to people who come to class when not many people show up. Is a fine professor, just don't get on her bad side and don't crack your knuckles during tests or she WILL crash out

Her lectures were not very interesting, and she doesn't take attendance however if you choose to skip you will most likely fail the tests. If you don't go to class, it's very important to watch the recording of the lecture to catch up as the tests are based majorly off the content discussed in class. She offers lots of extra credit opportunities.
Way too much content to know and exams are pretty detailed. Offered corrections for 2/3 exams. Offered extra credit points in class here and there. Would avoid.

read off of the slides during lecture and didn't explain the information at all. reading the textbook 1000 times is more helpful than going to her lectures/office hours. 3 exams and one final but only offers corrections for the first two exams. the material seems easy but her exams are difficult for no reason. i don't recommend taking potts-santone
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Genuinely the worst professor that I have ever had. I had read the reviews before taking her and assumed they were from students who did not try and expected a good grade, but that is not the case. It seems like she purposefully makes this class as hard as she can. Doing well on any test is a miracle. Avoid at all costs.

She has an attitude that made me not want to attend class. Her voice is irritating and all she does is word dump off of slides that are terribly put together. I feel like she made this course a lot harder than it needed to be. She isn't understanding and has yelled at people during exams(loudly) for having tech issues or cracking knuckles.

She does give a lot of information that you need to know for the exam, but she made that aware the first day. Just make sure to be going over your notes on a regular basis and you'll do fine on the exams. She allows for corrections on every exam and gives extra credit opportunities. Don't let these reviews scare you, I really enjoyed her class.

I took this class 2 years ago and am still scarred. Exams require you to know the content in extreme, unnecessary detail. The slides are very disorganized. The professor was unhelpful when I went for office hours.

She seems super sweet as a person-- I just could not learn from her. Daunting, wordy lectures that are impossible to understand. Only offered 8am so it was just bound to be bad. Tests are super heavy (+ stuff you never learned) but she will actually help answer questions mid-test. If you put in the effort, you will do well--it's just annoying.

I had her my freshman year and tried to avoid her at all costs. Fast forward and I also have her my senior year. Nothing's changed. She makes exams SUPER detail heavy and sometimes I have to wonder when she even mentioned certain things in lecture. I've taken all the upper stem classes and this class requires me to put in more work than those.
Take this class if you want to decipher the most disorganized, not descriptive, and JANK powerpoints. She is a lovely woman (such a sweetheart!) but her powerpoints suck.

I don't think I have ever met a more caring professor. I got extremely sick this semester, and Mary worked with me and helped me. She pushed back exams to when I could take them and was always available for Zoom office hours, even if it wasn't the normal time. People usually don't like her cause her tests are hard, but it's not if you study right.

The most material dense course I took my first semester at NU. Lots of memorization but she makes the material interesting sometimes which makes that a bit easier. She's serious about teaching, which is respectable, and she expects that to be reciprocated. I learned a lot, but it was not easy learning. She could've organized the material better.

Lecture-heavy but posts recordings so you can go back over it. She provides a study guide and if you just follow along with the study guide and review overall you will be fine. Exam questions were multiple-choice. Offers exam corrections for points back and small extra credit opportunities along the way. Sometimes a little rude.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Confusing and disorganized lectures. The instructor reads off of a script. An unrealistic amount of memorization is expected.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The worst professor I've ever had in all my years of being a student. Does not explain things in a clear way, but rather just spits out information we are expected to immediately absorb and understand. I don't feel as though I've been taught to understand concepts. She is throwing random facts and details at us and hoping they stick.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lecture-heavy with crazy slides (not pleasing to the eyes) as well as a script that she reads off of. This course does require a lot of memorization and requires a lot of time to study (expected to know every detail of a topic) and her office hours don't help too much. Avoid her, but if you can't, take notes of everything NOT on the slides!

Mary is extremely rude and demeaning to students. The icing on the cake was when she screamed "whoever cracks their knuckles next is getting thrown out of the room and receiving a zero" while we were taking our final exam in the dead silent lecture hall. Every question asked is returned with a sarcastic and humiliating response.Also awful teacher!!

Every lecture is a 60-minute info dump where the ONLY reason you don't fall asleep is because of how grating her voice is. It doesn't appear at first, but just wait until her rude and condescending side comes out. I couldn't be happier to be done with this professor and this class. I hope you have good memorization skills!

Not as bad as everyone says. She gives a ton of extra credit and automatically rounds your grade. Let me make up an exam when I had the flu as well. It is pretty memorization/lecture heavy but she records everything. Definitely not an easy A, however.

Ms. Potts-Santone reads from a script and expects you to memorizes every word of that script. Personally, I have nothing against her I am sure she is nice and whatever. However, her style of teaching is meant to take over your life. I was a top performer in this class and hated every second of it. Be prepared to spend all of your free time on this.

This class is just straight memorization. If you can memorize every vocab word spoken during lecture that's kind of the only way to do well in the class. She does offer exam corrections. Can be very condescending when a student asks a question. Class was basically an info dump, and very boring.

Potts-Santone's class is designed to be a weed-out class, and while I had to withdraw for personal reasons, it just wasn't a class I'd ever take.
