Classes are exam-based, with minute details on the exams. She was caring for sure outside of class, and actually does look out for her students. Buckle up, because the semester will be rough.

Every little detail matters to MSPS. Understanding concepts isn't good enough. She has designed this course as a weed-out, and it nearly worked for me. Stick with it, find a study buddy, and buckle up for a tough semester.

Prof. Potts-Santone has an unfair reputation, IMO. She is very knowledgeable and offers many opportunities to correct exams. You have to put in a considerable amount of effort to do well, but I'm walking away with what I think is a solid foundation in biology. Don't be intimidated: study hard, go to office hours and you'll see results.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Never take this professor. Avoid at all costs if you want to pass. Her voice is excruciatingly painful to listen to. The hour lecture is unbearable and time feels so slow.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Never experienced a professor who cares less about her students. Avoid at all costs.

I would not take class with this professor again. She does not seem to care about the success of her students. The test are extremely specific and volume of lecture material does not match the level of the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was the actual worst, i have never written a review on here before but had to after I took this class. AVOID if you can, you will be tested on the most minute details that have no meaning to your life or sometimes even the course. Save your mental health by not taking this class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Quite an intelligent woman who should be working in research. If you go to her office hours, this is the best way to seek assistance for her course. She is very hardcore and very thorough. Her lectures are not the most intriguing and you will be tested on minuscule details. (course completed in 2020)
Terrible teacher and voice.

Good professor with thorough lectures and many opportunities to earn extra credit. She is a nice professor and wants students to succeed, although her exams are highly specific and contain material she would mention briefly in class.

She is not as bad as people say she is. Just go to class, take good notes, and you will be fine. She records every lecture, allowed us to correct our exams for some credit back, and offered many extra credit points.

Incredibly tedious for an introductory course. Memorize every single bullet point. Says she wont test on numbers and then proceeds to ask for specific statistics -- misleading for the exams. Revels in the needless difficulty of her exams, then blames the students for being "poor test takers" and "not careful enough". Avoid if you can.

Worst professor I've taken in my entire academic career. Avoid at all costs. Incredibly rude to students in class & during office hours. I genuinely do not think she should be a professor. Not sure if she's unhappy with her profession, but it seems to take it out on her students. Will test on 1 sentence she said off-hand; Memorize entire lecture.

I took Mary Sue's class freshman year and am still amazed I passed. She is clearly incredibly knowledgeable but is often rude to students who have questions. Even when I went into her office hours for help, she was dismissive and I left more confused than when I went in. Avoid taking her class if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Potts-Santone is not as bad as some of the reviews on here say, her tests may be specific but if you pay attention in class and write down everything she says (and rewatch the lectures if necessary), you will be fine. The class consists of the final, 3 exams, and 4 quizzes, but she does give ample opportunities for extra credit.

Potts may be difficult, but she lets you know that exams are detail oriented and difficult. I would recommend taking notes directly on her slides, and writing down the important details she says. Her exams are on the content and what she says in lecture. To study, I rewatched the lectures on 2x speed and did active recall with friends. Good luck

The difficulty of her classes comes from the exams, which have very specific questions about the application of the material, however during her lectures she doesn't cover this type of application and doesn't prepare you for her own exams. Lecture slides are disorganized, prepare to self-teach
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Put in the hours and you'll most likely do fine. She gave lots of extra credit. Pay attention in the lectures and write down what she says, not what is on the slides. When you study, just review both. Do lots of active recall when studying, don't just read over your notes. Don't waste time with the textbook.

Despite lower ratings on this website, I enjoyed Professor Potts-Santone's lectures and class overall. Because exams are lecture-heavy, I suggest that you attend lectures on a regular basis and take good notes. She also gave multiple opportunities to earn extra credit and improve your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: While her class covered a lot of material in a very short amount of time, I feel that she was very clear about what you needed to know to do well. I definitely put more time into studying for her exams than I expected, but her study guides were very helpful. The content on the exams was the content she emphasized during class. Don't skip lecture!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: If you can try to avoid her, the exams are super specific. You have to take notes on literally everything that she says.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Her tests are insanely specific and her lectures go by so quick that it is hard to catch up. If you have Potts Santone, get ready for a crazy semester. Study more than you think you should. Each tests gets worse and the professor seems to not care.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Potts-Santone is an effective teacher and gives you opportunities to improve your grade. She does go through material very fast, so if you miss anything in class you should rewatch the lectures. It helps to have taken biology in high school.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This professor's exams are very specific to what we learned in class, but I think some of the reviews on here are exaggerating. She was an effective lecturer, I usually left class with a solid understanding of what we learned, and I translated this knowledge to exams (I do think that having prior knowledge of biology from AP Bio helped, though)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Surprised about these reviews on Prof. Potts-Santone. She makes exams very doable (given you listen in class) and even gave multiple opportunities for extra credit. People are dramatic, do not listen to the reviews on here.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: If you can avoid taking Potts-Santone, I would advise you to do so. Grade is essentially based on a few tests. She teaches at a fast pace-be ready to re-watch her lectures after class to make up for all the information you missed during lecture. When going to office hours, she was short with me and often unfriendly. Not sure why she chose to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Listen I know everyone dislikes her because she's really difficult but it IS possible to do well in the course, you just have to spend a lot of time studying. She is a personality most people dislike, but I get her teaching style. Rewatch lectures on 2x or download fast panopto to watch even faster. You don't need to go to class but WATCH lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Tests are very difficult and based on critical thinking. She's reluctant to help during office hours and seems unenthusiastic about teaching kids based on student interactions. I've heard dozens of complaints from fellow peers as well. Avoid if you can. If you have to take her, study hard and prepare to do worse than you think you will.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Super test heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This professor is the main reason I wish RMP allowed 0 as an option. Honestly, it's a disgrace that she's still teaching after so many students have had the same issues with her. The entire course is memorizing every word she says, tests contain random specific details from one lecture, and you'll have to rewatch lectures after every class. -100/10

This class is pure memorization. I did not learn a single thing, really unenjoyable experience. do not recommend. teacher is nice enough but the way she structures her class is absolutely horrid. she is aware of the unfavorable reviews on ratemyprof yet still remains delusional and does not take the very real criticism into account.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: If you have MSPS, buckle up and get ready for a crazy semester. Don't get me wrong- she is a smart woman. She is also very detail oriented, highly specific, and she has very high expectations. Tests are crazy hard- you need to actively study for at least 10 hours to get an A. She goes through lots of material in very little time, so pay attention!!

If I could give Potts-Santone a zero, I definitely would. She makes no effort to help her students whatsoever, and will get annoyed during office hours. Her tests are insanely specific and it is clear she takes pride in watching her students struggle. Averages for tests are almost always around a low C and are never curved. Study as much as you can
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: If you have to take Potts-Santone's class I am so so sorry. Every day is the same- listening to her read lecture slides to you and expecting you to copy down every word. If you don't type up an exact transcript of each class, it'll be hard to do well on tests. 3 midterms + a final, no homework- just study. Make sure to rewatch every Panopto video.

Tests are heavily lecture-based, each lecture is roughly exact 65 mins long and easy to get lost Tests are pure memorization, so if you are not a good memorizer, it's hard to get a high score on exams. The whole course's grade is merely based on 4 tests: 3 mid-terms (60%), and a final (23%). No curve! Even the average of the class is really low
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I would say that Gen Bio 2 material is a lot easier than Gen Bio 1 stuff, so you can expect the tests to be a little easier. If you want to do good on tests, I suggest you look at the recorded Panopto lectures + study guide she makes and take notes/make flashcards using them both. Lectures were boring for me. Take advantage of the extra credit.

Do not take this class unless you absolutely have to! It's a boring class with a dull teacher. Lots of memorization. The entire semester, it didn't really feel like I was actually learning anything, just memorizing stuff and answering questions for a grade. Seriously, stay far away from this class, and avoid having this professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: If I could give her a 0/5, I would. Horrible class. All you do is learn about random organisms, and spit out really really specific facts about them on her tests. She gives quizzes about random, kinda-related things, but nothing you learn on quizzes shows up on the test. If you don't do well on the tests, you're screwed because there's no curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Your grade is based on 3 exams and a final. No curve! Lectures are all basically her talking nonstop for 65 minutes. Tests are ridiculously specific. The whole class is basically a massive information dump, and you have to regurgitate specific facts on the tests. No application of the things you learn, just memorization. Boring class, waste of time
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Easily one of the worst classes and professors I've had in a long time. You can tell she loves what she does, but she makes no effort to make the class interesting for students. She doesn't teach in a cohesive way and you have to memorize everything. It's not actual learning, just cramming pointless information. Hated every minute of it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor expects you to memorize every little thing she says. Exhaust all other options before considering taking this class. She seems like a professor who takes pride in students scoring poorly on her tests. Lecture slides are disorganized, and lectures are mainly just her rambling on and on about specific zoology facts. No curve. Boring class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Terrible professor, tedious class! Her lectures are stale and her tests are too specific. She swears the exams aren't designed to be tricky, but the questions are so ambiguous, and most of the test content isn't even on the lecture slides. This is a dull zoology class with no curve. You're forced to memorize useless information. Avoid at all costs!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've had! Go to her office hours! She's extremely helpful and accommodating. Especially if you're registered with the DRC, she's the professor to have! She's very understanding and really cares. I wish all of my professors were like her!! Lectures cover a lot of material but she's enthusiastic and interesting to listen to

Dr. Potts-Santone is clearly knowledgeable. However, her lectures, are poorly organized for the high level of memorization and specificity she demands. The final grade is almost entirely based on 4 tests, including the final. Very little application of knowledge. She claims she wants everyone to do well but also doesn't curve a 46% average exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I dislike Professor Potts-Santone's lecture style. It can get boring and she expects you to memorize all the lecture material for the exams. The exam questions are often confusing and more focused on application. She does give extra credit, which is pretty decent. The textbook could be helpful if you need a supplement to the lectures.

I loved Dr. Potts-Santone. I practically lived in her office hours and asked question after question, but it paid off. When you put in the effort, she will reciprocate and help you a lot. NOT an easy class by any means, but that does not mean that she's a bad prof. As long as you do what she recommends and study ahead of time, you'll be fine.

I believe in my heart that she is not a bad person. That said, she has a tendency for self-importance and condescension. The structure of her class suggests that she values conformity and mindless memorization more than learning. Prepare for extremely dull lectures in which she regurgitates facts and leaves little room for thought or analysis.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Hated her, really doesn't help when you go to her because you are struggling. Makes it more hard on you to feel like you can pass. Doesn't give practice and exams are packed with applying and not definition. You learning from rewatching her recordings.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Potts-Santone is a very sweet and fun lady outside of class. Expect to work hard for exam grades! She is very clear about wanting test averages to be around 75. She is always available for office hours. Easy to skip class and watch the recorded lectures. Reading the textbook is not mandatory but will help a ton! Quizzes are all easy 100s.

Tests are very difficult and take HOURS of studying, purely memorization. Potts-Santone is so sweet and really cares about her students and their success, although this class was difficult for me and I barely got a C, I would definitely take again. She gives multiple quizzes based off of articles, so essentially opennote and online, per semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Great understanding of the material, awful conveyance. She obviously has an incredible grasp of what she's talking about, but lord alive she can't tell you anything with sense. No flow or logic, just kinda a whole period of fact after fact after fact. Super smart prof, but you'll need to memorize everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: While this was a diffcult class Dr. Potts Santone made it interesting with her excitement for the topic and interesting stories. You have to take notes and understand the lectures to even get a B in this class but overall I really enjoyed her teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Potts-Santone is one of those professors who take pride in how many students she can fail a semester. Combination of her inability to effectively teach, ridiculously stupid amount of information required to memorize and questions designed to trick you make it seem like she's trying to set you up to fail. She could not care less about you. Good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: It was my first time taking a biology class and she made me hate biology forever

Mary Sue was one of the best lecturers I've ever had. You could tell she really knew what she was talking about. Her tests were very straightforward and the content was fair. She also had a lot of extra credit opportunities.

This is a hard class but tegrity is a savior. She's a great teacher and is very knowledgable, you just have to put in the work and essentially memorize every word she says. tests can be tricky so start studying at least two weeks before the exam. you can't be lazy with this class
Lots and lots of memorizing
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Her tests are ridiculously hard. Don't take this class unless you have to, but she's the only professor who is teaching it.

I didn't even have her for a real class, I had her for my Intro to my major course, but I will actively try to avoid taking an actual course under her in the future. She advised us not to trust RateMyProf when choosing classes but I think it's because her score is so poor. She's disorganized and tried to be relatable, but it came off as annoying.
Lectures are heavy content, but at least she records them so that you can access later. You'll have to work to get a good grade in this class, but as long as you show some effort, it should be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Speaks too fast and goes off on a tangent too much. Can be unreasonable when it comes to grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Make sure to re-check the information she gives during lectures because some of them seem to be incorrect. Her tests are really hard so even if you think you know biology, you don't.

Dr. Potts-Santone is absolutely lovely! She is incredibly knowledgeable about her subject and her lectures are very in-depth.  Tests are difficult, but they are very fair, there's just a lot of information to remember.  What I really love about her class is how she applies the information she teaches to medicine and current scientific data.

She gives a LOT of information during lectures, but uploads everything to tegrity. Tests are very fair. You know exactly what to expect, though some questions are tricky. So long as you review the tegritys and memorize what you're supposed to, you should do fine. Overall really enjoyed her class, she's very knowledgeable & material is interesting.

She talks a mile a minute, and every second is relevant to the topic. She gives an outline of the topic on a powerpoint, but there is so much more she talks about, and it pops up on the exams. She doesn't care for memorization; rather, she cares for how you apply the concept. She uses Tegrity which is super helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Took this class even though I had AP Bio credit. It was way harder than expected but she is a really good lecturer. She never stutters and goes over everything in such detail. The way to pass is to memorize literally everything she says. Good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Hated this class, tests are super hard and questions are unclear. Don't take if you don't have to.

Dr. Potts-Santone was a good professor. She lectures intensely but puts up recordings on Tegrity.  She grades fairly but only 3 exams in the class(plus few other assignments). She isn't too approachable but emails quickly. Recommend the class if you like the material and are good at memorizing large chunks of information.

The tests are pretty tough and very detailed, there is a lot of memorization required. She gives you chances to get points during the semester with online quizzes and random bonus points for coming to class (usually 3 per exam). Posts recorded lectures online which is VERY helpful because she talks too much and too fast during lecture.
Had her for Human Parasitology - tests are super intense, lots of memorization, super detailed PPTs, but she's a nice professor
Avoid her at all costs. She actually made me hate Biology.

Go to class. She posts her lectures online but its helpful to go to class because 1. You can get the information twice that way and 2. she gives bonus points randomly throughout the semester. Her tests are very tricky even though she says she doesn't want to trick you, so study often. Her class is hard but worth it.

She isn't really that bad. She uploads a recording of all her lectures with the powerpoints online which is extremely helpful if you don't feel like going to class at 8am or if you want to brush up before a test. She also gives extra credit questions if you go to class. Her tests are fine if you actually study.

If you ever have to take her class, good luck guys cause you will need it. Her tests are really tough and you really have to know the topics really well because she is really specific with her questions. Her multiple choice questions are really hard and the answer is really hard to find because the answers are really close to each other.
Awful Presenter. Just goes through powerpoints with clusters of information which is organized terribly. A lot of random facts about parasites thrown in the middle.

I used to like Biology before I joined this class. While she is clear in her manner of speaking her tests so far have been hard. She posts all of the lectures on Tegrity. Class is very boring and monotone and she does not stop talking for 60 minutes straight. Her tests are not easy. You can't just know the material and pass the test. Dont take her.

She is a good teacher and can present a lot of information clearly. However, she presents a ton of info. I took Gen. Bio and it probably should have been MicroBio. She spends a lot of time on specific details that are not all necessarily on the test.

I really loved the class and she was great at teaching it but theres a ton of information to learn so you need to start studying early for the exams! Two tests, cumulative final, and some assignments throughout the semester. Book was helpful but not totally necessary and definitely go to class.

Great prof, crystal clear directions and assignments, gives extra credit! Has office hours, go to them! The tests really aren't so bad, you just HAVE to attend class (not everything is in the tegrity) and read the textbook! As long as one puts medium effort, you should do fine. The lectures are extremely helpful. She knew me by name out of 150 kids

Oh, Potts. Very likable as a professor but as far as teaching style, it's straight reading off of the (quite pretty) lecture slides. Her study guides give you a pretty good idea of what will be on the test, but bet your butt that she will throw in tangent things that you would not be able to locate in the lecture notes. Study hard, kids.

She's a nice person but she doesn't hand out grades for free. She requires you to know every specific detail she covered, and her tests are full of trick questions. That being said, she does give bonus points for showing up to class.

Honestly avoid Potts-Santone at all costs. She is a robot. She reads off the slides and thats that. Her tests are 60 multiple choice you have an hour and not a second longer. Good Luck! For some reason its not good enough to know the material you have to be able to figure out her trick questions and riddles too! This class is made to weed kids out.

Dr. Potts Santone was absolutely one of the best, most organized and inspiring teachers I've ever had.  It's clear she puts time and thought into preparing her lectures- she made the material  accessible and fascinating.  I looked forward to going to class!  Tests and scope of material covered are difficult but fair- be ready to study!

Talks really fast during lecture, but records and posts every class via tegrity. DO NOT RELY ON TEGRITY! She gives extra credit sometimes to those who show up to her class on a given day because so many people make the mistake of relying on tegrity and those are typically the ones who don't do well. Her tests are tough,  but shes very approachable.
Her tests are really vague. You are expected to know every tiny detail about everything. Be prepared to work

Prepare to have slides with barely any information on them and then writing 2 paragraphs worth of information and terms in lecture that she doesn't put anywhere (including the textbook). When material becomes a little more difficult for some reason she becomes extremely disorganized. Notes are unreliable and the textbook is unreliable. Good luck
Use Tegrity

She really knows her stuff and is really excited to share that material with you. At first, I was angry w/ all the material we had to learn for 1 exam, then I woke up and realized that I am in college = Hard Work.  Test questions are like riddles, wrapped in enigmas- they are not direct at all. That being said, really know all material. Use Tegrity

Hands down the worst teacher I have ever had. Her tests are not clear at all. She does not scale even when the test averages are are in the 60's. She doesnt care about her students at all and makes it seem like she likes to see you struggle. I would not recommend her to anyone.

Very clear and helpful professor. Posts all of her lectures on tegrity so it's not that harmful if you don't go to class. Tests are straight from lectures and some references to the textbook. Overall, challenging but fair class. You get a good grade if you put in effort

Keeps the lectures interesting. Sometimes she only has 8 AM classes, but she posts everything on Tegrity, unless it's not working. Tests are VERY wordy. 63 extremely wordy MCQs in less than an hour. Fair distribution of grades. 25% goes to lab (which is easy in BIO2)

She's a very smart, clear teacher who doesn't just give away As - her exams are tricky, you have to really understand the material.  That being said, if you do the work you'll do well.  I love her lectures.

I had her a few years ago (pharmacy), and looking back on old tests, they were terribly fair. Anyone who wants to succeed as a pharmacy major will get used to this type of test, or fail. It can be a bit of a transition, but She is a lot better than what's coming- she speaks clear English, uses a good textbook, and reviews everythi
Her tests are very wordy and hard to think through. You have to study. BUt her Tegrity classes online are useful. Overall she was okay.

Tests are very confusing and you have to figure out what the question is asking before you can answer. You can tell she doesn't write her own test and probably uses a test generator. You have to really work hard which can be difficult with a heavy course load.

Her tests are very wordy and confusing. The material itself is not that hard, but the test itself is like a riddle that you have to solve to figure out what each question is actually asking. Each answer also has multiple parts to try and trip you up. Lecture is very fast paced and it is easy to fall back. Plan on using the textbook a lot!
Tests are very hard. Questions are very wordy and hard to understand. Study for the next test 2-3 days after the previous test. Use office hours and e-mail.

She is a really great instructor and knows her stuff.  Study early Sometimes she goes off on tangents and it becomes hard to distinguish what information is important.  You do not need to attend class, TEGRITY = AMAZING.  She is really nice and readily available for help and is always timely with email responses even on weekends.  HIGHLY RECOMMEND!
i went to class all first semester and got a c- in bio 1, went to class maybe 3 times 2nd semester, got a b- in bio 2. you dont need to go to class, but study for the tests use tegrity and the book.

had for both Gen Bio 1 & Gen Bio 2...at first the class size, information and her expectations might overwhelm you but I learned so much from taking this class. The book helps to reinforce her lectures but Bio 2 was more about the lectures. Her tests are tricky but after awhile you learn to distinguish what she chooses to put on tests.

She's a really nice lady and helpful if you go to her and ask questions. she does a lot for her students and grades the tests within a few hours sometimes. Her tests are really hard esp in BIOL2 but if you study a lot you can still get a good grade because she helps a lot by dropping and averaging lowest tests and quiz grades

Had her for general bio 1 and 2. You need to put a lot of effort into this class, but its worth it, and you will learn a lot. Very knowledgeable.  I would print out a copy of the powerpoint slides and take notes on them while rewatching the lectures on tegrity on blackboard.  After that is done, skim the book. A lot of work, but it will pay off.

Had her for BIO 1 and 2. Prof is super knowledgeable... Lectures can sound repeated and boring sometimes. Tests are very wordy- come prepared! Use textbook, studyguides, online book website flashcards to help study... Lots of effort = good grade.
Her tests are very confusing. she gives out quizzes and she does not curve grades. Very strict.

ok, honestly i had her for both bio 1 and 2 and all i can say is the more often i skipped class the better I did, find a way to study that works, and use it, she has no mercy for you if you forget anything.

Invert. Zoology is very hard. Very exacting and strict grading, no curve. Half the test is take-home, so it's easy to get a passable grade, but the in  class portion is very hard. Very nice and friendly. There is a LOT of material in the class; even at a seeming breakneck pace, we were still behind. Hope you know some Latin; vast amt of terminology

The only thing that is a drag is the 8am time slot.  but she is very clear and knowledgeable about biology!  its definitely possible to get an A, just take the time to really study.  It's college, it's obv not going to be easy but you will learn a lot!
amazing teacher. she kept the topic interesting at all times. you must attend class if you would like to do well, but she made everything interesting
very hard class, lots of information.  Seems very mean and cold in beginning, but if you go see her in office hours shes a really kind woman. dont miss class.
very hard

pretty good teacher even though lectures are extremely boring. be prepared for a rough semester if you have her at 8am, not the most pleasant thing to wake up to. her tests are very hard, and need a good few days of studying for a decent grade.

A great teacher, very enthusiastic and easy to understand. Make sure you go to class, take detailed notes, read the textbook, study the labs and you should be fine. Pretty easy A and really enjoyable! Only negative was the 8am shocker

She is a good teacher who wants you to do good. The class is hard and she might come off as mean, You just need to put the time into it. You have to study, my advise is to study right from the class powerpoints and notes, and at the end read throught he book for any extra information, or to get a better understanding of the material.

STUDY HARD, READ THE BOOK, GET FRIENDS IN CLASS, LAB DEF HELPS!! Second semester practicals are a pain but could def help your grade. She's a pretty nice person it's just that she gives WAYYY too much information. Class was pointless to me but useful to others. Either read the book word for word or go to class to do well.

I struggled bigggg time last semester at the beginning. She's really nice once you go see her, and show her you care about your grades. I did horrible on the first two exams and still passed with a B. Go to class to get your attendance points (they help!). And study hardddddd because her tests cover everythingggg. She's tough, but ey work with her!
Gen Bio 1 &2 were my hardest classes. Hard/ very tricky tests. Unfriendly. Thinks everyone should care about bio..but we don't.
She's not very nice, but she's helpful and clear.  As long as you pay attention, you will get an A
love the class, and good teacher. Can be friendly if you give her respect but I've heard she can be not so helpful if your rude.
tricky tests. annoying voice. if youve taken AP bio, you can cake through the course
test are so hard...just give up guys!!

My least favorite class for this year. She is a nice person, but her exams are extremely hard, especially for bio 2. Memorize(I mean literally from a to z.) the book will help you out(if you can of course. A LOT of material in a SHORT period of time. For me, I've read the book 3 times and still couldn't do well.
attendance is not mandatory. her test is really hard and you might wanna change your professor. she ruined my freshman year
i really really really dont like this class.  There is a lot of material and questions on tests are often impossibly specific.  If you have a strong background in Bio, you'll be ok.
not a very nice teacher. actually was my least liked teacher freshman year. tests were very difficult. make sure to go to class and pay attention AND read the book.
Seems like a nice woman; pretty attractive too :)

Definitely a lot of material in a short time. Attendance isn't mandatory especially if you're just gonna sleep in class--you get notes some other way. Tests are very specific but not impossible. Reading the book and studying are musts. She's overall fair, it's just SO much material, overwhelming too. Doing well in lab should help boost your grade

Anal about cheating, seems very crabby and ****y, but is easy to do well in her class. show up to lecture if you feel like it, she only takes attendance when most people don't show up and it's not worth much. read the textbook well because she asks very specific questions.

a lot of material in a short time- made exams somewhat difficult. you pretty much have to know your stuff. not sure about clarity- fell asleep half the time in lectures and only stayed awake to take notes =T good thing she puts the ppts online.

WWould not recommend if not required. Material is interesting but there is ALOT of it.  Exam questions are not straight forward.  Slightly intimidating professor but nice in person.  Attendance in lecture is not mandatory but you will not do well if do not go. Class averages were in low 70s, hard class but not impossible.

Overall a great professor.  She seems like she would not be helpful but, if you go and talk to her she is a sweet, very helpful woman.  The tests are difficult but an A is possible with moderate studying. DO NOT leave studying for the night before!

must go to class AND read the book. When she finishes a chapter in class, start studying it. People who don't do well in her class don't study correctly and leave it to last minute. I started studying so early for tests and got A's.Shes not impossible but tough.Listening to her gets tedious

she is a nice person, but she likes to hear herself talk. she genuinely cares about you passing though so if you go to the lectures, take notes, and study in advance you'll be fine. if you don't go to class and only study the night before the test don't expect to get higher than a C. nice woman though and she is fair.
hard but not impossible ... second semester (Bio 2) much easier if you like anatomy, even though you will probably never need to remember it if you are a pharmacy major.
bio 2 is nothing like bio 1.  labs much harder.  reading textbook is helpful.  moderately hard class...but lowest test score and quiz is dropped.  class averages around 73.

The easiest way to learn in this class is to outline the text, memorize terms, and take the quizzes provided by the text's website. Don't bother showing up for class except to get answers to online quizzes. Also, don't blow off the quizzes or the first test--you may find yourself in trouble later on. An easy B but a hard A.
Wants students to learn.  Fair grader- all tests and quizzes are multiple choice.  Although attendance is not mandatory, she will occasionally give bonus points for going to class

I thought she had a kind of annoying personality and she talked wicked fast. Other than that however, she explained everything very clearly, and the class was so fast paced that you didn't have a chance to get too bored if you were at all interested in biology. Tests/quizzes were very wordy but all multiple choice. Pretty good,Ide take her again.

Class is a waste. Just use the textbook, study it like crazy, and use the book website. All you need is the book to do well,  but it's still a hard class. You definitely need to put time into it and actually read and study on a regular basis.

She talks way too fast that I stopped taking notes after awhile because my hand kept cramping up and I wasn't comprehending anything she was saying.  I got the same grade on all 3 tests and stopped going to class after the first one.  You absolutely need the book..many test questions are from the book, and most questions are on the little details.

The class is fast paced - she gives a lot of notes.  It's really hard to get an A but not that hard to get a B+ - one of those type of classes.  She can seem unapproachable, but if you seem interested in one of her topics, she will chat with you and help you out.

Good teacher, yet a very difficult class. She talks very fast, and the questions on the exams can be tricky because you really need to throughly understand the material. She can be a bit hard to communicate with at times, but overall is a nice woman that you can tell really knows what she is teaching.

She talks wayyyyy too fast. I got the same grade on the first test that i did on the second but I didn't go to any of the classes for the second one. The class is very BORING, not to mention it's at 8 am     She can be rude to students at times.
Her enthusiasm is contagious.

She is a great teacher and explains everything u will need to know. u def need to read the book for the exams because either you wont go to class and wont have the notes or the notes you took you wont understand.  Lab is an easy A, but the quizes and tests are not.  The tests are pretty hard but if you study for them you should be able to get a B.

This was my 2nd semester in her class, and this time it was much worse. the tests were too difficult, she spoke too quickly and half the test came from the book, not the notes in class. if she slowed down, the class would be boring, but do-able.

I had her for Bio 1 and 2.  Bio 1 was fine, lots of reading and studying but it was relatively easy to do well.  Bio 2 is a different story.  There is so much information that is really unneccesary, considering half the class are Pharmacy majors and don't need to learn about starfish.  She is very helpful and wants students to do well.  Lab= death.
Talks very fast in lecture and can get a little boring, but she is very helpful at office hours and makes herself very available.

Very easy to understand. Can talk a bit too quickly, but everything she talks about in lecture is in the book unless she specifies otherwise. Great professor, just look over your lecture notes, read the book, and you'll do great on the tests!

A Professional Professor. respectful and knows her stuff. Shes ready to answer any question...and shows that shes concerned about her students. Willing to sacrifice alot to make things easier. Bonds well w/ others. Slight problem is that she sounds like a book.very interesting but she always teaches morning classes, so u cant help but sleep. +Sweet
okay teacher...spend the time reading the material beforehand and you'll do fine.

She is a very nice woman who I enjoyed learning from. She loves teaching and wants you to understand the material. She has a reputation for being very hard but that is because you actually have to keep up with the reading and review in order to do well. Overall she is a great professor.
great

Good teacher, she talks about a lot at once, but she'll draw a lot on the overhead so that it makes sense. Talks too fast, but she can go to class and she'll teach, don't always have to teach yourself before class, and thats good. :)

she's kind of annoying, all she does is go with the book and uses her own terms for certain things.  Your better off reading the text a few times than going to the class.  Just make sure you know when online quizzes are.
I reccommend reading the material a few times. If you do that, you'll get an A.

nice woman, knows a lot of information and wants you to know it too.  tests are moderately difficult because they require thinking (like college tests should).  study using the chapter quizzes, online quizzes from the book, and her quizzes and you'll do fine.  keep up with the reading and spend youre extra time reviewing and you'll do fine.
Very hard class, but she is a good teacher, Any Bio class will have a lot of material. She is strict but will help you to understand.

It was a diffucult class. Bio 1 was alot easier. In Bio 2 she read straight from the book. If you know all the study guide it isnt good enough. You have to be able to understand her questions and if you can you will be fine.

Past raters are likely people that didn't do well in this class.She is not interesting,but bc the material isn't interesting.Just keep up with the reading, take the quizzes at the end of each chapter and you get an A.Those that struggle don't deserve to be in college and I'm embarrassed for them.
haaarrrrddd
Her explanations are a little unclear and she covers a lot of material in a short time. I took the quizzes on the cd that came with the book and that helped me prepare for tests.

Potts-Santone is crazy.  I was very interested in biology before her class.  Now, I'm not interested in taking another class with her.  If you don't go to lecture, you WILL have a hard time with tests, since the tests use lots of in-class, VERY specific, references.  She is boring and uninteresting.

Her class covers alot of material, but her tests aren't ridiculously hard as long as you put some time in to study. Her lectures are almost word for word of out the chapter outlines which can be found on the website. Bring these to class and just add any extra info. Study these and you're all set!
She knows her stuff like no one else, and loves teaching it.  Very nice and willing to do all she can to help students.  One of my favorite professors thus far.
Very clear on what she expects of you, but the tests are so specific and difficult.  Try to wake up for the 8 AM classes or you'll be lost.
crams a lotta stuff in one test & is extremely specific as to what she test you on...she sucks. i cant wait to get out of her class! ps u will be napping in her lectures

Her classes are very intensive. She expects a lot. Her method of lecture is a little boring at times and the tests cover lots of material. However she is more than willing to give extra help to anyone who wants it. Be prepared for a lot of reading.

If a teacher can't put a proper sentance together, she shouldn't be teaching.  Feels like she reads the chapter right before class, then explains it in great detail, confusing everyone, and then tests students on specific details of other information. run
tests are very hard.
This class is soo hard.  She goes in so much detail, and is hard to keep up with.  But usually she teaches right out of the book
Hardiest teacher i think i will ever encounter.  The exams are so specific.  Talks a mile a minute and goes through 5 chapters a class.  Have Fun!
Gives alot of notes in each class but pretty much goes by the book. Gives a study guide for every exam and drops the lowest score. Nice lady who just wants you to learn.
lots of notes and material covered, but it is presented in a way that is easy to understand.
wicked hot!
She gives a lot of notes...take them, study them,study them again and you will do fine
No Comments
No Comments
She just throws driagrams up on the board and writes random words all over them and then says that there are no diagrams on the tests

She might be hard but she is really understanding and helpful. I wouldn't want another anatomy professor - well unless there was one that just handed out A's. She is probably the best prof. I have had here.
No Comments
No Comments

The class was graded on two quizzes and a midterm, but as long as you take notes during class you'll be totally fine. If you miss a day get notes from a friend. He's not great with technology (doesn't upload grades to Canvas) but it's easy to calculate your own grade. He takes students out for coffee every week, really nice and knowledgable guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Mayer is great. Offers coffee after class for students. Lectures are just him talking so you must take good notes for midterms and quizzes. You can tell he tries to connect with students. Having a TA might make this class perfect as he struggles with technology.

Very very nice professor who tried to connect with all students in the class. Lectures made up the entire class and was him talking the whole time. As long as you take notes and pay some attention the tests can be very easy. He provides study guides for midterm and final, and for the midterm gave us sample questions to practice with.

I honestly love professor Mayer! His lectures just include him talking the entire time but as long as you listen and take efficient notes you will be fine. If you actually put in effort and time studying for the reading quizzes you will also be fine. He's super straightforward and sweet, don't let these other ratings scare you away!

Definitely a harsh grader but if you write down literally everything he says, memorize it, and regurgitate it you will be fine lol! The reading quizzes are incredibly difficult and 20% of your grade...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Long lectures with no slides, it was hard to take notes. If you miss a day, you're screwed. We had quizzes where he assigned 50+ readings, some with hundreds of pages, and expected us to recognize 1-3 sentence quotes. Very defensive if students were confused. He did not use Canvas grading, his email, or return final exams. Didn't learn much...
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Avoid at all costs! Grade is midterm + final. Only feedback comes from midterm. Won't require attendance. But expects you regurgitate his perspective on exams, so show up if you want an A. Boring lectures ONLY. They're outdated and sometimes repugnant. Used M*A*S*H as contemporary example once. Personal politics interferes with quality.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lecture-heavy course on what is sometimes outdated material. Unfortunately, little feedback/criteria is given for assignments, so you won't know your grade or what you're doing incorrectly. Hard to get in contact with via email but made himself available to students via office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Mayer taught an outdated, untruthful, and sometimes inappropriate Mass Media and Politics course. For most lectures, he went over an hour talking nonstop, never pausing for student input. The only graded assignments were the midterm, final, and an optional paper. Prepare for a conservative perspective and content 30 years out of date.

Avoid this guy. Mayer is smart but is awful at teaching. Class is exclusively lectures in which he regurgitates what is on the Canvas slides. You will have to teach the material to yourself. There are only 4 total assignments so you cannot afford to mess anything up. He grades very hard and will find ANYTHING to take points from. Take someone else.

Class is purely lectures with little participation or group discussion. Lectures are boring and sometimes lack important details (which are on midterm/final). Only 4 assignments the entire course so if you do poorly on one you need to do amazing on everything else to finish with a good grade. Office hours are helpful though.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The lectures and slides were so boring and hard to pay attention to. Only 2 graded assignments, then the midterm and final. The final is worth 40%. He is very knowledgable, with high expectations. He is also very old-school and not tech savvy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very knowledgeable. Lectures well and provides real world examples for everything. If you're coming in with a basic understanding of American Government you do not need to attend lectures. Will occasionally say something very suspicious, but is overall a nice guy.

Just lectures the entire class period little participation or interaction. Can be extremely boring however he is nice enough and is knowledgeable and the class is extremely easy if you put in just a little effort. The midterm and final do make up 75% of the grade though and the last 25% is only a couple assignments.

He does not take accountability, has an awful attitude, grades far too difficulty, and is extremely bad at accommodating people regarding disabilities, etc. He also forces purchase of newest editions of book. Heavy reading, awful lectures, do not waste your time.

Professor Mayer is an amazing lecturer. He is interesting and great at structuring information that helps when studying for his exams. Personally, I don't like when my classes have few grades and rely heavily on the midterm and final but I know a lot of people in his classes preferred this. If you are a confident test taker than go for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Do not take American political thought unless it is required for your degree. the work load isnt impossible, but the quality of work he expects for an A is unattainable unless you participate a ton. There are only like 4 grades the entire semester and he takes a long time to get them back. I wouldnt take this class again

Professor Mayer is adorable and SO knowledgeable on what he teaches. I have now taken two classes with him, and intend on taking more of his classes if I can. If you show up to class and take notes, you WILL get an A fairly easily. He is a clear lecturer and interesting to listen to for a 100 minute period, and he is really nice and accomodating.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Mayer is not prepared to be teaching from a virtual setting. Lectures were boring and grade was made up of three tests. Took over a month to get exams back. Was upset his class was too easy this semester and blamed the TA. Accidentally released final exam questions to the entire class before the exam. Purposely gave worse grades on final.

Professor Mayer gets a bad rep in my opinion. He is a conservative professor but doesn't let his views affect lecture in my opinion. I don't agree with his beliefs, but it's refreshing to not have a liberal professor teaching their views to students as facts. Class is easy and he is a stand up guy. Would definitely take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great lecturestake solid notes and you'll be good for exams. However, there were only 2 graded assignments for this class with an optional 3rd, which was daunting, and he didn't give good good feedback on the work. I was worried about taking this class as a first year, but I made it out fine even after skipping a couple classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Does a commendable job towing the line between his own political views and objectively teaching the course- and what he doesnt know about the course material isnt worth knowing. That said, his accessibility and email response rate need improvement; he isnt always at his office hours when the syllabus says he will be. Otherwise very good prof.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Was fine. Easy intro level class. Maybe he would be more interesting in a less basic class. Don't buy the textbook you don't need it.

Prof. Mayer is an excellent professor. I have taken two classes with him and both topics were thoroughly explained. If you genuinely do not take this class because he's one of the last conservatives on campus, consider a change in major. Very interesting, intelligent guy. Engage with him and it'll be worth your while.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Typical lecture style class. Professor Mayer clearly explains the material, so if you take notes you will do fine. Don't bother with the Logic of American Politics book, use your notes and you'll have all the information you need. You'll need Debating Reform for the recitations though.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: My fave thing about Dr. Mayer is that he's adorable (like how old people are adorable). He stutters a lot and wears sweaters that match the shirt he wears under and he draws TERRIBLY. Pretty much just take notes all class; he likes to give stats/graphs too. We had 1 piece of hw all semester. DON'T BOTHER GETTING THE TEXTBOOK. It's all in the notes.

The year is not done but American Government is a great class that Mayer lectures very well for. Lectures do not have Powerpoints but are clear and he makes what he's talking about interesting. There are very few assignments but his format and content at least for the midterm are unbelievably vague.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great guy, knows his subject very well. He has well thought-out lectures, and although he assigns readings most of the test material is from the lectures themselves. Only a midterm and a final exam, but he's a fair grader and gives you time to study. Lectures are long, but super interesting if you pay attention. Great professor all around.

great professor- definitely my favorite at northeastern. the lectures are really clear and well organized and the exams are a direct reflection of the material covered. it's very fair, no suprises. mayer made what could have been a very boring 100 minutes a very interesting time. i really don't have any criticisms, great guy, great class, period.

Mayer is a teacher's teacher. The guy clearly loves his job, and sitting in lecture with him is a pleasure. He class is all about connected historical narratives that are easy to comprehend. Tests can be somewhat quizzically graded, but you still learn a lot. I'd recompensed him for any class but quant.

He loves teaching this class, People who don't like him, are just cranky because you don't appreciate the awesomeness that is William T. Mayer. Super Clear, great guy, character, just an awesome teacher, super fair. I admire him greatly and he is very intelligent - Am Pol Thought

Mayer is the man.  Attendance not required but why would you want to miss class with this guy?  He is THE most fairest grader/professor ever.  Aside from being extremely intelligent,  his classes are very interesting.  Study like crazy two days before each test and you got a A or a B.

ok teacher. grades based on a midterm and a final, no attendance necessary, not really necessary to read the texts because he goes through things in class. pretty organized so easy to take notes, just listen in class and you'll do fine. funny guy, but not the most interesting nor intriguing class
good teach

he was awful. i attended every class, took down every note, restated what he said and more on the exams and got graded POORLY along with others. kids who didn't show up got good grades, unfair. boring as hell. will never take another class with him.. class was based on midterm+final. marked things wrong that he said were right in lecture.. AWFULLL.

Yes he's a dork, BUT he knows his stuff. Lectures are straight forward and his notes are clear as air.  Attending class makes it easy to pass without reading the "required" texts. NO HOMEWORK OR PAPERS. Take notes and regurgitate them on exams and you'll do well.

Listen up chumps... Mayer is the man. You can complain all you want, but until you've gotten one of his priceless gambling analogies, a show of his rightwing fury over Bill and Hillary, or some sarcastic response that solidifies his place amongst the academic elite, you just haven't lived. Give the man a B+; except when teaching Quant; that's a C-.

This is simple. He's rude to the point where its almost funny. NEVER take quant. tech. with him because he openly admits he hates teaching the class. BUT his other classes are easy, he's an easy grader, doesn't care if you're there or not, and makes the tests easy because by his own admission he hates grading/paper work.
Interesting class if you read the material. Gives clear notes so you know what to expect on the exam. Pretty easy grader too.
Get over the fact he's conservative- he's extremely smart, and unless you're a total cretin, you should easily pass this class.  It's very interesting, and if you take notes, the tests are a breeze.

Boring+Republican. Stutters like mad. Knows his stuff tho. only midterm and final for grades. I didn't buy the book, and never read anything for the class. If you go to class and takes notes, you'll do okay. Not particularly polite or helpful, but grades easy.If its between him and someone bad, take him. I hear hes not good w/ quant tech.

I'll make it simple. He's one of the worst teachers a Northeastern. Almost funny how rude he is. Way to many things to get into so the bottom is never take this class.For poli sci majors, try to wait until somebody else teaches it. During the first class,he made it obvious he didn't want to teach the class, so don't expect any extra effort from him

I can't figure out why this guy is allowed to teach this class. The class is the worst class I've ever taken. He doesn't make any sense and grades way too tough. Seriously, he'll yell at you for asking questions. Normal? Basically picture this; he's like that mean grade school principal you had. Terrible class, don't take it.
Light course load, only midterm and final.  But I didnt learn as much as i would have liked. His teaching style is a little lame.  If its between him and something you know sucks, take him.

DO NOT EVER TAKE THIS CLASS. He's rude, and very unhelpful. The subject would be simple, but his notes are unorganized and his examples and explanations do not make sense. This is the worst class I've ever taken in my life. Seriously. Unless you're a political science major, NEVER TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!!!!!!!!!

Okay, my last review was removed so maybe I was a little too truthful and descriptive, I'll tone it down. He's difficult to tolerate and he teaches a boring subject (Quant Tech) I apologize if I offend anyone but its a tough class solely due to the professor and his "Sicilian" accent. Unless you're Poli Sci major, avoid at all costs.
No Comments

Worst Class Ever.  He went over things about fifty million times and it was sooooo boring.  The final should have been easy but there was stuff that we had never gone over in class.  I would have liked the class so much better with any other professor.

Worst Class Ever.  He went over things about fifty million times and it was sooooo boring.  The final should have been easy but there was stuff that we had never gone over in class.  I would have liked the class so much better with any other professor.
hes a smart man, but not helpful at all, was very unclear, and added things on the final that we never learned.
I loved this class. Not the most exciting teacher, but very smart and helpful. Note: He's a Republican.

Very boring but organized notes. Literally no work, just midterm and final, most reading can be skipped too. A nice class to get a decent grade in that should be simple for most politically inclined students
No Comments
TOTAL DORK, the type u beat up for milk money..big blak was cool tho, Xcept 4 da Pru deal...meyer is a total nerd but since he is my neighbor its OK...lawn MOWERS FOR LIFE GEEK
No Comments
To warn you you will be bored to tears, but it's worth it for the limited amount of work and easy exams, for the most part the reading is unnecessary, easy A
Difficult class, lots of reading, and an impossible final.  What can i say except i might have learned something...and gotten a B, stupid ****
Lots of writing, very critical grading. Tests were hard to score well on, and every question was worth 1% of your grade.

Tolley is great. Super nice and kind, very approachable both in and out of class. His lectures are interesting, and he does a good job engaging with people. Def the best professor I have had. With that said, the class is very hard and the first test was brutal for me. I recommend going over the slides/cases in painstaking depth to study for tests.

Midterm and final exams were lowk tough but essays were easy. If you participate and do well on essays and final assignment he boosts your final grade. He's legit my Shayla- so sweet and hilarious. Can't recommend him enough

Professor Tolley is an amazing person who is truly passionate about what he teaches, The material we learned was valuable and our class discussions were thought-provoking. My only complaints are that some of the lectures felt monotonous, and the class grading system is way too harsh. Exams where each MCQ is worth 1% of your semester grade is crazy.

great professor who is knowledgeable and cares about what he teaches! even though the 8 am lectures are tough, they are worth going to. you will get something out of it and tests are very doable as long as you are covering the course material.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Goat. He really only teachers American Gov at 8AM, but in my opinion it's 100% worth waking up for. All of his exams are based exactly on the lecture slides and totally doable if you attend most classes. He's kind and wants to make sure you get something meaningful out of his class.

Professor Tolley is very sweet and very intelligent. His lectures are interesting if you're also interested in the topic. Grades are based off of 3 essays, 2 exams, and one project. Study hard for the exam's they're a big part of you're grade. Cannot stress enough how nice and knowledgeable this man is.

Professor Tolley was a good guy and cared about the subject a lot however the course was set up bad. If you get below a 85 on any test you are screwed for the whole semester. The textbook is "required" however I did not use it at all because the lectures go over it so do not waste your money.

Professor Tolley is great! You can tell that he really loves what he does- he's a bit of a political science nerd lol. If you've taken American gov in highschool its pretty similar. However, in addition to 4 mandatory essays his tests are also all essay-based. So if you don't love writing or it's not your strong suit this is not the class for you.

The lectures were not very engaging, and the graders were harsh on tests. Upon requesting help and assistance, the prof was not very approachable and quite rude, especially when asking for clarification on essays.

I had four papers, three exams, and one research paper. His lectures are based on the textbook, so I attended all of the lectures and did not read the textbook at all. He quickly responds to emails and is available during office hours or after class. There is a decent amount of work and studying, but it's not too difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Tolley is one of the best professors at Northeastern. He's really passionate about what he teaches and cares about his students. He wants you to come to his office hours and to ask for advice. He individually reads our assignments and throughly looks through them. His class is easy as long as you follow along in readings or lecture.

Essay intensive class. 2 tests that were timed essays. It's not too bad, mainly reading comprehension from his lecture-leavy course. Homework was mainly essays. Not too harsh of a grader, especially since his TA grades all his work. Pretty smart person though and has good recommendations of what courses to take if not his.

bro these ratings are not accurate. this class is very difficult I have no idea how people are passing it. the prof is nice but the assignments are impossible and you need to intensely study like two weeks before each exam. its impossible. only take if ur course load in other classes is easy. otherwise NO
Lecture heavy, hand written test, overall good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures aren't super engaging and not many opportunities for participation. Definitely knows his stuff, explains concepts well, and seems kind. Pretty lenient grader and never takes attendance, but don't skip a lot (assignments are lecture based). Assigns insane amount of reading but no need to read everything to do well. Final was kinda brutal.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I liked the topics but lectures could be a little boring. Lots of writing and papers which was also expected, but sometimes the grading criteria were unclear

Tolley is great! He really knows what he is talking about and he's passionate about it, too. His lectures are super important to go to but don't bother with the textbook readings. He's not a difficult grader and his assignments are usually helpful.

maybe it was the 8am timeslot or maybe it was the fact this class just felt like APUSH but this was easily my least favorite class of this semester. tolley knows a lot and he wants you to know that he knows a lot. the syllabus is clear in all the assignments and the textbook is useless. you'll be fine if you take any notes on his (boring) lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Michael Tolley is an amazing teacher. His class is lecture heavy, but he is one of the best lecturers I have ever taken. His class is purely based on essays, and the material discussed in class is extremely interesting. The textbook is not needed, as most of the information can be found on the presentations he uploads after class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Tolley is absolutely amazing. He is great at actually teaching and his lectures are full of information and really interesting. Attendance is not mandatory but you really won't do well if you don't go to class because there are no powerpoints or any class materials. His assignments are hard and thought-provoking but he is a pretty easy grader
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You have to show up to class and put the work in but prof Tolley will teach the course material in a clear and relatable manner. He will supply context that you cannot get from the textbook. Always accessible outside of the classroom. Completely transparent with students grades throughout the entire semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is a very kind professor. He cares about students understanding the material and effort. He knows everything too (like a legal encyclopedia), really loves the Courts & Constitution! His expectations are straight forward and he grades papers fairly! As long as there is somewhat of an intelligent analysis and references to class- you're good.

I loved Tolley so much, i'm taking another class with him next semester. The class is extremely easy, most of it I learned in high school. I never opened either textbook once, but if you get confused on a topic I can see how it would be helpful. Class grades are off of seven easy essays, midterm, and final. Take him if you can!!!
Prof Tolley is a great professor! all of the information you need is given in the lectures so the textbook is redundant but its good review. Very easy grader and still helps you learn alot.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Tolley was an amazing professor and I truly enjoyed his class. All the information you need comes from lectures, the textbooks were pretty pointless but the lectures were informative and helpful. I will definitely take another one of Tolley's classes in the future!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Tolley is clear about his expectations and his lectures are both interesting and easy to follow.

Awesome professor. If you are interested in law he is very clear and knows the topic well. Very helpful if you go to him for help. Takes a bit too long to grade exams or papers but not tough grader. Take notes of the outline and you'll do fine. The book might help but his lectures are enough.

P. Tolley is one of the smartest men I know.  I transferred in recently, and now understand why tuition is high.  Follow the outline he puts on the board daily, take the notes off that, and you will do well. He is very organized, so you will find no surprises. I love C. law, and learned so much from him. He was an english major, so PROOFREAD!

don't bother with the readings, he assigns tons but goes over important stuff in lecture. take good notes, exams are completely from them. he's very clear, though bad at hiding his very strong political ideas, but he's a great professor. Grade is midterm, final, an easy tiny court assignment, and a big research project (don't leave to end or else)

Great Guy. Definitely worth it if you are interested in law. He tests pretty much only on the notes, so I sold my book after the midterm. and did fine. Go to class and pay attention, it will be worth it.
excellent professor

If you are interested in Law, his constitutinoal class will give a great background. I learned a crazy amount in this class.  His lectures aren't bad and as long as you read and take notes you are good.
Hello and good morning  Good guy.

Professor Tolley is one of my favorites in the department. His con law class was so informative and interesting. I really internalized the cases and feel confident about my knowledge as I get ready to apply to law schools next year.

not a bad teacher, but not good either, all he would take about is, "in my book...blah, blah, blah" he was also my advisor but refused to talk to me after my frosh year about what classes I should take the following semster because he couldn't take 5 min out of his day...i never went back to him again for advise, thank god for my achedemic advisor

Prof. Tolley is one of the only professors I really remember from NU.  They aren't easy courses, but you'll remember his class nearly every day when you read the paper and realize how much the Court impacts your life.  You can even email him once you've graduated with a question and he'll answer it.

Constitutional law, the material is dry but once you've taken his class you have an entirely new respect for the constitution, he's a good teacher, he's pretty hard but you'll learn a lot, I'd reccomend his class.
Nice guy.  Boring lectures though.  But if you read his texts its easy to pass without going to every lecture.
Best professor I have ever had in my life.  Truly inspiring man.

He definately has his own style, but I have learned more from him than all other professors combined.  He basically outlines what the exams will be if you come to the classes, and his eloquent speeches are so compelling that the time flies!

excellent professor. i learned a lot in this class. it helped to have interest in it, though. he tests mainly on lectures, so if you don't go to class youre probably screwed. nice guy and helpful too.

Horrible professor. I never take anything out of his lectures. He's a nice man, but a horrible teacher. He tests very strongly on his lectures which are impossible to comprehend and not at all on the three ridiculously expensive textbooks he has us buy. I
excellent professor
had him for Civ Lib and Constitutional. Great prof once you get used to his style. Brilliant man.

Dr. Nancy is great. She is super kind and approachable and wants you to succeed. The exams aren't cumulative which was super nice and she provided exam prep material and will review anything you want. Hw and lectures were super straightforward and she loves to tell stories and was very efficient with her lectures
She's a nice person, but I just don't get what she's teaching

Dr. Nancy is great! She is kind and understanding, for ex the whole class struggled with a hw problem, so she didn't include that stuff on the test. The tests are not cumulative, so there are 3 midterms - no final. The class is totally manageable and super interesting because she is clearly passionate about what she is teaching!

Dr. Nancy is really great at explaining concepts. She is super down to earth and easy to talk to. Go to class and take the practice exams and you will do fine in the class portion. The project portion is an added burden over the semester but is a great grade boost. I found the homework to be the hardest part of the class.
Tareq provides high-quality instruction. The course difficulty is well balanced, and he pays close attention to important details in his teaching.

Prof Nasralah is a great teacher who cares about his students. The class might be a bit more difficult than other business courses, however his generous grading compensates for that. Definitely recommend him for business stats, one of the best professors.
easy A if your group members are reliable.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Nasralah was so nice and understanding. His lectures were a bit dry but you can tell he really cares and wants everyone to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A kind professor who is always accessible outside of class. We had 4 quizzes, 4 individual homeworks, 4 group homeworks, a group project (which wasn't bad at all), a midterm, and a final. Not too hard to do well in the class- beware he does spend class going over the lectures which can get boring. He is not a tough grader and you can tell he cares!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is a nice guy but what he doesnt realize is that he doesnt teach us anything by simply doing it in front of us. We had to teach ourselves everything which is very hard to do when youre learning to code for the first time.

I really enjoyed having him as a professor! He was very enthusiastic, kind, and was always happy to answer questions. The group project given was very logical and was not random like some other classes. One note: I wouldn't recommend getting the first textbook, it was rather confusing. 100% recommend taking this class with him.
Great professor! Very understanding and kind and always ready to help you.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Overall great professor. He really knows his stuff. Class was interesting and assignments and exams were not too difficult. 10/10 would take this class again.

I once arrived two minutes late and was marked absent. It took a surprisingly long conversation to get it revoked. She makes a big deal out of the smallest things. Fairly certain the 5 star reviews were written by the instructor.

It's quite a shame because the material in the course is super interesting, but the professor makes it less enjoyable. For instance, she doesn't take feedback kindly, acts condescending, and grades harshly. I really think she wants the best for her students, but some of the things she says feels questionable.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Avoid at all cost
The instructor can occasionally come off as passive aggressive. The directions for assignments are ambiguous and clarifying these questions with the teacher only makes matters worse.
Worst teacher ever.

I don't ever declare that someone is "the worst" but truly one of the worst professors I have come across. How she is allowed to teach is truly a mystery to me. She is condescending and incapable of accepting any feedback. She is truly self-obsessed and only likes those who feed into her ego. I'm sure she wrote the positive reviews on this page.
