I used to love this class, but she definitely has her favorites. And for a class that's all about fairness and values… it kinda sucks not getting that from her

LOVED professor James, really don't understand why the reviews here are so bad. The class is big on the social impact aspect and originality of thought in the projects. There is quite a bit of group work but most people in the class really cared about similar aspects which made it easier to work together. She is very approachable.
No, just no.

I had the unfortunate experience of taking a one-month dialogue with this professor. Think every bad review rolled into one—condescending tone, zero ability to take feedback, and a knack for making students feel small. If you enjoy being talked down to, this class is perfect. Otherwise, run.

By far the worst professor I've ever had at Northeastern. She creates a class environment that is hostile for students and is very condescending. She is not lenient with grades and will grade your work harshly if it is not to her personal liking. She is rude and doesn't respect her students. I have never felt this poorly about a professor before.

The class environment was hostile and felt uneasy at times. It is clear through her syllabus and in class lectures that she does not have any original content. All homework and class lectures are off a book, How to Become a Change maker, so if you read that then you can probably learn everything.

Dr. J has a clear respect for her students and interest and knowledge in her field and willingness to share it with her students. While her projects are dense in material it should be expected for the capstone of the concentration and she is the best person to take this class with.

Literally the worst teacher. Would actually rather take a mute teacher than her again. Seems like she read a self help book once and decided shes ready to be an entrepreneur professor. The homework and lectures are based off a book. The group project is pointless. She picks her favorites and will create a hostile environment.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I've had at Northeastern. She sent me a personal email threatening to start giving me absences because I came to class 5 minutes late twice. She sounds and talks super nice but is super tough, and a hard grader. Weekly homework, twice a week, which you have to write 300-500 words for EACH. Terrible grading system also.
Nikki James was aggressive & created a hostile working environment which made many uncomfortable & feel annexed.

I was on a one month dialogue of civilization with professor James to South Africa last year. During this period she conducted several ethical/moral violations towards me and others such as fraternization favoritism gender bias sexism swore at us when confronted she retaliated/threatened me then became nice when being threatened with being reported

Professor James is the best professor I've had at NEU. She walks the walk when it comes to social entrepreneurship and is highly knowledgable, respectable, and approachable. Her activities/assignments are hands-on applications of theory. Her assignments emphasize emotional intelligence and she treats students as social entrepreneurs.

Dr James is an excellent professor and the group projects in her class changed how I will approach group work in the future. There are a lot of papers, so be ready to write, but the grading criteria are very clear and you will get plenty of reminders to start early. Take this professor if you want to grow your emotional intelligence

First time teaching so he was def learning how to teach the class. I enjoyed his lectures as he was passionate about the course, but a lot of people found it boring. Class was very easy with the take home tests.
His classes were lecture dense and not very engaging. However, everything in this course is take home, so it's not a hard class as long as you complete all the assignments online.

He's knowledgeable in his field, but his lectures can sometimes be repetitive and also fast-paced. Everything in the course was take-home (including midterm and final) so it's basically a guarenteed A in his class. As long as you complete all assignments, quizzes, and tests, you'll do good.
40% final 30% midterm but both are take home. No attendance required, which is sad bc only 10 people would show up. A bit backloaded but a fun class, good content and good professor. Fair grader.
Nan zhou is the goat. Easiest A.
more profs should be like nan

Everything in this class is take home, including both the midterm and final, so it is a pretty light class in terms of work. Prof. Zhou gives interesting lectures as well, so if you are interested in the material, going to class is nice. Attendance is not mandatory as well, so if you don't like the material, you dont have to show up

super easy class. all of the assignments are online and open note, no textbook required, and attendance isn't mandatory. econ is generally super boring imo but he does a pretty good job using engaging examples. def recommend if you need an easy econ credit
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: really easy A. all of the quizzes and tests are online (midterm/final). he is a nice guy and great teacher, he uses a lot of interesting examples to explain the concepts. however, because attendence wasnt mandtory no-one would show up to his lectures 3 weeks in.
Nan is so chill. Sometimes the take home quizzes and assignments were a bit tricky but nothing that couldn't be figured out. Cool dude and really is so lax. Would recommend
Easy A dont sleep on him

Such an easy A, don't ever go to class... take home midterm and final. All the homework's and quizzes are also online. The TA literally says it's impossible to get less than 80 on the assignments/midterm/final as long as you complete it, as well as impossible to get less than 50% on any problem even if you are completely wrong. Such an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easy A, take home midterm AND final, it's so free
Absolute Goat
Absolute gem of a Professor. Attendance isn't mandatory, all assignments including the Midterm and Final are administered online. Easy A for every freshman taking this course with him.
take it. surface level lectures and speech pattern that will put you to sleep. But trust me, take it.

Avoid this professor if you can. Worst instructor I've had. Mandatory attendance for lectures that add little to your understanding. Everything is explained better and faster online. This is the only class where showing up felt like wasted time. Choose another section if possible.

Class was moved online last-minute at least 8 times over the course of the semester, and prof showed up 20+ minutes late to many many others... on top of that, making the students' attendance mandatory halfway through the semester was very ironic. Was an easy A tho (didn't learn anything).

Do not take this professor, if you need this class take the other professor. He has no respect for your time as a student, every day he is late by 10 mins. He just rips the lecture slides off the other professor and while teaching them it's like the first time he's ever seen them. You can get more information by just skimming the textbooks.
Good Lecture

professor is not great. super boring lectures with mandatory attendance. often makes mistakes writing homework and test questions. on midterm, received an impossible question that took time away from other questions. can see he tries to teach, but fails. he doesn't convey points well, makes things more confusing than just reviewing the slides.

Horrible organization, does not answer emails, reads directly from the slides, does not explain concepts clearly. Lectures are not helpful so you have to teach yourself. Don't torture yourself by taking this professor. Only upside is the homeworks are not that bad and lab reports were graded somewhat leniently which I guess depends on your TAs.

Classes were pretty bad, the professor teaches but its the same as reading the slides and watching videos during your own time. There was attendance at the end of every class so you couldn't skip. Homework and test question difficulties were okay but graded leniently.

Incredibly boring lectures that take an hour to cover what you can learn yourself by spending 10 minutes reading the class notes provided by a different teacher. For the actual rigor, the course itself is really easy, but the labs lowkey take a lot longer b/c the TAs aren't all that helpful and the lab report is tedious to write.

Naveen is Northeastern's dark knight. He gives only 4 homeworks which is lesser than what Kimani gives. If you do good on both midterms, you don't need to give the final as the score is dropped. He gives a practice exam which is same as the actual exam. He is not a good teacher but makes the course low effort.
Do not take this professor. He does not answer questions very well. He does not respond to piazza. Every class goes for five minutes longer. There is no organisation.

This is the most poorly organized class I've taken at Northeastern. Professor does not respond to emails and piazza questions, cannot manage time in class, explains concepts poorly and very slowly, and does not release important study resources for exams.

DO NOT take this class with this professor.
Rarely responds to emails or questions on Piazza. Content is exciting but lectures tend to be boring and he is not great at answering questions or explaining concepts in class.

Constantly late to class, would end class over 5 minutes late regularly. He moved so slowly on material(despite the fact no one was asking questions) that we ended up weeks behind the other professor, and he'd hold online classes at 7pm to make up for it. He also held online classes to "make up for" the holidays. Test guidelines constantly changed.

These were some of the worst lectures I have ever sit through. Prof. Sapavath belabors simple arithmetic and glazes over the interesting content of the course. A 20 minute YouTube video teaches me more than his 70 minute lectures. He also frequently arrives to class 10+ minutes late and will make mistakes on simple math.

Professor Nwogwugwu is really really amazing! He's so kind & caring, & genuinely wants us to grow & become better speakers. The class wasnt that hard necessarily, & Professor makes it enjoyable. Despite being at 9:50am I still really enjoy this class & look forward to it during the week. HIGHLY RECOMMEND YOU TAKE HIS CLASS EVEN IF ITS NOT REQUIRED.

Great professor and classes are super fun and engaging, but attendance is optional. Grades for speeches are mostly based on how well you can memorize the information and convey it clearly. Lots of work with speeches/interviews every other week with assignments, and journal entry's added to the mix. Wish he was clearer with grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super easy and understandable lectures. The speeches that you have to perform will drive you nuts, it's super stressful to be memorizing all of the details that you prepare for. You will be worried basically throughout all of the speeches. Even the job interviews, it will literally fear you. The whole class is basically fear of your grade.

I would definitely say take this over the summer (before fall of freshman year) or summer (after freshman year). Very light class compared to the other presentation requirement classes. I heard public speaking online is very light as well. Persuasion and Rhetoric has three exams, so don't do that. There's five speeches in this class.

The class was pretty easy overall. He's super nice and the assignments aren't hard at all. The only frustrating part was that he posted assignments really close to the due date or didn't clearly say when they were due, which made it a little stressful. There are 4 individual speeches, one group speech, a mock job interview, and a final project.
He was a loving professor who wanted the best for us.

honestly this class was easy, but i respected daniel as a professor. this was his first year at northeastern, so i believe he will evolve into the dedicated professors northeastern cultivates with time. his lectures were humorous, he did not grade speeches hard and was accommodating when i had to miss class for dr. appointments.

he was a humorous instructor and had interesting personal experiences , he didn't create a syllabus until three weeks into the session. After then, he did not follow the syllabus.He would point out errors in our work during grading, but he never instructed us to complete the task in a certain manner in the assignment instructions they were vague.

His classes were common sense, boring lectures, except on speech days. We only had 4 speeches, so every other day was a lecture. He posts speech assignments very late, sometimes the day before they are due, and the due dates didn't match up at ALL with the syllabus. However, very easy to do well and you only really need to attend on speech days.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Grades off vibes, AVOID
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Noonan is awesome. I enjoyed his new way of teaching the course, a way that doesn't involve strict assignment-based grading. Show up to class and do the work on time for credit. The final project was also fun and unique.
I took this class while on co-op. A much bigger time commitment than I thought. The readings were geared more towards English majors.

Honestly I really liked Prof. Noonan! He was very easy to talk to and made the classroom environment very comfortable/friendly. The grading was very holistic and it was based a lot on individual effort put in. If you try your best and you show up to class consistently then you will get a good grade. Take Noonan! He may be strange but he is fun.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Noonan is a very nice man, his class environment is welcoming and encouraging. This was my favorite class and I recommend everyone to take this class. The class was not that hard but taught me valuable insights and was overall very fun.

I had him for Honors First Year Writing. He was unorthodox with his loose grading and conversational class. I enjoyed it and grew as a writer. He made the course relevant by presenting the concept of audience to us in the context of AI. Some assignments were worded vague. The final project was too involved for a required FYW class.

Fine professor - relatively easy grader as long as you put in effort. However, the class is excruciatingly boring and pointless. The assignments are a complete joke and you will learn nothing useful in Adv Writing Arts/Media/Design. Put in decent effort and you will get an A, but be prepared to roll your eyes every assignment.

Doesn't seem to realize that Advanced Writing in the Disciplines isn't necessarily a class for English majors. Assigned readings (of which there will be many) are dense pieces written by and for academics that wax poetic about "genre" while teaching nothing relevant about writing for your discipline.

Professor Noonan is the worst professor Ive ever had. His assignments are extremely open-ended, not in a good way. He does not give clear answers but instead writes in circles, never telling you what he wants. He only hosts office hours for 30 minutes and is so rude and condescending if you ask questions or challenge him. Avoid at at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--So many papers

reviews.comment: So disconnected from reality. Acts as though the assignments are open ended but will then harshly grade for going somewhere off of his 6 page prompt. No changing of the class layout for COVID. Snarky short comments on pieces don't help to improve your project for the next assignment.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Doesn't teach during class. Assigns 10 paged papers a week before they're due. His assignment documents are 6 pages in length; you have to dig to just find the prompt. Gives no rubrics, no information on how you'll be graded (which assignments are worth more than others), and no course calendar. The only thing I've learned is to not take his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: He's awesome!

Noonan is certainly a challenging professor. His "vagueness" is admittedly on purpose. The assignments are very broad, and his vagueness is an attempt to give the student the ability to choose how they take on the assignment. It is accurate that he tends to ramble, and feedback is sometimes lacking, but he makes it a point to ensure everyone learns
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: 1 Noonsky is the man 2 He definitely makes you work for it. There are three projects that are particularly long. It takes a while to write them but if you don't procrastinate, make a plan, and churn it out the grading isn't that harsh at all. Attendance is non-mandatory but participation helps your grade. 3 I AM a better writer after this class.

Professor Noonan was by far my worst professor at NEU. His weekly letters are so condescending and rude, its like they are his own personal vanity projects. His assignments have nothing to do with writing in the disciplines and his expectations are maddeningly vague. He leaves short, pompous comments on peoples work publicly that offer no help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: No respect for his students. Rude & condescending, he attacks students if they have questions. His weekly letters have no clarity, tend to ramble, and are very difficult to understand. He's another example of the disappointing NEU English department. It is nearly impossible to succeed in his class because of the lack of direction and rude feedback.

Horrible professor. AVOID at all cost. Was not clear on our assignments and definitely pointless course. Does not apply to business knowledge / further development. Students are majoring in business not reading or english or journalism.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Noonan was great. A gifted writer and experienced teacher. Really good at getting you to think in the right ways. Tough grader but his assignments are fun if you get into it.

Noonski is the man.  At first I was apprehensive about the course as I heard he wasn't very clear, but I learned to embrace it.  He's a fun guy to have teach a class and his dry sense of humor was pretty great too.  Be sure to talk to him in class during the workshops and discuss the direction of your project and you should be fine.

Difficult professor, definitely do not recommend. Assignments are pretty vague, poorly timed (day after Super Bowl), hard grader. Places a lot of emphasis on post-paper reflections rather than paper itself. Pretty pompous guy, doesn't really teach just has class break into small groups.
Very hard and confusing, I dont recommend him at all
Excellent professor.  Made a dreaded AWD class very enjoyable.  Don't be shy about playing around with him in your work either- he's open to wit and humor.

Advanced Writing in the Disciplines. Difficult Class. Super super nice guy! He wants you to work, he wants you to think. Don't be lazy and you'll do fine. Haven't gotten my final grade yet but I worked really hard on our first three big projects and got either A or A-. Sometimes frustrating at how much work was required but no regrets on keeping it

Out of four of my classes Noonan's class is the worst. He is not clear with the projects, he asks for way too many assignments and is never satisfied. I do not recommend any one to take his class it is a nightmare

Ridiculous amounts of work.A lot of all nighters devoted to him. Very unclear when you ask for help.Honestly gave this class my all and still ended up with a C.Transferred schools and asked him to write a recommendation thinking I demonstrated hard work, his response "do you really want me to write a recommendation? You didn't really do well" PRICK

He is a strict and hard professor. I got crazy for this course at the beginning. It was hard for me to catch uo with him. And also ,like most people said, he may not usually tell you what is good and what you should do etc. H e prefer to let you think by yourself more. I can felt that he had definitely helped me improve my ways of thinking.
Had him for college writing. I hated him at first because he is a difficult grader and requires a lot of work, but he is a great teacher. I learned a ton, and I put in the work to get a good grade.

Not a class for the lazy. I hated the hard work at first, but grew to really love and commit myself to the projects. The class is a student workshop, I hate seeing people get upset that he's not doing the work for you. It's college. Grow up and put a little effort/thought into your work. Great Guy, always available for help/questions.

When he talks about the course matter, he's very ambiguous with words! Will never tell you straightforward what to do and what Not to do, juggles a lot with words and mostly bs-ing!! Attendance is "Extremely Mandatory" in this class and even though it's 10% of the grade he won't stop at failing you for that. Horrible Teacher, HIGHLY UNRECOMMENDED!!

Took the class a year back. Definitely worked for my grade. My writing improved drastically thanks to his class. Not for the lazy by any means. He genuinely wants students writing to get better. You just have to think outside the box for his assignments. Don't be afraid to experiment. I did not pay for NEU to watch monster movies in an Eng. class.
One of my favorite teachers. Noonan's class is hard, but he's always willing to help you as much as you need. And trust me, you will use the tools you learn in his class in real life.
Extraordinary amounts of work. Yes, practice makes perfect but this is way beyond the limit.
Professor Noonan is a great teacher- I took his AWD class which I was hestitant about taking, but he turned out to be a great professor, and the class was fun!

I love Noonan!!  I was so nervous about being in his class but we only have class 2 out of the 3 days we're supposed to and he usually lets us leave way early.  He's super nice and really wants to help you do well and explore your major and interests.  Awesome professor.

Amazing teacher! This classes really helped me define which area of my discipline I want to pursue. The class is a lot of work, but it's worth it in the end - TRUST ME. He's a really cool guy who's approachable and friendly. If your work ethic meets his expectations, you'll have no problem. The reviews before this are misleading, don't listen.

I had this guy a couple years back..at the time I thought life couldn't get any harder with his ridiculous papers but in all honesty I have used those papers since, for jobs and for transferring schools. Mr. Noonan is deff. a different style of teaching but I loved it.... Put in effort and he is the man and it will reflect in your grade! I miss him

Despite the class being hard as balls, he is the absolute man! and I love his bow ties and he is classy for not wearing a clip on, he ties that sh*t everytime he wears it. Keep it up Mr. Noonan, dont listen to the other D-Bags on this list.

this guys pretty much the hardest teacher I've ever worked with, and I'll tell you why: he's not teacher material. He knows his stuff, no doubt, but he's not committed to helping students, and not understanding of their occasional difficulties. He asks a lot from students in a short amount of time. And the whispering-while-talking thing. not cool

This class was so aggravating... He assigns a bunch of work that is supposed to help you but he doesn't check them or give credit. The work is tedious and he assigns huge essays. He tries to make it creative but he just makes things more difficult. I love English but he's the type of English teacher you don't want.

for an freshmen english teacher, he's is one of the hardest. with that being said, he is also one of the best. his style of teaching is very different compared to the rest of the teachers you will ever encounter. the work is hard, but its college.  as long as you ask for help and use his advice and don't wait til last minute you'll be fine.

All of these comments make it seem that it is Noonan's teaching that makes the class difficult. Yes the papers are hard, but not impossible. If you do what I did and work hard, while involving him in the writing process, you will do well. He makes time for all of his students and will help if asked. Just don't leave your work until the last minute!

you know hes really not as bad as all these comments make him out to be. its clear that he must have read this at some point because he didnt wear a SINGLE bow tie all semester, although there are some excruciatingly bright shirts for 8am. as long as you take all suggestions he makes about your paper and write about how they were genius, youre good

To get a good grade: 1: let him feel that he is soo smart at all times. 2: ask for his help a lot (office hours etc..) even though he won't do anything. 3. WRITE AS MUCH AS YOU CAN! number of pages mean the world to this dude.. for example if he want's 8 pages write at least 12!! if you want to get over a C of course!

he is not very helpull, if ur in is class, leave!!!!!!!!!, he is very very very hard and if you are an engineer you should never even think of taking this class with him, he never tells you what your supposed to do you go to class or you dont you still wont understand what the hell is going on, essays 8pg min and for some 15pg min, he is the worst.
DONT take this class. He is a nice guy, but teaching wise, he SUCKS. He will not help you on his essays, just give you bad grades, and explain afterwards why the paper sucks.
NEVER AGAIN!! its simple, DONT TAKE HIM!

NO NO NO NO!! if you want to have the worst experiance take him! i was so stupid not to have listened to this webstie in the begining! i seriously regret it. dont put yourself through his class if you dont have to!
Do not take his classes! Horrible, lots of work. Useless classes, you will have to write about philosophy... and not really learn how to read and write.

Please take my word seriously. If you are in his class, get out as soon as possible.  If it's too late, here are some advice.  Write much more than you should (if you want to get an A).  ex: If it's 8 page due, write 14+.  Next, KISS HIS ASS as much as possible.  Show him that you admire him and always ask for  help even though he gives you none.
Great professor. Work is extremely time-consuming and longer than most other classes, but truly pays. Get ready to write, but the writing is creative and fun.

i have never worked so hard in one class in my entire life. the grades you receive feel like they are lower than should have received. he is a really nice guy with a sense of humor.  I highly suggest getting out of this class if you can. way too much work for one class. way too hard. i was ALWAYS working on writing this semester. not fun.

Terrible, I have never seen in my live so much incompetence. Never and I repeat never have a class with him. He is insane. One essay need at least 20 hours work and you should do at least five of them in a semester. I'm writing  an encyclopedia of stupid things and this is a problem during busy weeks with really iportant subject.
hell no.....dont even set ur self up for failure!!!!!

Actually, a great teacher I though. Really pushes you. No bull sh*t type of guy, if you're not bright, he will let know, which is refreshing. If you see him during Office hours, he helps a lot. Bottom, if your intelligent and motivated . . .easy A, no problem. I'm a business major and I got an A.
AHHHH KELLY CLARKSON
what are you guys talking about? he is definately the best teacher ever!i mean come on, he cnat be that bad.....wow, thats a lie, he is the worst ever, i could brely control myself.

got a c- on the last paper, don't even think he read it. gives us 3 days to write 15 page papers while other classes are watching zombie movies and writing 3 page papers on them that they have a week to do, and yeah he does wear bow-ties.
In one word, no.

the guys loves assigning absurd amounts of work.  if you are scheduled for this class..switch out NOW. ive never had more work that doesnt make any sense. The worst part is even when you write a 30 page paper he doesnt even really grade it...just arbitrarily gives you anything under an A..and by the way bowties went out of style a LONG time ago
Too much rediculous work. Get out while you still can...and take off the damn bow tie.

He is definately the worst teacher ever, he doesnt care about you at all, or how much work he gives you.  he is an idiot who only cares about getting his point across and making himself look good. get out of taking his class if at all possible, again, he is the worst professor ever, and the bow ties are a freaking ratarded
noonan is insane, pretty much the worst prof ever. does not care about work load or clarity on when things are assigned/due.
No Comments

way too much work for engineers who dont care about writing in depth stories on what other people think they are trying to write baout.  I mena, teach me to write a lab report, not a freaking life story, dam those long papers
his ego is out of control. he thinks he's The Man but he's a pup.

DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS!!! I took it regardless of these ratings and it was terrible. He isn't knowledgeable and has a huge ego. He's also a terrible teacher and tries to make his office hours at times when noone will come. He also has no idea what he's teaching. Waste of time, didn't learn anything and hated him.

He is a challenging professor and expected more from my class than the other College Writing professors, but I'm glad I took his class because he pushed me and anyone who cares about developing their writing skills and being challenged should take him.
No Comments

He may be good in other classes, but DO NOT take him for Middler Year. He demands way more than any of the other profs & doesn't provide any concrete suggestions. I never knew what he wanted from me even after talking to him. Save yourself alot of stress and don't take this class with him.

dont ever take him for middler writing! middler writing is stressful as is and he doesnt make it any easier or less stressful or more helpful. he critiques without legitimate suggestions. very vague with his responses. huge power trip. dont take any class with him.

One of the best prof. I have ever had. He pushes you and holds you to a higher standard and good students appreciate that. The people who I have heard say negative things about his teaching style are, to put it mildly, not very bright. If you work hard, there is no reason to not get an A.
He sucks balls. please do not take him. you have been advised.
No Comments
This dude is horrible

Here's the ironic part. Class only met for 1-2 days a week on average out of 3.  Most classes were only 15-25 minutes long.  On the other hand, I did more work for this class than my others combined. Prof. Noonan was a lot of help during class. The course structure was presented well.
hes the man and hes so hot as balls

I had Noonan my freshman year and he was the hardest teacher I have EVER had, I learned so much from him, I would probably be a different person than I am today with out his constant pushing. Thanks Mr. Noonan!
He's so ****ing hot, and he is the bomb. Meet him in office hours and he is so nice, like Jeckyl and Hyde.

I thought he was a great professor.  He's very professional. He was very helpful, if you are the type of person who gets your work done, he'll be the best prof.  If you are a slacker you'll hate him.  He's always willing to help, but doesn't like excuses.
No Comments

Well, to be honest with you guys you really work over time for this class and you dont get the grades you deserve. Other freshmens dont have to work half as hard as us! they say he is the hardest professor! and i do agree.
No Comments
NEGATIVE! this professor licks nuts! honestly you write over 100pages in this course. Other students get to write 5-7page essays, while this professor desires 14-16! DONT TAKE THIS GUY!
great guy, somewhat hard work but cares for his students

I took his class 2 yrs ago, but i don't get why some ppl don't like him. He's def. a professional teacher who answers any Qs very coherently and I enjoyed being there..Top it off..he's one of the HOTTIEST prof. on campus!
No Comments
he is an ****! trust me!
Dont take this professor! Trust me! he is horrible!!!

Freshmen Class! Trust me do not take this professor. I write fairly well but he is a dick! Dont take this professor he makes the class much harder than others. He is Boring, and never gives "A"s. In general he sucks!

He is a really tough grader and the writing topics are ridiculous, and hard to get interested in. He's also a dick. Try to switch teachers if you get him, otherwise you're semester will suck writing 7 page papers in a style you will never use again.
He tries...but the assignments are all very hard. He is a tough grader too.
Terrible teacher. makes it so hard. does not do anything in class. Took eng2 with him. bad grader too...
Good guy, relates well with the students.
No Comments
she a sweetheart fr luh thus woman

I actually took this class last Spring. Such a sweetheart. Her class discussions are such a blast if you're willing to participate and she really cares about her students as people. What I remember from this class isn't the assignments but how I'd look forward to coming in and talking to her.

I loved Meredith! She is one of my favorite journalism professors. She really cares about her students and is really charming. You will learn a lot — she is great at teaching the foundations of journalism that every good and passionate journalist should know.

I ABSOLUTELY ADORE THIS PROFESSOR. She's such a baddie, and her class is my favorite this semester by a long shot. If you need a DD NUpath class TAKE THIS ONE. The way she presents journalism and real world issues in an engaging and digestible way is so fantastic, the class is also super easy, almost no homework. Cannot say enough good things!

She is just wonderful. Very knowledgeable and kind. Very interesting class content. She is very well rounded and intelligent. She is willing to always work with you and help you out. Love this class and this prof so much, probably my favorite professor ever.

One of the best teachers at this school. She's very caring, fair in her grading, and overall runs a great class. Not too much writing for being a journalism class. If the class is running, highly recommend taking it with her. Good nupath class as well.

Professor O'Brien is very passionate about journalism and provides great feedback and response to students' thoughts and ideas. There was a lot of material to read and analyze, both for in-class discussions and 5-7 page essay assignments. She does count for attendance and appreciates participation in discussions.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: She really cares about journalism and truly wants to see you grow as a writer. If you enjoy writing, which I assume you must to take this class, it should be easy and even fun. Her grading criteria was a bit unclear, but as long as you consider her feedback it should be fine. Genuinely learned a lot about journalism and writing in this class.
She's awesome! Professor clearly enjoys her job and grades very fairly. I learned a lot in the class!

Definitely my favorite professor at Northeastern so far. She is super caring and her class is not too difficult if you put some effort into the essays. The class is graded mostly on four 5-7 page essays, which O'Brien genuinely wants her students to succeed in. She has lots of practice in the field and is a great person to talk to.

Professor O'Kelly is extremely passionate about what she teaches and cares for her students, I loved the class towards the beginning of the semester, but the pace of the class became much faster when we got to more complex concepts...There's hw due every Sunday night, and there are quizzes in between midterms (she drops the lowest quiz)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Peggy is really kind and shows a lot of care for her students. Her explanations always break things down in really simple ways that are easy to understand. This was easily my favorite class this semester despite it being an 8 am. The 8 am part is kinda rough, but honestly I think if there is a professor worth taking that early it's definitely her.

I would die for her, quizzes are more exam "quizzam" as she calls em but like super fair where nothing is blindside but you should def know if you paid attention. Textbook lowkey doesn't matter if you can follow lectures and in class assignments. Show up only on lectures and solid 60 guarantee on any quiz TwT.

Don't take Acct 1209 asynch in a half summer class if you plan on doing anything else during those months. Condensing this regular class into 2x speed and offering it online seems nice in theory, however by the detailed math nature of the class, in-class Q/A time and days to study/learn the concepts is just missed in this format : (
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Lectures go by really slow and makes concepts harder than they are. Doesn't curve anything but will drop your lowest quiz score (only 4). Read the textbook and pray that's enough.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I love Prof. Peggy! The class itself is difficult (content-heavy and fast-paced), and my top recommendation is that you read the textbook before class lectures. Do practice problems as much as possible; that'll help most with quizzes and exams. Peggy is so sweet and extremely helpful with answering questions. Difficult class, but great professor!

Well-spoken, knowledgeable teacher who cares about her students and will take the requisite time to explain things personally. Lots of office hours, TA office hours, and stays after class. I found reading the chapter before class very helpful but I know a lot of people who didn't and still did well in the class. Would take again.

Professor O'Kelly's ACC1201 class is challenging but rewarding. She holds you accountable, but provides great resources. I found unstuck study really helpful for exam prep. Overall, a tough but worthwhile class.

Peggy O'Kelly was easily one of the best professors I have had at Northeastern. She is understanding of students' outside circumstances and makes herself easily available for help. The content of the course is difficult but she clearly outlines expectations and ways to succeed in her class. She genuinely wants her students to succeed.
Peggy is a great accounting teacher. FR2 is a pretty heavy course, but the exams/quizzes are easy if you can work through in class examples and the homework.

peggy was undoubtedly the best professor i've had at NU. im not math/finance-savvy but peggy held my hand and worked with me privately to make sure i succeeded as best i could. she is extremely supportive+a good prof. if you miss a lecture, you will fall behind. take good notes, and study the worksheets given in class bc its identical to the tests
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: FR2 is just hard. She's a great teacher and was very clear for what you need to know for the test. The class is very tough as it is the final acct class but it is rewarding to finish with her. The Project, Participation and HW grade act as a curve for your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This professor is really kind and she cares a lot about her students, however she's not a great teacher but her slides are good. The quizzes are really hard and long, so were the midterm and the final but there's a big curve.

Took her for 1201. there are 3 quizzes + 1 midterm + 1 final. ALL of that with homework due every Sunday and a 2 part group project. On top - attendance is marked and class participation matters. All exams are in person. She is a good teacher if you can keep up . You will miss homework at least one with no chances regrade.

Professor O'Kelly was the lecturer (not facilitator) for my online accounting class and was really sweet. I wish she was the facilitator (Annie Witte) as she was pretty brutal/condescending/would almost go out of her way to not help.
I failed ACCT 1201 the first time I took it, I got an A with Prof O'Kelly and it didn't even feel like trying. Amazing. 5 stars.

Lowkey a harder professor, but the best one you will receive. You need to know the information taught in the class for future classes. She also won't let you slack off so be prepared for her class. Class is curved a little bit and extra credit is offered.
Great teacher but the class was pretty difficult. She gives you a lot of resources to study and makes efficient use of class time.

ALOT of individual work, but I'm usually fine with that. However, she doesn't reall prepare you for the tests because they are always questioned weirdly, when you studied your ass off for them. She a nice person, but she talks rlly slow in class so context seems 1000x harder. I def think she could've prepared us alot better with practice questions.

Professor O'Kelly was a very good teacher and offers a lot of outside help. The class material is difficult and it is not a easy class, but it is explained well and there is lots of materials provided to help.

Professor O'Kelly was kind and caring. I left the class feeling like I learned a lot. She taught the material we needed for the exams, and didn't make us memorize ratios/ parts of the course we didn't need to know

There are many assignments and a lot of midterms and quizzes. She gives you the opportunity to receive good grades, but mainly if you study on your own and independently learn the topic. Her in class lectures are sometimes very brief and confusing. Overall amazing teacher and very patient. Goes out of her way to help a student if they need it.

There's a reason why there are so many great reviews of her. If I could have taken her for every single accounting class, I would've. I've never understood material the way I did when I took her class. Love her!!

I don't think I would have passed with any other professor. She is an extremely caring person and truly puts effort into every student. Her classes are a positive environment, and she will always be available to help you. She turned a relatively difficult class into a great learning experience. Love you peggy

peggy is so so sweet!! she is an amazing lecturer and explains complicated concepts super well. she is available outside of class to help and explain in depth. she held extra office hours before each exam. the material and tests are hard but fair and there is a participation grade too. no graded hw, but def do it for practice! 12/10 recommend!!

i love peggy!! she was so sweet and absolutely amazing at explaining concepts when we were confused. every class, she'd take the time to check in with almost every student as while we were doing the in-class problems. i genuinely learned so much. she had her funny moments too, a queen

Truly cares about her students and wants them to succeed. She's very responsive to her emails, and makes it a point to ensure the whole class knows both her and the TAs have office hours. She happily takes time to respond to any questions in class. Very organized. Homework is due weekly, and is through the texbook. Absolutely loved her!!
Very understanding and knowledgeable teacher. Grading standards are clear, and preparation for exams is done really well. One of my favorite accounting professors hands down.

Professor O'Kelly's class was so enjoyable and informative. She has a ton of experience in accounting and it definitely shows through her lectures. She is a sweet, caring woman that really wants to see her students succeed and will take the time to make sure that students really understand important concepts in the course, truly a great professor

She is such a great professor. She inspired me to add a concentration in accounting to my major. She is so understanding and really wants to see her students succeed. She loves participation and fosters a safe talking environment.

Professor Peggy cares about her students. I personally dislike accounting, but having her didn't make it worse. I'd recommend. Accessible outside of class. When we were remote, our homework was due at 11:59PM on Saturday, thus not promoting a healthy school/life balance, which I really didn't appreciate. Other than that, only good things to say!

Great teacher. Really brings up the value of our school. Responds to emails quickly, understanding of different situations, and truly cares about her students. 1 homework assignment a week in two parts, but really helps learn and is just like the quizzes and midterms. Love her! If you want a good environment to learn online and in person, she's it!

Peggy is amazing. You have to buy a classpack which has all the slides and is way better than the textbook. Homework is very manageable. Only one group project, which isn't too hard. Lectures are very helpful and she will remember your name and answer your questions. Must take!

I currently have professor Kelly and she's honestly one of the sweetest teachers I've met. You can tell she really cares about both her job and the students. The class itself also isn't hard. As long as you follow the lectures, do the worksheets that we do in class anyway, and the required homework assignments, you should be able to get a good grad
I really enjoyed taking the class with Peggy. I find her to be very inspirational and a great teacher! She has made me want to pursue accounting!

I had professor O'Kelly for Financial Accounting and she was amazing. Accounting can be difficult to learn for the first time and she does a great job communicating complex topics and making sure you are prepared for the exams. You have to work for an A, but it's definitely possible if you show up and do the work
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Don't take her class. Tough grader.

She puts up videos and her TA's are amazing they reply instantly. It's a good introductory course to fin acct. I suggest taking it online as it pushes you to actually learn but at the same time have the confidence of support from your TA.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Peggy is very caring and accommodating with her office hours. She even offers extra help if you are willing to learn. I took 2 courses with her and absolutely she wants her students to succeed and offers her best! Thank You Professor Peggy!

The tests in the class are difficult. Results aren't great, but I understood the material. Never really had to use the textbook because everything outside of the textbook could be used and were more helpful. Prof and TAs weren't very helpful. I recommend taking another prof.

Financial accounting is the most difficult class I've had at Northeastern, but I'm glad I took it with Peggy O'Kelly. She's an excellent teacher who makes you appreciate the topic, helps you learn, extends generous office hours, and offers lots of material to study from. Still, don't expect accounting to be easy, even with a great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor O'Kelly is the best professor I have had at Northeastern thus far! She is very articulate and clear in her lectures, and makes it a point to solidify our understanding with worksheets in class and Connect homework outside of class. She is helpful with office hours and she's extremely nice. Her tests and grading is fair!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She was a great professor. She knew what she was doing and explained things well and really wants you to pass. She gives you a lot of optional things to practice with which is really helpful for the final. Her class is top notch.
It was a fairly easy class but not taking anything away from the professor. was recommended by a friend am glad i took her class. explains well and comes prepared.

Best professor I've had at NU. Take her class! She teaches in a way that makes the material so much more understandable than it is in the textbook. Go to her office hours and homework help sessions if you are struggling and she will focus on the areas that you are struggling with. Focus on her class problems to do well on tests.

Peggy is the best accounting teacher there is! She really knows the information inside out and is extremely helpful. All the accounting sections learn the same thing and have the same types of quizzestests, but there is no question that she teaches the material the best. She is the most professional teacher I've ever had! Definitely recommend!

Accounting shouldn't be taken online, and Professor O'Kelly herself seems very uncomfortable with the format. That said, it's laid out fairly well, with reminders for all the homework that needs to be completed, etc etc. Her lectures don't fully cover the homework you are expected to complete, and you have to do online research on your own to do it
She's an amazing accounting teacher! Loved her class. Takes things step by step and gives examples to follow each lecture.
Professor O'Kelly has a great teaching style! I felt like I was learning the material well in class and then having that knowledge reinforced by the online work was really helpful.
quiz and exam are hard

She's absolutely wonderful! Explains things clearly and her in-class lectures and exercises are so helpful. She doesn't put anything surprising on her tests and quizzes. If you put forth the effort you should do pretty well.
Best professor I have had at Northeastern. Her classes are interesting and easy to sit through. You have to take accounting you should absolutely take it with her.
Best professor in acct department. Very clear and helpful. Three exams and four quizs (drop the lowest one probably). Just come to class and do the hw problems you should be fine.
she is a charming person and a very helpful professor
She is a good techer, her explanations are clear, I don't even need to use a textbook! I like this class, it is realtively easy for me

One of the best teachers I have ever had I would recommend her to anyone! she is so clear in the way that she teaches and breaks the class up between problem sets and lecture. She really cares about her students understanding the material. I never used the textbook and still got an A. She's the best!!

She is a good teacher, but you have to try in the class. There are online homeworks and online quizzes every week, which wasn't that bad. The midterm and final were all right, but you absolutely had to study. We also had 2 "Problem Sets" that are just problem packets. I would recommend her.

Don't get tricked into thinking this is an easy A. While the homework is pretty straightforward and easy, the midterm/exams completely were a stark surprise... I mean since it's online you tend to slack but people getting below 70% on the exams were pretty much the norm.. Not an easy A

Great professor who is very knowledgeable in her subject. Accounting is not something fun to me, but she made class interesting with handouts. Connect Assignments are challenging, but she makes the "Check My Work" button available, so that you can check your answers as many times as you want. Tests and quizzes are challenging.

Great professor, best one I had last semester. Very passionate about accounting, explains all the topics very thoroughly and prepares handouts for each lesson to practice what went over during the class. Go to class and pay attention to what she says, and you shouldn't have problems with the material. Takes attendance, so don't skip the classes.
outstanding
great teacher recommendable if you go to class your fine.

Pay Attention and you'll do fine. Not an easy subject,and if you fall behind its EXTREMELY hard to get back up to speed. Not a class to take if your looking for an easy grade. Tough grader on tests/quizzes, never gives a curve or extra credit. Requires a good deal of outside studying to keep up. She knows the material better than any other teacher.
Do the work and pay attention in class and it is an easy class.  She is very clear and cares about her students.

I had her for Managerial Accounting. Her lectures are a bit dry but I liked the class exercises. She hands out exercise papers and gives time during class to do them. And then you go over it. It helped me learn a lot.

Professor O'Kelly is awesome, and anyone who says otherwise is jaded by a bad grade. She very clearly lays out complicated material and offers office hours all the time to help anyone who needs it. She is genuinely interested in helping her students learn and is highly recommended.

Comes across as fake (I see right past her smile) and makes you feel uncomfortable to ask questions.  Also, unclear as to her expectations.  Her explanations are good but tacky.  Clearly rehearses before class and she often makes mistakes on the board which is evidence of her studying her examples without understanding them.
Reads her power point way too much and doesnt work enough problems. I can read the powerpoint myself.  Really nice, but could be so much better if she actually did problems in class and not read.
Great professor, highly recommend. I put in little to no effort and was able to understand all of the material and get an A.

she's not jaded like most profs. im probably getting a C+ in this class and i still like her(class avg is about a b+). don't fall behind on reading if it's a summer class. she'll go the extra mile for you, ex: she customized an explanation for me on her own time and gave it to me the next class. the material is challenging, but you should take her.

Very Clear. Perfect amount of hw, follow the syllabus and it's easy  to keep on top of it. Quizzes/Tests are similar enough to class exercises, no curve balls there. Extremely helpful, extremely nice, and makes sure you know the TA so that resource is available as well.
Clear and concise

Probably the most happiest professor I've seen at northeastern. She loves to teach, extremely friendly, and helpful. The class is much harder than Intermediate accounting 1, so be prepared to do a lot of work. She always curves. Two exams, 1 final, participation, quizzes.

Professor O' Kelly is a very helpful, crystal clear professor. Her mood in class is always positive, encouraging students to participate and making them comfortable asking questions. She also reminds the free tutoring sessions offered by the ACCT department. She also gives quizzes regularly, making sure no one falls behind.
Had her for both financial and managerial accounting.  Very good professor, nice person.  Explains topics very clearly.
Honestly one of the best classes I've taken at Northeastern solely because of Prof O'Kelly. She is very clear, always willing to help, and makes the material interesting.

Very good. Recommended! She is the best accounting teacher at Northeastern University. Very clear and concise. You just need a good logic and you'll be fine. Practice the exercises though, this will help you a lot in the quizzes and exams!

Lectures are clear and concise.  Homework assignments do not take long to complete and helps a lot with understanding.  Reading the textbook before class helps, but she explains everything so well that you'll still understand it even if you don't read.  Reading the text helps with tests though, which are not hard if you study a little bit.

Best prof ive had so far. She really wants you to succeed and is very understanding. I enjoyed going to class and she taught the material very effectively and simplified things. Quizzes and tests were challenging but if you go to class, keep up on the HW, and follow the review sheets and sessions she gives, you will do great in the class.

SO easy, but incredibly boring. You will definitely get a good grade, but you have to be prepared for excruciatingly long, dull lectures.  She always had us do practice problems in class, and gave us 20 min to work them through... and then would work through them completely on the board...so its like doing the same problem twice and its painful.

did not learn anything from this professor- taught all the material to myself... has a nice personality but doesnt seem interested in helping studetnts succeed. you really have to pay attention in class and even if you do its still hard to understand the material.

Prof. O'Kelly is so nice! She is willing to help you out as much as you need. She really wants you to do well in the class, and explains concepts very well. Exams are fair. Work hard and you'll get an A.
Best Professor at Northeastern. Goes beyond just teaching the textbook, makes class interesting and legitmately cares about making sure you're learning what you need to know. She ROCKS!

She's a good teacher. She gives good explainations and she really is concerned about your best interests. However, her Group Field Project (Group Project) is almost a waste of time because its a lot of work and its only worth 10% of your grade.
She's an excellent professor; this is the second time I've had her. Exams are fair, reviews are comprehensive, and she's just a great professor. Highly recommended.
No Comments

BEST professor at northeastern (and not just because she was easy on the eyes)... ONLY O'Kelly could make auditing interesting.  Best thing to do: make sure to ask her for a review before the test and fill out the study guide she posts BEFORE the review so you can check your answers with hers.  I had NO experience with auditing. O'Kelly's awesome!
Fabulous professor. Learned a lot and it wasn't difficult at all!
blah.....don't believe the comments written above
Awesome and amazing, great person with a great personality. I got a great grade all because of her!
No Comments
No Comments
Super Hott!!! kept me interested in class but might have led to my terrible grade
No Comments
She was an excellent professor; I loved her class. She made the subject so easy and you can tell she loves what she teaches. I definately recommend her!

She is a very nice person and very fair grader. It's not the easiest class but everything she requires can be done. I ended up with a pretty good grade and I didn't do well on everything so just show up for class and try your best and she will make sure you get a good grade.
awesome prof, she relays her information very clearly.  really nice lady, respects hard workers and made accounting somewhat interesting
Great teacher, easy to understand and very helpful. Take her class if you have the chance.

Fairly good class.  Class is not too dificult, however O'Kelly doesn't make it easier.She is one of those professors who simply just reads out of the book,well in her case a powerpoint presentation.Doesn't articulate on much.Her TA is this french man who speaks very poor English so that doesn'help.
She's pretty good. She's not super easy. but if you keep up with the work you'll get a pretty good grade.

She is a good teacher but the way she teaches was more in a presentation format just going by he PowerPoint slides and not really using the book. Plus she only gives 2 quizzes so you don't have much to work with if you do poorly.  I was not satisfied.

She is simple and plain, a great teacher, If you want to learn, take this class, just read, do your work and pay attention and you will do fine.  I have no idea, why that guy on the bottom wrote that about her, maybe he never went to class. I got an A
Peggy is very bubbly which is great for 8AM class when everyone is tired.  Really knows the material and teaches it well. Burps alot.
Easy, plain and simple, watch out for her misbuttoned top though.
No Comments

Bad structure of course and tons of micro management. Murmuring all the time and could not explain even the most basic OOD concept clearly. Also, expect to use some tech stack in HW that has never been taught before.

Be prepared for a ego-driven classroom that is filled with micro management. The professor expects 100% compliance and will call people out for not paying attention, yet the content being taught has no actual value. You'll spend more time managing the professor's 'expectations of respect' than actually gaining skills in the subject.

I took CS5150 (Game AI) in summer 2024 at Seattle. The professor faced a family emergency, impacting his teaching quality. He canceled several classes and struggled to prepare enough material. I bombed a few assignments due to lack of guidance. Thankfully, he allowed me to redo HWs and was generally flexible. I hope his situation has improved.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: she puts the moan in moana
oana bin laden

Veliche is a sweet lady and all, but she teaches old-fashioned with a whiteboard and while half of the class is fighting demons trying to copy down notes, she mumbles to herself proofs that no one understands. this course is too proof-heavy which means self-studying and practicing is a must to get a good grade. This class made me lose love for math

I have never done so bad in a math class. This class was conducted poorly due to the professor's old fashion teaching and only writing on the whiteboard. The entire class time is spent frantically copying down notes on the board instead of understanding concepts. There are also daily homework to be done as a group.

(MATH 2331) Overall good professor and clear lectures.  There is optional homework but if you want an A you should definitely do it.  I managed to get a B without really studying at all and just reviewing before exams, you just need to know how to apply formulas.  Maybe there is a bit more proof stuff in this section compared to other professors.

not the best during lectures, but she gives us a lot of material to work with and if you do optional textbook homeworks as well as the practice quizzes you will be fine for the weekly quiz. preparing for the midterms is also easy as she gives several past midterms to practice with.
She was fire.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Oana is a very nice and caring person and very available via email. Maybe she's just not my style, but her teaching style is just extremely old fashioned. We have exit tickets, notes are often not posted, and she seems to not be fully prepared to explain her thought process in lectures. Lots of arrows with vague labels. I just read the textbook.

ok she's a nice person but she REALLY CANNOT EXPLAIN AT ALL!!! skips over a lot of explanations and jumps around, you will be completely confused about her lectures if you don't already know linear algebra well or take tutors outside of class. Again she's a really nice person but I'm just not sure she qualifies as a math professor
Fantastic professor, very available via email, very caring.

Took me some time to get used to, but I grew to love the class. She is a really sweet person and has your best interest. I would recommend going to class, but she does post handwritten notes and has helpful office hours. The weekly quizzes are a good reflection of the work done in class and past years quizzes are given for ample practice.

Lectures were always recorded but attendance was encouraged because we had graded class problems that were done in small groups. She is a very caring and kind professor, responds to emails quickly, and offered generous extra credit. Sometimes its a little hard to follow her notes but she is always accessible for extra help.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Oana gets some hate, but I honestly dont see why. Her lectures covered everything you needed to know for quizzes and tests, and she did lots of examples in class. Homework is optional and she gives lots of extra credit. She really wants you to succeed on tests and quizzes, so she gives plenty of review and tells you exactly what you need to know.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: nice person but very confusing explanations especially in the first 3rd of the class when talking about gradients. gradewise, doing the homework will result in a good grade even if you dont understand why anything works

Her lectures could not be followed. Professor Veliche taught mostly on the whiteboard, and did not have a logical train of thought for solving problems or lecturing. She would just jump around not explaining the problem well. That being said, she is a very kind and caring person and obviously cares about the course.

She's a really sweet woman but she's definitely not the best at teaching. Homework load is the same as other professors (not mandatory BUT appear on the tests/quiz). Her grading can be pretty strict but she definitely doesn't want you to fail. She's given us take homes after we all bombed a quiz before. Love the woman but wouldn't take her again.
She is a very nice lady, but also a very tough grader. Heavy homework and hard exams
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She is a lovely woman, but not a good professor.  Her lectures are unclear and she does not answer question adequately at all.  I would have done a lot better in this class if I had a different professor.  She made me not like calculus anymore because I could not understand it.

This class (regardless of professor) is really tough. However, Prof. Veliche does her best to teach you all the material clearly. Extremely welcoming & helpful in office hours. At the end of the semester she gives extra points for overall attendance and homework (asks you show her all you have done in the semester). Gives practice tests.

She's a very nice woman who is always willing to help. That being said, she is not the best teacher. She sometimes lacks understanding of the material, and students often correct her in class. She does not explain material very clearly, and her thick accent sometimes makes things difficult to understand. However, she does want students to do well.

Veliche is a nice woman but an absolutely horrible teacher. She does not explain the material efficiently or effectively often leaving students unprepared for the final exam. Avoid taking her at all costs if you can.
She is friendly and nice but  did not show command over the material during teaching.
She's good at math, but she's not very good to teach. Confuses the majority of the students.
I KNOW THAT SHE DEFINETLEY WROTE PAST REVIEWS. LOOK AT THE RECENT ONES. THEY ARE ALL ACCURATE SHE IS A 1 OR 2 RATING. MADE ME WANT TO GIVE UP AND DROP OUT. SHE IS HELLEN KELLER FR

as a professor for chem major orgo, oye is a GREAT professor-- her lectures are engaging and with organized notes and her tests are SO FKING HARD but as a major, yk this is what i signed up for. i can see how ppl who arent here for chemistry dont like her bc you really have to sell your soul to get an A, but also watching breaking bad in class aha

Tests are similar to textbook problems and review sessions. There weren't any questions that were a surprise, and nothing on exams stood out as harder than anything I had seen on practice problems.

Chads Prep Orgo playlist on YouTube was very helpful.

words cannot even begin to describe how much i suffered in her class, you will need to be the luckiest person alive or the next einstein to pass this class with higher than a B+. she claims she is generous with partial credit, I am yet to see it. whoever takes her will be in my thoughts and prayers.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was genuinely the hardest I've ever taken in my entire life. Her teaching style didn't stick for me, I really tried to learn from her but she lectured very fast and didn't encourage questions. So I had to teach myself the content outside of class. If you have to take her, I recommend just doing as many practice problems as possible.

Have you ever wanted to meet Hellen Keller? If so, take this class and meet her for yourself. You will not be heard, seen, nor will she communicate with you well. Tough grader and expects you to know to the level of PhD organic chemistry to get an A on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Run don't ever think of taking her class
first time withdrawing lmao. i have a 3.8 GPA i cant afford a C
Already had past trauma from Sadie Piatt and now I have her. its not funny
It takes everything within me to not crash out on her every time im in class. something def happened to her and now shes taking it out on her students cause she miserable

My dad died 3 days before the final & when WeCare reached out to her she said I should still take it. I had to do her homework assignment the night he died. I took an exam with a concussion since she wasn't flexible on extensions. Very cold, people got 8s, 9s, & 11s on her exams. Honestly probably the harshest professor i've ever had. DON'T DO IT!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Holy grade distribution. The max on our last exam was an 88...
She must have been a different person a couple of years ago because she had wayyy too many 5s back then. theyre all 1 or 2 ratings now lmao
If you want an exam on halloween then this is the perfect class for you!
Do not take her.
If you care about your GPA, take any other professor
I KNOW for a fact she wrote herself reviews to keep her rating up. If you look at the past reviews, they all have the same style of writing. her real rating is closer to a 1-2...

I dont understand the idea of a professor being "nice," but having the worst grading structure ever. She does not care about her students. If she did, her averages would not be as low as they are. If averages are low, thats a reflection on HER and her unbelievable expectations, not the students.
Absolutely horrendous. She doesn't even tell us the averages of her exams because they're so bad. She is also the type of professor to think the students are the problem and NOT her.
90% your grades are exams btw.

sure shes a good at teaching material but she acts like you have all the time in the world to study for ORGO. she said you only need to study 30 min per night but expects you to read the textbook, do 50 textbook problems (each problem has 10 parts), watch extra mandatory lectures, do hw assignments, and attend review sessions + practice sessions.

Don' take this class this was genuinely one of the toughest professors I've had because of the grading, workload, and the atmosphere she creates. She has laughed at students questions and makes you feel dumb for not understanding chemistry topics she flies through. I practically taught myself orgo 1 without much help other than lectures and reviews
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Run. Take literally anyone else, she's awful. I put in a disgusting amount of time and effort into this class and only managed a B. You have to teach yourself everything. Her grading structure is significantly worse than other teachers, 90% of grade is exams (4) and 10% is homework (which is graded on accuracy).
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Oyelaren is honestly an amazing teacher. Yes, her tests are hard, but it's because she wants you to REALLY understand. You cannot simply memorize practice problems- she really emphasizes the “why” while we're learning. I'm taking her class for orgo 2 bc of how much I loved both her teaching and her lecture note shells.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Probably the hardest class I will ever take in college. This class is no joke. Treat every resource as mandatory, pay attention, and don't rely on "I'll get it on the test when it comes up." Oyelaran is super smart, but she doesn't mess around. Pro tip: she curves you higher if she knows you, so go to every problem-solving session. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is the coolest and smartest woman I've ever met but these exams will HUMBLE you. she throws the meanest and most merciless curveball south of fenway. do not take her if you don't have lots of free time to dedicate to studying. As long as you do practice problems and try to take extra time to understand concepts you will do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Oyelaran is very knowledgeable, however exams are not fair and are much more difficult compared to any homework and textbook problems. The class average was consistently between 55%-68%. I have never been spoken to or dismissed by a professor ever before in my life like her. DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS with her if you want a good GPA.

Do not take this her for Orgo 1. Her lectures are difficult to follow and her teaching style is rapid and not in depth. Her tests are extremely difficult. Regardless of how well you understand the material her exams will shock you. 3 tests 1 final and online homework worth 10%, that's all you get. I guarantee there are better professors.

reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretend that every resource she gives you is mandatory!! The exams are not easy, but the grading is super lenient!!

Stay away if you value your mental health. She's so so horrible. I put in so much work and effort but never ever got a solid grade. entering that classroom felt like entering a warzone.

reviews.comment: I liked professor Oyelaran. I learned a lot from her class even though it was the hardest class I've ever taken. reviews.comment: Get ready bruv. i'd rather go to church everyday and be a farmer then take the class again
