reviews.comment: don't recommend unless you truly have an interest for organic chem and aren't just taking it as a requirement.

LOVE Oye! She is the best prof. I've ever had. So inspiring and knowledgable, time with her is always enjoyable. Her class is hard, but she definitely gives you what you need to succeed. The exams were brutal, and all practice problems must be done for any chance of success. Huge time commitment, but I found the class very fun and learned so much.

Professor Oyelaran is a very knowledgable professor who wants her students to take something away from her class. Her class is very hard, so you need to be keeping up with all the material, going to office hours, asking questions, reading the textbook, and do all the practice problems she assigns. You need to also have a solid orgo 1 foundation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: extremely condescending and not helpful to a lot of questions in class. does not go through problems and lectures have guided notes but are not very helpful for exams. do ALL of the textbook problems to pass. Exams are fully open response and always involve orgo 1 concepts.

Professor Oyelaran can come off as intimidating (and tbh does sometimes demean students for their questions) but is overall incredibly knowledgeable and helpful. Will take the time to explain what you need in office hours, gives tons of practice problems, and the exams are difficult, but fair, and she gives great feedback. Would recommend.

Professor Oyelaran is the most difficult professor I have ever had. She definitely knows her stuff, but gives the harshest exams ever. I did all the homework, group homework, practice problems, and attended review sessions, and never received a solid exam grade. Oyelaran is very unforgiving and inflexible. Would not recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Oyelaran is a very smart professor, but honestly, her class was the worst. Her tests were unnecessarily difficult and she gave almost no partial credit. She is very accessible outside of class and does a great job of making you understand, but you could put in hours of studying and still fail. This class was a gpa killer
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was the most difficult class I have ever taken. She is also very unapproachable for help in the class. The exams are unnecessarily difficult, and she is a very tough grader. It was also virtually impossible to know what to expect on the exams. Your only chance at getting a decent grade is if you have already taken an organic chemistry class.

By far the best professor I've ever had. Also by far the toughest class I've ever taken. She hands you the tools you need to master organic chemistry and get an A in the class; that said, you have to take them, which is easier said than done. You will do practice problems until you drop, but you'll come out having a rock solid understanding or orgo
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I'm torn about this class. She's a great person and I liked lectures. Her exams are ridiculously hard. I did the practice problems, and still barely scraped by. I would be in worse shape if I hadn't done the practice problems for sure, but they're not indicative of her exams and each exam was like a slap in the face.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I believe she is the best orgo professor in the school. The class is very difficult but you will come out with a solid understanding of organic chemistry. She gives lecture note shells that you follow along with as she lectures. She goes through everything very clearly. You have to do the practice problems but if you put in the work you learn alot.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If any one got a C in her class, it was probably because they didn't do the practice problems *face palm.* It's simple, attend lectures and do the assigned practice problems; test material is based off them. At most, I studied 1-2 hours a night five days prior to the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Lectures are useless if you haven't read the book prior to class. She's an incredibly tough grader with even harder tests. I took orgo before taking her class and it was still really hard. She over-complicates things and you will probably get a better grade if you simply study the textbook. Would not recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Not the worst professor, but definitely isn't the best for Orgo. Tests are very hard, harder than the other professors that teach Orgo.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: LOVE Oyelaran! Very intelligent and confident, can make her seem intimidating but shes very approachable. Shes SUPER committed to ensuring that you actually understand the material, great lectures, problem solving sessions, lots of resources. The practice problems are a lot but you gotta just do em. Her lectures are great!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very intelligent and clear lectures. Difficult class, with hard exams though. Class is very passable but you need to go to class. She provides great lecture shells to study off of but attendance is necessary to fill them out. If you want to actually learn orgo, take her.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Oyelaran is slightly intimidating, but she's a great professor. She explains difficult concepts in terms that are easy to understand, and frequently asks students questions to make sure we understand the material. The exams are difficult, but the final grade is curved and there is also homework and group work that should boost your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Awesome Professor. Takes the time in office hours to go over anything you do not understand. Her lectures, especially the type of lectures she does, are remarkable. She uses shell notes which are extremely helpful for taking notes. Her tests are difficult and the final is near impossible. A ton of practice is necessary but you can see results.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Man, oh man, Oyelaran does not mess around. Her class was super tough and intimidating and exams were difficult, but doable. I learned how to think critically and I have a great Orgo foundation now. Oyelaran rewards you if you work hard. Also the fedoras she wears are dope.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Oyelaran facilitates the difficult process of learning  Organic Chemistry very well. While the tests are basically the only component of the overall course grade and are very difficult, you always come out of them realizing how much you learned. Accompany that with a generous curve and you get a surprisingly enjoyable and rewarding class.

She really knows her stuff and I like her style of note taking. You draw structures along with her as she lectures. She no longer puts her lectures on Tegrity so if you skip class, you miss LOTS of notes. Tests are extremely hard but a 50% gets you a C.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Oyelaran is AMAZING. She's incredibly smart and pushes her students to fully understand the content.

She teaches well, and her lecture style is great. She gives you lecture outlines that you fill out during class (you need colored pens - you're going to do A LOT of color coding) and uploads her lecture recordings on Tegrity. HOWEVER, her exams (2 midterms, 25 Q, multiple choice) can be difficult and her final is simply IMPOSSIBLE. Be ware.

Oyelaran is great. After taking gen chem I and II with toher, I found that Oyelaran was a little clearer, possibly as a result from her great notes system. She also puts every lecture on tegrity, to worst case scenario, if you cant make it to class, you can catch up on tegrity. Very clear, knows her stuff, and expects you to care.

Oyelaran is tough and intimidating in a 1-to-1 conversation, but she is a nice person and is a great teacher. Her notes system (she calls them shells) make everything SO much easier to understand, and it forces you to pay attention because you have to write things out in between spaces. Tests aren't too bad but they are very tricky so be careful.
GO to class, and office hours!

Professor Oyelaran is THE BEST organic chemistry professor that I know of at Northeastern. She is an amazing teacher and explains everything well. Her tests are challenging, but not impossible. She can be a little intimidating in office hours, so make sure you have good questions prepared when you go that you absolutely cannot find the answers to.

Professor Oyelaran was the best teacher I've had at Northeastern University!  I had her both for organic chemistry 1 and 2, and each semester she was extremely effective and taught the material amazingly well.  She focuses on having you understand the principles over memorizing them. You learn the material a lot better that way -  A+ professor

Dr. O makes herself really available to help. She's one of the only teachers I know who has office hours daily and review sessions weekly. Excellent teacher. Highly recommend her for orgo 2 especially.
she is not that good of a teacher but she is the best for orgo1. The best thing she does is that she gives out handouts for every class so you wouldnt have to write everything.

I had her for orgo1. I was so nervous going into the class because I heard the horrors of Organic Chem. She made the class interesting and easy to follow. She even provides outlines of her notes! To do good in the class, you really need to do practice problems and study her notes.

OChem is one of the most difficult classes in college. Professor Oyelaran is one of the best professors I've had so far. Very clear, informative lectures. Very caring about students, and gives all support for success. Nonetheless, she expects you to work. She definitely made OChem 2 interesting for me. Great experience.

Professor Oyelaran has a very clear, concise method of teaching, making her an efficient Organic Chemistry teacher. The course is as tough as you can expect Orgo to be but she gives you what you need to succeed. The exams I found were a bit tricky, but she sets the "B" range at the average of the class. Overall, a good professor.

The curriculum lacked clarity, especially around the paid online simulation. We were expected to “figure it out” without foundational instruction, and questions about how it worked were not clearly answered. Lectures felt disconnected from Canvas and the Sim. The 5 weeks of intense group work were challenging, and overall the course felt disjointed
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Amazing lecturer. That is about the only positive thing about him. I would say "avoid," but he is the only one teaching this required class, so "good luck." He is not reasonable. When he admits to mistakes on grading he still won't change the score. He is not rationale. Has a god-like complex and thinks he just has higher standards than others.

Dr. Odom is awesome!! Super knowledgeable in consulting and shares a lot of his personal experience. He's really down to earth and treats his students as equals. Class is very discussion and activity heavy. Few things make up your grade like the simulation and a couple presentations, so try to do good in that. 10/10 recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Inspirational--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I enjoyed Professor Odom's class. Throughout the semester, the professor uniquely integrated AI, and students learned how to write clearer prompts. To be transparent, the simulation is a significant portion of your grade, so I would try to get comfortable with it and ask questions earlier on.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you have a bad group in this class you will have a very difficult semester. The amount of work you put in does not reflect your grade. Unless this class is require I would not recommend.

I really enjoyed Professor Odom's class this semester and would say he is one of my favorite professors in DMSB! He recently restructured the course to include AI and is extremely receptive to feedback. The group airline simulation project is a lot of work, and it rewards detailed analysis and group decision-making. I recommend Prof. Odom

Professor Odom is genuinely one of the best DMSB professors! He really embraced the use of AI during class, gave really engaging lectures, and he is very passionate about what he teaches. The group simulation is a fair amount of work but so worthwhile, and he was always checking in on our progress throughout the semester. 100% recommend taking!

Professor Curtis cares, knows what he is talking about, and is passionate. He has experience and has been in the industry which provides stories, lessons and advice throughout the course. Heavily weighted on an airplane simulation & presentations that if not done well, can impact grade severely with minimal individual grades to make up for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Odom is easily one of my favorite professors I have had at Northeastern. He has completely restructured this course to remove exams and papers. It is heavy group project based and he has very clear grading criteria and a clear syllabus. He integrates AI into the course well and really wants student input into making the course better.

Professor Odom is awesome. The course is well-run, with a clear focus on preparing you for your professional life. There is an emphasis on AI as well. The course requires thought and organization more than it is difficult, so stay on top of things. There aren't any exams; it is all presentations and the airline simulation. Highly recommend.

Professor Odom is an amazing professor! His teaching style is structured very well and there are many opportunities to ask questions and learn from his real world experiences. He is very pro AI usage and always willing to meet with students outside the classroom setting to talk about consulting or give career advice. Everyone should take his class!

Professor Odom is great. You can really tell he has practical consulting knowledge through his own hands on work in consulting and previous work experiences. The course is not necessarily hard but there are many weekly timelines/deadlines. Be ready to organize yourself and work ith a team to successfully address simulation and presentations.

Enjoyable class. Professor Odom is upbeat, interesting, and treats students like adults. Forward on AI.

Class curates an authentic experience by putting students into groups to work through a realistic simulation. Students are graded on their team's simulation performance and several client presentations, rather than papers/tests.

I mean he really is a good Professor. Yeah it's a lot of work, but if you come to him for help, he'll work with you. He is also very receptive to feedback. Would take again, I would just recommend taking the class during a lighter semester

Professor Odom's humor and personality made the class enjoyable, and I always looked forward to it. He treats students like adults and values feedback. He can be inflexible on grades and seeing alternative perspectives on assignments, but he is consistent with his expectations. It was a fun, challenging class and a good way to end my last semester.

Although he can be pretty hard to reason with at times, he's genuinely a good professor. He has a lot of experience and he is a good person to ask about anything regarding post-grad plans. If you don't communicate or reach out to him, you most likely won't do good. Just talk to him regularly and you will be fine, don't worry.

This professor and this class are by far one of my favorite classes at Northeastern. This class is tough, and it isn't going to be easy, but it's gratifying if you attend class and pay attention. I recommend this class to everyone that I know, and I wish that this professor would teach more classes because I would take a class with him again.

EXTREMELY harsh grader - avoid if you care about GPA. Going forward this class has a lot of proposed changes that will make it even harder. He treats you like an adult, but is really inflexible with grades and deadlines. His class had a lot of potential to be more enjoyable, he's a fun lecturer. Wish my grade reflected the time and effort I put in.

Typically felt like meaningless busy work. Would NOT recommend.The whole semester you are working with the same group, and he offers no peer evaluations so if you get a bad group, you're screwed.I was hoping to get some real consulting experience in this course, but instead, I left with nothing but a lower GPA to show for it. RUN.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
One of the least understanding professors. Very tough grader and doesn't ever give partial credit on any of the assignments. 
Don't listen to the comments saying he provides ACTUAL consulting experience since this semester he switched to the simulation which has nothing to do with real consulting. 
Overall it was a useless class that tanked my GPA

Professor Odom creates a collaborative and dynamic work environment that enables students to absorb the material long-term. He will challenge you as a professional while providing support and autonomy throughout the semester (Make sure to take detailed notes and try not to miss class lectures, the info will be important for your deliverables).

Professor Odom makes his lectures very interesting and gives students a lot of autonomy throughout the semester. The course is extremely back heavy so it is important to keep up to date with assignments and manage your time properly. Tip: make sure to take notes during class (especially things Professor says 2x) and make sure to include in papers.

Prof. Odom is an engaging lecturer that prioritizes how useful the class is for students. We had "reading days" to work on our group project or written assignments, which were helpful in balancing this class with other responsibilities. Working with real clients is also a great experience! Grading is tough at first, but gets better.

Professor Odom was always available outside of class to meet and provide any support needed for the client project. He is very passionate about the class and has a lot of previous knowledge from his experiences. I felt like I learned a lot because of his teaching style and his deep experience in the field. His lectures are worth coming to class.

Amazing Professor! Professor Odom is a great lecturer and makes each class not only educational but also very fun to listen to and participate in. There are a few papers in this course, but he gives plenty of time to prepare, I would advise starting each paper a week in advance though so you're not rushing at the end.
Would not recommend this class. Gives no instructions when you meet him one on one to ask what he expects on the paper says just follow rubric. All the other comments about him are completely true
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The worst professor I met in NEU. His feedback to your papers totally useless (use same comments to students have the same grade). It's great that you can engage with real-world clients. However, it has nothing to do with the professor. Without the knowledge you learned from the course, you can still perform well in client project. No curve.
Opportunity to work directly with organizations as consultants!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I don't get why people claim he's a bad professor. The class offers a lot to apply to real world, the podcasts really get you thinking about how you choose your career, both as a consultant and otherwise. I feel like if you really pay attention to what he says and the things assigned, you learn a lot more and find this class is absolutely great.

Curtis is a disrespectful professor that acts like he cares about his students but won't even take the time to meet and discuss grading issues. Very unclear with his grading and is unreasonable when you challenge his feedback. Overall, one of my worst class experiences, and I regret taking this class. This class makes me not want to do consulting.

He really cares about the course and is able to bring relevant real world experience to his lectures. The grade is heavily weighted in a few large assignments (a paper and the final exam being worth 60% of the grade). He is in interesting and engaging lecturer, but the grading criteria isn't always clear. Pretty easy B but definitely not an easy A.

Professor Odom is a smart, professional professor who also runs/works for a consulting firm in Boston. For that reason, I highly recommend taking Consulting in Orgs with him. You get real experience with real clients and he is super helpful throughout the entire semester. Its not an easy class, but its one of the best ive taken here
Talked a lot how amazing his course is but grading is so confusingly unreasonable. Not an easy A
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: AMAZING - super nice guy who knows how to teach. He also has some amazing work experience that he shares, infused with Ted talks, which makes the classes overall interesting. No need to read the textbook - tests are an in class paper on a case study which is kindly graded. Super inspirational too!

One of my favorite classes and professors at NU and even better guy. Decent work load, but topics are interesting. He will change it up if the class is feeling stressed. 5 stars all the way. You have to take him at some point, very interesting, gets to use his real life experience in lecture

I wish to learn more about how to actually use and apply ML, but basically all the lecture is about theory and math. The homework is heavy, especially the coding part is barely taught in class. Her voice is so small and with a heavy accent. Instead of wasting two hours, you could just learn the lecture yourself online.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Thick accent and can not understand the first thing she taught. The type of professor to push the midterm 4 days ahead with less than a 24 hour notice. Lots of homework and not understanding at all. Do not take her and I doubt northeastern is going to keep her much longer

I was hoping I would love this class and learn a lot about implementing cool machine learning algorithms on cool datasets. The course material is bad(only have done GD after a month) and she is so boring that its impossible to pay attention. I genuinely hope she reads this and tries to do better.

Lectures are unreasonably complex, spends the whole class proving formulas rather than showing us how to use them which is what is actually on the homework assignments/quizzes. I learn more about the material from a google search. Gives random pop quizzes at the beginning of class and calls out students who are even two minutes late.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Prof. Oprea is incredibly knowledgeable in her field. The material can be dry at times, but is in depth and thorough. The homework can be very long, but she and the TA's are easily accessible. She listens to students requests and wants us to succeed. Most of the grade is a Final and Final Project.

I took him in Fall 2024. Thursday night, brutal. The challenges were hard, since it was hard to even know any commands or whatnot. No notes or anything on the two quizzes and midterm, final. You basically had to memorize and understand everything and there was a lot. The bonuses were unsolvable, so don't be praising yourself with any extra credit.

I literally never use RateMyProfessor but it would be criminal not to praise Kaan.
If you are thinking about taking this course, do it. Even if you aren't pursuing a career in security.
Kaan is an expert at making you address your weaknesses in the material without making you feel bad about yourself.
You WILL learn a ton-- challenging but rewarding

The most helpful class I've taken at Northeastern. Kaan's lectures were very helpful and engaging and the topics discussed in class are very interesting and insightful. The coursework can be challenging, however the support from himself and the TAs was incredible.

Kaan's class was extremely rewarding. It is important that lectures are attended as he does give live demos of things that you just can't really get on your own, but it's not graded on whether you go. The challenges he provides are genuinely tough but definitely not impossible especially given the support you can get from him and the TAs.

The best class I've ever taken! I gained a lot from every single session. Also, the teaching style is engaging and fun, and relevant to real life. 
The assignments are definitely challenging. But, if you attend the office hours, Kaan always gives just the right amount of guidance to help you solve it.

Kaan is the kind of professor you actually want to get.  This has been the most valuable class ive taken at northeastern content wise, and kaan does it best.  Each lecture is entertaining and relates directly to assignments.  As long as you go to class you will be fine for the midterm and final.  Challenges can be difficult but very valuable.

Kaan has been the best professor I have had the pleasure listening to at Northeastern. He's incredibly personable but also a master at his craft. This is the one course I can comfortably say is worth the however many thousand dollars each class at Northeastern costs. You will be leaving lecture every week with ACTUAL practical skills. 11/10

Class can definitely be difficult at times but it is so worth it, every challenge is truly a learning opportunity. Kaan cares a lot about student success and you come out of class feeling like a 1337 hacker with all your new knowledge ;)
Probably the best professor most people will have at Northeastern, and easily the most effort any of my professors have put into a class.
Easily one of the best courses all time.

Kaan keeps challenges fresh and engaging. He's very approachable and gives you enough help where you can move past walls in the homework but still feel like you learned something. He helps you learn both technical and problem solving skills very well and rewards high performance.

Kaan is one of the best professors I've had. CY 5770 is tough but incredibly rewarding, especially for those with a good C programming and Linux background. He pushes you to apply concepts creatively and organizes the material brilliantly.

One of the best professors I've ever had. I recommend anyone who has a background in software security to take CY 5770 with Kaan. It was a difficult class, but it's a class I got the most learning out of. He pushes you to be a top-tier problem solver using the material you've learned in class, and organizes the class material extremely well.

One of the most valuable classes I've taken at Northeastern. It was definitely challenging and at times demanding, but the experience was more than worth it. Kaan is one of the best professors I have ever had.

If you have a chance to take a course under Kaan, just go for it. Keep in mind though, you need a certain level of C and Linux knowledge. If you have got the prerequisite knowledge, go for it probably gonna be the best course you have done in your program.

Kaan is the best!!! He is one of the best professors I've ever had. He explains complex cybersecurity concepts in ways that actually make sense, and his lectures are engaging and well organized. The assignments are challenging but fair and help build real-world skills. If you get a chance to take his class, do it!! you won't regret it!

He is truly one of a kind—extremely knowledgable. Classes are fun. I learned a lot about cybersecurity throughout my degree, but I truly understood concepts when Kaan taught it. Some technical challenges he designs are tough but rewarding; builds technical confidence. Kaan gives his absolute best & his dedication to teaching is inspiring.
It's tough enough to make you question choices, but interesting enough not to skip it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The most rewarding class I took at Northeastern. It was super difficult, but once you solved the challenges, it felt so satisfying. Khan put a lot of work into the structure of the class and it just shows. Kahn was a big part of how great this class was set up and I wouldn't have enjoyed the class as I strongly do if he wasn't the professor.

Kaan did a great job at keeping the class engaged, using real world examples to explain the content. He uses his work experience to give us motivation behind the content and activities in class. Kaan also did many in class demos and examples to show us how to complete our CTF's. The coursework was very rewarding, and Kaan was a fair grader.

Easily one of the best classes I have taken at Northeastern. It was definitely difficult and time consuming sometimes, but incredibly rewarding. One of the few classes where I genuinely felt like I learned a lot. Kaan is an incredible professor. He's funny, engaging, and just brings a lot of enthusiasm to class which makes it worthwhile.

I've said this too many of my friends, "Kaan is the best professor I have ever had." I don't think a second of my time taking this course was wasted, "challenges" (hw, CTF style) are super helpful in applying your learning from class (with a little learning left for outside of class). Exams were like 70% understanding hw, and going to lecture.

By far the most engaging lecturer and most fun and educational class I have ever taken at Northeastern. In no other class have I ever felt this challenged and rewarded, Kaan really pushes his students to learn in so many different ways, and I feel so much more confident in all my cybersecurity skills. Super funny guy, just wish he had his beard :(.

Kaan is an incredible professor. If you want to get into security, you have to take this class. He goes over all the fundamentals and is also a engaging professor. The homeworks are hands on CTF-style challenges, while the tests are more conceptual. Overall 10/10 class.

Kaan says he tries to teach everything about security in 4 months, and he really does. He brings years of experiences to the classroom and is passionate, knowledgeable, and invested in the topics of this class and the future of the students taking it. This is easily my favorite class, and I would've regretted not taking it before I graduated.
Kaan is fantastic, it is obvious he cares deeply about the material. The class is tough, but lots of help is available.

Best professor by far. Almost failed just to take again. 
First and only CS class I feel I have learned relevant material in.

Before this class started I really wanted to be the first one to give him a bad review on this site but I literally just cannot do that. Fair play to the guy, he's an amazing teacher and I've never learned more than I have in this class.

Amazing professor -- class is difficult but well worth the effort needed. Would recommend taking even if you are not necessarily interested in security as the course touches many core topics in CS such as networking, distributed systems, compilers, operating systems, and more. Challenges are fun and there is lots to learn from them

Had a wonderful experience taking this class as CS major, by far the most I've learned in a NEU course. Couldn't have asked for a more amazing prof - Kaan is patient, inspirational, extremely knowledgeable, caring, and approachable. Lectures (theory, research, and demos) and challenges (hands-on, DIY) complement each other perfectly.
Best course this college

Kaan does what I have not seen before in other professors: keeping a 2-3 hour long lecture engaging, fun, and meaningful. Nearly every lecture is grouped together with a live demonstration of how to apply the concepts covered on the slides. These demonstrations often give pointers on where to start on challenges, giving some starting direction. -44

This class is the best I've taken so far. Kaan is an excellent lecturer who keeps us engaged even during the late sessions that run until 9pm. The challenges are the highlight though - each one is intellectually stimulating and genuinely fun to solve. However, be prepared to spend lots of time on challenges! I can't recommend this class enough. -68

Spent by far the most hours working on his homework out of the 4 classes I took this semester. However this was by far the best and most rewarding hours spent. His challenges/homeworks are always hard but relevant and fun. This class rewards you for your effort, if you take it be ready to give it your all. 
-86
Show up to class. I loved taking this class and doing his challenges. Lots of fun and so much learned. Highly recommend.
Kaan is a very engaging professor for such a boring time slot

If you're unsure about taking this class, take it. Kaan is one of my favorite Professors. He's highly knowledgeable and has years of experience in his field. CS3740 is uniquely structured, with most of your grade based on hands-on CTFs that expose you to real-world online threats. Kaan genuinely enjoys teaching and wants the best for his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment:
Kaan's curriculum is awesome. Even as someone who enjoys security and regularly explores advanced topics, I still learned a lot from his class.
Challenges are unique, fair, and engaging. They were by far the most fun homework I've ever done in my life. If you can figure things out and deliver results, you'll have no problem getting an A.

This class was tough and challenges could take quite a bit to complete, but it was very intellectually challenging and Kaan equipped you with all you needed to solve them, given that you put some creativity into it. Very caring professor who wants to see his student succeed, and I'm glad I got to take this course!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Kaan's class is certainly the most unique I've ever taken. Incredible teacher who knows his stuff thoroughly and cares deeply for students if you're willing to put in the effort.
Professor covers all important topics and ensures the lectures are interesting. The challenges are fun and tests the understanding of fundamentals thoroughly.

Prof. Onarlioglu's assignments are very fun and effective for learning practical skills. Lectures were well-organized and he was highly knowledgeable in all the content. He was very responsive and made sure support was always available outside of class (even answering questions at 4am). Overall, a funny and amazing professor that I highly recommend
Loved the lectures and the many challenges.

Kaan was the most prepared educator I've ever had. The challenge environment, concepts, and application of these concepts to real world scenarios tied together seamlessly. Have a question at 3am? Kaan's responding immediately. This is not an easy course, but I've gained more knowledge that I can apply to future work than from any other I've taken

I was told this before taking the class, and now I'll point out the same: It's the best class and instructor! I guarantee you will learn a lot. The assignments are challenging, but the feeling of achievement is priceless when you solve them. If you're not sure about your technical background, you better take this class alone just to fully enjoy it.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Kaan is an amazing professor. I struggled but I learnt a lot and did things I didn't think I could do. 11/10 would recommend. Everyone needs to take this course under Kaan.

Kaan is a great lecturer and his homeworks are difficult but enjoyable. Also, there is office hours every week and constant support on Piazza so you are not alone at all, you will have plenty of help if you need it. If you have an interest in security at all, take this class, it's the best one I have taken at Northeastern and I have learned a ton.

Kaan is an amazing lecturer. He will keep you engaged with his enthusiasm and the occasional funny slides that pops up. He explains things very well and is very understandable. His course is also very hands-on while also balancing it with theory side. All in all, I really enjoyed this class and I learned a lot from it. Would take again!

Absolutely one of the best profs at Northeastern. Weekly lectures are fast paced and intense, but the material is presented incredibly clearly and Kaan is always willing to slow down for tricky concepts. The CTF-style homeworks are super fun and a great training ground for your technical skills, and the exams are great too! Expect to learn a ton.

Syssec will be the best cybersecurity class you take at Northeastern. Not only is Kaan an amazing lecturer--complete with anime references and top-tier memes littered throughout his slides--but his assignments are second to none. His CTF homeworks are a blast and he and the TAs are always available outside of class for assistance (even at 4am).

One of the best courses I have taken at Northeastern. I learnt so much from this class, and the 3-hour lecture per week goes like a breeze. I always look forward to what is to come next week. The content is hard for someone like me who has no prior exposure to security, but the support to successfully complete the course is impeccable.

Kaan is extremely passionate about the course material and teaching his students. Without a doubt, this was both my favorite class I've taken at Northeastern, measured by my ability to retain focus in and out of class, and most rewarding. The challenges will push your understanding of the course material to the next level, in a gamified fun way.

Interesting, fun and challenging class. As a CS major this was the first security class I enjoyed. Lectures are well planned and entertaining. Homework is time consuming but you gain experience using real world attacks.

Class was very fun and interesting. Homework is hard. Require shell bash scripting, but it is easy to learn. Exams are in person and written. Bonus points available to help with low exam scores. Office hours are extremely helpful. Only recommend this to student that wants a challenge and is confident in their CS knowledge/finesse.

Professor Kaan's lectures are amazing, he provides extra support to understand the concepts. His lectures are super intense and funny at the same time. His classes are worth your time and money :) In short he is the CoOlEsT and BeSt teacher I have ever had!! If you have a chance to take his class, without a doubt TAKE IT!!
Coolest professor you'll ever meet, if you are given the choice to take only one security class in your life, BLINDLY GO FOR THIS CLASS !
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Kaan might be the best professor I've ever had. His subject matter expertise is inspiring, he gives detailed information in lectures, and he cares so much about the topic that he set up his own hacking lab for students to exploit vulnerabilities. This was the most demanding class I've taken at NU; it was well-worth the challenge. Take his class.

Hands down!! The best course I have ever taken in academia. The course structure is brilliant and you will get addicted to challenges(you get to see your number on class rankings website in real-time :) ). Great piazza and one to one support. Extra credits saved the day. The stories/memes are great too. Salute Kaan!

Professor Kaan excels in teaching, crafting real-world-aligned assignments, and fostering accessibility through Piazza. His support and mentorship leave a transformative impact on student's academic journeys. A true beacon of excellence in education. I wish I had taken his class earlier during my master's program.

For a three month course, the structure was perfect to go over a wide variety of system security topics. Anyone even remotely interested in software security should take Kaan's class. His commitment to the course, engaging lectures, availability outside of class hours are second to none. Challenges simulate real world attacks and are super fun!

Kaan is a security guru we all wish we had lol. His teaching style is spot-on, making cybersecurity a breeze. 
The challenges? Intuitively fun! His cutting-edge research and legendary stories are just the icing on the cake!

Kaan is an amazing lecturer, knows his stuff, and cares about students. The lectures teach concepts and a security mindset, while the challenges (homework) are fun and teach practical skills. Grading is straightforward and fair. Kaan made an entire Arch Linux lab environment for this class, which should say enough about how much effort he puts in.

Every student who has the chance to take syssec with Kaan should. Best class I've taken at Northeastern. Homeworks and challenges were fun and addictive. You learn a lot too. Kaan puts a lot of effort and passion into the class, and it shows.

CY3740 was an indispensable course that gave me a well-structured and fundamental understanding of system security. Kaan is funny and entertaining in class and always ready to answer questions for his students. He always finds a way to hold the student's attention. In other classes, I would be distracted, but never in kaan's class.

Kaan is extremely knowledgeable about security and is a very engaging lecturer. Lectures are very informative and the slide decks are great. Challenges are somewhat difficult but definitely a lot of fun.

Kaan is really great. He is knowledgeable, really funny, and is always trying to make himself available. He responds on Piazza fairly quickly and gets grades back usually by the next class. Kaan has no problem being real about his opinions and is always trying to educate on past security issues that we should learn from.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Kaan was an admirable professor, not only is he extremely knowledgeable about this field, he wants us to succeed as long as you put in the work. This is a very challenging class in terms of the homework "challenges" that are given to us, but I promise you will have a clear understanding of certain security aspects after this course. 11/10 recommend
He's an amazing professor. He is also one of the best researchers in security, just look up his google scholar. You will learn a lot in this course.

Kaan is an absolute legend, and this course might be the best one I've taken at Northeastern so far. The challenges are somewhat difficult with no prior experience, but he's super responsive online and has office hours after class--if you get stuck somewhere, you aren't left to bang your head against a wall. 10/10, take this class if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was honestly one of the most difficult classes I've taken. I spent a lot of late nights thinking about buffer overflows and cross site scripting. Nonetheless, I found that I've also learned so much from Kaan's class. He's incredibly knowledgeable, charming, and one of the most caring professors I have met. Also he's simply a security wizard.

Kaan is very likely one of the best professors at Northeastern. He's *extremely* skilled and *very* accessible after class. I'd highly recommend taking his class if you have the chance, even if it's at night (don't worry: the class almost never ran more than 2-2.5 hours). The challenges can be difficult; but it is definitely worth the experience.

Extremely effective lectures and assignments. Everything is usable in the real-world and the grading is based on whether you do the assignment or not. No scores, no partial credit. You either did the assignment or you didn't. The professor is super accessible if you need help and will generally respond quite quickly to questions.

A great course with a well-rounded syllabus and amazing lectures. Professor's delivery and explanations ensure that you understand the content from the ground up. The exams and assignments are fun and practically applicable in the real world. I feel better prepared to enter the world of security after this course. Would definitely recommend it!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Incredibly interesting and challenging course.  I really enjoyed this class and I learned a lot
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This is a great class and you will learn a lot, but you need to do the work. There's no way to bullshit the assignments for partial credit, you need to commit to figuring them out start to finish. Lectures cover a lot of in-depth technical material useful for security career. Tons of opportunities for extra credit for those who want it.

Coming from a Game Dev background, I was shown how beautiful security is and absolutely loved every challenge and especially the fact where we having to be self disciplined as there were only 2 deadlines.

Hey Folks, 

So if you are here, That means you are in dilemma to take this course?

I am a student who took this course with the same dilemma and you know what? This is one of the greatest dilemma decisions of my life which yielded astonishing results. Go for it. You will love what you are doing. This is the real world, real challenges. GOAT!
One of the best classes I have ever taken at Northeastern, Cant find anyone better at teaching security than him!

Kaan is easily the best professor I have ever had at Northeastern. Great lectures, great challenges, and very accessible outside of class. I learned more from him and his class than every other class here combined. If you can take CY3740 for CY5770 with him, do it!
Great real-world experience, hugely accessible and helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: If you are remotely interested in Security, take this class. Kaan is a great guy, and the challenges are by far the best part of it all.

This is probably one of the best classes I've ever taken, and Kaan is a major reason for that. If you like solving puzzles/challenges at all, you have to take this class. The homeworks in this class are pretty much "here's what you have to break, here's some hints, go", and they're incredibly fun. Plus, Kaan's always there to help if you get stuck.

CY3740 was one of the most entertaining classes I've ever taken. Kaan lectures on interesting topics and the challenges he creates are awesome. No doubt, Kaan's challenges will make you struggle and feel frustrated, but they are so well crafted that the sense of satisfaction you feel after completion is unlike anything.

Out of all professors I have had at Northeastern University, Kaan has been THE BEST professor. He is extremely knowledgeable and translates complicated topics into understandable lectures. His assignments provide practical experience in exploitation and are also fun/engaging :)
Fantastically designed the class with applicable challenges as hw. Very accessible outside of the class for hw help. 10/10 would take again.
Very interesting and fun challenges

If you have even a remote interest in cybersecurity you need to take this class with him. It's gonna be tough but you'll learn so much along the way and Kaan is a chill guy, so he'll always help you. There really isn't any book that'll teach you this stuff. You'll struggle through the challenges, but the high after solving them is worth it.

He is a great teacher and always ready to help you. The assignments will be tough if you have no previous experience in the Security field but if you ask a question he will guide you through the solutions.

He chill and shi

This class will erase your social life. Good luck.

O'Connell is a passionate, good professor. O'Connell's great. Available, gives good feedback, answers questions promptly. However, stacked cornerstone is so miserable that I wouldn't recommend anybody take it. reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: His directions for group projects often is contradictory. The rubrics were different than the guide and it ended with us at one point getting a 65 on part of a project. The overall class isn't that difficult, but taking stacked cornerstone is so much work, like 30+ hours a week. O'Connell is a pretty decent professor and responded to my questions and messages within a couple hours. I felt that the lectures did not connect with the homework. I would not recommend stacking cornerstone unless absolutely necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Nothing that conceptually hard about the course but stacked cornerstone is grueling. 25-40 hours a week. Never take stacked cornerstone by choice unless you have a lighter schedule. classes were completely a waste of time, as we were assigned to learn the same thing online before class.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

he definitely is a fan of what he teaches, and that much is apparent, whether you agree with his teaching methods or not

HOW TO DO WELL WITHOUT BEING A GENIUS:

Exams given seems unfair. The other professors gives 20 question multiple choice exams with a provided equation sheet and often multiple practice exams, while for this course we had 45 points worth of consisting of fill in the blank, set up equation, and other non-multiple choice question and instead allowed to make our own one page equation sheet.

The professor also does not provide sufficient resources for preparation, sure there are textbook questions and office hours, but refuses to give us anything else to help us prepare for exams. At the beginning of the year even stated that she "does not believe in practice exams".

Exam questions are very conceptual, and are nothing like the examples we are given during lectures. Like those tiktoks going "lecture, homework, then exam" where the exam is impossible, that is exactly how this class is like.

Worst professor I will ever have. Things that are taught in class, homework problems, and test questions are all styled completely, so how confident you are on what you learn/practice will not translate to the exam.

She uses an insane grading scheme and an unreasonable amount of drops to make up for the fact that she is not qualified to teach 2 semesters of chem in 1. And she can act like we're the ones doing the poor job. Avoid her at all costs for 1151.

Don't take her class. Trust me. All the good reviews are lying to you. She is a terrible teacher and deserves all the bad reviews.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: do urself a favor and don't take this class.

Like others said, many of the negative reviews were deleted/removed. Piatt's tests are not similar to homework or recitation questions, making studying effectively very difficult. Her homework was too much for the first half of the semester before enough people complained to her higher-ups and she had to decrease it.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Save yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: For some reason, the majority of the reviews made on this site were removed, only the bad ones. Chemistry made my first semester here miserable. Dr. Piatt's class was particularly harder than the other professors and her final was 90 questions. Although Piatt is nice and smart, homework was 60+ questions and her lectures were very hard to follow.

When everyone did horrible on the first exam because she changed the format and test material the night (literally less that 12 hours) before she excused everyone of cheating. She also makes things very difficult for no reason (like being able to see the test answers, recitation which turned into a group quiz, and others). Extra credit was nice.

Dr. Piatt is very passionate, however, the organization of the class is not good. Lectures, HW, and recitations are math info, while the tests are only conceptual questions. It is very hard to study for the very difficult tests, and even her lectures are ver disorganized. She is helpful in office hours and did shorten some of the HW.

What other comments for this class neglect is that this was her first time teaching the course, and she made that clear from the start. It's a hard class, but it's also a 5000-level. Everything was completely fair, and she had plenty of office hours to discuss anything. Some complaints are justified, but she's still a great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Offers extra credit but exams are difficult. Homework is unnecessary because it doesn't help with understanding concepts. Good professor, not her fault Northeastern combines chem 1 and 2 in a semester.

Dr. Piatt was always enthusiastic to teach us and clearly loves her job.  Although a hard class, she is approachable and easy to ask for help from. Many students associate a class being hard with the professor being bad which is false.  Dr. Piatt often provided  extra credit and pretty fairly balanced exams with other assignments balancing grades.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Expect to dedicate around 8 hours every week to this class on HW and reading only. HWs are long and difficult, and they make up 60% of your grade. Readings are upwards of 30 pages. Super fast-paced, but will clarify if asked to. She's super nice and very knowledgeable, though, but man, those HWs...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Piatt is a super nice person and makes lectures fun and interesting. That being said, she assigns 30-question homeworks that are around 90% short-answer problems that are either highly-conceptual or super math-heavy. A lot of the problems reference material not taught in class and require lots of reading as well as outside research.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I love Dr. Piatt. Many people give her bad ratings but that is just based off of the difficulty of this class. She is very enthusiastic and clearly wants her students to succeed. Lectures are engaging and she will always answer questions. Only downside is incredibly hard tests but she curves and offers extra credit.

Professor Piatt is nice and if you seek help, she is very helpful. The tests are extremely difficult, and the homework is long (I would like to note that she has shortened it since the beginning of the year when it was 50ish problems per week).  The class is simply very difficult. Be ready to study a lot, but if you put in work, you will be ok.

As a student who had taken AP Chemistry, this class serves as a perfect review of the materials that were covered in the curriculum. Even though Dr.Piatt's class moves really fast and the tests are not easy, it definitely worth a try

The class itself is hard to comprehend since it's moving at a fast path, but things are not that bad once you spend time and effort studying for it. Dr.Piatt's class pushed me to become an independent learner.
Not bad, just need enough participation and go to the office hour.

Professor Piatt is very nice and helpful in understanding course material. That being said, the required course material is very demanding and requires a lot of outside studying and learning. Tests are the biggest challenge of the course as they are concept based while most homework is calculation based
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Piatt is a nice person but her class is extremely hard. I had to teach myself for the whole semester. Homework doesn't relate to tests at all but it counts for overall grade so it's required. She has high expectations for her students shown through her tests, students must understand the materials and concepts 200/100% to score at least a 70.
messy, disorganized, terrible handwriting, terrible tests. would never take another class with her again.
Professor is heavy on attendance. but covers in depth topics within a course of 14 weeks.
Small class size, one-on-one attention throughout the semester. Professor made the subject matter easy to understand and brought in lots of industry experience.
Enjoyed the class, she makes it fun and interactive. Lots to learn in the subject area, and she uses real-world examples and her own experience to aid in understanding.

Industry expert, lots of experience to share in class. Make's tough concepts easy to understand. Good speaker, very inspirational! Values participation, tough on attendance and late homework, but easy with grading. Promotes teamwork and uses Project-Based-Learning method to teach. Gives great feedback, genuinely wants students to learn.

Industry expert, lots of experience to share in class. Brings in guest speakers from her industry network. Good speaker, very inspirational! Values participation, tough on attendance and late homework, but easy with grading. Promotes teamwork and uses Project-Based-Learning method to teach. Gives great feedback, genuinely wants students to learn.

She absolutely clocked the class on cheating yesterday and it was iconic #puttingherfootdown. 10/10 experience watching everyone who cheats panic, and she didn't stutter ONCE, ate and left no crumbs. A&P is hard so you have to study, but she at least makes it clear what to know. I rather get an 85% with studying then with cheating.

I loved her. I took her for both A&P 1 and 2 with her and she was great for both. All of the info you need is on the slides and if you actually study its an easy A. The labs also weren't as bad as everyone made it seem, I would study the day before and went to office hours one a week and got an A on every quiz/exam.

I sat in this class wondering if I would live to see the end of it. It's like sitting through a presentation that was prepared the night before. On the bright side, I had time to do the crossword 3x a week.

Genuinely seems like she doesn't know what she's talking about. She just reads off the slides and only chooses to answer questions that are on the slides. She gives many tests that are just so conceptual and not straightforward, so prepare to study a lot. It's only difficult because she struggles to teach in an effective manner. It's ridiculous...

All the bad ratings are for the lab which she has nothing to do with! This class is super easy the exams are right from the slides and theres plenty of ways to get participation credit (including ways outside of class). She's great, the lab is hell

Honestly she's like fine ig she's just a big pushover and everyone used chat on her exams cause they're all online and she doesn't have proctors which was annoying. Also her lectures are basically just reading off the slides w/ some small additions so don't really need to go to class tbh. Participation activities were kind of just checking a box
Lectures are decent and exams are fair, nothing really special about her teaching but I dont think its as horrible as everyone says it is.

Her low rating is unfair. She's VERY straight forward & intelligent, its clear what you need to know, very willing to clarify what will be on exams. Exams are all MCQ, you need to put in effort as A&P itself is very difficult, but people take their frustration out on her by low ratings. 100% recommend taking her class she is amazing. #queenpeacock

I don't get why her rating is so low. She's organized and makes it clear what you need to know, offering reviews and clarifying what lectures will be on exams. All exams are MC, and you're given plenty of time to finish. A&P itself can be very difficult, and people take their frustration out on her by low ratings. 100% recommend taking her class.

True honesty, I think she gets a bad rep and she's overhated. Peacock is organized and clear on how she grades, and she gives so many resources to help study: lecture videos, free textbook, weekly, short, open note take-home quizzes, etc. I understand how the course in itself can be difficult once you find a study method it'll be worth it.

Honestly think she gets a bad rep but she's not that awful. As long as you make sure to stay on top of participation that will help boost your grade. Course had 4 midterm exams throughout the semester that were each 50 MCQ. Each midterm usually covered around 5 lecture topics, so usually studying a few days in advance would help.
I think she's being unfairly rated here. The exams are based solely on the lectures, and as long as you follow along, you'd be fine for the exams. It's very lecture-heavy, so be prepared to study.

I've really enjoyed Dr. Peacock and her course! You do have to do participation activities every week, but they're easy to do. Lectures are fairly easy to follow and she truly wants people to understand. Exams are mainly application, but its MCQ and she does review sessions the class before an exam. As long as you put in effort you'll be fine!

honestly a fair professor, exams are usually straight forward. A&P is a hard class which in general requires a lot of studying so if you put the work in its fine. my only thing is that sometimes she doesnt super clearly explain things, but she keeps a poll everywhere open so you can ask questions which i like.

Overall, your going to find yourself studying to survive, not to actually learn and retain the information which sucks. Tests are made to trick you, not for you to succeed. At first, it bothered me that students would cheat in order to pass her tests, and although I still believe it's wrong and wouldn't do it myself, I understood why.

Her slides makes it really easy to understand and is much better than the other professor, she makes the slides simple while the other professor copies off the online textbook and doesn't explain as clear as Professor Peacock. Take her class instead of the other professor

I loved this class and I loved having her as a professor. I don't know if she changed her style or the course but after having her for both 1 and 2 I think she's a great professor. Exams are straight forward and she gives you practice before each one including the final. If you actually study and put in the effort it's actually pretty easy

Idk why everyone hates her but she's great. I had her for both a&p 1+2. Her lectures are clear and straightforward and the exam was exactly her lectures. She provided a practice exam for every one on topics that students have the most trouble on. If you actually study it's really easy. I loved her class and truly didn't think it was bad at all

i don't think professor peacock's low rating is fair. as long as you pay attention to lectures, it is easy to do well. i never saw an exam Q that i had no clue how to answer. she gives a lot of options to do well (exams, participation, quizzes, etc). she was accommodating when i had to reschedule an exam for an emergency. highly recommend her.

While Professor Peacock is not the best professor I've ever had, she is certainly not the worst, and I think her rating on here is completely unfair. The course is hard, but I don't think her exams are unfair - all of the material you need to know is in her slides.

I dont think Dr. Peacock deserves the hate she gets. Class participation is required 2x per week. Exams are pretty difficult and more complex than the slides, but if you study its fine. People rating her badly are freshmen who have never taken a college class before. It's as difficult as any other STEM class. Don't be scared by her reviews.

Professor Peacock is a great professor! It is very doable to get an A in this class. Everyone who gave her a 1 or 2, did not try enough. In class she said PEOPLE SHOULD PUT IN EXTRA TIME FOR THIS CLASS. If you studied each lecture, meaning you did not just memorize, then you should be able to get an A in this class. I would take this class again!

The slides, which she reads straight off of, are too simple to make a student gain the complex knowledge required for her tests. I recommend skipping class and going through the slides slowly and chatgpt concepts that are unclear (which is most of it). Lectures feel like watching a teenager that prepared for the presentation the night before.

Dr. Peacock really just is too nervous to teach in a college setting. She seems to be very timid when she is teaching, causing her to just read from the slides in a monotone voice while never explaining any definitions. A free online textbook is provided but doesn't provide any help at all and isn't used.

Overall, Dr. Peacock is a good professor. Her tests were hard but doable & it helped to rewatch lectures and note-take on each lecture. Exams are weighted heavily but there is an opportunity for corrections and she factors in participation. FYI Lab is a different animal & somehow harder than the lecture course! I spent more time studying for lab...

lectures aren't the best; they provide the material but exams sometimes don't correlate and it seems like questions are purposely trying to trick you rather than test your understanding. reads straight off the slides even when answering questions. explains concepts in a very convoluted way. i think she is a good person, but not a good professor.

Peacock is the worst professor I've ever had. She doesn't communicate with students and either takes days to reply or doesn't respond at all. What we study in class is never reflected on the exams, making it difficult to prepare and succeed.

Dr. Peacock is a great professor and I think she explains the content really well and is super organized with the way she structures her class. It's anatomy so it's supposed to be hard but if you put in the time to memorizing you will do well. I think a lot of the complaints here come from the lab (which another professor runs...).

The real problem with A and P is the lab. This lab is the worst thing conjured from the depths of hell. The class so far is much easier. In the first month, you will have to memorize every part of every bone and muscle for humans AND rats. It's like learning a new language and the expectation is you become fluent within a month.

As a third year bio student, I think that these reviews are because this is a class that mostly first year nursing students take. Adjusting to college is HARD and I mean no hate at all- class felt impossible at first. But if you take time to figure out what study methods work for you, doing well in this class won't be a problem.

Overall, Dr. Peacock is a good professor. Her rating is unfounded on here. Taking A&P 2 with her over the summer wasn't really a challenge, as the material on the tests aligned well with the content taught in class. Engagement is key to performing well, and she makes expectations clear for content on the exams. Would take another class with her!

This class is definitely manageable. There are 4 non-cumulative tests, all multiple choice and you have plenty of time to answer the questions. My advice is too make sure you focus on the content on the slides. Be familiar with definitions and make sure that you really understand more complicated topics by searching supplemental content on youtube.

Took over summer 1, very easy class. Look over slides, exams all multiple choice, final is non cumulative. No homework besides weekly multiple choice quizzes with 3 attempts. Gives very few extra credit points. Memorize as much from the slides, not textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. P doesnt deserve the hate she gets! Had her for A&P 1+2 & loved her class. She does a great job at breaking down difficult topics. Her tests can be tricky but they test your knowledge & I aced them by studying slides+lecture content (focus on what she says when notetaking!). She cares & offers opps to boost ur grade (participation, team tests).

Ms. Peacock is an overall lazy teacher that explain complex concepts poorly. If you ask her to further explain something she will just read exactly what it says on the slide. Tests were entirely multiple choice and online, but she would hide the grades and not release them until a week after we took them which was very frustrating.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Reads directly off of slides and rushes through content. Records all lectures but requires participation which wastes class time since this is a large lecture. Can only do well if you teach yourself the material. Tests are all MC and often strange scenarios so even if you know the content you can get easily confused.

I honestly pray for any one who takes this class. This class is extremely hard to pass unless you study extremely hard. I do not recommend her. Multiple people ended up retaking the course due to her heavy lecture and very minimal explanation. I wish the exams were open notes to make an easier exam .
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Utilize the textbook honestly cause all this professor does is read off of slides and not explain content in simple manner.

The class itself wasn't super hard but her exams made absolutely no sense at all. They were loosely based on our lectures but it goes way beyond them. (The question will touch on a topic but the answers make little to no sense). Her communication wasn't great and she didn't answer questions properly; she would direct you back to the slides.

Never seems to know what she's talking about and repeats the same concept like 3 times but in different ways making it hard to understand -she sometimes contradicts herself too. Lectures are lengthy but she speaks quickly. Exams are mostly based on what she says so make sure to take notes on that. She does record her lectures though. don't take her
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Peacock explains concepts to the class as if we already understand the definitions. We may learn the names of specific anatomy/content in the same class that she applies them to complex processes. Aka the class very quickly cannot follow her lectures because they do not know what she is talking ab. She also repeats herself and mumbles.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She cannot teach and is not helpful at all. You won't know what information is important or what to study for so you lowkey have to cram for a test and then when the test comes the questions make no sense and are situational types of questions. This class isn't for learning... it's for passing.

Would basically just read from the slides, and if you asked her a curiosity question she probably wouldn't be able to answer it. The exams weren't too difficult though, and she made sure to provide practice exams before each one.

Lectures are garbage. If you have a question about a section you didn't understand, she will read off the slide again in the exact way she did the first time. Your grade is based on 3 midterms and then the final. During lectures, she will say many extra pieces of info that are not on the slides and unimportant, but then pick two to add to the exams
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The lectures are her reading off the slides and the overall grading layout makes it next to impossible to get an A. After reading a lot of her RMP ratings I was scared to take her class, and although it is not as bad as everyone makes it out to be, it is still a difficult course as she does the bare minimum for her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is the only A&P teacher but my advice is to not slack on participation it can be the saving grace for your grade at the end of the sem. Weekly quizzes also act as grade savers in my opinion. She's not horrible but like you have to teach yourself everything so... Good luck!

Peacock does not know what she's teaching...you will have to teach yourself the topics because she overcomplicates them in lectures. Her tests are extremely hard and discussion questions are answered by her own students, who supposedly do not have the same knowledge as someone with a PhD. Please avoid this class or professor if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would not recommend taking her class. You were graded on only a few things and none of her exams made sense
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Let me start off by saying I literally had to drop the class. This class is very test heavy and if you do poorly on one test your next one will have to be almost prefect. The way she "teaches" is just by reading off slides and there are many questions that students ask that go unanswered. Her tests are not a reflection of what she teaches.

Lectures are very excruciatingly heavy & the tests are nothing short of a nightmare. They are excessively difficult and fail to assess our understanding of the course material accurately. Teaching methods, grading practices, and test structures are simply atrocious. AVOID IF POSSIBLE!!!!

If you can, don't take this professor. She does not care about her students at all and only cares about her agenda and course content being "taught". If you mess up on one test grade, your entire grade drops and you're possibly at risk for failing the class. There's no point in taking her cause you don't learn anything. SHE SHOULD BE FIRED.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Literally terrible. She told us that Type 1 Diabetes has nothing to do with genetics.. Thats all I have to say! So So SO terrible, good lord.

Only reads off of the powerpoint so students aren't engaged. Entire grade almost entirely consists of 3 midterms and 1 final so if you do not do well on one of them, your grade will be irreversibly affected. Every test question is application based but she never explains topics well so tests are extremely difficult. AVOID IF POSSIBLE!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very difficult class. The entire structure was lecture based so students aren't engaged. Material is already hard enough and it was never explained well. Going to class was the same thing as being read the textbook to out loud. Only reason I did well was because I got a tutor. Try to avoid if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She literally just reads from slides and do not actually attempt to teach the material, her tests do not reflect what we have learned, she is unhelpful

Would not take this class with Prof. Peacock again. She has clear & organized slides, but constantly overcomplicates simple topics & makes her test questions specifically tricky. She clouds her test questions with irrelevant backstories & information to confuse you. Avoid this professor. Additionally, lab is so extra & has a heavy workload.

I'd say avoid this professor but there's really no other choice. Grades were dictated by weekly quizzes & three assessments + an anatomy lab taught by a TA but much more immersing and enjoyable. All assessments MC which sounds easy but was actually difficult considering questions asked are often NOT covered by lectures and go beyond what is taught

Professor Peacock in all regards is a very poor professor. The course is completely evaluated on your ability to perform well on her tests which are quite tough, mainly because they are application questions of topics that really aren't well taught by her. I'd recommend dedicating a lot of time to this class- read the textbook and listen well!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I learned nothing from this class. She reads directly from slides every class and makes everything confusing. Grade is based off multiple choice exams and one bad exam will make or break your grade.

I thought knew what I was getting myself into, but somehow it's so much worse. She has three tests throughout the semester and then the final, accounts for 90% of your grade. She talks (well more like reads off the slide) way too fast. She records all her lectures so you can stop showing up to lectures. If you can avoid it, don't take her.

Terrible professor. She reads directly off a powerpoint so most people don't go to class and she doesn't seem to care at all. Grade is based almost entirely on exams that are all multiple choice but the questions make no sense. This class is such an important concept but I have learned nothing because of her.

Dr. Peacock was an excellent professor.  Her lectures were well-paced and informative.  She was always willing to answer questions, and would even write down the question if she didn't know the answer to find the answer for you.  Her tests were somewhat difficult, but definitely doable if you show up to lecture (where she had practice questions).
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Peacock is the most horrible and unprofessional teacher I've ever had. Each exam can make or break your entire grade so doing poorly on one tanks your grade. It's all MC but the questions are impossible. Nothing like what we learn in class. She reads off the board word for word so there's no point in going to class. Prepare yourself :/

Professor Peacock is knowledge in Human Anatomy, but her class is structured in a way where students are not well benefited and a single bad grade on a test can make or break your grade. Sarah Peacock's lectures are oftentimes her reading off the slideshow, so showing up to class makes no difference in learning.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was all lecture and she talked very fast. She would move on to the next slide when the whole class was still writing. This made it hard to learn any information.
