However that this class is very content heavy so I understand why she has to move quickly. But she was not confident in her speaking and just made lectures more confusing.

Class is basically only lectures and exams. Graded on three exams and one final that is partially cumulative. Exams are almost all application questions, and based on concepts that aren't on the slides but instead things she says aloud in class, so they're really hard if you aren't prepared. Just come to class and take notes on what she says.

Prof. Peacock seemed to be very knowledgeable but she mainly reads off the slides in addition to adding a ton of information quickly. I would advise writing every word because they tend to be asked in test questions. You are mainly graded on four exams, and weekly quizzes that are 10% of your grade. To do well, you need to dedicate a lot of time.

I honestly hated her class, her style of teaching is so difficult to learn, I literally get a seizure reading her exam questions--some straight up do not make sense. She only reads off lectures and talks way too fast to even understand. If you don't need to take her class for credit, please do yourself a favor and DON'T. #itwasbadformymentalhealth
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Peacock seems knowledgeable but the lectures are content-heavy with the occasional poll everywhere questions. It would be better if she asked more questions throughout the class so students can assess their knowledge of what she is teaching. The grade for the class is made up of 3 exams, the final, and weekly quizzes that do not get graded.

This class was hard as hell, and Professor Peacock did not seem confident when teaching. Lectures went super fast, and many things that were not on the slides but were said, were on the tests. The tests were extremely hard, all application-based and deep knowledge of the material is required. You will have to STUDY to pass this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof Peacock is a poor lecturer and doesn't seem knowledgable on the material. Her study guides are useless and often times misleading. She puts very little effort into the class and reads right off the slides. The tests are very difficult and require deep application of concepts not explained well enough in class. No scale, and lack of empathy.

Prof Peacock speaks fast. She sounds like she is nervous when she is speaking to the class and it is hard to pay attention. Grade is determined by three exams+final. The final is 1/3 cumulative. The tests are difficult and a deep understanding of the topics is necessary. Exams are application based. I hate this class, do not take it if you dont hv2
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had Prof. Peacock for a&p1, and I'm happy to have her again for a&p2. Her lectures are very thorough, she always stops for questions, and she reexplains concepts when necessary. The class/subject is not easy, but all material you need to succeed is given, so be diligent and you will do well (grade based mostly on four large exams, so be careful).

Seems like a nice lady just not a good professor. Unprofessional. When asked questions in class she says "ill write that down and get back to u" then NEVER does. majority of grade is 3 exams + final exam which is TOUGH. Exams are mostly applications based questions that are often tricky and way too difficult for an already tough class. dont take
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Peacock is one of the worst professors I have had a NEU. She has trouble communicating ideas which is crucial when trying to understand A&P. I swear some of the stuff she gave us in lectures was just wrong, or at least she counted it wrong on exams. Rarely accessible and gave no accommodations/help for the pandemic. dONT TAKE IF U CAN!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Did not make her office hours accessible for all students, no curve even when avg of the class was extremely low, did not record any of her lectures, spoke very fast which was hard to understand, lectures were never consistent, did not take any suggestions from students, never guided us for studying for her exams, just overall was not accomodating
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: If you absolutely have to take this class for your major, I don't recommend taking this course if you have other difficult classes to focus on. You will need to consistently memorize all the material. Her exams are all multiple choice, but they are so hard because you need to memorize every word on her illegible lecture notes.

If there is ANY way you can take this course with a different professor, please stop reading and switch immediately. 
You must come to every single lecture, and you CANNOT be late. The attendance code is up for about 3 minutes at most, even though all lectures are recorded, and she'll barely ever answer questions in class. 
Average grade is a B-.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was unnecessarily difficult for an intro gen bio course. She expects memorization of both lecture content & slides, but slides are disorganized, making studying inefficient. Exams are very challenging & feel misaligned w what's emphasized in class. I recommend considering another instructor for Gen Bio I & II if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: exams were so much more difficult than they shouldve been. shes also pretty rude. worst 8am i couldve taken

Mary is extremely rude and demeaning to students. The icing on the cake was when she screamed "whoever cracks their knuckles next is getting thrown out of the room and receiving a zero" while we were taking our final exam in the dead silent lecture hall. Every question asked is returned with a sarcastic and humiliating response.Also awful teacher!!

i was terrified to take gen bio 2 with potts, but i promise she's not that bad. she's very polite (now at least, i've been told she was rude yrs ago), very enthusiastic and knowledgable. she's also generous with extra credit, scatters free points throughout the semester. my advice is do ALL extra credit. i was bumped up from a B+ to an A bc of it.
Gives so many extra credit opportunities. The class is lecture heavy but she reposts her lecture videos so you  can watch them on your own time.

I had her for Gen Bio 2, a 1000-level course which is supposed to be fairly straightforward and on general biology knowledge and concepts. Dr. Potts-Santone makes it 4000-level, extremely detail-oriented, and unnecessarily difficult. It is possible to do well in this course, but expect to spend a lot of hours each day studying the vast material.

One of the most abysmal experiences I've had in a college course. Dr. Potts-Santone is easily one of the most difficult professors I've ever had, and she is not friendly, approachable, nor helpful in any matter. Test/lecture heavy, difficult tests, but the silver lining is that she offers test corrections. Other than that, 1/10 experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Slides are so hard to read and she'll sometimes use black text on a black background for some reason. Her tests are unreasonably hard and really specific. Studying feels so exhausting because there's so much material. Overall though, it is an interesting course and she's usually eager to help you if you have any questions.

I was nervous entering her class this semester because of many negative experiences, but I found her to be a really good professor. Her tests were very difficult, but she gave opportunities to get points back. I think that she is a good professor and would recommend her in the future.

She knows her stuff, but lectures are so heavy and impossible to follow sometimes. Grade almost entirely depends on exams, and the questions for the exams ask for info not presented in class. There are opportunities for extra points, but they only sometimes make a difference. 1/3 of my class dropped because of failing grades so avoid if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Potts-Santone really isn't as bad as people make her seem. She's a solid professor who really knows what she's talking about. If you attend the lectures and pay attention you'll do fine in her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: 100% not recommended

The tests were super specific. Even when I look back at the lectures, the tests were really difficult. Bad lecture slides. The questions on the tests were not about the slides half the time; they were on random specific things she said in class, so be prepared to listen to every little detail in lectures.

Sure, her tests were difficult and some questions were overly specific, but for the main part, she was very fair. Her expectations were clear so as long as you pay attention and study well you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I took Bio and AP Bio in high-school and still found Biology 1 challenging. You must be attentive during lectures and take notes. Expect to spend about 10-12 hours studying per exam and very detailed questions on the exam. Professor Potts-Santone is well equipped to teach the class and is passionate about what she does. Expect to put in work!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You don't actually learn anything in this class, you just memorize what you're told to. The tests are unreasonably hard, and she doesn't curve the grades, even when the class average is ridiculously low. She says she cares about our learning and wants us to pass, but she doesn't really act that way. There are lots of typos in her tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Most of what's on the lecture slides isn't on the tests. You have to take really good notes on the slides, and the random details she tells you. There's an insane amount of memorization, and she doesn't really present it in a way that makes it reasonable to learn. I've already forgotten most of what I learned, and my final was yesterday.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I normally like classes like this where you're given information and have to memorize it, but this was just too much! So much material and not enough time to actually learn it all. The class is structured makes it seem like she doesn't actually care about whether or not you learn the content. No curve, so there's no room for error. Avoid her!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: You're expected to memorize A LOT of stuff, without really learning any of it. The least the professor could do is try to make it interesting, but she doesn't even try to do that. It's just 100+ slides of pointless information for each exam. Tests are ridiculously hard and specific, and to top it all off, she doesn't even curve the grades. Terrible
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've had so far. Boring and disorganized lectures. Most of the class time is spent just listening to her drone on. This class was honestly probably one of the worst I've ever taken. I'm a health major so none of the content was interesting or meaningful to me. She tries to be funny and relatable, but it comes off as annoying.

The grade for Gen bio 2 was mostly dependent on 4 tests, which were difficult but not impossible. If you pay attention to lectures (lots of detail), study/rewatch her lectures you'll do fine, but I wouldn't say this class is an easy A. I thought this class was about the same difficulty as AP Bio
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Potts- Santone has it all together. Her lectures are well planned, and she posts recordings of lectures on blackboard. If you study and put in the effort the way she guides you to do so, you can get the A.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: LOTS OF INFORMATION. Her tests are tricky and aim to mess with your head. If memorization is not your thing take another elective.

She is very knowledgable and passionate about Biology which made it more interesting to learn from her. She does talk fast and it can be hard to take notes and listen to all she has to say, but as long as you put the work in, should do fine. Recommend to take notes on everything she says, including the small examples and facts. Very good teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Talks really fast during lecture, but records and posts every class via tegrity. DO NOT RELY ON TEGRITY! She gives extra credit sometimes to those who show up to her class on a given day because so many people make the mistake of relying on tegrity and those are typically the ones who don't do well. Her tests are tough,  but shes very approachable.
No Comments
Kind until he's not. Seems like a nice guy, but if you make an honest mistake he will get very passive aggressive
Half the class are non SLPA majors and he clearly tailors the class towards that. Work feels repetitive, boring, and unhelpful especially as an SLPA major.

don't take this prof- his lectures are so long and drawn out, and I never learn anything in his class
his tests are tricky and i've asked him for help, and he doesn't respond
Often relies on guest lecturers as he is not qualified to be teaching this class.
Unhelpful explanations, long homeworks, and boring lectures. Would not recommend taking this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: drops 1/3 of the grades the class is easy but often feels like a joke
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: as an slpa major he is not qualified to teach this class. we don't want a researcher!
Easy class ? Super. Good professor? Absolutely not.
incredibly dry professor with little regard for the students, incredibly strict and controlling for no reason

He's a nice person, but not right to teach this class. As someone with SLPA coursework experience, I found the content surface level and repetitive. Guest speakers came alot but added little value, mostly sharing their career paths rather than useful insights. Never left the class feeling like I learned anything new.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: my favorite professor this semester! he knows so much about everything related to the neuroscience of language, and grew my love for the brain. I found myself wanting to go to each lecture because it was interesting. readings each week but as long as you comment a few times you get the full grade. small open note quizzes and a final presentation.

Class was easy and 1/3 of most grades were dropped but I dreaded it when he taught. There were many guest speakers which were amazing but Dr Peelle simply does not understand the content enough to be teaching this course. He tried his best, but his background is not in what this class is teaching. Nice guy, wrong class for him to be teaching.

Insanely unorganized, took 2 quizzes in the span of one and a half months, now he realizes that we need to take 12 quizzes in total, now we have a quiz literally every class, 2 quizzes per class (2 quizzes per week).

His Chinese accent makes it impossible to understand anything he's saying, and he's a very tough grader, avoid at all costs!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The professor is creative with projects, but understanding him is difficult since English isn't his first language (he mainly speaks Chinese). You'll need to study on your own because what's taught in class often differs from what appears on exams. Clearer communication would greatly help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: class was not very helpful and also difficult to understand but still had mandatory attendance. Also required participation which is a bummer. one group project which was pretty easy, and 2 midterms. Study the practice tests and you'll do just fine in his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Super nice professor with a lot of help outside of the classroom. He is very dedicated to the success of the class. I recommend taking him if you plan to come and participate.
Loved him. Phenomenal professor and learned so much about principles of investing through him. Would totally recommend him!

I had Professor Chiou for Investments and he is a great professor that brings in real world events to teach and keep you informed. If you utilize office hours, ask meaningful questions, and show interest, he will go the extra mile to help you. Group projects are straightforward. I highly suggest taking him for any class he teaches!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Chiou is by far one of the most caring and supportive professors I have ever had. If you show up to class, show interest, and you are a dedicated person there is no reason you will not succeed. He continuously has acted as both a personal and professional mentor to me and I hope you have the opportunity to take his class.

Professor Chiou is extremely unprofessional. He does not grade fairly, does not explain his rationale for grades, and does not show any understanding of the course content or willingness to support students both in and outside the classroom. He's extremely rude & inconsiderate and overall the worst professor I've ever had - and I am graduating now.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The most unorganized professor ever. The classes are randomly online without any notice. He's a very tough grader when it comes to group projects. Made us do a 45 min presentation and then it was never graded. Worst professor for this class.

Professor Chiou is great. He is very knowledgeable about finance. He cares about his students and wants everyone in the class to do well. The class is difficult, sure, but he makes the lectures engaging and fun. He does have an accent, but he is still understandable and clear.

Professor Chiou's investments class was a class I looked forward to attending. Before taking this class, I was hesitant because of the reviews on here. I was surprised to find out that Professor Chiou is extremely kind, funny, helpful, and knowledgeable. The course itself is hard, but he fosters a very positive learning environment.

Before taking Dr.Chiou I was nervous because of his score here, but, I came back to say he was a great professor. He may be a tough grader on group projects, but he gives you ample time to ask him for feedback before its due date. His tests can sometimes be random, but the prep you receive is enough to do well. He made class engaging and fun.
tough class, but really helpful!

Three exams and two group projects. Lectures are unorganized, disconnected from assigned reading, and often rambling. Even with perfect scores on all 3 exams, your grade can be lowered by 2 group projects which are assigned alongside vague grading criteria and obscure guidance. Forced to teach yourself or learn nothing at all.

Run! Tough grader! The worst professor in all my classes! I got an average of 95 in midterms and final but ending in a B? The grades for cases and group projects are so mean! You will learn nothing from the class, but learn all by yourself! If you truly want to learn something, take another professor! Please!!!

The teaching style relies on Connect. He also lectures on material that barely shows up on exam. Exams are "how well can you memorize the textbook?".  He makes you do a lot of group work and believes that you should spend 15+ hours OUTSIDE of class to work on the material. Ridiculous, don't take.
Run. Why would you take this professor? Bc you have to- there is literally no other reason.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is the worst professor I have ever got in my life. Do not take any subject that he is teaching unless you wanna fail. He is very racist and is very partial to Chinese students and gives them better grade even if you are as good as them, even though you get A in all mid terms expect a lower grade as he wants to destroy students future. -R

Even with my full attention I cannot understand 1/2 of the words he says. VERY hard to understand which makes him a pretty bad teacher. I'm only one week into the class but I already know it will be challenging due to his accent and my lack of interest in finance. From what I can understand, for the tests I should just read the book. Don't take.

DON'T TAKE PAUL CHIOU!!! Even if you get all A's on midterms and finals you will end up getting a bad score due to this 1500 points. His class is full of cold-calling, and there is no learning in the class. In class, he cracks silly jokes to make it fun, but it will COST YOU SM.
An absolute incredible professor!

The content of his lectures had little to do with the content of his exams. He also creates non-anonymous question boxes for students to write about him, and then makes a slide show of the students' comments and spends an hour in class countering them. He liked to randomly call students' names, which made me feel embarrassed.

Avoid taking him for FINA6200. He acts like he's helpful but when you really ask for help, he judges, tells you to visit a stress center or reach out to his TA. Expects you to put in 10+ hours of studying each week as if no one has a full-time job or other classes. Be ready to be judged harshly on your group project too. Absolutely hated him...

He's a nice guy but you can barely understand him while he's lecturing. If you are not willing to read every chapter beforehand and teach yourself, you will have a hard time. He knows what he's talking about but just can't teach it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Avoid if possible. I learnt everything from the textbook, lectures were unclear and unhelpful. Professor made a lot of weird comments. Lectures never matched up with syllabus or the modules on canvas. Projects had no clear direction and when asked in OH would give minimal help. Take anyone else

This class was a trainwreck. Takes him 5-10 minutes to explain what another prof could easily explain with far more clarity in three sentences. Very unclear delivery (heavy accent if that matters to you). Constant noises over zoom made it hard to concentrate. I don't feel he respected students. Easy A, but if you actually want to learn, stay away.

Just great overall. He doesn't only teach from the TB and slides: he actively asks us questions from everyday news and opens up discussion on what effects each news has on the financial market and the economy. Not only that, he encourages students to do researches and provides detailed feedbacks in return. Life isn't complete if you don't take him.

At first I thought that this guy is a joke, but later on, it became clear that he really cares about students and is always available for help and explanations. We have done a vast number of questions in class so it was a lot of practice. Mid-terms are done as multiple choice questions on connect, so it's easy to get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Great professor!!! Highly recommend!!!
He doesn't teach at all, and you will leave the class having acquired no new knowledge.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Does not teach Investments. He teaches Corporate Finance in a Investments Class. He gives out 4 projects which are weighed heavily but teaches nothing about the concepts which are supposed to be used in those projects. He does not give enough feedback and cuts points if there are no page number on the projects submitted. DO NOT TAKE HIM. WORST PROF

This professor shouldn't be teaching! He has no real life experience and is simply the worst at teaching. Nobody can learn in this class despite the will to learn. Give less than poor feedback despite students asking him again and again. Very vague in grading and to be honest teaches Corporate finance in an Investments class.

I got all A's for homework and exams, but he decided to give me a 0% for participation and lowered my final grade to a C and later when I've tried contacting him, he refused to give specific details on what he discredited me for. I've been to all the classes and did not speak much, but it was surely not an F.

Very disappointed with this professor towards the end of the semester. How can one get an A- for the course after getting As for all of the exams? Obviously something is not right with the grading system. Seems like a nice person at first but it this will haunt you back at the end of the semester. Definitely avoid if possible.

This professor is easily the worst I've had at Northeastern. Here's the deal: the guy can barely speak English so in his lectures you can't understand any of what he's saying. My grade was an A until the end of the semester. In the final he added way more questions than the practice so I ran out of time.

Attendance is part of grade. Being in class doesn't help you complete the numerous cases or papers you could just look at the slides and learn it faster. Assigns multiple group papers and projects all due around the same time (like two days apart) and doesn't grade them and give them back in a timely manner. Trying to learn? take someone else.
The exam is not hard if you are prepared, while it is not easy to get an A for the group paper.

This prof does not lecture very clearly. The material is scattered and so is he. If you want to get an A you should see him during office hours to clear up his expectations for exams, participate as much as you can in class, and read the textbook. Make sure you have a good group for the project because that is a big part of your grade too.

Avoid at all cost. His grading is outrageous. Feebacks are worthless. You will learn nothing with this guy. You're better off taking Pichler or Muscolino. Near impossible to get an A in his class as the course is based on group projects only and two exams.

His assignments will scare you, but when you come out of this class with an A (its absolutely impossible to fail his class unless you're lazy) you will know it was all worthy. Now I fall in love with investments, a life-long useful subject. Just make sure you study. That's the key!
What a guy! Should be a stand up comedian!

Chiou the best professor I have had during my Northeastern! Once you get to know him, he's really down to earth and intelligent.  He will take the time to teach you as long as you don't slack off. Always willing to meet you  and available by e-mail. Use common sense and you'll be good to go.

Very hard to understand, I learnt almost nothing from the course, and passed only because I had some previous knowledge from FINA2201 and was taking Corporate Finance at the same time. I highly don't recommend him. The class is tough, and he makes it even more difficult, lacks clear explaining.

He is very very helpful and cares a lot about his students. At first I was horrified with his class because I couldn't understand him, but after two weeks everything got better. His exams are a joke, he puts the exact same exercises we do in class. It a very easy class.
Professor Chiou is a great teacher!  He assigned many useful projects and help us engage the real world. The portfolio projects allow us to manage money like fund managers.

I took two of Chiou's classes. Although they were VERY challenging, I learned a lot.  Chiou is very helpful, and provided class a lot of information beyond the scope of textbook.  I love the portfolio management project very much.   I believe it will help me in my future career in finance.

Chiou is an excellent professor and always is helpful to students.  He responses our questions very quick and help us to make sense cases.  Read your text and do your homework, the test is very reasonable.  He is a friendly nice guy.

Dr. Chiou is a difficult professor but he is very friendly and provides many opportunities for students to boost their grade. One of the only Finance professors that doesn't use Aplia. You need to put in time outside of class as his lectures are not very useful but overall he is a nice Professor that is always looking to improve his abilities

Do not, by any means, take class with him and encourage him in thinking he can relate to students. His English is abysmal, after class he would explain things in Mandarin to Chinese students, leaving the rest of us dumbfounded. He was rude, unhelpful, and unwilling to take steps to help in helping students learn. Don't fight this battle.

He's a nice person. But he shouldn't teach. His teaching style is confusing, unfocused and very very ineffective. The tests are a joke, as multiple choice questions do not have actual answers as the choices. Get ready to teach yourself everything, and avoid Prof. Chiou as much as possible.

He is literally the most ridiculous person I have ever met. His lectures and in class notes make no sense. It's hard to understand what he's saying half the time. He doesn't explain anything even when you ask for help and refuses to admit he may have made a mistake. I would not recommend him to anyone.

This guy is ridiculous. You cant understand a single thing he says, he is evasive and unhelpful every which way, and when you come to him for help he only tells you to either sleep less and study more. Every single moment around him was painful and I wont wish thin pain and misery on anyone. Wost teacher ever, avoid at all costs.
Hardest class in my life

10/10 prof. He has a very witty, dry sense of humor that I enjoyed a ton. No quizzes/exams, just reading assignments paired with a few questions once or twice a week + a final presentation with a legal topic of ur choosing. He's understanding when it comes to extensions + sick days, just be sure to email him before the due date/class.

take him! 1-2 short hw assignments per week, and one final project which is up to you to choose the topic and very interesting, he grades mostly on completion. super nice and def cares about what he is teaching. attendance is mandatory but you can join thru zoom if sick. overall a very chill and easy class.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Quinn is a really nice guy and I would definitely recommend taking his class. He has an underrated sense of humor that makes class more entertaining. It is really easy to get an A, just try your best and show up to class as most assignments are graded on completion and attendance is graded.

25% participation, 25% short answer textbook questions, 50% final presentation. I really liked his teaching style, feel like I learned a lot. Probably not hard to do well even if you don't care, though.

This was a really easy class - ended with a very high A, most of the assignments are on a completion basis - however, he was a really sweet and kind guy, gave really good feedback on our final presentation(50% of our grade), canvas was well organized  and classes were really content heavy.

Professor Quinn was passionate, caring, and did his best to make a very mundane topic enjoyable for the class. Attendance was mandatory, but you could attend via Zoom if you were sick. Most assignments graded on completion and you can tell he cares a lot about what hes teaching

I really enjoyed Professor Quinn's course. Most of the material was very straightforward and sourced from the textbook. His lectures are super slide/reading heavy so get ready to read about legal statutes. He tries to make sure everyone feels welcomed and everyone's opinion is heard which I enjoy! Attendance is mandatory too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: i enjoyed going to this class a lot! very straight forward expectations and fairly interesting lectures. we had weekly-ish homework assignments that were graded on completion and a final presentation on any law related topic of your choice and no exams
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Best NEU Professor, I never write reviews but this man deserves a 5!

He is awesome. No exams, just a few case studies and a final presentation. Lots of extra credit assignments. If you read the case and prepare for those discussion questions, you will be fine. I really enjoy his class and sometimes he will bring some snacks to share with us.
Great alsome professor I have ever had!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Best professor at NEU over the past five years!
One of the best professors here, very caring about students and their careers!
Best professor at Northeastern.
Obliterates your grade without telling you anything if you don't go to class.

By far the best professor at Northeastern. Such a caring, passionate, and funny professor who made me, a spring semester senior, want to come to every class. He offers so many extra credit opportunities and has the class vote to amend the syllabus in the beginning of the semester to make his policies even more fair.

Great professor, one of my favorites of all time. Replies to emails fast, is super attentive, and is always willing to help. Solid feedback. Let's students make decisions (eg: we voted as a class to not have to read the textbook). Short papers are straightforward, and the final essay is simple. Very smart guy who makes you want to learn. 11/10 prof
One of the best at NEU!
THE Best NEU professor that I have taken class from - humorous, accommodating, and inspiring. I came to class not only to fulfill attendance requirements, but simply wanted to.
The best professor at NEU, humorous and caring. Glad I took his class!
Very respectful, kind, and accommodating. Best of all NEU profs.
Very good profession, so fair and caring of students, and very accommodating as well.
Wonderful professor, IMO.

Very humorous professor. I also read all the comments and was surprised to see negative sentiments. I am sorry for them but I agree with the majority. This professor is wonderful. If you come to class and don't miss too much, you will get a good grade.
Very encouraging and caring professor!
Extremely accommodating!!!! Very fair and caring about his students. Great person.
Wonderful professor. Very fair, caring, and helpful.

Didn't use text book, no exams, but heavy reading, and participation in class is mandatory. Participation is 20% of the Final grade, he can give what every grade he want due to your behavior in class, crazy
Didn't have a clear grading criteria, don't know why Final essay is graded so tough. Beside that, a nice man with humor in class, but didn't learn much in his course

I thought I'm going to get an A until he gives the final grade, never seen any Prof ever give a 6 out of 20 in a Presentation. Not a fair grade and not a fair professor, don't come, or you will regret at last

I was deceived into attending his class by his previous 5 rating. The course read a lot, discussion based, and the class was relatively easy. However, the professor appeared to be friendly, but in fact, he would stab student in the back at the end of the term by giving terrible grade. Not a good man

Awful! On the surface and with a friendly demeanor, but behind the scenes with a narrow mind, he will remember every class you missed and give you a 0. Extremely low score on group presentation and won't warn you or telling you the reason. I never seen such a professor in all these years in the United States

Discussion heavy class. Speak less and absent willl result in extremely low grade on 20% of participation. One of my classmate failed for it. Tough grader, grade everthing at the end. If you cannot guarantee that you will be fully engaged in discussions and attend every class, then it is best not to be fooled by his humor and friendly in class.

I've never felt strongly enough about a professor to leave a review on here, but Professor Qiao is the real deal. He makes an effort to connect with his students and was incredibly accommodating throughout the semester. His lectures are engaging and he's incredibly knowledgeable. If you have the opportunity to take his class, you absolutely should.
Professor Kunyuan Q is an amazing professor, and I very much enjoyed his lectures and in-class interactions. He is brilliant and I just want to say he is wonderful.
Prof Qiao is very knowledgeable, fun, and caring!! He is my favorite professor and his lectures are so interesting :-)

Prof. Kunyuan Qiao is a very, very great professor - he cared so much about his students, knowing where we are from and designed very fun lectures and classroom interactions. I missed his class and should have done a better job!

Professor Kunyuan Qiao is the sweetest and nicest professor I have ever met!!! He cared so much about students and made lectures and class discussions fun and very easy to follow (although his questions are often intellectually challenging). I very much enjoyed his class and his cooking was amazing (the potato bread haha!).

Professor Qiao had very interesting lectures, which were organized by his RAPPORT framework and we did develop rapport ourselves! Very nice, accommodating, and caring professor. Would take his class again!

Professor Kunyuan Qiao is my favorite throughout my years at Northeastern. He cared so much about his students, bringing foods and even cooking for us! His lectures were more than fun and I very much enjoyed the class. I even expected to see him for the class. I was so luck to have selected his course!
Professor Qiao is my favorite professor at Northeastern University - he cares so much about students and is so accommodating. I am very lucky to have selected his class!

Professor Qiao is THE best professor at Northeastern University based on my experience. His lectures were so fun and most of us enjoyed. He was also so caring and made the class very inclusive. Would recommend to others!!!!
The best professor I have had at NEU. Extremely nice and informative.

i love cai laoshi. this class was super easy. there are dictations basically every class (5-7 characters & pinyin) but she is so forgiving with them. homework was assigned once in a while. she always lets you know what's going to be on the exams and the final is just a skit. if you show up and participate, you will definitely get an A.

The sweetest professor ever. She is so kind and flexible. There are daily quizzes/dictations, but if you study the material for 30-50 minutes each day you will do just fine. She is a lenient grader and the same applies to the homework. Exam revisions are also offered! As long as you make an effort and participate in class, it'll be an easy A.

Professor Cai was a wonderful teacher, and I really enjoyed taking her class. She is very caring, understanding, but do note that there is an emphasis on participation/actively attending class (in-person) as it is a language class. I just didn't enjoy the service learning portion of the class (as we had to cover transportation costs), but yeah!
do not take this class. ridiculous amount of work. required voyage to cambridge every week. somewhat fair grader
Even if you kinda suck at Chinese she's pretty forgiving. If you show that you're working hard and do all the homework she's pretty great.
she treats you like her children. fair on grading. you just have to practice the materials for 30-50 mins per day and you will be get an A. easy adn fun class. you get to meditate as well.

I took her class, only because I had no option to choose but her. She doesn't explain any characters during class nor write them on the board.  Once you get out from class you don't remember anything. Sometimes I get overwhelmed with her yelling to students when they don't speak Chinese like native speakers.
Very very nice and  teacher. Her class is really easy because you can see the exam before taking the class.

Really nice prof, wants you to do well, all quizzes/tests are done through blackboard. Quiz reviews done before class and are mostly the exact same thing you will see. Lets you see exams before having to take them.
Tough grader, literally take stats with anyone else. He takes off points for no reason what so ever.

Take psych stats with any other professor. He grades mostly on vocab that you have to go search the definitions for, and a lot of the time exact book definitions would be wrong. 23 of the exam werent even on math. I have no idea why he has a 4 for helpfulness because I went to office hours and he seemed confused that i was there.  Terrible class

He's burt out, doesnt care, doesnt teach in class but grades brutally. He's kind of funny but other then that he sucks. Maybe stats is fine but research design is the worst course ive taken at NU. I HATE the man becuase of how poorly he conducted the class. He stops teaching midway through the school year!!!

Horrible teacher!!!He makes easy things so complicated. He says examples instead of writing them for a MATH class. You cant follow the book because he teaches it a different way. Students would ask for help he would say " what do you think", students would ask " how do you put formulas in the calculator he would say "figure it out". DO NOT TAKE HIM

This man is a horrible teacher. If you had taken STATS in highschool then you will probably get it, however I didnt , everyone complained about the call and how he taught. He is so scattered, that he becomes confusing ! the Final was really long took all of the two hours, DONT TAKE !!! if you need step by step process.

I LOVED him! I had him for Stats and he made it fun and easy. Used real life psych examples which helped to see its real world applications. Answers questions well and will discuss test results if you ask. TAKE HIM! Also an advisor so he is a good man to know. Laid back and easy going. Makes sure you know the material before the test. LOVED him!

This guy is hilarious. He will make awkward comments during class that just makes everyone laugh. The homework load isn't terrible, you only have an assignment the week of any of the quizzes. All of the quizzes are the same format, so it's never really a surprise. Quinn is great at teaching, and I learned quite a bit. Good class!
I know a lot of people like him, and he is a nice enough guy, but personally his teaching style is really not for me and coming to this class was painful.
HOT AS HELL
He is a really great person and a pretty good professor.  I did well in Stats but I have heard he is one of the most difficult.  For an easy A, get some one else.

If you have any high school mathematical background, this class is fluff.  I had to take it depsite my AP stat credit (counts for everything BUT stat for psych).  But despite the fact that I knew everything already, I still enjoyed the class, cause prof Qu
great guy
Great great great teacher. A little absentminded, but very helpful and very throrough and clear. Fair tests and fair grader... take his class!

Requried for Psyc majors.  I had taken Stat courses before so it was fairly easy for me but it's not an easy class for those who aren't familiar with statistics.  He does a pretty good job of breaking it down though.  Typical crazy math professor
Slightly scatterbrained at points, but he knows the material and knows how to break it down for math illiterates like me.  The tests are pretty difficult, but manageable if you study.
I think Quinn is great-he has that psychology professor hair...his tests are tough-but he explains everything very well.
Very helpfull, really makes the effort to help everyone A+A+A+A+A+A+A+

The material is tricky, but he made it pretty clear and less intimidating. He's always willing to help, and overall he's a pretty cool guy. Everything on the tests he went over in class- no surprises.
He's a little bit tough for a stats prof...but i slacked off
Very cool guy, easy class.
Definition of a goofy guy. Michael know how to balance the line of work and play. Ma boy also gender neautral af luh you Prof Ruff, would love to light one up w you foo

Took him summer 1 and he's very nice and understanding. If you need extra time for an assignment and email him beforehand, he will give it to you. His lectures are good and he's just a nice dude in general. The material itself is hard but he is very helpful.
One of the nicest human beings on the planet. Seriously. He cares so much about his students. He's a terrific professor. I would highly recommend going to office hours.

His class is half lecture on the slides he posts to canvas and half exercises from the packet he hands outs. He's got chicken stratch handwriting and his lectures can be boring but he's a nice guy. I found connect homework to be helpful. Connect quizzes (online)(10%). Connect homework (15%), midterm (20%), Final exam (30%).

Took this class online and everything was extremely organized. You knew exactly what you needed to do and all the assignments given were incredibly useful in terms of preparing for an exam and learning the material.

His lectures are good. Unorganized though. For our most recent midterm, he made the midterm earlier and created lied about the reason, stating that the course was ahead of schedule, which is false as we are on schedule per the syllabus. He made the exam early just so they have more time to grade, which is very detrimental to students.

I'm 3 weeks into his class but I can already tell why he's rated so highly. Great at explaining, very approachable, breaks down a difficult class into easily understandable sections. Also love how affirmative he is when we ask questions, it makes the process of attending his class so much more enjoyable.
Great lecturer considering how boring the material is and incredibly willing to help in OH!

Raj is the goat at ALGO!!!
The most difficult part of the class are the exams, HackerRanks on HW's, and writing proofs.
Very slow and thorough lectures. Is accepting of students questions during and after lectures.
He also was a co-author of the discrete textbook, and has over 7,000 citations.

Raj was an amazing professor! I was very nervous before taking Algorithms, however, he made things much easier. He's an amazing lecturer. He answers every single question to make sure students understand what's going on, and his office hours were amazing. He's also very smart, with over 7000 citations I believe. I can't recommend him enough!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Really nice and empathetic and good at explaining
The best professor I have ever studied under and definitely the best course structure.
Amazing lectures! Starts from the basics and builds up as the lecture progresses.
A great professor. Very approachable and extremely patient.

One of the best professors in the CS department. His lectures are absolutely amazing. He stops the lecture to answer any student's doubt no matter how trivial it is with the same enthusiasm as he teaches. You will definitely learn a lot in his class.

Enthusiastic and dedicated towards teaching. He explains every concept with detail and takes a lot of efforts to make it understandable to each and every student. For a change, I like going to his classes. Assignments are difficult but midterms and quizzes are on bit easy side.  Grading is fair. Overall, I recommend him without any doubt!!
great pofessor
excellent prof

This is one of the best prof. you have to take course under his guidance. Excellent teaching and explanation. He takes efforts from his side to help the students. Which I haven?t seen in other prof. Assignments are tough , quizzes are ok. Grading is fair, totally how you performed in the coursework.
He teaches very well. Great Guy!!!! HomeworkAssignments are way to tough. Examzzz are cool....!!! Final Project was very innovative and technically challenged....!!

Great guy! Class was a little slow moving at times, but that's really Fell's problem. If you put a decent effort in, you'll get an easy A and learn a lot. Some of the written HW was difficult, but everything else was very fair.
Great guy and great teaching. Always available for extra help. This class does require a lot of work, but overall it was easy.

Raj is a tough professor, but you will learn a great deal in his courses. He is a nice guy, and willing to help those who need it.  Grading was fair overall. I usually enjoyed coming to class, which is strange.
Was a tough class for me but Raj did a great job presenting it.  Always willing to put in the extra time to make sure students understand the material.  Very organized classes.
The Homeworks were at times overly difficult when compared with in class amterial.  Exams were usually easier than HW.
Class can be boring. He teaches wierd concepts. Does give out A's alot though and you can get extensions on stuff easily
Raj is a nice person who cares very much about whether a person is learning.  He also focuses on effort for those who don't get the grades but try hard.  I would take another class with him.
The Raj! Really nice guy, and will give you an A for effort. Unfortunately, he rarely quizzes on what's covered. Somewhat easy course, though.
She's really passionate about the things she talks about which rlly helps you get through the long lectures lol. highly recommend taking her!!

Rachel is very kind and loves to talk to students. Looking over the assigned textbook chapters is a good way to make sure you are keeping up, but just taking notes during class is enough to do well on the exams. Exams are pretty straight forward, she gives a lot of extra credit and her grading system is to your benefit.

This has been my favorite professor thus far. Her teaching style is incredible. She will explain the same concept in numerous ways to ensure your learning style aligns with one of the explanations. She is approachable and makes class interesting. You must study for the tests and take notes, but they are not tricky. I recommend her highly.
She gives A TON of extra credit. Def more work than other sections but not hard at all.

One of the best classes I've taken at Northeastern. She is an amazing professor—super passionate about the subject and genuinely wants students to care and understand it. You learn a lot, but it never feels overwhelming because she explains everything so well. Readings are reasonable and well chosen. Would absolutely take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very intelligent and great teacher who is very very clear about what you need to succeed in the class and provides you with all of the resources and materials to do so. Will assign a good amount of readings but SPECIFIES PAGES and paragraphs so you don't have to read entire chapters, and all readings are super helpful for tests. easy to succeed

it's nice to see how passionate professor meyer is about sociology. it makes lectures very enjoyable and easy to follow along to. her exams are very straightforward. if you show up to lecture and do the readings (not graded), you're guaranteed a good grade. i can't recommend her enough!
Just somewhat pay attention and understand the course material and you will do great!

Prof. Meyer was great! The class was a piece of cake and she gave many opportunities for extra credit even though your main grade is comprised of 3 equally weighted tests. Lectures were straightforward and as long as you took notes on that and skimmed the textbook you were fine.

Each week there are biweekly assignments posted alternating each week; one week is discussion post writing and the next are chapter reading quiz and writing application. You really need to read the textbook in order to do the assignments. The teacher is nice with deadlines and extension, his grading and feedback is also helpful and generous.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The online aspect really made this course hard for me, but for others. than, the class was a good course with a great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Honestly, chill professor esp with deadlines.. js make sure to let him know in less than a week after the assignment. He has biweekly quizzes and discussion posts. Final group project sucks if ur group is buns but oh well. Finished with a 91 and it was asynchronous. Pretty easy if you put in a minimum # of work.
Very tough professor who rarely gives feedback.  He will grade by criteria not listed on the rubrics (if there is one).

It's ok. Quizzes weekly on Respondus w webcam, but the questions aren't insanely hard. They rely on what you read in the textbook, so you do have to study. The simulation he makes you do is actually the area that I learned the most from, but it is graded way too harshly. Only one person got above a B- and it's 20% of ur grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Sieloff is knowledgeable and nice but makes the work meticulous for no reason. Online accounting was not a hard class overall but he made the assignments super time consuming. Group projects were a huge part of the grade and he is strict on grading everything.

This Professor has pros and cons.
Cons: Tough grader, unclear instructions. Heavy workload.
Pros: Approachable, quick grader, great communicator-gets back to you very quickly.

Overall due to unclear instructions and not knowing what I could do to get a good grade, I would not recommend this Professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Requires you to download malware (lockdown browser) for tests which, let's be honest, is useless because smartphones, 2nd computer, roomates... Sends pretentious emails, often lacks punctuation. No feedback on homeworks unless you ask for it which is fine if I wasn't paying for the class.

He's a nice enough guy but going to class felt pointless because he just reads from a powerpoint. He is very tough grader, and usually won't do partial credit for math problems which sucks. Tests where hard because he wouldn't give detail about what we were being tested on. Textbook is ESSENTIAL as half of his test is based off of it.

Really accessible: literally spent two hours answering all my questions with great patience. He's kinda a tough grader and asks for a looot but I really liked him. Go to class and do the readings you'll be fine. Ask questions and think of everything possible when writing essays and doing projects you'll do great.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Really nice guy, unclear with paper/hw expectations. Easy to skip class as lectures were rarely relevant to the hw or presentations.

Although he doesn't give much guidance on the semester-long project, he has clear grading criteria. The tests are hard, but you can do well if you just read the chapter and look over his powerpoint. Class is a challenge but definitely not impossible.

Professor Sieloff is a great teacher. He knows what he is talking about and is passionate about the topics. His tests are tough but if you read the textbook and participate in class, they are reasonable. He assigns homework most nights but it is never a lot. I would take another one of his classes in the future.
He's strict on homework and tests are hard but presentations aren't bad. Just pay attention in class and put in the time out of class.

He's  a great guy overall. Very understanding and grades reasonably. The tests are hard and come mainly from the textbooks but to understand the concepts better you should still pay attention in class. I managed to pull of an A after hours of reading the textbook and going over the ppts but I'd definitely take him unless you want the easy way out

He's a good professor compared with other professors teaching the same course. The exams for this course we're so hard. There were many presentations. Not a easy grader but not too hard. Participation was essential.

Professor Sieloff is a pretty passionate teacher, but unfortunately his lectures had nothing to do with what was on the exams. The tests are very detailed and you have to read the book to do well. A lot of the stuff on the test is not even mentioned in class.

The class itself is easy/common sense, but Sieloff manages to make it harder than it should be. Don't get me wrong, he's a great lecturer and always gives relevant and interesting examples. But, his tests are extremely detail oriented and he's a hard grader when it comes to homework. I had to work REALLY hard in this class, but got an A in the end.

Sieloff was my section's professor for the on-line class. He is a bit tough when it comes to grading HW and participation, but the quizzes were very easy. He was also extremely understanding when my group ran into issues for the group project. I would definitely recommend him.
He is a good professor. He use a lot of PPT in class. He grade HW very strict. But He is a very nice willing to help.

lectures are difficult to follow. she goes through examples without really explaining why/what she's doing, makes the lectures difficult to sit through. 3 exams worth 75% of the grade, the average of which generally hovers around ~70%, i don't think she's the worst prof in the world but idk why she has a 4.7.
One of the best economics professors out there. Beyond caring and understanding, and is always willing to put in the extra effort to make the class a great experience.

Super helpful professor and very knowledgeable about her work. She wants you to succeed, evident by the in class prep and recordings being available for each class. She expects you to put in effort and participate but you learn a lot. If you pay attention during class and do the problems you'll be set. She is very helpful if you have questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very helpful outside class especially during office hours. She provides enough materials such as practice problems for each topic and practice exams that are relevant to the exam so you can be well prepared. She makes it hard to not do well and wants success for her students. Also she's got a good sense of humor.

Taken over 3 classes with her, highly recommend her for econ classes. She's a good teacher, encourages participation, but will not penalize you if you don't talk at all (me) as long as you show up to class. The way she teaches makes it easy for students to understand, which means you have less to study for exams since you already know it.

BEST PROFESSOR!!
would recommend.

Great professor!   
would recommend.
THIS IS THE BEST PROFESSOR IN THE WORLD!!!!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Her lectures r insanely hard to follow

Professor Xiaolin is very knowledgeable in economics and statistics, and she provided many resources to help you succeed. All modules were posted on canvas; professor outlined what we would cover each week; example questions, study materials, and many other resources were all posted online.
She is such a kind and fun professor; the classroom environment is amazing and she seems so happy to teach. Such an awesome professor.

Professor Shi is very thoughtful and supportive of her students. She posts all course materials on Canvas, including plenty of practice problems and exam study guides. She also records every lecture on Zoom in addition to teaching in person, making it easy to catch up if you miss a class. Highly recommended!

Professor Shi is a wonderful professor; she is interested in student success, appreciative of critical thinking, engaged with her students, and flexible to student needs/concerns. Extremely funny as well. Highly recommend! btw, no final exam, just a group research paper, which you can get a lot of help/guidance from her.

Very friendly and helpful professor. Grading consists of exams, homework assignments, participation, and a big final paper. It is an easy course but don't slack, she can tell and will be less kind. Most classes are lecture-based. She is not the best lecturer but makes up for it by being available for individual review with every student.

Prof. Shi is an absolute angel. She wants you to succeed and is willing to go out of her way to make sure you get there. She is the most helpful professor I've ever had during office hours; take advantage of that. Her classes are lecture heavy, but she uses a good amount of repetition to make everything stick. Pay attention = good grade.
Amazing professor! Makes a tough subject extremely engaging. Tests are fair, lectures are recorded, and she wants to see you succeed. She really knows what she's doing, and I learned a lot.
Professor Shi has a strong academic background and extremely responsible attitude. I have learnt a lot from her. She is the best! God bless this woman!

I learned A LOT from Prof Xiaolin, such as having faith in what you love, treating everyone equally, and being sincere with people as if never been hurt before. She is always cheerful and optimistic, passionate about teaching, and full of respect, love, and sympathy toward students. I feel highly honored to have met such a role model in my life.

Professor Shi is very good; she often interacts with the students in class, memorizes the names of the students by heart, and sometimes has a roll call interaction; such a learning atmosphere is enjoyable. She also posts all the notes and in-class practice with annotations on Canvas. I think Professor Shi is excellent!

Professor Xiaolin is sweet and always open to answering students questions. However, she is terrible at teaching, has an un-engaging lecture based class which is not structured very well, and you end up learning nothing. Another $7k down the drain for a useless class where I have to memorize all the problems for the exam, and then forget everything

Professor Shi is so nice and really cares about her students. She is a good professor but often goes on tangents that become difficult to follow. If you keep up with the material and make sure you pay attention you'll have a great time in her class.

Prof. Shi is very easy to talk to. She's willing to help you anytime with anything you ask. She's super organized, you can literally find everything in Canva files. But she could improve on teaching us how to run regression on STATA.

Professor Shi has the sweetest soul! She is uper understanding, caring, respectful, and such a good person. We also learned a lot, and the course was challenging but well-organized. Zoom is always an option, so no one would miss content if they couldn't attend class. Plus, every lecture was recorded and easy to access shortly after each class.

Professor Shi attaches great importance to attendance, and each lesson is very careful, and uploads study materials and class notes in a very timely manner after the lesson, an there are consolidation exercises in each lesson, which is very helpful to my study.

Professor Shi is the funniest professor I've had! The course will be super difficult to understand in the first weeks and you will want to cry and get angry at her, but give her time! Eventually, you'll totally understand it and succeed and realize it wasn't even that hard to start with.

I loved taking this class with Xiaolin! She can be a bit scattered in class sometimes, but she's always willing to go back and clarify questions. She's very accessible if you need help and her tests are fair. I found it helpful to go into class after skimming some of the course content beforehand, which stopped me from getting lost !

Participation and attendance matter. About 8 easy homework assignments graded on completion. 3 hard exams, but she is very clear about what is on the exam and provides great practice problems and wants to give students as many points as possible. Super caring and good lectures. Scales final grade.

WOW! Best online experience I have ever had with a professor here at Northeastern. She is very organized, and everything is accessible via Canvas modules. She also has Zoom recordings posted immediately after class. Very tech-savvy, and it is much appreciated.
The BEST Professor I have ever had at Northeastern! Very good at explaining difficult concepts. Always made herself available for her students outside of class time.

First of all, Xiaolin has the sweetest soul! The course was extremely entertaining and pragmatical. She forced us to think outside of the box and encouraged dedication and attention to details. If you wanna take econ2316, highly recommend prof. Shi!
She is amazing at accommodating students. If you were sick, missed a class, etc. would make zoom so accessible. She was also very accessible outside of office hours.
Phenomenal organization and online canvas page. EVERYTHING she talks about is uploaded and zoom classroom has past recordings which help out a TON if you miss a class.

Although the course was one of the most intellectually challenging of them all, Xiaolin clearly communicated the material and guided me through it masterfully. btw, she has the sweetest soul ever! Highly recommend!!

Xiaolin knew what she was talking about and was straight to the point in teaching what we needed to know. She was effective, especially at helping us model which was the main draw of the course. Would definitely take her again.

Professor Shi told us on the first day this was one of her favorite courses to teach and I can see why. For such a complicated subject, she explains everything so easily and makes it understandable. She spent the first week going over statistics and it helped so much. Overall, the best professor I have ever had at Northeastern.

Sweetest professor ever, but gives the most unstructured, convoluted lectures I've ever seen. She just constantly starts to teach something and then interrupts herself to explain something that's irrelevant and may or may not ever return to the original topic. Incredibly difficult to follow. Would be better if she just used some slides.
I think her name should be Xiaolin if anyone could correct it. An approachable professor who clearly explains the content and rubric of exams and homework.

Grades came out today so I decided to do a review. I am honestly not the smartest person ever but still got an A. If you show up, partipate, and do the work you will get a good grade. This might be one of the nicest prof's i have ever had fr. She just wants her students to do well and will go the extra mile to do so. Take her if you can.

Was scared because she had no reviews but she is beyond great. The content can be confusing at times but thats also because its a confusing topic. She did a really good job of simplifying everything. Homework was also just graded for completion which was nice. Take her if you get the chance, she is one of the best econ professor's at the school.

Amazing professor. Tests were the examples from class and zoom lectures were available if you missed class. This professor is super tech savvy so the canvas page has EVERYTHING you need (so no textbook!). She has textbook slides though so if you like that it is there. You will do well if you try and leave with so much knowledge. 10/10 recommend.

Overall cool guy, your grade is just 20% quizzes, 40% midterms and 40% final, but the quizzes are similar to the practice ones which is good. You can honestly skip his lectures and just teach yourself the material in the same amount of time and get easy A's on the quizzes.

He's a pretty good professor for Diff Eq; he teaches the material well and the quizzes/tests are very similar to the practice problems. He also posts all of the lecture notes onto Canvas and the notes are well organized. He does occasionally trail off and the lectures can be boring but he's not a bad teacher.

I didn't have to attend to any of his lectures to get an A in the class, the notes are really good if you understand everything on notes you will get 100 in all the quizes ez. Also the slides for notes are only 3-5 pages so lock in.
I liked Dmitre. His lectures were wildly boring and he sometimes struggled with solving the example problems. However, he posted goo

I'm not sure why his rating is low because he's a great professor. He explains concepts thoroughly and focuses on understanding over memorization. He posts practice tests for every quiz/exam(a week+ ahead), and the real tests are very similar. He also tells you what's on the exam a week in advance. Highly recommend.
He posts practice problems which are very similar to his real quizzes and midterms, if you go to recitation you will pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do NOT take this class with Dimitre. He is a nice guy; however, he is extremely difficult to follow and pay attention to. He is very wandering and unformatted. He often backtracks and goes off on tangents. By 3 minutes in, everyone is lost. As I said, reasonably nice guy, BAD teacher.

This was my favorite professor I have had for a math course since being at NEU. He is very nice and has a great understanding of the material. He goes over some notes, then does a few examples which really make sure that you know what you're doing. Go to office hours because he is always willing to help.

I took this class in summer 2, and idk if it was just the summer semester vibes, but I felt he was very forgiving in grading. Partial credit can get you pretty far. Also he allows cheat sheets on exams and there's really no trick questions on quizzes. I had a terrible Calc 2 prof so this class helped me like math again tbh.
he is SO hard to understand and hella unapproachable, buttttt he gives great detailed lecture notes and llets you have a full page of notes for every test including the final.

hes a nice guy but his accent is literally so hard to understand and his vibe is so unapproachable. the lecture notes were very detailed tho, and hes a generous test grader. the course material is not very hard andddd you get an entire page of notes for every exam

Just took this professor and he pretty much sucked. If you really care to learn the material do not take this teacher. I basically taught myself the entire course since Dimitre was hard to understand and really really boring. hw is pretty similar to the quizzes but a few of the quizzes are just super hard for no reason.

This class was one of my least favorite because of not only the course material, but the homework. Homework was very hard.
the professor always mumbles when he speaks and it is hard to understand what is going on in lecture, I could never keep track of what was going on. I will say, he does grade the exams very generously

Professor Dimitre Tzigantchev was honestly the best professor I have ever had in my life, he brings the life into the classroom. While many may have the opposite reaction to him, I think that he brings fun to what is not a very fun subject. He is consistent with class examples, pictures, homework that matches what he puts on exams. GO DIMITRE!
lots of lecturing, most people have already taken calculus so he doesn't hold your hand through it; however, there is lots of help available and most importantly he is really genuinely nice guy.

Honestly I did not feel that Professor Dimitre was bad at all. There were times where he would mix up his words or forget some but it wasn't to the point where I couldn't understand what was being taught. Your grade is dependent on only quizzes and exams, so attending the lectures is important and the practice quizzes given are insanely helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Tzigantchev means well, but his lectures are useless and end up being confusing. If you haven't take calc before I wouldn't recommend him, also your grade is only dependent on quizzes and exams.

Would not recommend. Would often approach him after class to ask questions and would receive vague and unhelpful answers. Doing the research myself proved to be more helpful than reaching out to him for extra help. 

Quizzes involve lots of multiple choice, which I have not seen before in a class like this.

His lectures aren't very helpful he spends time talking about why he;s doing something irrelevant to the problem he's solving. There's a lot of quizzes, if you haven't recently taking an AP calc class in high-school I wouldn't recommend him. His office hours aren't really helpful either and he doesn't check or post the answer to hw assignments.

I took AB Calculus in high school (got a 3 on the exam so I didn't get credit), and this course was a lot easier. I never went to class because I didn't find it very helpful - when I did I just did practice problems on my own. He posts practice tests that are almost exactly like the actual tests - definitely do them!!! Super easy grader.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are informative and this class is not hard if you pay attention. Unfortunately, he doesn't really explain the ideas conceptually and just teaches you how to do the math. Homework is optional and grading comes entirely from quizzes and tests.

Absolutely do not take this class if you haven't taken calculus before. He treats the class like review and is not good at answering questions. The class is mostly self-taught and he often mumbles during lectures.

I do not recommend taking this class with Dimitre if you did not take calculus in high school, my grade is solely on background knowledge. He is hard to follow and strays from the topic. I recommend using the guided notes that he provides. You are graded on 6 highest quizzes of 7, a midterm, and a final. He gave substantial bonus points at the end.

Nice guy, teaches everything by the textbook. He often does calculations in his head which can lead to confusion. Homework is not mandatory as long as you understand the topic. There are quizzes mostly every week but as long as you do the practice problems you'll be ok. Tests have formula sheets.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU DID NOT TAKE AP CALC!!!!! His lectures are very confusing and he has messy hand writing which it very hard to understand. Anyone who didn't talk AP calc is struggling. Also 40% of ur grade is ur final. He does give extra credit though which is kind.

Good guy, kinda bland but he teaches the material in class pretty well. It's all very formulaic. No practice problems, I did no homework the entire time bc it is optional. Do the practice before the quiz because they're pretty much the same thing. Quizzes every week. Tests allow formula sheets.

The professor is incredibly boring and hard to follow. He simply writes on the board without engaging with the class and when presented with a question, he struggles to give a clear answer. The only reason why I am doing ok here is because of AP Calc last year. The quizzes/ tests are graded harshly and minor mistakes cost you greatly.

This variation of calc1 should be avoided as it covers concepts way into diff eq and linear algebra. Prepare to solve systems of ODE and use lambda substitution to find eigenvectors. Dr T did not help digest this rather challenging material which became more apparent as the class shifted to more difficult topics. U can still do well if ur smart tho
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not a great teacher, be prepared to study tons outside of class. Would brush through problems and start new topics without ever explaining the content or the methods used. There is a significant amount of extra material posted on canvas which made it somewhat helpful to practice with, however the entire grade was dependent on quizzes and the final.

Don't buy the textbook! everything you need is uploaded to Canvas including practice quizzes, lecture notes, and homework problems. the math in this class isn't too complicated as long as you listen and participate in class. His lectures can be difficult to follow because he goes on tangents but generally, he isn't a terrible professor.

Professor Tzigantchev means well, but his lectures are often confusing and he does not explain concepts well. He does provide practice quizzes that are very similar to the real quizzes. He also allows you to drop your worst quiz grade. If you haven't taken calc before, you will struggle in this class.

Do not take if you haven't taken calc already. I took calc before and the class was still bad. Someone else grades the tests, and they are not fair with scores. He is hard to understand at times and when asked to elaborate on a concept, he gives no explanation or overcomplicates. Practice problems uploaded are helpful, go to the recitations!!!!

Just shows no care for his students. A genuine disregard for how well his students are doing and whether or not they understand. Class moves so fast and covers topics well into calc 2 despite being a calc 1 class. No matter what you do, just take regular calc 1 for the same credit. Office hours and recitations are available and u will need them.

He is very scattered during lectures and always over complicates concepts. If you go into this class without having any prior knowledge of calculus, you will struggle a lot. The final is worth 40% of your grade and there is only 6 other exams, but the lowest grade will get dropped.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: People aren't lying here. I thought I could beat the odds.....don't take it
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I honestly feel that dimitre is given too much hate. I think he gives enough resources to do good in class like practice problems from a textbook. The test questions are almost exact to these questions and sometimes he will take questions straight from the textbook. If you took AP Calc then this class will be easy, i found it easier than AP Calc.
