Has a very thick Russian accent that can be difficult to understand. There is a separate grader for the class who is not generous with partial credit AT ALL. I already took calc in high school but he tended to overcomplicate things.
If I didn't already know the content of this class from AP classes in high school I would have been completely lost otherwise.

I loved his ambition to practice lessons as much as class time goes. Please come to class every day, it's worth it. There's a separate grader for our exams and he is very evil when it comes to taking off points. Go to office hours and get them points back! ;) P.S. do the homework! It helps you with exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was an average professor-that is if you took calc before. If not, you will have to put in more work for this class. The grading was limited to 6 midterms (60%). If you get a bad grade on one, it takes a toll on your grade. Lastly, the last grade is the final (40%). Idk who graded the midterms but sometimes the points taken off are inaccurate.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His lectures can be difficult to follow, but he does a lot of examples and answers questions. There's biweekly quizzes and a final, all of which he posts a lot of practice material/past quizzes for. If you do those practice problems, you'll do well. There's also two homework assignments, which boost your grade. Funny guy.

This professor was the worst one I have had in my 2 years at Northeastern. You are graded on 6 tests and 1 final exam. With the limited things to be graded on it does not help that the grading in the class is inconsistent, you could have exactly the same work and answer as a person in the class and receive completely different grades. DON'T TAKE!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A walking disaster giving Fs everywhere. Every morning I go to his class and see his face, I have PTSD and wish to un-alive myself. Does NOT care about his students at all. He only grades exams and the final exams. He is devil reincarnated. Horrible sense of styling and his checks are made by BLOOD, SWEAT, AND TEARS of students in his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is literally satan reincarnated. I wanted to unalive myself every single time I stepped out of my dorm because of this class. DO NOT TAKE.

Professor T was very hard to understand in his lectures, and is not clear when answering questions regarding the material. The class is based on 6 exams and the final, and although the difficulty of them was not terrible, the lectures just confused me and I spent a ton of time outside of class before assessments teaching myself!

no quizzes, your entire grade is based off 6 exams and a final. calculators weren't allowed on exams, yet he would teach example problems that you needed calculators for. some questions on the exams weren't like what we did in class nor were they like the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dimitre seems like a good guy who is pretty funny. It's a lecture heavy class and it is very difficult to understand what he is saying or what he means when he is teaching. But he gives homework which most MATH1251 professors do not give that helps to boost your grade.

Not a good professor. Goes over problems on the board that take up almost the whole class and then says they won't be on the tests. Only graded based on 6 tests that are very hard to do well on. Doesn't provide any resources or extra credit opportunities even though class grades aren't good. Easily distracted and goes over class. AVOID HIM!!!

Overall ok professor. Does go over time but if you take his class go to every lecture if you can and DON'T take the remake tests if you are not able to take the test at the original time. The remake test was way harder than the original test for no reason and only has fewer questions with more weight to the points you can get.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The professor constantly talks in circles in a way that makes content so much harder than it needs to be. If you didn't take calculus in high school, don't take his class. No quizzes, and the only grades are tha
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Out of the few Calc 1 professors, he is one of the better ones. Few days before a quiz, he will send out a recitation PDF that literally look like the exam and I would recommend just reviewing that for the night, and you will be all set for his quizzes. He assigns only two homeworks which count as a quiz and is very helpful to boost your grade.

Prof is okay: make sure you, like, really know all the material before bi-weekly quiz day or you'll inevitably lose 10% on ur exam b/c quizzes are ~6-8 Qs each. Grading demonstrates the 'butterfly effect': if you make a small error w/ your numbers you lose most of ur points on that question. Also does a 'meh' job of explaining course material.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I felt like I learned more from my online research to study than from his class. The only reason you make a good grade is from recitation and the online example quiz, not from his teaching. If you try you won't get a bad grade, but if you haven't taken calc in HS the prof is almost impossible to understand. Go to the recitation!

The lectures were straightforward and super easy to understand. As for exams/quizzes, he gives a practice quiz on Canvas that is literally the exact format as the quiz we get, so it was easy to pass. He also was just a nice/funny person.

Lectures are pretty boring, but the quizzes and tests are pretty easy and he gives a lot to practice from so there are never any surprises. Honestly, you probably won't get much out of lecture unless you already know what you're doing, but it doesn't take much time out of class to teach yourself what you need to get a relatively easy A.

Tziagatchev's lectures are soft-spoken and difficult to follow. However, his quizzes are very fair, and he always posts a lot of online material that help prep for the quizzes. There is also endless online (youtube, etc) material for Calc 1, so it's very possible to teach yourself the content necessary for quizzes.

Lots of people find his lectures boring but I got a kick out of them cause he's unintentionally a pretty funny guy. As long as you put in the work on homework you should expect no surprises on his tests and quizzes and get a good grade.

Hard to understand accent, very messy handwriting, and can talk in circles but you can tell he's trying. He's an easy grader and gives lots of partial credit just make sure you understand the material. His explanations aren't very helpful, and many times can confuse you more. Although he's nice I would take with Prof George instead
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hard class to take at 8 in the morning (or any time). Lectures are dull, and he moves very quickly over new topics and on to the next . Will answer questions, but gives long, meandering answers that may or may not give you the answer you needed. Be prepared to spend lots of time outside of class teaching yourself the parts you don't understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I don't know why he has such a bad rep. Lectures are boring, but it's an easy A with no surprises! Only thing is that you need to have a solid background in calc to understand. His quizzes are very straightforward, exactly like his practice quizzes. He is so sweet and always willing to help even though his feedback isn't great. Love him!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: One of the worst math professors I've ever had. He puts a slide up and talks about it for 30 minutes without writing anything down. The notes he does right down don't make sense and he talks himself in circles. Wouldn't reccomend.
Quizzes are straightforward, and there are no surprises. He's very nice, but his lectures are a bore. He will help you if you have a question but his answers aren't always straight forward.

Lectures are boring, but easy to follow. He's a really nice person and will help you if you ask. Quizzes are straight forward, no surprises. Does practice problems in class that are like what you see on the quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I went to the first week of zoom lectures him, and bailed on learning the material from him after that. His lectures were extremely difficult to follow and his notes were hard to read. I ended up teaching myself the material using youtube videos instead.

He teaches this class like its the most boring thing hes ever taught. Its very hard to stay engaged and he rarely ever checks zoom chat. He constantly goes back on his sentences and it makes it hard to follow what hes saying and he goes on very long tangents trying to answer questions and still doesnt answer them.
The lecture is one big discussion, and no material is taught. Test material is not discussed in class, and I was teaching myself. 90+ page readings for homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: None of the terms for test 1 were defined in class. Doesn't take feedback well and rather blames the learning style rather than teaching style. Insane for a 1000 level class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
For a Film Music class, he really doesn't focus very much on the intersection of film and music. He has a background in music which shows, but otherwise makes up for his lack of knowledge/teaching skill by assigning way too much reading.

If I wanted to learn about African poetry and drumming, I'd have taken a class in that. I wanted Film Music. :/
easy A take if u need elective, mostly entirely group projects but no test or quizzes

I really did not enjoy experience design with him. He based a lot of his class around his own work in music, which is not related to design. Overall the class was very disorganized and he was very unclear all around. Even when students voiced concerns to him directly. The class had to write to the department about our concerns as well
hands down best calc 3 teacher at neu, no glaze. very lenient with quizzes.

Lectures were great and fairly easy to follow, and the quizzes and final all had very fair questions and we were given great review materials. My only complaint was this professor would only give us a couple days notice for quizzes which made it hard to plan in advance.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof Topalov is the best teacher/prof I've had for any math course so far. Communicates ideas clearly, provides relevant examples, gives little HW. The final is worth 40%, make sure to study enough. I had a 99.5 in the course before the final (based on 5 quizzes and 3 HWs), but didn't study enough for the final, ended with a B+. Rarely posts notes.

Prof. Topalov is pretty chill as a professor, he is willing to help out anyone who shows up to office hours and generally cares about whether his students learn the material well. Exams and quizzes weren't terribly hard, just utilize the practice material on Canvas and get an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Topalov is a solid professor for 2321; he gives no homework, only optional practice problems, and only grades on 5 short quizzes and the final. His in-class lectures are very helpful for understanding the intuition needed for this course and is very easy to approach after lectures or in office hour and discuss any questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He did a very good job of explaining concepts in class, but he is definitely not tech savvy. There is no way to receive the information from lectures if you miss a class. He does not post notes anywhere, doesn't record classes, or even have a zoom option when you can't attend class. Missing a class makes it impossible to catch up.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Topalov is a great math teacher. He makes sure you understand the geometry behind the math so it becomes intuitive. If that's not for you, his tests are pretty much the review problems from the book/the ones he provides. If you have to take math, I'd highly reccomend Topalov. Great class overall.

Dr. Topalov is THE BEST! His lectures are very clear and organized, and he always gives practice quizzes before each quiz that are very similar to the actual quiz (and same for the final). Quizzes are very short (usually 3 or 4 questions) and he gives you plenty of time to complete them. Do the optional homework problems and you should be fine.

He always started class on time and ended early, and handled remote learning very well. His lectures were easy to understand if you paid attention, and he was available for extra help and happy to stop his lecture to answer questions. Do the practice tests and it's an easy A.

Professor Topalov is a great teacher and really knows his stuff. He is very good at explaining and drawing a picture of what is going on for each formula we use. He gives 5 quizzes and they are all relatively easy and have no surprises on them.
He delivers the most detailed lectures, and explains the calculus concepts by drawing the diagrams and he explains thoroughly. He also provides regular office hours if you require any clarification.
Possibly one of the best math prof at NEU. He makes sure that everyone understands the material and is very helpful.

One of the best Calc 2 teachers out there. Explains the material well and is always willing to explain a difficult topic a second time. Gives practice quizzes that are identical to the actual quizzes, same with the final.

One of the easiest professor's I've had in a long time. He made Calc 3 a breeze. Although I may have failed any other professor's Calc 3 final and don't remember a single thing only a few months out, this class was an easy A.

Best calc professor I've ever had.  Very helpful and clear when teaching, he can really spell everything out to you so understand exactly what is going on.  5 quizzes and most hw is optional.  Pretty easy grader as well.

While his lectures can sometimes be a bit dry, he is one of the most talented teachers I've ever had.  He spells everything out extremely clearly for his students, and if you show up to the class and do at least a good portion of the optional homework you should get an A.  The class got a bit boring at times, but you learn the material thoroughly.

excellent teacher, highly recommended but he is veryyy slow after the first few weeks. if math, calc in particular, comes easily to you, you will most likely be bored. go to class tho b/c examples are almost identical to those on quizzes. couple of graded homework assignments, 6/7 quizzes (practice quizzes given prior), and dept final.

The Man.  Possibly one of the best if not the best math professor at NEU.  Go to class, do the optional homework and you are garunteed an A.  He is clear, and very intelligent. If I could take differtials with him I would in a heartbeat.

My favorite math professor I've ever had and I'm a math major. Explains in a way where you absolutely understand what is going on and will give further clarification if needed. Also, the best at drawing in 3-D which was really helpful for calc 3. I would take every remaining math course with him if I could!

Attendance not mandatory but definitely helps. His voice can put you to sleep sometimes so try stopping by DD or Starbucks before class =) . His notes are among the neatest I've ever seen. Collects hw a couple times throughout the semester, most are optional though. Practice quizzes posted on blackboard, and quizzes are fair.

absolutely the best professor ive had! he really explains well, great notes, great clarity. he makes sure that everyone understands the material and is very helpful at office hours. he is very fair, and quizzes are very similar to practice quizzes.  he doesnt try to trick you on quizzes or tests and asks very straight forward questions. A+!!

He is an amazing professor. all he cares about is to have learn from this class. his notes are very helpful for the quizzes. he walks around in the class to make sure that everyone is on track . . if there is someone having difficulties he will find him there anytime to help.
pretty good teacher, he teaches the subject well but i know that if i hadnt already been taught some of the concepts he taught that i would've had a hard time.
He is an excellent professor. Not very enthusiastic, but he explains everything really well and helps with any questions.  He makes a hard class very easy, try to get him if possible.!!

Amazing math teacher. I had him for Diff Eq. He made what is supposedly a very difficult class extremely easy. Very good at giving clear directions, will explain things again if you don't understand. The quizzes are never unfairly tricky or surprising. A great guy, I definitely recommend trying to get him if you can.

Absolutely the best calc teacher I have ever had. Attend his office hours and go over practice quizzes and you'll have no problem. His accent can be difficult for some, so sit in front and take good notes. Topalov is very good at simplifying vector calc, and the class is much easier than calc 2. An easy A if you put in the effort.

He knows the subject! He writes very clear on the blackboard and will to go over the stuff as many times as you need if you didn't get it. Explains everything very clear.Gives sample quizes several days before each quiz(they look very similar). Great at office hours. The only minus for someone could be his accent. Despite that he is great.Recommend

Somewhat difficult to understand over his accent.  Examples in class are very helpful and clear.  Gives study sheets for exams that are almost identical to exams.  Extremely helpful if you go see him during his office hours and will go over and do any problem you want clearly.
Difficult to understand at first, but he is very clear and detailed in his calculations.  Shows you exactly what to do.
No Comments
This class is the only reason I understood discrete and everything was explained really well. She is super sweet and cares for all her students that show up.
She is super helpful and caring and makes sure we understand each topic. I enjoyed showing up every time, and left knowing a lot more than when I came in. Definitely recommend!!

My favorite recitation was the one for Discrete Structures taught by Professor Ghita. She has the power to make concepts digestible for someone learning them for the first time. Additionally, she always took the time to clarify even as after the section ended. Finally, she gives you just enough to let still reach that "aha" moment. Thank you Ghita!

so nice and so ineffective. there were many typos on the shared materials (notes shared, etc.) throughout her teaching, with not enough support to resolve them, making understanding concepts frustrating. explanations were ambiguous and missed the mark. poor teaching quality. highly suggest avoiding if you can. some things just don't change.

Had her for recitation for Discrete Strucutres (CS1800). She was amazing! Not many people went to the recitations, so you are able to have very small group discussions with her and get personalized help. She knew the content very well and did extra practice problems to make sure that we understood the material.

Said that "for computer science" natural numbers start at 0 in the lecture, did an induction example with said assumption, took points off in homework because "natural numbers start at 1". A must take if you enjoy casual gaslighting :)

A terrific professor. Dr. Amor was always ready to answer questions and she explains concepts super well. I got an A in discrete because of her. She provided so much help and kindness. My advise is to show up to recitation - no one does, so she'll spend as much time as you want on your questions. I cannot stress enough how nice and helpful she is.
Gives good recitation lectures. Seems to care if students understand content or not
For CS 1802 (CS 1800's lab), pretty good, although sometimes the explanations may be a little difficult to follow, if you just ask for clarification, she will happily oblige. Very caring and nice.
I didn't like the attitude or teaching style very much

She is nice but she got mad at me once during office hours because I didnt understand a question, like???? Oh and be prepared to take a very important exam the Monday after thanksgiving break very early in the morning. Your end of semester results will be delayed as well.

Just watch the lectures and you can pretty much figure it out. Go to TA office hours/clinic hours!!!!! They will help you with all the assignments they literally were the reason I did so well! Prof is super sweet and caring!

Extremely hard, weekly homework that takes hours to complete, final exams are so important. Very useful information but avoid if not necessary. Prof was available and helpful. Hardest and one of the most demanding classes I've ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The online lectures are done by a variety of teachers and are comprehensive. Professor does a good job of explaining in class. TAs are very accessible during "clinic hours" to help with homework. They are the reason I did so well. The homework and quizzes can be challenging without help. Tests are a big percentage, but they are the same as the HW

She is a very easy professor to get an A grade under. It does make it unfair for students who are willing to work for the course since she frequently gives extensions, allows late submissions and entertains any sort of regrading if you just ask for it. Take it to get points, do not try to work hard for the course since you'll just be disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Everything you do is online. Attendance is 4%, but CS, in general, is a very intense and time-demanding class. Long assignments every week + quizzes. The grades add up fast. Don't risk your grade unless you're a CS major. Take an online class somewhere else if you want to learn the skills (especially for co-op) but this class is not worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class is based on watching tutorials online before class, and then practicing the material during class. There are a lot of homework, which are a pain, however it is the only way you are going to learn the material. The things you learn are also extremely applicable to what you may be doing at coop.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Easily the worst class I've taken in my 3 semesters at NEU. TA is AWFUL. If you sit in the front, maybe you can understand a bit more. But regardless of who I've talked to, they struggle. Unless this class is absolutely required for your major, take it with another prof. She doesn't know much of the material, she needs to look stuff up in office hr

The man is just so old, absolutely impossible to learn anything from this class. He just mumbles information from his terribly written slides for an hour and a half while not a single person in the classroom is paying attention. If you value your sanity, take econometrics with another professor.

He's super nice chill, especially for Applied Econ, which is supposed to be an intensive writing requirement. Zooms every lecture, only grades are problem sets & final project that he assigned around Halloween. He isn't as tech savvy but that was fine. Make an effort to get to know him & for him to get to know you he really enjoys it!!

Guys he's chill. His grading is amazing. He's not a tough grader. If you want a chill class this is it. Idk what the other reviews are talking abt. I recommend him. Attendance isn't mandatory. The final is a project. Your gonna be graded on the final project and problems sets.

Mr. Tardiff is a nice guy but not suited for teaching. Struggles with technology. Teaching style is boring and bland. He just reads off of prewritten slides and I have learned nothing from this class. Assignments are extremely easy, no quizzes, no tests, and the final is just a project that he assigns basically a month before its due.

Professor Tardiff is a little too old to be teaching and this "introductory" statistics class is impossible to understand if you haven't taken AP Stats. Problem Sets are incredibly confusing and half of the class time is spent correcting something he wrongly put onto them. I would highly recommend taking this class with anyone else if possible.

Great Teacher, really helps you understand material. I honestly don't think I got a great grade on the final but i feel like if I had actually listened to is advice about studying I would have done way better. Really genuine guy and he teaches the material very effectively in class.Attendance is mandatory and tests are most of the grade.

The good ratings are tripping tbh the work he makes you do in class is all Excel but the tests are on paper. He does not give any guidelines to study for any tests, nor sufficient resources to prepare. The problems he makes you do in class are completely different from the tests. He is not understanding of personal/ extraneous circumstances either.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Funny, caring, and entertaining short guy who sounds like Gru. Grade comprised of 2 midterms and a final, 2 case studies with randomly assigned partners, 2 excel analytics assignments, and chapter homeworks, both on McGraw (infinite check my works). Coursework is easy as long as you pay attention in class and do the exercises/understand the hws.
DO NOT TAKE HIM

Makes it easy to do well as long as you put in the work. Does an excellent job explaining everything and reinforces it with solid practice. Clearly cares about his students and wants to help them do well. Definitely take his class if you get the chance!

Udi is an excellent teacher. If you attend the lectures and follow the in-class exercises, I promise you will get an A. Grade comprises 2 case studies (done in pairs, both selected at random), two midterms, homework, and a final. Midterms are easy as long as you do the exercises he provides. He's very passionate and caring and I highly recommend.

Udi is my favorite teacher. Has a good personality, teaches everything well, and teaches you excel. The tests are on paper, but if you pay attention to the Excel it easily translates over. Probably not only the best, but the easiest, and the most informative managerial teacher. He's engaged in research, available outside of class, no downside.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: If you want to become a managerial accountant, go for it, but it's really not worth. Mad excel work in class but nothing comes out of it since the tests are on paper. The exams are a lot more difficult than what we practice in class. Attendance is mandatory and this guy won't know your name the whole semester (uses name cards lmao). save yourself
terrible teacher. dont take him. this rate my prof is a catfish for you all. please please please do not take him. this is a trap.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Udi has been one of my favorite professors at Northeastern. He has been teaching Managerial Accounting for many years, and is very experienced in the field. This class is extremely fair, you get out what you put in.

Great lecturer with clear grading criteria, consisting of a couple of exams, homework, and group projects. Mandatory attendance but classes are pretty engaging. Teaches and requires use of Excel a couples times which helps develop skills. Definitely learned the managerial accounting content well.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Fantastic lecturer. Knows what he is talking about and gives great examples for students to understand. Grading is straightforward and exams are fair. He wants you to succeed and provides all the tools to do so. Just show up to class and listen.

Very happy with Prof Hoitash's teaching style. Graded mostly by exams (20% exam 1, 20% exam 2, 30% cumulative final) but simple to study for and if you attend class, you'll be well prepared. You'll also have 2 cases, 2 excels, and Connect HW. You don't have to read the book to do well - just show up, listen, and do the hw/class exercises.

THIS MAN IS AN AMAZING LECTURER. He uses excel to teach & teaches excel skills which are so valuable. He teaches lots of shortcuts in Excel which are helpful elsewhere. tests are mcq & mcq calculation problems. You need to study for the exams, but if you study, it is very doable! Overall - highly recommend! also super available to help!
By far my favorite professor. Such an amazing guy who genuinely cares about his students and makes sure everyone understands the topic at hand. Would highly recommend him to anyone
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Hoitash made accounting, normally a challenging course for me, very easy to understand. He also integrated excel into the course to help us with our future jobs. Highly recommend.

Professor Hoitash is great to learn from. I had him for an accounting class, which is a subject I'm not very comfortable in, and he made the material easy and understandable. The class is a bit textbook heavy, but his lectures are great for expanding on the textbook, and on all written assignments he grades them quickly and gives good feedback.

Great at structuring the class. Always begins with a summary of the last class and ends with a summary of the current one. Requires participation, but is incredibly fair and cares about his students. Solid teacher.

The Professor is AWESOME!! The class itself is difficult but the professor makes it so much better. He will help you with whatever concerns you  have. Just study the practice problems from the textbook and you can ace his tests. TAKE HIS CLASS!! seriously.   He gives out CHOCOLATES at the end of the semester too ;)
Easy yet helpful homework, interesting case studies, and keeps his lectures clear and entertaining. Hands down one of the best professors I've ever had.
One of the best professors at Northeastern Business School.

Makes the class, homework and case studies very easy and straightforward, however, his exams are the worst.  He jacks up the degree of difficulty and continually fails to create a test that can be completed within the time frame of class.  The final exam was 16 pages long, no one in the class finished...
Great professor! Genuinely cares about his students and makes the class funny! Highly recommended
Really good Professor. Take him!

One of the best professors Ive had at NU. He uses alot of powerpoints and worksheets in class. He makes the material very clear by summarizing the material before and after class. He gets the class to participate during class by asking alot of questions. Test might be alittle tricky but if you go to class it shouldnt be a problem.

Udi is the CUTEST WEEEDLE man I have ever been educated by.  Sweet, helpful, and full of love. All about summarizing...he puts everything into clear perspective after each class.  Lots of slideshows but good times always.  Genuinely sweet man.

loves him! In his class I feel like I am back in the highschool class! He was very friendly and make class very fun. It is not just attending, and he giving u participation something like that. he wants u to participate and smile! loved it ! final exam was very hard though almost everyone was stuck in the chairs right before the exam time ends!

He helped students a lot. and very kind person but his test is somewhat different from what we learn in the class. Triky questions always pop up during the test and always got wrong of it. He teaches very easy and easy to follow but that was problem too! It was too easy to solve problem in class but test included some tricky question u never seen

Good Professor! I was afraid of the class but he makes the material easy and is great at explaining concepts. He is very vague about the material in the tests and that makes it difficult but if you understand and study hard you will be ok... He is willing to help, has patience and knows the material. I would recommend him!

Professor Hoitash tries to help his students to understand.He is available just about all the time. His tests however seem to be tricky. His tests consist of intuitive problem solving, but his teaching is that of simple formula puggling.I would not reccamend this professor unless you enjoy studying extensivly and being tricked on the tests.

Great professor.  Learned alot.  Reinforces what was learned in class with in class exercises which really helps.  Going to class helps you to do well and I rarely used the book.  Slides were enough to do well.
Out of all my professors, he is my favorite. He's funny and makes class enjoyable for everyone. Even though he's a little disorganized sometimes, he cares about his students and helps us do our best.

He is my favorite professor. His teaching style is very clear and you can tell he cares about his students. Teaches in an engaging way and takes his duties very seriously. He really does make teaching fun and funny, and very accessible. DEFINELTY would take his class again ;)

Dr. Uhalt is a very kind, approachable professor who is always open to discussing concepts. That being said he quizzes on the lecture material before lecture, there are typos in the assigned textbook, he taught us the wrong statistics software, he doubled the length of the final, and worst of all, he did not show up to lecture prepared.

He was often confusing and difficult to follow and difficult to get in touch with. The grading was very unpredictable, especially for exams, as rubrics were never provided. He also expected us to have taught ourselves statistics sufficiently to take a quiz (which is worth a 1/4 of grade) every single day when he was regularly unprepared for class.

I absolutely loved Dr. Uhalt, he is very approachable and the lectures are funny and engaging. The tests are fairly easy as there is a practice exam before it that is almost the exact same as the exam he just changes the data sets. The tests are also themed, our final this semester was Spaceballs themed :)

Uhalt is a pretty funny guy and this course was not bad at all. Tests and daily quizzes were all online which made it easy to do well. Every once in a while it can get boring, but I can't complain since reading the textbook wasn't even necessary. Definitely a fun course most of the time!
He is the best ever. SO funny and friendly, our whole class adored him. He truly wants everyone to succeed. I took stats with him, I hope I can take him again.
So passionate about helping students and super funny. Genuinely just wants you to learn and succeed.
Course on advanced algorithms.

Homework is difficult, but in my opinion if you actually try to learn the material and do the homework without just looking up the answers the exams will be a breeze. I struggled on the homeworks but did very well on the exams while most of the class did the opposite. A bit of a boring lecturer but a very friendly guy. Both my midterms got curved.

The homework is pretty hard, but doable with classmates. 
He is a pretty bad lecturer and not very approachable. Would avoid if possible.

Positives: you can get an A
Negative: energy vampire prof
His homework is challenging and not doable. Tough exam and worst of all, not helpful in lecturing.
Lectures are somehow confusing and the professor is not very helpful.
Not responsive for students' questions. Have trouble understanding the lecture.

His lecture is clear and organized. His homework is challenging but doable, start looking at them early. He "lost some friend" in the previous semester because of tough exams, so the exam is easy in my semester. Easy to earn an A since 30% of the class receives A.
My favorite class ever. Interesting problems in class and homework. Homework challenging but you learn a lot. Exams are in line with class and homework.
I loved Dr. Untersee! He truly is a super kind and sincere man, and cares about his students a lot, I see no evil in his soul.

He makes it very clear at the start of the semester that because he knows this class is a Senior Capstone course, he is not trying to prevent anyone from graduating. In fact his course makes it extremely easy to get another A on your transcript and raise your GPA

Most of the work is done in the end of the class.You do not receieve feedback with sufficient time to complete next required task. In fact, when the semester ended, I received 2 grades. He understands and will grant extension if necessary. He is a fair grader
Tough grader. Classes were useless.

This class was the most useless class and a waste of money. He gives you little to no feed back and waits till the last minute to give you feed back and expects a revised version of your draft the next day. This guy is a total tool and had no right teaching college students, he should be working with children in pre-school.
Capstone, he grades hard in the beginning but just go up to him in class and he will help you out

I think the Biology department got this guy to single handedly revive the capstone class to something useful.  You spend the entire semester writing a grant proposal, deadlines can be a bit too fast at times, but other than that it was actually remotely fun.

Very good class, really easy.  Seriously, for two classes we learned how to write sentences for our draft.  Take work to do because it is sometimes really boring.  If you are confused Untersee is really helpful and provides good rubrics for how you are going to be graded.  Take this class with him!

Professor, Untersee Is one of the most helpful professors I have ever had. He makes capstone class very efficient, and interesting. He always provides guidelines for every assignment and goes out of his way to help you out. I really recommend him! You might think Ohhhh nooo capstone...... But with a professor like Untersee you will love capstone!!!

Course is very poorly organized. Professor is very slow at responding to student queries sometimes he just leaves them unanswered. Course grades are released very slow. Overall just a really terrible course to take.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great prof, pretty knowledgable
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: I learned a lot. You must work for it (attend the office hours and the class sessions).

Class 30 mins a week.
Canvas Module is all research papers
Learn everything yourself, worst experience ever.

Professor's lectures aren't bad, but the overall course organization is atrocious. Assignments are seemingly random, quizzes often do not relate to class material, and less than half of the submissions were graded by the end of the semester. It wasn't even clear when/how to submit our final project! In the end you're much better off with Andrew Ng.
Boring lectures that feel like a waste of time when you can study for the tests straight from the textbook. She has weird subjective grading on papers, but she curves the exams well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Participation is so important in the class, yet it's somewhat hard to find opportunities to do so. This is because she asks a lot of questions about life experiences that most kids just don't have. She is very positive and the subject matter is interesting. Group projects and debates are a big part of the class - make sure you take time with those.

Weird Manerisms. Participation in class is a HUGE part of your grade but she only called on international students. Group projects and debates are easy. You genuinely do not need to attend lectures todo great on tests but its mandatory so mainly a waste of time. Lectures do not cover any content on the tests but she provides good study guides.

She doesn't know how to organize the canvas nor give clear instructions about assignments. Despite non of her lectures are related to the test, tests should be an easy B+ or above as long as you read the chapters that's in the exam. She grades presentations and debates really leniently. If you don't want to learn but get a good grade than take her.

Her lectures are completely irrelevant to then tests, yet she takes attendance? She also grades HEAVY on participation, only calls on the same ppl, and gives no options to participate. If you read the chapters, you will do fine on the tests and they are all multiple choice. This isn't the hardest class ever but the prof is really unreasonable.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: 2nd review here. Just need to emphasize how much I recommend not taking this class with this professor. The class is boring, the exams are ridiculously hard. I got 3+ tutors for the first test and still failed as the test has nothing to do with the study guide. Market entry report it long and tedious and a complete waste of time. Do not recommend.

Absolutely the worst professor I have had so far, her laugh is annoying, she talks in circles, she can never fully answers any question. Make sure to read the textbook and start preparing for your market entry report. If your looking for a class that will put you to sleep but also stress you out I totally recommend this one.
I'm taking her class right now and its the worst class I've ever taken. She doesn't stop talking and her laugh is so annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This class is an absolute snooze fest, multiple kids sleeping every single class. Dont recommend taking unless you like sitting through 1 hour+ lectures while she talks the entire time about nothing on the tests. Nothing to be learned in class. Would recommend skipping every class and only reading the textbook as that is the only thing on tests.

She's very generous grading-wise, and got curved a lot for 3 exams. Although your participation matters, she is really lenient on it, thought it was gonna bring my overall grade down but actually the opposite.

Dry lectures and a lot of it isn't useful information. The exams are so hard to do well on and the group projects depend on how good your group is. She doesn't understand specific student circumstances and always assumes you're going to cheat on all the assignments. I highly recommend taking a different professor because she also teaches horribly.

Participation grade tanks grade if your not 3-5 most talkative kids in the class. Most useless information when. in class I question why I'm paying $80,000 to be there. It is such a waste of time, if a university director sat in the class they would make it so that it isn't a mandatory class anymore. Canvas page is unbelievably unorganized

She's sweet, caring, and grades very very leniently!! Getting a B/A is easy in this class as long as you read the textbook which is like the Holy Grail. But, her assignment instructions are super unclear and there are also soooo many group projects which make up a big % of the grade. You want a good group b/c you're stuck with them all semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Her lectures have nothing to do with exams, laughs at herself. Extremely knowledgable when it comes to international affairs and will prove you wrong. Definitely do the readings, they are reflected on the exams.

She just doesn't stop talking. Her lectures make no sense she rambles about random things all class. She made participation 20% of the grade but gives no opportunities to participate. Attendance is mandatory but a waste of time because she only tests based on the text book and does not add anything from her lectures to the tests.
really fun

awful, she gave me an under-average participation score even though I come and talk in every class and said I was using the laptop which it wasn't allowed to? she talked fake information and I really don't think she respects mine and all other countries' cultures. just receive the updated and newest information instead of using some video from 2000

prof un is very knowledgeable about international relations and keeps up with current events. however, she is very culturally insensitive and shares her opinions about other cultures with no regard for students in the class. 
her lectures are summaries of the textbook, and the group projects drove us insane. she does grade fairly, which is a plus.
worst professor ever. I don't mind she's a ppt reader for teaching us nothing in class. Just so unfair for her crazy workload while also as a TOUGH grader compared to other sessions' professors.
Every thing she said turned out to be right.
Knows very little about other countries' policies and cultures but likes to talk about it a lot on class. Truly feel disrespected.

She is definitely pretty generous grading-wise. As long as you put the effort in, you will probably get a high B or low A. I didn't like how, oftentimes, she shared her opinion on controversial topics. It's almost like she says her opinion and that is right and no other is right.

Professor Un is a very nice professor and she definitely knows her material. Make sure you read the book. The tests are purely based on book material. If you read, you will do well on the tests. The debate and presentations are graded pretty easily, but make sure you have good material. This will make the final project less stressful. Start Early!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Un is one of the best teachers Ive had at NEU. If you do the readings and keep yourself up to date with current world events this class will be a breeze. Her instructions are clear and shes very understanding when grading papers or group projects. I always recommend her to those who want to take this course!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: She hardly ever had instructions for the assignments in canvas, so if you weren't in the class when she randomly explained it (with very little detail, and not recorded) you didn't know what the assignment was. Poor understanding and use of canvas. None of her lectures were more than a retelling of the textbook or a video she just showed.
A fair grader.

Her lectures were boring, and the tests were heavily textbook based. She does curve exams which is a plus. However, she is quite rude to students and insensitive. I was excited for this class as well due to her high ratings.

Maybe she is a different type of professor in person. But online, her lectures were boring. She forces our cameras to be on and mentions she will screenshot to check back later. She is quite rude if you answer wrong or have differing opinions than hers. She is also insensitive to different cultures considering she teaches an international class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: I hated this class. Boring, useless lectures. Textbook-heavy proctored tests. She's rude and dismissive, enjoys laughing at students when they answer something incorrectly. Video needs to be on for the entirety of class for participation, takes note if you turn it off. Likes randomly taking photos of the class to later check if you were focusing

Professor Un's lectures aren't super interesting but she does try to keep students engaged! She's a very nice professor who is very fun to talk to after class. Her exams are heavily textbook & case study based; she does give generous curves on her exams. She's also a lenient grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: extremely intelligent professor who puts a lot of effort into engaging students in class. She responds well to class participation as she enjoys discussion about the day's current events. Very caring and funny

She is very intelligent and responds very well to participation and questions. The class is one where you have constant work and have to be on top of your game, but Professor Un makes the work enjoyable and relevant. She begins each class with discussion about the news which was a good way to ease the class in. Great professor!

Annique's lectures are boring; her exam 2 and exam 3 are easy. Exam 1 is a bit tough but as long as you read the textbook, you will do well. She grades debate, participation, and group projects very easily. If you put work in, you will get at least an A-. She gives extra credit as well. Overall, this class is an easy A for me.

Professor Un is super nice and really cares about your students. She wants her students to do well in the class and is willing to meet with you outside of class for extra support. If you put in the work, you should be able to get at least an A- if not better. Multiple choice exams are pretty tough but they are curved.

Fine class, nothing crazy. There is almost no work throughout the semester and then quickly ramps up to almost unmanageable levels towards the end. Easy exams, but I still learned some international business. Definitely a good choice if you want a chill class with an easy A. Also, she constantly giggles at her own jokes which is pretty entertaining

Waste of my time. Made me dislike international business. Extremely boring lectures nearly every day. Exams are all multiple choice based on the textbook, often with repeats or multiple questions on the same topic in a row. & most of the time they will have nothing to do with whats talked about in class. Huge curves (16+ points).

Prof. Un is nice and the class is very average. She makes the material easy and is a lenient grader. She likes to check in with groups to see their progress on papers which was helpful to know what she wanted and had reviews before exams. Most of the reading is super boring and unnecessary she has you do a lot of case studies. Overall easy grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: A very nice kind professor who really cared about her students. Excellent teaching styles, using different approaches to ensure everyone understood the materials.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Amazing teacher who made hard topics easy to understand by using industry and international experiences to show how things really work in other countries.
Excellent prof who cares about everyone!

I agree with the previous posters about her having favorites. The class itself was not too difficult; it was heavy on reading and group projects but the exams were straightforward. Participation matters!

She means well, but this woman cannot teach for the life of her. She picks favorites (if you're not international, she is disinterested) and goes on the most wild tangents about her job and how much she travels. Class was very hard to sit through as there was always a very awkward tension.

Professor Un is nice and approachable. Your grade is based alot on the group projects that you do so If you get a bad group then you might have a tough time. However, the assignments and exams are not that hard and it isn't too hard to get an A on them.

This class was super easy. Tests aren't worth very much of the final grade and is mostly made up of a bunch of group work and presentations. I somehow ended up wth an A- even though i had gotten high A's in every single assignment and project.. weird... besides that this class is easy. Prof Un is super nice too.
Lectures are Long and Boring There are only 2 tests a semester You need to have a good and well prepared group to do well (Hint: make PowerPoints for every presentation, even if it is a debate)

Professor Un is extremely nice, helpful, and informative. Her extensive experience in international business helps us connect the material to the real world. The group projects are the worst. She assigns groups alphabetically, don't be surprised if you end up with a useless group. Tests are easy. Final paper is 10 pages MAX, she tells you this late
Cool professor! Debates in her class are awesome! Apply business concepts to real world cases, like why Walmart failed in Germany, South Korea? Really get you thinking.
She's really funny! Brings stuff from other countries to explain how companies do business in other countries!
Best prof ever! Her ARAMCO case was amazing. Changed my life. I don't think she ever taught MGSC2301. Is MGSC2301 a real class?
Worst teacher in the history of colleges.
Fantastic professor!
Trade within Africa was interesting!
loved this professor! Learned a lot!

Avoid her. Terrible teacher and ended up being a ridiculously hard and unfair grader. Goes on tangents about irrelevant topics. Chooses 2 or 3 favorites and makes it known she's disinterested in everyone else.

Nice lady but cannot teach effectively. Focuses far too much on Asian countries in attempting to tie in course content with reality. That being said, incorporate Asia into your papers or group project and you'll get an A. Didn't learn much form this course and that's a shame given International Business is a topic of growing importance for managers
I did too. Awesome class!
I did well in her class!
She is a nice person but seriously, one of the worst teacher I had.
She can bridge the cultural differences in Europe and Asia because of her experiences in many Asian and European countries. I agree with all the positive comments provided.

This is the only professor who would go out of her way to help students prepare for the exams by giving a practice exam before each exam. If this is not nice, I don't know what is. I did less well on EXAM 2, but it was a fair exam.
Professor Un has been posting all of our scores on BB and still constantly reminds us that her door is always open to talk about any thing we want. I visited her recently and found to be true.

I am enjoying professor Un's class immensely. She makes extra effort in helping international students adjust to the American business academic environment. The textbook we use has many Japanese examples. Since she has a lot of experiences in Japan, she elaborates on these examples using her own professional experiences there.

This is one of the worst teacher I have ever had. She interrupts the students all the time which is very frustrating, she has an obsession with Japan… Everything is related to Japan. This is the most boring class I took, and she did not make it fun to learn at all
This is the best class I ever had at NU. I never thought I could learn so much about the world in just a few months!
Lots of work. Had to read and summarize every thing!
I learned so much in this class about how to do business in other countries.
Awesome professor! extremely knowledge, easy to talk to, and always available to help!

She's nice and very knowledgeable, but the class is pretty silly overall and she doesn't explain things too well. However, she does give you power points for every chapter and gives you so many free points. She curved both exams by 10% (i.e. 82 -> 92) and gave us 10 points for doing the TRACE eval, so an easy A if you participate and do the work

Annique is very knowledgeable in her field and delivers material clearly.  Debates Class participation is key to earning an A in her class.  Debates were very common and actually helped in learning the material.. Overall I enjoyed this class
She is such a great professor. She makes sure everyone participate in the class. Interesting class not boring. Two debates and one project but a lot of summaries.

Great prof & great class. Grades are a big paper, a small paper, participation, and final exam. Lots of reading but you can skim. Really amazing guest speakers. Urman is SUPER smart, funny, and respectful. Def only take if you are actually interested in law.

Only take this class if you want to go into law. Around 50 pages of reading for each class, definitely a heavy load. You will learn a lot but its a very demanding class. No technology allowed, must write notes on paper. Fair but challenging assignments. He is a charismatic, intelligent guy. Often had guest speakers who were great!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Urman is so knowledgable about the Supreme Court, it's kinda scary. But if you don't have a similar passion for it, you probably shouldn't take this class. He tells you that on the first day and offers to transfer you to a class that counts for the same credit. I learned so much in this class -- would recommend if you like this topic.

Fantastic lecturer and super into his class but he's the hardest grader. He's also very accessible but his feedback isn't great and he's very vague then gives bad grades even after he knows you've tried really hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Urman is a condescending, arrogant lecturer. If you don't agree with his beliefs he certainly will make it known that he does not like you. It is difficult to take a class that is loaded with such slanted views. A professor should educate, not belittle. Don't take this course if you value your cognitive autonomy.

Hands down the best professor I've ever had. Professor Urman has forever changed the way I view law. If you're interested in law, you'll never feel like you are working in this class! Long lectures but amazing none the less. I'd recommend this class to anyone interested!

One of the best professors I've had at Northeastern so far. The reading load is heavy but lectures are interesting. He makes the material fun and has a lot of humor. As long as you go to class and do the readings you'll do fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Prof. Urman is one of the best professors I've had an NEU. Classes are long but never boring; they're super interesting and he is hilarious. Also, he is a very fair grader. As long as you show up and do the readings, you will do fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: If you are willing to do the work, you will love this class and will gain much from it. Professor Urman is a straight shooter, always willing to spend time with you and readily accessible. He has a way of connecting anything and everything to a current culture event, its brilliant. Great Professor.

Professor Urman is a life changer. You will work hard but will leave the semester with a tremendous amount gained. You not only learn about POLS but LIFE! He opened my eyes to so much that Ill take with my for years to come. Urman is brilliant and always willing to help and mentor. Oh and hes hysterical. Best professor at NU hands down.

Some people should not teach and this prof is a leading example. He is condescending and does use majority of the class time to boast about himself. As far as course material, I have learned more about the Supreme Court from documentaries.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: If you're down to watching Stephen Colbert every class while paying $5,000 for the entire semester this professor is the one for you. Incredibly disappointed in this professor, considering I at first had the utmost respect for him. Toots his own horn, the only interesting things you learn are from the visitors he has who are terrific.
Game-changer. Not only is he extremely knowledgable, he is one of the best teachers at Northeastern. Keeps lectures interactive. If you put in the effort, you stand to gain tremendously.

Dan Urman is by far the best professor I have had at Northeastern. Make sure that you are interested in the subject that he is teaching in his course because he expects a serious commitment. He is incredibly smart, clear, and interesting to listen to because he is young and relatable. Also his jokes are hilarious

Professor Urman is by far the best Professor I have ever had at Northeastern. Don't enroll if you're looking to coast. I've learned a tremendous amount-more than I expected from taking his classes and I am substantially more prepared for college, grad school, and life entirely. Students who dislike him typically dont do the work

Easily one of the best professors I've had. Took his Judicial Process course as well. Will stick to everything in his syllabus. Be willing to do the work, you'll be fine. A funny guy. I would not recommend this class to freshmen who just want to sleep. He will destroy you. Otherwise, amazing prof.
One of the worst professors I have ever had.  Unhelpful, arrogant, and condescending.  Should be fired from NEU.  Avoid classes with Prof. Urman!

This is an awesome class if you are willing to do the work- about an hour of reading the night before class and NYTimes every day.  He gives at least one pop quiz a week, so you have to make sure you do the work.  However, if you are willing to put in the effort, you will learn a lot and do well.
A tough but fair professor, and truly the funniest I've ever had. He covers the American government in an accessible, informative manner. Highly recommended.

The first day of class I was seriously put off by this professor. He came off as condescending and egotistical. A week later my opinion was entirely different... sure he demands a lot from his students, but if you pay attention you learn a lot. Lectures are always interesting (he's funny) and it became my favorite class.

He knows what he's talking about. Nice guy. Respects you if you respect him. Assigns a lot of articles, and you're expected to read the NY Times a couple of times a week. Video clips and the occasional pop quiz at the beginning of class so do not be late.

Straight shooter. If you get one his bad side, there is a reason for it. Brutally honest and very clear about what he expects from students in terms of behavior/participation/attendance. Any student that complains about getting on his "bad" side did not read the syllabus and should not be in college.

Very condescending to his students and treats an entry level 100 course as if it's a graduate class. Attending class is mandatory as he gives pop quizzes based on previous classes/the NY times. If you are late by less than even a minute you'll automatically be on his bad list t

It was an online class so he did not teach at all just read power points in 10 min. He is the worst prof ever. He treats students impolitely. He is obsessed with grammar and language mistakes he does not care about the content.Do not take his class if you are international because none of the international got better than B

Firstly, attend the class. Recently he has been giving out random pop quizes that make up a huge chunk of your participation grade. Also, READ or SKIM the articles he sends you, they are often on test and quizes. Go to HIM and the TA for midterm/paper help.
Great professor, very smart, knows a lot about what he's teaching.
Funny teacher. oListen to lectures, attend recitations and read textbook. Try to impress the TA, Dan is an extremely hard grader. He's a lawyer *D'UH
His lectures are extremely funny, and definitely one of my favorite classes! The textbook isn't really necessary, but as long as you take notes in class, you'll do well on the final!

Textbook not necessary, but you SHOULD read the articles he assigns. Midterm, final and paper, his tests are based on his lectures, not the book so attendance is a must and take good notes. He integrates modern day examples and about 2 or 3 youtube videos a class. Overall good guy, I recommend him despite what the other review says.

Professor Urman is lazy, had three useless T.A.'s who extremely difficult graders without explanation. Professor Urman would say one thing in class, while the TA's would grade entirely opposite. He ordered a custom $170 book that can not be resold anywhere! Tests were difficult, stated short answer,then graded as essay. Awful professor&TA's BEWARE

Everything people are saying about tests is true they were long ash and hard ash (nice) but Moneesh himself is a pretty good professor. He's good at explaining the material and is very responsive and helpful outside of class.

He gives excellent feedback and is willing to provide extra help. His helpful lecture slides are provided, but it is very necessary to attend lectures in order to understand all the necessary details. If you pay attention in class, though, exams and quizzes will be reasonable. He also provides study sessions prior to every midterm.
Avoid at all costs. Tests are insanely long and brutal! Made what should be a relatively easy class extremely difficult. Take this course with another professor if you can.
Tests are harder compared to the assignments and assignments are long

Professor is very knowledgeable on the subject and his slides are very organized for the class. He also cares about his students and will try to help you if you don't understand something. There is a textbook for this class, but I was able to learn the material from his slides and in-class explanations.

Thought I would hate the class, but Moneesh made materials science really interesting. His slides are scary but he explains things very well. Homework is very easy and exams are reasonable. He is a fair grader and cares about his students. TAs for the lab section were mean though. They take off 10+ points for the smallest things in write-ups.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Incredibly long midterms. Hard to finish them on time. Overall a tough grader
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Quizzes and homeworks are fine. Midterms are ridiculously long and not reflective of the practice midterms. Is ok at teaching the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof. Upmanyu is a passionate teacher who is great at teaching the assigned material as well as impressing why it is important. Lectures were informative and he made a point to answer any questions brought up. He is accessible out of class and was good at responding to emails. It is difficult to get an A (95), but he tries hard to help students.

I unfortunately have a low GPA& had to do this compulsory class. Contrary to what I expected, Prof Utku was amazing&replied very quickly to my emails with a lot of tips&patience. She uses contemporary real-life examples in her teaching-I got B+ instead of failing as I had worried! Thank you Prof Utku for being so supportive&helping me do well !

Absolutely amazing professor: interesting and informative lectures, class is clearly organised, easy homework. Finally a professor whose exams are ACTUALLY based on what she taught in class! Many struggle with the research proposals because they can be confusing, but I attended her office hours and she is very helpful and will walk you through it.

Comparative politics is definitely worth taking. Prof Utku is one of the nicest most caring professors I've had. She is very helpful, informative, encouraging loads of discussion. An eye-opener for me; I actually had to use my brain to think, rather than just memorise for exams. Learnt a lot, and enjoyed her use of current affairs (very topical) !

Prof Utku is super nice and knowledgeable in the area. Homework was straightforward and easy, no more than 20-30 mins a week; but research proposals that were worth 20% of the overall grade had unclear instructions and no feedback given to help improve your grades, pretty strict grader for the lack of instructions given, take-home midterm and final

Class was really chill and not very stressful. Ahsen is super nice and honestly really cool with dope style but her lectures are boring and some are not structured well at all. Readings are long but mostly unnecessary. Homework assignments were easy but the research proposals were never really explained. No essays just two take home tests.

Professor Utku is super easy-going; midterm and final were not difficult, and she grades leniently. The homework assignments take about 20 minutes per week. Her class was structured very linearly, which fit my learning style. I never did any of the readings because her lectures provided you with all the information you needed to know for the class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Vaulont is a very caring, passionate professor, but his rubric for his essays are very difficult. He gives you feedback, but it seems very open-ended on a lot of things, and it's very frustrating. The group activities are fun, the tests can be a bit hard, but if you study, you will do fine. Very strict attendance policy as well.

While I just started in Professor Vaulont's class, I can confidently say that he is an incredible professor. I believe he recently started at NU, so I thought I should leave one of his first reviews. He is incredibly passionate about the material, has an amazing energy, and I look forward to going to his class. For ORGB, I easily recommend him.

Manuel makes a boring class as fun as possible with in-class activities and a fun and easy group project. Papers are a bit difficult, and he grades them tough but gives good feedback so you can improve on the following ones. Exams are easy and a lot of elements go into your grade which is nice. Overall, very manageable to get an A, highly recommend

She's very kind and passionate about the subject. Class topics can often feel repetitive, and she does go off topic a lot in lectures. Lots of HWs every week, be ready to read a lot. For an intro econ class, it is intense. Also, TA is the worst I've ever experienced. He's extremely nitpicky in grading, often not helpful, and rude in emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I am graduating this semester and looking back Economics of Sustainability was not only one of my favorite courses but one that challenged me to be what I wanted the world to be and to understand the significance of economic assumptions of human behavior as incentives…Dr. Venkatesan provides the connection to dots crafted in my other courses.
LOVED HER, YOU WONT REGRET TAKING HER.

Professor Venkatesan was one of the best professors I've ever had! Her class made me question everything and actually think—not just memorize. No electronics, but trust me, the convos (weed, prostitution, homelessness…) were the most engaging I've ever had. Just a tip: she can be picky if she's not a fan, but if she likes you, you will get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great Professor! Adds so much to the understanding of the relevance of history and culture in the framing of present day society and economic perspectives. The class transformed how I view economics.

One of the best professors I had at Northeastern! She is very passionate about what she does and very smart. She is very kind, cares a lot about students, and tries to do her best so everyone succeeds. There are readings and hw every week and 3 non-cumulative exams. If you pay attention in class, all quizzes are easy. The TA is annoying, though.

This online course (Ecological Economics) was engaging; informative; clear in explaining new concepts; and the professor was available to speak and explain. Overall a great experience that highlights that economic literacy is a needed foundation to promoting sustainability. As the professor notes: change the goal, change the outcome!

I had no background in economics but the online materials and professor feedback made the material accessible and applicable to my public policy focus. I learned a solid foundation and hope to take an in-person class with the professor to build on! Highly recommend the professor and course for those interested in operationalizing sustainability!

Economics of Sustainability was my favorite class at Northeastern University. From day one, it challenged my worldview about the role of economics and climate change, and the lessons continue to influence my life choices. Prof. Venkatesan is dedicated to her student's intellectual development and learning outcomes. I highly recommend the class.
Third class with her and would recommend if you are interested in the application economics to social situations. Homework, lectures and a lot of reading but overall good course.
Going to class was like listening to a Ted talk. The professor is passionate, and gets you to think about how economics has become our culture. Recommend if you want to exercise your thinking.

I did not know what to expect, most discussions on race deal with statistical differences. This class focused on the economic context of what creates statistical differences and highlight the relationship between beliefs and societal outcomes and how culture normalizes and legitimizes based on dominant beliefs. Good course.
really nice and easy course to take with her-- she does a lot of preaching though which are true but not always engaging

Dr. Venkatesan is very passionate about microeconomics and her lectures are usually her going on tangents. She really only cares about making sure you know the material. She said to buy the textbook but I never purchased it and we didn't use it once. There are three exams and there's an in-class and online portion. Its a lot of writing but easy.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is a very passionate and invested professor in class and brings a lot to lectures. However, her lectures are extremely loosely organized, there are no slides and hardly any notes. She seems to just go on tangents. Almost a full month of the course was either not in class or over zoom.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Respected

reviews.comment: Madhavi is extremely passionate about the subject and has a broad knowledge. Although difficult and time-consuming, the homework and assigned readings push you and make you understand the subject well. In the end, it is well worth it. She hosts talks for extra credit that hold tons of valuable information and good discussion.

Super easy grader, tests are very doable (also she only gives tests in ECON1116!) and doesn't give a formal final. Lectures often go off on tangents about things she's passionate about, but we manage to learn material and she makes it easy to understand. She gives a lot of grace and is willing to hear students out. Easy A with actual learning too.
The class was awesome. Would definitely recommend in my opinion one of the easiest sections of principles of Microeconomics you can get.
She is an extremely sweet professor and goes over the context in depth. You are basically guaranteed an A as long as you complete all the homework and self-study the material.

Prof Venkatesan is my favorite professor EVER. Reading-intensive course, required to write a lot of papers but she did not deduct a point from all the assignments as long as you have a proper citation format. She loves to offer opportunities for students. Greatest professor ever.

Sustainability Econ challenged my notions of the entire field. Prof V is an excellent individual and professor. Some days, upwards of 60 pgs reading nightly, but most students will earn an A in her courses. Lots of writing. Short essays due every day during summer and 2-3 large papers a course. Easy grader, but lots of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Discussion-based class with lots of readings and an assignment every day. I didn't really like the way she ran the class with somewhat random lectures, but overall if you do the work it will be an A. She is generally nice and is willing to help if you need it. I recommend if you are interested in sustainability/the environment.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Venkatesan is my favorite professor. She does a good job at explaining her concepts clearly. The class made me critically rethink everything I knew about Economics. She is so inspirational and everyone here that says the class was boring are total losers. Dont take the class just for the easy grade, take it if ur genuinely passionate.

Her lectures are long and she doesn't give a break but you can take a break during her lecture as you please. I honestly didn't find the lecture super helpful to the actual assignments but sometimes it was very interesting to participate in them. She's a very nice grader and will ask you to re-write a paper if she can't at least give you a B!
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class required a lot of work and writing. However, I didn't lose a point the whole semester, so it's more about just doing everything assigned.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: amazing prof!

These are facts, not opinions- 1. An assignment is due before almost every class 2. No exams 3. Philosophical talks in the middle of lectures is common 4. Rarely gives breaks in 100-minute lectures 5. Full points for almost all assignments (grades are uploaded weeks after submission) Conclusion- The class is easy but gets irritating quite soon.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The professor will try to give you as many CV opportunities as she can throughout the semester. she is very very understanding, and very passionate about the subject that she teaches. She is a popular economist, one of the very nice teachers at northeastern. Something due every class, its a small writeup, no exams, only assignments. Best for 1000 l

Vaid is THE goat. You don't rly do math in this class, you learn how to use excel (which is much more useful imo). He's barely accessible by email and will do schizophrenic bits during class instead of teach sometimes but I'm telling you, if you can take Vaid, do it. Everyone I know who has taken a class with him has loved it.
