This class is completely taught on Excel. You get to learn about alot of functions on Excel, but nothing about definitions or math compared to a regular statistics course.  He can be bipolar at times, but is a pretty kind and funny person. Everything is take-home/open-book, but you can miss some things if you don't go to class.

Prof. Vaid is a great professor who will teach you the materials from the textbook. He is serious while teaching but also has a comical side which makes for an enjoying experience. Exams and Quizzes were online open book.

Wouldn't recommend. Not accessible outside of class, no office hrs + never answers any emails. Not passionate about statistics. Not a difficult class, but pls take someone else. Haven't learned a single thing in that class. He is a nice guy when he's in a good mood and will goof off from time to time.

Not a terrible teacher, just not passionate about Statistics at all. The class was pretty boring and there wasn't an opportunity to learn. Some assignments are weighted far too heavily and one mistake can drop your entire grade by a letter.

This guy does not want to be teaching. But, he's funny and weird which is always entertaining. 
If you actually want to learn stats, don't take him because he never really explains any concepts. 
If you want to learn excel, do take him, as the entire class is done through it. 
Quizzes and homework are easy. Definitely easy A but didn't learn much

He's chill. Really chill. He knows excel, so I learned a lot in the start at least. The tests are open book - i found them very easy. No attendance required. The class is an easy A, not very intresting. You can get by with the bare minimum. The prof is nice, not very serious, kinda funny.

Very kind and caring professor. Three total exams worth 15% each (non-cumulative). Exams were open note and very manageable. All exam questions are related from the Cengage textbook. Everything is taught via excel. Don't worry if you are new to using it. Professor Vaid is willing to go out of his way to help students navigate excel at any time.

Prof. Vaid is alright. He does the bare minimum regarding teaching, he does not want to be there. However, all tests and quizzes are open-book/internet. Ensure you have Excel downloaded and know the bare minimum skills like making formulas, etc. He is understanding and will drop quiz grades at the end if you just ask nicely!

Class is taught through excel practice. Quizzes are on Cengage, but you will need to pay $150 for the online hw and quizzes, but we never use textbook. Quizzes are easy, if you get the content. He teaches for you to understand but it's not engaging and most in the class were confused. I wish he made the effort to learn the names of his 20 students

Professor Vaid was very fair and chill. He taught statistics using Excel and allowed notes to be accessed during tests. He did not want you to pointlessly memorize things but actually know how to do the problems. We used Cengage for homework, quizzes, and tests. The final was optional which was a plus.

Professor Vaid did not teach me anything about statistics. He taught me how to efficiently use my notes on his open note tests, but I did not actually gain any knowledge. All of the classes are lectures but he does not explain anything. He never asked us our names and was not accessible outside of class; he never mentioned his office hours.

Everything is very straightforward. This is a class that can be done alone. Quizzes and tests literally mimick the practice questions on the textbook. 3 Exams, and if you take his final, he drops the lowest one out of 4. A quiz every week, but very doable. Average time spent doing hw per week: 3hrs
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever had. Uses cengage and a textbook. The prof "reviews" topics by choosing one example problem, waiting for one person to respond with the answer, then moving on without reviewing/providing any explanations. Classes are pointless, he's not available outside of class, no TAs or office hours, doesn't know how to teach. goodluck

Professor Vaid is super funny and a very approachable person. He's very laid back and this translates to the way he runs his class. 4 Exams including the final are assigned but the lowest is dropped at the end of the course. Quiz every week which is light. Everything is online open notes open book and uses excel. Easy class but still learned tons.

I took Business Statistics Honors last year with Professor Mustafa Vaid; he is funny and caring. Homework was on Cengage and the exams were open notes. He sometimes would skip slides when teaching and said he would go back to those later and then he wouldn't. However, overall, I think this class is pretty chill and easy A if you do all the work.

Professor Wells makes his class very interesting and fun. We are assigned a reading after every class, then next class we have class discussion which he encourages the students to participate in. There are 4 major assignments that you need to complete by the end of the semester, most are pretty easy. Definitely recommend his class!

Professor Wells is an amazing professor and sparked an interest in Philosophy for me. Every class has a 15-20 page reading. As you annotated it, you don't have to participate in class. There are few assignments, and grading is easy. He gives so many chances for good grades, as long as you show up and try at least a little you will do well.

Mark's lectures are engaging and easy to follow along with. The rubric for the class is clear, and you get unlimited attempts on all four major assignments. He's very down-to-earth and connects well with students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Participation does matter for grade but not much. He is nice and allows for lots of second chances!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Prof.Wells is one of my favorite professors at Northeastern. He made me enjoy philosophy so much and every reading that was assigned was very engaging. He is super enthusastic and knows what he is teaching. I found his feedback helpful on assignments, If he is teaching please take the class he is the best I promise it will make you a better student
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Inspirational--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: As Tyra Banks once said, "It is so bad, I want to give you a 0. But that is not possible. So, I give you...a 1." This is the worst class I have ever taken, it made me absolutely despise philosophy. Homework is long, repetitive, and useless, professor is extremely unhelpful, and the directions for assignments are unclear but he won't explain them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I ended up transferring from Northeastern, but PHIL2302 remains one of my favorite classes I've ever taken. I found it challenging but reasonable, and I think he teaches in a very unique and engaging way that I continue to seek in other classes. As others have said, he genuinely cares about students and is accessible outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great Professor, you're graded on if you complete 4 assignments. There are of course rubrics for each of those assignments that you have to follow in order to get the complete but it is definitely doable. He is very helpful during office hours so if you're having difficulties make sure to go to his office hours for help. Participation also matters!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Wells is a very nice guy and his class is feasible. There is a lot of reading and writing involved with this class, which did make it difficult. I wish his syllabus and course was more clear. If you stay on top of your work and SHOW UP, you can easily get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Respected

reviews.comment: There is a lot of homework but few formally graded assignments. It's feasible to do well in his class, although many will tell you he is a harsh grader, but you need to spend a lot of time and effort. Wells is very engaging and passionate about philosophy, however he asks a lot of his students who are mostly there for a major requirement.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There is a lot of reading for this class but overall this class became my favorite not because of the material but the professors he's wonderful very understanding and  helpful. he makes the class discussions the best and supper enjoyable.

40ish pages of reading between classes. Your actual grade is based on 2 tests (1 being take home and the other just a 10min oral exam) and 2 projects (1 of them being a dictionary-like definitions thing). If you do your work, you will be completely fine. He is a wonderful professor and his lectures are very interesting
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Reading/annotation heavy for homework assignments. He grades you based on a couple of assignments. I think as long as you somewhat put effort into his class, you will do fine. He does care about his students a lot and want everyone to succeed. If you need help in his class, i strongly recommend talking to him outside of class
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The worst professor I've ever taken. Grades so harshly and directions are unclear. If you have a choice do NOT take this man.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I loved the content of the class, but I was (personally) not a fan of the amount of reading for homework; however, I understand that it was necessary for class discussion and to wrap your head around philosophy. Prof was funny and made class fun. There are lots of discussions during class!
Mark is by far the best professor l've had so far. I looked forward to class every day due to his funny yet passionate classes that were a place to learn. He wants his students to pass!

I never thought I would enjoy a philosophy class, but I'm really glad I was required to take this one. It is a great eye-opener to disparities in healthcare and he helped us develop our argumentative skills for when some of us have to make these decisions in the future. Professor Well's passion for the subject also drove the class. I recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Weird at first but he grows on you. The more you participate in class the more you'll get out of this class. also he meant it when he said this class is truly a place to practice. He never seemed to judge anyone for not being able to complete a thought or phrasing something poorly. Really understanding and funny
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I enjoyed this course! Many people would say that there was a lot of work which there is but if it is a Mandatory class for you I think that should be expected. Does not grade the normal way has 4 assignments 3 which you get in total 7 tries to complete. Go to office hours because he will help you figure out what you need to do to pass.

One of my favorite parts of the classes was the engaging hypotheticals he asked, as if we were moral judges of the subject at hand. I took War and Peace with him, and although a serious topic that has a lot of taboo pieces to it, the class provides an extremely in-depth view and it is definitely the class I've learned the most in so far.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Extremely smart guy. He is pretty reasonable but his grading system is odd and he's a tough grader. He assigns a lot of work. Lots of writing. He is very accommodating and seems to be a genuine person.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: A expert when it comes to Philosophy, he really knows and understands what he teaches and he isn't afraid to get you thinking. His attitude and passion made the material and subject (which I thought was a bit boring) much more enjoyable. And above all else, he does what other teachers only claim to do, he lets you speak your mind.

Professor Wells loves to make an impact on students and strives to make them think deeply about their views on ethics. He creates a welcoming class environment and lectures well. There is a lot of reading and many annotations are required. Discussions in class are great if people participate. Would recommend getting the most out of this class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor was great! The office hours ensure you are doing the assignments correctly and this course is honestly very stress free. As someone with a huge class load, I was able to put this class on the "back burner" (obvi. not completely disregarding) but still received an A! This class will teach you a lot, get ready!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I loved this class, and Prof Wells was amazing! It wasnt an easy class, but I learned so much. Expect a good amount of (interesting) reading. Highly reccomend.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
Highly recommend Wells, especially if you're a philosophy major/have an interest in philosophy. I took a 2nd class with them & loved it

They are passionate, good at directing the conversation, extremely knowledgeable, & want you to succeed overall.

The assignments have clear expectations & can be resubmitted after receiving (very clear) feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! Had him over the summer and loved the class as a CS major. It opened my eyes towards more of the philosophy sector of studies.
The professor was amazing in explaining material and overall just really helpful in class and out of class! Would love to work with him again.

He is honestly such a sweet professor who truly cares about his students and loves to teach philosophy. His class is very lecture heavy, but in a fun and engaging way. Every class, the students sit in a circle with him and have long group discussion. Furthermore it is pretty easy to get an A in the class as long as you finish the few assignments.
Great Professor, always ready to help out and loves to talk about the topics taught in class.

Professor Wells is an awesome teacher! I had him for the summer semester and honestly wish I got to experience the full semester with him. A truly passionate, caring, and intelligent individual. His attitude is always motivating and each class leaves you hungry for more. I honestly have nothing bad to say.

Must complete 4 assignments to get an A. You get 7 opportunities to complete 3 of them and the last one is just a presentation you post on the discussion board. If you complete 75% of participation or less, you will get an A- but if you complete 90% or more, you get an A. Lectures were hard to follow and kind of boring but he's pretty easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very specific grader, the different grading system makes you do 1 assignment for half of a semester until you satisfy him. You will learn a lot but good luck with a homework.

Professor Wells was a good professor for someone who is excited about philosophy, or is intrigued by it. The syllabus is designed so you get out exactly what you put in.  A lot of people stopped showing up to class throughout the semester but can still get A- in the class (I think?)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Wells gives you 4 assignments and you get 7 tries to get a "complete", if you complete all the assignments then you get an A. He is a tough grader so it will likely take you all 7 tries. You have to do the readings in order to understand what is going on in class, and his readings are extremely extensive. His syllabus is insanely dense.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very unprofessional in class and then grades as if every student has a deep understanding of how to write in different forms correctly. You have to learn the lectures yourself outside of class and then apply it to assignments with virtually no help from him during class. Hard to go to office hours when he assigns hours of reading every week.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Wells uses a unique contract-based grading system that makes it easier to make a high grade in the class, however, it comes at a cost. He assigns a lot reading for each class (that is not suitable for a typical college schedule), and does not provide good feedback on his more difficult assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Wells is very passionate about his class and has a very engaging class period. He welcomes all thoughts and is a very kind professor. His grading is pretty tough but he gives numerous opportunities to complete classwork.

You don't actually have to understand the class content to do well in this class. You go to class to sometimes get a participation grade, but the topics of convo are useless. You are graded on 3 things and have 7 tries to get them all correct. Wells is definitely interested in the topic and is a good teacher if you r actually interested in the topi
Don't take this class unless you have to. So boring and assignments were always unclear what we were supposed to do. Grading is very strange with little feedback. Interesting topic taught poorly.

Honestly I loved this class- based off of 4 assignments- 4 "complete" is an A. While he is a hard grader you get 4 retries AND he is so so so helpful in office hours, so if you don't pass an assignment just meet with him in office hours and he'll help! He's a very passionate lecturer and hilarious I highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: i know i performed well in this class, but that doesn't mean much honestly 1. literally the assignments don't require u to actually attend lecture, u could do the readings and do well literally 2. rubrics and grading are WEIRD and USELESS in terms of providing what he expects 3. feedback is virtually none besides asking a question

Professor Wells is a passionate, very funny and nice guy. He is super helpful outside of class if you email him, but this class is a challenge. Concepts are abstract and sometimes it can be hard to follow. Also hard to understand what you got wrong on exams, but talking to him helps. Class is graded on about four grades, so that is a challenge.
The only thing this class has taught me is that I don't respect philosophers.

Makes no sense when he talks and assumes that everyone has the knowledge he has. Breaks us into breakout rooms where 99% of the time no one knows what we're supposed to be doing. Grades like a grad level course, not an entry level course. Gives no sensible feedback and again just assumes you have knowledge that you probably don't.

Research Ethics offers simple content but challenging writing assignments. Mark is extremely helpful in office hours. If you're stuck, go to office hours. Cannot stress how much office hours help. Philosophy is hard, even introductory philosophy. Go to office hours and he will help so much.

Wells is obviously very knowledgeable and passionate about what he's teaching which makes his classes very enjoyable and interesting but he is very unclear when it comes to grading and gives virtually no feedback. He will take points off for things that don't make sense, or is not even clear as to where points were lost and gives no explanation.

Prof. Wells runs a somewhat difficult class, but he seems to genuinely want to help you master the information if you reach out. His lectures can drag on a little, but his tests cover the material (especially the readings). Fair amount of reading outside of class but definitely doable. Obviously cares a lot about his students & the material.

Professor Wells is clearly very knowledgeable and passionate about the course material (of which there is a LOT of). The course is very reading focused and was by far my most labor intensive this semester. I found the material very interesting and the pace of the course reasonable. However, the feedback and grading for exams is very unclear.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Gives little to no feedback on obscurely and toughly graded tests. Seemingly assigns random percentages as your test grade, since his point system does not add up to what your final grade is. Claims to be an entry-level philosophy class; grades like a third-year class at best.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Wells is one of the worst professors I've had at Northeastern. He grades like it is a graduate level class with little feedback. The work he assigns is not manageable. I wish I didn't take this class with him, and would not recommend him to anyone.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Great lecturer and suoer friendly guy
Swell
Grades harshly but with little feedback. Material is slightly interesting, but not presented well. Class isn't taught like it's meant for non-philosophy majors.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: really passionate about what hes teaching and gives great support and feedback
Chill dude. He's the goat.

Prof Wolfe is super nice guy. His lectures are boring and I didn't really pay attention ngl, but that's finance for you. The tests are hard but as long as you study the assessments/hws A LOT you will be fine. Overall difficult class but he makes it a lot better and is always willing to help.

Super nice guy and he clearly wants everyone to succeed. Every test can be taken using excel and with a cheat sheet, but even if you don't do great on the tests, he offers lots of extra credit opportunities that go a long way.

Attendance isn't mandatory, but you're graded on participation for each unit using polleverywhere. He allows cheat sheets for both midterms and the final, and a bunch of easy points assignments and extra credit opportunities. I barely studied for this class (mostly just by making my cheat sheets) and got a solid A.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very nice guy but the class is entirely based on Connect assignments which can be random and not reflective of what we practice in class. The midterms and final are all on Connect, and they were a lot harder than the homework we had been given or the problems we did in class.
great guy and great teacher overall
Chill guy, reasonably difficult class. I sold with my grade, but that's on me, not Professor Wolfe. Zero complaints about the guy and would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's my goat, the best professor at NEU. He literally would let us go on 30min walks mid lecture because "people think better when they walk". If you have an option to take him, please do

Professor Wang is extremely passionate regarding what he teaches, and keeps class time engaging with thought-provoking group discussions. The final average is graded on few assignments and participation, but he is very lenient with attendance. If he is teaching a course you are anticipating to take, definitely take his class

Very good professor! Lectures are very interesting and he tries to make it very engaging and participative. Assignments are very straightforward with clear grading criteria and graded quite leniently. He initiates very interesting discussions on ethics and is very good at asking questions that make you retrospect. Overall, amazing experience!

Such a kind prof! I took his class summer in-person. The lectures and in-class discussions are very interesting! Attendance is graded, but you are allowed to miss a lot of days. Every assignment is allowed to be turned in 2 days late before points are taken off. There is 1 homework, paper, and a 5-minute presentation. A very fun class, I recommend!
Overall very chill class, grades very leniently and gives you a bunch of absences and opportunities for extra credit, highly recommend!
Great prof, very fun to be in class with. He's flexible and allows several absences, and has extra credit opportunities to earn extra points, late days, or absent days. Highly recommend.

Amazing professor - I've learned so much from this class. Each week is dedicated towards a specific topic, and it is presented in an engaging way with lots of class discussion. You can really tell he cares about philosophy and his students, with constant check-ins on homework and kindly granted extensions.   Definitely would recommend!!!

Great professor! Classes are almost exclusively discussion based, which I really liked. He gives very little homework and structures his classes so that they're very engaging with few lectures. It is obvious that he cares about his students and is very receptive to feedback. Overall, his Tech and Human Values class was my favorite of the semester!

Great professor. As long as you pay attention to the rubric and formats he mentions during class, it's a chill class. One homework per two weeks and a final paper determines most grades. He's super chill, and participating in discussions with him during classes is fun.

Wonderful guy. First year teaching, and he has it all down. Incredibly understanding, very open to feedback, genuienly works on making class more fun, better, and engaging. Class is genuinely interesting with many in class discussions. Truly cares about the subject and hopes that he can make his students interested. Nothing but respect for him.

Professor Wang's class was great. Every class included group discussions on interesting topics in tech and society. And Professor Wang is inviting and encourages a diversity of perspectives on every topic. There's a few papers throughout the semester but as long as you write your arguments in logical form and actually try, you'll do well.

Focuses on teaching practical skills rather then on grades making the class engaging for me. lectures were always fun and interactive. So many extra credit opportunities and he is very approachable when someone reaches out. This class genuinely motivated me to take more cybersecurity courses.

Engagingly teaches cybersecurity fundamentals through lectures and group activities. He's also one of the funniest teachers I've had. He tries to make the homework as interesting and enjoyable as possible and offers many extra credit opportunities.

I'm actually in the online section of this course (different prof), but he interacts with remote & in-person students so much that he might as well be the prof for both (also the online class uses mostly his lecture materials anyways). He's funny and there is a lot of EC that can be earned in the class. Justin hate is not tolerated!!
learning cybersecurity from justin was a lot of fun. he provides a ton of extra credit opportunities, so if you have any interest in the subject you will do very well in his class. great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Assignments can sometimes be hard but a lot of extra credit opportunities and extremely helpful and constantly online TAs. Justin's also really helpful and easy to reach out to. Generally not too hard a course though.
Justin is a great professor! I enjoyed this class, and although the assignments can sometimes be challenging, there is a lot of support outside of class. Highly recommend!

Chill professor that tries to make class fun and interactive. Final and midterm can be a little hard but projects are interesting and can make up for it. There are also extra credit opportunities and is a nice guy. Him and his TAs are also very accessible through discord.

Justin is a great professor who teaches a poorly made class. In regards to Justin, I would gladly take a class with him again. I have never wanted to engage in material more than when I can feel a connection to the professor, with Justin, he is cool guy who has fun while teaching, I literally played Valorant with him, he is a great guy and fun prof

Not an easy class per se, but you do end up learning a decent amount about the basics of cybersecurity and how to apply its principles. A fair amount of homework, but it's at least comparatively fun and does a lot to demonstrate the principles shown in lecture. 

Also Justin's such a troll he put a rick roll link in our final LMAOOO
He might seem good at the beginning but …. Make sure you read the syllabus before the drop without W

Future students be aware!! This course requires you to spend extra money to buy homework questions! I don't get how this professor's rating is still over 3, very lame if not disappointing professor. Be ready to get mad
Never seen a professor worse than this one. Low professionality, low empathy.

These reviews man. Some of you seem too dense to realize that just because you don't enjoy the class doesn't mean no one else does. Justin "gaming RMP"? Yeah right. Last reviewer even mentions no coding💀 Yeah because it's a CY course. If you guys can't just come to class or follow the instructions or do the EC (3+% so far), that's on you, not him.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Have learned nothing about cybersecurity, his lectures are so useless. All I;ve learned this semester is irrelevant history of some ciphers. No coding, and he is extremely unreasonable with expectations and does not provide relevant material in class to succeed in assignments. Picks favorites. Learned absolutely nothing. do not recomend.

Definitely the worst professor I have taken at my time at Northeastern. Funny to see that he is leaving himself reviews on rmp to try to improve it 💀. Completely unreasonable, trolls the class for no reason (eg: said he prepared a note sheet for the exam and then linked it to a rick roll). Opened up a project worth like 6% due same night. Worst.

Justin is fun and nice, and he's a good professor, but the class feels tedious. I need to beg the TA for hints for the assignments, which was painful. Also, it's not as easy to get an A as he says. Most people got between 77% to 85% on the midterm, and my assignment average is 87%. However, it's also a fun class with interesting assignments.
VERY DISAPPOINTED he tries to control this rating
yooo… the say about low rate comments disappearing are real… wtf
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: istg half the recent positive reviews are from the same person glazing because of the easy A, and half the negative reviews are from the same person typing LOL in every review. Just take Justin if you need the CY credit, he's not bad and you'll learn a bit from the stuff he posts outside of class as well.
They're literally so insane (in a good way though)

The lectures aren't super rewarding but he rewards you for participation and will give you a ton of professional resources and advice about the field if you ask for it. Lecture slides generally have more emphasis on security than attacking, so the CTF-style homework requires you to learn on your own. Notes-heavy since midterm is open notes.

The latest 5-star rating seems like be written by the profesor himself loll, yes this class is not hard, but will trick you if you don't beg the TAs for hints. The behavior of the lead TA emma and justin is just unbearable. Better take other profesors

Almost impossible to fail because of extra credit opportunities. You could probably sleep through the midterm and end the course with a 99. You can always get a response from him or his TAs in less than 10 minutes. You can even ask his chatbot trained with assignment info if nobody is available.

DO NOT TAKE!!! Quiz every week on random day for attendance. 2 midterms and final exam (My friend told me he didn't got any exams, only 1 final project, when he took it 3 years ago). You will need to deal with 1000+ slides for just the first midterm. LOL, this is the only class I regret taking for years in NEU. I hate this all so much.

If you do not have to take this class then you shouldn't. This class doesn't feel rewarding and feels tedious for the sake of it being tedious and you learn nothing from them. Not to mention he is a harsh grader and will find ways to take points off.

Just take Syssec with Kaan. That is an actual rewarding experience.
good video lectures but hard grader

there's a lot of writing assignments such as discussions or projects that are graded SO harshly. also literally nothing in the materials was related to any projects or homework. you can get by just so much extra work is needed it's insane
Insane TA..
the goat, work is easy and hw is fun

Awesome teacher, attend the lectures if you want to understand stuff. If you like cybersecurity definitely take this class. So many extra credit opportunities, it would be hard to finish without an A.

The most entertaining computer science professor I have ever had. He knows how to teach the material so that you understand, and also so that you don't fall asleep. He will work with you outside of class pretty much anytime if you need help, and also has a class discord for questions. Almost baited me into doing Cybersecurity lol
Professor Wang is super nice and always willing to help students and go over topics again.

This class definitely is interesting to say the least. I'm honestly not really sure what I learnt either. What I will say is that I would give it a higher rating if it wasn't for the two ridiculous exams and information from the class being hidden in discord.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: my favorite professor in khoury!! he's incredibly accessible after class if you have any questions and usually responds in less than 20 minutes. every student ik likes him. he's very enthusiastic about the material and engages with students even if the topic is lecture-heavy. the homework is fun but time-consuming, nothing crazy. def recommend!
very good prof.

The assignments takes hours every week, but as long as you get them done, you'll get the A. He said he's going to be making his quizzes harder for future semesters so that his class won't be such an easy A anymore, so beware.
I love justin wang
very good
Very easy class, professor is funny and give lots of extra credits

Justin is the goat. to say that I loved going to his class is truly an understatement. the homework assignments are easy to follow thru as long as you attend lectures and the quizzes are easy to get through. could not recommend him enough!
